### JanSz — 2016-09-05T18:03:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-blood-test-results-cause-for-concern-or-acceptable.18306/post-200918>

> [Videha said:](/goto/post?id=200888)
>
> thanks for that...was tad confused on why. will look up EPA to learn more.. i knew about rancidity of fish oil caps, so i was using orthoplex bio-omega liquid fish oil...expensive...
> You are being very helpful, thank you!!

Rancidity or freshness are other issues that may be real but I do not even consider them.
EPA is one of the omega3 fats.
Seafood and fish/krill oils have high amount of them, much higher than people should have (some or most are able to self regulate desired levels).
If I had it right, people process more omega3 when they farther from equator.
Eventually, everybody responds differently, plus within last centuries lots of people migrated around the world.
Also, we see lots of neolitic diseases. Many of them result in excess of omega3.
For some time I was observing lab test results of fatty acids analysis that people here are posting.
I noted that about half of those who posted had excess of omega3.
I am one of them.
Relatively to omega6 the omega3 fats are stronger. Excess of omega3 is suppressive to omega6 (but the reverse is not true).
I ended up dropping (most not all) of seafood from my diet.
You may want to ask your doctor for this test:

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

If you get it, post results, I will help you in evaluation,
but
it is rather self explanatory.
Ideal level=mid range of laboratory range for each fat
acceptable level=plus-minus 25% of range

////
Omega3 but specially DHA are very important to our well being.
The good news is that we need minute amounts of it and the one that we have, have very long half life, so the need for replacement is not frequent. Other that seafood there are other foods that contain omega3's.

### JanSz — 2016-09-05T18:40:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-blood-test-results-cause-for-concern-or-acceptable.18306/post-200920>

> [Videha said:](/goto/post?id=200886)
>
> Thanks for video JanSz.. my flutes are part of my healing regime, i am becoming the **semi-naked** flutist in backyard....wonderful feeling, more & more birds come daily, beautiful eagle yesterday circling.

Try totally naked at least for some time.
The skin that semi-naked covers also needs sun.

Hormones- skin.

Fat under skin and elsewhere contains aromatase (CYP19).
Aromatase is an enzyme that converts testosterone or androstenedione to estradiol or estrone respectively.
Fat people have more aromatase, they tend produce estrogens.
(they may need more progesterone to balance that excessive estrogen load)

Skin contains 5Ar and 5Br (reductase) enzymes.
There is significantly more 5Ar and 5Br in scrotal skin (and I think in breast skin specially aureola and nipples).
Those enzymes assist in conversions of
progesterone
androstenedione
testosterone
cortisol
cortisone
to downstream metabolites
[http://imageshack.com/a/img923/1937/AhxmtP.jpg](http://imageshack.com/a/img923/1937/AhxmtP.jpg)

Like anything else, sun exposure can only help.

////

plus 1 tablespoon Collagen Hydrosolyate (Great Lakes-usa)

### Lahelada — 2016-09-06T12:45:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-blood-test-results-cause-for-concern-or-acceptable.18306/post-200975>

Videha, at the risk of confusing you, not everything you read here is what Dr K recommends. A lot of people build their own n-1 which is fine it it works for them. Dr K recommends selenium and iodine in its natural package ,seafood in other words. His view is that we cannot eat DHA as fast as blue light destroys it. He does not recommend supplementing,heck he does not even recommend eating these days... I am facetious about the latter of course, but just to make a point that diet is the least of your worries . Cook like your Great gran did, add seafood, done. Follow the seasons once well, stay ketotic until you are. Whatever you put in your stomach has a slim chance of getting where you want it to go, at least at the beginning of your journey as your gut may be leaky. It is very tempting to want to speed things along . I got focused on DHEA supplementation in the first days of my journey for no better reason than it seemed to fit the bill . How little did I know and how little do I know still. Knowing little keeps the thirst for knowledge awake though.

### JanSz — 2016-09-06T19:34:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-blood-test-results-cause-for-concern-or-acceptable.18306/post-201004>

> [Videha said:](/goto/post?id=200969)
>
> Re: Lugols link you posted>[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00E3B49R2?redirect=true&ref\_=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o04\_s00](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00E3B49R2?redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_detailpage_o04_s00)
> i checked amazon>**International Shipping:** This item is not eligible for international shipping.
> Will speak to health food store manager & see if any way to get it into oz.where there's a will,there's a way..i won't quit. I need it. thanks J.

It does not have to be exactly the same product.
It is about content.
I have seen (unfortunately lost) information on how to make it home (gallons) from easily accessible ingredients.

Possibly right person will see this.

///
The idea is that adult person benefits by taking
50mg/day of iodine in form of Lugol's solution.
(Sometimes even 100mg/day)

By other standards this is very large dose.
Body will not use that amount,
but
it is easily discarded in urine.

That large amount of iodine/iodide will hopefully protect from assaults from other halides that are unfortunately very common.

Only iodide is halide (beneficial) to our health
the other (first three)
fluoride, chloride, bromide, astatide,
are common in our households

fluoride water, toothpaste, added to multivitamins (when prescribed by doctor)
bromide is added to flour, spa water
chloride added to pool water
\----------

A **halide** is a [binary compound](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Binary_compound), of which one part is a [halogen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halogen) [atom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Atom) and the other part is an [element](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chemical_element) or [radical](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radical_%28chemistry%29) that is less [electronegative](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electronegative) (or more electropositive) than the halogen, to make a [fluoride](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fluoride), [chloride](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chloride), [bromide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bromide), [iodide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iodide), [astatide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Astatide),

\==
Nuclear fallout contains radioactive iodine.
Doctors use radioactive iodine to destroy cancerous thyroid gland.

/////////////

on small budget.

1\. no plans to move. I am afraid that my new location may not be any better than what I have now. It is easy to overlook important data. Changes to my new location may have already been planned. The new discussed locations are hurricane/tornadoes prone. In present location I have lost one small tree due to bad weather within 40 years I am here.
Now I have family all around me, some few houses away.
population density increased within last 40 years, but it is still about 2100/sq-mile,
but significantly less near my residence.
I am originally from 10degree higher latitude (Berlin, Warsaw), I have moved significantly south already.

2.Spend maximum time outside, specially during day time. Barefoot, naked, no glasses.....

3\. 24hrs divided into (14hr day) + (10hr) night, that goes 365/24/7

### JanSz — 2016-09-06T20:42:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-blood-test-results-cause-for-concern-or-acceptable.18306/post-201008>

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Videha](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Videha)

**Videha** was an ancient Vedic Kingdom established by King [Janaka](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Janaka) whose trans-boundary is presently located in [Mithila](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mithila,_Nepal) federal state of eastern [Madhesh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Madhesh) in [Nepal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nepal) and the northern region of [Indian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/India) state of [Bihar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bihar).

During the late [Vedic period](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vedic_period) (c. 1100-500 BCE), Videha became one of the major political and cultural centers of South Asia,

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vedic\_period](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vedic_period)

The **Vedic period** (or **Vedic age**) (c. 1500 – c. 500 BCE) was the period in [Indian history](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_India) during which the [Vedas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vedas), the oldest [scriptures](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scriptures) of [Hinduism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hinduism), were composed.

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Historical\_Vedic\_religion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Historical_Vedic_religion)

The **religion of the [Vedic period](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vedic_period)** (also known as **Vedism**, **ancient Hinduism**, **Brahmanism** and **Vedic Brahmanism**[\[note 1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Historical_Vedic_religion#cite_note-5)) was the religion of the [Indo-Aryans](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indo-Aryan_peoples) of northern India.[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Historical_Vedic_religion#cite_note-FOOTNOTESamuel2010-6) It is a historical predecessor of modern [Hinduism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hinduism), though significantly different from it.

//

### JanSz — 2016-09-07T00:35:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/videhas-blood-test-results-cause-for-concern-or-acceptable.18306/post-201013>

I came across this site that sells internationally Lugol's solution.
(Very expensive)
[http://www.artpharmaceutical.com/pages/lugol-iodine-solution](http://www.artpharmaceutical.com/pages/lugol-iodine-solution)
I normally buy
J.CROW'S¨ Lugol's Iodine Solution(2 oz.)
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01FWM4J84/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o00\_s00?ie=UTF8](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01FWM4J84/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o00_s00?ie=UTF8)
I use 1 dropperfull=0.8cc a day, put it into my coffee
the label says
60mL
2% iodine
4% iodide
I do not know how much iodine is in potassium iodide, so i bunch the two and have 6%
dropperfull contains 0.8cc=0.8mL=0.8mg
6% of that is 48mg
so I assume that I use 48mg/day iodine
pretty close to doctor recommended 50mg/day (I thought)(see slide from presentation below)
now
on this artpharmaceutical.com site I see that
2.4mg potassium iodide = 1.8348mg iodine
so my 4% of iodide becomes
(4\*1.8348/2.4)=3.058
(2+3.058)\*0.8/100\*100=40.464mg
so I am not taking 48mg/day iodine
it is closer to 40.464mg/day
I am not changing my dose, one dropper-full is convenient to dispense,
but it is good to know about this.

Dr. K. Couldn't be more clear about supplementing .....he has been beating this drum for a long time now.

### JanSz — 2016-08-23T22:04:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-how-to-get-started.18308/post-200102>

> [Joeakim said:](/goto/post?id=200098)

>
> [https://www.bulletproofexec.com/is-nicotine-the-next-big-smart-drug/](https://www.bulletproofexec.com/is-nicotine-the-next-big-smart-drug/)

These downsides are the reason I recommend that, if you decide to use nicotine, you do it at very low dose **(1-2mg), and only occasionally** as needed. I’ve used nicotine a handful of times over the past four years and I’ve tried out many different forms, but it’s certainly not something I do regularly. It’s useful for an occasional boost.
\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[\[
The problem with nicotine gum is that chewing gum fires your trigeminal nerve more than it should be firing. Save your chewing for eating, and your jaw (and nervous system) will be healthier.
'''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''
**Nicotine lozenges mostly suffer from the same problem as nicotine gum;** they’re full of crappy chemicals and sweeteners like aspartame, acesulfame-potassium (Ace-K), and sucralose. **The safest one I’ve found is the Nicorette mini-lozenge, which is very small, and contains no aspartame**. You do get a small dose of unsafe sweetener, but it’s so tiny that it probably doesn’t matter. **I take ½ of the smallest 2mg lozenge, and feel a cognitive shift in about 15 minutes.** These are easy to find in the US. Go for the mini lozenges; large Nicorette lozenges are full of chemicals you don’t want.

.......................
**Nicorette mini Nicotine Lozenge Mint 2 milligram Stop Smoking Aid 81 count**
from [Nicorette](https://www.amazon.com/Nicorette/b/ref=bl_dp_s_web_2597365011?ie=UTF8&node=2597365011&field-lbr_brands_browse-bin=Nicorette)
[https://www.amazon.com/Nicorette-Ni...1-spons&keywords=nicorette+mini+lozenge&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/Nicorette-Nicotine-Lozenge-milligram-Smoking/dp/B0036WTVH2/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1471979156&sr=8-1-spons&keywords=nicorette+mini+lozenge&psc=1)

### JanSz — 2016-08-26T14:13:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-how-to-get-started.18308/post-200261>

**[https://www.drugs.com/dosage/methylene-blue.html](https://www.drugs.com/dosage/methylene-blue.html)**

**Renal Dose Adjustments**
No dosage adjustment recommendations available; however, should be used with caution in patients with severe renal impairment. Methylene blue is metabolized in the body to leukomethylene blue which is excreted primarily in the urine. Some unchanged drug is also excreted in the urine.

### Penny — 2016-12-15T00:04:54+0200

In my humble experience, do some face dunks prior to nicotine and be in the sunshine - good water with some methylene blue is also a hit - I have used the gum and just chew the first part and spit it out - after about 15 chews, I stick it between my bottom teeth and gums - I think even that wrecks my teeth though - this placement of the nicotine gum I got from nonchalent (member on this forum) - I have found research that states it rots your teeth/gums, so I do this instead:

I take BH4 to make dopamine (dopamine+tyrosine (found in any protein)=melanin)
sunshine hits melanin and splits water into hydrogen and oxygen -

However, I don't think you get the small superoxide burst with just BH4, so I'm doing fermented papaya for that... the superoxide burst is the signal for your mitochondria to repair - which is also what nicotine does -

The stuff made me nauseous without the face dunks - more good news for my biology

I think you can also use berberine (Oregon Grape root) for the ROS burst :
upperroomwellness.com/.../Berberine\_use\_in\_Stimulating\_Caspase\_Apoptotic\_Pathw.ay
"There are numerous other studies that may be cited, however, taken together, berberine may exert an antitumor activity in cancer cells by reactive oxygen species generation and mitochondrial-related apoptotic pathways. These findings provide an insight into the potential of berberine for cancer therapy."

In fact, here is a great article on regenerating melanin:
[https://doctorglen.wordpress.com/2013/07/18/regenerating-melanin/](https://doctorglen.wordpress.com/2013/07/18/regenerating-melanin/)

and here is one on glycine and ROS:
[http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1567576908002889](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1567576908002889)

and here's a nice article on why you would want a superoxide burst
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4197410/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4197410/)

and here is an article on nicotine and periodontal issues:
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2933534/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2933534/)

# Thread: Is Neurosurgery beginning to use light?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-neurosurgery-beginning-to-use-light.18313/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2016-08-23T00:33:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-neurosurgery-beginning-to-use-light.18313/post-200046>

Yes they are but not in the way they should. Stressed cells release ELF-UV making them easy to target with fluorescent dyes. You'd think they get that.........but nope.

Current Applications and Advances in
Fluorescence-guided Surgery

Wednesday, Sept. 14, 2016 | 6 p.m. CDT

Participants will receive an overview on the concept of fluorescence-guided surgery (FGS) for the resection of brain and spinal cord tumors. Neurosurgeons will understand the current technologies available for FGS and the different tumor types that can undergo FGS. Safety, diagnostic accuracy and Level 1 evidence in support of FGS will also be discussed.

Learning Objectives

Upon completion of this activity, participants should be able to:

-   Define the use of FGS for brain tumors with 5-aminolevulinic acid (5-ALA) and fluorescein;
-   Identify different brain and spinal cord tumor types that are amenable to FGS;
-   Distinguish the technical aspects of FGS, including the use of FGS for real-time intraoperative guidance, tumor margin visualization and reported extents of tumor resection;
-   Explain patient safety with FGS;
-   Demonstrate the difference between current and future FGS technology; and
-   Summarize the current U.S. regulatory state for FGS.

# Thread: So was Atkins wrong or just missing the sunlight and EZ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-was-atkins-wrong-or-just-missing-the-sunlight-and-ez.18312/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Richelle Jones — 2016-08-23T12:46:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-was-atkins-wrong-or-just-missing-the-sunlight-and-ez.18312/post-200068>

Atkins was my first step in this direction. I read his book in about 1998 with absolute fascination. His weight loss strategy worked for me (lost 30kgs of pregnancy weight) and I couldn't get enough of the info on the effects of lack of certain vitamins & minerals on mental & physical health...It is why I enrolled to study nutrition at uni..I was disappointed with how his website and message was hijacked by low carb phoods after his death. I think he would not have agreed with that.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-04T17:16:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-was-atkins-wrong-or-just-missing-the-sunlight-and-ez.18312/post-200855>

Scientific thinking is non linear. Small shifts in thought, and small changes in the energy of change, can lead to massive alterations of the end result. There is a lesson here for those who are way in front of a paradigm. The members of the American Medical Association should be embarrassed how they treated one of their own pioneers. They were caught with their pants down in broad daylight because of nutrition dogma of Walter Willet et al. The AMA bet on PhD Harvard researchers and not some "quack city slicker doctor" from NYC. They spend 45 years ridiculing Dr. Robert Atkins, and even brought him up on charges of fraud. Now in 2016 we have a complete paradigm flip were fat is once again good and refined carbohydrates might be problem in a low quantum yield world we've built. Atkins did not have it all correct, but was more correct than his accuser's could ever be. The link here is a pilot study so one should not expect any proper controls. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/27582035/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/27582035/)

### Valerie — 2016-09-04T18:23:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-was-atkins-wrong-or-just-missing-the-sunlight-and-ez.18312/post-200856>

In my forties I learned about the Atkins diet and it helped me maintain my weight. I continued to eat that way for about 10 years at which point I could only eat about 1000 calories/day to maintain my weight. After running into JackKruse.com and after about 1-2 month of CT (adding more DHA), I doubled my intake of food and was still able to maintain my weight. Later Dr Kruse then taught us about cell senescence and light. That was a break through for me. I am so much healthier today for it.

### caroline — 2016-09-05T09:36:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-was-atkins-wrong-or-just-missing-the-sunlight-and-ez.18312/post-200896>

I guess I took the same path more or less ....I had all of Atkins books, and did well - except for the no gall bladder problem.

His message really resonated with so many of us - it made so much sense to me - even in those days of low fat and high carb!

# Thread: flux optimal settings?

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Videha — 2016-08-24T17:33:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flux-optimal-settings.18319/post-200135>

Been using flux since i heard Daniel Vitalis talk about it quite some time back, I configured it, not really understanding the hows or whys, i just made it so my screen didn't hurt my eyes.

It's set on 5200 for day & 2700 for night...

There is a good chance i have it set all wrong.....lol Anyone have any experience with it & able to point me to the best settings for Optimal please? Thanks

### Rubicon — 2016-08-24T18:06:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flux-optimal-settings.18319/post-200137>

I found those settings still had too much blue for me. To address that I set the night mode to 1200k, and adjust the location throughout the day so that flux thinks it's always nighttime. It's a bit of a hassle but leaves my monitors very red all the time.

Screenshots here: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/thanks-for-all-this.18080/#post-197089](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/thanks-for-all-this.18080/#post-197089)

### Andrea — 2016-08-24T18:44:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flux-optimal-settings.18319/post-200138>

> [Rubicon said:](/goto/post?id=200137)
>
> I found those settings still had too much blue for me. To address that I set the night mode to 1200k, and adjust the location throughout the day so that flux thinks it's always nighttime. It's a bit of a hassle but leaves my monitors very red all the time.

That sounds complicated... At least on a Windows computer, you can make whatever settings you want in the registry.
HKEY\_CURRENT\_USER\\Software\\Michael Herf\\flux\\Preferences
There, select "Indoor" or "Outdoor" for night vs. daytime settings. Select "Decimal", and in the data field enter whatever number you want. Exit registry editor, restart Windows - et voilà.
For the time being, I like 900K...

### Jack Kruse — 2016-08-24T19:16:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flux-optimal-settings.18319/post-200140>

Removal of all artificial light at night is required.

### Rubicon — 2016-08-24T19:51:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flux-optimal-settings.18319/post-200145>

> [Rerun said:](/goto/post?id=200138)
>
> There, select "Indoor" or "Outdoor" for night vs. daytime settings. Select "Decimal", and in the data field enter whatever number you want. Exit registry editor, restart Windows - et voilà.

Ah, that's actually much easier. I've got mine permanently down to 900k now. Thanks @[Rerun](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9579/) !

I get that optimal is NO artificial light at night, however my context is i have very long history of injuries (all listed in my journal/blood tests) & a big black dog who constantly follows me around,we collide even with full lights on lol
tonight i tried walking around in the dark, had way too many mishaps with dog, don't want to injure him or myself.

# Thread: Looking for advice re home lighting

**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lisbeth — 2016-08-25T14:10:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-advice-re-home-lighting.18321/post-200177>

I wondered does anyone here have any suggestions that might be helpful for me.

I live with my husband (who gets home around 7pm most nights) and 8 year old son (who has homework most nights)

In winter it will be dark by 4.

I don't tolerate artificial lighting well at all. I also have problems with both candlelight and firelight (flicker is very uncomfortable and triggers severe migraine/fatigue)

Last winter we used Philips Hue lighting on DC in certain areas of the house, but as this requires wifi I guess I won't be using that this year. It did make winter bearable (that and not going out after dark)

I also will probably need to drive occasionally (and would like to frequently as we live in a rural area) and find headlights/indicators/any kind of flashing light, even during daytime in winter is also a potent trigger.

Anyone?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-08-25T15:24:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-advice-re-home-lighting.18321/post-200180>

Full spectrum bulbs. Read my London calling blog on linkedin for a lead.

### Lisbeth — 2016-08-28T09:46:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-advice-re-home-lighting.18321/post-200345>

Thank you Dr Kruse

I couldn't find much information on Nano-Lit but I will keep them in mind.

looking at Nature Bright now - I used to use one of their reading lights 5 or 6 years ago, and I just found it in the shed. Think I may email them about their room lighting. They seem to be saying on their website that this changes in spectrum throughout the day. (Obviously not a substitute for the real thing. )

# Thread: Sleep ... what is the definition of great sleep according to Jack Kruse?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-what-is-the-definition-of-great-sleep-according-to-jack-kruse.18322/>

### Joeakim — 2016-08-25T16:54:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-what-is-the-definition-of-great-sleep-according-to-jack-kruse.18322/post-200191>

I've had nearly 9 hours of sleep last night. Yeah, I woke up 6:30am got ready and went to my sun gazing ritual. As of late, I have been getting up within 6 am range despite my sleep time starts at 1am past midnight due to work shift (I'm working to change this, btw). Until last week, I woke up in 7am + and went to reset my circadian rhythm.

I wonder if my earlier wakeup times are due to reset of my circadian rhythm?

I know by sleeping around 1 am I am not getting full dose of sleep cycle including autophagy. So then what gives? Am I getting sicker by not sleeping enough till 7am or 8am? What is the min/max sleep time for the masses? What is it for me? Is it differ due to n=1? Most likely, and I feel like need minimum of 6 hours imho. The funny thing is that lately even though I am only getting less than 6 to 5 1/2 hours of sleep, I don't need to nap. Is it due to the morning sun and mid-day sun exposure?

How many hours of sleep hits your sweet spot?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-08-25T17:04:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-what-is-the-definition-of-great-sleep-according-to-jack-kruse.18322/post-200196>

That will be coming in a new sleep blog. You can read the Quantum sleep blog for the initial ideas. More to be teased out soon but members will understand the new ideas because of the last 4 webinars best.

### Joeakim — 2016-09-14T16:47:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-what-is-the-definition-of-great-sleep-according-to-jack-kruse.18322/post-201433>

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) You said the sleep is an outlier since better the circadian rhythm, better the sleep.

I've gotten up and sun gazed every morning including cloudy days, for a month(?), to reset the Master Clock, yet my wake up time varies from 6 AM to 8 + AM although I tend to go to sleep by 12:30AM - 1 AM. What's happening? And on the days I get less than 6 hours of sleep, psychologically I stress a little knowing that I "should" get at least 7 hours. I try to nap but to no avail since I'm alert and have energy.

### Mover — 2016-08-26T03:36:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing.18326/post-200238>

From recent study and talking with experts in the field of Dirty Electric and grounding, it appears to me that ground, or Earthing as people call it is useless indoors surrounded by electrical fields, or Dirty Electric as they call it. The following makes sense as it was explained to me in an email by an expert in the field named
Eric Windheim, the founder of Windheim EMF Solutions. A certified Building Biology Ecology Consultant (BBEC) and Electromagnetic Radiation Specialist (EMRS)

When grounding inside with your feet on a grounding mat or bed sheet surrounded by electrical fields...

"Your body now has an outlet for the electricity to go to but you are now an antenna and part of the conductive circuit for the electric field in the house going to the ground. It is better to reduce the electric field by shutting off circuits or at the very least filter the DE (Dirty Electric) out of the field."

So, Do your grounding outdoors. Shoes and socks off, feet on the grass, or hug a tree..

### Eugenia — 2016-08-27T04:37:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing.18326/post-200302>

If I setup a camping tent with a normal air mattress on my backyard, would I be grounded while sleeping?

### Michael Polito — 2016-08-27T04:51:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing.18326/post-200303>

Hello Eugenia, If the fabric or vinyl you are sleeping on is conductive yes. Plastic, wood, vinyl is not conductive. The closer can expose the skin of your body to the skin of the earth, the better. barefeet againist wet grass is best. Naked body against wet grass is awesome !

### Eugenia — 2016-08-27T05:16:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing.18326/post-200304>

The mattress is plastic. However, I've only being real camping once, and I slept so well, and then I was sleeping extremely well for a week after that (I usually would sleep at 1 AM back then, and after camping, I'd sleep by 9 PM). So whatever happened in that 1-day camping trip, with that tent and mattress, worked.

### Michael Polito — 2016-08-27T05:55:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing.18326/post-200307>

Well you are certainly getting closer to the ground camping outside, lying in a tent on the ground, rather than in your steel framed bed in your house lying on a mattress, and box spring. I tend to take by shoes off and sit outside year round for 10 -20 min per day minimum. Normally in the morning, facing the sun, wearing as little clothing as possible. I am naturally a barefoot person year round whenever possible. Your goal is to get as close to the ground as much as you can everyday. Ultimately, skin of your body to skin of the earth, or as close to that as possible. Do it and make it a habit. Good luck Eugenia

### Lola Roman — 2016-09-03T18:15:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing.18326/post-200795>

Question on grounding sheets (pardon my ignorance on the subject): Are they still bad for you if you ground them with a rod to the dirt outside your room instead of using the power outlet?

### Michael Polito — 2016-09-03T18:26:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing.18326/post-200797>

> [Lola Roman said:](/goto/post?id=200795)
>
> Question on grounding sheets (pardon my ignorance on the subject): Are they still bad for you if you ground them with a rod to the dirt outside your room instead of using the power outlet?

Hi Lola,

From all the a research on this, it is said that its best to ground directly to a grounding rod outside your house. Running the wire out a window.. But running it to the ground in the wall outlet works fine also. I do both. Some of these pads and blankets come with outdoor and indoor grounding cables. Some companies slice in a 100k ohm resistor in the grounding wire or plug for safety in case of lightening strikes. I just do not use my grounding pad if there is a thunderstorm. In fact, i don't use any electrical gadgets during a lightening storm. TVs. Computers, Phones etc.

### Remo — 2017-03-11T17:34:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing.18326/post-210130>

I also came across this idea some years ago. They also mentioned that instead of grounding yourself it would be better to ground your electrical devices directly to the ground and use a faraday cage if possible.

Personally, I ground myself with a rod straight into my backyard and find that I sleep better with it but it is imcomparible with sleeping directly outside. It would be interesting to see some actual studies being done on this subject.

### Jan Christer — 2017-03-23T23:17:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing.18326/post-210979>

The reason it might not be good to ground yourself to an electrical ground is that it might not be clean, but rather often carries stray voltage, commonly of the order of about 100 mV, from what I have measured.

### drezy — 2017-03-27T22:50:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing.18326/post-211244>

@[Jan Christer](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19850/)
You're right. I spent more years than I care to admit chasing "dirty" grounds down in the semiconductor world. If any section of grounding in your system was "too small" for the instantaneous power requirements in the system, your system will fail. My job was to create scenarios to promote these fails before they got into the field. In short, the easiest way to do this is to toggle as much sh\*t as possible as fast as possible in a system (aka flicker/high frequency change).

Though great grounding systems can be designed, I suspect Jack suggests good old fashioned grounding for the following reasons:

1.  Nobody knows if your electrical grid is clean, runs 40 high power computers, shares a grid with others who do, is close to a radar installation, is close to a cellphone tower, or is literally disconnected from the earth (a whole factory once had this problem with what they assumed was a safe ground)
2.  If you run a cable to a separate ground nobody knows if it runs right past your mains supply conduit, runs past a higher power line (water heater/oven/...), or if your grounding rod is touched by a root of a tree that is also nearly touching a high power line (that nightmare was in one of Jacks podcasts though it was a plumbing line in a previously well tested house--the world changes on you)

Simply standing or laying on the actual earth (or in the ocean/streams/...) is really hard to beat when it comes to having as fail proof a system as possible. I'm as guilty as the next person when wanting to devise gadgets and systems, but you have to look yourself in the mirror and say "I'm smarter than evolution and the universe." If you can keep a straight face when you do that I wish you all the luck.

### drezy — 2017-03-28T03:00:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing.18326/post-211254>

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)
I hope all is going well.

I've worked on these engineering problems on the semiconductor level. I can totally geek out, and will below, but I've come to see things Jack's way. It may seem harsh when Jack drops a "It's your environment. You have to change it/move", but more and more I think that is the best fix.

It's kinda like people (part of me as well) are asking Jack something like:

"Hey I have this 900,000 V 12 foot tall tesla coil on in my living room all the time and when I get anywhere near it a fluorescent tube the tube lights up. Just help me get the tube to not light up and please don't comment on the 12 foot tesla coil in my living room."

Jack is seriously attempting to help with his "relocate" advice and not being glib.

\*_Geek out time with @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) and @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)_ \*
Commercial grade DC to AC power inverters kinda suck from the perspective of this site/JK findings. If I had solar panels, I'd start just looking into DC powered(boat/RV) appliances. Even disregarding all the nasty junk that inverters dump out (harmonics-wise) they are also power inefficient(aka often you're throwing out 30-50% power).

I'd love to crack one of these DNA units open. I highly suspect that the frequent mention that they are not capacitive (shunting unwanted energy into chemical charges) filters implies that they are inductive (shunting unwanted energy into magnetic fields -- uhoh bad). They don't explicitly say on their site but the "heat" conversion they mention jives with inductors (wound wires & magnetic fields \*bad\*). With higher GigaHz signals waveguides could direct signals away but the 16kHz,32khz... is nowhere near the waveguide/skin effect frequency.

When you filter frequencies (sub GHz) you're doing it with low-pass filters using capacitors OR inductors(sometimes both).

I would not buy that unit unless I saw a schematic first.

### JanSz — 2017-03-28T14:53:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing.18326/post-211279>

@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/)

Interesting, I was trying to reach their web site and got this:
**This domain name expired on Mar 26 2017 01:18:16:000AM**

So there was warning that in case of excessive vibrations their unit would have to be installed on separate foundation.

So, it looks like, those filters convert electrical dirty energy into mechanical vibrations (mostly).

It is a lots of energy.
You said 30-50% power may have to be wasted in DC to AC power inverters.

any solar or wind power (assuming that it is successfully collected and is already stored in (expensive) batteries) would have to shake out 30-50% of its content before it could be usefully used.

[http://electronicdesign.com/energy/build-efficient-500-w-solar-power-inverter-using-igbts](http://electronicdesign.com/energy/build-efficient-500-w-solar-power-inverter-using-igbts)

The issue for mitochondriacs would still be the harmonics produced by rapid switching that mimics an AC wave.

Given that. If I had Solar (DC) available I'd be highly tempted to just upgrade at least my "always on" appliances (like the fridge) to DC and wire it appropriately.

BTW I grew up playing on the shores of that river! It was literally part of my backyard!

Back to the core Grounding issue. I'm low tech when it comes to important things like grounding.

Fill Beer pitcher with water
Walk outside
Pour half of water on ground
Stand barefoot on wet spot
Drink other half of water

Interestingly, we're looking at an off grid property right now, and naturally it has solar already installed. I'm not going the filters route, so the only chance of moving forward is if we rip out the inverter and use all DC. Without much expertise in this area, it seems doable but of course not being an engineer there are unknowns for the cost and know-how to make it happen. There's already one or two DC circuits in place, and the major appliances that come with the place (fridge, stove) are already set to use propane. Or I suppose keep AC circuit off but available for occasional and specific and very temporary uses --- maybe.

### drezy — 2018-01-25T01:11:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-earthing.18326/post-233408>

> [JoeBranca said:](/goto/post?id=233405)
>
> Interestingly, we're looking at an off grid property right now, and naturally it has solar already installed. I'm not going the filters route, so the only chance of moving forward is if we rip out the inverter and use all DC. Without much expertise in this area, it seems doable but of course not being an engineer there are unknowns for the cost and know-how to make it happen. There's already one or two DC circuits in place, and the major appliances that come with the place (fridge, stove) are already set to use propane. Or I suppose keep AC circuit off but available for occasional and specific and very temporary uses --- maybe.

Hey Joe! First of all congratulations for getting a far as looking at places. That sounds pretty good. You'd want to do your shopping for RV and boat appliance and tech.

Keep in mind that small inverters can be stationed (hopefully not under your prostate) and left off until needed. Like this one ( [https://www.amazon.com/Ampeak-Power...921&sr=8-2-spons&keywords=400w+inverter&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/Ampeak-Power-Inverter-Converter-Charger/dp/B0714B231X/ref=sr_1_2_sspa?ie=UTF8&qid=1516833921&sr=8-2-spons&keywords=400w+inverter&psc=1) ). There are very likely even "cleaner" ones but the utility depends on your use model. If only for very infrequent use I'd probably throw in a cheapie and stay away from it while it's switched on.

Also there are some easy to use calculators to check wire gauge (cross sectional area) vs the DC load you will be using vs your current wiring if you ever want me to point you to it.

Good luck moving forward. This sounds exciting.

### Cdk29 — 2016-08-26T14:08:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-france.18327/post-200260>

Hi !

I'm french, so pardon me if my English doesn't make any sense once in a while x)

I'm from Britanny and goes often on Paris for my studies (RNA genetics).

Btw, I've done a Leptin RX once in the past, to solve my hyperphagia. I do it again to loose my weight/heal my body (and perhaps my gut, with the leaky gut prescription except for a morning coffee ) I'm here mostly to get advice on realy specifics questions\* and writte a journal

\* Question be like : I swim one hour or more in cold sea almost every morning. Quitte enjoyable for the body, make the leptin RX far more efficient and handlable. It's not as cold than the Cold Thermogenesis but not so far (~3-4 °C in difference). But that make me awfully sleepy at 22 h 30 - 23 h instead of 23 h 30 - 00 h, and so make me get up one hour before my usual pattern the next day. (disclaimer : before you tell me it's nice, our schedule time here have a decay with the sun, which rise up at 7 am instead of 6 am for example ^^) At least it's a sign that it's work (Brown fat make melatonin, Am I wrong ?) I will try some multi vitamin B supplement to slighty alter my circadian rythm in the next day !

Bref, thanks for the reading and have a good ~day~ morning x)

# Thread: I never wanted to write this to be honest
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-never-wanted-to-write-this-to-be-honest.18332/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hairless Samson — 2016-08-28T12:33:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-never-wanted-to-write-this-to-be-honest.18332/post-200347>

This is a rather long text and I didnt reread it, as I don't have time for it right now. Please excuse the amount of writing errors because of that and also because my native language isn't english

Normally I wouln't write this but a while ago Lilly asked me if I had a personal story written down about me. So today after some sun I thought oh well, let's just do this. Maybe some people can relate to this.

Grandma and mother:

My grandmother was raised and worked on a farm her whole life (which is good) and had a lovely child (my mother) who ended up in medicine as a radiologist (not good) for 30 years shining x-rays on other people and herself spending hours at night in front of a screen caring for others. Not only that - she worked also as a medical journalist which made her work day start at 5 am til 2 am. Who needs sleep anyway? She never had luck with her husbands and divorced three times, although she beared two children (my older brother, and me - one being rather complicated...guess who) - so yes I am a second son and some might think about depleted resources when getting a second child, combining it with her workaholic past.

Personal Story:

From what I remember I was a sugar addict and couch potatoe until I reached the age of around 16/17 years, being frustrated with life spending most of my time in front of a computer, playing games or writing poetry. I rarely spent time outside and had some serious conflicts from time to time with my mother (like I said - one of her childs was rather complicated). At the age of 17, having done martial arts since I was 5 years old, I started doing mixed martial arts with some friends at school. Getting excited about fighting I started running in the early mornings before heading to the university and even started lifting weights. I slowly got addicted to competitions (which was rather my true nature). I even had a girlfriend for one and a half year until i was 20 years old. Life couldn't get better and I actually looked rather healthy. Well sometimes life takes some really shitty turns.

After one and a half year of a happy relationship with my girlfriend (we were planning a lot of stuff we wanted to visit, try out etc.) she one day crashed into a tree with her car. She made her license 2 months ago. She died instanly without me being able to say goodbye or anything. Describing how I felt when i heared what happened from her mother is not really easy and to be honest I actually can't remeber much, which my be a protective thingy. The only things I remember rather good was her pale face surrounded by her red hair in the hospital and the terminal feeling when the coffin was pushed out of the church, telling me that everything was real.

Shitty thing about all this is that I am rather a very loyal person and I was very empathic in those days. I couldn't handle the whole thing. I didn't cry at start - I rather thought to myself that I would end my own life. But not the easy way. I wanted to get smaller and smaller while still functioning as good as I could with all the things I knew about nutrition at that time. Sounds crazy maybe, but thats how it went. I was muscular and around 85/90 kilos at start. I totally messed up my studies and spent hours researching stuff, learned to read things on pub med at the age of 20/21 and slowly got down to 57kilos (~125lbs) at the hight if 5,9 feet.

I was obsessed with reaserch and control. Most of my friends distanced themselves from me and I still remember one friend (Elena she was called) asking me where the other half of my body was. I didn't care what they said. I felt supreme in my knowledge and influence. I didn't realise how impulsive, impatient and asocial i've gotten - but who cared anyways? Well there was one person, who cared a lot about it and it was almost too late until I realised it.

My mother took me to some doctors to get some tests to see if I already had some serious diseases. My immune system should have been pretty trashed by now. My mother was a tough person, she didn't show any strong emotional reactions to my behaviour, tried to support me with the thing, called life. What I didn't know is, that her own life suffered and her work wasn't top notch anymore. Her colleagues asked what was going on, as she didn't perfom anymore as she did before. She then told her colleagues about me and how I slowly dissapeared every day a little more without her being able to stop it. Well back to the tests.

The doctor had to try three times to get some spinal fluid out of me, as there was almost nothing left (if I remember correctly). When we got the results and the doctor said that there was no sign of a serious disease in me (I'm such a lucky bastard), my mother - and that was a first for me - started to cry. She was so relieved that she couldn't hold her emotions back and finally, after two years of fanatical madness - I realised, what I had done. Not to me - that was still of non importance - but to those who still cared for me, who cared for me the whole (fucking) time and never left my side, while I was a stupid fucking idiot. And yes, while writing this, I feeld like shit and tend to swear, because I'm getting rather emotional.

Shortly after (end of 2007) I had one goal in mind. Get your other half back as fast as possible and well - I went back to around 90 kilos in about 3-4 months eating cookies, cakes and everything in my path. You could say by now that that trashed my body even more. My mother, again, was happy that I changed my mind but was a little concerned about my (yet again) extreme reaction. Anytime something about healthy eating came up from this day on, my mother had problems talking about it. It always remembered her about what happend.

Instead of distancing me from food, health and anything that has to do with it, like my mother, started to get even more obsessed with it - but this time in a rather good way - at least thats what I think. I knew how to read scientific papers, when I was 20 years of age. I didn't want to spend hours every day sitting in front of a computer until my own brain circuits would break down. I started to do my trainer license instead and wanted to see if getting into fitness and health would be something that I could do as my job. I enjoyed it pretty much, loved the scientific background about how the body worked (even though it wasn't really getting into details) and decided to study health and fitness management with focus on rehab and prevention. I wanted, and that is still part of my conviction even today, to help others avoid what happened to me, help them with the pain they had (even though I degenerated to a rather introverted person I was still rather empathic).

Remember that I had a tendancy to be rather extreme with my decisions and loyalty? Well I started my dual study system (work at a gym and do your studies at the same time) at a bigger, but rather family-oriented gym in the southwest of munich (yes I'm German, so excuse the wrong spelling ;-) ). After the new clubmanager ,who started half a year before me, kicked out almost all the old workers, who had been there fr 10 years or longer, we were rather low on manpower. The reason she did that, was that they were giving people protein-shakes on the house, drank alcohol with the members while working etc. - like I said that gym was rather...well it had seen its best days a couple of years ago and degenerated ever since. All this ended in me standing at the reception like an idiot for at least 60 hours a week, while almost every member in the gym looked at me like I was an enemy of the state because I was the unqualified replacement for their friends. My clubmanager spent her time mostly in the office, trying to avoid direct contact, when a class had to be canceld,or someone had some issues with payment and all the good stuff. People can get pretty pissed at the reception - and they don't care if you are responsible or not. Time for me to get a little less emotional, maybe - just for my own sake.

Slowly more and more students (theyre cheap as f...) started to work for the gym and we slowly increased our strength. As i already had my trainer-license I was able to make trainingsplans and the manager was able to hide even more in her office. I had to take care of people with diseases, or in pregnancy and some trainers would say that they don't have the expertise to do that (you don't as a trainer - the license is a joke tbh), but I was brave, or stupid enough to get all the informations I needed - no I WANTED to be of use.

My studies progressed and so did I. I started to develop skills besides taking care of sick people in the gym, like making pictures with photoshop for the marketing of the club and even learned to make a website. Both were great for the gym - they didn't have to pay $$ and I was happy to be of service! I even got a license in Bodypump and soon gave one of the best visited classes of the week (wednesday 17:30 was packed). I had bonded with that club and with all the members. While others spent their weekends with friends or other stuff, I was in front of the computer, trying to learn more about anything that was related to human health (or was working at the gym - who needs free time anyway...). I didn't know shit about Jack at that time and surely would have dismissed all of it as bs at that time. If I remember correctly I slowly started to learn about posture, supple lepards and myofascial release.

Finally I finished my studies with good grades and knew more about the human body by my own research than my studies, but at least i now had a license to k...train people rather professionaly (Germans are all about licenses). The bad thing was, that the owner of the gym always tried to produce more money by reducing costs (and quality) and even though all students did a great job at being true "servants" - people got pissed off (I mean hey, we knew how much members we got a month by making lines on a paper to count them - software is such a complicated thing and most of our pcs were suffocation in spam and viruses anyway!). So it looked like I would have to find a new gym where I could start working again.

Not so fast. A new owner of a gym chain appeared who had the habit of taking over run down gyms, renovating them and expaning his business that way. Holy shait! If that would work I could still take care of my members in that gym, which I had bonded with for a couple of years now! Not only that - The two owners of the new chain visited us by surprise and I was actually giving a class at that moment, but it seemed like I peaked their interest or maybe some members spoke well of me. Anyhow they proposed to me, that I would be the new clubmanager at the age of 25 in october 2013. My mother was excited (she was watching my workaholic extreme behavior all the time and yes i spared you alle the nasty things that happend at that time like puking of exhaustion and internal conflicts, intrigues etc. and ye I also forgot a lot - again I think as a defence mechanism), I was excited - everyone was excited. So - fully motivated I started my new assigned job. Suddenly I had to lead people, who were my colleagues before and of course I had to prove to them, that I was able of leading. I was at the gym at 8:30am till 9:30 pm 5-6 days a week, working either as a manager, helping with the renovation together with my team (with hammers, chainsaws and everything - the new owners wanted to save money), selling memberships to people or train them in the small changing place we had left. Of course the gym didn't close while we had to renovate. We still had to make money and I remember clearly how me more or less only had the reception left while the foor was ripped out, lights were coming from flashlights all over the place and cables were laying around everywhere. We still made 50-80 members a month selling a vision we had. The new design we proposed for the club was designed by me and my right hand, implementing everything we knew about new training methods, functional training etc.

I could talk about that time a lot more, but this text is getting huge already so ill spare you that. A few things might be of interest though. The two biggest burdens I had to carry at that time was, that payments for the workers weren't paid by the company. They always assured me that the money would come the next few days, but it didn't. I got calls from pissed off floor layers etc. which had to wait for their money for months or even longer. I felt miserable and stressed because I coulnd't do anything and I also had to pay the class instructors, my team and had seriously no money. But I wanted this gym to rise and shine again. At whatever cost I would have to pay. My behaviour changed also. At start I was very direct looked grim alot and didn't understand why my team sometimes avoided me. Well I was told afterwards that I sometimes had an aura around me that looked like I would kill someone if they would even dare to open their mouth in my presence. One could assume that I was a little overwhelmed with what I had to do.

One year passed and somehow my team and me went through hell and came out of it alive. There were still a lot of things to do, but the gym finally looked again like a gym. One thing the CEOs of the company were puzzled about was how we managed to get so many new members and had such a small fluctuation at the same time. I mean yo! We had nothing but a reception, crappy air and a lot of garbage most of the time.

### Hairless Samson — 2016-08-28T12:33:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-never-wanted-to-write-this-to-be-honest.18332/post-200348>

We replied that probably our motivation, bonding and our vision about the new version of our beloved gym pulled people in. We suffered enormously and I am still greatful for my team at that time, bearing all the shit we had to carry. All of us wanted this nightmare to end. We pushed ourselves with the expectations we had to reach every month, coming from the central, 3 1/2 hourse away. The CEOs almost never visited us. It took too long and even though we didn't have any experience with what we were doing, we seemed to be doing pretty damn well and were announced one year later as a best practice for all other clubs of the chain. Huge motivational boost for us again.

I still remember 2014 when we finally got a visit from the two managers and their right hand. They were taking a stroll around the club and I was talking to Chris (right hand) in my office. He was complaining about his hip. He loved running. So I started to check his feet, hip flexors etc and worked on him for about one hour. When he was back in Karlsruhe (thats where the central is), he wrote to me that he didn't have any issues anymore and was amazed how I knew that. Long story short - at my birthday (23.03.2014) the CEO asked my if I wanted to become an areal manager for the company, mainly for educating the trainers and giving lessons. My mother was exited, I was excited, everyone was excited!

First thing I did was to visit all the other (10) gyms and speak to all the personal. I wrote a 25 page report about who would be suited to be the leading trainer in which gym, what theirs strengths and weaknesses seemed to be and why I decided to recommend them. I wanted to build a network for the trainers, where I focussed on some trainers in every gym, that would share their new knowledge with every other new trainer. So the first few months I was driving around in southern Germany, talking to everyone, sometimes meeting up in Karlsruhe and still taking care of my homebase in munich. Shortly after, I started to give lessons at the central for anyone who wanted to go there. Don't forget that I still worked all the time in the standard blue lit environment for very long hours, drove by train a lot and worked out like a mad man all the time (I had to be a shining example of strength and fitness, ya know? And ye I was pretty strong actually).

The CEOs were happy with my performance but wanted more. I was in. I loved responsibility and liked the comparison of my chief with the warriors attitude even though I knew he only tried to manipulate me into an even more motivational frenzy - I didn't care I just wanted to help people with everything i knew.

Something strange happend suddenly after I once again drove from gym to gym and after I returned. I felt really crappy the folloing weekend and though that I had catched a cold. I was 26 years old, actually walked in a closeby forst at 4am in the morning with my mother waiting for the sunrise (we had that silly idea) and my whole body was in pain.I was told to just relax the rest of the weekend and even though I still felt crappy on monday, I went to work anyway. I have responsibility, right? Don't be such a whimp!

Well my left foot got swollen and started to hurt from sunday to monday and i started to walk like doctor house. It hurted pretty badly and I thought to myself that maybe I had torn some muscle fibers or something else. Anyway I just continued training on high intensity and worked my normal 12ish hours every day. On wednesday I was so bad - I just wanted the pain to stop. I asked my right hand to drive me to the hospital close by. Many of the nurses were actually training in our gym which was rather cool. I didn't had to wait long and a lot of similar faces were taking care of me, wondering what such a fit guy would do in a hospital.

Moritz - I dont know why, but it looks like you have a thrombosis.

What? How could someone invincible like me, an achiever and leader like me who knew everything about health and fitness, have a thrombosis. Clearly this was a mistake. Others get sick but dude - my body is more a cyborg that human. I had proven that for years now. I was able to take on everything!

Well they were right. I had a stuffed vene in my left calf, which was the reason why my left foot looked like a coconut. I was cool, laughed it off and they told me that I would have to take a drug called Xarelto until January 2016 (it was November 2015). My mother came over to pick me up from the hospital. She shook her head, seeing me there lying on the bed, telling me not to overdo it again and to stop being so extreme. I laughed that off too. Then one thing happend when we left the hospital after me getting an injection to stop the pain.

I cried. I never cry, but I cried. I couldn't retain ist. It was like a burst of snot (sorry) and tears that I couldn't stop from holding back. My mother looked at me totally puzzeled and tried to calm me down a bit. Do you guys know why i cried? It wasn't because I had something ugly on my leg or had to take meds. It was for just one stupid reason. I was disappointed with my body. How THE HECK could my body not handle the things that I had to do? Why did it break down? I was, no, I AM invincible and I have things to build, people to help and responsibilities to take care of. I don't have time for this. My body was pathetic!

The drug that I had to take seemed to work and I went back to work the day after tomorrow. Bad side effect was that it also worked like a sleeping pill as a side effect for me (even though I took it in the evening). But oh well, you know I had a big presentation on January 31 (2016) that I didn't want to postpone because of my internal failure and I was looking forward to it. I was a two day class for all the top trainers in the company to get them on the same level in diagnosis (e.g. FMS) some hormonal stuff etc. People loved my presentations, or so I was told, as I always put in jokes, visualised most of the complicated stuff and was used to talk in front of others. And well ye - even though I was tired all the time, I continued training (I didn't sweat one bit under Xarelto which I didn't look into until now) like always, took care of my gym, wrote my presentation whenever I had time and the two day class for the trainers was a total success.

CEOs got greedy again! After that decent job I did, they pro posed that I should drive from gym to gym, take over the management for a while and focus on getting that studio back on track. I didn't have social life, all my girlfriends agreed that I was nice but never had time for them anyway - so yeah nothing was holding me in munich - if I can be of service to my company that way, why not? Oh yeah, I totally forgot to mention that somehow the company always had issues to pay bills, but well I got used to it and was getting better at finding excuses why I couldn't pay for some classes directly etc. Maybe that was just how it was. It still stressed me because it felt like i owed people some money and I still tended to take it personally, but well there was more going on, that I would learn soon enough.

I will keep it short from here on out and thanks for reading through all the lamenting and writing errors until now. You must either be very inquisitve or amazingly bored (just kidding  ).

I moved to Bayreuth and started to take care of the gym located in that area. As always even though I had to take care of financial stuff and things a club manager should do, I focussed mainly on my team, teamwork and was writing trainingplans for as many members as possible. Money was, as always, an issue and I had to talk about payment almost every day at least once. Well I was used to it anyway. People were greatful. I was able to help people with MS a little, as I already knew about the link with ketosis (not yet with light) and people with morbus bechterew slowly were able to make a whole thoracal spine rotation again. People looked at me though as if I was a little crazy because for SOME REASON I started to sit in ice cold rivers for as long as I could... Bayreuth is not that big of a town and people started to greet me on the streets (I was pretty amazed about that). Everything went well. We made some deals with leasing companies, lowered our monthly payment and well we had to pay our rent. 130.000€ were still not paye which was around one year of missing payment. The clubmanager was worried about that and as everything in small cities, it was not a secret for most people. Well Chris (right hand of CEOs) talked with the owner of the house and they came to an agreement. We would have to pay half the price for the rent for one year and could pay the open payment in small portions. I was extremely happy! I cared about my team and my members (as always) and combining lower costs and the progression of the club (we got 1/4 more members from march-June 2016), it looked like Bayreuth would get to break even in August! Well time for one more traumatic event!

two days afterwards the manager of the club in Bayreuth called me on the phone. "It's terrbile!" She said. "We just got a direct cancellation of our contract with the housekeeper and we have to move out in august!". Now what the bleep happened? One day after Chris was playing snakecharmer on the house keeper, a personal trainer in the club had the idea of taking over the club and proposed to work under the house keeper as the new club manager in the gym. He would do all the work for her. She agreed. When I got that message I directly called the central and asked how we should proceed. What about my team? They are still in the middle of their studies. We worked out a plan as far as we could and I would talk to them monday next week. I took my time to prepare for monday on that weekend.

Now I had promised my team that they would be safe early on. That they could finish their studies under this company and as long as I was with them, I would do anything to help them. In return I asked for their loyalty and friendship. I needed a team. Alone everyone is powerless.

Monday first thing I did was a small workout before I would meet with everyone to tell them what is going on. I didn't really pay attention and didn't notice how the whole team went into the meeting room and disappeared for a while. As I was done with my routine, I painfully learned what happened. Do you guys know how much it hurts when people who trusted you look at you in full disappointment. They don't even have to tell you that they are - they just look at you and tear you down. The old club manager was so overwhelmed with everything that she took the whole team into the meeting room without telling me, went on a rampage about all the bad things that are going on under this company (payment, charging people multiple times a month regularly, promising things they don't do anyway, 130k € open rent and so on), started crying and told them that they could pack theri things, screw that company and hope for better times.

Being loyal as always I was the only idiot who defended the company and told them to relax. That I would, as promised, do anything possible to keep them safe from anything that might happen. Find them a new place to work, organize a payment if they had to move etc... but actually I wasn't really able to say anything. I pretty much had a mob infront of me pushing all their anger on me because I was the stupid heretic, that was still pro-company and not gathering up all the torches. On a side note - All of that happened while the gym was open and a lot of members were listening. It took 30 minutes and we got spammed with phonecalls if the gym would close down. The whole team laughed at me and went out of the door. I was powerless, heartbroken and the only thing I could do, was to keep the club running. So I stood there for hours, answering calls, taking care of the reception, helping people with their training, thinking about what I should do about tomorrow, when finally, the team came back in.

They smiled at me. A litte more friendly than before. They said they were sorry about how the went all out on me with nine people, whilst I wasn't the one at fault. They knew me and respected me but couldn't believe that I was still loyal. Well, to be honest I had been suffering under they way the company worked for a while now, but I was a areal manager, what do you guys expect me to do? Also I was extremely loyal. That company game me to possibility to change things on a way bigger scale (even though the payment was shi...).

This is where I stop telling you guys about Bayreuth. There was much more ugly stuff going on like an article in the papers, insane amounts being charged from members (oops, system error - our bad), the law was involved, private detectives and me being in the center of almost 30 pissed of class instructos, team members etc. telling me how stupid I was, being still loyal to my company while the personal trainer I mentioned above was advertising himself being the new captain of that shit in the stormy weather, promising everything they wanted to hear. I felt like shit. I almost had that club on break even - I was doing my job extremely well and suddenly, without me doing any real mistake, the floor under my feet gets pulled away and shortly before I reached that goal line the whole successtory crumbled up into a pile of crap. I got sick. I couldn't move out of my bed anymore. No joke. I had tonsillitis. Had to take antibiotics and my whole body was hurting.

Age 28, systolic bloodpressure of 170 right after waking up in the morning. I measured that not only once but ever since I left Bayreuth and went to Bretten (area close to Karlsruhe). The whole management area told me that I did a great job in Bayreuth and that I was not responsible for what happened. Well I still felt like shit. Slowly, after no longer thinking that someone called Jack Kruse was totally crazy and reading his work for around two years I started to think about what I did to myself the last years, ever since I started to study health and fitness. I had learned alot, knew what I had to do with almost all injuries I encountered in the gym, had a huge bump in my carreer but something was going wrong. To be honest I didn't want to die of an heart attack at the age of 30 and it looked like that was where I was heading to. I made a decision. I would stay in Bretten for 3 months and decide if I wanted to stay of drop everything, return to munich and start all over.

Bretten started well. Actually really freaking well. People knew me and were happy that I was taking care of the gym. Some of their members were in munich while I was managing the place and they somehow though that I was a rather neat person. So I had an easy start early on. The team was amazing! At the first meeting we directly connected. The leading trainer knew me from the lessons I held and respected me. Still there were some things in the background that made my decision pretty easy.

1\. Because my team didn't get paid I asked to pay them first and postponed my own paymend leading to 30.000€ that I still had to see

2\. Bretten hat 267.000€ in rent open

3\. My health

4\. My old girlfriend that I lost contact with (you might know why), messaged me about casual things and we started talking again. I missed her. We never had arguement or anything. It always came back to time and me moving around. I wanted to see her again. She had a boyfriend and I didn't think about anything - also I didn't think that I was able to be with anyone anymore. I was so used to lie to people, pretend to be someone I am not. Talking small talk which I detested and tell everyone that everything is fine, that I avoided any extra social contact as much as possible.

Somehow my team in Bretten (those smart little buggers) knew what was going on. I loved working with them, but in September it was clear that I would stop working for the company. I made a deal with the CEOs that they could pay the money they owed me in parts per month (which was a mistake actually, because I am still trying to get it). and spent most of my time getting the next manager ready to take care of the gym and my team. Actually that team got so good at everything while I had the honor to work with them, that they didn't really need someone to tell them what they had to do. I still felt like doing that. November 30th I left the company I gave my all. I felt relieved, strange and free but also unsure what will come next.

December was actually pretty fine. I met with Hanna (my ex girlfriend) and she directly took me to old friends, made me social again - well thats a whole different story - short part. We are together again and I feel like the luckiest bastard about that.

I am now self employed and part of what I do is having a German website where I write about health. Free for everyone to read. While I was working with people I was surprised how few felt comfortable with reading english. I really wanted to direct them to Jack but couldn't. That's why I spend now as much free time as I have on writing articles about diseases, hormones, light, circadian rhythms, water, magnetism. Keeping it to myself would be comparable as hurting others on purpose. I am happy that my seemingly curious mind that kept me looking into science for about 13 years now, made me stumble up Jack. Even though I have to admit that my first reaction was "no way! This is over my head!", I maybe somehow felt some truth behind it after all and kept reading. And what happens then to your mind after you start to open pandoras box - well I guess most of you guys knows how that feels.

### Hairless Samson — 2016-08-28T13:10:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-never-wanted-to-write-this-to-be-honest.18332/post-200350>

And yes Jack was the one person that made me realise what an idiot I was. And I am greatful for that, bro. Seriously and from the bottom of my respiratory chain!

### Lahelada — 2016-08-28T16:27:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-never-wanted-to-write-this-to-be-honest.18332/post-200354>

Thank you for writing this all down. I can relate to the pressures of perfection and functioning at all cost. Being German does not help in getting off that treadmill.

We are omnipotent in our thinking, but do not realise that our body is not omnipotent in the conditions of existence we have created. We are not all that we could be because we think that we already are. Everything is fine, it is the way things are and it is good. Rattling the cage ? What cage ? There is no cage.

I meet so many people now who are 20 years younger than me, some thirty. Beautiful people and all without fail tell me that they feel old in their twenties and thirties. Your story helps everyone to examine their life

So you sharing your story helps everyone who is now at your age then to understand that their shameful shortcomings are not shameful at all, that suffering through this shows strength but misdirected strength due to trying to bow to expectations.

My father said it to me many times - EXPECTATION KILLS. Worst of all expectations of yourself, next to that surrounding yourself with "expectatious" people. It took years for me to understand this simple sentence fully.

Other's expectations, hidden criticisms are internalised and all over sudden you are driven by a force, that you do not recognise as your own , a river that carries you through beautiful or not so beautiful landscape wondering how you got here. Well, gotta earn a crust right ? And so heteroplasmy increase and HRV decreases,until you flat line... Some wake up just in time,very few, or get hi"jacked" into a different canal.

Thanks for your honesty , your blog and making a difference. I am currently trying to bring the same energy from the South . Still confused about the how, but there seems to be a yellow brick road appearing, leading to the wizard.

Adding thanks to Jack and Dr K for being here for us and thanks for the lack of mollycoddling. Why Jack and Dr K ? Because it must take a lot out of Jack to be Dr Kruse, the teacher, to all of us.
