What an insightful and honest and from the heart post Aleina. You add so much value here and to all those in your life.

What a journey you are on! What a journey we are on - you just gave me lots to think about too!

As you know - I am German as well. Lots of truths here.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-08-29T02:59:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-never-wanted-to-write-this-to-be-honest.18332/post-200418>

Thrombosis........is a ferritin and iron problem. Have you ever looked at a map or Europe or Germany closely? I have.

Here is my insight to you after reading all that:

So if you read Time #21 and you wondering how ferritin works as a semiconductor you are in luck if you have a blood clotting disorder. Iron is an essential trace element that is used to form molecules in the body, such as hemoglobin. Ferritin is the major storage protein for iron. Free iron is toxic for cells because of its reactivity. So what controls its release? Its charge within the ferritin protein controls its release. Since iron is very reactive to sunlight becuase of its large amount of D shell electrons. Iron has a higher rate of emissivity than other atoms in cells and atoms with this ability readiate more energy than expected. When their emissivity is in the IR range of light, the proteins containing iron seem to be more energized. Emission of light energy reflects the intensity of charge movement in a protein. Ferritin is a solar active protein. Sunlight wavelengths can change when light travels through the atomic material in a cell or in blood. This change in incident light frequency is called a Stokes shift. The reason for this is when sunlight energized blood plasma solar radiation drives the charge movement which generates new electromagnetic waves that can also generate other electromagnetic waves that change the charges in their local environment. So when ferritin rises it is usually because people are not spending enough time in the sun. The body up-regulates ferritin production in this case awaiting its activation. Many people think that ferritin elevation is a sign of inflamamtion but it is actually a sign that these people live a solar spectral deficient life. If you lack sunlight, you have less electrons and more protons in tissues and this lowers the pH and this decreases the exclusion zone (EZ) in water. Ferritin is the protein within the body that stores iron and releases it through channels in a controlled fashion. The unique structure of ferritin forms a spherical shell in which the iron is "stored" as Fe(III) in a crystalline mineral. Ferritin consists of 24 peptide subunits that form two types of channels where these subunits intersect; the 3-fold channel is polar and the 4-fold channel is nonpolar. The residues that line the channels determine the polarity of the channel. Polarity refers to significant differences in charges due to the electronegativity between adjacent atoms in a molecule. The polarity of the channel link to the size of the EZ in blood plasma. The iron in the ferritin core is stored as Fe(III) in a crystalline solid. This crystal acts as a semiconductor inside of ferritin. The smallest repeating unit of the ferritin lattice is called the unit cell. The unit cell for iron is ferrihydrite. In the mineral ferrihydrite, every Fe(III) ion is coordinated to six O(II) ions. Oxygen is very electronegative compared to Fe(III). However, in ferritin, the mineral core has approximately 10% of the Fe(III) ions coordinated to five O(II) ions and 1 phosphate group. Phosphate is very active photosynthetically with sunlight. Most of the phosphate groups that are coordinated to the iron ions lie on the outside of the crystalline structure in ferritin, and are used to bind the mineral to the residues on the inside of the ferritin protein shell. When the Fe(III) in the crystalline mineral is reduced (added electrons from phosphorus or the charge separation of water by sunlight) to Fe(II) state, the iron becomes solvated in blood plasma and ferritin releases the solvated iron to tissues, in this form Fe(H2O)62+, through ferritin's 3-fold polar channel. In order for iron to be released from ferritin, the mineral lattice must be dissolved (i.e., to allow the iron atoms to break away from the lattice structure deep inside ferritin). This removal is accomplished by reducing iron from the Fe (III) (ferric) oxidation state to the Fe(II) (ferrous) oxidation state. In the Fe(II) state, iron breaks away from the lattice as the Fe2+ ion. The POSITIVE CHARGE of the Fe2+ ion attracts the electronegative oxygen atoms (ELECTRONEGATIVE) in water, and so a water "cage" forms around the ion. That cage is a Faraday cage which shelters the iron from other electromagnetic waves. Hence, ferritin can control the amount of available iron in the body electrostatically, preventing iron disorders like anemia and iron overload. If someone's environment ruins the electrostatic mechanism expalined above for any reason it will effect iron status and ferritin levels in the blood. This mechanism is linked to presence or absence of charge in blood plasma or in phosphorus as a dopant. The EZ potential energy holds the key to what your iron status ultimately will be. Anemia is becoming epidemic in women and children these days for a deep reason: humans have created a low quantum yield world and this alters the charge of blood plasma which directly impacts the release of iron from ferritin.
The three-dimensional structure of ferritin is crucial to understand its quantum function within the body. In fact, the three-dimensional stucture of any molecule is critical in determining a molecule's properties and function optically. Hence, to better understand ferritin's role in the body, we must familiarize our self to different types of molecular representations to study ferritin's three-dimensional stucture.

So about the map of Germany........and those mtDNA haplotypes..........have you really looked at it carefully?

### JanSz — 2016-08-29T16:20:20+0300

[https://mathildasanthropologyblog.wordpress.com/2008/06/16/mitochondrial-dna-haplotypes-for-dummies/](https://mathildasanthropologyblog.wordpress.com/2008/06/16/mitochondrial-dna-haplotypes-for-dummies/)

Mitochondrial DNA haplotypes for dummies.

Mitochondrial DNA is a DNA that is only inherited from mother to child, and it tracks back to the most recent common female ancestor of all humans (Mitochondrial Eve). I have collected a few items from all over the net to explain the age of the haplotypes, their relationships and population affinities.

### lilly antic — 2016-08-29T22:01:48+0300

# Thread: Reading ......
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading.18333/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2016-08-29T02:42:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading.18333/post-200412>

We all have soooooo much to read ......there just doesn't seem to be enough hours in the day!

Dr. K. Is giving us so much .....so how are you getting a handle on all this?

Dr. Mercola talks about walking at the ocean everyday and reading on his kindle. Great multi tasking!

Are you reading on a kindle???

I had gone back to paper .....but it is so hard to get books here in Australia - ebooks are so much more efficient.

So - if you are reading on your kindle outside in the sunshine ....?Do you wear your blue lockers?

I bought a cheap pair of half readers at the drug store and have been using those. - pushed way down my nose.

Is this a good idea?

What is everyone doing to absorb all these amazing gifts we are given?

Especially on the days that Dr. K. Has verbal diarrhoea???

### Sue-UK — 2016-08-29T12:34:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading.18333/post-200439>

I don't use technology to read outside, it has to be a book or a printed out blog, and only after I have felt a "shift" .... Because of our lower quantum yield I spend time grounding, scanning the skies, until I sense that my mitochondria have read the sun's barcode, and tuned in to my environment....on a good day that takes 10 to 20 minutes, the longest was 2 hours after a difficult visit to mum in the nursing home. I do download books onto an iPad, not ideal but I use airplane mode, and have replaced the clear plastic in the cover with an amber one. I also have a tray for it, and it sits on a bag filled with crushed oyster shells, putting something else between it and the table my legs are under. Not sure what difference it makes .. I also use it when I want to pop indoors and check out a link mentioned in a printed out blog. I removed the speakers from my office computer because of the emf readings, so I also listen to the webinars on it, which is why I've kept it and not bought a kindle. My office screen also has an amber cover so I only use blublockers for artificial light in the evening, or if the TV is on.

### Lahelada — 2016-08-29T13:03:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading.18333/post-200441>

my modus operandi :
\- skim a new blog for keywords and match with older blogs that fit the current subject at hand when back reading. While everything is connected some blogs have more nuggets than others depending on what the subject of the week is.
\- copy paste everything into mail messages creating my own "book" with pithy titles like "Ferritin the iron ferry". The titles really only really have to make sense to you
\- I have also printed off catch up blogs for reading at night by red light, on the bus or when sunbathing. Basically multitasking.
\- I don't like using a computer outside. I have done when listening to webinars but it disturbs me on an "esthetic" level, feels too incongruous with its surroundings.
\- Talk to the dog about the about matters at hand. I find I need to read less as hearing myself expound crystalises where I need to delve deeper or where surprisingly I have a reasonable grasp already. No idea what the dog thinks about it..

### Greenie — 2018-01-31T09:11:47+0200

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-26T00:03:15+0300

I have considered getting an e-reader as well, to read the vast amounts of Dr.K's teachings and others.

### Greenie — 2018-03-26T00:41:37+0300

### karenr — 2018-03-26T00:43:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reading.18333/post-237958>

I use an old Kindle Touch, e-ink, no backlighting, and it has text to speech, which I like when I'm tired and don’t feel like reading. The speech isn’t terribly robotic sounding, either.

The newer ones don’t have text to speech - now they don’t want to give you too many features on the lower priced Kindles, so you'll buy the higher priced tablets with bells and whistles and blue light and wireless. You do need to use wifi to download books onto the device initially, but then I turn the wifi off.

I also use my iPad now without WiFi, so it’s more like an e-reader, but I just don’t know the effects of looking at that screen, even though I have it so heavily blue-blocked. An e-ink screen definitely looks more natural than any back lit screen no matter the color temperature.

Been trying to find a way to hook my phone to an ethernet connection, though it seems that many phones don't recognize a Micro-USB to Ethernet connection.

# Thread: Quantum Biohacking- What does it mean to you?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-biohacking-what-does-it-mean-to-you.18335/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 29
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Curves — 2016-08-29T03:03:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-biohacking-what-does-it-mean-to-you.18335/post-200419>

I have a passion for sharing Jack's brilliance with others -- especially women in mid-life. I often hear from my peers that I'm "inspiring. I think they say that because they've been told (and they believe) "it's all downhill from here" yet my life stands in stark contrast to what they've been led to believe.

When they ask me more about "biohacking" here's what I share:

_Biohacking is a philosophy or way or thinking about your health in which you self-experiment by making specific small tweaks to your lifestyle, environment, nutrition and exercise and carefully monitoring the results. It can be the difference between finding the source of your health problems versus using medications to suppress your symptoms._ ​

To drill down even more:

I let them know that as a _**quantum biohacker**_ I know that the keys to health, longevity and happiness lie in "light water and magnetism" rather than meds, supplements or food.

Here are the tenants I describe:

(1) There is no one size fits all

(2) Your n=1 matters

(3) Educating yourself about your body is a necessity

(4) Test & experiment with these ideas yourself

(5) Your environment is primary
​

What do you think? Would you add or subtract anything? Have I expressed this correctly or sufficiently?I really want to know

### caroline — 2016-08-29T03:19:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-biohacking-what-does-it-mean-to-you.18335/post-200423>

In my opinion you expressed this very well Elaine.

I, personally, think of bio hacking as a man's game. I suppose because of what we read and it seems to be men primarily doing a bio hack on strength and training.

As women - especially those here .....we are continually bio hacking without putting that name to it???

A lot of the ladies here are continually working on their diet and hormones .....and trying to find the path that works for us ...Our own n=1

Men and women are so different ......thankfully! But it is really hard work to find our own optimal path.

Of course we have the three legged stool - but often our hormones put a spanner in the works.

Men seem to gain muscle and shred fat and sleep like a baby ...... Whereas I often feel like I am floundering to get the details right...... We, as women, seem to need to tweak things a bit differently ?

### JanSz — 2016-08-29T15:52:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-biohacking-what-does-it-mean-to-you.18335/post-200449>

Less allegory, more facts.
I am 76yo, working on 77.
For many years, not 5, more like 10, I had to use DHEA 300-400 mg/day to keep my DHEAs within (300-500)ug/dL range.
Without that DHEAs<100
Keeping DHEAs at that high level helped me to deal with pregnenolone, progesterone and specially cortisol,
without getting into problems with my Estradiol(E2) or Estrone(E1).
Sometime in March/16 situation changed.
Mainly that affects erections (estrogens high, progesterone to low).
Suddenly problems appeared. They could be solved with use of Arimidex (aromatase inhibitor).
But why does that happened?
Blood test in May, DHEAs topped laboratory range at 1000.
E2 ok if not low at 10pg/mL
E1=140, twice laboratory range.
cortisol AM ok
\---
Stopped DHEA supplementation.
Blood test week ago.
DHEAs below 100
E2 low
E1 high but within range
Cortisol, progesterone falled

What is going on?
Plenty of sun, vit D over top laboratory range
Now sitting on the beach in Myrtle Beach.

With all this I still need Arimidex to get it up.

3betaHSD overactive (?), why, how to slow it down?
IGF-1 =130 my usual
fasting insulin low single digits
Fatty acids O3, O6 looks good
L attest Spectracell shows little less Inositol, but still acceptable, something with membranes.
\--
overall E2 rather low
E1 too high
that is with no DHEA supplementation and 3 Arimidex /week

Need some ideas?

### JanSz — 2016-09-02T16:47:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-biohacking-what-does-it-mean-to-you.18335/post-200715>

> [Joeakim said:](/goto/post?id=200672)
>
> So...either your environment has changed or your DNA/blueprint has changed, no? What is your guess?

I do not have much of a guesses.
But hope that all is eventually sign of positive change.
The usual, sun/night cycle, nnEMF, grounding, I have under decent control (obviously always could be improved).

Something similar happened to me when after about a year of heavy duty pregnenolone supplementation I got a (sudden) break thru.
I had to drastically reduce dose, from 900mg/day to 300.
Eventually to nothing.
That was the end of my low cortisol(AM).
Since that event it continued for 3-4 years as good (very good) without any pregnenolone support (well, I still was using this huge DHEA 300mg/day).

Now, 3 months after stopping DHEA supplementation, my blood shows
low DHEAs
cortisol(AM) still good but little lower.
Estradiol ok, but lowish
Estrone ok, but highish

It is a waiting game.
Have to wait until it possibly settles.
To have a sex I still need to lower my aromatase with Arimidex.
This is unusual for me because in the past I was ok with those numbers and newer in need of AI (aromatase inhibitors).
Likely it is about transient that hopefully will settle.
Hopefully with time I will need less or no Arimidex.
I think it is getting better.

What I worry about now is that now with so low DHEAs my E1 is still to high by my estimate.
I am itching to introduce 25mg/day DHEA, but not sure because that will reinforce already high E1.
But now, I again must raise progesterone, little higher DHEAs would help.
Something with enzymes. Some of them respond to coconut oil. Have to try increase that.

What I hate is lack of support.
There must be here someone watching, who knows or have better idea of this type of interactions than what I can come up with.
Well, likely there is no one.
I have not seen this here yet, well, just once couple years ago.

The good news is that I was able to convince my GP to get me Arimidex script.
I can get it over internet but it cost more.
Plus, there is always hope that my GP get interested in this as a medical problem.
Well, when asked, he told me that he is busy enough as is and wants to stay within his job description.

Some years ago I tried few endos, they all are hopeless.
Good at writing scripts for insulin.
Guy, specializing in sex problems told me to look at my age and prepare to die.

### Valuation Man — 2016-08-29T14:40:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mercola-message-today.18336/post-200442>

I get Mercola's daily feed and today's featured article was about Diabetes and below is an excerpt from this article. I really enjoyed Joe being on the Q&A of last months Webinar and I am happy he is preaching the following message.

**Optimal Sun Exposure Required for Health and Diabetes**

You may have the perfect diet but if you have failed to optimize your exposure to the sun and assiduously avoid blue light from artificial sources, not just at night but all day, then you will likely be challenged to achieve high level health. Why? It's a very complex explanation, but the end summary is that it helps to optimize your mitochondrial function. Please also review my recent article on using [indoor sunglasses](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2016/08/11/blue-light-blocking-glasses.aspx) to minimize your exposure to blue light from artificial lights. They only need to be used when the lights are on and you are indoors, even in the daytime. Most people think they are only for night time use but this is incorrect. Additionally, establishing an optimal circadian rhythm is important, so going outside, ideally barefoot, shortly after waking up and close to sunrise, exposing your eyes to natural sunlight for 3 to 5 minutes, is an important health practice. If you have a night job, please consider changing your job as it is seriously damaging your health. Additionally, it would be ideal to get one to three hours of unfiltered sunlight (not through a window or sunglasses) every day and expose as much skin as you can.

# Thread: 10 Gauss Magnetico Daily Progress Report
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/10-gauss-magnetico-daily-progress-report.18338/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KalosKaiAgathos — 2016-08-29T15:52:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/10-gauss-magnetico-daily-progress-report.18338/post-200448>

Started using the 10 Gauss Magnetico since yesterday. I am extremely tired today. The problem is, that I'm working from my bedroom, as I'm still living with my parents. I do not get within 4 feet of the positive magnetic field that is projected to the sides however.

Yesterday, I was also very tired. I was working security in the weekend, and only sleeping for one hour between Friday and Saturday, and five hours between Saturday and Sunday. Quit the job, as it is not worth it. Bedtime was 4:30 (AM!) during both days. These days ruin me. I only used to work this job during the summer holidays, but I consider it no longer worth it. For two years now, all year round, I have blocked all toxic blue light after sunset, except during the time I work this job. Then, when some of these days come around, it takes a few additional days to recover. My mental performance is shot.

So, the first night on the Magnetico went amazing. Very deep sleep and I remember some very good dreams. Immediately after getting up at 6:15AM, a 30 minute CT session in my 400 gallon tub outside. Water temperature was 60F, so very hot. It will get colder soon in the Netherlands.

Does anyone know whether the Magnetico projects north and south in addition to the 4 feet it projects a positive magnetic field sideways? And does anyone know whether the Magnetico also leaves a diagonal positive magnetic field behind? I need to know this in order to make the choice whether to study in my bedroom during the day.

Another option for me would be to fold the Magnetico EVERY day, and to move it in a rolled-up state another room. The only reason I'm considering _not_ doing that, is that it will damage the magnets in the long run.

I'll keep you updated.

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2016-09-01T08:30:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/10-gauss-magnetico-daily-progress-report.18338/post-200631>

Second day went oke. I was still pretty tired in the evening, but no longer in the afternoon as the first day. On the third day, there was no tiredness during the day. That night I had the best sleep that I can remember in my life, except maybe sleeping in to 13:00 in my teens

It surprises me HOW much quicker I fall asleep, and how much deeper the sleep is. This thing is worth every penny IMO. And I'm using only 10 Gauss. Imagine what 20 or 40 Gauss will do, or even more!

Edit: Still a bit tired during the day. My mind is not yet working optimally.

# Thread: Ferritin and its link to mitochondria
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ferritin-and-its-link-to-mitochondria.18340/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2016-08-29T20:54:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ferritin-and-its-link-to-mitochondria.18340/post-200468>

So if you read Time #21 and you wondering how ferritin works as a semiconductor you are in luck. Iron is an essential trace element that is used to form molecules in the body, such as hemoglobin. Ferritin is the major storage protein for iron. Free iron is toxic for cells because of its reactivity. So what controls its release? Its charge within the ferritin protein controls its release. Since iron is very reactive to sunlight becuase of its large amount of D shell electrons. Iron has a higher rate of emissivity than other atoms in cells and atoms with this ability readiate more energy than expected. When their emissivity is in the IR range of light, the proteins containing iron seem to be more energized. Emission of light energy reflects the intensity of charge movement in a protein. Ferritin is a solar active protein. Sunlight wavelengths can change when light travels through the atomic material in a cell or in blood. This change in incident light frequency is called a Stokes shift. The reason for this is when sunlight energized blood plasma solar radiation drives the charge movement which generates new electromagnetic waves that can also generate other electromagnetic waves that change the charges in their local environment. So when ferritin rises it is usually because people are not spending enough time in the sun. The body up-regulates ferritin production in this case awaiting its activation. Many people think that ferritin elevation is a sign of inflamamtion but it is actually a sign that these people live a solar spectral deficient life. If you lack sunlight, you have less electrons and more protons in tissues and this lowers the pH and this decreases the exclusion zone (EZ) in water. Ferritin is the protein within the body that stores iron and releases it through channels in a controlled fashion. The unique structure of ferritin forms a spherical shell in which the iron is "stored" as Fe(III) in a crystalline mineral. Ferritin consists of 24 peptide subunits that form two types of channels where these subunits intersect; the 3-fold channel is polar and the 4-fold channel is nonpolar. The residues that line the channels determine the polarity of the channel. Polarity refers to significant differences in charges due to the electronegativity between adjacent atoms in a molecule. The polarity of the channel link to the size of the EZ in blood plasma. The iron in the ferritin core is stored as Fe(III) in a crystalline solid. This crystal acts as a semiconductor inside of ferritin. The smallest repeating unit of the ferritin lattice is called the unit cell. The unit cell for iron is ferrihydrite. In the mineral ferrihydrite, every Fe(III) ion is coordinated to six O(II) ions. Oxygen is very electronegative compared to Fe(III). However, in ferritin, the mineral core has approximately 10% of the Fe(III) ions coordinated to five O(II) ions and 1 phosphate group. Phosphate is very active photosynthetically with sunlight. Most of the phosphate groups that are coordinated to the iron ions lie on the outside of the crystalline structure in ferritin, and are used to bind the mineral to the residues on the inside of the ferritin protein shell. When the Fe(III) in the crystalline mineral is reduced (added electrons from phosphorus or the charge separation of water by sunlight) to Fe(II) state, the iron becomes solvated in blood plasma and ferritin releases the solvated iron to tissues, in this form Fe(H2O)62+, through ferritin's 3-fold polar channel. In order for iron to be released from ferritin, the mineral lattice must be dissolved (i.e., to allow the iron atoms to break away from the lattice structure deep inside ferritin). This removal is accomplished by reducing iron from the Fe(III) (ferric) oxidation state to the Fe(II) (ferrous) oxidation state. In the Fe(II) state, iron breaks away from the lattice as the Fe2+ ion. The POSITIVE CHARGE of the Fe2+ ion attracts the electronegative oxygen atoms (ELECTRONEGATIVE) in water, and so a water "cage" forms around the ion. That cage is a Farady cage which shelters the iron from other electromagnetic waves. Hence, ferritin can control the amount of available iron in the body electrostatically, preventing iron disorders like anemia and iron overload. If someone's environment ruins the electrostatic mechanism expalined above for any reason it will effect iron status and ferritin levels in the blood. This mechanism is linked to presence or absence of charge in blood plasma or in phosphorus as a dopant. The EZ potential energy holds the key to what your iron status ultimately will be. Anemia is becoming epidemic in women and children these days for a deep reason: humans have created a low quantum yield world and this alters the charge of blood plasma which directly impacts the release of iron from ferritin.

The three-dimensional structure of ferritin is crucial to understand its quantum function within the body. In fact, the three-dimensional stucture of any molecule is critical in determining a molecule's properties and function optically. Hence, to better understand ferritin's role in the body, we must familiarize our self to different types of molecular representations to study ferritin's three-dimensional stucture. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3105608/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3105608/)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-08-29T20:55:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ferritin-and-its-link-to-mitochondria.18340/post-200469>

Evolution is physics in motion. There is a lot of motion on the electron chain transport. You should be particularly interested in the Q-cycle. The ubiquinones in the Q-cycle re-emit light that has excited electrons that enter ECT at cytochrome 1. Recall that the Q-cycle ferries electrons from cytochrome 1 to 3 and 4. Cytochrome 4 is a HEME protein. Why is this important? When sunlight excites electrons in foods this energy is transfered in the electrons of Acetyl -CoA from TCA and beta-oxidation. When the electrons enter at cytochrome 1 the Q-cycle quinones can change the incident light that originally excited the electron in the environment. This change in incident light frequency is called a Stokes shift. The reason for this is solar radiation drives the charge movements developed in electrons on the inner mitochondrial membrane. The ubiquinone/Ubiquitinol proteins allow for polarization changes to the incident light and also a spectral change of the frequency of light which becomes able to generate a new electromagnetic wave that can effect other proteins around it. Well, cytochrome c oxidase (cyto4) is a HEME protein that responds to IR and NIR frequencies because of the Q-cycle's ability to Stoke shift food electrons. This allows cytochrome c oxidase to prime the ATPase to improve its efficicienct to close to 100% without needing any new food electrons. The cytochrome c oxidase HEME proteins can also generate other electromagnetic waves that change the charges in their local environment where the ATPase is located. From the mixing of motion and quantum mechanics emerges the a new emergent concept we call evolution using these non linear apsects of light. Light frequencies are the levers that turn the gears of water to make life possible. They key it appears in cells is to slow light down. When we slow light down the circadian clock genes in front of every human gene can become synched to act as the ideal metronome for creation of life.
Cells are transparent during daytime for one reason: The optics of sunlight. All stressed cells emit uncolored ELF-UV. Cells under duress release massive amounts of ELF-UV. Might this excesses light be to improve the ATPase function without need for any other food electrons? I think so. All transparent substances show some dispersion in the visible spectrum, and, if uncolored, have a higher index of refraction for purple (UV) then the red light. This difference implies that higher powered visible frequencies (UV) are slowed down more by the medium than are the red frequencies. The Q-cycle and cytochrome c are invovled specifically with red light which acts at very fast speeds to make ATP at 100% efficiency as the link below shows. This explains why cells are designed to release UV light, it also explains why they EZ has a higher refraction index than bulk water, and why red light penetrates tissues 10-30 cm’s into the human body to work directly on the Q-cycle and the ATPase to run it as a quantum torque nanomachine. [https://arxiv.org/pdf/1305.6590.pdf](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1305.6590.pdf)

# Thread: Hello from Springfield!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-springfield.18342/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matthias — 2016-08-29T21:43:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-springfield.18342/post-200479>

Let me tell all you wonderful people a story about a young man who saw the movie _Limitless_ in 2012 and thought that it was based on a real-world drug. The stand-in is a compound called modafinil. In my search for a place I could get it, I came upon Dave Asprey's Bulletproof blog. What impressed me was his presentation of himself as a savvy modern man, and his claimed insistence on peer-reviewed studies regarding his doings.

I discovered that modafinil was a prescription drug, so I had to get it from India. And I gotta say, it actually worked! Having seen the results, I decided that I'd follow Asprey's other instructions for his diet and whatnot. And it worked for a time--I became a better version of myself ("upgraded" in his parlance), but still kept some of my niggling problems. Anyway, at one point I came across his collaboration with Jack Kruse about cold thermogenesis. In the elapsation I'd become hungrier for the _next_ improvement I could give myself, and so I made the leap to reading Jack's blogs about CT that Asprey had linked.

Oh boy was I in over my head.

Dave had linked to Cold Thermogenesis 9, so that's where I tried to start. It was like walking into the middle of a NOVA special that was dialed up to 11. I tried to go back and start reading from Cold Thermogenesis 1 and work from there, but even that was a little... initially frustrating. Fortunately, I'm an engineer and was schooled enough for a cursory read, but--as always for anyone--it was my constant curiosity that got me over the hump.

What was immediately clear was that Jack's level of actual, lower-level understanding (which led to my own follow-up googling & investigating) was so far ahead of the information from where I started. For example, if I'd asked _Why is fluoride bad_? I previously would have been told: _it just is_; where Jack would say **_because it reduces water's dielectric constant to ~20 and that effectively causes dehydration and improper chemistry for the proteins in the cell_** and elaborate on it [with a blog post](https://www.jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-4-ubiquitins-connection-to-light/) after mentioning its effects in other places.

The real tasting of the pudding was just plain **doing** what Jack instructed and seeing what happened--sometimes without even spending that much time familiarizing myself. And dammit, everything he said to do worked--_especially_ the stuff that other people had previously told me was nonsense (like the orange glasses I wear after dusk and at work while under the fluorescent lights and in front of my monitors).

So since about early/mid-2014, I was fully on the hook. No going back. No in-car wifi. No bluetooth. None of this nonsense in my house that would make it worse.

The burden on my heart now is to help my parents. They're both 68, my father is where I got my psoriasis and it's only gotten worse as has his weight, and my mother is in remission from breast cancer after having it surgically removed in 2011. I try my damnedest to convince them with any kind of language and with as much love as I can show them. I want them to be around long enough to see their grandchildren. They might come around, but... right now it's like [Luke 4:24](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke+4%3A24&version=KJV).

I eat as much crab as I can get, occasionally splurge on a lobster, always drink Pellegrino when it's on the shelf, and go into Cambridge to get $1 oysters at Red House when I'm seeing friends. (And of friends I haven't yet met--Reuben, my offer for dinner and Malbec is still on the table!). The only thing left is to change more of the environment that had been holding me back. I'm looking forward to keeping a log with you guys about the stuff I try out.

Up and atom, everybody!

P.S. Anybody else from the Northeast is welcome to take me up on my offer as well! I'll meet, and I'll buy. Except New York City. Until I have my Quantlet, I'm not going back. (And even then...)

P. P.S. _Limitless_ is a wonderfully-structured movie, but a fantasy. Get your circadian rhythms and your mitochondria right, and modafinil doesn't even do anything.

# Thread: How does RF radiation work to disrupt life below the thermal threshold?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-rf-radiation-work-to-disrupt-life-below-the-thermal-threshold.18345/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2016-08-30T05:02:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-rf-radiation-work-to-disrupt-life-below-the-thermal-threshold.18345/post-200493>

The energy in an RF current can radiate off a conductor into space as electromagnetic waves (radio waves); this is the basis of radio technology. Now think about all the conductors in you. Think is toll free?
RF current does not penetrate deeply into electrical conductors but tends to flow along their surfaces; this is known as the skin effect. This is why the TSA thinks their machines are OK. You do know in the skin we make thinks using light frequencies that can interact via the Stokes shift? For this reason, when the human body comes in contact with high power RF currents it can cause superficial but serious burns called RF burns (Radiation burns). Rarely happens with PEMF devices but people with certain color tattoos have to be careful with High Tesla MRI magnets. I have had 4 patients this has occured in so it is a real thing but rare.
RF currents applied to the body often do not cause the painful sensation of electric shock as do lower frequency currents so they tend to occur below our perceptual level. This is because the current changes direction too quickly to trigger depolarization of nerve membranes, but the this may not be true with the photoreceptors of the eye which are exquisitely attuned to all electromagnetic frequencies including RF.
RF current can easily ionize air around us because we are FILLED with conductors for light, creating a conductive path through the medium. The path is determined by the charges in the medium. This property is exploited by "high frequency" units used in electric arc welding, which use currents at higher frequencies than power distribution uses.
Another property is the ability to appear to flow through paths that contain insulating material, like the dielectric insulator of a capacitor. Water is life main dielectric? water. What is its main capacitor? EZ water. Bulk water has a dielectric of 78 and EZ is 160. So RF is way more dangerous to things with HIGHER dielectrics.
When RF is conducted by an ordinary electric cable, RF current has a tendency to reflect from discontinuities in the cable such as connectors and travel back down the cable toward the source, causing a condition called standing waves. Therefore, RF current must be carried by specialized types of cable called transmission line. We know standing waves are generated in mitochondria........so the RF from your cell or PEMF device that is transmitted wirelessly where there is no cable.......what happens to us when the RF heads back to the antenna of the device in your pocket or on your body? These are just a few things for everyone to consider..........few do. RF = micronuclei to DNA. Why don't people get it? Science has been pushing the beliefs that ionizing radiation is the only form of radiation than can do it........not true and we know it is not true for a long time. The real key is what humans and the military use to communicate is the light frequencies that are killing us. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11835258](http://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov%2Fpubmed%2F11835258&h=aAQHZDl9w)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-10-18T01:27:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-rf-radiation-work-to-disrupt-life-below-the-thermal-threshold.18345/post-226084>

The EM drive – a radio frequency (RF) resonant cavity thruster – appears to produce an ‘impossible’ thrust. Impossible – in that it apparently violates Newtons third law of motion: “For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.” Now in a recent paper by a group of Portuguese physicists, led by Prof. Jose Croca from the Center for Philosophy of Sciences at the University of Lisbon, present a possible explanation for this observed ‘impossible’ thrust. This goes to show you there is a lot about light we just do not appreciate yet in science.

**Pilot Wave Theory Explains EM Drive data.**

**The EM drive was first proposed in 2001 by British inventor Roger Sawyer and has subsequently been tested by numerous groups around the world alongside possible explanations for its propulsion. However, still a hot topic of debate, a consensus on the level of thrust and an explanation for the thrust has not been found.

Croca and his team hope to change this through their explanation utilizing an old theory known as Eurythmic physics. The word eurythmic comes from two Greek words _eu_, meaning well and _rhuthmós_, meaning symmetry, or rhythmical, and in the context of physics implies ‘the best path’.

Eurhythmic physics is a kind of pilot wave theory, first proposed by Louis de Broglie (I've written about him) and further developed by his students and colleagues and now Croca. It includes both quantum mechanics and macroscopic physics to describe interactions from the perspective of non-linear wavelike dynamics. In the context taken here – in an attempt to explain the EM drives ‘impossible’ thrust – Newtons third law is taken as an "emergent property" due to the interactions between particles and fields where the field is thought to exert a force determined by the size and geometry of the resonant cavity. This fits into the April 2016 webinar and really is a manifestation of the electromagnetic force as scale changes.

Such an explanation is in agreement with the electric universe theory, in which space is not empty and instead is an energetic field of information - with this understanding the EM drive does not violate Newtons third law of motion – it is pushing against the vacuum, where the tapered resonant cavity provides the asymmetry or differential gradient needed to produce the thrust.
**

# Thread: An introduction from the Chicago Burbs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Madison28 — 2016-08-30T08:21:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200496>

Hello everyone... my name is Sonya and I am going to be 54 years old next month.

I am divorced and live with my 20 year old son and most recently my mother moved in with us. We all get along great and for the most part life has been pretty good.

My health has been a little haphazard over the years however. I had my first daughter at age 24, via a healthy vaginal delivery and two years after that had an emergency appendectomy, a knee surgery, and a horribly painful ectopic pregnancy that almost killed me. I didn't realize until just recently how bad antibiotics can hurt a person's gut.

It was shortly after this that it seemed like I was having to go thru a surgery for one issue or another. I had my gallbladder removed, and a miscarriage that required a D&C which I found later to have a sponge that was left inside of me. After that I had major issues with my bowels and was chronically constipated. This led to a very large polyp inside my bowel, as well as multiple hemorrhoids. Surgery for that removed four fingers worth of tissue and left me in incredible pain. I have had fatty tumors removed behind my knee, a breast lump removed, and I was finally once again pregnant 10 years later after my daughter was born. I was so thankful that this pregnancy resulted in a healthy 10-pound baby boy. My one tube that was left after the ectopic pregnancy was tied off during the emergency C-section so I could get pregnant no more. After years of terrible menses bleeding and cramping I had a partial hysterectomy... leaving in my ovaries.

Finally after life had settled down approximately 2005 I was diagnosed with MS and Arnold Chiari malformation. I still had horrible gut issues but did not know the connection between antibiotics and gut health. After an in Hospital stay for high-dose IV steroid treatment then multiple rounds of steroids here and there the MS was brought under control.

In 2008 after 29 years of marriage my then-husband decided he'd had enough and left. He thought he was too good to support me and the children and has kept me in court 6 out of the last 9 years. The last two years were the most brutal and finally ended in February of 2016. During those last two years my mother moved in with us and was diagnosed with bladder cancer. I was her primary caretaker throughout the three surgeries she had to remove the cancer. It was shortly after that that she had a really terrible case of shingles. To say my stress load was over-the-top is putting it mildly.

When the court case I wrote about above ended on February 1st 2016 my body physically crashed on February 3rd and I have not been the same since. It seems I have chronic fatigue and sleep constantly however I always wake up at 3 a.m. every morning and then stay up for up to 6 hours only to crash again for the rest of the day. My skin is a mess full of acne that I've never had before as well as horribly dry and flaky skin. My hair is falling out. I can't stand for more than 5 or 10 minutes due to extreme pain around my left side kidney. And now I am getting strange boils that are pus filled and very painful, usually in the breast area. I probably have had 20 of them in the past 3 months. I've also gained probably 40 pounds since February.

I am a very happy-go-lucky, joyful person who loves to laugh. I have 3 beautiful grandchildren from my daughter whom I adore. And I also enjoy the memories my mother and I are making together as we are constantly laughing at how silly life can be. What I can't stand is my current state of health and the feeling of hopelessness of never getting better.

I've turned to several different practitioners who want thousands of dollars to help me heal without a guarantee of helping me to feel better. Of course I don't have that kind of money as I live on a limited budget and Medicaid. There has to be another way to help my body heal and I'm ready to learn and do whatever it takes.

I've read a little bit here and there on this website even as I am confused as much of it is over my head LOL. However I want to learn as much as possible and like I said I'm willing to do the work. I'm excited for a whole new future ahead of me.

I'm really sorry this was so long and I'm really happy to meet you all.

Sonya

### caroline — 2016-08-30T11:33:35+0300

what are you eating? drinking?

Do you have blue blocking glasses?? extremely important

I live outside of Chicago in the 60538 postal code area.

I am eating a lot of fruit (watermelon, grapes, apples) and then toast and soups, 2 cups of coffee per day, and drinking higher ph water, about 64 ounces of that with lemon in it. I do not eat any meat nor do I eat fish because Seafood has always been kind of yuk to me. I recently learned that I am Mercury poisoned and forgot to put that in my introduction.

No I do not have All Electronics out of my room and need to do that. I wasn't aware of how important that is until visiting this site. The cable modem is in my room and needs to be removed ASAP. I also keep my cell phone in here in case my daughter needs to contact me and I use my computer in here as I've been trying to do research on helping myself to get well. ### caroline — 2016-08-30T12:27:08+0300

You need Real sunshine and grounding ....and you need it now!

Please get your butt outside with little to no clothes on .....as soon as you wake - you want the early morning sunshine.

"My optimal Journal" is fifth down I think.

Lots of folks here have done it .....or are doing it - so can you.

So - get on it! I want to hear some progress tomorrow - easy stuff ...everything out of your bedroom. Your bedroom is only for sex and sleeping.

### Madison28 — 2016-08-30T15:29:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200511>

Thanks for the encouraging comments, everyone!! And I love the detailed explanations, Caroline! My brain struggles a bit with stuff lately, so I love things broken down into bite size pieces.

I am a survivor....if you only knew....

Yes I can turn the breaker off until I get the stuff out of my room. I will do that starting today. And no to smart meters, got it.

Where I live does suck however I cannot move at this time. I need my mother's financial support and she is thoroughly against moving. I wish it were not like this because I have told her we BOTH really need to move, and she is against it. Ugh. The money I have coming in does not afford me the opportunity to live on my own. I can try to work on that however at the moment I'm just too sick to get any kind of employment.

I will go back to the home page and read the getting started stuff as well as start listening to all the podcasts. That will be a good start for me today. As far as the Circadian rhythm stuff I wish I knew how to fix that. Any suggestions? I know I need to change the sleep patterns... That being said I am physically exhausted to the point that it's hard to move at all. I was so desperate that I tried different medications offered to me by my dr. And I can not sleep through the night no matter what I try to do...grrr.

To those that mentioned divorces, how did you heal? I want to get to the point one day where this does not affect me at all. I thought my in-laws loved me as if I was their own daughter but I had to come to find out that I was nothing to them. They funded my ex-husband in his divorce proceedings and I can only imagine how much money they all paid after going through three attorneys. On my end I had to invest $55,000 just to survive it all and to keep my son...my ex and family (them) were as low as low can get, they even had me followed, and not once followed a court order until absolutely having to because of the threat of jail. Really darn pathetic... his entire family would walk into the courtroom like piranhas ready to feast on me. I always went alone with my attorney.... and I was as brave as brave could be considering the situation and circumstances.  One day I will heal completely!! I fully believe in karma and they may think that what they did was small beans but in life in general Karma will come back on them one day. The ex hasn't spoken to our children in almost 2 years...! Crazy!

I want to thank you guys for the book recommendations... I am a fan of reading so I will get on that as soon as possible.

Again, thank you for your help and encouragement.

### fitness@home — 2016-08-30T16:02:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200516>

> To those that mentioned divorces, how did you heal?

I had to forgive first and foremost. Divorce is a two-way street. Both parties play a part in it. During the worst of it, I went to counseling. Helped to have a neutral 3rd party to discuss things with. Although his family will always side with him, they were (and still are) aware of his issues. Nice family and I have no problem with them.

Believe it or not, I got a phone call a couple of years ago from his _next_ wife out of the blue when I was at work. She found me via LinkedIn and called our main reception desk. She introduced herself and said, "Please don't hang up!" Turns out it was far worse for her. She is now divorced also and we are friends. Recently she said, " We have to be there for the next unlucky woman..."

### Valuation Man — 2016-08-30T16:31:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200518>

I agree with what fitness@home said and you have to forgive him and yourself and get a counselor. The other important thing I did was to be part of a support group. I went to DivorceCare through a church (national organization) and there are other support groups that deals with divorce (look at meetup.com). It was helpful to be in a group where others are going through the same thing and your feelings and struggles are normalized. Also, it was helpful to see others that were further along in the process that provided encouragement that it does get better. You will get better, follow the advice here and find a group and friends that been through the process that can support you

### Madison28 — 2016-08-30T17:58:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200519>

That's a great idea Valuation Man! When fitness@home mentioned forgiveness, I thought I had done that. It's a battle for me, I keep continually going over for forgiving him over and over again as well as his family. And I've gone to counseling and I thought I had it all done and over with but apparently I don't. You both really nailed it and have some awesome ideas.... thank you so much.

I've seen some of those Divorce Care signs around in our town. I'll that have to get involved in that one too.

Sincerely grateful!

### caroline — 2016-08-31T03:27:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200536>

I have a different perspective altho I have never been divorced.

I did have to end the life of my beloved DH of many years .....the grief and guilt is paralysing but .....

After three counsellors I realised I/ we were just talking about the same shirt over and over and over. It was helping nothing really ..... It wasn't going to change anything.

Dr. K. Helped me with that ...... Endlessly talking about the same crappy situation gets you no where.

You go over the situation, examine it, turn it over and over and over ......and then put it aside -,and move forward.

You need to get yourself in forward motion .....that is why you are here. The past is the past and it hurts like hell and you can't figure it out .....so be it.

Get on with it girl!

You need to reset the clock in your head .....get outside and lay on the grass - naked if possible. Soak up as much sunshine as possible, drink good water, eat seafood.

Tell us what you have done for yourself today......

Your mother has cancer? And she isn't interested in moving?

You need to show her a thing or two or three......you got lots to do!

### Madison28 — 2016-08-31T04:16:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200539>

YES! I have lots to do!

Today, I went outside and ran my toes through the grass and soaked up the sun for as long as I could, and it felt ahh-mazing!! I had my three grandbabies today and I'm whooped, but its a good kind of tired.

I can relate to everything you wrote Caroline...re-hashing things over and over gets you no where, and I have to say that's where we were, my ex and me. HOW DO I GET OUT OF THE PAST?

Ohhh... I don't mean to shout but I need to get out of this hell-hole and I need to do it soon. You are right when you say I have lots to do..... I just don't know where to begin.

Thank you for the nudge...I need more of 'em.

### caroline — 2016-08-31T04:44:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-introduction-from-the-chicago-burbs.18346/post-200544>

The past is part of us .....we are like a tapestry - and it makes us who we are now
there must have been lots of good times too ..be thankful for all of the good stuff.

You need to focus on regaining your health - and most of all - your joy? You have children and grand babies to be an inspiration/example for. ....we all need to stop feeling sorry for ourselves - so not productive in any way shape or form.....been there - done that.

Learn from the past and move forward now

It will be hard for awhile because your dopamine levels are low ......so concentrate on all the positive things you need to do for yourself every single day.

One day, if you work hard at all of this, I promise you - the lights will come on!

That doesn't mean that everything, suddenly, will be perfect - it certainly won't be ....but you will see everything with new eyes and a wonderful new perspective, and an open and forgiving heart.

Have you thought of taking up drumming?

Do you listen to music? There are a couple of wonderful music threads here. They are incredible healers.

Just go outside and be .....make yourself sing and dance and laugh. And I promise you - Mother Nature will weave her magic!

One day you will wake up and really, really see.

### caroline — 2016-08-31T04:48:20+0300

That's it ......you don't have to write the great American novel - not just yet anyway!

### Madison28 — 2016-08-31T06:10:13+0300

I believe it's time for me to heal... Time for me to get to know Mother Nature really well... and time to introduce myself to Seafood. I can honestly say it's time for me to spend time on me to make me the best person that I can become.

### caroline — 2016-08-31T08:34:01+0300

When you are outside with your grand babies - let them crawl around naked in the grass and sunshine - what an amazing gift you will be giving them.

Your Mother and daughter and son and grandchildren will be watching you ...... You are doing this for you - but you will also be giving your family the gift of life and showing them all the possibilities that Mother Nature is bestowing on all of us.

vitamin D level is 11.6
2005 I was diagnosed with MS

I am eating a lot of fruit (watermelon, grapes, apples) and then toast and soups, 2 cups of coffee per day, and drinking higher ph water, about 64 ounces of that with lemon in it. I do not eat any meat nor do I eat fish because Seafood has always been kind of yuk to me.

I've also gained probably 40 pounds since February.

The money I have coming in does not afford me the opportunity to live on my own. I can try to work on that however at the moment I'm just too sick to get any kind of employment.

\=======================================================================================
Taking into account
that you cannot move, live in Chicago
do not have financial resources
do not like meat or seafood
gained 40 pounds since Frebruary

I suggest the following:
Get at least one, better up to four or six lamps

get one

If you happen to have electrical heating, it would be good idea to use as many as you want of:
RubyLux Infrared Bulb NIR-A Near Infrared Individual Bulb
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00N4JE9U6/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o06\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00N4JE9U6/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o06_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

\----------------------
Artificial lighting management for a human locked in small room.

Day length=24hrs
Light--14 hrs
dark--10hrs
sleep--8hrs
Use minimum to no clothes 24/7
\----------------------------------------------
Wakeup-6AM
Bed time-10PM
\----------------------------------------------
At 6AM for 2-3 minutes look directly at one Solar Glow 160W
\----------------------------------------------
Light 6AM-8PM using
Exo Terra Solar Glow 160W
\----------------------------------------------
Light 8PM-10PM
using RubyLux ALL RED
\-----------------------------------------------
Night
No light at all, any windows blocked completely from the light
Hand held red light similar to RubyLux ALL RED can be used on the way to the bathroom
\---------------------------------------------

Do not go to bed in other times unless sick.
\---------------------------------------------
\----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Get selenium, Lugols, vit D

every day eat
one selenium pill
five drops of vit D
ten drops of Lugols

First two months take vitamin D twice a day, latter just one a day
All three above take indefinitely.

# Thread: Matthias's notes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matthias — 2016-08-30T18:49:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-200523>

Hello everyone! This is where I'm going to keep my ongoing collection of biohacks (of varying degrees of control and observation). I'll try and keep everything dated, and also be as comprehensive as I recall about any other environmental conditions.

**My background**

My father is an American from Boston, MA; he's mostly Irish with some German. My father has a handful of niggling health problems and only a few bad ones: he has been myopic for my entire life, needing glasses since he was in his mid/late 20s; he has psoriasis sores across his body, but typically on places covered by clothing (**clue**). He had chicken pox when he was younger and an episode of Shingles the mid 2010s. His father passed away in the early 1970s (age in his 50s) from pancreatic cancer; his mother passed away in the mid 1990s (age in her late 80s) from complications arising from arthritis.

My mother is from [Taiwan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taiwan) and came to the US in the 70s. She has bipolar disorder and is currently medicated. She was diagnosed with breast cancer in the early 2010s, had surgery, and has been in remission since ~2012. Apart from those two, her health hasn't had any major indications. Her father passed away in _his_ 50s also in the mid 1970s and _also_ from pancreatic cancer; her mother is still alive and kicking in her late 90s. (My grandma seemingly has undiagnosed mental illness, based on the anecdotes that come back from Taiwan).

My parents were both born in the late 1940s, and they had me when they were in their early 40s. I spent most of my formative life in suburbs outside of Boston, MA; about a decade ago I moved to Springfield, MA. (Population density about [4800 people/sq. mi](http://www.city-data.com/city/Springfield-Massachusetts.html), i.e. _not good_).

**My current state**

I still currently reside in Springfield, MA, because I have developed a personal and professional network in this area. (And there's a little bit that I have yet to finish).

I sleep on the second floor of my house in a room with the lowest electric and magnetic fields, according to my TriField. (No Magnetico yet, but I'm saving for it). I have a weak Wifi router in the basement, and frequently disable it. I chose a house with a large yard (and tall fence), so I can go out first thing in the morning and get almost naked sunlight daily.

Unfortunately, my professional life consists of working at a desk in front of computer screen (cf. profile picture). Additionally, _at work_ I'm exposed to at least a 5-6mG magnetic field and with frequent Wifi hotspots around the office and the shot. Worse yet--my personal interests involve some level of screen usage--ones which often require accurate color definition (I like movies and make short ones in my spare time). Both at home and at work, I wear my blueblockers as often as is feasible

Days when I eat breakfast (probably ~3 days of the week), I'll have a BP coffee, a half dozen eggs, and probably some beef. If I ate breakfast, I usually skip lunch, but both are almost every day devoted to seafood. Some amount of crab, oysters when they're fresh, lightly-cooked wild salmon, etc... Gotta get my DHA. Usually after dinner--and I try to get this done before dusk--I'll do my cold thermogenesis. In the winter, I don't heat my house, and I make a point of walking around outside almost naked on the non-windy days..

**My goals**

Concerning just my health, my goals are modest:

The things I _really_ want to fix are:

-   Cold hands/feet
-   Minor psoriasis/dandruff/skin indications (which I'll include pimples)
-   My myopia (slightly stronger than 20/40 vision--both eyes)

And the stretch goal is a meager:

-   Be biologically 25 for the next 3 decades

The biggest design constraint for them are: I have to spend a large part of those next decades in California--both for the desire of my heart, the movie business, and also because I need to accomplish certain things in California.
