### Matthias — 2016-09-15T23:12:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-201493>

Biohacking notes

In the past few weeks, I’ve done some one-off trials of different things based on what I’d read and learned:

-   Wine consumption
-   Nicotine
-   Slight reappearance of pimples

**Wine consumption**

In Mid-late August, I stayed over with some friends in, and in the evening I had some red wine with dinner socially. It had been months since my last drink (which had been under similar conditions).

Over the evening, I drank 3 glasses within about an hour. While doing so I had made sure to drink plenty of Pellegrino water, and I kept my blueblockers aligned with the rest of the evening. I started drinking ~7:30 and finished at around ~8:30. By 10PM, I noticed that I felt totally sober.

Having explored this, I would later discover [Rub Sal’s talk at Nourish Vermont](https://youtu.be/wHDNW4qQI9I?t=3836) (timestamp included). Between both forms of information, I decided to try and reproduce this once I was at home. I bought a bottle of Malbec (100% Argentinian Mendoza) to have with dinner in the evening while I ate (almost) naked in the setting sunlight before dusk.

Didn’t feel a thing.

Through the week, I would try one or two glasses, all with the same effect. (**Note**: I made sure to abstain from CT during this time based on Jack’s FAQ: [https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-13-the-faqs/](https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-13-the-faqs/) and also a stray post I’d read where Inger had also mentioned things going badly having done CT after drinking).

During the weekend on the subsequent week, I deliberately drank 3 glasses of wine within 30 minutes with my lunch; I did so while almost naked and around ~2:30PM, just after solar noon. Same thing: I didn’t feel a thing beyond a minor motor inhibition, which dissipated after an hour or two.

The idea for drinking mid-day with the sunlight on my skin comes from either Jack’s or Rub Sal’s talk at Nourish Vermont wherein they mention that resveratrol absorbs UV light, and another stray remark that one of them makes is “when you’re getting UV light and doing CT, it almost doesn’t matter what you eat or drink”.

**Nicotine**

Subjectively, I’ve noticed that I always find myself struggling with is being motivated for non-self-initiative projects (mostly at my workplace, but also just tasks for/with people in my personal life). This was partially alleviated whenever I would drink coffee (usually black, but I really enjoy bulletproof coffee).

I would later develop a thought that it might be related to my dopamine metabolism. In the recent period of time of the past ~2 months, I had noticed that this effect of unmotivation had gotten worse. What I hadn’t realized at the time was that I wasn’t doing cold thermogenesis (outside of having done it every day when I was staying on the 15th floor in downtown Philadelphia for a chess tournament).

Jack had remarked on nicotine has a worthy biohack and having regulating effects on dopamine. Both Jack and [Rub Sal](https://youtu.be/wHDNW4qQI9I?t=4049) (timestamp included) and [Luis](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/nicotine-gum-biohack-ubi-17.15797/page-5#post-197918) and the had emphasized that it was to be used synergistically with UV light. (Jack had also mentioned that this is partially why [there’s a lower incidence of cancer from those who smoke outside than those who smoke indoors](<http:// https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/nicotine-and-mb-and-high-uv-exposure.18173/#post-198460>))

At the time I decided to try the nicotine experiment, I had wound down the Malbec trial. (I abstained for two days before trying nicotine).

I bought the 21mg dosage of NicoDerm CQ from the Pharmacy. (It cost fifty dollars. Fifty Dollars!). I cut up a single 21mg patch into quarters and put on one patch in the evening with dinner while I ate almost naked in the backyard. I felt—at most—a mild effect, including both my heart and my focus.

The next day I tried the same thing in the early morning sunlight, and later got sunlight around ~8:30-10AM.

**Woah** it felt great. I got _so much_ done that day—and it was all without any serious indications that I’d get from an overdose of caffeine (e.g. heart palpitations, racing mind, inability to focus for long durations, etc…). My performance rating on the chess tactics server even went up, too. My response time _decreased_ for individual actions.

I tried it again the next day with the same sunlight conditions, and it felt the same. Nicotine is great.

However, this biohack told me an important lesson: **do cold thermogenesis**. I realized after digging more that the reason that my dopamine metabolism wasn’t performing as it should—and other things like my sleep hormone cycles leaving me feeling a little too groggy—was due to my not doing enough CT.

Nicotine is great, but it doesn’t replace changing the internal, cellular quantum yield.

**Reappearance of pimples**

Within the past ~six weeks, I had noticed I had been getting more pimples. Minor sebaceous ones that clear out in a couple days—nothing drastic. They had appeared, gone away for a time, and then recently reappeared.

I did notice, however, that I had almost none for a period where I was traveling to/from Philadelphia on a trip last week. On said trip, I was in a hotel at altitude and without a Quantlet. So I did heavy CT for the day I was there, and I exclusively ate seafood. Oysters, littlenecks, shrimp—everything.

No pimples.

Even when I'd been in a magnetically compromised environment without optimal levels of sunlight. When I had come back, I resumed my usual diet. It’s more land food than I would like—primarily for budget and availability reasons. (Turns out getting living oysters in the Massachusetts inland is not as convenient as being on one of the coasts).

But something I happen to really enjoy is _pork_. Specifically pork belly. Anyway, I suddenly came to my senses and realized that the pimples and sebaceous indications are due to my O6 being imbalanced.

\~~~~~~

Supensaa, great finds! I’ve got some stuff I’ve learned about my condition as it relates, and I’ll elaborate on all of the bulletpoints:

-   Critically control **all** of your the (blue) light environment
-   DHA intake must be very high; supplemental iodine food is essential
-   Fournier effect on extremities
-   Sunlight & Grounding heal ailing tissues
-   Improve the magnetic sense/fields/environment

Now, I know all of this sounds like straightforward, run-of-the-mill Jack Kruse Forums advice, but particular technique is why I’m elaborating.

**Blue Light Environment** – this one was the key for the biggest reduction of my symptoms _across the board_. In the winter, I had subjectively noticed that my conditions get worse (this should be a **big hint** to myself and anyone else). This meant my scalp was/is flakier, I’m more prone to get pimples, etc…

From some biohack notes, I had decided to start wearing blue blocker glasses indoors in the winter (keep in mind I’m in Massachusetts where UV light totally goes away). As you can see from my picture, I spend my whole day bathed in blue light and light from computer screens.

After as quickly as ~2 weeks, I had noticed that the amount of skin that I was shedding was _way down_, and I hadn’t had any skindications from either pink psoriasis-like discolorations or pimples.

Big takeaway: **it’s basically about a blue light/nnEMF imbalance in your body caused by the environment**

It’s that simple. Use the blueblockers to take away the excess blue light—both day/night and indoors—and you’ve already reduced the symptoms.

**DHA intake & Iodine** – this goes hand-in-hand with the previous point, and the previous point on the above recent biohacks. We get the pimples because that’s how we express inflammation from excess O6 in the diet. (I haven’t had any mass spectrometry done on any of the sebum from pimples, but I’d _guess_ that’s basically what it is—that and keratin).

Blue light destroys DHA; the blueblockers prevent the destruction of DHA. _Iodine also prevents the destruction of DHA_. And pastured eggs by themselves aren’t enough, you gotta get seaweed; [dulse](https://www.amazon.com/s/ref=nb_sb_noss_2?url=search-alias%3Dgrocery&field-keywords=dulse+leaf) is great, and [arame](https://www.amazon.com/EDEN%C2%AE-ARAME-SEAWEED-2-1-OZ/dp/B001AS975U/ref=pd_sim_sbs_325_1?ie=UTF8&psc=1&refRID=Z843JKQ31WP7C6EZ0V8F) is the best.

If you’re blocking the destruction of DHA while also supplying plenty of it, then you’re going to see symptoms reduce. When I’m eating oysters, my skin is as full as it is with the way I want it. (The only problem is… for me, it’s difficult to practically have them for every meal).

**Fournier effect on Extremities** – I mentioned in my bio that I don’t heat my house in the winter and walk around the house without clothing. (I’m not sure how advisable that is if I’m around sub-optimal light on my skin, in retrospect).

However, in the winter time I wear a [Cool Fat Burner](https://coolfatburner.com/) basically all the times I’m at/around the house. (I can’t keep my tub filled with water because the water will freeze, and it will make it impossible to take a shower for days I have meetings).

For the cold hands/feet, I’ve found that filling my tub with **warm** water, and submerging both my lower body and my hands in the hot water. My head, torso, and arms are exposed to the cold bathroom while I’m wearing the ice vest. This is Fournier effect; essentially I’m gaming the electron flow to my hands (and lower body parts).

This acutely helps with my symptoms for a duration, and has eliminated most of the indications. I usually only get the cold hands/feet in the winter now, if I’m doing everything else (CT, DHA, etc…).

**Sunlight & Grounding** – This is where it starts to seem like “good advice”, but it’s really really high-impact. But it’s just cellular reality of how light moves across the body.

Getting sunlight directly on a portion of affected skin helps, but getting it helps everything. I’ve had my scalp clear up even when it was blocked from sunlight (i.e. thick hair and a hat) _because I was getting plenty of UV light on my skin elsewhere_.

This is where being almost naked as often as possible and grounded (read: go to the beach as often as you possibly can!) is really important. It doesn’t happen if you’re just wearing shortsleeve shirts without a hat. All of your skin can develop skindications; all of your skin needs sunlight.

**Magnetic sense** – This is the big one:

I get cold hands because my hands are on a keyboard & mouse for 9 hours of every day. When Jack mentioned in his Nourish Vermont video that he’s living on “borrowed time” because of his light/magnetic environment at work, my current state is a shade of that.

Any number of the niggling issues that I, you, or anyone else has is a direct result of modern living. (Much as we all love cell phones). It sounds like woo, but when I moved my computer from the room with the breaker box to the room _as far away from the breaker box_, I saw my skin clear, and my response to CT skyrocket.

_You can improve all you want, but it’s meaningless if your environment un-does it_.

There are things that I know would improve where I am: getting a Magnetico, moving away from Springfield, shutting off the breaker to my home at night, etc…

And there will come a day when I’m doing all of these things, and I’m writing about how they worked right here in this thread.

Blue light is just another _form_ of nnEMF, and it’s typically what’s the biggest cause of our skin problems. But, in general, it’s the whole magnetic environment you’re in that’s the problem.

\~~~~~

Anyway, the big takeaways for you are:

-   Always wear blue blocker sunglasses with artificial, non-incandescent light (and all the time at night)

-   Get lots of supplemental iodine from seaweed in addition to eating the oysters
-   Do cold thermogenesis, and use the Fournier effect for ALL that it’s worth
-   Expose your skin to sunlight more than you think you need to

### Matthias — 2016-09-27T18:18:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-201974>

**Sleeping Cold**

I deliberately don't use any form of air conditioning in the winter or summer. For the winter, the reason is to maximize my cold thermogenesis; for the summer, it's cumbersome to manage an air conditioner because I use push-in blackout shades in my windows.

This past summer, I noticed that the greenhouse effect in my home of the hot (still, humid) air can really disrupt my circadian rhythms. Throughout the summer, I was waking up groggy, missing getting awoken with sunlight, and usually not "waking up" until I'd gone outside and done something active to start the day. And this was true even though I was getting a _lot_ of sunlight on my skin, and using infrared at bedtime.

The temperatures are winding down (as is UV in the atmosphere--yikes!), and so in the evenings, it's colder. Such as I can with the season, I make sure to sleep with as little clothing on, and use as little blanket covering as well.

What's been happening to me in the past few weeks that crosslinks from my trip to Philadelphia; in Philly, I had deliberately set the AC in my room to be as cold as possible all the time (as you'd expect). I would do the same sleeping routine to be in the cold at night.

Anyway the short of what's happened with me is that my libido has skyrocketed. Like... crazy.

And I'm now convinced that it's a mixture of plenty of dietary zinc, but also prolonged cold exposure at nighttime.

### Matthias — 2016-11-02T21:33:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-203531>

**The bad kind of update: Got a new cell phone
**
The following might be one of the worst mistakes that I've ever made while improving my redox potential more and more: I got a new smartphone: a Motorola [Moto Z Play](http://www.gsmarena.com/motorola_moto_z_play-8310.php).

I got it yesterday. Ever since then, I have felt **_awful_**.

My previous phone was a LG [Google Nexus 4](http://www.gsmarena.com/lg_nexus_4_e960-5048.php). In my Trifield measurements and subjective with/without testing, it was really great on Airplane mode. Didn't much move the needle, was fine to have against my body (ONLY in airplane mode). And, in terms of design and usability: it was nice and small, good for single-handing, unobtrusive, and I loved the physical appearance of the phone's back.

The poor girl hit the floor on Sunday, and the screen became unusably cracked.

Somewhat foolishly, I had decided to just get a new phone without testing any from the market. I ended up choosing the Moto Z Play, but I imagine that if I were to live with the phones of any of my peers, I'd have the same feeling. All phones now have: LTE, excessively power Wifi antennas, and similarly-powerful Bluetooth and Location antennas.

Whenever I _hold_ the phone, I get a sensation in my hands. When I'm at home in my nice cold house, my hands get freezing after a few minutes.

My heart starts _racing_ after I've been using it for a few minutes off-airplane mode. And that's even when cell data/LTE is off! Like, it's insane! I'm getting symptoms that I _never_ got with my Nexus 4. And they all get worse when I turn LTE on.

Honestly, it even feels like it's still generating some EMFs when it's in airplane mode (and even with location off). I biohacked keeping it in airplane mode next to my bed (something that I'd also tested with my Nexus 4 to minimal effect on my sleep); it was terrible--I woke up at least twice in the middle of the night.

\*Edit: Also, across today, I've had to drink THREE times as much Pellegrino as I normally did/do over the course of the day just so I didn't feel awful. I had to go outside at least twice as much to be asymptomatic.

\~~~~

When I was first setting it up, I had to use it heavily just to make sure all of my contacts were updated, that my email was good, etc... My mind felt _numb_ by the end. I realized:

_This is exactly why some of my friends are the way they are; why it's hard to actually engage them, and their brains just don't work the right way_

I feel some resentment to this being the direction of the world is going; there are certain goals and network that I actively pursue (specifically, I want to run for local political office), and I need a phone like this to really make all of it work.

But this--the things it does?

It sucks.

### Matthias — 2016-12-04T04:06:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-204745>

**Early Winter Update**

Now that the bad season of no-UV, cold all the time, high-fat, high-grounding season is on us, I thought I'd share a little bit about the effects that I've measured in myself (relative to previous winters), and any data-driven anecdotes.

**Response to coffee
**
Something I've observed is that in in the summer with lots of UV, my coffee metabolism is very smooth: any jitters or side-effects I get are minimal, the upside to my cognitive performance is high, and I don't get much burnout or ill effects.

This winter, I haven't experienced that much of ill reactions, but my symptoms definitely change. I'm not as reponsive to it, for a start. (I suspect this has to do with the upside of caffeine relative to the amount of supplemental UV that I get). Additionally, where I could easily abstain from it in the summertime--as much as I _loooooove_ coffee; shoutout to my girl caroline--my cravings/impulse to chain consecutive days with coffee are much higher.

My tolerance for coffee has slightly diminished. I experience moderate compulsion (which I imagine--but can't empirically confirm--is dopamine imbalance) for a slight period about ~2-3 hours after first consumption. It doesn't last beyond several minutes.

Finally, I get more ache in my bones than I did during the summer. Typically, when I abstained from coffee for any period in excess of ~2 weeks, resuming consumption would leave me with some achy bones. Jack or someone more studied would have to shed some light on exactly what's happening--and why I can feel it--but it would only last a single day even through continued usage. Drinking coffee daily in the winter, however, gives me this feeling on a much lower level--however, it happens every day.

This leads me to believe that coffee in winter is a circadian mismatch. However, I've put myself in a place where I also feel an increased dependence on it. What I'm guessing this indicates is that I need to up my Cold Thermogenesis amount and/or further reduce my carbohydrate intake. I'll try biohacking both.

**Blue light without blueblockers
**
As you nice people can see, I wear my blueblockers indoors. I worked/work at a manufacturing facility, so it was easier for me to keep them on during the day because they're safety glasses. However, something I intend to do in the near-future is apply therapeutic tints to some cheap non-prescription lenses from Zenni Optical. ([hat tip from Jason Lauritzen](https://medium.com/@jasonlauritzen/blue-light-and-health-plus-how-to-make-your-own-blue-blocking-glasses-1a3d2694ad60#.rmqtomz20); that link will show you how you can do the same). However, I haven't made them yet. So in situations where I would use attractive frames, I just go without.

Today I had a workshop/seminar in which I was without my blueblockers but also kept in a windowless, fluorescent-lit room with a projector for about 6 hours. Over the course of the session, I could _feel_ my eyesight worsen: I'm normally just under 20/20 because of some astigmatism that would correct itself if I were in a location of the world with UV light all the time; at this seminar, I was losing focus on typical things I'd been able to focus on by the end. Additionally, I'd found that my eyes were giving off floaters--something I found more common as a kid, but that I rarely get since changing a lot of my quantum/magnetic environment.

Anyway: I can't make my special glasses and ersatz Carbonshades soon enough.

### Lucien Burke — 2016-12-09T18:43:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-205057>

Matthias!

What's up man ~ I grew up in western Mass and spent the first 15 years of my life there...

I know springfield's not the most optimal place to live but there are plenty of great spots in the area... The Berkshire region is just a short drive away. Definitely worth it for even a day trip just to get out of the city and go camping or for a hike. All kinds of good swimming holes for CT and spring water and awesome trails for grounding, sun, and O2 in the woods. Also really spotty cell service in the hills...

I would recommend checking out the Bullit Nature Reservation in Ashfield MA. It's got an amazing granite overlook where you can rock climb ( Connect with your hands + feet) and an ice cold swimming hole with year round access and a few water falls.

Get out of the city dude!! Nature awaits!!! Even in western MA...

### Matthias — 2017-01-01T15:10:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-205983>

**Magnetico: Day 1
**
Shortly after posting last night, I went to bed, natch. I noticed I had some difficulty falling asleep--though I don't know if it was related to the pad, to my excitement, or to having been slightly stressed just beforehand after lugging and setting all that weight. Gretchen mentioned that there are possible detox symptoms, but also mentioned that her read on it was that if my methylation pathways were working right, I shouldn't have much of a problem.

Anyway, at around 1:30AM, I woke up in the middle of the night. I was aware disrupted sleep was a possible symptom, so I'd assumed that it might happen to me. But what I'd noticed was:

I was really, really warm

My house is cold--especially in the winter--because I deliberately don't heat it. But because it can get _very_ cold, I have a fleece blanket, and two comforters. (I'm not married and like the pressure on my body. What do you want from me?).

When I'd woken up, seen the time, and gone back to sleep I had trouble getting back _because_ I was so hot. It dawned on me:

_Oooooohhhhh_ **this** _is how humans stayed warm at nights in winter_

Seeing the effects of an unadulterated magnetic field--even after a single day--was pretty eye-opening. Looking forward to the next four week to see how it works.

I will say, though: the Pellegrino bottles of tap water I put under my bed seem to taste about the same. I'll leave them there, though.

### Matthias — 2017-01-02T16:20:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-206037>

**Magnetico: Day 2
**
Last night I slept similar to last night, everything considered. I went to bed at ~9PM because yesterday morning I had gotten up at just after 6AM. I had omitted that detail, but that was a large departure from getting up closer to ~7AM (with natural sunlight) as I had been. In the evening, I felt the orange glasses _really_ start working at around 7:30 all throughout going to bed. It felt so weird not to want to stay up and watch the football game.

_Key observation: I suspect that the Magnetico enhanced my response to the orange glasses again; they were having a strong effect similar to when I first started wearing them--much as the **red** glasses do/did when I would put them on_

In the middle of the night, I woke up at ~1AM again. Checked the time, and then went back to sleep. I was **not** very warm this time, however.

At around 5AM (5AM!) I woke up. I'd seen that it was still dark, and thought it might not be optimal for my circadian rhythms--given that there's no light--so I tried to fall back asleep. I couldn't easily fall back asleep, and was in a half-asleep, half-woken state until just after 6AM. I'd gotten up because I'd started to feel the need to urinate. It reminds me of when I first installed all of the pads on my bed: I mysteriously had to urinate after I'd finished setting them all. (I presume because of the effects of having the magnets that close to my body). I ended up urinating **way more** than I'd thought I had to--and probably more than I had across... the past few months.

_Key observation: My need to urinate feels very similar to my need to urinate after doing cold thermogenesis. It reminded me of a post I came across--that I can't find now, of course--in which Yew Wei compared thermogenesis and its magnetic effects to that of the Magnetico for the water chemistry in the cell_.

And finally, between yesterday and today, I've been keeping bottles of tap water in Pellegrino bottles both **under** and **on top of** my bed. I haven't been able to "taste" a difference between the two. However, after having kept the water there, I can notice a difference \*between Magnetico water and regular tap water.

While I can't measure the amount of exclusion zone that the water is constructing (it certainly isn't gel-like), I _have_ subjectively noticed that I don't have the desire to drink as much of it as I would with other, normal water from the tap. That effect will probably normalize over time--as will the effects of the Magnetico--as my psychological bias to the details subsides.

I know Jack mentions the copper pot for water **on top** of the Magnetico, but given that water is a dipole, can I still get the same effects as keeping it on the bottom of my bed? (honestly, it's just more convenient that way).

### Matthias — 2017-01-03T16:25:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-206083>

**Magnetico: Day 3
**
The previous night was similar to the first two, except I don't _think_ I woke up in the middle of the night as I did. (I can't actually recall, so I'm guessing I didn't). I went to bed at around 9:30--about 30 minutes later than the previous night--and I woke up at **4AM**. Four AM!

However, even at 4AM, I woke up feeling more refreshed/less groggy than I had in the two previous mornings. I had considered getting up because I was alert. However, I again thought "this can't be wise, given that I'd be up very early, and be around artificial light, etc..."

_Key observation/question: So far, the amount of actual sleep I "need" while using it has plummeted. Normally, I'd go to bed at around 10-10:30-11 and wake up around 7-7:30 with sunlight. Here, I've netted as much as 7.5 and as few as 6.5 hours. Is this how early humans could live? (Or at least get the most out of their day). If this is how it's going to be, this will be **awesome** to have more waking hours to get things done. It's just weird to think of it in this way._

Anyway, I decided to force myself back to sleep. I think this might be a mistake because it's a circadian mismatch. At the time I went back to sleep, I felt myself getting a slight chill around my shoulders (indicating, I think, that there was a changeover in my mitochondria to environmentally/activity-driven thermogenesis, instead of resting/sleeping thermogenesis). However, I only slept for ~45 minutes and re-woke at 4:45AM.

When I'd re-woken, I felt myself with some minor sweating. As-in, I think I had been generating heat after having gone back to bed. It still being early I **again** went back to sleep. I then slept until around 7:10AM. (Which I considered more reasonable--there was light out--and I got up).

Either on second pass or third, I _dreamt_. The previous two nights, I hadn't dreamt--or at least I hadn't had conscious recollection of dreaming. In retrospect, I _think_ I dreamt between 4 and 4:45, but I'm not sure. (The dream was great; I was part of a Presidential transition/reservation meet--that was **not** the current administration; I would have remembered The Donald--and I was networking with various people. I had my great pick-stitched blue pinstripe suit on, and there were attractive women who I was effortlessly approaching. I mean, it was like real life in that way, but it was at a ranch with meetings and business. It was a fun dream).

I think tomorrow I'm going to be more conscientious of the time, and just say "screw it" and get up wicked early. Sleeping the whole night--again, in my recollection--seems like a major watermark in my adjustment.

**Coffee with Ubiquinol**

In a follow-up to my remarks earlier about coffee in the winter, I'd done more biohacking (or at least... written observations about a biohack that I'd probably done earlier) with coffee in the wintertime.

For a business trip I took to SC two weeks ago, I purchased some Ubiquinol for the flight. And I had the balance of the bottle left over. While I had recalled that any non-whole food supplement should be accompanied by ubiquinol to offset the increase in protein turnover with any supplement. When I'd biohacked _not_ using ubiquinol in the summer with coffee, I'd turned out okay. (\*Which, in retrospect, was likely due to all the UV light I'd been getting. Recall: I'd been _drinking **serious** amounts of alcohol in a biohack and_ also _been turning out okay_)

So, I'd lapsed and _continued_ using coffee without any ubiquinol into the winter. Anyway, having resumed consumption of both (usually around 400mg Ubiquinol and 20mg PQQ for synergy), I can say that my caffeine symptoms such as loss of focus, compulsion, racing mind, etc... have all been strongly mitigated.

Just like GE (via Reuben) says:

_The tool you use to measure is more important than the improvement that you make_

### Matthias — 2017-01-11T03:40:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/matthiass-notes.18347/post-206594>

**Started a new job, chipped my tooth, got sick**

On Monday, I started a new job at place that does lots of work lasers, powdered metal, and exposed hot metal. It's great--I really like everybody there, and what we do. However, it had been awhile since I was around very powerful non-native EMFs, as I'd been funemployed. At my new workplace, not only are we around the EMFs from major CNC machines (and it's _much_ more hands-on), but there are also _3kW induction heaters_ around at various parts of the shop to heat up workpieces. I'd had to spend some time around one yesterday for most of the day.

One of my coworkers was sick with a bad cold yesterday, and he ended up being out for the day today. However, I felt unvanquished and immune, given that my exposure around such people didn't lead to any illness. **But...** Today, while I felt fine and not ill, my voice became more and more hoarse as the day went on. If I had to guess, my body resisted whatever it was, except in my throat. I'll likely report back if it gets worse (or gets suddenly better).

However... The most interesting thing to me happened _this morning_. I'd decided to make some bulletproof coffee, as I'd been getting up before dawn in order to make it to work on time--something else I didn't have to worry about when I was waking up with light during my funemployment. Anyway, I happened to bring the pitcher too close to my tooth, and it hit against my left upper front incisor.

_My tooth chipped
_
Every other time that's happened, my tooth was fine. Totally intact. This time? A small part came off. (For which I'm very thankful, as I'm glad it won't require any kind of inordinate dental care. I might have it examined--I might not).

However, this forum has shown me that there aren't really such things as coincidences if you look deep enough for the proximate causes of everything that happens--both metabolically **and** in life.

_I don't think all three things happening at once are a coincidence, either_

### Lola Roman — 2016-08-30T23:25:25+0300

Hello All!

I'd love some guidance on where to start considering my constellation of symptoms.

Look forward to getting to know the community!
**Age:** 55 **Sex:** F **Joined JackKruse.com:** 8/28/16

**Location:** Oakland, CA, USA

**Dx:** _Longstanding:_ PTSD (complex and shock), insomnia, anxiety, depression, headaches, extreme myopia (-12). Depression not an issue with 5-HTP.
_New (2016):_ autoimmune thyroiditis (have antibodies for Graves and Hashimotos, but recent TSH = 0.44 – 0.68 so closer to expression of Graves), osteopenia, fibroids (small, 9 mm)
Of most concern: sudden vitreous detachments with floaters, both eyes, just happened Aug. 10
Also: hair test shows elevated lead, stool test shows dysbiosis

**Surgeries:** 1975, facial surgery following car accident; 2003, cosmetic surgery

**Current Rx:** Tiny dose of clonazapam, 0.125 – 0.25 mg, for sleep/few nights week (down from 1 mg/night); lots of supplements for sleep; occasional propranolol for anxiety; caprylic acid

**Current Therapies / Protocols:** Gut restoration, detoxing (frequent saunas, coffee enemas + Pectasol), started eating meat/fish (have been nearly vegetarian most my life) and exposing myself to sunlight daily without glasses/contacts, sleeping on grounded sheet, changed home lighting to incandescent or halogen, use blue blockers on computer/phone, experimenting with red/near infrared light

**Health WINS:** Fewer headaches, reduced autoimmune activity, got off Gabapentin and drastically reduced Clonazapam, better energy

**Health Goals:** Take charge and reverse conditions! _Especially_ eye problems.

**My Optimal Journal:** Coming soon!

### caroline — 2016-08-31T04:16:53+0300

Welcome....
Please read Jack's book Epi-Paleo RX. - it is the first couple of years of the blog and is a great way to get into all this.

Listen to all Jack's podcast ..... Fast and easier way to get info..

Blue blocker glasses

Smart meter?

Water?

### Lola Roman — 2016-08-31T19:52:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-get-started.18349/post-200595>

Thank you so much!

I'm not sure a Paleo diet is right for me—but I'm all ears for your thoughts! My thinking:

I never liked meat, was a vegetarian from age 17–34. I read Eat 4 Your Blood Type and I'm A- (compatible with vegetarianism), so I thought my diet was suited for my genetics. But over 20 years ago I added occasional chicken and beef to avoid deficiencies.

Now: Following Weston Price diet. Added daily high-quality pastured animal protein + fat + bone broth to my diet (w/ lots of veggies and far fewer grains/fruit). I hesitate to let go of grains/fruit because I'm underweight (5' 10'' 130 lbs) and can't put weight on. I don't need to burn fat or increase my energy. I don't have food cravings of any kind.

Jack says that diet isn't everything, and my intuition says to focus on my environment, esp. in getting more sunlight, reducing toxic exposures (I've totally detoxed my home/products/diet), doing infrared saunas, and drinking great water.

That said, I'll do any diet that will help me reverse my various conditions.

I drink lots of filtered/remineralized water (use the 10-Stage Filter). Considering a filter upgrade (looking at Pure Effects) to remove fluoride, and also a a shower filter, but choosing the right products is a challenge! My goal is to get spring water locally using findaspring.com. Yay! Do you guys have thoughts on Pure Effects?

I'm extremely concerned about the vitreous detachments/floaters. This seems to be a problem with collagen/elastin/hyaluronic acid/hydration, no? I have very dry eyes and frequent urination. My sense is that water goes through and doesn't get absorbed into the cells. Thoughts on that?

I've listened to MANY of Jack's podcasts, will listen to all!

I don't think I get much blue light now that I've eliminated it from my cell phone/computer (f.lux and Nightshift continuously on). Do I still need blue-blocker glasses?

The sheet is grounded outside in the dirt (not in a power socket). Can it still draw dirty electricity?

In my bedroom I don't have anything plugged in and no electronics.

Yes, I have a smart meter! It's not near my bedroom, but still not good. Can anything be done to mitigate effects?

I have a fairly clear idea of the overall program Jack advocates, and I'm moving in that direction A-SAP! Would love to proceed as surgically as possible to the next step best suited to my biology. What do you think?

Deeply grateful for your time!!!

### Lola Roman — 2016-08-31T20:01:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-get-started.18349/post-200596>

P.S. My insomnia began immediately after surgery in 2003. I already had a PTSD diagnosis and the trauma of surgery put my nervous system over the edge.

I run on the hyper side and am mentally overactive (!) but my 24-hour cortisol is oddly normal. I think the sleep issue is related to H-P-A axis dysregulation, but I'm not in adrenal fatigue.

My experiments with decreasing blue light/increasing natural light haven't helped yet. I also light candles at 8PM and turn off the lights. Shifting to parasympathetic mode seems to be the problem.

Would love to hear your thoughts.

Overhead power lines? Electricity in walls? Floors?

I understand he does phone consults and you can rent meters from him.

Can you sleep in your backyard? Go camping and see how you feel?

You need to reset the Rolex in your brain....

Have breakfast within 30 minutes of rising ....BAB

Leave 4 hours between meals and at least that or more before bedtime

The leptin reset.....

### Lahelada — 2016-09-03T16:07:48+0300

@[Curves](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1418/) Hiya, yes it is something I do. I walk around my garden in long armed T shirt and jeans playing in the puddles. Certifiable one looks I am sure but great fun !! I only have fluoridated water so this is a welcome break . I hope anyway.

The reason for full clothing is to get as close to "a being surrounded by water effect "as possible. Cotton a must obviously. I discovered it when a problem had me running around in circles around the loquat tree until 2 am and had the best sleep ever after that even with the problem unresolved. By rights there should be a moat around that tree already.

I extend the practice when the weather is good, but still cold and put on wet shirts. It has happened that I also wear wet jeans inspired by a lack of laundry preparation  Looked at the right way it takes stress out of life twice...

Instead I got up, went to the bathroom drank some water and tried really hard to go back to sleep. It worked, and I woke up around 7 a.m. It was starting to be a pretty day so I got up and went outside for my sun connection. (thats two!) Believe me, the sun was nice, but I was so tired, but I stayed out of bed (3) and did some reading. I think I even read some things on here, and tried to understand what Dr. Kruse meant, but realized I'm going to have to take notes. I really want to understand what he means in his writings and I'm surprised that it's so hard for me to 'get it'. Anyway I did some dry skin brushing today to try and stimulate my lymph (4).... I ordered the brush some time ago and just have had no energy to do it however I really need to try and change my self talk, and push myself more than I have been. It felt so good! Mom has been wanting to go to a little shop that is her favorite...so I took her, and it was a really great time. ### Madison28 — 2016-09-02T19:04:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saving-myself.18350/post-200732>

Good morning friends.... yesterday I decided to delete my overly detailed childhood Journal. I was afraid that it would get into the hands of the wrong person and if that indeed did happen many people in my family would be upset with me for sharing all of my dirty laundry. Oh well. **Hasta la vista!**

Also it was quite depressing. I've suffered a great deal most of my life because of the way I was raised and I've always believed I had forgiven him for most of it, but then I realized a corner of my heart hadn't. I really want to work on doing this if I get any spare time during the day.

So, anyway, as anyone can see from my signature/introduction I have a lot of health issues to conquer. I admit that I used to be a very high-energy person and basically took care of everything for everyone in my family. Obviously that hasn't always been the best idea but now that I am older and wiser (lol), I would really like to finally get my shit together.

One of the first things on my list is learning how to not let other people affect my stress level. How? One way is learning how to say no.

I'd really like to make these things I do becomes lasting changes. I suppose the key to doing exactly that is consistency, so I'm going to try and learn as much as I can about this light exposure stuff and get my butt out in the sun so I can fix my circadian rhythm troubles. Oh how I'd love a great night's sleep... sometimes I actually fantasize about it, yep I do.

Until next time...

### caroline — 2016-09-03T04:30:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saving-myself.18350/post-200763>

Writing it down was very therapeutic. .....now you get to move forward!

5 things today?????

### Madison28 — 2016-09-04T07:03:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saving-myself.18350/post-200834>

Oh my gosh I forgot to put those down...
1\. Sat out in the sun for 30 minutes.
2\. Drink 64 ounces of spring water.
( I really need to get into 100 ounces)
3\. Only napped for an hour today.
( this is huge since my adrenals are fried.... I don't know if I should be giving in to the fatigue or not but it's hard to not give in)
4\. Whole body dry skin brushing.
5\. Received some detox herbs I ordered and started them before bed.

Thank you so much for the reminder I needed !!

### Madison28 — 2016-09-04T07:46:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saving-myself.18350/post-200835>

Today was a beautiful day and I absolutely enjoyed it. I remember reading on here somewhere that someone said when you first get up in the morning to look up at the sky to stimulate the melatonin to stop producing or something like that.  Anyway I did just that first thing this morning and then I got myself ready and went outside and baked in the Sun for an hour and 15 minutes. Oh my gosh I completely forgot how hot the first Morning Sun of the day can be, but it was a gorgeous day here and I sweat my butt off. I truly believe sweating is a good thing for me since I've been feeling so toxic for the last 6 months.
Today we were scheduled to watch my grandbabies for the afternoon so I cleaned up and got ready to do that. I had read somewhere on here also about not using sunglasses to block the sun rays from coming into my eyes, so I left my sunglasses at home and went the entire day without using them. I somehow think that I was using my sunglasses to hide behind in the past.... it may sound weird but today without my sunglasses I felt totally naked. Crazy.
I know this may be TMI but for the past 4 years I've had horrible chronic bowel issues from the MS. Since I've been drinking good water I've noticed a little more activity in that department.  and actually since using the herb stuff I received it was a really good bathroom day, and no diarrhea at all either.
Since I have struggled with candida for so long I'm hoping that starting the leptin RX will help that issue. I will find out I'm sure. Anyway since I have the most beautiful grandkids in the entire world I really enjoyed hanging out with them. I am a crazy, intense, and goofy grandma and it thrills me when the boys get a good laugh in & ask me to 'do it again'. They love the singing I do the most. It cracks me up that they fall for my silliness... I told them I was getting ready to leave today and I would be very upset if they touched me or tried to give me a kiss goodbye. I told Camden "Don't you dare even think about giving me a kiss because if you do I'll have to run as fast as I can out that door." Ha, totally works every time.  ( they never liked giving goodbye kisses before lol)
I don't know what it is but today I felt so happy and light-hearted, I really didn't have any physical pain like I usually do. Can the sun be kicking in that fast? Then I was thinking what in the world am I going to do about the winter? But after I thought about that I told myself I was not going to worry or even let myself start obsessing about it. I'm sure the sun has to be helping since my last vitamin D level was 11.6...way too low!!!
Watching/playing with the kids is a very exhausting thing, especially due to the chronic fatigue, and I have to admit before I came into this website I would come home from my daughter's house thoroughly exhausted and I would sleep the rest of the night away. Today that was not the case for me and I've been awake ever since getting home. ( getting ready to do a quick lymph brushing and then to bed soon here)
Anyway I drank a good amount of filtered water today which is a good thing for my dehydrated body.

I'm trying to figure out how to use more natural products on my skin so I spent some time today reading about natural cleansers that I could make at home for my face. So excited to get started on that tomorrow.

Last night before bed I played some music for about an hour and sang, by candlelight. I think I'll do that tonight again because it was just so relaxing and really filled my heart with good stuff.

I want to try all of Dr. Kruse's protocols and hacks... I'm going to do as much as I can to get well.

### caroline — 2016-09-04T11:01:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saving-myself.18350/post-200843>

You are one awesome woman Madison!

Have you looked at the C/T protocol yet? - there is a getting started guide. I think you are gonna love face dunking!

I loved that - playing music and singing by candlelite!

look at you ......You are unstoppable!

### Lahelada — 2016-09-04T14:24:32+0300

Grand ! I replied to you in your post on the study guide but wanted to say here as Caroline suggested that maybe CT preparation would be a good thing now. Have a read in the Cold thermogenesis series. Prepare now and you can step it up in the winter days when you have little sun.

I will leave you Dr K ' s list of healers . The order they are written is the order they should be followed in.
Time for some Sunday morning music down here now.

Ok.... I don't know what I've done wrong but today I've crashed. # Thread: Question about ice
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-ice.18354/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DPH — 2016-08-31T20:48:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-ice.18354/post-200597>

I have started to prepare for doing my first CT, but one area that is still a bit confusing to me is what would be the best approach to handling all the ice. How do you do it? And what kind of ice is the best?
I can imagine it can be quite messy to have to freeze new ice between every session, so what method would be preferable? Is there some products out there that makes it simpler? I was thinking if you could perhaps freeze whole bags with water? I have also seen plastic icecubes that where links to in the page about CT, these seem easier to handle since you don't have to refill them?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-01T04:12:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-ice.18354/post-200612>

What kind of ICE? The freezing type is best.

### freesia — 2016-09-01T05:34:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-ice.18354/post-200623>

> [DPH said:](/goto/post?id=200597)
>
> I have started to prepare for doing my first CT, but one area that is still a bit confusing to me is what would be the best approach to handling all the ice. How do you do it? And what kind of ice is the best?
> I can imagine it can be quite messy to have to freeze new ice between every session, so what method would be preferable? Is there some products out there that makes it simpler? I was thinking if you could perhaps freeze whole bags with water? I have also seen plastic icecubes that where links to in the page about CT, these seem easier to handle since you don't have to refill them?

I like Jack's answer.

Frozen water is best I think.  I've never found those ice gels packs as effective for lowering water temperature quickly. I freeze 20 - 40 2 lb "bricks" in large yogurt pottles. In winter they don't fully melt so I just scoop the icebergs back up with enough additional water to re-freeze the full pottles again. I start with fresh water periodically...because bathing in recycled bathwater loses it's charm after a bit...
I ice bathe 2 - 4 times per week 3/4 of the year and the above method works well for me. If you were wanting to CT daily the re-freezing every day could be a bit of a hassle.....depends on how big a freezer you have and how easy it is to get ice bricks/blocks back and forth to your bath.

### caroline — 2016-09-01T13:31:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-ice.18354/post-200636>

Hi freesia ......how are you doing? Long time no see!

C/t was/is so incredibly important for you .....maybe you can fill in all the newbies?

### DPH — 2016-09-02T08:52:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-ice.18354/post-200696>

> [freesia said:](/goto/post?id=200623)
>
> I like Jack's answer.
>
> Frozen water is best I think.  I've never found those ice gels packs as effective for lowering water temperature quickly. I freeze 20 - 40 2 lb "bricks" in large yogurt pottles. In winter they don't fully melt so I just scoop the icebergs back up with enough additional water to re-freeze the full pottles again. I start with fresh water periodically...because bathing in recycled bathwater loses it's charm after a bit...
> I ice bathe 2 - 4 times per week 3/4 of the year and the above method works well for me. If you were wanting to CT daily the re-freezing every day could be a bit of a hassle.....depends on how big a freezer you have and how easy it is to get ice bricks/blocks back and forth to your bath.

Thanks! By Ice gel packs, do you mean something like this? : [https://www.conrad.se/?websale8=con...Xt-W3EQLqUoHNGj42RvIG4HudVXoekgREkaAitl8P8HAQ](https://www.conrad.se/?websale8=conrad-swe&pi=841278&ws_tp1=cp&ref=PLA_BIL&subref=841278&utm_source=PLA_BIL&utm_medium=PLAfeed&utm_campaign=PLA_BIL_feed&utm_content=841278&gclid=Cj0KEQjwo5--BRCS8ceLjv-XppUBEiQAGp15EBCfLrv9yXt-W3EQLqUoHNGj42RvIG4HudVXoekgREkaAitl8P8HAQ)

They do look convenient, since they would be easy to handle and you would not have to fill up with new water. What kind of yogurt bottles do you use? Any specific brand that is good for this?

### freesia — 2016-09-06T00:13:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-ice.18354/post-200946>

Hi, yes that's the kind of gel pack. I used some like them at first but I find ice melting directly into the bath water to be more efficient for cooling quickly.

The containers I use for freezing (or refreezing icy bathwater) are a slightly flexible plastic with a recycling symbol 5 on them. (I'm in New Zealand so not sure how that will translate to be useful for you). They contain 800 mls of liquid.

### freesia — 2016-09-06T00:23:06+0300

>
> Hi freesia ......how are you doing? Long time no see!

Doing well - hope you are too!
I was travelling in Europe & the UK for a couple of months. Slipped slightly health wise on return - no doubt in part due to circadian disruption. (Brutal 30 hr flights/transfers each way.)
I really need to write a "freesias guide to cold adaptation" somewhere.

# Thread: Journal of a french guy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-french-guy.18356/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 65
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cdk29 — 2016-09-01T12:54:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-french-guy.18356/post-200634>

Hi !

As I was saying in the presentation post, I'm french, so you will probably feeling like reading the Voynich manuscript once in a while.

About my personnal story : years ago, I get more fat. Looking for a solution, I found the book of Tim Ferriss, 4 hour body, which was quite efficient, I get slim for the first time of my life, and made the first fooststep in the biohack (remember the first time I read about increase vitamin D levels, I found that crazy at the time, aha).

In 2014-2015 I get depressed and gain fat. I develop some hypophagia, which finaly make me up in the night. What an awfull period. With a lot of research I finaly put the word "leptin" on my trouble. But the scientifics papers, with reductionnist approach instead of systemic protocol don't allow me to find a solution. I was quite desperate until I found the Leptin RX.

And all was solve quite fast, in three weeks. Thanks again doc !

I get back on my usual diet, the one popularized by Tim Ferriss (which the same as the leptin RX btw, but now with a big breakfast, and a cheat day). I've continue to loose weight until I reach a stand, and the cheat day began to make me sick.

So my plan si to do a proper leptin RX, some mounths, until the inflammation problem is gone, the body/gut fully restore, and get back to the usual diet, more convinient and I think more efficient if the T4 -> T3 conversion is operanting (when the cortisol/inflammation will be low enought).

I want also to improve my sleep (post incoming).

Hmm. Here some supplement I take :

\- my best friend PAGG (Polyconasol, Alpha-Lipoic Acid, Green Tea Extract - to be more acurate here, just the polyphenols and the ECCG - Garlic) what I've found on my past data to be really efficient to enhance weight loss.

\- a curcumin + piperine pills : to specificaly tackle the inflammation, as describe in the blog post " WHAT SHOULD HCG/PALEO USERS CONSIDER AS ADJUNCTS?" on the blog. More interesting fact, I also use it to enfance the effect of the ECCG (the piperine also blocks the execretion by the liver of the ECCG).

\- in more than the faty fish every lunch at midday, I also use liberal amount of salmon oil on my lentils or bean (miam), to enfance "Omega 3' composition. I'm not realy sure about the bioavability of the pure oil add to the food, but in the article _Dietary polyunsaturated fatty acids and inflammatory mediator production 1,2 Michael J James, Robert A Gibson, and Leslie G Cleland_ is that what they use, so I can at least presume that it works for the membrane composition of the monocytes. Btw it's a Salmon Oil for dog aha, not a pills, far less expensive and incredibly more convinient to use x)

\- A daily Brazil nut. (but can we be realy sure about the amount of selenium in it ?)

Some fact on my metabolism :

\- If I drink coffee in the afternoon I wake up earler the next day. I suspect some mess with hypercotisolism of overweight people. I drink some coffe in the morning.

\- If I take vitamin C (in the form of the classic complement sell everywhere, ascorbate, 500 mg), I wake up early the next morning too. I've no idea why. Vitamin C can interact with cortisol, but not the resting cortisol if I believe examine.com.

Truly I'm very curious about this. Especialy since the lack of Vitamin C is one of the factor correlate with shorter sleep. This the only nutriment which could possibility lack in my alimentation, so if there is any clue on which form I could use. And I realy wonder why vit C have this effect.

I've take Vitamin C this morning on a whim, to test it (last time was several mounth ago). I'll see if it works.

\- Magnesium threanate destroy my sleep. And my stomach.

Edit : I forgot about Vitamin D. My statut is _a priori_ good. I've haven't test it since few months, but it's normaly okay. Btw, I can't take small amount of vitamin D in daily routine because ... well guess what. It make me wake up earlier :v. So I have take "huge amount", like 70 000 UI or 30 000 UI when I've a to wake up early the tomorrow like to catch a train.

Edit 2 : I've also use PQQ, but since I go swim often in cold water so I drop it. Btw my weightloss seems to be starting again fiercly since I've drop it, so I even begin to get suspicious about it. (even if the chances that's it block weightloss where quite low since the huge amount of people using it and checking for their body parameters).

Oh, btw, I'm approximatively around 25 % of body fat, I'm not so fat anymore (but I am). This time I rather use indirect method to judge my weight like shape or clothing. I've noticed yesterday that I wear my bathing suit more loosely than before, so I'm quite happy on this topic.

Next post on my sleep (and the correlation with Eggs (true variable involved : choline)).

It's not realy true, but I consider this day to be the third of my new leptin RX.

Thanks for reading

### Cdk29 — 2016-09-01T13:57:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-french-guy.18356/post-200637>

Thanks !
**
The Eggs problem**

Few weeks ago, I try to supplement myself with choline, as I've done in the first Leptin RX. It's a supplement efficient for weight loss, popular in 80's, and support « fat burning » (the reason why I've used it the first time, with little understanding of the leptin RX, to pass to sugar burner to fat burner).

It make me sleep less. So I drop it.

Soon after that I was drinking coffee while looking a broadcoast of one of my favorite youtuber and think « hey, eggs have choline them into it, that's why I eat them, can the amount could be enought to influence my sleep? ».

Nonetheless it was possible, it would also perfectly match the distribution of my sleep during the year : I'm studying in Paris, use temporary location to do so, and I've noticed that I sleep diffently in different flat.

And I do not cook the same in all of them.

Look on the internet. Seems consistent.

I sacrifice few days to test it :

I add two eggs at diner. Sleep 6 hours instead of 6 h 30 - 7 h. Experimental confirmation.

I lowered the eggs at breakfast. 6 (big eggs, 75 g in average) , the usual amount is okay. 5 begin to be not enought. I remember didn't use eggs this year at breakfast, so I guess the absolut need for eggs is temporary. (meaning the more the leptin RX will go on, the less I will need egg - even If don't want drop eggs, I quite enjoy them.)

Currently the minimum I need at breakfast is 6 Eggs, enought to kill hunger, and made me sleep a little more than 7 Eggs. I will run on 7 eggs few days to be absolutly sure to kill hunger and get back on 6 after. I think people here can understand the fear of a metabolism which goes off if the breakfast wasn't big enought.

Etienne.

### Lahelada — 2016-09-01T14:23:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-french-guy.18356/post-200639>

Understanding the fear of hunger but it will reduce .
Welcome !

### Cdk29 — 2016-09-01T22:09:04+0300

### caroline — 2016-09-03T09:31:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-french-guy.18356/post-200767>

I would croak if I ate that many eggs! ....but never connected it to sleep - need to think about that.

### Cdk29 — 2016-09-04T10:30:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-french-guy.18356/post-200839>

Actually I'm not entirely sure that is the pure amount of choline. The peak of choline is 12 hours in the blood, so it could possibily be "eating egg and choline" later than the breakfast which influence (my) sleep. Somehow like coffee.

Btw I'm sleeping again nicely ! As I was saying in my introduce post, by doing swimming every morning I get my circadian rhythm pass to 00 - 7 h to 22 h 30 5 h 30, which is kind of unconviniant.

However, one day I've go to bed at midnight, wake up at 5 h 15 but succeed to fall asleep again. Next day I was easily stay awake until 23 h 45, and take some sublingual melatonin (I've phone all the pharmacy to found it), advisable on a american website speaking of specific trouble of sleep and the correct use of specific melatonin for all of them.

Work perfectly !
Now I will swim not often in cold water, and in the afternoon aha

Since my sleep is nice now, I rather consider the beginning of the leptin RX until this date, so today is day 2 !

Have a nice week end.

"**The current status for the public, is that modern healthcare, does not appreciate how altered melatonin cycles uncouples ubiquination from the cell cycle to cause disease.** Taking exogenous melatonin, in this case, a go to maneuver by many functional medical professionals, will worsen the situation because of the optical physics involved. I have discussed this above. Any time you take some exogenously, that is designed to be part of a coupled system, ruins the built in feedback control. ### Cdk29 — 2016-09-17T11:09:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-french-guy.18356/post-201541>

**Sleep improvement**

Hi ! Two weeks completed  As you know, I've test several complement to improve my sleep.

Magnesium Threonate, Magnesium bisglycinate, made my stomach upset and shorter my sleep.
Vitamin C, Vit D, shorter also my sleep.

I've try a random bisglycinate zinc complement, with no effect.

I've test two days a form of L-Monomethionine Zinc, witch Copper ... success oO. I haven't really espected something on it, but my sleep passed to seven hour (or more like 6 h 30) with some variability to 8 hours more or less  happy. With two caps of 30 mg.

So the question is why. Some speculations :

\- More bioavalaibility ? Dunno but I don't think so, Zinc Methionine and bisglycinate has the same bioavability, but I should check again the percentages of assimilation.
\- Better form, or juste the effect of methionine ?
\- The copper in it ? I should look of the balance between different mineral. For example, if copper help me sleep, perhaps the Magnesium disrupt my sleep by messing with the copper in food.
\- Something actively deplete my zinc. Sportive guy speak of a deficiency in Zinc when they go to often a the gym to do some musculation, but it's not my case. Some speak of some link with the alcohol (to much alcohol, or that the Zinc allow them to be fresh after a night of drinking), but I dont' drink and it's not a deficiency correlate with alcohol consumption. Perhaps with leaky gut ?

Today or tomorrow I will try one caps at diner, to see the differences.

I'm a little more "calm" today, not sleepy but something like that. So perhaps two caps make me groggy. (I said that juste because I drinking more coffee than usual, but perhaps it's an effect of more sleep/coffee beverage less strong in cafein, it wasn't the case yesterday).

I've success to put myself in a size 44 jeans. Next step : closing the zipper aha

Edit : I've found that be overweight people tend to have lowered Zinc, and problem in assimilation, probably due to some sequestration by adipocytes. And zinc tend to increase circulating leptin. So I guess there is something around that. Btw, zinc and green tea extract seems to collaborate for assimilation, so, combo ! x)

### Cdk29 — 2016-12-27T11:41:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-french-guy.18356/post-205819>

Hi !

It have been a long time.

So. I'm back on full lepin RX, with no cheat day. I'm still somehow leptin resistant, and get trouble with my diet and sleep with all my journeys to Paris from Brest.
But now I think I've get back too early on the Tim Ferriss/slow carb diet.

With thinking, a cheat day is not optimal for now.

So, I'm back on letpin RX since 1 week and fews days. I think I will do it for a long period, even when it would be no necessary, by precaution ^^

Btw, I've notice some interresting fact, not really in accordance with the original protocol of Jack Kruse :

\- Breakfast : amount of fat is the key. Not only protein, which is important but no more after a threshold is reach (50 gramme or so for me). But on a the other hand, fat is freaking important. So butter in my omelette. And for travel, when it is "hard-boiled egg", Pancetta with it

\- Lunch : fat is important. Fiber too. Glucide (beans ...) is necessary for a good night sleep. Cooking/microwave them is freaking important too. A recent discovery, I'm actualy guessing that it's just a matter of glycemic index : cooking them give more carbohydrate avalaible. (glycemic index) (IG).

\- Dinner. Nothing to mention. Just get enought carbohydrate.

Every complement exept PAGG (I don't take it for now anyway) was a disaster.

Curcumin cause to me sleeplessness. Actually, supplement of fish oil do the same. Bot with days of delays (ten day for curcumin).

Perhaps two different mecanism involved. But I begin to believe that you can't alleviate inflammation in overweight people without consequences. I'm guessing that it's the "normal state", a steady state that the body can handle of his new condition.

So I'm sleeping well exept the last few days, cause of the fish oil, I think.

So the plan is to stay on the leptin RX (with a little more carb than the original Jack Kruse's protocol but it's work well for me in normal situation). Can't wait for my body to ride of the fish oil. Doc, I'm realy curious about this matter btw.

I will move in Rostock (Germany) for 6 months in a week for my bioinformatics internship. A nice cold place  Sea is too cold for now for me, but in few months time, and with less fat, I will could swim in it  ( my thoughts is : too much fat = > too much BAT => to much melatonin). Feeling sleepy at the time the sun diseapear isn't such a good idea, especially when the sun diseapper at 16 h.

So It's 1,5 weeks of the reboot. I know that 3 weeks is enought for me. I will do the reboot at least until the end of february, or March. I will update this journal by the end of January I think.

But any comment are welcome

Today's factoid for mitohacker's: CT at night is different than CT during the day............
**The sun’s light is a cathode ray. When it hits the Earth in daytime, the Earth acts as an anode in this cosmologic circuit and this allows for more evaporation of water on the planet's surface to create more protons to dissipate in the atmosphere. Simultaneously the evaporaation effect will liberate more electrons from Earth that our tensegrity system connected to the Earth via our limbs is designed to collect. We collect and harvest this energy to store or use to do physiologic work to lower the resistance of our inner mitochondrial membrane. This reduces the electrical resistance of mitochondria and this helps stimulate autophagy and not apoptosis. During daytime the electric field of Earth is higher than it is at night when light is absent. Magnetic fields, however, are higher at night, and this is likely why sleep is linked to a loss of the DC electric current in diurnal animals.**

> Today's factoid for mitohacker's: CT at night is different than CT during the day............
> **The sun’s light is a cathode ray. When it hits the Earth in daytime, the Earth acts as an anode in this cosmologic circuit and this allows for more evaporation of water on the planet's surface to create more protons to dissipate in the atmosphere. Simultaneously the evaporaation effect will liberate more electrons from Earth that our tensegrity system connected to the Earth via our limbs is designed to collect. We collect and harvest this energy to store or use to do physiologic work to lower the resistance of our inner mitochondrial membrane. This reduces the electrical resistance of mitochondria and this helps stimulate autophagy and not apoptosis. During daytime the electric field of Earth is higher than it is at night when light is absent. Magnetic fields, however, are higher at night, and this is likely why sleep is linked to a loss of the DC electric current in diurnal animals.**

So many ways to do CT.
CT can be done:
Naked, in cold air, day or night, sunny day, cloudy day, morning, noon, after sundown
Naked, in cold water etc etc
water can be a ocean, lake, bathtub,
bathtub can conduct electricity or not
\---
For scientist it is cornucopia of variables
for pedestrian just do it, best you can
most of the time it is a plus
sometime it is not, rarely
Snp, ... that may or may not be another variable
....

Anyway I want to ease the "refalling asleep" part. So I put this from examine.com here :

**Non-sedative sleep aids**

If you have problems with sleep quality, or staying asleep, but have no issues actually falling asleep, can try supplemental [glycine](http://examine.com/supplements/glycine/) or [lavender](http://examine.com/supplements/lavender/).

-   Glycine is an amino acid. About three grams of glycine before bed can improve sleep quality with no sedative side effects.

-   Lavender can also improve sleep through aromatherapy. Dabbing lavender oil on your pillow (men can apply directly to mustache) or near your bed can improve sleep, while taking 80-160mg of the oil orally can reduce anxiety, which can indirectly improve sleep. Prolonged skin exposure to lavender may result in irritation, so don’t put too much lavender oil on your pillow.

Hello !

So everything goes fine. I'm loosing my weight slowly. I've new glasses and some lens to clip on to work on computer (which block blue light regard to the day).

My appetite is reducing a little, even if I still get have trouble if I miss breakfast. PAGG doesn't work well for me btw aha.

I was thinking of this :

> Circadian cycles for the obese are dramatically altered compared to non-obese individuals in the morning. In the normal person, Ghrelin is high when cortisol is highest in the early morning. In them, ghrelin drops fast when food is eaten too. In the obese, ghrelin is much lower in the morning than expected. Moreover, when food is eaten, ghrelin stays elevated for an extended amount of time. This happens because of the inflammation associated with the higher leptin levels in the morning in the obese. Melatonin is known to acutely decrease ghrelin and sometimes in tough cases I will use supplemental [melatonin](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000QISSD2/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B000QISSD2&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20) to demolish the morning ghrelin spikes in people with huge appetites. This is most common in the obese, eating disorders, and in those with severe leaky gut who crave dairy and carbohydrates. It is also very common in young paleo enthusiasts because of how they embrace blue light technology gadgets of the modern world that destroy melatonin levels in the brain. Ghrelin spikes and stimulates NPY in the hypothalamus increasing our desire and ability to eat a lot more. Leptin makes NPY decline normally, but if one is leptin resistant this does not occur and appetite is out of control at the brain level.

I have a huge appetite. I've just spend a bad night. I've try melatonin in the morning today to see (2mg). My belly sensation after the bad night disapeared immediatly.

I was thinking of taking a little melatonin in the morning to kill the ghrelin spike as long as I'm still overweight.

Opinion on that matter ? Or something more safe to kill the ghrelin ? I'm not enthousiast for the melatonin, it was just a try for today.

I'm really curious on that matter because i'm feel really nice sudenly.

Otherwise, I sleep nice now, since I take 5 htp before bed time.

### Cdk29 — 2017-05-07T11:51:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-french-guy.18356/post-213582>

Some thoughts for the eyes.

I'm still kind of concern about my eyes, but for the long term run. I've read about the effect of sunlight to cure the myopa on some success story, here. Could be interresting. But to do that, you are supposed to not wear your prescription glass everytime. But on the other hand, the opthalmologist told me to wear them more to rest the eyes.

But there is no really matter to overthink about it. Glass in front of computer is the best I think, mostly because my amber filter to rest the eyes have to be on correction glasses. Any alternative doesn't work as much as it. And the best way to alleviate the bad effect of screen to eyes is to wear this amber lens (or use stuff like Iris as I currently do).

I have to force my self a little to wear the amber lens sometimes. It's still kind of weird I admit. ~It's rest the eyes, but the iris app do it too. I'm kind of worry to filter too much the sun light too. I could go for a mixed solution : Iris when it's sunny to enjoy the sun, the amber clip on when it is not.~

Working on computer on my desk tired my eyes. But it was just about sun reflection on the screen . So now if I want to enjoy light I can just work on my bed. Or close the "hide sun stuff" which provide a good orange light in the room. Anyway, I've order a anti reflective and mat screen protector, that is not expensive. And will be always usefull.

Still there is the outdoor deplacement. I don't know. I'm not sure that wearing glasses all the time is a good solution either. I will check with a eye doctor in France, and read the jack's article (if someone have the starting article I would love to read it).

So an eye healthy protocol for the long run will be : glasses in front of computer, ~with iris~ or amber clip on) and no glasses outdoor or when it's not need. + looking at sun at dawn and dusk. I've also read about books on that matter. I take any good references.

Edit : I did the try and my lens do the best for alleviate all stress to eyes. It's a little strange to have this clip on, but you adapt, but you have to do what is necessary. And it's kind of fun.
I have compilate some articles about how to act on computer (frequence of breaks, exercices to do).

I remember as a kid having a class of musculation, which was moslty in order to learn ourself how to carry weight without destroying or back or else. We should have the same for computer. Mostly in academia when you go to this area.

How, and I have also ordered a wire keyboard to put my screen of my portable at a best distance (and having a better sit position).

### Cdk29 — 2017-05-28T13:16:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-french-guy.18356/post-214913>

Update !

So after some trial and error, I've discover that the PQQ and vitamin B-50 are mandatory in the morning to sleep properly. I don't know why, but it is. I don't think you can really assess the truth behind this whatever, I just stick to empirism. One interresting review let me think that it could be something bound to choline metabolism. But maybe not.

5 htp help make the result reliable. And diminush the appetite.

So I run on : 20-30 mg PQQ, 1 x vitamin B-50 in at breakfast.
400 mg of 5htp before going to bed.

I began to be used to my new life. I've put an anti reflective screen on my iphone, for the few times I us it. I have ordered a special blue light filter. To put on.

I have troubleshoot my desktop environnement. My clip on are really effective, but the space between my amber clip on, put on the glasses, and the eyes, make that I have to take some precautions : any direct light from a lateral angle is deadly in this configuration. I will make some gunnar glasses with correction, so I will not have to bother to much on my workstation in the future.

I don't do smartphone in the evening, nor in dinner anymore, but I use it to hear podcast or else. I have bought the audiobook A Dance With Dragon to have something to listening to in the evening . I get a PhD offer in Australia, so that way i will prepare myself for the some english test  (the orange screen if for this few moment when I need to use my smartphone).

I am looking for book on computer vision syndrom. After I will look for some on myopa or else.

In few words : I like to go lie down on my bed, listening to stuff. Sometimes I fall asleep without noticing it. But now that I am sleeping 7 hour again, that is not a problem. Work on computer is okay. Internship extended accordingly. Don't have to look for a PhD. Peacefull life.

### Cdk29 — 2017-05-28T18:54:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-french-guy.18356/post-214935>

Double update

So I did find a blog/youtube channel on the matter of reversing myopia, by taping "biohacking myopia" on google. The stuff is complexe, but it is meant to be so, each people dealing with is one etiology.
(endmyopia.org) for those curious.

I will also add a sub routine, something simple, that I can stick on it. Looking on all the success story, looking at the sun seems at sunrise and sunfall seems to be efficient and a constant feature. For various reasons I supposed, for some exposed on Jack's blog but I do think that it is also working as an workout for some muscles, and targeting the primary cause of myopia, nearsightedness.

# Thread: If One (1) hour a day keeps the doctor away...what is your choice?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-one-1-hour-a-day-keeps-the-doctor-away-what-is-your-choice.18360/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 10
