### Joeakim — 2016-09-01T23:44:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-one-1-hour-a-day-keeps-the-doctor-away-what-is-your-choice.18360/post-200673>

I have asked this same question to a friend recently: "If you had just one hour a day, what would you do: sunlight exposure or CT?

He said, he would sit in the CT tub while getting the sunlight.

Mind you when I asked him that question, I was thinking of a busy 9 - 5 working folks and my family especially my son who works and lives in downtown Chicago.

So, I thought the answer would be getting sun for an hour during lunch and do CT later in the day or early early. Furthermore, for the working folks the Fournier Affect may not be applicable.

And of course mitigating nnEMF and artificial light as much as possible is given in the formula.

### Scompy — 2016-09-02T00:42:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-one-1-hour-a-day-keeps-the-doctor-away-what-is-your-choice.18360/post-200679>

This

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/)

### caroline — 2016-09-02T06:57:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-one-1-hour-a-day-keeps-the-doctor-away-what-is-your-choice.18360/post-200694>

For me ..... A deep and loving relationship gives me wings - and that is 24/7 if you are very, very lucky.

And hugging ...... After I left Dr. K. In Orlando I ran into this extraordinary young man - a bell hop at the hotel.

He gave me the biggest and bestest bear hug I have ever had. I asked him about it and he told me his whole family were huggers. I asked about his girlfriend - if she was a hugger? And he said "yes". "I would never be with anyone that wasn't a hugger - that didn't love hugging"

That told me so much about this gorgeous young man and how he handled himself.

### Lahelada — 2016-09-02T13:26:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-one-1-hour-a-day-keeps-the-doctor-away-what-is-your-choice.18360/post-200700>

I have been wondering recently if your haplotype and percentage of heteroplasmy dictate the ratio of sun , grounding etc. that you need. I think my answer for now is that it would vary throughout the year and how my journey goes.

Example in spring I would give priority to sunbathing to get sun callus going for summer.

Summer I would have the full hour of sun exposure, autumn a gradual shift to more grounding again winter grounding as in most places you have insufficient quantum yield. Grounding can then easily be combined with CT as you can undress even in the city in your lunch hour and throw yourself into the local park. It is the old hibernation period and so seems apropos for more grounding to reduce inflammation acquired during summer.

Never leave the sun out of the equation though. However, starting out follow the order of the six healers _as written_ .

As I said my musings and I am a Nordic type so the answers maybe different for someone with a different haplotype.

# Thread: IF Sleep is so very important, why don't we have a Sleep Rx slot?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-sleep-is-so-very-important-why-dont-we-have-a-sleep-rx-slot.18361/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Joeakim — 2016-09-01T23:56:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-sleep-is-so-very-important-why-dont-we-have-a-sleep-rx-slot.18361/post-200675>

I have some questions regards to sleep and sleep patterns, so I it would be good idea if we had Sleep Rx thread. We might have one, just not sure.

Question: With added sun light exposure from sunrise gazing to noon to burn (30 min), my sleep period has been shortened with bit of concern.

Typically I get to bed/sleep by 1 AM, and prior to practicing Sphinx first thing in the morning, I usually got up 8 AM'ish. Then it changed to 7:15 - 7:30 AM'ish. With the consistent sunrise gazing and more sunlight exposure during the day, I have been getting up 6 AM'ish. So I am getting 5 hours of sleep. When looked up National Sleep Foundation, an adult needs 7 - 9 hours of sleep. I am not biohacking sleep to see how little I need to function but rather I know I want enough sleep to maximize optimum health. Sometimes I get 9 hours of sleep like yesterday: it was simple delicious and felt awesome.

Any and all feedback information appreciated.

[https://sleepfoundation.org/how-sleep-works/how-much-sleep-do-we-really-need](https://sleepfoundation.org/how-sleep-works/how-much-sleep-do-we-really-need)

### Joeakim — 2016-09-01T23:58:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-sleep-is-so-very-important-why-dont-we-have-a-sleep-rx-slot.18361/post-200676>

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) said to read Quantum sleep which I did, and I have more questions like "why don't we just have a magnetic pillow to induce and assist us with better sleep instead of using the whole magnetic bed cover?

### Sue-UK — 2016-09-02T12:45:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-sleep-is-so-very-important-why-dont-we-have-a-sleep-rx-slot.18361/post-200699>

1 AM would be too late for me. I seem to remember reading that 12-3am is how humans have condensed the hibernation period, and because of when cortisol starts to rise I try to get the max from that window, so it's rare I stay up after 11 (and if I do I don't feel as rested). 10 to 10.30 pm is more usual.

### Joeakim — 2016-09-02T13:39:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-sleep-is-so-very-important-why-dont-we-have-a-sleep-rx-slot.18361/post-200701>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=200699)
>
> 1 AM would be too late for me. I seem to remember reading that 12-3am is how humans have condensed the hibernation period, and because of when cortisol starts to rise I try to get the max from that window, so it's rare I stay up after 11 (and if I do I don't feel as rested). 10 to 10.30 pm is more usual.

1 AM is late, I know. But currently I work 4 to midnight shift and in the process of switching to 8 AM to 4 PM. When I am off work, I am getting 7 to 8 hours of sleep, once 9 + hours.

Strange thing is that there is a pattern now: waking up 6 AM'ish regardless of what time I go to sleep. Last night I slept from 10:30PM to 6:15 AM.

I know I am not getting proper autophagy when I sleep from 1AM to 6AM'ish but now with the changes of getting more sun and water, I can not sleep past 6:30AM. It's strange. I am not tired throughout the day except around 10:30PM I start to yawn, mind you I wear UVEX around 8PM or shortly after sunset when in indoors. I tried to nap after getting the afternoon sunlight session but can't sleep, I'll just lay there. I believe JK said we need at least 7 hours of sleep.

### Sue-UK — 2016-09-02T16:01:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-sleep-is-so-very-important-why-dont-we-have-a-sleep-rx-slot.18361/post-200712>

I was waking up at 5.30 to 6 in the longer light cycle, its a bit later now, 7 is the latest. Have you read CT7? Perhaps what is happening is your circadian rhythm for cortisol and BP is improving.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-02T17:30:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-sleep-is-so-very-important-why-dont-we-have-a-sleep-rx-slot.18361/post-200717>

You've been told in many blogs.......read them. What did Ubi 24 say? The entire time series? Light and the eye.........is all tied to regeneration all regeneration is tied to sleep efficiency.

Photosynthesis is all about light and how light organizes our tissues from embryo to adult form. Nocturnal mammals and daytime animals have eyes that are different. Since you all now know that the eyes control all growth and metabolism do you realize what it means for modern nutrional and bio-chemical data? Why did Ruben and I have to build the Quantlet to modern humans? It was done to augment the human battery that uses photosynthetic pathways. Today the lighting in virtually all research laboratories is still the responsibility of the janitor and is classified as ordinary building maintenance instead of being part of experimental design. Humans are diurnal and nocturnal and this means their skin and eyes are radically different in how they interact with light and induce sleep. This is why the research in nutrition, sleep, and biochemistry is worthless, in my opinion. When you add in the fact that they usually study mice and rats who are nocturnal they completely miss all the good data because humans have photopic eyes and skin. Why? Your human photopic eyes mean your biology is not designed around scotopic eating of food or light. Simple quantum rule says Mother Nature's mitochondrial design. Mice and rats retina’s are scotopic FYI. [https://www.chronobiology.com/study-finds-vision-light-key-players-sleep-wake-cycles/](https://www.chronobiology.com/study-finds-vision-light-key-players-sleep-wake-cycles/)

# Thread: TIME 21 and LASERS
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-21-and-lasers.18362/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-02T05:14:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/time-21-and-lasers.18362/post-200685>

Sound familiar ? All living organisms are known to emit ultra weak light throughout the UV, visible and NIR range. Mitochondria are the sites of chemiluminescence (also known as biophoton emission). Chemiluminescence is caused by redox reactions and this is how mitochondrion signal each other.
When incident light hits a filamentous mitochondrion (these form networks in the cell) this light is amplified throughout the mitochondrion through induced emission of photons and this can propagate along the network to other mitochondria. Essentially, light traveling through mitochondria acts as a fiber optic waveguide. In a respiring state, mitochondria can act like a laser.
Theoretical models show that respiring mitochondria reflect light most in the 270 to 290 nm range (UV) while transmitting light mostly in the 300 to 800 nm range (visible light) in respiring and resting mode.
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15302557](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/15302557)
full text: [https://www.dropbox.com/…/PropagationOfEMFInMitochondria.pd…](https://www.dropbox.com/…/PropagationOfEMFInMitochondria.pd…)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### Juha — 2016-09-09T15:49:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/protection-from-artificial-light.18366/post-201187>

> 1) what lights protection sunglass's work best and why?

I've been wearing Uvex Blue Light Blocking safety glasses a few hours before going to bed. They are very affordable and in my experience get the job done, as it comes to blocking blue light.

If this is what you meant by lights protecting sunglass's...

Simultaneously, we obsess over all the constants society crystalizes in the ceremonies and traditions in life, and we pay way too much attention to them.
The trick to rediscovering the waves and small motions around us is to focus on the greater panorama of waves moving around us. While everything flickers and vibrates at the smallest scales, the important things remain in place to measure these small effects. Their stillness is an optical delusion of our perception because our retina is not capable of seeing these perpetual motion machines at work. And because we don't see these motions, things appear as reverse motion to our perspective. In this way, relativity seems to have the ability to resets our motion sensors. Relativity of time exists because light has a constant speed and this speed we cannot judge properly. Because of this queer circumstance, light reboots the perpetual motion machines by resetting time in the dark. This cycle begins to allow us once again to perceive things in a world filled with light. When we begin to observe more carefully, we suddenly we realize that those "still things" are not so motionless after all. They are simply gliding on the smallest waves with a slow individualistic grace against the backdrop of the immense universe that vibrates quantum jazz.
For example, an atom of hydrogen, whether in Sirius, Arcturus, or a mitochondria executes its vibrations in precisely the same way each time we measure it. Each atom, therefore, throughout the universe, bears impressed on it the stamp of a cosmic metric system as distinctly as does the meter of the Archives at Paris or the double royal cubit of the Temple of Karnac. We know them only from their spectral fingerprint. I have a sense we will soon do the same for mitochondria. We will discover the secrets of life by understanding the spectral density of the emission of mitochondria in ways that will astound us.
This set of circumstance requires a cell to contain many sensitive motion instruments to track the cosmic quakes of light in a lifetime. Nature built you to be able to shift your perception to a specific frequency to tap an optimal tune in your perpetual motion machines called mitochondria in every cell. Each one tells its own version of time and from this multitide of clocks manifests biologic time with its arrow of direction. The sun's rays are cappable of giving us multiple perceptions of vision and these multitudes give us the ability to perceive and observe some relative differences of space and of time in our present moment. This situation makes time a delusion of our reality. Some of these waves are for the eye camera, others for the eye clock, and yet others for illumination that vibrate every stick of collagen in us. The last vibrations are like the skilled hand of the guitarist playing an addicting riff for cells. Cells are the audience in this cosmic concert. Cells are a veritable playground for light. We have no idea why we are addicted to this cosmic tune, but we constantly sense it, below our perceptual abilities and it only manifests via our instincts. Nothing goes unnoticed or unused throughout the cosmos by Mother Nature. Wherever there is a vibration, she builds receptors and antenna's for these vibrations. How she deciphers the tune is unique and used in some rather bizarre ways. From this recipe time emerges light is captured and its power and infromation is harnessed stored and used when needed. Some cosmic energies don't need ears or vision to see, hear, or sense; these sensors can feel what is in your heart and tap into all your sins and fears.
Life really is a random casino game. There is a dealer, a player, and the probabilties of quantum mechanics at play. It is an uncertain game as Heisenberg told us in the 1920's. It is hard to accept randomness, when life and reality seem to be precise to our senses. You should listen to this story of life. It is remarkable and extraordinary. Mitochondria are the most queer metronomes nature could give us.
The universe deals "the cards" to a cell as a meditative oscillation in an isolation tank. And all those mitochondria sit within these isolation tanks and they have to play those cards in some way. This manner of execution is beyond the minds ability to perceive. Mitochondria and life are not playing for riches or respect they are playing lto create time. And that time is only present time. It is not past time or future time. And for life to manifest time has to manifest from the cards nature deals to it. Time is a reflection of these vibrations. Nature is the casino dealer and only passes out the cards so a mitochondria can find an answer to gain time from the slowing of light. The respiratory proteins hold the "sacred geometry of chance" in this musical charade. Within these proteins is hidden the law of a probable outcome for the living. Only the correct numbers can lead to a dance where mitochondria and light create time for life to manifest.
The electromagnetic forces in nature play instruments in cells. That instrument is a mitochondria that is set in perpetual motion until death, because motion stops inside the mitochondria. ### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-05T19:36:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-mitochondria-perpetual-motion-machines.18371/post-200921>

Do mitochondria work in the present moment, but as the metronome is forced to move at the end of the oscillation/vibration cycle, at the point of complete stillness, does time stop and potential futures become briefly observable? Can the future be predicted this way? My instinct tells me no from my understanding of physics.

Do I think the dealer can sense the hand coming? Yes.......I believe our ability to "thin slice" is a function of mitochondrial energy flows. This flow however is linked to the tensegrity system and not the nervous system directly. It is indirectly linked to the CNS and I think this is what a deja vu really is. Would heteroplasmy lower the mitochondrial colony in our cells to act coherently? Yes I think this is very true. Why? **One of the main advantages of an inner clockwork mechanism is that of anticipation to predictable changes, thus permitting the organism to be prepared to respond to future challenges in the optimal way.**

A cell can have some mitochondria that have a mutation in the mtDNA and some that do not. This is termed heteroplasmy. The proportion of mutant mtDNA molecules determines both the penetrance and severity of expression of some diseases. Homoplasmy refers to a cell that has a uniform collection of mtDNA: either completely normal mtDNA or completely mutant mtDNA. This sameness is key to a timing mechanism. Why? All mitochondria will sense environmental change on a ratline basis within a tissue based upon their positions in the body. A unique feature of mtDNA is that, at cell division, the mtDNA replicates and sorts randomly among mitochondria. This is called stochasitic inheritance. Mitochondria keep their ability to reproduce by lateral gene transfers too. This is another mode of replication that I believe is suppressed by the mitochondrial genes that are now housed in the nuclear genome. Recall that mtDNA have 37 genes. 13 are in the mitochondria and 24 are in the nuclear genome. I believe when heteroplasmy rates become quite high........the mitochondria is still able to harness the information buried in those 24 genes to try to survive. I believe this is the mechanism of how a cell become metastatic. Once a mitochondria is fat tally wounded by a chronic Warburg redox shift and it just cannot meet a tissues energy need.........it goes back in evolutionary time to recapture its own ancestry. This gives a cell the ability to move again and undergo lateral/horizontal gene transfers. I believe these lateral and horizontal moves are what cancer researchers see when they just look at the chromosomal changes in cancer lines. I don't believe they understand they manifest because of the chronic energy drain in the mitochondria. The other link in cancers is the sudden change that occurs in the collagen tensegrity system. Why?

Here is why losing your EZ in cancer state are a huge quantum link. Remember light meets water and forms huge polymers of EZ water adjacent to collagen. When a wave of energy in the form of energy in the form of an electric, magnetic, or an electromagnetic pulse along any protein, it will cause adjacent water molecules to vibrate or spin about their axis. The vibrations and spins of the water molecules at a distance far away from the protein will be less influenced by the wave when the EZ is not large. It will be much more influenced when the EZ is large. The key importance in this geometry is that the cell system is designed to to have interfaces between highly ordered structures around collagen and less organized states in places like the extracellular fluid where the vibrations and spins are less correlated. These two dissimilar spaces create a steep electrical gradient across them. Life appears to be only possible on the interface of order and disorder between differently ordered domains. The same relationship exists between the local zip code around all the different cytochromes and the ATPase in the mitochondria as well. This is the fractal design in side a cell. These ideas have been modeled by computers using math. Cancer cells have a higher order in the tensegrity system. This lowers the electrical gradient. The cytoskeleton microenvironment contains many thick filaments instead of the distributed fibrillar network found in healthy cells with a brisk DC electric current. This loss of electrical gradient is what a metastatic mitochondria is looking to move away from. It also shows you why they know to visit tissues where there is a larger electrical gradient between the tensegrity system and cytoplasm of the cell. I believe cancer cells are reacting to this "present time stress signal" to make dense fibrils to protect itself from a chronic bombardment of electromagnetic fields that are penetrating into the cell. The EZ normally is the best natural Faraday cage, but in cancer states that defense is compromised. These fibrils provide a stressed mitochondria so degree of protection from environmental damage. This is why micronuclei are often found in RF-microwave blood samples in people who live in toxic environments. It is also why intracellular dehydration and a low DC electric current are cousin symptoms or high % heteroplasmy. I think metastasis is a disease of a broken wireless communication system in a cell due to electronic damage. Healthy cells are all built around coherent vibrations and I think mitochondria oscillations work in similar fashion. This is who a mitochondria oscillation entrains an entire tissue and how an entire tissue gets its OWN energy footprint bar code. I also think this is why certain cancers migrate to certain distant tissues. My bet is those tissues have a similar vibration of the badly damaged tissue. A damaged mitochondria is looking to be re-entrained by the vibrations it is most familiar with. These mathematical models imply that living tissues act in kind like a superconductor would from a phenomenalistic perspective.

If we consider that Pollack's experiments have shown us that light alone is capable of drawing particles suspended in water together we might begin to see how a lack of light stored in the EZ would disorder things like mitochondria which are suspended in a sea of EZ (UBI5) this is why sunlight is the major pacesetter for the SCN.

Mammalian circadian rhythms are controlled by endogenous biological oscillators like the suprachiasmatic nuclei (SCN). Since the period of this oscillation is of ∼24 h, to keep synchrony with the environment, circadian rhythms need to be entrained daily by means of Zeitgeber (“time giver”) signals, such as the light-dark cycle. This means light is the metronome of all living things. A lack of light during the day or too much light at night ruining the timing mechanism. So how does light improve time synchronization in the SCN? I think it begins by making all mitochondria dance to the same vibrations and spins. This is why Time 20 is a big deal. In the chronobiology literature there are now several aspects of the mechanisms for photic entrainment of mammalian circadian rhythms published. These including retinal sensitivity to light by means of novel photopigments like melanopsin, neuropsin and the Muller cells as well as circadian variations within the retina compared to peripheral oscillators that contribute to the regulation of retinal physiology. The retina's physiology than have major effects on the tensegrity system by way of the Muller cells and over peripheral oscillators by way of the mitochondria. Downstream from the retina, I have shared with you already how the central retinohypothalamic tract controls the flow of optical communication through melanopsin which affects the neurotransmitters that are triggered by photonics events on an din cell membranes to activate or deactivate signaling. This is why glutamate, aspartate, pituitary adenylate cyclase-activating polypeptide are important. I spoke about glutamate in the CPC#13 blog to give you a clue. These NT's interact optically with SCN receptors and the resulting signal transduction pathways in suprachiasmatic neurons to affect entrainment or how the colony of mitochondria in a tissue become a singular functioning unit. This is why light is critical in lowering %heteroplasmy in most of my Rx's.

In turn, in healthy tissues, before a cancerous state manifests, the mitochondria sort randomly among daughter cells sampling altered vibrations and spins. Therefore, in cells where heteroplasmy is present, each daughter cell may receive different proportions of mitochondria carrying normal and mutant mtDNA. This is the remnant of bacterial DNA transfer. This means that within organ systems, or anatomic systems, that mtDNA can radically alter the tissues energy function and this can lead to several diseases without ever having any changes to the nuclear genome. It points out why Neo-Darwinism is dead wrong. What does this unique bio-energic quantum mechanism imply for modern biochemistry beliefs?

Biochemistry can only be fully understood when you understand how the energy that runs it. Nature has provided each cell with an exquisite timing precision to make guesses based upon energy flows. Life cannot manifest without energy. I am speaking of quantum mechanical precision that is built into the mitochondrial design. Its shell is a bacteria but its guts are decidedly eukarytoic and tied to UV and IR light mechanisms. Mitochondria are not built for the precision of Peter Mitchell's chemiosmosis. Chemiosmosis is important to unfold proteins to bind more water to make a larger EZ. That is his contribution in my way of thinking. ATP hydrolysis fails this test miserably (5000 fold in fact) and no one seems put off by it as I am. We are ruled by two genomes, a nuclear one that determines our anatomy, and a mitochondrial one that provides all the energy the fuel these genes and the tissues that DNA codes for, Heteroplasmic and homoplasmic mitochondrial energy flows change biochemistry from a “static science” to one with a gradient, scale, slope. That gradient slope is mirrored by the size, shape, and crystalline atomic structure of the EZ in a cell. Mitochondria swim in this EZ. The EZ is a dialectic mirror and mitochondria sample those reflections

Mitochondrial bio-energenics give biology a dynamic geometry that relativity requires. That relativity manifest because not all mitochondria will get the same energy imprint in a tissue. This is a key gear to the clocks in front of every mammalian gene in the nuclear genome. This is a universal requirement for all things that exist with one exception. That exception is light. Light is the only thing in nature that is not relative by design. Its speed limit is constant in a vacuum and with a cell it follows a least time principle set forth by Fermat's law. No one in bio-chemistry seems to realize it because they don’t understand how energy flows in cells and they certainly do not understand how light reacts in tissues. Opticogenetics is opening their eyes. But they do not see what I see yet. They will.

Homoplasmy is a term used in genetics to describe a eukaryotic cell whose copies of mitochondrial DNA are all identical. Homoplasmic cells are easier to entrain by light because the colony of mitochondria acts in unison When in normal and healthy tissues, all cells are homoplasmic. Homoplasmic mitochondrial DNA copies may be normal or mutated; however, most mitochondrial mutations in humans are heteroplasmic because the mutations are passed along by bacterial DNA transfer this is why mutations in mtDNA only occurring in some copies of mitochondrial DNA. That is why heteroplasmy is important to understand. It has been discovered, though, that homoplasmic mitochondrial DNA mutations may be found in several human tumors. So has nature solved it by binding atoms into molecules so it steps down to a lower vibratory rate because of the larger mass, then is it stepped down again in a cell to an acceptable level for the mitochondria to be able to respond to? Is 100hz "it" (all or nothing) or does better heteroplasmy allow for a Goldilocks zone .... ?

I think % heteroplasmy is critical, but since that rate of change is slower (definitely all relative), and since I felt the difference very quickly (also N = 1 situation), it led me to think that the oscillation rate is what makes the most obvious impact. I am still mulling over the 100 Hz 'all or nothing' idea.... because I have been dealing with a 'dislocation' between internal and external sound frequencies. I am currently biohacking it and if I get somewhere, I'll post it in the Biohack Music thread. If not, then only the OB can answer my question.

### Sue-UK — 2016-09-09T13:25:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-mitochondria-perpetual-motion-machines.18371/post-201179>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=200996)
>
> The universe, past, present and future, are energies. When our mitochondria is oscillating at the perfect frequency of 100Hz, perhaps we can tap into these energies?

Apart from the day to day synchronising of the light cycle, could it be that an organism is using the mitochondrial swarm to make comparisons or perceiving differences between oscillations, (like comparing an emitted beam to the returning echo), to sense the trend of changing environmental light conditions?

But there seems so much more to it …. I was reading that Heisenberg's principle of uncertainty states that in trying to measure two parameters of a particle, for instance its momentum and its position, the more accurately we can measure its momentum, the less we can know about its position, and vice versa. So likened to an on/off switch, if a particle/vibration/ oscillation reaches a standstill or point of rest, (on) and its momentum is measured at zero, its position could be anywhere, as if it had expanded rapidly into space in all directions, before collapsing just as rapidly at the second point of rest (off).

So if a mitochondria is a quantum measuring device, as a mitochondrial swarm oscillates, at the on position, if momentum is measured as zero, and the position of the oscillation is indefinite, what happens to time if the probabilities are there but are not being “observed” by a measuring device? Perhaps as the oscillation reaches the second point of rest (off) the now reshuffled deck is “observed” (and time emerges in the now) as a (light) reflection in the EZ mirror?

In terms of immediate survival, perhaps “safe” or “same” cards are ignored, but a “different” or “unsafe” or “anomaly” prompts a response …. The animal may “sense” something useful (lunch!) or danger, such as being stared at by the lion …. Time for the usual senses to pay more attention, or use gut instinct (via the tensegrity system?) to take evasive action just in case …. ?

I am thinking that the more in tune it is to its natural environment and the better the heteroplasmy, together with good structure and functioning of the respiratory proteins and EZ, the more efficient the mitochondrial swarm, and emerging consciousness. Then what if we apply intention to the recipe…..

# Thread: Menopause from a QED perspective
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menopause-from-a-qed-perspective.18373/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-04T14:46:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/menopause-from-a-qed-perspective.18373/post-200850>

Why are women the most unique quantum being ever created? They can use both the particle and wave form of light in more non linear ways than men can. That is the simple answer. So it raises the point what happens to women at menopause when they lose the ability to make estrogen distally in their ovaries, but not rostrally in their retina/brain? The retina and brain have the highest amount of DHA present in humans. Women tend to have even more than men. Women also tend to be under-myelinated compared to men and this has the effect of making them MORE SENSITIVE quantum antenna's to their environment to pass this information to their children and set the epigenetic programming of the child's heteroplasmy rate on top of their mitochondrial haplotype. A loss of estrogen would alter this antenna function in cell membranes and mediated by DHA. This is where SNP's play a role in how light work with mitochondria. DHA is capable of change light into chemical and electrical signals.

They occur via three pathways.
1\. Eye camera for sight.
2\. Central retinal pathways that control all growth and metabolism
3\. Muller cells which are light shafts behind certain photoreceptors that photoelectrically activate the collagen/tensegrity system in every cell of the body.

Low carb high fat diets are logically fallacies: Why? These guru's believe that food and diet alone are linked to diseases like obesity and diabetes. The real essence of what they miss is that a SPECTRAL DEFICIENCY IN LIGHT via the retina/mitochondria can do the same thing. The energy regulatory mechanisms in mitochondria become damaged by % heteroplamsy changes that manifest by the geometric lengthening of the respiratory proteins in mitochondria. This can also be compounded by exposures to chemicals like those mentioned above. It is also influenced by RF, UHF, VHF, and microwave nnEMF's as well. This situation ruins how fat is burned via beta oxidation and why fat is now stored that cannot be burned. This is why modern humans have found diet and exercise to be an endless Sisyphean task we all know it to be via our observations in reality. It is NOT NATURAL or normal to have to avoid carbohydrates until one loses this antenna ability in mitochondria. The LCHF guru's have no clue about these mechanism because they are ignorant on light's actions in mitochondria. This is why they jump from theory to theory about blaming foods and nutrient to nutrient in supplements without ever eliciting a clear etiology. They want you to believe we are fighting a inner demon in our RNA/DNA when really the demon is a stretched out inner mitochondrial membrane.

My goal is to teach people how to recoil that stretch using QED. QED provides us a clear tangible, manageable, and environmental cause for these disease phenotypes. The best example for me is post menopausal women. They avoid sunlight, stay inside, cover their bodies from solar light while continue bathing themselves under artificial light and all these new nnEMF they embrace tasty in touch with their jobs, families, and friends. All this occurs while the face a DROP in estrogen (distally in the target organ) due to natural ovarian failure we call menopause. Women in menopause become more loosely coupled and they over eat as a result and their mitochondrial haplotype drives the amount of fatness they experience because this determines beta-oxidation rates and fat storage. In menopause, women rely more on the TCA cycle and this makes sunlight more important to their wellness. The less they get the fatter they get. Why? The drop in estrogen distally in the ovary also simulates a stretched out cristae in their mitochondria and they lose control of energy flow to and fro their own mitochondria. This can be overcome with a solid stimulus of light rostrally in the eye and skin. The lowered distal production of estrogen lowers the effective rate of beta oxidation and up-regulates AMPK pathways and blood glucose and glutamate. This immediately changes the way their adipocytes and their haplotypes in mtDNA operate in a very short timescale. This women lose control over energy flow and fat storage responds by adding to adipocyte stores until the light can restore estrogen secretion centrally in the brain.

A woman's ovaries fail naturally, but their eyes and brains still can make it. So can their adrenal glands. To produce estrogen but their brains, via their retina's still produce enough basal estrogen to offset the changes in their mitochondrial DNA in the hypothalamus where the leptin receptors are.

Fritz Hollwich's work 60 years ago has given us these links and now Wallace is cementing them..........but ladies with menopause and PCOS do not like my Rx of getting naked outside in the sun to reverse these bio-energic problems. It is too easy to be lead to a diet book, a diet pill, or a diet doctor for a half truth. That is the reality I live within.

DHA mediates all three pathways mentioned above. The uniqueness of docosahexaenoic acid has been maintained in eukaryotes for 600 million years with not one change made in all this time via evolution. This is an extraordinary record unknown in any other biologic chemical.
Since the evolution of the cephalopods and possibly as far back as before the Cambrian explosion in the dinoflagellates with their eye spot, DHA is found as the principle structural component of the visual system, synapse and neurons. It is present in more than 50% of the photoreceptor membrane lipids, which include di-docosahexaenoic acid molecular species of phosphoglycerides in the cephalopods, fish, amphibia, reptiles, birds, mammals and ourselves. This richness of DHA in the photoreceptor is shared with the synapse and neurons. There are two molecules that differ from DHA by only two hydrogen atoms (the O6 and O3 docosapentaenoic acids, DPA; one of these is a precursor for DHA. Yet, neither was used throughout this 500–600 million-year period of evolution. Thus, biology seems highly sensitive to the slight difference of the one double bond between DHA and the DPA molecules. This is a finger print for a non linear system built around light waves. The presence of DHA’s full complement of six double bonds is for some reason an important priority in neural membranes, and from the evolutionary record would seem to have been conserved in this capacity.

This is the most compelling evidence for the absolute essentiality for DHA in the brain/retina. It is a far superior order than any randomized clinical trial, which cannot hope to test the significance of 500 million years of conservation which was tested by natural selection and genomic change over the whole stretch of invertebrate and vertebrate evolution. This evidence combined with the experimental evidence, especially in gene instruction, adds to the case for a coastal origin of hominid cerebral expansion.

Another remarkable feature of DHA is its oxidation product, neuroprotectin D2. Described by Bazan (@ LSU when I was in medical school in 1989), it is a powerful anti-oxidant with the potential to protect against oxidative damage, which could explain the low incidence of Alzheimer’s disease in fish-eating populations (Bazan, 1989; Niemoller et al., 2009). Nature’s preference for DHA in the brain is strikingly demonstrated in large, vegetarian land mammals, in which DPA is the dominant O3 metabolite found in non-neural tissues and is thus abundantly available (Crawford et al., 1969). Yet, even in these mammals neural membranes still conserve the DHA-rich composition. During the evolution of land mammals, this retention of composition was associated with economy in brain size. There was a logarithmic decline in relative brain size as they evolved larger, protein-rich, bodies based on food structures, with only the precursors linoleic and alinolenic acids found in plants (Crawford et al., 1993). The slow rate of desaturation of the fatty acids, especially in the insertion of the last double bond, explains the difficulty of accumulating DHA from land food resources. In the marine food chain, the photosynthetic systems produce a profile of the simpler O3 fatty acids, with some producing DHA itself. The animals which browse these substrates are eaten by small sea animals, which are then eaten by bigger animals, which themselves get eaten. At each step, the DHA proportion is stepped up, but that of the 18 and 20 carbon chain lengths is diminished. This trend is something critically missed by food guru's.

This principle of biomagnification is the same as Crawford reported in human fetal development: figuratively speaking, the placenta eats the maternal blood loaded with DHA and the fetus then eats the placental product, which is deposited in the liver and then eaten by the developing brain (Crawford et al., 1976). A study of the human placenta indicated that the process was nature’s preferential selection for DHA and not its synthesis from a precursor. There is very little precursor in the fetal circulation and beyond: biosynthetic conversion would be academic. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4465628/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4465628/)

# Thread: SLEEP SUGGESTIONS
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-suggestions.18377/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael Polito — 2016-09-05T17:44:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-suggestions.18377/post-200917>

Wondering if anyone can offer some sleep aid suggestions. I have been trying set my C.R. Up in the morning 20 min facing the sun, I dont wear sunglasses, Im outdoors quite a bit, I have corrected the lighting in my home to amber at night, use amber glasses while watching tv, iphone computer etc. Try to stop eating by 7pm. I do however drink coffee in the morning, maybe a cup in the afternoon and sometimes a cup after dinner. I do have an occasional glass of wine for dinner. I know most will say, "duhhh" ditch the coffee especially at night, which i do sometimes, but it does not seem to help. 56 years old. Thank you.

### caroline — 2016-09-08T09:54:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-suggestions.18377/post-201096>

Is your bedroom very, very dark?

Can you turn off the power to your bedroom? What is on the other side of your bedroom walls? above? below?

Does your house have a smart meter?

All electrons out of bedroom?

How is your libido?

What do you eat for dinner?

### harmony — 2016-09-08T11:24:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-suggestions.18377/post-201101>

Before sleep Jack says get cold and exercise ( havn't quite got to that stage yet myself). Getting to sleep - try visualising things, work on turning something into an image, preferably in colour. Counting sheep is a classic example, I used to visualise peeling an orange. Staying asleep - darkness - cover the light sources, noiseless or white noise - my son used to turn on a fan for white noise. Waking and not getting back to sleep - get up and make a warm drink - just warm water if you're spartan, or chamomile tea, maybe read for a while if reading makes you sleepy, or visualise. Don't use this time to think about work, save that for the hour before you get up as it can be your most creative and productive thinking time.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-08T19:26:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-suggestions.18377/post-201144>

Cells are designed to structure water during daylight hours and the effect lasts long into the night when quantum coherent excitations are occuring in the absence of light and during REM sleep when we are paralyzed. Motion disturbs the quantum state. Fake light night at light also disturbs a quantum state to a degree. Why is artifical light at night DEADLY: During daylight hours the sun builds a massive battery in cell water and water stores that potential energy for a long period of time as Gerald Pollack has showed. As that charge is built up during day this allows to a decoherence as the cell physical properties allows for decoherence with the environment under the power of the suns waves. They to understand is that in warm environment coherence can be maintained in transient fashion at night. During the absence of sun at night, while using the massive charges stored in cell water called the exclusion zone, cells become able to transiently able to become quantum coherent. This allows for regeneration and the creation of the living state. This implies that sleep was likely the original state that life began and living things evolved wakefulness during daylight hours. Nocturnal animals reversed this process and used other formation of cosmic radiation from the Van Allen belts at night and IR light present day and night to yoke their circadian systems. No matter how improbable this appears to physicists nature laws do not forbid quantum coherence by any know law or math proof, therefore, given enough time nature will find a way. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/27499509](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/27499509)

### Penny — 2016-09-08T22:30:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-suggestions.18377/post-201151>

As the planetary expert on insomnia, I think I've tried everything... zero screen time at night - I mean, when that sun goes down, watch it and you are done... I have great luck running a red incandescent light next to me which I read by and then I pass out - in fact, my night time ritual is to take a shower, rub epsom salts and baking soda on my skin, and finish it with cold water - then while I'm still kinda not all the way dry, I head for bed where there are 2 UV lights running over the bed and one red light running next to me - I have lately been also throwing ice cold face dunks at night as well as in the morning - really, whenever I want a hit of energy and I have been hacking an IR Rubilux light which I shine on my forehead over my left eye - I think this is where dopamine/serotonin might be made in the PFC - it is where the sensor in my "Mindflex" was put so just a guess on my part - then I start OMing - no covers on either - you have to get cold - then I'm out -

This all goes to crap if it's a hot night, if I haven't gotten rid of enough blood sugar and if I have too many carbs at night - then it's bathroom time por moi... so, for dinner which also needs to be finished by say 4:00 optimally for me - because when that sun goes down, digestion goes to crap - you're either digesting at night or doing cellular repair IMHO - if I eat even at 6:00 P.M. it's GERD time for me... dinner should be some protein and fat - I also find too much protein and I'm getting rid of ammonia instead of making serotonin - watch the bacon because the salt will dehydrate you and you won't sleep either - personally, I skip sugar, salt, caffeine and alcohol after noon because it's too dehydrating -

A word about oysters... it is just astounding to me how a silly little can of oysters can work better for sleep than any pill I've ever tried... I get a can down every day - they have electrons, zinc (which is used to convert B6 into P5P which is a cofactor for serotonin), viruses - think RNA here... and remember, zinc is used in the insulin molecule so anytime you look at light at night, you won't make melatonin and won't shut off the insulin pump which has a melatonin receptor - it takes 4 hours of complete darkness to make melatonin so you are leaving on that pump an extra 4 hours every time you "surf"... also, I wear black eye shades -

Not sure about exercise after dinner - I have used the trampoline after dinner - it's funner to watch the sunset that way, but I think it would be way better to exercise between 2:00-5:00, then have dinner - Dr. K has mentioned that in his book -

Give it up on the coffee - truly stupid IMHO - try ice cold face dunks instead - my new religion - also, methylene blue in the afternoon isn't a hot idea either - but that's just me -

I've also slept with a giant ice pack on my abdomen or back and that works great also - you can also try thinking about someone/thing you love or even thinking the word "love" can do it and your next breath will be a perfect belly breath - it is crazy cool how that works - which is a direct hit to your vagus nerve -

### Mover — 2016-09-10T03:11:57+0300

> [harmony said:](/goto/post?id=201101)
>
> Before sleep Jack says get cold and exercise ( havn't quite got to that stage yet myself). Getting to sleep - try visualising things, work on turning something into an image, preferably in colour. Counting sheep is a classic example, I used to visualise peeling an orange. Staying asleep - darkness - cover the light sources, noiseless or white noise - my son used to turn on a fan for white noise. Waking and not getting back to sleep - get up and make a warm drink - just warm water if you're spartan, or chamomile tea, maybe read for a while if reading makes you sleepy, or visualise. Don't use this time to think about work, save that for the hour before you get up as it can be your most creative and productive thinking time.

Thank you . Going to try these things. Sometimes it is not worth fighting it. Just get up like you said. Thank you.

# Thread: sun gazing in Australia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-gazing-in-australia.18380/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hello — 2016-09-06T02:44:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-gazing-in-australia.18380/post-200952>

Hey guys, I've just realised that the right time to get sun exposure is in the morning and that looking at the sun is important. I've been sitting in the sun in the middle of the day with my glasses on, but just realised through reading here that actually the thing to do is to look at or near the sun, glasses off, early in the morning........

For anyone who knows, anyone in Aus, etc., - is it safe to look at or near the sun? What time of day should it be done? I assume its just dangerous to do after mid morning.

if anyone has any thoughts or has experimented with it would love to know! thanks!

### Jenny S — 2016-09-06T05:20:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-gazing-in-australia.18380/post-200956>

Hi & welcome. Yes you do need the sun in your eyes first thing - preferably before you get any artificial light in your eyes. It's ok to look at sun or near up toabout 20 secs altho I find I can look at it longer when I get up - definitely no glasses when outside whenever possible. Morning sun is optimal for all body but afternoon still good. There is a UV checker which I will put link - handy to find max UV times so best time to spend in sun if time limited [http://www.arpansa.gov.au/uvindex/realtime/bri\_rt.htm](http://www.arpansa.gov.au/uvindex/realtime/bri_rt.htm)
Somewhere on forum there is a blog to read first for newbies - can't see it at moment but am sure someone else will link it for you.
Where are you located? I'm on Bribie Island - moved up here from Vic for more sun. You're off to a good start finding Jack's work anyway - good luck

### caroline — 2016-09-06T07:29:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-gazing-in-australia.18380/post-200971>

Welcome.....

There is lots of stuff on the home page on how to get started here.....

Do you listen to Dr. k's podcasts? They are a great way to get information

Do you have Dr. K's book? It is the first couple of years of the blog

Please get John Ott's book .....health and light

### Lahelada — 2016-09-06T12:57:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-gazing-in-australia.18380/post-200976>

The link Jenny mentions is in my signature. There is an error though in thinking that you need to stare into the sun. Don't make me look for the quote but Dr K says to look 15-20 degrees east of the sun rather than practice direct sungazing. The sunning in the am should be done at sunrise or as close to it as possible. This is done for resetting your clock. Sunning yourself for Vitamin D happens in another window. Afternoon sun between 3-5 is healing due to the shift of frequencies towards red.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

"So, I read Jack's article, and like the few others I have read, it doesn't seem to make scientific sense to me or Mark, either the biology or physics standpoint, which I realise can't be actually separated. But I like to check out such things because I know only a little physics, so I asked a friend who has a PhD in physics, and this is his response (via Facebook) below.
I'm not saying that all Jack's strategies won't work, because he has some stuff right, but I don't think it's because his science is all correct.

The science in this is so terrible, I'm thinking of reposting it just so my other science friends can destroy it
I only made it through the first few paragraphs and I can tell you it's complete bollocks
It's almost as though they've opened a physics textbook, and put all the words they don't understand into sentences."

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-06T15:06:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-mercury-studies.18382/post-200981>

Why do you have to be careful with alternative meme's and narratives that are linked to money? Often they are not true and people get "herded" into a belief system that they need expensive testing and therapies to remove their risk of heavy metal toxicity. I see it daily. Mercury is prevalent in apex marine predators, it can be a problem with eating these types of meats often. (tuna). However the lower in the food chain you go the "less prevalent it is". So eat your oysters as the Epi-paleo Rx tells you! The selenium in the seafood is what protects against metal poisoning. If you are worried, eat a Brazil nut every day. What about quick Mercury bio hack from my upcoming bio-hacking book? In high mercury toxicity states you might notice that your balance is not good after an acute exposure. Any time I go to my dentists office for anything or eat predator fish as I often do, I do this test on myself. I also do it after I eat seafood to see if the fish might have been tainted. Try to do some yoga poses that require you to be on one leg. You will notice a big change and your balance will be decidedly off from normal. When this happens I usually supplement immediately with Selenium and eat a lot of Cilantro the next two weeks. Why? It’s a natural mercury chelator and selenium is the antidote for mercury toxicity. Now if you have high heteroplasmy rates and your mitochondrial makes a ton of ROS/RNS then you need to get spectroscopy testing to truly see if you are toxic. Most people are not. For those who are then you really need to worry way more about the light in your environment and not the fish you're eating. Why? Hg in your body can act as the receiver of resonant energy from light if it is present. I only tell folks to avoid mercury vapor fluorescent bulbs if they have documented mercury toxicity. Hg bulbs are a light source that can act as a sender of resonant energy to the atoms of mercury in your tissues. This is one of the reasons, as a former dentist, I am not in favor of amalgam removal in most cases. It seems counterintuitive to my core beliefs I share with you here daily, but let me explain why. In its free form, Hg easily vaporizes at room temperatures. It likes to find harbor in fatty tissues below the skin. When Hg is charged by a current or photoelectrically by blue light it can be a real nightmare. Mercury has a spectral array that give two lines in the violet/blue at 404.66 And 407.79, one in the indigo range at 435.84, one in turquoise at 490.05, one in lemon at 546.08 and two in the yellow range at 576.92 and 579.03. It binds to sulfur enzymes which act to remove these compounds from biologic use and interferes with ECT in mitochondria directly below its location by lowering energy production in mitochondria. I am not worried about Hg resonance with outdoor lights/sun because UVA/UVB light does not penetrate our surfaces deeply. The blue light in sunlight is balanced by the red light in sun.

But I worry about it a lot in dental reconstructions done indoors because most biologic dentists are completely unaware of the risks of resonant light transfer from fluorescent or LED bulbs in their dental lights, offices, or their oral work lamps. If they are going to work on you teeth in this type of light environment, you should request a red lit environment when undergoing removal of amalgams. I have yet to hear of one biologic dentist in the USA that even knows about this mechanism of energy transfer. Their offices are built with blue/white LED and fluorescent lights which all have higher color temperatures as a result. This is akin to putting a match to gasoline with respect to Hg toxicity. Blue light is very dangerous in this case because it can penetrate very deeply into fat layers where Hg lies dormant, and dentists wear lamps on their heads that have extreme blue spikes at 435 nm with high color temperatures that can photoelectrically energize Hg in your tissues. So those with mercury issues focus in on fish when they should be focused in on their light environment. This risk is much greater with blue artificial light because it penetrates far deeper that UVA/UVB light can as the picture shows. Hg is rarely found in surfaces of the body. there is speculation it maybe in the retina but if true I should be dead considering how much DHA is in my RPE.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-06T15:07:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-mercury-studies.18382/post-200983>

T[obias Prime](https://www.facebook.com/tobiah.mcgrath?fref=ufi&rc=p) asks: I've been turned off seafood since Fukushima.. Should I just increase the iodine to protect from any radiation ?

Radiation has been condemned by the entire scientific community as being a “dangerous thing”. This is surprising, however, since there is strong scientific evidence for the molecular mechanisms of how transient “radiation drinks” could promote health. I tell my patients seafood radiation has many surprising benefits. Today we know that low doses of radiation stimulate DNA repair through the activation of four transcription factors, PARP-1, PARP2, ATM and Ku70. In response to low dose, sublethal radiation (or xenohormetic compounds). PARP-1 and PARP-2 activate two DNA repair pathways called “Single strand break repair” (SSBR) and one called “Base Excision Repair” (BER). Both of these repair single-stranded DNA breaks. In response to low dose, sublethal radiation (or xenohormetic compounds), Ku-70 activates the DNA repair pathway called “Non-homologous end joining” (NHEJ). [https://scholar.google.com/scholar...](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fscholar.google.com%2Fscholar%3Fq%3Dradiation%2520DNA%2520repair%2520parp1%2520parp2%26btnG%26hl%3Den%26as_sdt%3D0%252C22%26as_vis%3D1&h=yAQGY3swW)
Science evolves even if your thinking remains stagnant. We must expand our grip of science…………or risk falling from the cliff. I tell this to my patients and all the alternative functional medicine doctors who keep telling their patients seafood is bad. It is really bad advice based upon out dated thinking.

### DLO — 2016-09-10T07:31:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oysters-mercury-studies.18382/post-201238>

Very interesting contrarian take on things.
Would love to hear your thoughts Jack!
I already know that we should be taking things from whole food rather than pills and your onto the next level of throwing food out the window and relying on sunlight all together, however how much sunlight do fish loaded with dha get?
He prefers PEO Parent Essential Oils
[http://www.docrowen.com/essential-fatty-acids.html](http://www.docrowen.com/essential-fatty-acids.html)

Now consider something else. Those waters are real cold and have low oxygen tension. At room temperature and in our oxygen rich environment, we know that the fish oils quickly become rancid. Just smell a 2-day dead fish (even just one day can be tough if not refrigerated). These oils are extremely vulnerable to oxidation (rancidity). In fact, DHA, with is 6 double bonds, is a whopping 340 more times susceptible to oxidation/rancidity than monounsaturated oleic acid from olive oil. The oils in a fish oil capsule start to autooxidize (rancidity) instantly in your body. Why? Because, unlike a salmon, which lives at about 34°F, your body is about 98°F and is oxygen rich. **Heat and oxygen don’t mix well with the highly vulnerable marine oils.**

Do you want to be stuffing in highly oxygen vulnerable omega 3 derivatives into your mitochondria (cell furnaces) where combustion will **immediately oxidize those oils and age your energy furnaces?**

Mitochondria, like skin, have little or no omega 3 oils due to such vulnerability. For this reason—your body made by God—carefully controls its conversion of PEOs to the highly vulnerable fatty acids to about 1%. When you take supplemental marine oils, you overcome the protection God gave you. Normally recommended amounts of fish oil often equate to taking pharmacological (overdose) amounts of highly reactive compounds your body needs sparingly. Your skin and highly vulnerable energy furnaces become **susceptible to irreversible damage.** I don’t believe that God made mistakes, so I fervently disagree with the pundits that our conversion rate is “slow.” I believe, as Prof. Peskin does too, it was created deliberately in the wisdom of our Creator, and our efforts to overcome that wisdom with supplements, except in extraordinary circumstances, is foolhardy at best, and downright dangerous at worst.

The May 2013 trial3 showed that macular degeneration victims were not helped despite fish oil’s significant DHA content.\[iv\] The year 2013 was very bad for fish oil findings. Why the failures?

Let’s turn to logic first. **Our bodies are made for ingestion of PEOs, which are fully functional.** What does that mean? A ladder with broken footholds might be the right length, but you’ll come crashing down. Oils are the same. A major problem of the SAD (standard American diet) is that our alleged “foods” are loaded with oils that have been heated and highly processed. That loads our cells with adulterated (trans, oxidized and rancid) oils, like a painter standing on a damaged rung of a ladder. This is one of the great and poorly understood causes of disease of our times. Fish oil cannot repair this damage. Only ingestion of non-adulterated, organically grown and processed plant-based oils will correct the problem. That is why fish oil has to fail. But what of the possibility of toxicity?

In 2013, a landmark article appeared in the Journal of the National Cancer Institute. It confirmed prior post-2007 findings of increased prostate cancer risk among men with high blood concentrations of marine oil type lipids. The authors warned, “The consistency of these findings suggests that these **fatty acids \[EPA / DHA\] are involved in prostate tumorigenesis**.” They argued for reconsideration of marine oil use!

The **early humans would have had NO access to omega 3 rich fish in Africa**. So, logically, our need for consumption of marine oil and its supplements must be low, and the conversion of PEOs to the LCPUFAs as designed by the Creator must be for a reason. And consider the longest living human societies on the planet. Of the 5, only one group, those in Okinawa, consume fish. Most of the rest are largely vegetarians eating ripe foods grown on rich soils. I am a vegetarian for spiritual reasons. I’ve had my omega fats checked and I fall well into the reference range eating NO marine oils! Most vegetarians also will have well over 80% of the EPA and DHA that fish eaters have in their bloodstream. And, that’s enough!For example, your skin ratio of omega 6:3 is 1000:1. For your brain, it’s more like 100:1. Not only does fish oil improperly force and increase LCPUFAs in your cell membranes, but also overdoses your mitochondria, which absolutely don’t want the rancid prone omega 3 series. Your mitochondria are the equivalent of blast furnaces. **The vulnerability of LCPUFAs in your mitochondria would be analogous to replacing the steel walls of a blast furnace with copper, which would oxidize and crumble. Force-feeding these critical structures marine oils is a sure way to age them.**

The cell membrane is like a keyboard to your hard drive. Your keyboard controls it. And so, we are learning that the real action in health is occurring at your cell membrane. One key function of cell membrane is to permit oxygen into the cell. I often wondered how cells, with an oily membrane, permitted oxygen to pass through. The secret is in the omega 6—not omega 3—fatty acid content. The former has the ability to latch on to oxygen from without the cell and release it within the cell suffering no damage. Omega 3 oils can suffer oxidative damage, as you’ve seen. And, since parent omega 6 is essential to pass oxygen into your cells, **a deficiency of unadulterated, fully functional omega 6 can lead to cellular oxygen starvation.**

Simultaneously, they neglect to tell you that your most important vascular lubricators, **key anti-inflammatory compounds, and circulatory protection molecules are PGE1 and prostacyclin—both of which are 100% derived from Parent omega 6.** These are molecules you really want, and they **can’t be made from adulterated omega 6 or from intact omega 3 or fish oil.** It’s likely for this reason that Prof. Peskin’s IOWA study saw PEOs shellacking fish oil. The PEO users were a whopping 11 years biologically younger in their arteries once the fish oil stopped and the PEOs began. And it just took about 6 months to get these results.

If you've made it down here it's good to see Kresser has seemingly reversed his thoughts on mercury and fish

If you are generally healthy, the best strategy is to consume about 12–16 ounces of cold-water, fatty fish or shellfish each week. Most studies show an inverse relationship between fish consumption and heart disease and mortality, so while fish oil may not protect you, eating fish does seem to. P**erhaps this is because fish and shellfish contain many other beneficial nutrients that fish oil does not, including selenium, zinc, iron, and highly absorbable protein. (Fortunately, most cold-water, fatty fish and shellfish are also low in mercury and other toxins, and [mercury in fish may not be as big a problem as some have led us to believe](http://chriskresser.com/5-reasons-why-concerns-about-mercury-in-fish-are-misguided/).)**

# Thread: lipo-lymphedema
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipo-lymphedema.18383/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Zsuzsi — 2016-09-06T21:50:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipo-lymphedema.18383/post-201010>

Greetings from Budapest, Hungary (Central Europe) again!
Caroline suggested me starting journaling about my current condition and hopeful transformation - for that I thank her.

I'm a 45 y/o woman with lipo-lymphedema. Not technically overweight (164 cms tall and 60 kgs) but expressively pear shaped with big, chunky legs. No abdominal fat at all.
Other: venous insufficiency.

Mother's condition: lymphedema and lipoedema, diabetes 2, macular degeneration, venous insufficiency, slightly overweight.
Maternal grandmother was a lean lady, had been healthy and active until the last year of her life.
Paternal grandmother was extremely obese. She died of leukemia.
I have no sisters. My two teenage daughters don’t seem to have inherited the condition.

I'm currently reading Dr. Jack's book and have watched quite a few videos by him. I have been leading a Paleo lifestyle, with more or less success: I would say 70% of the time, but 30% falling off the wagon due to extreme food cravings and binge eating. I mostly crave carbs and creamy desserts. This is the only thing that makes me feel terrible about myself and my life. And that I cannot wear nice skirts.

On the other hand, when I manage to rein my cravings, I seem to get in better shape both mentally and physically. I don't have problems with the 5-hour pause between meals, I have breakfast and lunch, and skipping dinner works absolutely OK for me.

Supplements I take:
Vit D3 ,K2, fish oil (for 8-9 years)
curcumin, vit C with cultured nutrients
N-acetylcysteine
Calcium D-Glucarate
Pregnenolone 50 mg
These latter 3 have helped me completely cure my heavy bleeding (menorrhagia)
ashwagandha complex
horse chestnut, butcher’s broom, Diosmin-hesperidin complex for venous insufficiency
Zn, Se, Iodine (kelp), Mg
Sometimes I take activated charcoal pills before lunch in the office canteen.

Food choices (when the binge eating doesn't nullify my health efforts):
extra virgin organic coconut oil, grass fed butter, raw goat milk and cheese, pork hams, bacons, sausages from controlled farms, sometimes fish, home-made pickled (lacto-fermented) cucumbers in the summer, sauerkraut from autumn till late spring, bone broth every day from Sept till May
I eat fruits rarely, in the morning I have a glass of lemon-water
and I love veggies
81 – 90% dark chocolate

Lifestyle changes:
rebounding on a home trampoline 15 mins 3x-4x a week
lukewarm-cold shower every evening - transitioning towards cold therapy, this is my aim
1 hour walking but due to terribly long office work hours, only in the weekend
I have introduced sunbathing (I had never liked being in the sun, my skin is very white, i easily get sun urnát, and I have lots of freckles).
grounding, but due to my work schedule, only in the weekend
blue light is blocked on my laptop, computer and smartphone
evening routine: from 9 p.m. only reading books, no laptop, no phone

Since I have introduced the last two things, I have slept much much better than before.

My only problem in this life is that I feel totally unattractive due to my out-of-proportion body, and although there was a few years I could shed pounds off of my legs and looked better, it was because I was starving myself.
I know that lipedema is an inflammation-related, officially progressive condition. I'm going to follow what Jack is recommending in his book and in his videos but I'm afraid I should dig deeper (sialic acid that is found in red meat, probably is not a good choice).

If you have any suggestions, advice, remarks, please, do so.
Thank you for your kindness.
Zsuzsi

### Zsuzsi — 2016-09-08T21:12:05+0300

Yes, thank you, I'm trying to reorganize my life accordingly. I'm doing the leptin rx reset, and all the rest are on my agenda too. I will report back in 1 month to see if there is any improvement in the fatty tissues of my legs. I have read extensively about this condition, and currently the verdict of lipedema and lymphedema experts is that these conditions cannot really be cured, only treated and somehow kept under control to prevent further progression. There's one surgical solution to completely get rid of these tissues: lymph-sparing liposuction, which must be a very expensive treatment, so I try to find a natural way, that's why I'm here.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-10T01:39:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipo-lymphedema.18383/post-356094>

Lymphedema is a stromal problem associated with loss of piezo, flexo, and pyroelectric properties in collagen. Lymphedema and Stromal Weakness Lymphedema arises from defects in the lymphatic vessels or nodes, leading to fluid accumulation in the interstitial spaces, often in the limbs. The lymphatic system relies on a stromal framework, a connective tissue that surrounds lymphatic vessels and nodes, providing structural support and regulating fluid dynamics. Stromal weaknesses in this context align with your thesis as follows: 1. Stromal Composition and Lymphatic Function Lymphatic Stroma: The stroma of lymphatic vessels and nodes consists of: Collagen fibrils (e.g., types I, III) and proteoglycans (e.g., decorin, versican), which maintain vessel integrity and ECM stability. Lymphatic endothelial cells and pericytes, supported by stromal cells, regulate lymphatic flow and valve function. Mesenchymal stem cells contribute to lymphatic repair and regeneration. Role in Lymphedema: In lymphedema, stromal integrity is compromised, leading to: Weakened lymphatic vessel walls, causing leakage or collapse. Fibrosis or ECM remodeling, reducing lymphatic drainage capacity. Impaired stromal cell function, limiting repair of damaged vessels. Parallels to My Thesis: This mirrors stromal thinning in keratoconus (corneal stroma) or enamel pitting (dental stroma), and breech fetal positioning where collagen and proteoglycan defects reduce biomechanical strength. In lymphedema, stromal weakness in lymphatic tissues disrupts fluid flow, akin to stagnant dentinal flow in teeth.

### Skylarc88 — 2025-09-04T16:52:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipo-lymphedema.18383/post-359123>

> [Zsuzsi said:](/goto/post?id=201010)
>
> Greetings from Budapest, Hungary (Central Europe) again!
> Caroline suggested me starting journaling about my current condition and hopeful transformation - for that I thank her.
>
> I'm a 45 y/o woman with lipo-lymphedema. Not technically overweight (164 cms tall and 60 kgs) but expressively pear shaped with big, chunky legs. No abdominal fat at all.
> Other: venous insufficiency.
>
> Mother's condition: lymphedema and lipoedema, diabetes 2, macular degeneration, venous insufficiency, slightly overweight.
> Maternal grandmother was a lean lady, had been healthy and active until the last year of her life.
> Paternal grandmother was extremely obese. She died of leukemia.
> I have no sisters. My two teenage daughters don’t seem to have inherited the condition.
>
> I'm currently reading Dr. Jack's book and have watched quite a few videos by him. I have been leading a Paleo lifestyle, with more or less success: I would say 70% of the time, but 30% falling off the wagon due to extreme food cravings and binge eating. I mostly crave carbs and creamy desserts. This is the only thing that makes me feel terrible about myself and my life. And that I cannot wear nice skirts.
>
> On the other hand, when I manage to rein my cravings, I seem to get in better shape both mentally and physically. I don't have problems with the 5-hour pause between meals, I have breakfast and lunch, and skipping dinner works absolutely OK for me.
>
> Supplements I take:
> Vit D3 ,K2, fish oil (for 8-9 years)
> curcumin, vit C with cultured nutrients
> N-acetylcysteine
> Calcium D-Glucarate
> Pregnenolone 50 mg
> These latter 3 have helped me completely cure my heavy bleeding (menorrhagia)
> ashwagandha complex
> horse chestnut, butcher’s broom, Diosmin-hesperidin complex for venous insufficiency
> Zn, Se, Iodine (kelp), Mg
> Sometimes I take activated charcoal pills before lunch in the office canteen.
>
> Food choices (when the binge eating doesn't nullify my health efforts):
> extra virgin organic coconut oil, grass fed butter, raw goat milk and cheese, pork hams, bacons, sausages from controlled farms, sometimes fish, home-made pickled (lacto-fermented) cucumbers in the summer, sauerkraut from autumn till late spring, bone broth every day from Sept till May
> I eat fruits rarely, in the morning I have a glass of lemon-water
> and I love veggies
> 81 – 90% dark chocolate
>
> Lifestyle changes:
> rebounding on a home trampoline 15 mins 3x-4x a week
> lukewarm-cold shower every evening - transitioning towards cold therapy, this is my aim
> 1 hour walking but due to terribly long office work hours, only in the weekend
> I have introduced sunbathing (I had never liked being in the sun, my skin is very white, i easily get sun urnát, and I have lots of freckles).
> grounding, but due to my work schedule, only in the weekend
> blue light is blocked on my laptop, computer and smartphone
> evening routine: from 9 p.m. only reading books, no laptop, no phone
>
> Since I have introduced the last two things, I have slept much much better than before.
>
> My only problem in this life is that I feel totally unattractive due to my out-of-proportion body, and although there was a few years I could shed pounds off of my legs and looked better, it was because I was starving myself.
> I know that lipedema is an inflammation-related, officially progressive condition. I'm going to follow what Jack is recommending in his book and in his videos but I'm afraid I should dig deeper (sialic acid that is found in red meat, probably is not a good choice).
>
> If you have any suggestions, advice, remarks, please, do so.
> Thank you for your kindness.
> Zsuzsi

Any updates ? is it working for you? I have undiagnosed Lipo and my family on both sides also have this condition in female members
anything ,more on the diet side? I eat tons of beef sense i follow clean low carb diet and i excluded pork because it kicks up histamines and cause inflammation for me. My diet is very restricted and i also trying to figure out how to deal with CIRS/Mold not detoxing and Lyme and Babesia, leaky gut. 8 months ago i Moved out of city apartment in to a house in countryside to try and get access to grounding and Sunlight and nature.
