### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-07T04:17:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/monsanto-is-a-problem-for-human-health-that-is-now-spreading-to-europe.18385/post-201020>

What is the problem fundamentally with glyphosate? It replaces glycine in proteins and collagen is the number one protein in your body that uses glycine. Glycine is unique among amino acids in that it is achiral. Chiral amino acids offer the ability of cells to polarize light. Glycine is used in places in cells where we do not want to use the Faraday effect of polarization so this changes optical signaling. Glycine can fit into hydrophilic or hydrophobic environments, due to its minimal side chain of only one hydrogen atom. Glyphosate is unusual and very different than glycine achiral ability because its nitorgen bind to a metal atom and then its sterochemistry becomes fixed. Glycine does not do this and this affects how light is polarized in these cells and it also affect collagens ability to remain piezoelectric. For those who do not know collagen is the major N-type semiconductor in humans so when glyphosate replaces glycine all proteins where this replacement occurs under go dielectric collapse. This means energy leaves drop in these tissues. My recent Time 21 blog covers these N type semiconductors and how they operate. None of this information is contained in the video below but it is the main reason these chemicals are a problem for living things. Glycine has an innate ability to be a chameleon for exclusion zone of water because it can be hydrophobic to reduce the EZ or hydrophilic to raise the EZ in places it is used in proteins construction. This ability is also lost when it is replaced by glyphosate. Protein chemistry is highly specific and small changes lead to massive outcomes in non linear systems. Glyphosate is Roundup weed killer for those who don't know that is used to grow most foods in the USA and EU. It is a chemical you should avoid and one you should not use on your grass to ground. Glyphosate Pretending to be Glycine: Devastating Consequences - Stephanie Seneff, PhD |

Published on May 25, 2016
Slides>
[http://www.autismone.org/.../Seneff\_Stephanie\_052516.pdf](http://www.autismone.org/.../Seneff_Stephanie_052516.pdf)

The following vaccines were found to be contaminated with the herbicide glyphosate: MMR, Varicella (chicken pox), Zostavax (shingles), Proquad (MMR, rubella, varicella), Fluzone Quad (flu vaccine) and Hepatitis B (B Energix-B). Multiple vaccines from different manufactures were found to be contaminated. Folks, this is a big deal. Injecting a vaccine contaminated with a known herbicide that is “probably carcinogenic to humans” should be prohibited. We need a Congressional investigation into our vaccines. We keep hearing the mantra that vaccines are safe. Injecting a vaccine containing an herbicide is safe? You can see Dr. Samsel talk about his research here: [http://www.tonu.org/2016/08/31/vaccine-glyphosate-link/](http://www.tonu.org/2016/08/31/vaccine-glyphosate-link/)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-07T04:18:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/monsanto-is-a-problem-for-human-health-that-is-now-spreading-to-europe.18385/post-201021>

So my last post was about Round up, a herbicide. This chemical can affect your ability to think and your immune system and muscle sketelal system to work. If your immune system is faulty it set you up for many communicable diseases. These diseases are often treated with vaccines. Do you know who makes round up? Monsanto. Do you know who helps makes vaccines? Monsanto. Do you know who supports the FDA and the CDC with $$$$? Do you know the former FDA head under Obama is now working for Monsanto with Obama/Clinton blessings? Do you know that Monsanto is a major donor to the CLinton campaign? Now I'd like you to look into this. The CDC and Monsanto have some deep corporate ties. Do your own due diligence and make up you own mind what this means for you and for your family. Just when we realized the threat with California's SB277. Could they be going for the throat here? They could be but there is some debate about this if you carefully read the government's public wording. The article below referenced by name a 15 August 2016 Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (NPRM) document published to the Federal Register pertaining to updated rule changes proposed by the Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS). That document was prefaced with an section explaining that changes to quarantine regulations were being proposed due to recent outbreaks of communicable diseases (such as Ebola, MERS, and measles) around the world. Through this Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (NPRM), the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) in the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) is amending its domestic (interstate) and foreign quarantine regulations to best protect the public health of the United States. These amendments are being proposed to aid public health responses to outbreaks of communicable diseases such as the largest recorded outbreak of Ebola virus disease (Ebola) in history, the recent outbreak of Middle East Respiratory Syndrome (MERS) in South Korea, and repeated outbreaks and responses to measles in the United States, as well as the ongoing threat of other new or re-emerging communicable diseases. The provisions contained herein provide additional clarity to various safeguards to prevent the importation and spread of communicable diseases affecting human health into the United States and interstate.
In a section explaining the rationale behind the NPRM, the DHHS specifically stated that it included due process provisions to ensure that individual civil liberties would be well-balanced against proposed measures to protect public health:
This NPRM contains due process provisions (requirements relating to administrative records, quarantine, isolation, conditional release, medical examination, and agreements; authorization for payment for medical care and treatment; and an explanation of applicable criminal penalties) which are intended to inform the U.S. public of what steps HHS/CDC might take to protect public health during an outbreak while safeguarding the rights of the individual. Although these processes have been implemented through internal standard operating procedures, these procedures have not been codified, explicitly set forth in regulation, and made publicly available until today. These provisions are needed to provide transparency and assure the traveling public and any individual potentially placed under a Federal public health order that HHS/CDC will protect their individual liberties.

The provisions in this NPRM describe the regulatory activities that HHS/CDC may undertake to reduce and mitigate the risk of outbreaks of Ebola, MERS, measles, and other communicable diseases in the United States. Greater transparency and public understanding of its processes, authorities, and procedures, will allow HHS/CDC to respond more effectively to these public health emergencies. So who has the power to declare a state of emergency with respect to communicable disease folks? The president and the CDC is the short answer. This wording in the Federal register has me a bit concerned considering the CDC is under Congressional control and the congress has yet to deal with a very public whistle blower lawsuit regarding communicable disease in our countyr. To me, it appears that the CDC/President might both be of the opinion to protect everyone in our country, your right to informed consent might not paramount and important when the public good is at stake. It appears to me from their words above they'd like to see an exemption in writing in place at some future time to throw informed consent out the window with respect to therpies RELATED TO communicable disease TIED TO THE PUBLIC GOOD. This is very vague and very slick wording for my taste. This is being proposed in the federal government now: can you tell me how much freedom and liberty you really believe you possess today? It is important because we have elections pending where TRUTH TELLING is the most important issue on the table with repect to health. In case you do not know, the CDC has been recently exposed for fraud by one of their own scientists named Dr. William Thompson. He filed a federal whistle blower lawsuit two years ago one 5 leading scientists in the CDC tied to one of Monsanto's older products designed to PROTECT the PUBLIC. You should get informed about these connections! Roundup is one problem but there are several others you need to be aware of too.

I follow the Federal register closely because it affects my profession in a large way. When clinicians see vague proposals of new Federal rules, by Executive Order of the President I worry about the separation of powers set forth in the Constitution. Many alarmists will see this as act of desperation, but I view it is as an awakening for a public who is asleep at the wheel. My concern stems from the fact that two states have already enacted this type of regulation without any public discourse. California and Mississippi are those states, in case you were wondering. These are precisely the reasons Thomas Jefferson argued that the 2nd Amendment was needed and had to be second amendment and not tenth. It was too important to be lower on the list of ammendments of the original framework of this country. He viewed protection against tyranny very high up in the list of things a government is capable of considering what Britain did to colonist in the 1780's. The second amendment is being attacked right now by the Obama/Clinton ticket. I find it disturbing that both Obama and Clinton have deep ties to Monsanto and people running the CDC. FYI: Hillary Clinton is in full support of the assault on your second amendment right and that will be decided in 65 days. You have choices that are in opposition to her. In fact, you have three choices, beside her to be clear. Consider this post from the Federal register to understand what the CDC under Obama and Clinton really mean for your health. That is all. Carry On. The link below is alarmists in nature........and many will say it is untrue but if you look at the facts as I presented them above and see the EXACT words I took from the Federal register you might see why folks are worried. [http://www.march-against-monsanto.c...tee-working-to-force-vaccinate-all-americans/](http://www.march-against-monsanto.com/cdc-quarantine-committee-working-to-force-vaccinate-all-americans/)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-07T05:27:17+0300

Semiconductors change the direction of light's electric field to alter the polarization of light to optimized cell function. Since cells are filled with proteins, do proteins have some specific stereochemistry associated with them to take advantage of polarized light? Aren't all amino acids chiral? Yes they are. Isn't one achiral? Yes, glycine is achiral. Could this be how the quantum mechanism of evolution works that I mentioned in the OSF 3 blog?

**THE QUANTUM MECHANISM**

An important step in the central dogma of modern biology (DNA encodes RNA encodes protein-RL) is the folding of the peptide to form the protein, which can then become biologically activated and contribute as a component of the "electrostatic attractor". This electrostatic attractor links to energy production or lack there of in a cell. Energy production is linked to mitochondrial function and the exclusion zone of water in the cytosol. This mechanism can eventually lead to the phenotype change of the protein, the gene, the cell, the tissue, or the organism very quickly. Modern science already knows the first two folds are determined by DNA code itself (and gave a Noble Prize for that discovery). They, however, have no idea precisely how the last two folds occur. I believe I might. The last two folds are 100% determined by the redox state the cell finds itself in. This is why so many proteins, like melatonin, oxytocin, and serotonin, can have so many varied actions, because the redox state is dynamic and fluctuates with the charges around proteins. All proteins are hydrated. This means that the exclusion zone of water is likely a critcal piece of this quantum mechanism of nature. I believe the quantum mechanism of evolution is akin to the "condition of existence" in Darwin's theory of evolution.

This might confuses neurobiologists and evolutionary biologists, because they do not understand the bio-physics of water nor do they understand the power and range of light. The cellular environment determines the protein's possible tertiary and quaternary folding with precision by bringing together charged portions of the amino acids after they are made from DNA and RNA. All moving charges are affected by the wave function of electromagnetic radiation. Since light is a form of electromagnetic radiation, this mechanism has a sensitive and specific quantum controlling lever for substrates of biochemistry in cells. Every human cell has 100,000 chemical reactions occurring per second. It seems like an incredible task until you realize the one octave of the electromagnetic spectrum that falls to Earth to interact with our cells has 8,683,317,618,811,886,495,518,194,401,280,000,000 different frequencies of light between 260nm -700nm. One frequency of light can control the individual atoms in a protein to program them with redox infromation, and those atoms make up the amino acids that make up proteins with their triplet code, which then can be controlled by other frequences that determine the last two folds in proteins to determine protein phenotype and ultimate physiologic function in a cell.

A proteins final bend eventually leads to their function and phenotype just like [the bends in an antenna](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Evolved_antenna) control how it works. This belies how light utilizes the conditions of existence to set the table for natural selection to begins in nature. Natural selection by itself cannot explain life. It only describes morphologic aspects of life. It is electronic induction of protein side chains by the movement of electrons and protons in the hydration shells around proteins where life begins to solve the problem the Second Law of Thermodynamics presents.

If the charges are altered along the back bone of the amino acids for any reason at all in the endoplasmic reticulum or Golgi apparatus, _proteins mis-fold_ and a new emergent protein shows up as a result. Light is capable of altering the charge of any atom, gene, or protein because light can program atoms. Atoms are the most simple quantum in the universe and light is capable of altering a quantum dots energy or information profile in many linear and non linear ways most fail to realize. Light is also without mass so it has the innate ability to move quickly in any media.
_**
The movement of these small subatomic particles (by addition or subtraction) leads to massive changes in potential energies of these new emergent proteins.**_ Because energies are massively altered by light, and light can alter charge and affect energy density, light is fully capable of altering function of atoms, amino acids, proteins, genes and the phenotype of a tissue or organism. Just about anything can be made from light because light can be altered in many ways as well. This idea is buried in mass equivalence. Energy and matter are equivalent in this equation. The environment energy is in determines the matter it will become. These changes can be dramatic and rapid on timescales when there are many subatomic particles involved in the carrying of energy without much mass. This is why the conditions of existence are far more powerful than natural selection and why Darwin theory leaves a lot to be desired from a biologic perspective. It turns out that electrons, protons, neutrons are matter than photons can become. Paul Dirac told us light can become matter in 1931.

When a protein's energy is altered for any reason, we see species variation in morphology. Just think about how many light frequencies are present in the one octave of light's spectrum that falls to Earth. The number is staggering and that number can alter atoms in proteins in very sensitive and specific ways that the environment signals by the Earth's new specific resonance as time marches on. This is how evolution really operates by coherent design. This is how natural selection and conditions of existence merge in the new theory of quantum evolutionary biology. This is the smallest stage possible for life to have begun to organize.

> The provisions in this NPRM describe the regulatory activities that HHS/CDC may undertake to reduce and mitigate the risk of outbreaks of Ebola, MERS, measles, and other communicable diseases in the United States. Greater transparency and public understanding of its processes, authorities, and procedures, will allow HHS/CDC to respond more effectively to these public health emergencies. So who has the power to declare a state of emergency with respect to communicable disease folks? The president and the CDC is the short answer. This wording in the Federal register has me a bit concerned considering the CDC is under Congressional control and the congress has yet to deal with a very public whistle blower lawsuit regarding communicable disease in our countyr. To me, it appears that the CDC/President might both be of the opinion to protect everyone in our country, your right to informed consent might not paramount and important when the public good is at stake. It appears to me from their words above they'd like to see an exemption in writing in place at some future time to throw informed consent out the window with respect to therpies RELATED TO communicable disease TIED TO THE PUBLIC GOOD. This is very vague and very slick wording for my taste. This is being proposed in the federal government now: can you tell me how much freedom and liberty you really believe you possess today? It is important because we have elections pending where TRUTH TELLING is the most important issue on the table with repect to health. In case you do not know, the CDC has been recently exposed for fraud by one of their own scientists named Dr. William Thompson. He filed a federal whistle blower lawsuit two years ago one 5 leading scientists in the CDC tied to one of Monsanto's older products designed to PROTECT the PUBLIC. You should get informed about these connections! Roundup is one problem but there are several others you need to be aware of too.
>
> I follow the Federal register closely because it affects my profession in a large way. When clinicians see vague proposals of new Federal rules, by Executive Order of the President I worry about the separation of powers set forth in the Constitution. Many alarmists will see this as act of desperation, but I view it is as an awakening for a public who is asleep at the wheel. My concern stems from the fact that two states have already enacted this type of regulation without any public discourse. California and Mississippi are those states, in case you were wondering. These are precisely the reasons Thomas Jefferson argued that the 2nd Amendment was needed and had to be second amendment and not tenth. It was too important to be lower on the list of ammendments of the original framework of this country. He viewed protection against tyranny very high up in the list of things a government is capable of considering what Britain did to colonist in the 1780's. The second amendment is being attacked right now by the Obama/Clinton ticket. I find it disturbing that both Obama and Clinton have deep ties to Monsanto and people running the CDC. FYI: Hillary Clinton is in full support of the assault on your second amendment right and that will be decided in 65 days. You have choices that are in opposition to her. In fact, you have three choices, beside her to be clear. Consider this post from the Federal register to understand what the CDC under Obama and Clinton really mean for your health. That is all. Carry On. ### Klee — 2016-09-08T02:22:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello.18391/post-201071>

Hi, I'm Krista from Canada.

I have recently read Dr. Kruse's book and I'm hoping to learn even more here in a great effort to feel better.

I am 48 years old and was diagnosed with spinal stenosis in my C6, C7 two years ago. I've been off work since then.
Recently I have had various other health issues. I am developing allergic reactions following eating or drinking anything that has sulphites, actually any preservatives. Last week, embarrassingly, I ate a hotdog, while camping, and I ended up breaking out in hives nearly everywhere. My doctor has since prescribed me an Epi-pen and I am scheduled for allergy testing tomorrow.
As well, I don't know if I am developing hypothyroidism or Hashimoto's but it runs in my family. I am continually gaining weight, even though I eat minimally and well, and I've noticed my energy level shift down to the most minimal it has ever been. I am also losing hair and have very dry skin. Every time I get my blood levels checked, my Doctor says they are within the range, but I don't know.
I broke into tears today because I am overwhelmed, mostly with these allergies. I've had hives all day today and my skin feels like it is burning from the inside.
I guess I feel like I am falling apart.

Wow. I guess I am holding a lot of worries in, as it seems to have erupted here in my intro.

Other than all of that I am a happy, Canadian woman, a writer, a hiker, a lover of nature. I also love to travel but most of all I want my health back and that is why I am here.

ETA: I am so new to this I know nothing about the acronyms and need a crash course. Is there a link for this?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/point-8-on-redox-rx-ldl-cholesterol-above-200.18392/post-201083>

> 8\. **When LDL cholesterol goes below 200, your redox potential usually is low.** This is also a sign that you likely are lacking that ability to store infrared spectrum light within your cells and their water micelles. Cholesterol is a polar molecule designed to be surrounded by water and energized by its ability to transfer electrons to the cholesterol. If it is not in a state of reduction, cholesterol can open the “doors to hell” inside your circulatory system. **When your redox potential is high, DHA is high and cholesterol becomes sulfated and becomes a life-saving protein.**

Is this meant to be correct?

I have been told my ldl is extremely high at at 200-215 from my last 2 tests.

If ones redox potential increases should their LDL go up as well?

I am not overly worried about my LDL numbers as they are mainly larger particles from an old particle test.

just interesting reading LDL needing to be over 200 seems high to me

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-08T05:50:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/point-8-on-redox-rx-ldl-cholesterol-above-200.18392/post-201090>

1\. 100%
2\. LDL is high because your cells are stressed and the first thing a cell does is raise LDL cholesterol to make steroid hormones to respond. Steroids like Vitamin D3. Different solar spectrums are needed to complete the photoelectric catalysis to biochemical substrates for action. So a rising LDL means you lack the sunlight to power the change.
3\. Answered above in 2.
4\. I don't worry about numbers I worry about trends in labs. They are far more illuminating.
5\. LDL over 200 30 yrs ago was a nothing clinical event. Today it makes the doctor react with a Rx pad and the patient is left feeling if I don't take this statin my doctor thinks I will die or become injured. Since I gave up my power to know because I allowed my instincts to be usurped. You must regain that power.......by becoming informed why your doctors have been systematically misinformed by a paradigm that is broken and up for sale to the highest bidder. # Thread: Am I heretic for eating legumes & avoiding saturated fats?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-heretic-for-eating-legumes-avoiding-saturated-fats.18393/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mmarston — 2016-09-08T06:23:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-heretic-for-eating-legumes-avoiding-saturated-fats.18393/post-201092>

Hi all! I'm determined to heal my son's food allergies and eczema. I recently found Dr. Kruse's website while trying to find answers for my son's issues. Subsequently I've read the 'Epi-Paleo Rx', which I found quite intriguing especially since I love biology & physics (my degree is in environmental engineering). However, now I'm pretty confused on two major points: legumes & saturated fats. For the past year, I've been working with Karen Hurd (aka 'The Bean Queen') who essentially recommends a low-carb diet with good proteins, veggies, fats (but NOT palm/coconut oil), veggies, and of course LEGUMES. Her story is very inspiring - over 20 years ago, she saved her 18-old month daughter's life by teaching herself how to detox her family from pesticide poisoning. You can check her story out here: [http://www.karenhurd.com/thegreendragon.html](http://www.karenhurd.com/thegreendragon.html). Long story short, Karen believes eating _soluble_ fiber on a _daily_ basis (legumes naturally have the highest amounts of soluble fiber) is essential to eliminating fat-soluble & metabolic waste (including allergens, pesticides, toxins, etc.) in our bodies via the 'Enterohepatic Recirculation' pathway. Here's a link to her PhD research paper on this topic: [http://www.karenhurd.com/kh-assets/kh-textfiles/EnterohepaticRecirculation.pdf](http://www.karenhurd.com/kh-assets/kh-textfiles/EnterohepaticRecirculation.pdf). As I mentioned above, Karen also recommends NOT eating coconut oil, palm oils or saturated fats in general because when the chemical bonds are broken to utilize these fats, they cause significant free radical damage of DNA (basically the 'angry' Carbon damages the DNA when trying to capture electrons). Please help me understand the pros & cons of legumes & saturated fats! Otherwise, I think Dr. Kruse's protocols sound pretty sensible. Thanks in advance for reading my lengthy intro!! Michele

### caroline — 2016-09-08T07:54:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-heretic-for-eating-legumes-avoiding-saturated-fats.18393/post-201093>

Welcome..

Hi Caroline, Thanks for your suggestions! I have read Dr Kruse's book and have listened to many of his podcasts/videos. I will definitely get John Ott's book. As for my history, I grew up in MA with my mom, step-father and 5 siblings. For the past 20+ years now, I have lived in GA (30075). My mom grew up in Nova Scotia in a small fishing village - her dad was a lobsterman. Anecdotally, my mom and siblings/cousins are all on the intelligent side (PhDs, Drs, etc.) and now that I'm learning about Dr Kruse's ideas I suspect they had the advantage of lots of DHA in their diet! On my mom's side, my grandmother is the offspring of parents from Ireland (hence my mom and I are fair with freckles) and my paternal grandfather's parents were from France (his dad was a a ship builder who sadly died young from overexposure to lead-based paint). On my dad's side, his parents' heritage is English & Finnish. I will put this all and more details in my journal.

### JanSz — 2016-09-08T17:34:04+0300

> [mmarston said:](/goto/post?id=201092)

> Hi all! I'm determined to heal my son's food allergies and eczema. I recently found Dr. Kruse's website while trying to find answers for my son's issues. Subsequently I've read the 'Epi-Paleo Rx', which I found quite intriguing especially since I love biology & physics (my degree is in environmental engineering). However, now I'm pretty confused on two major points: legumes & saturated fats. For the past year, I've been working with Karen Hurd (aka 'The Bean Queen') who essentially recommends a low-carb diet with good proteins, veggies, fats (but NOT palm/coconut oil), veggies, and of course LEGUMES. Her story is very inspiring - over 20 years ago, she saved her 18-old month daughter's life by teaching herself how to detox her family from pesticide poisoning. You can check her story out here: [http://www.karenhurd.com/thegreendragon.html](http://www.karenhurd.com/thegreendragon.html). Long story short, Karen believes eating _soluble_ fiber on a _daily_ basis (legumes naturally have the highest amounts of soluble fiber) is essential to eliminating fat-soluble & metabolic waste (including allergens, pesticides, toxins, etc.) in our bodies via the 'Enterohepatic Recirculation' pathway. Here's a link to her PhD research paper on this topic: [http://www.karenhurd.com/kh-assets/kh-textfiles/EnterohepaticRecirculation.pdf](http://www.karenhurd.com/kh-assets/kh-textfiles/EnterohepaticRecirculation.pdf). **As I mentioned above, Karen also recommends NOT eating coconut oil, palm oils or saturated fats in general because when the chemical bonds are broken to utilize these fats, they cause significant free radical damage of DNA (basically the 'angry' Carbon damages the DNA when trying to capture electrons). Please help me understand the pros & cons of legumes & saturated fats!** Otherwise, I think Dr. Kruse's protocols sound pretty sensible. Thanks in advance for reading my lengthy intro!! Michele

### caroline — 2016-09-09T01:10:35+0300

### Eugenia — 2016-09-09T02:29:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-heretic-for-eating-legumes-avoiding-saturated-fats.18393/post-201159>

My experience with legumes and diet: I had to change things around to feel healthy again, by trying things out. I started Paleo in 2011, and started the Epi-Paleo protocol a few years later. I'm sorry to say that while most of my health problems are now largely in check, none of the two Paleo versions made me lose weight (which means that I'm still inflamed somehow). I seem to just not be able to fix my metabolic syndrome, and that includes buying a new house away from the Bay Area with more space between houses, all technology is shoved away at the other side of the house away from the living/sleeping area, waking up early, sleeping enough, seeing the sun more, fluoride-free water etc. etc.

What have started to change things around for me (I'm still in the beginning of this, so results are still coming in) is adding more carbs (including in the form of legumes and white rice), and I stopped eating land meat altogether (I still eat lots of seafood). I now follow a _high-raw pesco-vegetarian diet_ that is otherwise epi-paleo (lots of seafood for one, plus the rest of the lifestyle protocols, and of course, the standard stuff: no pseudograins or grains apart from rice, no sugar (not even artificial ones), no soy apart from GF tamari, no industrial seed oils). I've lost 4 lbs in 2 weeks: slowly, but surely.

I got my 23andme results a few years back, and it said that my APoE was good, so I kept eating fats as the good Paleo girl that I was. However, I didn't notice that another genetic variant said that I can't handle saturated fats, and that I'd likely be obese if I'd consume them (this is not true for everyone btw, not everyone is built the same way). I only found out about it a few months ago, so I decided to change around my diet towards a lower-than-Paleo fat diet (NOT low fat, but simply, a moderate amount).

So, now I drink a fruit+greens smoothie with home-made goat kefir for breakfast. Maybe 1 boiled pastured egg too if I feel like it (not fried).
I have a big raw salad with veggies for lunch, with some yogurt or pesto or tahini or nut dip. Some fruit too.
More fruits for snacks, less often (soaked) nuts.
Dinner is cooked: legumes twice a week, rice once a week, various veggies the rest of the week -- all that, usually along with seafood. Some feta goat/sheep cheese, or real yogurt. More fruit.
Additionally on Sundays: some raw dessert (using raw honey).

Overall, I'd say that depending on the day, I consume anywhere between 120 gr - 180 gr of net carbs. Not low carb per se, but definitely much lower than the standard American diet of 250-350 gr. Basically, half.

So far, I feel better, and I now get my crazy celiac-like autoimmune response (out of the blue, and without gluten contamination) only once every 8 days or so, instead of once every ~4 days that I did on regular Paleo (pre-Paleo it would be 4-5 times \*a day\*). Still in the beginning of the new "pegan" journey, I hope things get even better.

I have to mention though, that I don't react to legumes like other people might do. My ancestry is 99% Balkan going back many generations (confirmed by 23andme), and legumes there are a staple. It seems that genetically I'm well-adjusted to legumes and higher-carb. Might not be as good for others though, especially if from Northern ancestry.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-09T04:40:39+0300

Balkans/Coordinates

### Eugenia — 2016-09-09T05:24:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-heretic-for-eating-legumes-avoiding-saturated-fats.18393/post-201162>

Consider that I'm from Greece mostly, so the coordinates would be even lower in my case.

In reality, the diet I follow now is that of my ancestors (minus the wheat). Because of the type of religious fasting that the Greek Orthodox church was imposing to its believers depending on the calendar (no-animal products type of fasting), Greeks were traditionally vegan ~30% of the time (as an average), 55% of the time vegetarian, 10% pesco-vegetarian, and only about 5% of the time land meat eaters. Without taking the vegan fasting into account, they were predominantly vegetarian by their own will, because they were slaughtering their (numerous) animals very rarely. Given the realities today (terrible wheat, non-native EMF), slightly modifying my ancestral diet by adding more seafood and removing almost all grains, seems to have helped me, even if I live in hot California now and not in Greece anymore.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-09T06:00:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-heretic-for-eating-legumes-avoiding-saturated-fats.18393/post-201165>

But California allows UV interference......hence why you are stuggling with what you are. People forget what population density ,eans to mitochondrial energy flows once heteroplasmy has already manifest

### Eugenia — 2016-09-09T17:09:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/am-i-heretic-for-eating-legumes-avoiding-saturated-fats.18393/post-201193>

I now live in a small CA town, not in the bay area. I'm sure there's plenty of UV and openness here. If that doesn't help either, then there is not much I can do. My husband won't move to Costa Rica or something (or back to broke Greece for that matter). After retirement, we're thinking of moving to Oregon. Oregon has less sun, but it has a healthier mindset on average. If nothing works, then maybe it's time to give up and stop resisting fate. If anything, I found paleo exactly when I stopped resisting the last time around. I was visiting my mom in Greece, when I was sure it was my last year that I'd be alive (I was that sick, and had tried all standard medical avenues). I was there for my goodbyes. A few days later, paleo came into my life. I don't think this was a coincidence. It only arrived when I had given up. Maybe that's what's in store for me in this life. Learning to let go. Or maybe not.

Hi Eugenia! Thank you for sharing your story. It is very helpful to know that there is definitely not just one perfect diet for everyone. You mentioned you feel that you are still struggling to reduce inflammation. I think it would be worth listening to some of Karen Hurd podcasts. Her typical recommendation healthy adults is to eat 1/2 c legumes per meal (or 3X's per day) mainly to get rid of normal metabolic waste. When dealing with more chronic health issues (gallbladder disease, heartburn, hormonal imbalances, allergies, etc....basically anything due to accumulation of fat-soluble waste in the bile), she recommends eating small quantities (couple of bites to 1/4 c) of legumes 6-8X's per day. Here's a link to Karen Hurd's podcast on how she saved her baby daughter's life from pesticide poisoning: [http://podcast.wwib.com/2016/05/5-25-16-karen-hurds-emotional-story-of.html?m=1](http://podcast.wwib.com/2016/05/5-25-16-karen-hurds-emotional-story-of.html?m=1). And here's a link to more of her podcasts: [http://www.karenhurd.com/podcasts.html](http://www.karenhurd.com/podcasts.html). Keep me posted! Best, Michele

# Thread: Trying to find the energy to go!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-find-the-energy-to-go.18394/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Angelarose82 — 2016-09-08T08:29:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-find-the-energy-to-go.18394/post-201094>

Currently, I am 34 years old, 182 lbs at 5'8''. I have lived in Sri Lanka for 7 years. We live close to the beach and right on the edge of a small town.

My best guess of what is going on is that I have Fibromyalgia, with chronic fatigue. My mother and mothers' mother have Fibromyalgia. I have had arthritis like pain since I was a teenager. Aversion to cold. I constantly had cold feet until January 2016. Before Keto, I had food cravings, sugar cravings, and daily episodes of shaking and sweating, worse if I was late to a meal. I still get hot and feelings of low blood sugar when it is near meal time. Foggy brain is still a problem though much improved since I started the Leptin Reset. I still have days where I have bad coordination, foggy brain, high fatigue, and heart palpitations.

Obviously leptin resistant, probably since childhood. Typical SAD.

I have delayed onset muscle soreness (DOMS). Over the last few years it has grown progressively worse. If I exercise 24 hours later I will be in sever pain. Headache and tension from heel to head on the back side of my body. Last about 4 days. Typically I can do very little to relieve the pain. I use to use Ibuprofen 800mg up to 3 times a day. I have quit Ibuprofen. Every few weeks I test my DOMS by completing 5 squats. Last week my pain was significantly reduced and still lasted 4 days.

I have never had an official diagnosis but a acupuncturist once told me that I seemed like I might have PCOS. I have many of the symptoms. With the onset of puberty at 11, I have had heart palpitations, mood issues, facial hair (on chin), painful heavy menstruation, acne in teen years, and headaches that are like clock work. I have had weight issues since I was 19. Reoccurring infections on my thighs. Spider veins on my legs starting at 16.

I was in the Army Reserve from 1999-2006. Weight issues became prominent early on through a combination of excessive exercise and poor nutrition. From ages 19-22 my military career was dominate by my failure to lose weight. I finally lost weight during duty in Afghanistan by restricting my food intake to only Trader Joes food boxes that my mother sent me and exercising twice daily. At that time I was taking about 2-3 grams of Ibuprofen a day for my chronic back pain. Upon returning to the real world my weight slowly returned.

I developed depression and suicidal thoughts that subsequently led me to seek out a psychologist and then a psychiatrist. I tried many pills and settled on Lexapro (SSRI) and was on it for a year. Frankly, what it did was make feel neutral. I had no emotions. Shortly after getting off Lexapro I fired my psychiatrist. I then changed therapist. Mood issues have dominated my life since puberty. My belief was that I was hormonally imbalanced. No doctor gave me any help. I was "normal" and just fat (eat less and exercise more). Thanks to Dr. Kruse I can see that I was and still am in fact hormonally imbalanced. By tracking my months I can see that I typically (but not consistently) have mood issues (anti-social behavior, fear, anger, disordered thinking, fatigue) during 2 weeks of the month. For years I have tried to change my thinking and moderate my behavior with short lived success. If I could manage to make it through a menstruation without freaking out, I would have a breakdown the following week. I learned to warn my husband of my impending emotional craziness and we could manage to get through my monthly sessions with less stress to him and our relationship.

About 6 years ago, I had my gallbladder removed because I had stones. Interestingly enough, I went to the doctor because I had pain and indigestion so badly. The indigestion did not resolve till I changed over to a low carb diet. I have a DVD of the surgery and I think I can see some liver damage. About 3 years ago, I had mononucleosis and a full body rash. No doctor could tell me what the rash was. I was completely red all over my body and I felt like I was swollen. After two days my skin was very itchy. All in all it took two weeks for the rash to resolve. After the mono I was tired all the time. My belly fat expanded quickly after this point and the rest of my body swelled in concert regardless of my nutritional and exercise efforts.

Depression deepened and suicidal thoughts returned. I was tiring more quickly and my mood swings were including more scary swings of intense anger. In October of 2014, I started getting sores on my legs that would not heal. Antibiotics helped some but they were small persistent wounds.

I started a ketogenic diet based on "Low Carbohydrate Living" and "The New Atkins for a New You" in December 2015. After 8 months I lost 50 lbs (starting weight was 240 lbs) and I was in bed for 80% of the time. Almost immediately, I felt relief from low level chronic pain that has been so apart of my life that I didn't even realize that it was constant and having such a dramatic affect on my mood. I thought with weight loss I would begin to feel some energy but it never happened. I started looking through YouTube videos looking for something more and that is when I discovered Dr. Jack Kruse. I am currently on the third reading of "Epi-Paleo RX…". Reading the blogs at a slow pace. On week 5 of Leptin Reset.

My first few days of Leptin Reset I developed a sun rash on my forearms. On week three, I tried to do some early morning stretching in the sun and I developed a rash over 50% of my body excluding my arms. Very itchy. The rash seemed like two types of rash. Part was like the sun rash and the other was like the Mono rash. I used ice packs to try and treat the itchiness. I would pick the worst itching places and ice for as long as possible. At night I would sleep with the ice pack. I was sleeping very hot on my skin but also feeling cold inside. I tried using ice water on my face but I find that brain freeze is limiting my ability to stay submerged.

Seafood is now my number one go to food. I found the information about iodine very helpful and the result is less pain. Shrimp, squid, crab, fish, fish eggs, and seaweed are daily foods. I just started taking D3 and K2. I take time release vitamin C 1 gram am and pm. Omega 3 fish oils. Also taking an iron free multivitamin. Besides fish and seaweed, I try to eat brazil nuts and/or pumpkin seeds (I am not fond of nuts) for magnesium. I also eat green vegetables like broccoli and brussel sprouts, as well as a wild picked spinach (I think it is a pseudo spinach). Espresso twice a day and green tea. I eat three meals a day without snacking. BAB is mainly eggs with cream and butter plus fish eggs or cod liver or shrimp or left over crab. Lunch is usually fish or crab or shrimp depending on the catch of the day. Dinner is either seafood or meat. All meat is grass-fed here except some pigs but we only buy small feral pigs if we want pork. Beef we prefer older cows 6+ years. Goat is available. No chicken. Chicken eggs till my ducklings start laying eggs.

Questions

Can I drink green tea anytime of day? I don't add anything to the tea.

Should I use the D3 (5000IU) twice a day till I no longer get skin rashes?

With the Vitamin K2 (Mk-4 (as menatetrenone) 500mcg and MK-7 (as menaquinone-7) 100mcg) do I take it only once a day and does it matter what time of day?

With the Omega 3 fish oils 300mg ,can I take this to increase my Omega 3's toward 4g? Right now I am just over 2g a day with diet.

With CT does it do me any damage to do the ice water on the face if I get brain freeze within two minutes?

Can I do full body CT to treat my full body rashes when they recur?

In addition, my husband was diagnosed as type 2 diabetic in November of 2015. We like to think that his getting diagnosed was the best thing that ever happen for us because our lives are so improved. My husband has had fungus problems first dental with sinus infections and a infection in his armpit. The tooth is dead (root canal) and the sinus infection comes and goes weekly. He uses nasal wash daily. He uses antifungal cream on his under arm. Can we contaminate each other with fungus? Twice a year with the rain fungus is visible spreading over everything - how do we protect ourselves?

With regard to grounding outside, I am worried about getting worm infections because this place is very alive. We have two dogs and they have to be treated for worms every month. Deworming is basically poison and generally makes me feel ill (our dogs also feel sick) is it necessary for me to deworm every month?

We recently remodeled our bedroom and it now resembles a cave with one small window facing the rising sun. New AC. New bed. Well insulated from the heat.

When I do extra activities or when we travel to the main city (two hours both ways plus time at location) I have more heart palpitations.

I am taking birth control. I know this is an issue but my stress from thinking I might get pregnant is at this moment quite great. I have still to get the labs done. I have to work with a friend who is a doctor here to write the prescription. Our doctor friend freely admits that he is incapable of understanding. He still thinks my husbands blood sugar will eventually be high again and insists that my husband eat fruit for his health.

I realize the labs are important and I will work to get them done.

I am grateful for all the information provided by Dr. Kruse and by members of this forum for their invaluable tips.

### caroline — 2016-09-08T09:45:27+0300

### Angelarose82 — 2016-09-08T13:01:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-find-the-energy-to-go.18394/post-201103>

Thank you Caroline.
Mother - to what I know- has fibromyalgia. chronic dieter. never obese. Emotional problems.
Mother's mother - 80 years old, fibromyalgia, prediabetic, low potassium, she take a lot of pills. Slim most her life until after 60s where she grew a belly and lost it and currently has the belly. Depression.
mother's father - died at 80. I am unaware of problems he had. His death followed a bad fall.
Father -71, Diabetic, As a veteran he has been diagnosed through the VA system as having bipolar, schizophrenia, Hep. C. - He doesn't take any pills. Hep C is not active. History of one stroke.
Father's mother die at 100 - to my knowledge she had cataracts and was quite round in body.
My half brother (same mother)- suffers from depression, suicidal thoughts, obese, SAD, chronic dieter, chronic pain (self medicates with pot).

I will figure out what NNemf means and get back to you.

I have blue blockers. Just got them in the mail a couple of days ago. I have very sensitive eyes and light just hurts but the glasses really help relieve the pain in my eyes. Especially from computer use.

Our bedroom still has a TV and AC, that is it. Our house is made of concrete and brick.

I will have to look up the rest and educate myself.

Thank you for your help.

### Angelarose82 — 2016-09-09T13:49:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-find-the-energy-to-go.18394/post-201181>

My sun rash is back. I was in the sun for less than 5 minutes attending to some farm animals and by evening I see some puffiness on my arms. My skin is also itchy. Scalp, forearms, lower back, high hip, and front on my lower legs are all itchy. My face is a bit red. This is like my other rash I had two weeks ago but not as bad. Yet. My mood is irritable and I feel stressed about everything. This usually leaves me feeling as though I should withdraw from socializing or interacting only to save everyone (including myself) from the emotional fire that is burning. It almost seems like the skin is bubbling up from the stress burning inside. I am starting to feel the irritation come on earlier than when I use to notice and it is a warning of the impending inflammation building. I am not sure what I can do to jump on the inflammation except jump into the CT.

I guess I have some reading to do.

Also I remembered that my mother was taking a lot of sleeping pills at one point; my mother's mother has restless leg syndrome and I remember when I was young my grandmother saying that when she took sleeping pills it felt like she was awake and aware of every noise and movement but unable to move. I use to be able to sleep anywhere. In college I would go to class and come home and sleep for 3 hours get up and eat dinner and go back to sleep all night. The last few years I have found that I can rarely take a nap and still go to sleep at bedtime. I have always been an early to bed person. I can take a nap if I feel a illness coming on.

That is all for now. Thank you.

### Angelarose82 — 2016-09-13T07:11:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-find-the-energy-to-go.18394/post-201373>

Yesterday we went to the city and had to eat lunch there. Nothing optimal to eat. I found that last night my skin became more itchy and red and hot. The rash started to spread. I woke up around midnight all itchy and I had to get one of my ice packs for my belly. Maybe the lack of Omega 3 in the meal allowed the inflammation to grow. I started using fresh ground flax seeds (ground up in my Vitamix). I remembered that way back in 2004 or so, I lost weight after a very long struggle, and one of the things that I was eating was flax oil. I had no idea what flax was but my mother sent it to me in Afghanistan and I took some of it every day. Hopefully it will help with the inflammation.

### Angelarose82 — 2016-09-13T18:23:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-find-the-energy-to-go.18394/post-201392>

My rash is full body. Hot and itchy. I am bordering on insanity. I filled a kiddy pool with water and all the ice I could get and only got the temperature to 18 degrees C. Felt relieved from the itching for the time that the pool was cool. Not even close to 12 degrees C goal. But that was all the ice I had.
Showers are not very cold. The weather is tropical and the water is tanked and stored at what ever the temperature is on the day. Best guess is that it never goes below 19 degrees C.
I consume cream and butter and ghee. I don't think I ever had problems with dairy but who knows.

We have one tv (only internet no cable) in our bedroom hooked up through the brick wall to our office where most of our electronics are located. Most everything is wired because my husband hates WiFi. I have a cell phone that I rarely use. I make about one phone call a month. I do read books on my phone if I am away from home. I have a laptop to get the internet.

AC unit is necessary to sleep at night.

I do not swim in the ocean as it is so dangerous here. I've never been so scared of the ocean. The beaches are usually hot and covered in garbage.

### Sue-UK — 2016-09-13T18:41:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-find-the-energy-to-go.18394/post-201393>

Hi

perhaps try with ice "soap"? In the past I've made up a block of ice the size of a bar of soap, stood in the cool shower and "soaped" myself down, just skimming it over my skin surface to avoid ice burn, and flipping the ice to the other hand if my hand got too cold (or use a glove).

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-13T22:57:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-find-the-energy-to-go.18394/post-201405>

Questions

Can I drink green tea anytime of day? I don't add anything to the tea.

Can you? Yes. I would not. During daytime is best

Should I use the D3 (5000IU) twice a day till I no longer get skin rashes?

Depends on context without it hard to say.

With the Vitamin K2 (Mk-4 (as menatetrenone) 500mcg and MK-7 (as menaquinone-7) 100mcg) do I take it only once a day and does it matter what time of day?

If youre using D3 and K2 to raise the D3 faster taking it three times a day is more wise.

With the Omega 3 fish oils 300mg ,can I take this to increase my Omega 3's toward 4g? Right now I am just over 2g a day with diet.
Not a fan of fish oil pills........seafood yes.

With CT does it do me any damage to do the ice water on the face if I get brain freeze within two minutes?
CT will always help. In fact CT ing your rash makes sense.

Can I do full body CT to treat my full body rashes when they recur?
see above

In addition, my husband was diagnosed as type 2 diabetic in November of 2015. We like to think that his getting diagnosed was the best thing that ever happen for us because our lives are so improved. My husband has had fungus problems first dental with sinus infections and a infection in his armpit. The tooth is dead (root canal) and the sinus infection comes and goes weekly. He uses nasal wash daily. He uses antifungal cream on his under arm. Can we contaminate each other with fungus? Twice a year with the rain fungus is visible spreading over everything - how do we protect ourselves?

His diagnosis make me think your environment is BAD with nnEMF. Blue light tops the list. No contamination but you likely share bugs.........and you don't need his right now. Sunlight does wonders for fungal infections FYI

With regard to grounding outside, I am worried about getting worm infections because this place is very alive. We have two dogs and they have to be treated for worms every month. Deworming is basically poison and generally makes me feel ill (our dogs also feel sick) is it necessary for me to deworm every month?

Stop worrying. Ground all the time.

We recently remodeled our bedroom and it now resembles a cave with one small window facing the rising sun. New AC. New bed. Well insulated from the heat.

Bad. Plan to get out when you can. I have a sense the environment your in is the real issue.

When I do extra activities or when we travel to the main city (two hours both ways plus time at location) I have more heart palpitations.

see my last point. You need a trimeter, gaussmeter and maybe good RF detector.

I am taking birth control. I know this is an issue but my stress from thinking I might get pregnant is at this moment quite great. I have still to get the labs done. I have to work with a friend who is a doctor here to write the prescription. Our doctor friend freely admits that he is incapable of understanding. He still thinks my husbands blood sugar will eventually be high again and insists that my husband eat fruit for his health.

Birth control is not good for you considering what you posted. How about rubbers?

I realize the labs are important and I will work to get them done.

Work on your environment first. Hacking it should come before labs in my opinion.

Welcome,

Angelarose82
34 years old, 182 lbs at 5'8''

I have Fibromyalgia, with chronic fatigue. My mother and mothers' mother have Fibromyalgia.

bad light
bad Circadian Rhythm
sunlight replaced with electric light

Sri Lanka Latitude 7.00° N, 8
lots of good sunlight there, use it
spend lots time outside, naked, not inside covered in clothes
no sunscreens, no eyeglasses
you are wearing glasses, minimize that, specially when outside
\================================================================

Post where you are now, say ZIP code or country + latitude

\================================================================
I might have PCOS. I have many of the symptoms. With the onset of puberty at 11, I have had heart palpitations, mood issues, facial hair (on chin), painful heavy menstruation, acne in teen years, and headaches that are like clock work.

low progesterone
low freeT3, (low thyroid + high GI carbohydrate diet)
GI=Glycemic Index

Get over internet:
[https://dutchtest.com/](https://dutchtest.com/)
DUTCH Cycle Mapping & Complete Bundle $599.00

eat selenium & Lugols solution (iodine + potassium Iodide)

after 3-4 months on selenium+Lugol's
get thyroid Panel (TSH, T4, FT4, T3, FT3, RT3)(+antibodies)
if
FT3 is not in upper 1/4 of laboratory range
start thyroid hormone supplementation using (Natural Thyroid Hormone from pigs)
for that to be effective
you will have to get your cortisol and DHEAs (as per DUTCH test) in at least somewhat reasonable levels.
That highly depends on you sun exposure management
sun exposure management= (night=total darkness)+(day starts by looking at sunrise, minimal clothing during day, there must be time in the day when your scrotal area and breasts see sunlight)
Do not
supplement with vit D
but
get tested and then you can asses compliance to light exposure by your vit D level.
aim at
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
include sulfur and carotenoids in your diet
onions, garlic, leeks, MSM
carrots, tomatoes, peppers

With sun light it is about timing and total amount.
You can also get idea by looking at your cortisol profile (included in DUTCH)

\==============================================
Reoccurring infections

check fatty acids
\==============================================
Ibuprofen 800mg up to 3 times a day

that further messes up micronutrients
\==============================================
daily episodes of shaking and sweating, worse if I was late to a meal

low blood sugar, high insulin (insulin resistant)

do not eat
sugar, anything with wheat or other high GI food, fruits
emphasize fat in diet, increase good fat, specially saturated fat
coconut oil, palm oil, butter, beef lard, pork lard, chicken fat, duck fat, olive oil, avocado oil

\==============================================
gallbladder removed
I am taking birth control.
(bad news, you will have to change method of contraception that would not further mess up your hormones)

Our doctor friend freely admits that he is incapable of understanding.

Good news to have friend doctor who admits his shortcomings.
Hopefully he will come thru when you ask him for specific tests and scripts.

\===============================================
Mother's mother - 80 years old..............

Mom can benefit also from dr Kruses wisdom

light
nnEMF
mostly high fat content and low GI diet, but dependent on latitude, altitude, seasons, haplotype,

note,
use of blue-blockers encourages blue light exposure (ie; minimize reason for using them)

sunlight =we evolved in sunlight its timing and frequency content at different times of day and seasons
artificial light does not have this qualities

\===============================================

fish oil

do not eat flax seed or fish/krill oils

you will need fatty acids analysis to figure out your particular needs

Right now I have red, itchy, and puffiness legs, back, stomach, breast, and worst on my arms, neck and face. if I am incapable of eating more fish for omega 3 what are you recommending. I mean that I literally stuff myself with fresh seafood, shrimp, squid, mackerel but my dietary intake still not helping with my skin problems which probably means that I am not absorbing Vit. D through my damaged skin. Plus the itching all night long is enough to drive a person to suicide. my hands just started getting bumps and it hurts and doesn't itch much.
I don't take Ibuprofen anymore.
If I told my family members about this diet they would call me crazy and I would rather not get into an argument with my 80 year old grandmother about the health or lack of health benefits of bread. She doesn't get it and she can argue for weeks on any subject. I don't speak to my mother.
I don't have wounds anymore.

I live in Sri Lanka, I am from Oregon. I can't walk around naked because my neighbors will have me committed plus I already have a full body rash and don't need anymore boiled flesh. Did I mention the itching is driving me crazy. I have been doing CT as best I can. Cold showers are not cold here because it is hot everyday all day.

Most likely all my issues started when I was young and living in Oregon because when I arrived in Sri Lanka 7 years ago I was fat, sick, and depressed already.

Thanks again for spending the time to give me your advice. I will take anything I can get.

**CT will always help. In fact CT ing your rash makes sense.**

see above

In addition, my husband was diagnosed as type 2 diabetic in November of 2015. We like to think that his getting diagnosed was the best thing that ever happen for us because our lives are so improved. My husband has had fungus problems first dental with sinus infections and a infection in his armpit. The tooth is dead (root canal) and the sinus infection comes and goes weekly. He uses nasal wash daily. He uses antifungal cream on his under arm. Can we contaminate each other with fungus? Twice a year with the rain fungus is visible spreading over everything - how do we protect ourselves?

With regard to grounding outside, I am worried about getting worm infections because this place is very alive. We have two dogs and they have to be treated for worms every month. Deworming is basically poison and generally makes me feel ill (our dogs also feel sick) is it necessary for me to deworm every month?

**Stop worrying. Ground all the time.**

We recently remodeled our bedroom and it now resembles a cave with one small window facing the rising sun. New AC. New bed. Well insulated from the heat.

**Bad. Plan to get out when you can**. I have a sense the environment your in is the real issue.

When I do extra activities or when we travel to the main city (two hours both ways plus time at location) I have more heart palpitations.

see my last point. You need a trimeter, gaussmeter and maybe good RF detector.

I am taking birth control. I know this is an issue but my stress from thinking I might get pregnant is at this moment quite great. I have still to get the labs done. I have to work with a friend who is a doctor here to write the prescription. Our doctor friend freely admits that he is incapable of understanding. He still thinks my husbands blood sugar will eventually be high again and insists that my husband eat fruit for his health.

Birth control is not good for you considering what you posted. **How about rubbers?**

I realize the labs are important and I will work to get them done.

Work on your environment first. Hacking it should come before labs in my opinion.\[/quote\]

### thisbirdhaswings — 2016-09-18T17:38:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-find-the-energy-to-go.18394/post-201577>

Per Caroline's earlier question, "Do you turn all that stuff off at night?"...(I skimmed and didn't see an answer, so I'm piping in...) Bedroom over a kitchen (appliance EMF, especially from fridge, which you may not be turning off) is not optimal. Above you? Be aware of above ground power lines, the potential for antennas or cell towers near by. You are generally going to be closer to these influences on a second floor than a ground floor. If you haven't already gotten a meter, you should get one. I use the Cornet ED 88T. It's a great all around device for measuring magnetic field, radio frequencies and electric fields. I had some hidden sources in a former residence that remained even when I turned off the circuit breakers...including unusually high electrical fields from a phone line...a good meter will help you hack your environment; otherwise you are just guessing.

>

Greetings
Itching sucks - I would say up your intake of zinc - to get it to absorb, take it with HCL tablest, or take zinc lozengers (easiest), or take zinc with l-carnosine - zinc is always low in a diabetic - in fact, except for a kid that was a swimmer, everyone I have ever tested was low - this is because zinc is used in the insulin molecule and blue light uses up insulin which tanks zinc with it - I forget which time blog that was in - the fact that you're bloating also means digestion sucketh too - this means stomach acid is low - zinc is required to make stomach acid and also to be absorbed, zinc needs acid - you can see the problem...

Next, I am a huge fan of nettle - it is the absolute bomb for histamine - I once stopped a bee sting in like 5 minutes by popping 2 of them -

the next thing I would also take is quercetin - also great for itching - the sun increases histamine BTW - I forget which blog or forum post that was in but google histamine in the blogs/forums - download flux (getflux.org) as a quick sortof fix to your immediate blue light issues - and get some blue blockers pronto - in fact, I would really step up my light game - like none at night save a campfire or maybe a red incandescant light -

Since zinc is in the toilet, this leads you at risk for h pylori - oysters have crap tons of zinc in them BTW -

a cheap wifi test is - if I recall - get an AM radio - turn it to the lower stations (88.something) and start cruising around - when it gets really staticky, you have high emf hitting the radio - I tried it, it works -

In your baths, add oatmeal - I think it's called an oatmeal sitz bath -

Man, I really feel for ya - Srilanka is probably loaded with mold BTW - my inlaws are from there and they were like - mold is everywhere, so what? It probably used to be so what and now not so much - wifi make bacteria without a cell wall (mold/lyme/yeast) grow like a weed - are you anywhere near where the tsunami hit? I mean, all those houses must be loaded with mold - which would upregulate your immune system - thus the sunshine would have "blown your bucket" -
[https://draxe.com/zinc-deficiency/](https://draxe.com/zinc-deficiency/)
If you do green tea, make sure you let the tea bag soak for 5 minutes then use it with new water - that gets rid of 95% of the flouride -

Doing bone broths with coconut oil would also be great for your inflammation - and it's probably safer to add kelp or other seaweed products than Lugol's as it might be too much for you - coconut oil kills viruses, fungi, bacteria -

### Joe Gavin — 2016-09-08T16:53:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-thread-for-proof-of-quantum-biology-in-living-things.18395/post-201123>

The paper indicates the specific molecule for magnetoreception was unknown at the time. Found this paper that refers to molecule Cry1a, a highly conserved molecule with action in the UV range.

"One additional remarkable aspect is that Cry1a is restricted to the UV/V cones in both chickens and robins. This is in agreement with behavioral data indicating the same type of magnetic compass for both species [\[2\]](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3102070/#pone.0020091-Wiltschko2),[\[3\]](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3102070/#pone.0020091-Wiltschko3). The two species belong to avian lineages that separated already about 95 million years ago in the late Cretaceous [\[35\]](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3102070/#pone.0020091-Ericsen1). Hence our histological data also support the idea of a very early evolution of the avian magnetic compass, which seems to have taken place already in the Mesozoic in the common ancestor of modern birds."

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3102070/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3102070/)

Modern birds all come from ancient therapod dinosaurs.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-08T18:09:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-thread-for-proof-of-quantum-biology-in-living-things.18395/post-201141>

During daylight hours the sun builds a massive battery in cell water and water stores that potential energy for a long period of time as Gerald Pollack has showed. As that charge is built up during day this allows to a decoherence as the cell physical properties allows for decoherence with the environment under the power of the suns waves. They to understand is that in warm environment coherence can be maintained in transient fashion at night. During the absence of sun at night, while using the massive charges stored in cell water called the exclusion zone, cells become able to transiently able to become quantum coherent. This allows for regeneration and the creation of the living state. This implies that sleep was likely the original state that life began and living things evolved wakefulness during daylight hours. How would nocturnal animals have powered their metabolisms and mitochondria? Could it be the varying release of solar neutrino's at night? We know nocturnal animals reversed the diurnal process that uses sunrise and sunset as the major oscillator. Could they also use other formation of cosmic radiation from the Van Allen belts at night? John Ott thought about this possibility. We know IR light is present day and night to affect ATP creation and help yoke their circadian systems with other frequencies of light. What about the other frequencies of light found in the spectrum of light we do not observe. Could they be a source of energy that nocturnal animals use? No matter how improbable this appears to physicists nature laws do not forbid quantum coherence by any know law or math proof, therefore, given enough time nature will find a way. We've got to follow the data. I usually drink 3 - 48oz spring water (one aspirin, one chia seed, and just plain) and 1-32oz sparkling spring water per day. Is that a lot? Probably not. Probably is based on the need, no?

Who or what research said to be "right" amount of water to drink? What is "drink lots of water" mean?

More isn't always better, and many cases, less is more in many areas of life.e.g, food and exercise(?), drugs, material things.

But I guess water, sunlight and magnetism is different? If yes, how so? I'm not so sure why so inquisitive today...Even my dMinder Apps says I get more vitamin D during 20 minutes +/- solar noon than other times. So I get my mooring sun-gazing and then more later in when UVB at 10+ around noon. If this is an effective strategy for the sunlight, how about water and magnetism?

Appreciate you sharing thoughts.

### WalterNL — 2018-05-17T14:07:02+0300

Well, fasting isn't a bad idea (some exceptions, depending on health), but eating should be done between sunrise and sunset. If I'm correct with ramadan, people start eating after sunset, and people stay awake long after sunset to celebrate. That will negatively impact circadian processes in the body.

This problem may be limited if one gets strong sunshine, grounding and CT during the day. And if you only eat epi-paleo type foods after sunset, that would probably be better than eating lots of pastries and such.

# Thread: Learner :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/learner.18400/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Klee — 2016-09-09T00:26:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/learner.18400/post-201153>

Ok, I am not quite sure how to approach this Optimum Journal but here goes.

I am a 48 year old woman who works from home, and has a 14 year old son. I love in Southern Ontario, on the east end of Lake Ontario. I can see the lake from my house and I walk there every day.

Health Concerns: Food sensitivities (nitrites, sufates, lately get hives if I take ibuprofen) Previous issues are C-section surgery, appendix surgery in my early 40's and following this gall bladder removal (I feel I am sunk with this last one.. ) Trying to remain positive. Have experienced low grade depression since my teens managed on and off with antidepressants but currently I have not been on any antidepressant for over three years. I am 155 lbs and 5''3' and I feel uncomfortable at this weight that has slowly edged up over the years, and even though I have tried increasing exercise and eating a paleo diet, I have never succeeded in lowering this weight. Also, in 2012 I was diagnosed with spinal stenosis in my cervical spine. I had to leave my job as I was debilitated for a long time and worked my way back to health and have now decided to work from home. I am a writer. I manage the pain in my neck by trying to eat well, walk daily, get massages and physio. I have been quite successful in keeping the pain at bay.

Sleep: On and off I experience insomnia. Can occur more often when pre-menstrual and often coincides with anxiety then.

I am about to embark on the Epi-paleo Leptin reset but right now I am reading as much as I can first.

Generally we eat grass fed beef, pork, local free-range chicken, lake fish like trout and seafood such as salmon. I also like sardines. I, have been nervous of shrimp lately as I had a hive breakout recently after eating it.

I walk outside at least 30 minutes every day by the lake, not barefoot but would consider beginning to do this, as I like walking barefoot.

I have not cleared electronics from my bedroom yet. Will implement that this week. I have also ordered the blue light protective glasses today and the book suggested by Caroline.

As I say above I am a Learner - and I am very unfamiliar with many of the abbreviations so I will continue reading to get a better grasp on that.

I have not had any blood levels tested. Question to other Canadians: Has your doctor been okay with doing extra tests or do they 'roll their eyes' like my doctor does whenever I ask. If I even mention a vitamin or herbal supplement he rebuts me and basically says hogwash. And it's not easy to get another doctor here.

Oh family background: There are many stories of thyroid imbalances on my father's side. My grandfather had a severe one. My mother was diagnosed with hypothyroidism in her sixties. My father was diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes in his sixties. My mother smoked during pregnancy and throughout my childhood. My sister suffers from migraines and what appears to be a mental illness that presents as obsessive-compulsive disorder but could be another disorder. She has also been diagnosed with fibromyalgia. My mother was born in Ireland and was raised eating a lot of seafood and I did eat a lot of salmon growing up. On my father's side there seems to be a number of examples of what looks like depression and perhaps bipolar disorder.

Today I ate:

Breakfast - Black coffee, apple and shredded chicken (the inside of homemade enchilada)

Lunch - Locally made sausage with no preservatives, sauerkraut

Dinner - Lake trout, green salad and small amt. of rice pasta with home made pesto sauce

Supplements: Multi-vitamin, 1000 mg Vit C, 1 Omega 3 oil capsule

Drank: lots of glasses of water - we have a filter that filters fluoride out

That lowers the energy in your cell and makes you rely more on mitochondria to make energy and if they cannot you get all the other symtpoms you mentioned. Pretty straight forward. # Thread: Buying blue blocking glasses for the office.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-blue-blocking-glasses-for-the-office.18401/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Juha — 2016-09-09T12:02:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-blue-blocking-glasses-for-the-office.18401/post-201175>

I'm in the process of purchasing a pair of blue blocking glasses for my office job. My intention is to protect myself from the blue light emitted from computer screens and fluorescent lighting.

At home I've been wearing a pair of amber tinted Uvex Skyper Safety Glasses a few hours before bedtime. They have worked wonderfully for me! However, these safety glasses aren't very stylish, to say the least. I can wear them in my own apartment but I wouldn't consider using them in a public setting!

I've done my best to research what the most important characteristics of blue blocking glasses are for an office environment:

-   I think the tint of the lenses should be as close as possible to an amber color. (Many of the gaming/computer glasses on the market are closer yellow than amber. I think these yellow lenses won't block as much blue light as amber lenses.)

-   The build of the frame should be such that too much light doesn't come in through the sides of the glasses. In other words, it doesn't matter if the lenses block blue light, if blue light gets in through the sides.

On top of these two characteristics, **what else would you consider to be important in a pair of blue blocking glasses, for an office environment?**

### lawrencep93 — 2016-09-09T13:03:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-blue-blocking-glasses-for-the-office.18401/post-201176>

I bought "PAVOSCREEN BLUE LIGHT BLOCKER COMPUTER GLASSES" for the office, because the glass colour is close to clear, I know it only blocks 50% blue light.
However I pair them with the UVEX blue light blockers once the sun sets, also keep the lights down low at home once the sun sets.

Take all glasses off when going outside

### Juha — 2016-09-09T13:24:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-blue-blocking-glasses-for-the-office.18401/post-201178>

Thanks for the tips lawrencep93!

Did you have any other reasons for buying the Pavoscreen glasses other than that the glass color is close to clear?

### lawrencep93 — 2016-09-09T14:08:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-blue-blocking-glasses-for-the-office.18401/post-201183>

Price and availability.

Hard to get something good here in Australia at a decent price with out paying large sums for shipping.

I had a pair of yellow gaming glasses but they have a slight zoom in effect which gives me a head ache if I am looking at anything from a distance.
But mainly they are super yellow compared to Pavoscreen which look more like every day glasses

### Juha — 2016-09-09T15:40:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/buying-blue-blocking-glasses-for-the-office.18401/post-201185>

Yep, got it!

I know how you feel with the expensive shipping costs. I live in Finland and this has been an issue for me for a long time.

Luckily, it looks like the market of blue light blocking glasses is expanding. There's plenty of different models of blue light blocking glasses on UK's Amazon these days.
