# Thread: Looking for Help - I Also Have a Unique Validation of the Light/Water/DHA Principle
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-help-i-also-have-a-unique-validation-of-the-light-water-dha-principle.18455/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ken Rich — 2016-09-21T16:03:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/looking-for-help-i-also-have-a-unique-validation-of-the-light-water-dha-principle.18455/post-201693>

I recently started a Keto diet with intermittent fasting as a response to a diagnosis of diabetes. My fasting blood sugar has come down and I've lost about 20 lbs. so far.

Initial weight loss was rapid but has slowed. So I've been trying to educate myself as to how to increase efficiency. Then I ran into Jack Kruse videos on Primal Edge.

I can't afford devices like a quantlet or a magneto bed as I am on a tiny disability pension, so low budget light hacks are what I need help with.

I have so far made an "el cheapo" near infrared set-up, got blue blockers, and have a SAD lamp for mornings. _However, I need some guidance on a cheap source of UVA which I understand is also critical_.

Location is supposed to be an important consideration to any potential advice. I live at 48.9490° N, 57.9503° W in Newfoundland Canada. It is a low light environment but I have access to good spring water, and have started supplementing DHA.

Although overweight, I am healthy enough for HIIT training with uphill sprints, and lift weights besides. I am eating just once a day, but it is a large keto meal heavy on the greens and supplements.

I am blue eyed with light skin and dark brown hair, Primarily English/Irish stock with a little bit of French and Indian in the mix. 6' 3'' and 280 lbs at the moment.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

One of the reasons I think there may be something to the light theory is the unusual case of a local "strongman". He had (now deceased) _**superhuman strength**_ and those that knew him hailed him as the strongest man in Newfoundland - perhaps the world. He routinely did feats of strength and endurance that were astounding and seemed outright impossible. Someone should have done a documentary on him.

I recently asked his son what his father ate and drank that might have led to his extraordinary strength. He said that his father came from a poor family and went to work as a "woodsman" at an early age. So he was outside in the sunlight doing heavy labor all his life. Moreover, the "mainstay" of his diet was smoked capelin (smelt family of fish) which are quite oily. The traditional smoking process made them salty to preserve them. So his father had said he spent a lot of time with "his head stuck in a brook" (own words) drinking copious amounts of water and no doubt cooling from hard work in the summer heat.

So here I see all the elements Jack talks about at play, the sunlight, the DHA, then structured water from free flowing streams, and cool water right to the face and head on a regular basis. Just saying - hmmmm.....

I'm 53 by the way. Not sure how relevant age is to recommendations but I forgot to mention earlier.

The Sperti lamp I've seen before. It's out of my price range but the reptile lamp I can afford.

However, with both of them I wonder about all the conflicting information I've seen about the dangers of UV light. I have been led to believe that A is safe and necessary but B and C not so much. I've also heard standard tanning type lights have been banned in the EU for safety reasons. I don't know if any of that info is true but it certainly raises concerns.

Also, I saw something in one of Jack's videos about mercury lamps (the reptile light is mercury) potentially interacting with mercury we may have inadvertently stored in out bodies (some type of resonance effect I guess). I must confess I didn't really understand it all, but that was the "take away" message that stuck with me and it makes me "wary".

So I have been holding off on the UV side of things until I see something that settles the question of what band(s) of UV are safe, what dosages are recommended (keeping in mind I am in a Northern latitude at low altitude with winter coming on). Also, what gives the most "bang for the buck". For instance, the reptile lamps are an affordable option if they are indeed safe for humans.

I see your statement _"Everybody chases fasting glucose. Fasting insulin is underappreciated, specially when on high fat diet._" That may be so but I have no way to measure fasting insulin. Is there a way I am unaware of?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-21T22:09:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ctia-is-going-to-go-to-big-lengths-to-protect-profit-over-health.18456/post-201705>

CTIA is going to go to big lengths legally to protect its massive profit margins over human health because of the 1996 FCC law that exempts tech companies from future health risks lawsuits. San Francisco attempted this and was sued by the wireless industry, forcing it to give up on the ordinance. Since this is hosted on a technology site, the author sides with industry and ignores A LOT of the science. What's particularly telling is that the warning the city of Berkley is proposing is so minimal. It doesn't even come close to addressing all the documented health concerns about Wi-Fi, cell phones, etc., found in HUNDREDS of published studies. [http://arstechnica.com/tech-policy/...berkeleys-cell-phone-radiation-warning-stand/](http://arstechnica.com/tech-policy/2016/09/should-appellate-court-let-berkeleys-cell-phone-radiation-warning-stand/)

### Penny — 2016-09-21T23:15:46+0300

San Fransisco was just going to highlight what's already in the manual... keep it away from your body and they lost - Berkely is doing the same - IT'S ALREADY IN THE DAMN MANUAL!

I guess not enough people are dying yet... or cancer is the new norm...

# Thread: Jen's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-journal.18457/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jencole — 2016-09-22T06:45:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jens-journal.18457/post-201723>

Thanks to everyone who contributes on this site! It is truly a lifesaver.

I learned of Leptin from an internal medicine doc who I thought would “help”. He mentioned weight loss surgery as a solution to my problem, I asked “can we simulate weight loss surgery and get the same results?” I’ve not seen long term data on the surgery and health risks and after 3 family members had this procedure, I was not convinced the weight would stay off.

So he told me about leptin and suggested I research, then prescribed phentermine. I had success, then gained the weight back. I would exercise and 15 minutes in I would could have laid down on the floor and taken a nap. That’s when I knew something was wrong, something that I didn’t have control of and something that had plagued me since puberty.

Law school, two masters degrees, a bachelor’s degree and an outstanding career, I could accomplish anything in these arenas, but I could not for 25 years, conquer weight loss. Man this pissed me off.

I returned to the same doctor to seek help with the leptin, he said that I was exhausted because I was carrying so much extra weight. I walked out and spent hours researching experts in the area of leptin. I knew I needed direction and conventional medicine wouldn’t get me there.

I need help, I want to be better, and I want to fix this. I am committed.

I have spent 2016 changing every aspect of my environment I can think of. I work from home so that I can get outside throughout the day, stay out of the wireless world and be in the sun when I have the opportunity. I have changed to a less stressful job and I have made major modifications to every aspect of my life.
It’s been in stages, but I am ready for next steps and I believe that includes looking at my total health picture and creating a plan.

I have a biohack scheduled with Dr. Kruse in January, but am looking to get started and have direction.

**Family History**

-   I have the body of my maternal grandmother. I am the oldest daughter of her middle child (she had 9). I feel like I am her clone and that scares me. She unfortunately died of lung/live cancer, but prior to she had breast cancer and heart problems. I once tried on a coat she had custom made for herself, it fit me like a glove- this scared the shit out of me. She was a size 22 and obese most of her life. She tried every diet she could think of, religiously and was never successful in her quest to lose weight.
-   My mother doesn’t seem to have health issues, she is a little overweight in the stomach area, but no hx of cancer or anything else.
-   Dad is diabetic and has high BP, he is very very active, always outside.

**My History**

-   Currently 38, 275#, 5’3
-   Started gaining weight around age 10, was skinny until then. Started gaining weight at this time.
-   Grew up on a horse farm, didn’t have cable until college, always outside growing up. Was very very active.
-   Was diagnosed with hypothyroidism at age 20. TSH was 26, I have not seen it that high since then.
-   Menstrual cycle has never been regular.
-   I have had 1 pregnancy/1 delivery. In the beginning of the pregnancy, I felt great and even lost weight. At the end I was diagnosed with preeclampsia and delivered a month early.
-   In the past, I could do an extreme diet and lose around 20 pounds. I could hold that weight off for a month or two, then it would always come back.

**Current Health**

-   Menstrual cycle not regular
-   Fatigue often
-   Brain fog
-   Hursutism
-   Restless leg syndrome
-   Thyroid not optimal
-   Obesity (BMI 49, weigh 285)
-   Iron Deficiency

### Huck — 2016-09-23T15:20:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-allergies.18459/post-201797>

Do you always have an allergic reaction when you eat the food(s) and how long after eating before the symptoms appear?

The reason I ask is because I have been reading about histamine, which I think is what caused my reactions. I had a 1 time reaction to shrimp and a 1 time reaction to cod. In both cases, the symptoms appeared between 1 and 2 hours after eating. From what I have read, what I had was more likely a histamine reaction than an allergic reaction. While histamine reactions usually occur an hour or 2 after eating, an allergic reaction takes place almost immediately. Symptoms are similar.

# Thread: Goosey's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gooseys-optimal-journal.18460/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Goosey — 2016-09-22T19:09:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gooseys-optimal-journal.18460/post-201750>

I've been thinking about doing this for over a month, and am tired of having all the thoughts clanging around in my head and not actually putting them out there. So this is me, kicking the first pebble down the hill.

### Goosey — 2016-09-22T19:56:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gooseys-optimal-journal.18460/post-201758>

WATER Fall 2016

So I live in AZ, and the first reply I got from the Doc when I did my introductory post was, "Don't drink the water in AZ because of the fluoride". So here's some context about my water.

I live in Chandler and the water here is super hard. Sometimes it reeks of chlorine. Having lived in AZ for 30+ years, I'm used to the water tasting like dirty stone, and avoid drinking it for the most part. However, Doc's warning has me avoiding the ice too, for the most part. I don't take showers, and when I bathe, the water is pretty tepid. I also mix a little ascorbic acid into the bath water to neutralize the chlorine.

As for my drinking water, I've been using a distiller for over a year. In years prior, I've tried getting gallon plastic jugs of distilled or purified water from the grocery store, but found them to have an astringent quality that dried my mouth out. Then I found a brand of bottled water that was slightly alkaline. It shipped from Nevada and had a somewhat more viscous quality that wetted my mouth better. If I drank that exclusively, I could probably kill two cases a week. After a while, I realized that having someone in Nevada, a drought state, bottle up their drought water, and have a trucker drive it to a warehouse in the drought desert I live in (wasting gas), then someone else drive it to the store (more gas), so I could guzzle the water down in an hour and then generate X number of empty plastic bottles that need to be recycled... It just seemed silly and wasteful, which is how I decided to try the distiller. I got it used on eBay.

I just recently heard a podcast featuring Jack being interviewed by a stylist/nutritionist from Los Angeles, who drives up into the mountains to collect his own spring water, and in that interview Jack mentioned running RO water through an aerator and over volcanic stones to help structure it (he said something about knocking electrons off as the water ran over the stones). So I bought some volcanic rocks and an aerator, along with a 1 gallon glass jug for storage. I was also concurrently searching for a better collection bottle for my distiller, because the one I was using was plastic, and had a tiny opening that was hard to clean. Ultimately, I settled on a glass Kitchenaid 5 qt bowl for a stand mixer. I started using the aerator, the rocks (in a steel funnel), the glass jug, and the mixing bowl all at the same time, and I noticed immediately that the water had a different texture. The aerator was kind of a pain in the process (I only have the two hands), so I dropped it out for a few cycles, and I realized it wasn't really contributing to the change in water texture. I assumed it was the rocks that were making the difference, but I started reading The Fourth Phase of Water and, after a few chapters, I realized that it was probably the hydrophilic surface of the glass collection bowl.

My distiller is an older Kenmore countertop model, and I positioned the drip spout of the distiller over the pour spout on the glass collection bowl. The result is that every drop of water that comes out of the distiller has to dribble across a minimum of an inch of glass, before landing in the body of distilled water at the bottom of the bowl. When the process is just starting, the water has to trickle over 6 inches of glass to hit the bottom.

The water that I get out of the distiller now has a viscous, syrupy texture that has some of the mouth feel of a sugared soda (which is one of the things I enjoy about soda). Another observation: I decided to purchase a bottle of Hawaiian spring water, partially because I like the water and partially because the empty bottle is useful. On this particular day, I had a rare-for-me dehydration headache, and I kept drinking the chichi Hawaiian spring water, because... Hawaiian spring water. But this particular bottle had that same astringent mouthfeel and it wasn't doing anything; I was still thirsty and my head still ached. I grabbed a glass bottle that had a little bit of my distilled water in it, and drank a couple of swallows. My thirst went away immediately and so did the headache.

I work at a golf course and my days are frequently 10-12 hours long. My options at work are filtered water and ice, Dasani, or standard F&B beverages (iced tea, fountain sodas, coffee). I now take a half-frozen plastic bottle of distilled water, along with one or two big glass Voss bottles of distilled water to work. I keep one out to drink, and bury the other two in ice. The bottle I keep out can get up to 100 degrees, but it stays syrupy and kills my thirst. That bottle can be glass or plastic - the quality of the water holds up, in my observation.

At home I store the water in glass. I have a trivet covered in magnets, and the 1 gallon glass jug lives there. My dogs' bowl sits on another magnetized trivet, and they get the same water. I have a glass jar of water in the refrigerator on a third trivet. I water my plants with the water from the distiller now.

I run the distiller overnight, and usually also in the morning when I get up, so that's 2 gallons a day.

I had a DEXA scan in the spring, I'll probably have another one this winter, to monitor my bone density and make sure that this water isn't causing leaching issues. All my water, including water run over stones, comes out at 3 ppm using a TDS meter.

I want to have a sediment and contaminant filter installed on the water line into the house. I would rather bathe in less garbage, and the stalagmites that develop in my distiller are appalling.

# Thread: Negotiating with the utility company to remove a wireless smart meter
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/negotiating-with-the-utility-company-to-remove-a-wireless-smart-meter.18461/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rubicon — 2016-09-22T19:23:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/negotiating-with-the-utility-company-to-remove-a-wireless-smart-meter.18461/post-201751>

I have a wireless smart meter in my basement that transmits a signal every ~14 seconds 24/7.

A few months ago I wrapped the meter with a layer of [microwave absorbing sheet](http://www.lessemf.com/fabric5.html#259) to absorb the signal, and then a layer of [mylar emergency blanket](https://www.amazon.com/Emergency-Mylar-Blankets-84-52/dp/B01KCAL1MS) to reflect any energy not absorbed and give it another chance to get absorbed (h/t to lilredgirl [for the idea](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/rubicons-journal-a-return-to-optimal.17325/#post-190317)). After wrapping the meter I found I could no longer detect the signal with my Trimeter. It seems to have worked!

Smart meter before/after:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I'm now receiving notices in the mail from the utility company saying that they've been estimating my water usage for the last 3-months, and they're requesting access to my basement to determine why the meter is not working.

Previously I've contacted the utility company to request a non-wireless meter, but they were not receptive. Now that I know I can block the signal, I'd like to try again to get the meter replaced. I was wondering if anyone has had experience negotiating in a similar situation and might provide some advice? My current thoughts are to:

-   make it clear that I don't want a wireless transmitter near my house
-   share my Trimeter readings and explain my concerns that the levels detected exceed limits that I am comfortable exposing myself to
-   request a regular analog meter, or a wired smart meter that I can plug an ethernet cable into

### Rubicon — 2016-09-22T19:48:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/negotiating-with-the-utility-company-to-remove-a-wireless-smart-meter.18461/post-201755>

> [Snowie said:](/goto/post?id=201752)
>
> mind you they may think you are unlawfully tampering with the device to defraud them by extracting electricity without paying?.

That's a good point. I could see them jumping to conclusions. I was thinking of dancing around exactly how I've blocked the signal for now.

It's actually a water meter and I would assume it's still metering...just not able to transmit the totals. I did not do anything to the mechanics of the device itself, and I have no intention to defraud anyone or avoid paying for utilities. I simply want to avoid exposing myself to the wireless signal, especially when sleeping.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-22T19:49:10+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-22T19:50:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/negotiating-with-the-utility-company-to-remove-a-wireless-smart-meter.18461/post-201757>

My bet is they will replace it........to be sure. If I was you as soon as the guy leaves I would re-wrap it.

### Rubicon — 2016-09-22T20:19:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/negotiating-with-the-utility-company-to-remove-a-wireless-smart-meter.18461/post-201761>

If all else fails and I can't convince them to switch out the meter, perhaps I could then confess that I'm placing some removable "microwave absorbing boards" next to the meter to shield myself from the signal when sleeping, and that the boards must be unintentionally preventing the meter reading. I'll be sure to let them know that I'm completely willing to move the boards if they let me know when they're in the area to read my meter. Honestly, before I got a notice in the mail I wasn't even sure if I was affecting their reading.

It's a lot of work to wrap the whole meter in multiple layers though, and I'd rather not do that over and over. Now that I know I have a way to block the signal, I feel like it's less critical to actually get them to change out the meter. That should make it easier to rationally negotiate without losing my cool at the absurdity of their position. It could be a fun experiment to see if I can actually persuade them to change policy.

I would not explain or show any photos. Your job is not to educate the utility company. Well done on eliminating some nnEMF from your life. Rewrap it and then every 3-4 months they can come back a new reading.

### JMO — 2016-09-23T04:31:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/negotiating-with-the-utility-company-to-remove-a-wireless-smart-meter.18461/post-201785>

Ya. They just think you are crazy. They don't care one bit about you. I held off the Gas company for a year but then they came unannounced one day and said they had to replace the meter because it was no longer 'code'. I said no I had called and told them I had issues with it but the installer said ok we can turn off your gas then. Boy did I feel violated. They prob get you back by turning your house into a neighborhood collector where all the signals come to your house. if you are stealth about it you will get to be without it for a little while longer but they will eventually figure it out.

### Sue-UK — 2016-09-23T10:39:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/negotiating-with-the-utility-company-to-remove-a-wireless-smart-meter.18461/post-201791>

In the UK the smart meter roll out is behind the U.S. so they are some way off using the meter isn't code excuse..... There's still a lot of other low hanging fruit ...

But the roll out is gaining momentum .... We are now at the stage of TV adverts offering that if you get a smart meter you can have free electricity either on a Saturday or a Sunday. For how long isnt clear. My brother rang me on Sunday about something and in the conversation told me what appliances he had going courtesy of the electricity company .....Didn't get the response from me he expected .

Over the last couple of years our electricity company has issued written bills, some from actual readings but some from estimates when they can't be arsed to come round and read the meter. We pay, then before the next quarter they send a statement cancelling the previous bill and recalculating the readings from several bills before, suddenly we owe additional money, and it's unclear if they've accounted for all our payments ...... I think many people would phone the utility company worrying about the unexpected expense, and be ripe for smart meter conversion, but we've learned to wait for them to phone us, we don't understand the bill, can they issue an itemised statement showing all electricity used in the last X months, detailing individual payments we've made. Waiting on one of those at the moment ..

But after reading rubicon's first post I feel much more optimistic ..... When they do catch up with us, I know what will be in place on day one ...

### Optimalbound — 2016-09-24T09:13:15+0300

My meter is definitely wireless only. Would you happen to know the model # of your smart meter? Is it for electricity or water?

### Rubicon — 2016-09-25T18:56:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/negotiating-with-the-utility-company-to-remove-a-wireless-smart-meter.18461/post-201883>

A comparison of benefits/drawbacks of various kinds of meters

****Wireless smart meters****
benefits - frequent readings
drawbacks - rf radiation emitted near living spaces, rf transmission not typically timed to minimize biological effects (ie: pulsed transmission every few seconds 24/7)
These could be better if they were set to transmit at only certain times. For example, during daylight hours, and only once every 10 min or 1 hour
**
Wired smart meters**
benefits - frequent readings, probably minimal EMF risk
drawbacks - requires a constant internet connection from the homeowner, lots of potential for interrupted readings

**Analog meters**
benefits - no rf radiation
drawbacks - costly to have someone manually read the meter once a month. No ability to variably price electricity at times of peak demand
If utility companies don't want to send meter readers, consumers could use apps to read meters automatically and eliminate the need for utilities to send people to the home. Here are a few apps for this purpose:
\- [Utility Meter Reader](http://utilitymeterreader.com/)
\- [Energy Elephant](https://energyelephant.com/)

### Optimalbound — 2016-09-25T23:09:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/negotiating-with-the-utility-company-to-remove-a-wireless-smart-meter.18461/post-201887>

>
> I'd like to have a few alternative wired smart meters to present to my water company that could help move improve my chances of a good outcome.

So far, I've managed to prevent a smart meter from being installed in my home, so I do not know for sure what model would have been used. From what I understand, they are individually connected to the Internet, and there is no communication between meters in different homes. Not sure, but I think they look like this:

It is definitely for electricity. Water is abundant here, and is not being measured.

Most spices and herbal food supplements cannot fix a light problem, with one large exception. And when you add in pepper and seafood it gets even better.

[](https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse?fref=ufi)
CURCUMIN is like sunlight in many ways because it has shown to affect the Faraday effect in tissues That spice is turmeric/curcumin. Curcumin undergoes massive protein fluorescence naturally. So this means it scavenges excessive bio-photon release from stressed cells. All cells in an inflamamtory state release massive amounts ot ELF-UV. One of the major challenge in medicine is to find alternatives treatments for inflammatory conditions like arthrtitis and cancer with "a chemotherapy" or pill that is safe and clinically efficacious. Tumeric has a low toxicity profile and this makes it something that everyone should consider. Many practitioner's do not have a good backround in the bio-physical aspects of disease so they may not know that curcumin (diferuloylmethane), a naturally occurring flavinoid derived from the rhizome of Curcuma longa is an ideal candidate because of how it sops up UV light because it really absorbs it well. If you add some of the shells of shellfish and black pepper to the mix in seafood bone broths you make it even stronger anti-inflammatory. Chitosan and curcumin are a big part of the Epi-paleo Rx. Curcumin is fluorophore protein for those who don't know. In vitro and in vivo preclinical studies have shown its inhibitory anticancer, antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antiproliferative, and proapoptotic activities in mitochondria which lower inflammation. The multiple mechanisms of the anti-inflammatory effect of curcumin putatively include down-regulating the expression of gene products such as nuclear factor-κB, growth suppression, inducing apoptosis, and modulating various signal transduction pathways and the expression of many inflammatory genes and oncogenes. Based on resonance light scattering and UV-visible absorption spectroscopic analysis, mechanism studies have suggested that the highly enhanced fluorescence of curcumin has resulted from synergic effects of a favorable hydrophobic microenvironment provided by albumin and chitosan to facilitate efficient intermolecular energy transfer between albumin and chitosan in the blood plasma. Right now the latest science is that albumin seems to bind to curcumin through its hydrogen bonding network, which causes the protein conformation to convert from β-folded helix to α-helix. This radically changes the Faraday effect in the local environment and improves the local electric and magnetic fields in tissues with inflammation. Curcumin can combine with the the albumen chitosan complex through its center carbonyl carbon, and the chitosan from the shells of seafood can play a key role in promoting the energy transfer process by shortening the distance between albumen and chitosan to transfer the light energy without shorting out the cells microenvironment. Bio-physics is remarkable when take the time to understand it. Few do

Awesome thank you very much Dr. Kruse! Should I also through a little bit of coconut oil in bc of the MCTs? Probably a no no given my northern latitude.....

Should I even be consuming Curcumin if I live north of 60 degrees parallel in strong fields (ie the rocky mountains)? I have a feeling that it would still help given that we have all the modern killers up here....but could turmeric interfere with UV assimilation some how? I love turmeric and used it a lot when I was down south in the city. Now I am way up north and there are northern lights above my cabin/home everynight. No wifi, cell service. We turn our breakers off at night. But the sun is just super week already (I think my have mitochondria from English parents but my guess is they are yoked for more southern latitudes). Given that I can use cold and magnet fields to my advantage, I am not so sure I need the turmeric much. But I do go to town often where all my good choices are put to the test (blue light, nnemf) and perhaps turmeric could still play a role as a fearless warrior on the battleground for light. I guess the only way to know is to bio-hack ?

\=========================================
Nitric oxide (NO) contributes to vessel homeostasis by inhibiting vascular smooth muscle contraction and growth

\------------------------------------------
The vasodilating antihypertensive drug minoxidil contains an ·NO moiety and may act as an NO agonist. Likewise,
Sildenafil citrate, popularly known by the trade name Viagra, stimulates erections primarily by enhancing signaling through the nitric oxide pathway in the penis.
\------------------------------------------

Methylene blue, a nitric oxide inhibitor, prevents haemodialysis hypotension.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-03T05:02:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-turmeric-curcumin-help-quantum-diseases-linked-to-light.18468/post-204693>

> [Lava said:](/goto/post?id=203172)
>
> Would you use coconut oil if you lived in the tropics through winter? or avoid for winter?

Yes I would.

Light inside the tropics is very consistent.........much more so than you get above 20 latitude N or S.

Astronomers work is always concerned with the past; because light they deal with from stars takes a lot of time to move from one place to another, they see things as they _were_, not as they _are_. Human work is always concerned with now and the future because light from our sun is captured in foods and slowed down and excites the foods electrons and we eat it in the here and now. The food is like an an astronomer........it captures old light that took eight minutes to arrive to Earth and then it holds it in purgatory until we consume the light in the present. Our mitochondria sees light from food as it is now, which is why seaosns matter with respect to food.......and not what the light was when it fell to Earth to become an exciton to make food.

### Samy Colddigger — 2016-12-03T22:22:29+0200

The kids at school seem to get a kick out of my yellow teeth/hands and sometime orange lipstick  I seem to notice an immediate increase in gut activity. Marvelous stuff thank you Doc for making me pay more attention to the stuff that matters  Got some of my students consuming it now. One of them was particularly brave (hes also 13 and has shot 10 moose and trapped and caught food for most of his life) and he ate the whole root raw right in front on my eyes. I was shocked....but he felt something I guess and asked for more. Hopefully it will help him bio-hack the rest of his school years as they will most likely be wi-fied and blue lit!

Hi JanSz,
That ozone generator saved our lives. We moved into a rental house we owned after the tenant's children accidentally set a fire. The insurance company sent out inferior restorers and the attic was not properly sealed, 2 rooms were rebuilt. I had the attic resealed.
However, the house had a burnt smell that made my daughter sick when she came to visit.
With good fortune I met a lady who sold ozone generators and I think it cost me about $600 at the time, worth every penny.
We moved in the house in 1996 and I had that ozone generator running 24/7 until I moved in 2010. It cleared the burnt smell after several months. It penetrates wood and removes the burnt particles.
If we turned it off to test the difference, when you came into the house, it had a dead feeling in the air. When we turned it back on, it was like living air.
If anyone says this kind of ozone generator is dangerous, they don't know what they are talking about especially for the long time I had it working in the house. Also, my health is excellent especially after discovering Dr. Kruse in March 2016.
I followed the manufacturer's instructions on its care and cleaning to the letter. It gave me great service. I took it with me when I moved to my daughter's house as I had a 37 pound dog and 16 pound cat in my room at night. It worked great. My ozone generator may have had a different brand name, but it looked identical.

**Turmeric** (_Curcuma longa_) ([/ˈtɜːrmərɪk/](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:IPA/English))[\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turmeric#cite_note-merriam-webster.com-2) is a [rhizomatous](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rhizomatous) [herbaceous](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Herbaceous) [perennial](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perennial) [flowering plant](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flowering_plant) of the [ginger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ginger) family, [Zingiberaceae](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zingiberaceae).[\[3\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turmeric#cite_note-3)

# Thread: Refitting my factory.  Replacing CFL lights.  Suggestions?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/refitting-my-factory-replacing-cfl-lights-suggestions.18472/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Simon Wright — 2016-09-26T03:14:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/refitting-my-factory-replacing-cfl-lights-suggestions.18472/post-201894>

HI All,

I am refitting my factory and want to replace all the CFL lights with broad spectrum lighting. I wanted to ask if anyone has had any experience with this but on a commercial and not residential level. (even what you have done residentially would be appreciated as i can pick some specs or products out of there)

I live in the tropics (thus have not looked into this before) and whilst my light habits are pretty good I have a whole factory of workers who spend all day under CFL's and I want to 'share the love'. I prefer to do this on a discreet basis and replace all the lights rather than giving a speech on biophysics will be met with blank looks and some gaping of mouths.

Any websites or specs would be greatly appreciated.

Many thanks

Simon

### Rubicon — 2016-09-26T04:45:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/refitting-my-factory-replacing-cfl-lights-suggestions.18472/post-201897>

I'm imagining your factory floor having workstations with hanging lamps that contain cfl bulbs. Something like these:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

If that's that case, it seems the best fix might be to change out the cfl bulbs with 60 or 100W incandescent.

I'm actually in the process of getting my wifes office renovated. The rooms are 9ft high, have suspended ceiling tiles, and fluorescent tube lights. I've been looking around at various offices and found a design that replaces the tube fluorescent with 4 recessed pot flood lights that hold [incandescent bulbs](http://www.homedepot.com/p/Philips-65-Watt-Incandescent-BR30-Flood-Light-Bulb-12-Pack-per-Case-248872/100560688?MERCH=RV-_-rv_nav_plp_rr-_-NA-_-100560688-_-N). It produces a nice atmosphere. I was thinking of augmenting that with a reptile light that would run on a timer for a couple of hours every day at solar noon, and/or a UVA light.

Another option might be LED lights. I've heard some discussion about systems that adjust color temperature based on time of day to try and mimic the sun. Those would likely not contain the infrared component of light...so perhaps incandescent is still the best option for now.

# Thread: Parkinsons
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parkinsons.18474/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 30
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jennie — 2016-09-26T17:07:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parkinsons.18474/post-201907>

Dear Dr.Kruse,

I am not sure how to reach out to you apart from this space, I am a gold member , but do not seem to have ability to ask you on the 'ask Jack" area.

Hence I am writing this here, and I am not too sure you will see it. If not I will see what I need to do to get to ask a question to you directly.

The question is this, my brother was diagnosed with "Parkinson" like symptoms approx 2 years ago. A full diagnosis has not been provided, and I am not sure why.

He however shakes terribly on the left side, mainly arm and hand, however in the last 6 months things have gone downhill. Stomach cramps, massive wight loss, memory loss. The doctors in Australia where he lives, treat him with drugs. It is not helping. His entire sleeping pattern is ruined, he wakes up 4 am and walks 3 hours then goes back to sleep, The doctors prescribed sleeping tablets, which he refuses to take. The only solution he has been provided is more drugs, I have his full drug list , and when I visited his neurologist July whilst I was in Sydney, it was a joke. Absolute joke. They had done an MRI, which showed nothing, yet he is getting worse, daily. His stomach cramps are now so bad he had to visit hospital last Sunday to have x-rays, which we both now is an absolute waste of time.

The only hope he has is to see you, or be treated by you. Is this possible?

He has not been exposed to light water magnetism, but I will say his body knows intuitively what to do, as every holiday he has, he goes to NZ where it is freezing cold, which helps his systems , shock horror.

He is getting a rash from the drugs, and again I explained to the neurologist, it was a reaction to the drugs, and she told me , no it is not, it is dermatitis, he has touched something.. lets send him to the skin doctor! total BS! Would not listen when I indicated the rash appeared when she prescribed a new drug! How could she refuse to see the correaltions?

I am passionate about helping him, and would do anything for him. Can you provide any help, apart from suggesting he reads the website. I have suggested he do so, however you once told me, when we had a skype call, low dopamine people, are the hardest to shift, and he has low dopamine. It is a catch 22.

Apologise once again if this is not the correct location to ask this.

Your help is most appreciated, and I mean it when I say this, you are the only one in my opinion outside of himself who can guide him to recovery., and he just don have the time to get up to speed, nor the physiology. The medical system drugged my mother, and she died of massive stroke after years of auto-immune disease, I do not want the same fate for my brother , which is inevitable with traditional treatment he is being prescribed.

Thank you.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-26T17:12:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parkinsons.18474/post-201908>

Sunlight and water are key but elimination of blue light and nnEMF from his environment is critical. If you or he doesnt change his environment the protein misfolding prion disease will continue. If you want to learn more OSF 3-5 are quite important as is the recent blog Time 23 that talk about protein folding and structure.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-26T17:17:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parkinsons.18474/post-201909>

Mitophagy: lowers % heteroplasmy in mitochondria by causing regeneration. Mitochondria can induce their own regeneration.

As heteroplasmy percentage increases in the skin, or any tissue really, NAD+ drops, pseudohypoxia develops, ROS/RNS profiles change, and the amount of ELF-UV increases in a cell. These are the fingerprints of heteroplasmy changes in a mitochondria

Lowering the voltage on the inner mitochondrial membrane is the stimulus to mitophagy because it change the bio-electric signature and the size and shape of mitochondria under electron microscopy: Dr. Jodi Nunnari.

Parkin protein is the protein that begins the process of repair. Parkin is named after Parkinson's disease. This is a disease that destroys dopaminergic neurons.

Mitochondrial electron transport chain (ETC) defects are observed in Parkinson’s disease (PD) patients because the geometry of the respiratory proteins stretches; this slows ECT and makes more reactive ROS.

It has been shown already that irradiating isolated fly or mouse mitochondria with 808 nm light (RED) that is absorbed by ETC-Complex IV acutely improves Complex IV-dependent oxygen consumption and ATP production, **_a feature that is wavelength-specific._** Irradiating _Drosophila pink1_ mutants using a single dose of 808 nm light results in a rescue of major systemic and mitochondrial defects.

Blue light stimulates cause heteroplasmy directly. Published data exists.

### Jake Tassell — 2018-01-31T14:51:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/parkinsons.18474/post-233875>

> [Jake Tassell said:](/goto/post?id=233872)
>
> \*something called "Azilect".

OMG...

**"Rasagiline (Azilect, TVP-1012, N-propargyl-1(R)-aminoindan[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rasagiline#cite_note-1)) is an [irreversible inhibitor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enzyme_inhibitor) of [monoamine oxidase-B](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MAO-B)[\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rasagiline#cite_note-2) used as a [monotherapy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monotherapy) to treat symptoms in early [Parkinson's disease](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parkinson%27s_disease) or as an adjunct therapy in more advanced cases"

The FDA label contains warnings that rasagiline may cause severe [hypertension](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hypertension) or [hypotension](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hypotension), may make people sleepy, may make motor control worse in some people, may cause hallucinations and psychotic-like behavior, may cause [impulse control disorder](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Impulse_control_disorder), may increase the risk of [melanoma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Melanoma), and upon withdrawal may cause high fever or confusion.[\[7\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rasagiline#cite_note-Label-7)

Side effects when the drug is taken alone include flu-like symptoms, joint pain, depression, stomach upset, headache, dizziness, and insomnia. When taken with [L-DOPA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/L-DOPA), side effects include increased movement problems, accidental injury, sudden drops in blood pressure, joint pain and swelling, dry mouth, rash, abnormal dreams and digestive problems including vomiting, loss of appetite, weight loss, abdominal pain, nausea, constipation."**

Thanks for that, Susie. I'll pass that on with the other stuff I sent him.

Also...

Whilst trudging through the food guru swamp, I came across a couple of 'anecdotals' that suggest VCO might be of help...

**'Coconut oil miraculously helps Parkinson's patient regain quality of life.'

Imagine for a moment that you are a 74-year-old man diagnosed with Parkinson's disease, and you are quickly losing your ability to think, move and function, despite adhering to an intense regimen of all the latest pharmaceutical offerings for this miserable disease. Then imagine that nearly all of your symptoms subside almost miraculously after beginning a dietary routine that involves taking large doses of all-natural coconut oil every day.

Would you not tell the world about your amazing healing experience and encourage others in similar situations to do the same thing for their own health? This is what one older man did recently when he shared his powerful story on HealthUnlocked.com, a question and answer support forum for people suffering from all sorts of illnesses and health conditions.

When drugs failed him, man adopted coconut oil regimen that changed his life.

In a forum posting entitled "Coconut oil has improved my life," the man, who identifies himself by the handle "fwes," explains how his quality of life deteriorated quite rapidly after agreeing to undergo conventional treatment for Parkinson's at the renowned Mayo Clinic. He explains how he had been taking drugs like Mirapex Er (pramipexole dihydrochloride), Azilect (rasagiline) and Sinemet (Carbidopa/Levodopa) to no avail.

His symptoms reportedly worsened on these drugs, leaving him in a state of catatonic pain and immobility. He describes his symptoms as including severe slowness, distorted facial expressions, chronic stiffness and joint pain, unsteadiness, severe swelling on his left leg and extreme lower back pain. These and many other symptoms are characteristic of late-stage Parkinson's, and eventually lead to a total inability to take care of one's self.

After three-and-a-half excruciating years of taking these multiple drugs for his condition and seeing no improvements, the man decided to take matters into his own hands. Research he discovered over at CoconutKetones.com, the blog of Dr. Mary Newport, M.D., led him to start taking coconut oil every day and building himself up to a tolerance of eight tablespoons daily. And the results were astounding.

Drastic improvements observed after just a few days on coconut oil
According to his personal account, the man experienced "significant improvements" after just a couple of days on the coconut oil. Family and friends were both shocked and delighted that he was suddenly able to move around the house quickly, for instance, and rise from any chair unassisted. He was also able to start performing what he describes as "football agility drills," or shifting right and left and moving forward and backward in response to commands.

As long as he takes his coconut oil every day -- four tablespoons at breakfast, two at lunch and two at dinner is his routine -- then he experiences minimal symptoms and is able to go about his daily life in a mostly normal fashion. Besides improved agility and higher movement capacity, the man says his facial expressions have improved as a result of the coconut oil, and he no longer has any swelling on his left leg.

"I still have Parkinson\['s\] symptoms, but my quality of life has vastly improved," he writes. "We are about at the end of 3 months, and the benefits are maintaining. Something real has happened to me."

**

# Thread: A few questions about fine tuning environment, CT and Leptin RX
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions-about-fine-tuning-environment-ct-and-leptin-rx.18477/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Miss Lizzie — 2016-09-26T21:49:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions-about-fine-tuning-environment-ct-and-leptin-rx.18477/post-201937>

Hello Everyone!

I'm brand new to the forums but have been reading the blogs, book and listening to podcasts. I've been implementing as many of the recommended environmental and dietary changes as possible.

I work at a university and I have installed f.lux on my computer and turned it all the way down to the lowest setting. I also wear blue blocking glasses on top of that. I have turned off the lights in my office and have a standing desk. At least twice a day, I get outside (usually 10 and 2) set my d-minder and walk and get my sun exposure (I live in Southern California, so my sun is great). I also get my 15 min of early morning sun before work. My office does have a window, but it is north facing and in the shade all day. _**My question is, should I just keep my lights off or should I invest in getting full spectrum artificial sunlight bulbs that have uva and uvb light?**_ [http://www.scienceoflight.org/lightactivatedwellnessgt?gclid=CJz-0Z\_Xrc8CFUlNfgodB-IHIg](http://www.scienceoflight.org/lightactivatedwellnessgt?gclid=CJz-0Z_Xrc8CFUlNfgodB-IHIg) or [http://sunshinesciences.com/](http://sunshinesciences.com/)

For Cold Thermogenesis, I bought the cool fat burner ice vest and "gut buster". I wear it for about 60 min at a time. Even after 60 min, I'm not shivering. I figure this is 1 because I'm fat and 2) a woman, so it will take longer for me to adapt and lose my body fat. _**My question about CT, is there a better time of day to do CT?**_ I usually do it at night after dinner.

With regards to the Leptin RX, I'm eating my 50g of protein first thing in the morning. It is hard. It's a lot of food. I know that using whey protein is not ideal, so I've avoided it. _**My question is, what about collagen?**_ 2 tablespoons of collagen contains about 11g of protein. _**Can I have this in conjunction with my eggs and meat to help me get to the 50g recommendation?

**_
Any help is greatly appreciated!

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-26T22:09:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions-about-fine-tuning-environment-ct-and-leptin-rx.18477/post-201940>

collagen in bone broths is smarter. I'd turn off the lights and try to open windows.

### Miss Lizzie — 2016-09-26T23:24:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions-about-fine-tuning-environment-ct-and-leptin-rx.18477/post-201942>

Hi Jack,

I do drink homemade bone broth everyday, but have no real way of measuring the quantity of collagen in it. I will keep the lights off and the window open! That's cheaper and easier.

### caroline — 2016-09-27T07:09:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions-about-fine-tuning-environment-ct-and-leptin-rx.18477/post-201952>

# Thread: When Do You learn This Stuff?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-do-you-learn-this-stuff.18480/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2016-09-28T01:29:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-do-you-learn-this-stuff.18480/post-201994>

I just watched a khan academy video on the difference between a gene and an allele. In the comments, someone asked when you learned this. One person responded 7th grade and another 10th grade.

Things sure have changed in the last 50 years.

And I still don't know what an allele is. For example, if I am A T at a specific point on a gene, is the allele the position I am looking at, the value, or both values?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-28T04:39:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-do-you-learn-this-stuff.18480/post-201999>

1.  An allele is one of two or more alternative forms of a gene that arise by a quantum mechanism/mutation and are found at the same place on a chromosome. Huck its OK not to know. The alleles can be A or T so that what the environment your in will determine which one is expressed and the junk part of the DNA controls the program of expression. That is why calling it junk is crazy. It is the key piece of how DNA works.

### Huck — 2016-09-28T05:06:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-do-you-learn-this-stuff.18480/post-202001>

I still don't quite get it. Let's say I get A from mother and T from father. What is the allele?

Is an allele on 1 strand? So if I am AT, is that an allele pair?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-30T01:10:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-do-you-learn-this-stuff.18480/post-202093>

They are both genes with slightly different codes in them.

### Penny — 2016-10-02T23:55:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-do-you-learn-this-stuff.18480/post-202218>

> 1.  An allele is one of two or more alternative forms of a gene that arise by a quantum mechanism/mutation and are found at the same place on a chromosome. Huck its OK not to know. That is why calling it junk is crazy. It is the key piece of how DNA works.

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3

### Jenny S — 2016-09-29T03:21:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-difference-between-high-low-energy-blue-light.18486/post-202056>

I was looking for some blue blockers on line & found ones that state this -
blocks 90% of high energy blue light (To provide maximum protection)
Lets through 70% of low energy blue light (For minimal color distortion)
Does this mean they are good or not so good?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-29T19:37:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-know-difference-between-high-low-energy-blue-light.18486/post-202082>

### Boga — 2016-09-29T09:50:32+0300

Greetings guys,

New guy here looking for a advice to a problem i have had for many years.

Cold hands and feet is my problem, and i still dont get it why this is a problem. I have a healthy diet, trying to have a good circadian rhythm, exercise/ strength training, cold showers (always) since new year but still i see little improvement. Really sucks when im out on a mountain trip/tent trip and cant have a long break because im afraid that i will go too cold on my hands and feet.

But the strange thing sometimes at evenings, i can get really hot on my feet and i need to take my socks off, but when morning comes im dead cold again.

Would really appreciate if Jack or someone else could come up with a theory about my problem and how to fix it. Recently this has been very annoying because winter is coming and im livinig in north europe.

Thank you guys, and sorry for my low quality of english

### Boga — 2016-11-19T13:35:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-hands-and-feet.18487/post-204079>

Hello everybody,

I just wanted to update my journey to solve my problem with cold hands and feet.

Last week i was at the doctor and got back my blood results (she didn't allow me to take blood test without a appointment with her first :-/... )

Anyway, my blood test was good, didn't see any deficiency (also according to my doctor). So i think my new diet helped me considerable with my nutrition. Even my vitamin d level was good from 34 (2015) to 107 (now).
The only problem my doctor said was that i had high cholesterol. It was there things began to get really annoying. She dropped almost everything about my problem with cold hands and feet and began focusing on my high cholesterol. She even consider putting me on meds, and said to me that i had to go take a gen test to see if i have any "bad cholesterol genes"...
I was still complaining about my issue with cold hands and feet but she only said: i cant help you, it seems that everything is fine with you. Maybe it is your nerves which are damage. Then she was considering sending me to a neurologist to check my nerves in my hands and feet...

"We cant fix your problem if we dont know what is wrong with you, but we should focus on things that we can do", and mention my high cholesterol again. Now she want to me to check my cholesterol levels every 2-3 months. I will not going back there no more. What a waste of money and time, atleast i got my blood result which was the main reason for going there.

Enough with that.

Back to my cold hands and feet.

Seems that my problem is getting worse, because the weather is getting very cold at the moment(ice outside now)
I still try to eat as much seafood as i can (have harvest sea vegetables).
When there is sun i try to go outside, but like i said earlier, almost no sun here at this season. (much sky at the moment)
When i workout i still feel very dehydrated even if i bring my 1L flask with water and drink it. Little sweat, much pee if i drink to much. And when i drink much water at the gym, i always feel full so much that it begins to mess with performance. (seems that my body dont hydrate effective)

So i have an idea to considering buying a sunbed to see if that will help my problem. I found some lamps with these >>[Uv-ray](https://postimg.org/image/iijlgvtbx/)<< . What do you think guys think? Really want to hear your opinion.

PS: hope i will get an answer on this one, i know that im not so knowledgeable in this topic but i hope that i atleast got some replies back, i would really appreciate it.

Thanks!

**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2016-09-29T19:14:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-science.18488/post-202080>

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4378297/](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov%2Fpmc%2Farticles%2FPMC4378297%2F&h=LAQGP4GRO)

### nonchalant — 2016-10-07T03:16:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-science.18488/post-202428>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=202080)
>
> [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4378297/](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov%2Fpmc%2Farticles%2FPMC4378297%2F&h=LAQGP4GRO)

"Ground substance polyelectrolyte molecules associated with the collagenous connective tissue matrix are charge reservoirs ([Figure 12](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4378297/figure/f12-jir-8-083/)). The matrix is therefore a vast whole-body redox system. The glycosaminoglycans have a high density of negative charges due to the sulfate and carboxylate groups on the uronic acid residues. The matrix is therefore a body-wide system capable of absorbing and donating electrons wherever they are needed to support immune functioning."

I am unfamiliar with the term "ground substances" used in this article. I previously thought the extra electrons clung directly to the collagen backbone.

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4378297/figure/f12-jir-8-083/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4378297/figure/f12-jir-8-083/)

# Thread: I'm back, and I brought my kids!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-back-and-i-brought-my-kids.18489/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NaturalHealthForLife — 2016-09-29T20:37:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-back-and-i-brought-my-kids.18489/post-202084>

About two years ago I was doing HIIT and CT (7 minute cold showers at the gym) and was the slimmest I had been since freshman year of high school (130 lbs at 5'9"). I was pretty happy (except my boobs got tiny!) lol

Then I got lazy, we moved (still in Seattle area) etc and I gained 15 lbs over two years.

Now I'm back and I brought my kids!

My 8 yo son has almost completely recovered from autism and he has been really good about a paleo/GAPS type diet since he was three and is doing great!

Anyway, we are starting homeschooling this year and I'm trying to think outside the box to get them used to an optimized schedule.

Any thoughts on how to make this better?

1) I am doing Leptin reset with BAB of 2 oysters, 1 can of sardines, and 4 eggs daily. Also going outside to drink 4 cups of ice water with bare feet and looking at the sky when I first get up at 7 am.

My son eats 2-3 oysters plus two eggs for breakfast. He also likes his carbs and I've been letting him eat his fruit and carrots as long as we get the seafood in. Maybe this is bad, esp tropical fruits like mango. We are grain free and he is at a fine weight. No muscle tone, skinny with bad posture.

2) We go for a barefoot 1 mile walk on our road at 7:30 am and 6:30 pm daily. Try to wear minimal clothing (chilly walk).

3) turn off wifi at night and during day (only on for a few hours when mr IT hubby is home. )

4) we have a full spectrum sauna and I try to do an hour in there 3-4 times per week. Kid hasn't done this much.

5) yellow glasses and lights off after sunset

6) kids asleep from 8/9 pm - 7 am. In bed with red light at 7:30. We kill the electricity in kids rooms when we go to bed.

7) I have started opening a lot of Windows at night which cools the house down to about 58 degrees. Brrr! Freezing when we go to bed and get up. (Hard to get out of blanket!)

8) I am getting back into CT with five minutes of cool-cold water showers after sauna and ice packs on stomach/neck while watching tv at night.

9) grow our own veggie garden and keep chickens, quail and rabbits for meet and eggs. Mostly feed them weeds and greens from garden. Plus chickens get kitchen scraps and meat bones etc.

Q1) Any other crazy stuff I can do? I am thinking of making the kids study/read outside all morning to get cold and light. We may have to go into garage with garage door open when it rains. We are in a cloudy maritime climate so uv in winter is very low.

Q2) I also want to start lifting heavy and or sprint training in a couple weeks with the kids. I see I should do this after 5 pm. Kids also do swimming and gymnastics (indoor) for exercise.

Q3) We live a couple miles from super cold lake and a little farther from ocean, but I will have to work up to that so I can actually drive home safely/ don't drown or freeze. Hehe. Need to get more hardcore with the cold.

Q4) Any thoughts on what time of day is best for sauna? I'm currently doing daytime (mid day when sun is high) but after dark would be more convenient.

Q5) drinking 4 cups EZ ice water 3 times daily. is this enough?

Hope to optimize myself and make the next generation think these bio hacks are all normal.

Looking forward to getting our quantlet and taking turns with that!

Concerned Americans should be working toward a major overhaul of the current response as the Autism CARES Act is reauthorized.

If you want to have a kid and avoid autism begin educating yourself about blue light toxicity and how it changes the epigenetic switches in your own germ line. That is where the disease begins in my opinion. Once the epigenetic changes occur it changes the free radical signals made in mitochondria which change signaling to the nuclear genome and this can hard wire nuclear changes into the offspring. Usually, this is not the case. Most of the changes associated with autism are transgenerational post-translational modifications that can be changed. The reason is obvious. They are mitochondrial defects. If you become a mitochondrion before you have sex to have a kid you can prevent this condition. If it was a nuclear genomic disease, there statistics over the last 30 years in autism would be impossible. Because the mtDNA changes 3-5 times more quickly it should be obvious this is a mitochondrial disease at its core.

The CDC report, “Prevalence of Autism Spectrum Disorders Among Children Aged 8 Years—Autism and Developmental Disabilities Monitoring Network, 11 Sites, United States, 2014,” states that in some of the communities represented in the network, nearly three percent of eight-year-olds had an autism diagnosis in 2014. It uses the same methodology that produced the CDC’s 2010 prevalence findings of 1 in 68 children with autism.

The extraordinary cost of autism care is expected to escalate dramatically as prevalence continues to increase. University of California Davis health economists estimates the national cost of caring for all people with autism in the billions, heading towards $1 trillion. Their forecasts for autism-related medical, nonmedical, and productivity losses were $268 billion for 2015 and $461 billion for 2025. The researchers noted that if ASD prevalence continues to increase as it has in recent years while effective interventions and preventive treatments are not identified and made widely available, the costs could reach $1 trillion by 2025.

Notably, the economic and social costs related to the autism crisis will continue to impact every American taxpayer as funding and priorities are redirected into areas, including, but not limited to: WE need to recommend all kids AVOID all blue light devices until the brain is fully myelinated. We need to educate pediatricians, teachers, school boards, and technology companies how their beliefs are COMPLICIT in this pandemic.

Medical and other health insurance expenditures for co-occurring conditions. Research shows that 47 percent of children with autism had at least one co-occurring condition
Increased funding for social security disability and SSI benefits
Training of public safety resources to manage the explosive increase in wandering and development cases, as 49 percent of children with autism wander/elope
Programs addressing the needs of adults with autism including employment, housing, and community integration.[https://www.ihmc.us/stemtalk/episode-67/](https://www.ihmc.us/stemtalk/episode-67/)

I thought of this because everytime I shower the water just beads up on my skin and it is not oily or anything like that...it's like my body is keeping it out purposely...since it is happening with water it would make sense that it would be happening with other things as well.
Only adding blue blockers has cleared my tinnea fungus amazingly..(skin sun didn't help this)-what?

Additionally: My sense of hunger and thirst are pretty much non existent. I have a really hard time eating anything before noon and virtually have no thirst ever-even when hot or exercising-what?

What am I missing here? Any thoughts or ideas would be greatly appreciated.

# Thread: Love my ice baths!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Miss Lizzie — 2016-10-01T02:53:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/post-202147>

I started doing ice baths, cold showers and using an ice vest as part of the Leptin Rx. I have to say, I look forward to my icing every night!

When I take my ice bath, I find it relaxing and even my heart rate starts to lower (not into a bad range).

I'm a person has always been cold. I'd be in my office with my space heater on complaining about the A/C, Now since doing CT, I'm warm. It feels way too hot when I'm near my coworkers with their space heaters on.

Has anyone else had a similar experience?

### caroline — 2016-10-01T08:47:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/post-202154>

There is a huge C/T thread......

### Valerie — 2016-10-01T15:47:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/post-202159>

Prior to CT I needed to wear a sweater or jacket all the time unless over 75 deg F.

Now I love 65 deg F weather and can tolerate colder.

CT built up BAT and I run much warmer, and it didn't seem to take that long to build up. Maybe within 1-2 months of CT.

What is interesting even if I stop CT over the summer I have maintained by BAT tissue quite well

### DLO — 2016-10-05T07:32:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/post-202308>

How long do you guys shiver for after? Sometimes I shiver so bad i can't type or write or do much of anything.... Except exercise to warm myself up but that kinda defeats the purpose doesn't it?

### Valerie — 2016-10-05T12:36:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/post-202311>

For the first 5 month I would shiver about half hour, maybe 45 minutes. But after that I no longer shivered.

### NaturalHealthForLife — 2016-10-08T17:37:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/post-202509>

Wow! I am impressed!

I am up to 5-7 minutes of cold water at the end of the shower maybe 3 times a week. Ice vests/belt on other days - but with too many other clothes on, I guess I just found out... I suppose I have to be mostly naked to get the best out of the ice vests.

It's good to hear you say these positive things about CT. This has been the most challenging thing for me to implement.

Emily

### Valerie — 2016-10-08T22:36:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/post-202521>

Cold showers are harder. Although two weeks facing dunking is recommended, I just jumped into an ice bath. I hyperventilated for about 1.5 minutes and then was fine. What I told my DH to do was to get into room temperature bath water (20 deg C) and add the ice. This worked well for him and he was able to adapted fairly easily without hyperventilating.

You get far more benefit from sitting in the ice water. Being outside in the sun is even better.

### DLO — 2016-10-09T06:58:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/post-202538>

What do you guys do while CTing aside from listening to Jack's Podcasts? I mostly use the CFB/CGB so I can still move around and be productive...
Valerie are you saying that eventually you will no longer shiver while doing CT?
I thought the point was to shiver?

### caroline — 2016-10-09T07:29:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-my-ice-baths.18500/post-202542>

Are you guys reading the monster C/T thread? lots of nuggets there....

### Valerie — 2016-10-09T15:40:16+0300

# Thread: Which Fat Cells are Used First?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-fat-cells-are-used-first.18509/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2016-10-02T18:17:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-fat-cells-are-used-first.18509/post-202201>

Actually, 3 related questions.

1\. Are fat cells "burned" in a FIFO, FILO, or neither order?

2\. When a fat cell dies that holds a toxin, does the new fat cell hold the toxin or was the toxin put into the blood stream?

3\. Is a fat cell holding a toxin marked in some way so that during the night, when the load on the liver is lighter, it can be dumped back into the bloodstream?

### Sue-UK — 2016-10-05T14:45:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-fat-cells-are-used-first.18509/post-202313>

I think it depends on leptin sensitivity and mitochondia being able to resonate at 100hz, and the heteroplasmy rates of different tissues, and that there are differences in how and where women burn fat. As fat is released from storage, (or during general cell metabolism when there is likely to be some in and out going on) I think the toxin is released into the bloodstream. So if I am fat burning during the night, I'm hoping my liver is coping with it (TCM organ clock....) because it's not also dealing with a late meal.

I used to hate my fat cells, but now I think they are pretty amazing, putting toxins into fat storage away from important organs, until I'm better able to detoxify and excrete them.

### Huck — 2016-10-05T17:01:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-fat-cells-are-used-first.18509/post-202317>

I just read an article that said fat cells are constant. The number of fat cells you have as a child are the number you have as an adult. I think it was in the same article it said that approximately 10% of your fat cells die and are replaced every year. Fat cells just get fuller and fuller. No mention if fat and toxins were stored within the same fat cell. This is new information.

So it might be that no one has looked at how a fat cell is selected both for storing fat and for giving it up. I was thinking of it as a stack. The first one you pt on the stack is on the bottom. Then you keep stacking on top of it. When you go to pull from the stack, you pull the top item of first. So using that logic, fat burning would be a first in - last out process. On the other hand, it seems to me the body would want to get rid of oldest fat first. If that's the case, it would require some pretty sophisticated means for tagging fat cells with a "time" stamp and a way to query to find the oldest stored fat.

Same with toxins stored in fat. How does our body determine which cells hold toxins and not fat?

### JanSz — 2016-10-05T17:27:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-fat-cells-are-used-first.18509/post-202322>

> [Huck said:](/goto/post?id=202317)
>
> Same with toxins stored in fat. How does our body determine which cells hold toxins and not fat?

I do not have any proof for it, but my logic is as follows:

We are daily assaulted by myriad of toxins (since beginning of time).
Storing toxins is a second line of defense.
First defense is to process and get rid of toxins.
Second line is meant to be used as temporary storage.

Body that have deficient toxin processing system is not optimal,
and
that is the reason why it collects toxins on permanent basis.

So really,
we should not concentrate (so much) on periodical campaigns of toxin removals,
but rather
on what to do to streamline toxin removal process,
usually some enzyme is not working as it should (for some reason).
\--
This comes as flashback from our high histamine discussion,
where the high histamine is not a cause but result of
low metallothionein
which may or may not be result of copper/zinc imbalance

Using anti-histamines allows to live another day but is not a permanent solution.
\-
Similarly detoxification campaign have a place
but it is not permanent solution.
////

////

### Huck — 2016-10-05T18:51:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-fat-cells-are-used-first.18509/post-202330>

Jan, what I am concerned about is when someone starts out 100 lbs or more overweight. Have the fat cells already been purged of toxins or do they still hold toxins?

All the detox articles say that the toxins are still in the fat. That as you start burning fat the toxins are released. Seems to me to be a terrible way to run the body. Why haven't the toxins been eliminated at night when the liver is less busy? That way the only thing held in fat cells long term is fat. Toxins would be put back into the blood stream to be filtered by liver at night.

### Sue-UK — 2016-10-06T12:09:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-fat-cells-are-used-first.18509/post-202379>

I see my body fat cells as part of a dynamic endocrine organ, not designed to be holding "old" fat. If my system was operating optimally toxins would be going in and out for excretion regularly, but I am still dealing with past and present environmental mismatches. I think that perhaps males and females fat systems operate differently because a single man can impregnate a lot of females, whereas for females there is a limit to the number of pregnancies.

I think there would be a natural increase in the number of fat cells in certain locations (for females at puberty for example), and fat is conserved in specific areas in case of say a famine to increase the chances of a pregnant woman going to full term and breastfeeding. If a woman has faced several famines, it would make survival sense for fat cells to multiply in certain areas to be able to store more in the good times to help protect future pregnancies. The more famines, the more multiplication? I think there's a correlation between famine and the modern chronic crash dieting - I've experienced the face dropping and looking haggard, losing muscle mass, whilst fat on my hips and thighs refused to budge ... Looking back my fat cells were responding to the environment they faced.

### HopingMom — 2016-10-09T03:53:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-fat-cells-are-used-first.18509/post-202532>

I agree that men's and woman's fat do seem to be different. Probably something with protecting reproduction for women maybe? For me it seems that new fat is burned before old...kind of like layers coming off I guess. I don't understand either why the body would store away toxins to deal with later - I understand prioritizing, etc, but it kind of still seems off. I'm wondering if maybe some fat is acting as a protector to other fat, kind of like a buffer? Then I was thinking maybe the fat isn't holding toxins at all, but something good instead? Something the body wants to save or thinks it will need? Again...back to environment, that is the key I think. Perhaps I will hibernate with a friendly bear that could teach me more.

### Sue-UK — 2016-10-09T12:26:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-fat-cells-are-used-first.18509/post-202546>

In 1970 and 1971 tests were done on farm animals contaminated with organochlorines. Tests showed that extra rations of specific vitamins and minerals led to them being better able to get rid of the chemicals, but the better technique was taking advantage of natural recycling of fats in the gut. As fat was broken down it released into the gut taking the organochlorines with it. Some of the fats are secreted from the blood into the gut in the gastric juices, travel through the gut until they are absorbed again in the small intestine, and put back into the fat stores. They used charcoal to take this "window of opportunity" to bind the toxins to stop re absorption and carry them out in the waste... Another reason I don't see my fat cells as just for fat storage, fat recycling in the gut is metabolically protective ..... What if those same organochlorines were lodged in say the collagen network ....?
