### JanSz — 2016-10-09T16:36:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/which-fat-cells-are-used-first.18509/post-202555>

Food is spending 2-4 hrs in stomach, then travels to small intestines.

\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Nice explanation.
[https://www.quora.com/Eating-How-long-does-it-take-food-to-reach-your-stomach](https://www.quora.com/Eating-How-long-does-it-take-food-to-reach-your-stomach)

**Eating: How long does it take food to reach your stomach?**
From the time you chew and swallow your food. How long does it take food to travel from your mouth to your stomach. I'm not asking about the digestive process, I just want to know how long it takes food to get to stomach from mouth?
8 Answers
[
Bibek Ray](https://www.quora.com/profile/Bibek-Ray-1), Ph. D in Food Science; currently professor emeritus
[Written 26 Apr](https://www.quora.com/Eating-How-long-does-it-take-food-to-reach-your-stomach/answer/Bibek-Ray-1)
It depends upon the type of a food you just swallowed. If it is liquid, like milk or soda, in 1-2 seconds it is in your stomach. If it is semisolid, like breakfast cereal of porridge ( cooked oats and milk), or yogurt with some cereal, it might be 4-6 second; but if it is more towards solid, like hamburger ( bun, cheese, meat, and other fixings) or a biscuits ( with sausage, egg, cheese), it can be as much as 7-8 seconds. These are just approximation.

**Once the food is in the stomach, it will go through processing ( grinding with stomach juices) and will take 2-4 hours ( depending upon texture of your food) to getout of stomach in the small intestine,**

In the Small intestine food will go through breakdown to simpler chemical components by chemicals secreted in the intestine and these simple components are absorbed through the wall in the blood stream. **That happens in about 5 to 6 hours.**

In its third stop, unabsorbed food materials get inside large intestine ( colon). While waiting there, most water is absorbed through the wall ( with few more things). **That can be about 12- 18 hours.**

Last stop is rectum, where everything not used are stored. May be a few hours, before they, as poop , are removed out of the body.

The whole time . between your swallowing of food and removal its unused portions from the body can take 16 to 24 hours ( depeding upon the nature of your food, food habits and health of you gut).

**Just for the time from mouth to stomach: between 2 to 8 sec.**

10.9k Views · [View Upvotes](https://www.quora.com/api/mobile_expanded_voter_list?type=answer&key=mxiLHLBtMnq)

\===============================================
[http://www.naturalnews.com/040798\_aromatization\_estrogen\_testosterone.html](http://www.naturalnews.com/040798_aromatization_estrogen_testosterone.html)
**Anti-estrogenic foods and herbs:**
1\. Cruciferous veggies: Cruciferous vegetables such as broccoli, cauliflower Brussel sprouts and cabbage contain several powerful nutrients that detoxify bad estrogenic molecules. These nutrients include Indole-3 Acetate, Indole-3 Carbonyl and Diindolylmethane. These cruciferous veggies also contain sulfur containing nutrients such as Sulfuraphane and Phenyl Isothiocyanates that enhance liver detoxification processes and destroy cancer cell formation.

2. **Healthy fat-rich [foods](http://www.naturalnews.com/foods.html):** These are foods that are rich in saturated and **omega-3 fatty acids.** It includes plant based fats such as coconut oil, extra virgin olive oil and avocados. Raw nuts (other than peanuts) and seeds contain anti-estrogenic plant sterols. Organic poultry, wild-caught salmon, wild game and 100% grass-fed beef are great for healthy hormone function.

3\. Alium family: The aliums include garlic, onions, scallions, chives and leeks. These are all rich in sulfur-containing amino acids and the powerful flavone anti-oxidant quercetin that both help the liver detoxify at a higher level and reduce the production of [estrogen](http://www.naturalnews.com/estrogen.html).

4\. Flavone rich herbs: Dietary flavones and flavonones have been demonstrated to be the most potent aromatase-inhibitory flavonoids. The most powerful flavones include chrysin which is found in passionflower and apignine which is found in chamomile. Several brands of herbal tea can be purchased that feature both organic passionflower and chamomile. These teas are typically marketed for healthy sleep but they are also powerful anti-estrogenic activity.

5\. Flavonones: These are found in citrus fruits and in particular lemons and limes. They are classically called citrus bioflavonoids and they include diosmin, hesperidin, rutin, naringin, tangeretin, diosmetin, narirutin, neohesperidin, nobiletin and quercetin.

6\. Antioxidant-rich herbs: Herbs such as oregano, thyme, rosemary, sage and [turmeric](http://turmeric.naturalnews.com) are loaded with volatile oils that promote liver detoxification. Turmeric has a potent ability to destroy estrogen receptor positive cancer cells which are the most commonly found cells in breast, uterine, ovarian and prostate cancer.

7. Chlorophyll: One of the most powerful life-giving substances on the planet is chlorophyll. This is the pigment that gives green foods their color. Chlorophyll-rich foods have a very deep green color and they are extraordinarily useful in purifying the body of toxins.

8\. Fermented foods: These contain organic acids, anti-oxidants, enzymes and probiotics that enhance gut function and eliminatory channels. Fermented veggies include sauerkraut, kimchi and fermented soy. Fermented raw dairy from 100% grass-fed cows is amazing for the body. Fermented drinks such as apple cider vinegar, kombucha and herbal botanicals are great estrogen detoxifiers.

Learn more: [http://www.naturalnews.com/040798\_aromatization\_estrogen\_testosterone.html#ixzz4Mp1mWir9](http://www.naturalnews.com/040798_aromatization_estrogen_testosterone.html#ixzz4Mp1mWir9)

### JanSz — 2016-10-13T15:16:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tweaks-to-dutch-hormone-test-results.18510/post-202726>

[http://tahomaclinic.com/2011/01/iodide/](http://tahomaclinic.com/2011/01/iodide/)

**Iodide – One Mineral Can Help A Myriad Of Conditions From Atherosclerosis To “COPD” to Zits**
Jan 8, 2011 | [Dr. Jonathan V. Wright's Articles](http://tahomaclinic.com/category/dr-jonathan-v-wrights-articles/) | [0 comments](http://tahomaclinic.com/2011/01/iodide/#respond)

If you’ve read or heard anything at all about potassium iodide, it’s probably been in association with terrorist attacks or nuclear power plant disasters. Potassium iodide (usually taken in tablet form) is recommended by “public health” authorities to protect the thyroid gland against accumulation of radioactive iodine released by a “terrorist bomb” or by nuclear power plant “meltdown”. But in reality, potassium iodide is a very effective “home remedy” with literally dozens of uses.

Older readers may remember Mother putting “iodine” on cuts and scrapes. It kept infection away as well as any “modern” antibiotic ointment, with the added benefit of not inducing “bacterial resistance”. But what else is potassium iodide (usually abbreviated by it’s Latin initials “SSKI”) good for? Read on…and then make sure to read the separate section about keeping SSKI use safe.

Some years ago, a retired Indian physician told me about his use of SSKI during more than 30 years traveling from village to village in rural Africa. Most usually, the only drinking water available was from a local stream or river, muddy and contaminated. After removing sediment and debris by straining the dirty water through cheesecloth, he’d add several drops of SSKI, and wait two to three minutes. He and his team could then drink the water. In over 30 years, he never got an infection from contaminated water. The SSKI killed any micro-organisms present.

Fortunately, the water available to most of us when traveling is considerably cleaner. Despite this, when Holly (my wife) and I travel, we always carry a small bottle of SSKI, and put one or two drops into any water we’re not absolutely certain about. We’ve cut back considerably on airline travel this year because of the thoroughly un-American and extremely unpleasant “airport Gestapo” experience. But when we’re forced to travel by air, we drink a few ounces of water with 10 drops of SSKI, and prevent any “airline sinusitis” or other respiratory infection that so often follows several hours spent breathing re-cycled, germ laden air. (SSKI rapidly accumulates in any and all body secretions, including in the sinuses, where it inhibits or kills bacteria, viruses, and fungi before they can cause an infection.)

Although Holly has never had the experience, occasionally another woman at one of the conferences we attend has developed a bladder infection when far away from home and her own physician. Holly gives her our “back-up” small bottle of SSKI with instructions to take 10 to 15 drops in water or juice every 3 to 4 hours (while awake) until the infection is gone.

\[Although SSKI is close to 100% effective in the elimination of bladder infections, this is a “high dose”; make sure to read the section “Keeping SSKI safe”. If possible, it’s best to use the simple sugar D-mannose to eliminate bladder infections. D-mannose is effective over 90% of the time, and is very, very safe. For details, see D-mannose and Bladder Infection by Lane Lenard Ph.D. and me, available through the Tahoma Clinic Dispensary.\]

When our children were teenagers, they always knew where to find the SSKI bottle. Whenever one of them “popped a zit”, she or he would rub SSKI into it every hour or two. The offending “zit” would be gone in 24-48 hours or less; an innumerable number of social events were rendered “zit-free” by this approach.

So far, I’ve been telling you about SSKI’s ability to “kill germs” in one place or another. We’ll return to this important “home remedy” use for SSKI, but let’s digress for now to other uses.

Many women develop “fibrocystic breast disease”. In the 1970s, I learned from pioneering trace element researcher Dr. John Myers that iodine (a close “relative” of SSKI) would eliminate even the most severe cases of fibrocystic breast disease. \[For the full details of this treatment, see pages 169-174 of The Patient’s Book of Natural Healing by Alan Gaby M.D. and me.\] In “medium” to “minor” cases, 6 to 8 drops of SSKI taken in a few ounces of water daily will frequently reduce fibrocystic breast disease to insignificance within three to six months. Please do not do this without monitoring your thyroid function…see “Keeping SSKI Safe” on page whatever.

One of our daughters and at least thirty other women I’ve worked with in nearly 30 years have helped ovarian cysts disappear within two to three months with the same quantity of SSKI. Again, make sure to monitor your thyroid function!
It’s very likely that SSKI helps eliminate fibrocystic breast disease and ovarian cysts at least partly through it’s interaction with estrogens….which brings us to another important use for SSKI (and other forms of iodine such as “Lugol’s solution” and “di-atomic iodine”). All of these forms of iodine help your body to metabolize estrone (a slightly carcinogenic human estrogen) and 16-alpha-hydroxyestrone (a much more dangerous metabolite of human estrogen) into estriol, an “anti-carcinogenic” or at worst “neutral” form of human estrogen. I’ve reviewed literally hundreds of hormone tests in over 26 years which have proven this point. This testing and treatment usually requires the help of a physician skilled and knowledgeable in nutritional and natural medicine, who can also help with monitoring thyroid function.

[Learn More About The Tahoma Clinic](http://tahomaclinic.com)

“Dupuytren’s contracture” and “Peyronie’s disease” are two “fibrotic” conditions that can be helped considerably by SSKI. In Dupuytren’s contracture, thickening (fibrosis) occurs along one of the tendons in the palm in the hand, pulling the related finger down towards the palm. As the problem progresses, the finger often can’t be straightened any more.

In Peyronie’s disease, a very similar thickening occurs along the shaft of the penis, making erections increasing “curved” and painful. In both cases, rubbing SSKI into the thickened tissue at least twice daily softens and lessens the fibrotic area over a period of several months, allowing for more normal function.

For these conditions, it’s additionally helpful to take para-aminobenzoic acid (PABA) 2 grams, three times daily, and to rub a mixture of Vitamin E and DMSO into the thickened areas, also. However, if “caught early”, SSKI alone will often “do the job”. (It’s also advisable to have glucose-insulin tolerance test done, as there’s an unusually high incidence of “insulin resistance” in people with Dupuytren’s contracture or Peyronie’s disease.

“Keloids” are abnormally thick scars, sometimes as much as an inch thick, that can form after injury. Although anyone can get a keloid, they’re more common among African Americansthan other ethnic groups. Rubbing SSKI into a keloid at least twice daily will ultimately flatten them down to a “normal scar”, but it can take many months to a year for particularly bad ones. The treatment goes faster if SSKI is mixed “50-50” with DMSO.

“Fistulas” are literally abnormal tunnels through tissues, “tunnels” prevented from healing by chronic infection. Two not-unusual types are “peri-anal fistulas” (a “tunnel” from outside the anus to the inside of the rectum) and “recto-vaginal” fistulas. Although these fistulas can be treated successfully by surgery, they can often be healed by frequent swabbing inside and out with an SSKI-soaked “Q-tip”. Patience is required: Complete healing often takes several months. The treatment appears to work better if the SSKI is mixed with DMSO, which enhances “penetrance”. My colleague Richard Kunin, M.D. of San Francisco, is a “world class” expert on the use of SSKI and other forms of iodine. He has found that hemorrhoids will sometimes disappear literally overnight, when SSKI (20 drops) mixed with flaxseed oil (1 ounce) is rubbed in them at bedtime. He’s also found that SSKI alone will do the same job, although it “really stings” when applied to a hemorrhoid by itself.

When I was a pre-med student at Harvard University, the famous chemistry professor Louis Feiser made a point of demonstrating to all the pre-medical students that iodine and iodide would make oils, fats, and waxes (cholesterol is actually a wax) more soluble in water. He urged us to remember this in our medical practices, as he was sure it wouldn’t be taught in medical school (he was right!). This known action of iodide likely explains why SSKI can be useful in the next two applications.

Over 30 years ago, two ophthalmologists observed that a combination tablet called “Iodo-niacin” (iodide 120 milligrams, niacin 15 milligrams) taken for several months could actually reverse atherosclerotic clogging of arteries. They proved this effect by taking pictures of clogged arteries in the backs of the eyes (“retinal photomicrographs”) before and after treatment. The published photographs showed a significant lessening of the cholesterol-laden artery clogging in the “after” pictures.

Amazingly enough, no follow-up study has ever been published (probably because niacin and iodide aren’t patentable). Despite this, the published pictures speak clearly for themselves. I recommend 4 to 6 drops of SSKI and niacin-containing B-complex daily (along with many other things) for anyone with significant cholesterol-related atherosclerotic clogging. Thyroid function must be monitored!

“Sebaceous cysts” are cysts which contain oily, fatty material. They usually appear rather suddenly on the face or in the groin or labia. Rubbing in SSKI mixed 50-50 with DMSO will almost always persuade these cysts to go away in a week or two; it appears that the iodide “dissolves” the fatty, oily material contained in the cysts, allowing your body to slowly re-absorb and dispose of it.

Although it’s not a common condition, from time to time someone will come to my office at Tahoma Clinic inquiring about alternatives to surgery for “parotid duct stone”. These are “stones” which can form in the saliva-carrying duct(s) from the major salivary glands (“parotid” glands, located at the “angle of the jaw”). 3 to 4 drops of SSKI taken in water daily will almost always dissolve parotid duct stones within four to eight months.

If you have chronic bronchitis and or emphysema (“COPD”, “COLD”) SSKI is an invaluable tool. SSKI “gets into” all body secretions, including often thick and hard to cough up bronchial secretions, which get infected very easily. SSKI takes care of both of these problems. It “loosens” secretions remarkably, making them much easier to “clear”, and it prevents micro-organisms from growing easily. With regular SSKI use, bronchial infection is a much less frequent happening. Depending on the severity of COPD, I recommend 3 to 6 drops of SSKI taken in water once daily. As COPD is usually a chronic condition, SSKI use will usually be indefinite, so make sure to monitor your thyroid function! (See the August 2002 Nutrition & Healing for a more complete discussion of natural COPD treatment.)

Now, back to other infections. For this group, using SSKI mixed “50-50” with DMSO works better, as the DMSO enables SSKI to penetrate much more deeply into the tissues and kill germs.

Infected “hangnails” are perhaps the easiest to clear up this way, as are nagging bacterial infections around the edges of the toenails. Rub in the mixture several times daily, and the problem’s usually gone in a few days. Herpes simplex (“herpes”) ourbreaks can be “stopped cold” in the same way, but it often takes longer for the “sore” to heal itself over.

If you have persistent “swollen glands” in the throat or groin areas, see a doctor first! These can very rarely be signs of leukemia, lymphoma, or another cancer, especially in older people. But if all tests and studies are negative, and the doctor says “it’s just swollen glands”, rub in the SSKI with DMSO. In a large majority of cases, the “just swollen glands” will gradually fade away.

Fungus under the toenails (“onychomycosis”) is a difficult problem to treat. Even “conventional” anti-fungal drug treatment takes months to work, and (for safety) monthly liver function tests are necessary. SSKI and DMSO rubbed on, around, and under the affected toenails doesn’t work any faster, but it’s just as effective, and definitely safer. Make sure to wear old socks, because SSKI and other forms of iodine leave an orange-brown stain. (Other safe and effective alternatives include geranium oil, oregano oil, and tea tree oil. These and other anti-fungal oils also require “help” from DMSO to penetrate the toenail and soak the fungus underneath.)

SSKI can also help clear up vaginal infections. 20 to 30 drops in water, used in a small “douche” once daily for five to ten days will usually do the job. (There’s actually a prescription-only iodine preparation of available for vaginal infections, too.) However, iodine preparations of any sort for vaginal infections are often not popular because of the inevitable orange-brown stains they leave on clothing.

What about SSKI to help a “weak” thyroid (hypothyroidism)? Even though iodine and iodide are absolutely essential to thyroid hormone formation, in most “developed” countries, hypothyroid conditions are not usually due to an outright lack of iodine or iodide. Still, hypothyroidism is occasionally helped by 1 drop of SSKI daily. Make sure to work with your doctor on this one!

Lastly, there’s a gastronomic use for SSKI: reducing the gas we all get from eating beans! If you’re soaking beans before cooking them, add 1 or 2 drops of SSKI, and let them soak for an hour or more. (Pour offf that water before cooking, and add fresh water.) You’ll be surprised at the reduction in resulting intestinal gas! (For those who want a technical explanation: There’s a naturally occurring enzyme inhibitor in beans which interferes with starch digestion, producing gas. SSKI inactivates this enzyme inhibitor.)

**OBTAINING SSKI**

In the past, SSKI and other forms of iodine and iodide were available only on prescription, or through chemical supply sources. Since the mid-1990s passage of the Federal DSHEA law, we’ve regained the basic American freedom to buy and sell natural substances (narcotics exempted) without prescription. You haven’t read or heard much about SSKI because (despite Federal Appeals Court decisions) FDA still maintains it’s First Amendment violating policy of complete suppression of truthful product use information on package labels or in advertisements….unless of course FDA is paid an enormous amount of money ($250 million minimum, according to Congressional testimony) for “approval”. Since SSKI can’t be patented…well, you know the rest.

SSKI can be obtained without prescription in some compounding pharmacies, some health food stores, through “on-line” sources, and at the Tahoma Clinic Dispensary (with which I am of course affiliated) in a convenient travel-size dropper bottle.

**USE SSKI SAFELY!!**

There are three “hazards” to using SSKI: staining, allergy, and a very small possibility of thyroid suppression with longer-term use of “too much”.

Staining can be a big nuisance, but it’s not a health hazard. When SSKI is applied to skin, it can impart a faint to moderate orange-brown color, which fades away once SSKI is no longer being applied. SSKI and other iodine stains in clothing can be semi-permanent or permanent, so don’t plan to wear anything “nice” in the vicinity of recently-applied SSKI.

Iodine allergy is a possibility, although in nearly 30 years of medical practice I’ve seen it only a few times. Usually, it causes a red, bumpy skin rash, which goes away after SSKI or other iodine is discontinued. Topical (applied to the skin surface) iodine allergy is almost never a serious emergency.

SSKI or iodine can very occasionally cause acne, which also goes away once the source of iodine is dicontinued.

The type of so-called “iodine allergy” that can interfere with breathing and occasionally sends us to the emergency room is usually not allergy to iodine or iodine molecules, b**ut instead to much larger, possibly iodine-containing molecules found in lobster, crab, clams and other “shellfish”.** These molecules are not present in SSKI or iodine. However, if there’s any suspicion at all of iodine allergy, it’s best not to swallow any without testing for allergy or sensitivity.

Too much iodine for too long can suppress thyroid function. Many of the uses described for SSKI in the accompanying article are short-term, from a few days or less to a week or two. If SSKI is then stopped, there’s almost no chance of significant thyroid suppression. However, if SSKI is to be used for two to three weeks or longer, and especially if it’s to be used continuously (for example, COPD or cholesterol-related atherosclerosis) monitoring thyroid function is very important. To find a physician near you who can help order and interpret thyroid function tests contact [Meridian Valley Lab](http://meridianvalleylab.com/).

Fortunately, in my experience with over a thousand individuals, internal use of than nine drops of SSKI daily has or less has very rarely resulted in thyroid suppression. On those very few occasions, discontinuance has resulted in prompt recovery. So far, I’ve never seen thyroid suppression result from “topical” (skin surface) use of SSKI.

A final “safety” note: Dr. Kunin cautions (and I agree) that if you use SSKI or other iodine “long-term”, make sure your diet contains plenty of essential fatty acids (both omega-3 and omega-6) as well as the sulfur-containing amino acids methionine and cysteine. If you eat animal protein daily, that’s usually sufficient as a source of these two amino acids, but if you’re vegetarian (or close) and using “long term” SSKI or other iodine, then take 300-500 milligrams of each daily.

**IODINE, IODIDE, WHAT’S THE DIFFERENCE?**

Iodine is a basic element, as are calcium, zinc, oxygen and other elements. The word “iodine” usually refers to two iodine molecules chemically “stuck together” (I2), just as the word “oxygen” usually refers to two oxygen molecules “stuck together” (O2). Since iodine is more reactive, and therefore more likely to cause problems, iodine is usually used as “iodide”, a word which refers to one iodine molecule combined with another molecule such as potassium (KI) or sodium (NaI). In chemical terms, such molecules are called “salts”; the best known salt is sodium chloride (NaCl), a “salt” of chlorine (Cl2).

The “SS” in “SSKI” refers to “Saturated Solution Potassium Iodide”. Other medically useful forms of iodine include “Lugol’s solution”, invented by Dr. Lugol of Paris in the 1840s, which contains a mixture of types of iodine and iodide, and “di-atomic iodine”, which is another name for iodine, but usually prepared as a solid in a capsule instead of a liquid.

_\-Jonathan V. Wright, MD_

You have been sold on nascent. I am not.

\==================================================
You have two big problems to solve, well three:

get the best light that you can arrange,
even if it is mostly artificial

Get vit D to the level I suggested, and do it using light and no supplements.

Get your TSH to within (0.5 -2), and FT3 within upper 1/4 of laboratory range, both at the same time

After you get all three above right
wait six months or more, re-evaluate.

\================================
Look up my posts above for the group of tests that you should do every 6 months,
**in my opinion, I am not a doctor and do not have any medical education.**

///////

stroke, diabetes, obesity, and other diseases.”

To get natural vit D you need UVb **and** sulfur (garilc, onions, leeks, MSM)
To get naturtal vit A you need UVa **and** carotenoids (carrots, peppers)

White Sperti = UVb
Red Sperti = UVa + UVb

This master photonic hormone acts in the brain as an energy homeostasis regulating factor that triggers a decrease in food intake and an increase in energy consumption by inducing anorexigenic factors and suppressing orexigenic factors by inducing size and shape changes of other photonic hormones in the brain during day and night cycles in a complicated quantum dance. Since leptin is responsive to food it implies it really pays deep attention to spectral frequencies from light in the human environment to alter growth metabolism body wide. This effect causes eating-related hormones dynamism diurnally and seasonally, but also depends on energy status created locally by mitochondrial flux. Leptin modulates the ENTIRE TSH axis. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tweaks-to-dutch-hormone-test-results.18510/post-225756>

Would love to hear your take on: (does this mean I should stick with seafood and away from land protein? Or will digestive enzyemes/gutflora changes/light allow me to go back to sat fats eventually?)

PPAR gamma rs1801282(C;G) abnormal fat metabolism

Peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor gamma (PPAR-γ or PPARG) is a master regulator of fatty acid storage and glucose metabolism. The genes activated by PPARG stimulate lipid uptake and adipogenesis by fat cells. It increases both lipid metabolism and adipogenesis in fat tissue. It increases insulin sensitivity in muscle tissue and increases gluconeogenesis in the liver. PPARG is mostly found in adipose tissue, colon and immune cells called macrophages.

**You may have an abnormal fat metabolism**. When people with the genotype (C,G) or (G,G) have a low polyunsaturated and monounsaturated fat intake and a high saturated fat intake then their obesity and type 2 diabetes risks are dramatically increased. But when there is a higher mono- and poly-unsaturated intake and a lower saturated fat intake their obesity and type 2 diabetes risk is normal. Polyunsaturated fatty acids activate the PPAR-gamma gene.

Monounsaturated fat is found in foods such as avocado, olive oil, and nuts. Polyunsaturated fats are found in foods like fatty fish such as salmon, herring and polyunsaturated fat is also found in nuts. Saturated fat is found in fatty beef, pork, butter, cheese, and other dairy products.

Individuals with the genotypes (C,G) or (G,G) may want to make sure their dietary fat is higher in mono- and poly-unsaturated fat and lower in saturated fat.

Pterostilbene is a plant compound present in blueberries, cranberries, and almonds [have been shown to activate PPAR-gamma (and PPAR-alpha) and lower triglycerides, and blood glucose](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3649683/)

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=225421)
>
> I can raise HDL by drinking. Nothing else needed. Now what I drink to get the effect............pay attention. the next few weeks. An education is coming.

# Thread: Blue-blocking pc screen (teleporting sunlight)

### smith5 — 2016-10-03T12:48:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocking-pc-screen-teleporting-sunlight.18514/post-202227>

Are there any blue-blocking pc and smartphone screens? It sounds kind of not very optimal to print out hundreds of books and the blog with multiple hyperlinks and then carrying out this luggage with oneself instead of just reading them and making notes on a pc.
It would be a bit more optimal to have this screen also to emit IR while being on a pc to mimic sunlight.
As housings can be quite probably assumed to be an extrapolation of neocortex, the necessity of being outside for a while doesn't mean the impossibility of recreating full-spectrum sunlight wherever one might decide to.
On the other hand, information about photon teleportation has been in the news for several years. I'd suppose optimal use of the technology might imply adapting it to one's goals and needs rather than getting in opposition to it.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-03T15:19:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocking-pc-screen-teleporting-sunlight.18514/post-202229>

you cannot teleport sunlight........you can only capture it and use it.

### Lahelada — 2016-10-03T17:39:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocking-pc-screen-teleporting-sunlight.18514/post-202231>

To illustrate the problem let's do what in German is called a milkmaid's calculation. For this we assume sunlight is measured in grams :
Your natural sunlight contains 1gram of IR light
and 1g of UV and
1g of blue.
Now you add the blue from your PC .That is another 1 g .

So you have 1 g more... Big deal...
Big deal!
You have the full 1 g from the sun for 2 hours normally lets say , from the PC you have it for a full 7 hours if you work with it. Your second mismatch. Your third mismatch is your FULL spectrum bulb as you add extra blue. So you only add UV and IR. Still a mismatch as you need to account for your latitude and the season and calculate which percentage you are receiving naturally and how much you are assimilating. It helps but is not anywhere near optimal. Somebody correct me if I am wrong, but we do not know the exact quantity of what we receive/assimilate when in "grams"

We only just rediscovered that we need sunlight. Just as we have lost our instinct for what to eat when, we should treat the light thing with the same caution. Dr K quite rightly says that humans are the only animal that needs to be taught what to eat. The same goes for light I think. We are re learning,solving our dilemma, getting to optimal maybe. To get to optimal you need to read while avoiding artificial light. Printing and writing is good for you. The trees? We sacrifice them now for much lesser goals.

I am a teacher and a lot of my students cannot write by hand anymore. There is even a study which says that students in New York answered a poll saying they are not able to sign a contract anymore as they have lost the art of writing in cursive.

How long did it take us to develop written language? How long did it take us to lose it...If you have not written by hand for a long time try cursive writing at speed. The protests you may get from your brain..

### smith5 — 2016-10-08T13:18:35+0300

### smith5 — 2016-12-11T12:20:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocking-pc-screen-teleporting-sunlight.18514/post-205124>

I discarded static PC's for some time and I'm using f.lux on my notebook with 2700K day and 1200K night (set at minimum) colour temperatures, and Twilight on the smartphone at ~1800K colour temperature and ~50-100% intensity and I'm VERY MUCH OK with that. I do not use wi-fi at home at all, although there are up to 10 wi-fi spots in the vicinity, but if I move to live in a very low wi-fi and sunlight abundant area 20km from where I'm now next year I'd most probably get the 1 wi-fi spot for myself in order to work and study for the Quantlet. I still do not find reading the blog in other formats better than it is now, especially when you need to simultaneously consult other sources of information which are also in electronic form.

I measured the magnetic field in the vicinity of my notebook with the Samsung Galaxy S5 and generally it's not higher than the background (40-50 microT), it's twice as high only in the closest (~1 cm) vicinity of the power cord outlet and near the number keyboard, and I consider it's VERY MUCH OK.

Generally speaking, I do not find a kind of a sense from an (classic) evolutionary standpoint that just a few freaks like Tesla and Edison (having highly abnormal sleep patterns - from our contemporary perspective) installed analogs of their nervous system into the nervous system of those who have way more information about this system and the latter can't do much about it (at least on a statistical scale).

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-11T15:32:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocking-pc-screen-teleporting-sunlight.18514/post-205139>

You're not looking deep enough because you are stuck on your current beliefs. So let us expand on this. Darwin had high mitochondria heteroplasmy and this is why his sleep sucked he was always sick and why he poured cold water on himself incessantly to feel better. History is littered with high heteroplasmy stories. Henry the 8th is another.

Photomultiplier use will prove this all once they become cheap enough for public consumption. Right now we have the GOME satellite that proves it from space but no one understands how to use for population mito-hacks.
Nature is axiomatic in telegraphing to us that "things do not have a reason"; There are a given a set of thermodynamic constraints, and Nature finds a way to work with those constraints.
The major constraints on this planet as far as we can ascertain:

1\. The contents of the atmosphere only allows a narrow band of light to be used -- the higher energy particles get blocked from the organisms of this earth. ie: UV radiation is more scarce that IR radation.

2\. Water happens to exists in huge abundance, and when charge-separated on this planet happens to have a huge absorption peak at the IR-A range and a smaller band at 250-270nm

If I were a deity with quantum power, I would see these constraints, and design my "replication activator" to be sensitive to the scarce photons that can be transferred and emitted by water.

All 4 DNA bases contain a pyrimidine ring, which will absorb the 380nm band. This absorption is fundamentally excitatory in nature and can be our stimulant to growth.

To make use of a piece of DNA for protein transcription, the strand of DNA needs to become "more uncondensed" by unwinding and exposing the correct sequence of bases. This is where chromatin and histone proteins work and why they were evolved early on. They are highly conserved in evolution. This explains why you need weak signals to uncondense DNA slight for protein synthesis, but strong signals leave you with completely uncondensed DNA that is functionally destroyed and unusable by sunlight in most cases. Unwinding replication is danagerous for life so the stability of the electromagnetic message must be contained in coiled form to protect its magnetic message from the environment.

EZ water absorbs light very well with a UV and IR absorbance wavelength range and it has an emission spectra at 270 nm. This affects refraction and diffraction of the hydrated semiconductors created in our ER/Golgi apparatus. During this process, it also emits light (fluorescence). I can't find exactly what wavelengths are fluoresced, but the general rule of thumb is that the photons lose some energy, and the fluorescent photons are of longer wavelength.
If you look at the absorption spectrum of EZ water, it tapers off somewhere in 330nm range. This likely means that any photons emitting by water have to be from 330nm and upward.
The short proximity of water and 380nm-absorbing compounds containing a nitrogen-containing hexagonal ring mean that information transfer using 380nm light is pretty efficient.

270nm is mitogenic for eukaryotes. "Mitogenic" just means "activates mitogen" and therefore cell division. Alexander Gurwitsch and his onion experiments with quartz and glass.
If I were a quantum deity, I would recognize that membrane-bound EZ water would increase the size of that EZ significantly when exposed to (relatively scarce) 270nm light. This increase in EZ would increase cell membrane potential (assuming DHA is adequate there) , increase viscosity, increase optical density (more red-shift; ie -- toward the 380nm wavelength), and exert extra pressure (solitons and phonons) on the cell membrane to create excitons for mitochondria.
I would let this be a "cell division" signal, just for the arbitrary reason that the effects of 270nm light can amplify the factors I mention in the previous paragraph (given the thermodynamic constraints on Earth)
Molecular carcinogens absorb 380nm photon (Fritz Popp alert) and re-emit those photons at different wavelengths. DNA also relies on emission of this 380nm to signal other nearby DNA to do the appropriate thing.

This frequency signal is very confusing to an emitting DNA because of the Faraday effect. It effects the optical and mangetic componets of signaling in EZ water which can be generalized throught the cell at the speed of light. It is very likely, the 380nm photons they emit are entangled to the local electrons present in the pyrimidine rings because benzene rings trap UV light and are playgrounds for UV photons. The pyrimidine rings are also the functional groups needed for protein transcription in the ER/Golgi system which is connected to the mitochondria by way of the outermitochondrial membrane and a web of carbon nanotubes that are filled with water. These tubes confine water to 1.4 nm to create a nature semiconductor fab production unit in the ER/Golgi system.
New biophysics data have shown that RNA and DNA has an unusual spectral pattern of light it absorbs and emits. The current belief in both dermatology and ophthalmology specialties is that UVA and UVB light is something humans should avoid. But if we are to accept this belief, why does the skin and cornea have a UVA light receptor in it from birth? Moreover, if UV light is so horrible for human cells, ask yourself why DNA has been shown in thousands of experiments to have a spectrum of fluorescence peak at 350 nm? That peak is 100% in the UV range. Why would this be the case if this radiation is said to destroy DNA/RNA? Moreover, there are two proteins that are tightly associated with DNA/RNA, called histones and chromatin. Both proteins have been found by experiment to delay this UV light release in the DNA of skin cells that lower mitosis in normal cell lines? This raises the point that UV light exposure might not be the cause of cancers, it actually maybe part of the "breaking mechanism" for unopposed chronic blue light exposure that really is the villian in diseases that are driving % heteroplasmy higher.
The only way you know that a signal has been received is to get a signal back in tissues and in cells. In this case, the emitting DNA strand will get a signal back by virtue of absorption of its emitted entangled photons by another DNA strand.

Carcinogens will disrupt this process by absorbing those emitted entangled photons, and then re-emitting mixed signals. In any case, lacking the same magnitude of negative feedback, emitting DNA strands ramp-up their activity, and this can lead to uncontrolled growth because the heat shock protein ubiquitin can no long work properly because the Faraday effect signal is interfered with it.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-11T15:35:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocking-pc-screen-teleporting-sunlight.18514/post-205142>

When the energy in ATP is utilized during cell work in a process called ATP hydrolysis, protons are naturally produced. In REM sleep no ATP is used. Water EZ is the key to energy generation. Let's jump down this rabbit hole. With ATP use, the mitochondria normally incorporate these protons back into ATP via the ATPase, thus preventing buildup of protons and maintaining neutral pH. High pH forms larger EZ's and low pH destroys EZ's. If oxygen supply is inadequate (hypoxia or pseudohypoxia), the mitochondria are unable to continue ATP synthesis at a rate sufficient to supply the cell with the required ATP. In this situation, glycolysis is increased to provide additional ATP, and the excess pyruvate produced is converted into lactateand released from the cell into the bloodstream, where it accumulates over time. The brain hates superoxide. It also despises fatty acids. It's a bit ambivalent about glucose, contrary to popular dogma. It does not reject glucose, but it is built to recognize there are better fuels to be used.

Is there any fuel the brain does like? Well, ketone bodies seem to be reasonable, but what the CNS/PNS really seems to like is lactate. Glucose is often fed to neurons, but is always pre digested by the glial cells, as lactate. This is not well known. The FFAs are fed as ketones, yes, the glial cells in the brain are ketogenic. They replenish NAD+. The brain is not just the onlt tissue that does this, the liver that does this too. This is why blood glucose really rises in pseudohypoxic low NAD+ states........it is not a food or fuel story. It is a lack of oxygen story. What atoms is your terminal electron selector is a real big deal. When O2 is lacking you can't make free radicals and you cannot release ELF UV bio photons well to signal properly. Your cells begin too release too much light. When you release too much light, it is not monochromatic. Too much light release correlates with higher ubiquitination rates. While increased glycolysis helps compensate for less ATP from oxidative phosphorylation, it cannot bind the protons created resulting from ATP hydrolysis. Therefore, proton concentration rises and causes acidosis. The EZ is destroyed. Lactate doesn't need any glycolysis to take place simultaneously in neurons themselves. Lactate is the most pure substrate for complex I with low NAD+. This is why the brain and heart love lactate fuel but most other cells do not. Lactate usage appears to be the best way of postponing apoptosis. Apoptosis is not something you want if your a neuron or cardiomyocyte is it? It might be if you are a gut enterocyte...........CONTEXT matters. Frequency of light provides the context of how a cell can or cannot work.

### smith5 — 2017-02-12T20:09:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocking-pc-screen-teleporting-sunlight.18514/post-208474>

I find it quite out of balance when basing on various quantum information the main strategy (and usage of ATP-time) shifts towards escapism rather than transformation. What about 6 month polar circadian cycles then? Biosystems living in there have probably developed some adaptation.
The developers of LED lightining and similar tecgnologies were probably not those rare 90% hydrocephals who were still able to work as accountants and ride bicycles.
There are fluorescent unpolarized light sources and the light coming from the sun is not always uniformly unpolarized because it is scattered and reflected in the upper atmosphere and the clouds gaining some polarization. I do not bias towards inward or outward, just proposing an experimental scheme for sunlight replication (that first appeared with no systematic elaboration), but if you are truly unbiased you'd just estimate the difference for a local biosystem rather than taking into account the terawatts of unused energy from sunlight that hit earth's surface every second.
What about to just wear an overall made from a metal coated netting fabric (thus allocating the dopamine and cortisol for more fundamental issues)?

# Thread: Elizabeth's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elizabeths-journal.18515/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Miss Lizzie — 2016-10-03T21:32:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elizabeths-journal.18515/post-202233>

Hello,

I'm a newbie and it was suggested I start a journal entry, so here I am. Bear with me. I will try to be as succinct as possible.

Background: Haplogroup H5. Blonde hair, pale skin and brown eyes. 99.9% Northern European. Pretty boring. :/ My mother is first gen born in Canada. Her family is Scottish, Irish and English. My father's side of the family is English/German. Father's side of family has severe history with Crohn's disease. My grandfather almost died from it. Also a tendency to develop type 2 diabetes. That side tends to be pretty overweight. Mother's side is a train wreck. Dairy allergies, chronic fatigue, hayfever, inability to deal with stress, sleep walking... you name it.

I work at a university and have a stressful desk job. Co-workers and boss are great, but it's a deadline driven, high stakes position. I do like the work though. I always enjoy a challenge.

I grew up in Vancouver, BC the land of rain.

\-Age 6-22 chronic bronchitis with antibiotic treatment.
\-Age 10 puberty
\-Age 13-19 erythromycin everyday for bad acne
\-Age 17-23 symptoms of hypothyroid, but no treatment. (Canadian drs wouldn't run the tests)
\-Age 18-22 university with screwed up circadian rhythms.
\- Age 19-28 IBS/crohn's symptoms.
**\-Age 23 moved to Santa Barbara, CA. No more bronchitis!**
\-Age 24 diagnosed with hypothryoid. Normal TSH, high T4, 0 T3. Put on synthroid and cytomel
\-Age 24 bad car accident. Sandwiched between two SUVs on the 101.
\-Age 26 surgery to L4,L5 microdiscectomy and laminectomy.
\-Age 28 severe crohn's flare up with intestinal bleeding. Also got a horrible case of shingles that year.
\-Age 31 did GAPS diet and protocol to heal gut. No more crohn's and also fixed my thyroid.
\-Age 31 no more thyroid meds needed.

At age 34, I had my first child. I made sure all my hormone levels, vitamin levels, omega levels were good before getting pregnant. Got pregnant first try. At 6 weeks, hyperemesis kicked in and I puked everyday until I gave birth. I gave birth to a super health 8lb 5oz baby boy. Only gained 20lbs. I breast fed for 18 months and was a milk cow. I would pump about 50oz/day! I felt pretty good during that time. Baby slept well and started sleeping through the night at 8 weeks. Lost all my pregnancy weight plus some.

That brings me to the present. I'm 36 and had my third health crash. About 3 months after I stopped breastfeeding, I had started suffering from severe exhaustion, brain fog, weight gain (30lbs) and insomnia. I'd be exhausted in the morning and wired at night. Any exercise made me want to sleep for a week. It was like my body just decided to crap out on me and give me a giant \*&%^ you! I gave it a couple of months, since I thought it might just be hormones adjusting. It didn't feel like a thyroid problem. I went to see my doctor. My tests came back and it pointed to screwed up adrenals. So I started doing tons of research. This ended up in going down the rabbit hole and now I'm posting here.

My test results from May showed:

CBC with differential- all normal except RBC
RBC count -5.59H range 3.8-5.10
Odd thing. My RBC count is always out of range high, unless I have intestinal bleeding... then it slips to normal.

Comp Metabolic Panel. -Everything normal.

Glucose- 94 range 75-99
CRP High sensitivity 0.4 range < 3.1

Lipid Panel
Triglycerides - 58 range <150mg/dL
Cholesterol - 174 range <200 mg/dL
HDL - 63 range >40mg/dL
LDL- 99 range <100mg/dL
VLDL- 12 range 7-35mg/dL
Chol/HDL Ratio 2.8

Thyroid
Free t3 3.2 range 2.3-4.2 pg/mL
Free t4 1.0 range 0.8-1.7 ng/dL
TSH 1.920 range 0.350-5.5 ulU/mL

Vitamin D 25-OH total - 37 range 30-100ng/mL I was also supplementing with 5000 iu of D3 per day at the time of test.

DHEA-sulfate 94 range 26-460 ug/dL

My doctor did not do a cortisol test, so I'm blind in that area. However, with my symptoms and my vitamin d and DHEA being low, it definitely seems to point to an HPA axis dysfunction.

Starting in September, I've been doing the Leptin Rx and modifying my environment at home and work.

At work, I have installed a stand up desk, turned off all the lights in my office, installed F.Lux on my computer and wear blue light blockers. I do have a window, but it's north facing and receives no direct sunlight. I make sure that I walk and get sunlight a minimum to 2x per day, using my D-minder app. My coworkers have started coming with me!

At home, we have a south facing house with tons of windows. They are open during the day and we never have to turn the lights on until it gets dark. We now turn on minimal lights, wear blue blockers and have amber lights and salt lamps to keep the blue light at a minimum. All electronics go into airplane mode and I have black out blinds in the room. My 2.5 year old also sleeps in a dark room with no night lights. He goes down at 8 and wakes up at 6 pretty much everyday. I go to bed at 10 and wake up at 6. I do cold therapy at night after dinner.

I can say that my circadian rhythms have switched back to normal! I'm tired at bed time, no insomnia and relatively awake in the morning (takes me a full hour to wake up). I lost 10.5lbs last month and the brain fog is clearing up.

I'm going to go back to my doctor this month for follow up tests to see where I'm at. I'm also going to have him test my omega levels this time too.

### Miss Lizzie — 2016-10-04T21:18:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elizabeths-journal.18515/post-202283>

I forgot to add my hemoglobin and hematocrit.

Hemoglobin 14.1 range 11-15.5 g/dL
Hematocrit 44.4 range 33.0-46.5%

### Miss Lizzie — 2016-10-20T20:34:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elizabeths-journal.18515/post-203124>

A little update. I still haven't made it back to my doctor for follow up tests, but I have been implementing more things around our home and my office.

I start work at 8 AM so morning sunbathing is a little hard. I do go out into my back yard, stand in the wet grass and look directly into the sun for about 5 min before leaving for work. I can now look directly into the morning sun without tearing up or blinking.

I have started riding my bike to work and on my ride to work, I face directly into the sun. I wear a tank top so more of my skin is exposed. It's cold in the morning now, so I get both the cold effect (although pretty minimal) and direct sun on my upper body, face and eyes for 15 min. On the way home, I'm also facing directly into the sun.

At work, I go outside and get sunshine for 15 min at 10 AM, 12 PM and 3 PM. No sunglasses and I have started wearing a tank top under my clothes, so that when I go out, I take off whatever long sleeve shirt/dress I'm wearing to get more skin exposure.

I've been getting amber bulbs and salt lamps and placing them all over the house so we can have really low light at night. Also wear my blue blockers and have ordered a pair for my two year old and my husband. I've been getting push back from hubby on this. He will grudgingly wear the blue blockers sometimes, but he thinks I'm going over board.

As for diet, I'm having my BAB within an hour of waking up (2 year old generally prevents the within 30 min of waking) and now I'm not eating lunch! I'm simply not that hungry. I might have a small snack of some raw nuts, but that's usually it. Dinner is pretty small too. Usually just a protein and some vegetables. Of course drinking tons of spring water during the day.

We have a whole house water filter, but it doesn't filter for fluoride. Our water in Santa Barbara has naturally occurring fluoride. Luckily they don't add any. They periodically try to get it passed, but everyone always votes against it. The naturally occurring fluoride is at about half of the government "recommended" amount. I've bought a water filter that will filter out the fluoride and will be installing that this weekend. It's a triple filter that will also get rid of other unwanted contaminants. I bought a device to help structure water and I'm going to do some experiments on plants to see if there is any merit to it. I have a science degree and am a bit skeptical by nature and a big nerd, so I like to test everything. I figure that if I see a difference in the plants watered with the "structured" water vs regular water, there is probably something happening.

**I've been looking into crystals, shungite and orgonite. I'd like to place some in my office at work and maybe around our house. I'm a bit confused as to what is best to use and where to place it. Can anyone weigh in on this? I was thinking of getting a shungite braclet to wear too.**

I'm saving up for a magnetico sleep pad. I think this is going to help a lot.

I'm reading the fourth phase of water and John Ott's book. I shouldn't be surprised, but I'm really angry that this information (particularly about the sun) has been out there for quite some time and most people/scientists/government have been ignoring it!!!

I'm slowly but surely changing our house and my work space. Our diet is spot on, but it's that three-legged stool that I'm focusing on. Diet isn't going to matter much if I don't get that set up correctly. My health is definitely better, but still needs a lot more improvement to be optimal. The big thing for me is not giving up because I get impatient. It's taken a lifetime to do the damage to my body, so it's going to take time to heal it. I'm trying to intervene early for my son, so he can continue to grow up healthy and strong.

### Miss Lizzie — 2016-11-01T23:21:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elizabeths-journal.18515/post-203495>

Hi Jan,

Thanks for the info. I am actually taking a DHEA supplement right now. At first it made my skin break out, but now it's settled down.

I'm a little confused about using the Sperti light. Jack has said that nothing replaces sunlight. I live in Southern California, so we have good light all year round. I use the D-minder app so that I don't burn. I'm very fair skinned and my husband calls me stain glass Lizzy for a reason. I get out 3 times per day during the week when I'm at work (I go down to the beach and lay in the sun on my lunch break at least twice a week, and I also lay out on the weekends.

I get up in the morning and go stand outside in the sun barefoot for about 15 min too.

As for the red light- well, I have hit a brick wall with my husband, so salt lamps and amber lights it is for now. I tried to use red bulbs and he completely balked at that. I go to bed at 10 PM every night and wake up at 6 am. On weekends, without an alarm, I wake up between 6 and 7 am.

I did have blood panels when I was pregnant and everything was fine. Progesterone and all steroids were good. As soon as I passed my placenta, all the nausea and vomiting stopped. I thought it would take a lot longer, and was very surprised that it just suddenly stopped and I immediately felt better.

Funny thing is, my son received sunlight treatment from the day he was born. The midwives told me to take him out, butt naked and put him in the sun for 10 mins front and back for the first week he was born so that he didn't get jaundiced. He still runs around naked in the back yard and has a beautiful, light tan all over his body. He's never had a sunburn and he is as fair as me.

Interesting thing... I just read the blog on Hashimoto's and Melasma. I had a pretty big melasma on my forehead. Since starting this protocol, it's been fading and disappearing even with all of the extra sun. I had no idea why, but I was happy about it! Now I know why it's getting better!! My eyes are also getting better and my prescription keeps dropping. Maybe I'll be back to no glasses one day.

I am considering various options as to what I will do in the near future.Biohack , permanent move etc.
Something has come to my attention that I had never considered.
My questions
1 . AB is a blood group only a 1000 years old with the invasion of the mongols into Europe. Do I have leeway to change percentages between light water and magnetism for that reason ? The area I am considering has hot summers and very cold winters,no seafood to speak of unless you consider cans . However magnetism and water must be great as it is possible to do CT in the mountains all year long.

### Lahelada — 2016-10-04T13:16:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-group-ab-quite-rare-is-it-a-game-changer-in-our-world.18516/post-202254>

Me too. Well, I mean the positive part. The AB was already clear from my question I think.

We have to cede the rarest blood type title to another one not very often mentioned,though.
From Wikipedia : "**Bombay phenotype** has an interesting blood type that reacted to other blood types in a way never seen before. The serum contained [antibodies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antibody) that reacted with all [red blood cells](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Red_blood_cell)' normal [ABO phenotypes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ABO_phenotypes). The red blood cells appeared to lack all of the ABO blood group antigens and to have an additional antigen that was previously unknown."

### Sue-UK — 2016-10-07T15:14:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-group-ab-quite-rare-is-it-a-game-changer-in-our-world.18516/post-202451>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=202241)
>
> input,thoughts ,facts ,gut feelings all is welcome.

Peter D'Adamo 's book The Genotype Diet dips into the fetal landscape and blood type. Comparisons between torso and leg length, whether your upper leg bone is longer than the lower, ring and index finger measurements, asymmetry, finger print patterns, dentition etc. It's like we have a physical imprint of our time in the womb. In his system IIRC each blood type could fall into one of 3 categories. Me and DD are the same but DS2 is different, he's the canary in the coal mine, although we share the same blood type. (O neg). Looking forward to Jack's blog!

### Martha Ray — 2019-12-31T06:37:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-group-ab-quite-rare-is-it-a-game-changer-in-our-world.18516/post-284094>

Like with APOE 4 multiple sites online reporting that those with blood type AB are more likely to develop Alzheimer's and cognitive decline.

My understanding is that Alzheimer's is ten to twenty years in the process and when it becomes noticeable it can decline quickly. All the more reason it should be on everyone's radar. I believe that **epigenetics matter mor**e but no matter what our APOE status or blood type at the rates cognitive issues are accelerating we should all be concern. I was young when my father began having early onset Alzheimer's and none of his doctors had any clues for almost 7 years...It has been a interest of mine since for I never forgot how it took my Daddy's brilliance from our family and he was not the only life mentor I have lost to Alzheimer's.

I really like Dr. Dale Bredesen's book, _**The End of Alzheimer's: The First Program to Prevent and Reverse Cognitive Declin**_**e** [https://www.amazon.com/End-Alzheimers-Program-Prevent-Cognitive/dp/0735216207](https://www.amazon.com/End-Alzheimers-Program-Prevent-Cognitive/dp/0735216207)
Great for Health in general not just cognitive decline. Dr. Kruse's work would greatly enhance their protocol and I believe Dr. Bredesen was cracked open by the results he has seen and I hope he would be open for more help for this pending epidemic.

> [Richard Watson said:](/goto/post?id=299018)

> Thanks for posting that video Martha, very interesting.

One of the most inspiring 'health books' for me... as my Father past from complications of Alzheimer's. He developed back in the 70's when early onset Alzheimer's was not well known. I did not know where my Daddy went but it was clear he was not the same brilliant, witty, loving and outgoing man we grew up with.... Alzheimer's was a daily part of our lives for many years. I've since determined a number of things he did that I believe added to his onset of this and it has been on my radar ever since.

I think Dr. Bredesen's book

# Thread: L1 injury
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/l1-injury.18522/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Linz — 2016-10-04T16:37:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/l1-injury.18522/post-202269>

Three weeks ago I fell backwards and caused 10% compression fracture of L1 and severe bruising to my right hip.
Medical response was 6 hours on my back looking up at LED and fluorescent tubes (asked for them to be switched off most of the last two hours), codeine, no ice packs available except the one I was able to hold onto from home, and not a single glass of water. Discharged with more codeine and follow up 4 weeks later.
Before going to hospital I had used a cold hose, ice packs and IR/red light pad on the injury. First week regular cold showers (bath once steady enough), ice packs, sunrise outside, red light, UVA light, outside in autumn mini heatwave for long hours, fish and fish broths. A partial cranio-sacral treatment, to head only, after a week made an enormous difference towards restoring comfortable pelvic movement and better sleep, Painful nerve spasms in right leg after two weeks meant a return to ER for another xray but no further damage.

This morning, still with nerve twinges through the night I did brief ct, red light, outside in sunlight and attended a spine clinic (managed to bring forward a week!). What pain medication was I taking? Two codeine in ER. One diazepam for muscle relax on second visit (big mistake!). Leptin sensitivity/ct/red lights/uv = no acute pain!!

Interesting examination when I could lean well back, drop hands right forwards down legs, no pain at all with pressure over injury and full rotation/ lifting of legs (not sure where the nerve spasms went. I felt rather a fraud). A huge appreciation Dr K. Not least for the confidence to take responsibility for myself.

### Lahelada — 2016-10-07T02:02:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/l1-injury.18522/post-202427>

Wow ,Linz ,what a story. So sorry it happened but exemplary how you turned the story around.

### caroline — 2016-10-07T09:05:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/l1-injury.18522/post-202440>

Hi Linz ..... Thank goodness for all you have taken on board here - and then you could apply it all to yourself.

well done! Hopefully your fast recovery has impacted on the medical people who saw you.

How are you progressing?

### Linz — 2016-10-07T18:33:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/l1-injury.18522/post-202462>

Thanks Lah, Caroline,

Making good progress (but perhaps not as good as the young gymnast!). I think one of the young medics will be thinking hard about Leptin and pain and I'll be armed with a blog or two when I go back.

### Linz — 2016-10-08T11:35:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/l1-injury.18522/post-202486>

Sleeping well. Very dark and we have rewired recently with armoured cable and breaker switches. Really makes everything feel very still at night.

# Thread: Cryotherapy Sauna vs Ice Bath?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryotherapy-sauna-vs-ice-bath.18525/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Miss Lizzie — 2016-10-04T20:28:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryotherapy-sauna-vs-ice-bath.18525/post-202280>

I recently bought a three pack deal to use a local cryotherapy "sauna" . If you haven't heard about them, you basically stand in a tube and they shoot you with liquid nitrogen for 3 minutes at a temperate of -200F.

I went for my first treatment and was a little underwhelmed. No shivering, skin warmed back up immediately... it really didn't feel like much was happening. Their claim is that you don't want the cold to penetrate as deep and that they treatment will cause the same hormone cascades as an ice bath, but in a much quicker time frame.

I'm used to my ice baths and ice vest. The cold definitely penetrates deeper, it takes me longer to warm up, but after I warm up, I feel super toasty. I just didn't feel that with the cryotherapy. Plus, my ice bath is much cheaper!

Does anybody have any experience with this? Any comments or opinions?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-04T20:53:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryotherapy-sauna-vs-ice-bath.18525/post-202281>

always pick water because of how it moves charges. Things always flow hot to cold and water increases the deliver 24 times faster than air.

### Miss Lizzie — 2016-10-04T21:14:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cryotherapy-sauna-vs-ice-bath.18525/post-202282>

Thanks. I will definitely stick with my ice baths. I look forward to them now and love how they make me feel! It's amazing. I never thought I would look forward to making myself cold!

I thought I would try the cryotherapy sauna since I got a very good deal, but it didn't make me feel anything. I got out and went and sat in the sun for 20 min.

# Thread: Hi from Toronto!

### FM9000 — 2016-10-05T06:05:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-toronto.18530/post-202302>

Hey Everybody!

I discovered Dr. Kruse, through my research on matrix repatterning, which is a manual therapy based on tensegrity. I wasn't sure if matrix repatterning was legit, so I did a bunch of research. I found it interesting as it appears that it is based on improving conductivity of fascia. My searches about conductivity, collagen, cytokines, and more eventually lead me to Dr. Kruse and the EMF-7-Prometheus epic post!

I've had health issues for around 10 years, since a facial trauma that messed up my jaw, which subsequently created more and more health issues, including:

\-IBS
\-Chronic Prostate and pelvic pain issues
\-Muscular-skeletal issues and pain
\-Facial pain + TMJ issues + messed up occlusion
\-Chest/rib inflammation (I have trouble breathing)
\-Seizures
\-Depressive tendencies
\-Brain fog/difficulties focusing
\-Get cold easily
\-and more!

I'm assuming I have central sensitization syndrome and/or fibromyalgia, as I feel my nervous system is hyper sensitive and stuck in sympathetic mode.

I've managed my issues with no help from doctors (all of them told me I have psychological problems), with a primarily vegetarian diet concentrating on organic whole foods and a macrobiotic approach. I do eat a lot of eggs, and occasionally fish. Before I changed my diet, I couldnt eat anything without getting sick, but after some time got to a somewhat functional level.

I love learning, and I'm pretty good at assimilating large amounts of information, but I have to say that I found the amount of information on here overwhelming in both the amount and difficulty in digesting. I also realized that even if I do understand everything on here, it wouldn't be much help unless I can make it actionable.

So, I'm looking forward to connecting and learning how to action from Dr. Kruse's paradigm.

# Thread: Nicotine + Alzheimers/Dementia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-alzheimers-dementia.18531/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DLO — 2016-10-05T07:26:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicotine-alzheimers-dementia.18531/post-202306>

[http://www.naturalnews.com/055531\_nicotine\_brain\_health\_dementia.html](http://www.naturalnews.com/055531_nicotine_brain_health_dementia.html)
**Texas A&M: Nicotine can protect aging brains against Parkinson's, Alzheimer's disease**
Wednesday, October 05, 2016 by: Ethan A. Huff, staff writer
_Tags: [nicotine](http://www.naturalnews.com/nicotine.html), [brain health](http://www.naturalnews.com/brain_health.html), [dementia](http://www.naturalnews.com/dementia.html)_

(NaturalNews) [Cigarette angst](http://cigarettes.fetch.news/) and all the associated media hysteria over the health effects of smoking have deterred millions of people from using tobacco in any way, shape or form. But new research out of Texas A&M University in College Station has revealed something about the tobacco plant that many people don't know: When used properly, tobacco can actually help _promote_ health rather than destroy it.

It's true: Tobacco contains a compound known as nicotine that, aside from its use in addiction patches, has been shown to help protect the brain against the age-related onset of degenerative conditions like [Parkinson's disease and Alzheimer's](http://www.dementia.news/). So, while smoking tobacco may not be the best approach for maintaining vibrant health, consuming its constituents in extract form just might have a place in the supplemental regimens of health enthusiasts seeking optimal cognitive function.

Dr. Ursula Winzer-Serhan, Ph.D., an associate professor at the Texas A&M College of Medicine, collaborated with some of her colleagues to come to this conclusion, as shocking as it might be. They tested the effects of nicotine on animals, and found that heavy consumption of the compound causes changes in the brain that, believe it or not, are overtly positive.

At low and medium doses, nicotine was found to have minimal effects on the animals' brains, and showed almost no substantial presence in their blood. But at high doses, [nicotine](http://www.naturalnews.com/nicotine.html) was found to not only curb the animals' appetites, effectively minimizing excess weight gain, but it also triggered an increased number of receptors in the brain associated with anti-aging.

Though the precise mechanism of this action has yet to be uncovered, the team believes that nicotine somehow acts in such a way as to minimize the degenerative factors that lead to early-onset memory loss and the eventual progression of more serious conditions like [dementia](http://www.naturalnews.com/dementia.html). And the best part is that it does this without producing any noticeable side effects.

"Some people say that nicotine decreases anxiety, which is why people smoke, but others say it increases anxiety," Winzer-Serhan explained in a statement. "The last thing you would want in a drug that is given chronically would be a negative change in behavior. Luckily, we didn't find any evidence of anxiety: Only two measures showed any effect even with high levels of nicotine, and if anything, nicotine made animal models less anxious."

**Does natural tobacco leaf have any health benefits? Science suggests yes**
Winzer-Serhan and her team were quick to note that people shouldn't [go out and grab cartons of cigarettes and start lighting up](http://www.stopsmoking.news/) simply due to these findings. Isolated nicotine is obviously very different to dried tobacco leaves wrapped in paper – especially when those leaves are intentionally [spiked with hundreds or even thousands of other added chemicals](http://science.naturalnews.com/chemicals_in_cigarettes.html), as is the case with most commercial cigarettes.

Still, nicotine extract and potentially even natural tobacco _could_ have some health benefits that get overlooked or ignored because the science behind them doesn't align with the popular, and mostly negative, narrative surrounding tobacco. Tobacco shouldn't be completely written off, in other words, as it's not entirely evil – [and the science shows this](http://science.naturalnews.com/).

"I want to make it very clear that we're not encouraging people to smoke," Winzer-Serhan warns, noting that further research is needed on tobacco and nicotine in order to gain a better understanding of all the unknowns surrounding its effects on the body.

"Even if these weren't very preliminary results, smoking results in so many health problems that any possible benefit of the nicotine would be more than cancelled out. However, smoking is only one possible route of administration ... and our work shows that we shouldn't write-off nicotine completely."

**Sources for this article include:**
[MedicalXpress.com](http://medicalxpress.com/news/2016-09-nicotine-aging-brain.html)

[DiscoverMagazine.com](http://discovermagazine.com/2014/march/13-nicotine-fix)

[Science.NaturalNews.com](http://science.naturalnews.com/chemicals_in_cigarettes.html)

### Evgeni — 2016-10-05T17:27:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-thinking.18533/post-202321>

What are some ways to optimize our thinking ? What would be some tools or biohacks to improve our thoughts and emotions towards optimal, besides meditation ?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-05T18:52:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-thinking.18533/post-202331>

Being outside........most of the time. More time your outside higher your dopamine level is and less likely you'll be addicted to morphine.[http://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10863-015-9626-8](http://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10863-015-9626-8)

### Lahelada — 2016-10-06T03:44:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-thinking.18533/post-202362>

I make decisions outside or check my inside decision for veracity outside now. I used to say that I think well on the bus. Looking back I think better because of having been in the sun waiting for the bus.

### caroline — 2016-10-06T07:05:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-thinking.18533/post-202369>

I notice a huge difference if I can't be outside most of the day. My mood, my energy, my sleep, my productivity goes down the drain if I have to stay inside.
We spend a few hours every morning outside having breakfast and puttering around. The rest of the day I am darting in and out - doing things that are an absolute must.
A lot of days nothing else matters and we head to the ocean......

TSH asks for much more FT3.
Get more complete thyroid panel.
15 ------- Thyroid-stimulating Hormone (TSH)- Test Number: 004259 CPT Code: 84443
16 ------- Thyroxine (T4)- Test Number: 001149 CPT Code: 84436
17 ------- Thyroxine (T4), Free, Direct, Serum-Test Number: 001974 CPT Code: 84439
18 ------- Triiodothyronine (T3)- Test Number: 002188 CPT Code: 84480
19 ------- Triiodothyronine (T3), Free, Serum- Test Number: 010389 CPT Code: 84481
20 ------- Reverse T3-Test Number: 070104 CPT Code: 84482

may want to also figure your antibodies

//
Nascent = iodine
Lugol's=iodine + potassium iodide

**Use Lugol's**

//////////////////////////
iodide=halide
it is more about halides
to get the undesirable halides out.

Nascent iodine is a supplemental form of iodine. Iodine is an essential mineral that the body requires for normal function.\[[1](http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/natural-health/what-is-nascent-iodine/#1)\] For nearly a century, nascent iodine has been regarded as a more effective form of the mineral than many dietary iodine forms, such as _potassium iodide_. In fact, most forms of dietary iodine become [mere iodide](http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/natural-health/how-are-iodide-and-iodine-different/) in the digestive tract.\[[2](http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/natural-health/what-is-nascent-iodine/#2)\] This is important to note because iodides are not as easily absorbed and utilized by the body, meaning that iodine-dependent organs and processes may not be adequately supported.\[[3](http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/natural-health/what-is-nascent-iodine/#3)\] This is what makes nascent iodine such a popular form of iodine for supplementation.

[http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/natural-health/what-is-nascent-iodine/](http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/natural-health/what-is-nascent-iodine/)
[http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/natural-health/how-are-iodide-and-iodine-different/](http://www.globalhealingcenter.com/natural-health/what-is-nascent-iodine/)

Muscle mass I'd say i'm at 12% BF but want to gain more lean muscle. No problem doing anything physically be it pull ups or pushups or HIIT.
