# Thread: midazolam or clonadine for widom tooth surgery in a 17 year old?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/midazolam-or-clonadine-for-widom-tooth-surgery-in-a-17-year-old.18536/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2016-10-05T22:34:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/midazolam-or-clonadine-for-widom-tooth-surgery-in-a-17-year-old.18536/post-202347>

Greetings!
I am now in full freak out mode because my kid is getting 4 wisdom teeth out and the guy is using Versed (midazolam) - They love using this because no one remembers a thing but they could use something called clonadine - I found this:

**Clonidine in paediatric anaesthesia: review of the literature and comparison with benzodiazepines for premedication.**
[Bergendahl H](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Bergendahl%20H%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=16430532)1, [Lönnqvist PA](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=L%C3%B6nnqvist%20PA%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=16430532), [Eksborg S](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Eksborg%20S%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=16430532).
**[Author information](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16430532#)**
**Abstract**
**BACKGROUND:**
Children undergoing anaesthesia and surgery can experience significant anxiety and distress during the peri-operative period, but whether routine premedication is necessary is currently debated. Benzodiazepines are the most frequently used drugs as premedication in paediatric anaesthesia. In the US, 50% of young children undergoing surgery receive premedication and midazolam is the most frequently used drug in this context (1). Nishina and coworkers (2) concluded in a review article in 1999 that clonidine, administered via an oral, rectal, or caudal route, is a promising adjunct to anaesthetics and analgesics to enhance quality of peri-operative management in infants and children. Later publications also support the use of clonidine for premedication (3-6). The aim of this communication is to review the use of clonidine in paediatric anaesthesia and to propose clonidine as a promising alternative to midazolam. Clonidine is associated with a number of beneficial effects in the context ofanaesthesia both in adults and children. Why clonidine is not routinely use in clinical practice despite the massive publication list is to a large extent due to the lack of marketing efforts from the pharmaceutical industry since multiplegeneric preparations are now readily available on most markets. Midazolam is also associated with a number of beneficial effects, but is far from an ideal premedicant in children, especially with regards to the amnesia, confusion and long term behavioural disturbances. Clonidine has contrary to midazolam no effect on respiration. We believe that clonidine is a good alternative to midazolam as premedication in infants and children.

I was wondering if Dr. Kruse had any thoughts on either one and if there is anything I can do to help prevent neurological damage from the induced amnesia... Jesus it never ends...

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-05T23:36:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/midazolam-or-clonadine-for-widom-tooth-surgery-in-a-17-year-old.18536/post-202354>

If they are not impacted and the OMFS was good........you can take them out with local anesthesia alone. All you need to do is field blocks. OMFS know how to do it.

When I was a 4th dental student my sister needed hers out and she had no insurance so I told her to come to the dental school ER and ask for me........and I came and saw her as a student and told the on call staff guy I could do this with local and he looked at me like I was nuts. I told him I was matching into OMFS already and spent 6 months doing this already in New Orleans during my externship and he was OK..........So I did it with local. My sister was shitting the bed...........so I numbed her up and told her I would be back in ten minutes to check her again to see if the medicine would work. I cam back and checked her. When I check somebody they think I am checking anesthesia levels.........I'm not I know their entire trigeminal CN 2 and 3 complex is mine for the taking and I took the teeth out. She thought the pressure in her mouth was " me checking". I laughed. I stuffed gauze in her mouth and told her to get out of my chair and follow the instructions. She was well when are you going to take my teeth out? I said look over here...............4 teeth sitting on the tray.

When somebody says they cannot do something find someone who can and who has done it. Many patients want the versed........because they a scared. No one could have been a bigger wuss than my sister with respect to the dentist. Now a general dentist might need to sedate you. But for my money local anesthesia is way safer.

[http://www.scientificamerican.com/article/hidden-dangers-of-going-under/](http://www.scientificamerican.com/article/hidden-dangers-of-going-under/)

How long do you think it will take for those who study things like this to realize that when we synthetically lower electrical potentials on cell membranes that it might effect the endogenous coupled cycles in durinal biologic cycles? I wonder if they ever wonder if the time of the day and the lighting all this goes under has any role in these issues. We learn that death comes fast when electron chain transport stops. We also know that a slowed ECT is linked to illness. The dynamic nature of how drugs can effect ECT flow changes the border zone between the classical world we live in and the quantum world that makes us operate as we do. When we uncouple these two things in a synthetic fashion you'd think we expect some collateral repercussions. Quantum theory is the most successful and reproduced theory in science making it quite unique. The only “failure” of quantum theory is its inability to provide a natural framework for our prejudices about the workings of the Universe. States of quantum systems evolve according to the deterministic, linear Schroedinger equation. Life is called a macroscopic system in physics. Macroscopic systems, like life, are never isolated from their environments. Therefore they should not be expected to follow Schroedinger equation because it is only applicable only to a closed system. Life is not a closed system either. As a result, systems usually regarded as classical (reality) suffer or benefit from the "natural loss" of quantum coherence, which “leaks out” into the environment. Synthetic losses, no matter how short or long would lead to other possibilities........other outcomes in reality. This paper lays out some interesting facts that can teach us about the boundary zone between the quantum realm and reality we observe.

### Penny — 2016-10-06T19:17:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/midazolam-or-clonadine-for-widom-tooth-surgery-in-a-17-year-old.18536/post-202405>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=202354)
>
> If they are not impacted and the OMFS was good........you can take them out with local anesthesia alone. All you need to do is field blocks. OMFS know how to do it.
>
> When I was a 4th dental student my sister needed hers out and she had no insurance so I told her to come to the dental school ER and ask for me........and I came and saw her as a student and told the on call staff guy I could do this with local and he looked at me like I was nuts. I told him I was matching into OMFS already and spent 6 months doing this already in New Orleans during my externship and he was OK..........So I did it with local. My sister was shitting the bed...........so I numbed her up and told her I would be back in ten minutes to check her again to see if the medicine would work. I cam back and checked her. When I check somebody they think I am checking anesthesia levels.........I'm not I know their entire trigeminal CN 2 and 3 complex is mine for the taking and I took the teeth out. She thought the pressure in her mouth was " me checking". I laughed. I stuffed gauze in her mouth and told her to get out of my chair and follow the instructions. She was well when are you going to take my teeth out? I said look over here...............4 teeth sitting on the tray.
>
> When somebody says they cannot do something find someone who can and who has done it. Many patients want the versed........because they a scared. No one could have been a bigger wuss than my sister with respect to the dentist. Now a general dentist might need to sedate you. But for my money local anesthesia is way safer.
>
> [http://www.scientificamerican.com/article/hidden-dangers-of-going-under/](http://www.scientificamerican.com/article/hidden-dangers-of-going-under/)
>
> How long do you think it will take for those who study things like this to realize that when we synthetically lower electrical potentials on cell membranes that it might effect the endogenous coupled cycles in durinal biologic cycles? I wonder if they ever wonder if the time of the day and the lighting all this goes under has any role in these issues. We learn that death comes fast when electron chain transport stops. We also know that a slowed ECT is linked to illness. The dynamic nature of how drugs can effect ECT flow changes the border zone between the classical world we live in and the quantum world that makes us operate as we do. When we uncouple these two things in a synthetic fashion you'd think we expect some collateral repercussions. Quantum theory is the most successful and reproduced theory in science making it quite unique. The only “failure” of quantum theory is its inability to provide a natural framework for our prejudices about the workings of the Universe. States of quantum systems evolve according to the deterministic, linear Schroedinger equation. Life is called a macroscopic system in physics. Macroscopic systems, like life, are never isolated from their environments. Therefore they should not be expected to follow Schroedinger equation because it is only applicable only to a closed system. Life is not a closed system either. As a result, systems usually regarded as classical (reality) suffer or benefit from the "natural loss" of quantum coherence, which “leaks out” into the environment. Synthetic losses, no matter how short or long would lead to other possibilities........other outcomes in reality. This paper lays out some interesting facts that can teach us about the boundary zone between the quantum realm and reality we observe.

Thanks for that massive detailed reply Sadly, I think they were impacted plus 2 were wrapped around the nerves - but on a happier note, I did get his surgery report from 10 years ago when he got his tonsils out, and he didn't seem to have any trouble coming out of the anasthesia - the other happy thing is this surgeon didn't require the 3-D x-rays that the other one was going to do because of the nerve wraps... my other son got a local for 2 of them and was knocked out for the other 2 -

I learned a lot looking into this whole anasthesia thing - like some herbs cause bleeding and you shouldn't be taking them before surgery - yet another minefield I avoided by sheer luck Also, there is this brain monitoring device you can use to keep you under without giving you "too much" anasthesia - I suppose in the end it's how long you are under - tonsils are probably pretty quick as are wisdom teeth - anyway - thanks for the reply

# Thread: Red film over lights?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-film-over-lights.18540/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NaturalHealthForLife — 2016-10-06T05:40:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-film-over-lights.18540/post-202366>

My house has LED light panels under the kitchen cabinets for countertop lighting.

I was wondering if anyone had tried putting a red film over this type of lighting strips to make them red.

Would this be effective in blocking blue light do you think?

I am thinking of some type of cellophane, maybe like what the crew guys would put over a spotlight in play. I looked for red LED lightbulbs to install in the fixtures, but did not see them on Google, Amazon or a few specialty lightbulbs stores.

### Sue-UK — 2016-10-06T13:05:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-film-over-lights.18540/post-202382>

I've got a gel filter, two names I know of are Rosco [https://www.rosco.com/filters/roscolux.cfm#colors](https://www.rosco.com/filters/roscolux.cfm#colors) and Lee Filters. [http://www.leefilters.com/lighting/colour-list.html](http://www.leefilters.com/lighting/colour-list.html). They show the spectral energy distribution, I picked which one I wanted and got a sheet off Ebay. I've currently got it on a 2 computer screens, 3 leds on a floor standing vertical light DH wants on in the evening, and covered a pair of clear safety glasses for if the tv is on.

### NaturalHealthForLife — 2016-10-06T21:55:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-film-over-lights.18540/post-202417>

Awesome! I will check those out. I assume you're liking it pretty well, if you've got it up all over?

### Sue-UK — 2016-10-07T12:21:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-film-over-lights.18540/post-202445>

> [NaturalHealthForLife said:](/goto/post?id=202417)
>
> Awesome! I will check those out. I assume you're liking it pretty well, if you've got it up all over?

I didn't want red because I have an eye problem, poor night vision and have difficulty seeing through red glasses at night, but I wanted more blocking than my normal Amber glasses. I've got a lee filters swatch so picked a couple of very deep Amber ones that I can function with, and then bought the gel with most blue blocking. Sleep is better ...

### Lahelada — 2016-10-07T14:29:49+0300

### brookway17 — 2017-06-13T15:28:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-film-over-lights.18540/post-216059>

I found an orange packing tape that seems to work well I have black and blue light. Are used a single layer of it in my LED lit refrigerator and it seems to work. Since you don't that much time in your refrigerator it's probably adequate. I also have some LED under cabinet lights in my kitchen, where I'm using two layers of tape. I got the tape on Amazon here is the link. I rearranged the majority of my life around solar redox. Other than when I shop for groceries, I'm outdoor all day long.

At any given time I'm either:

-   Sun baking (whilst writing software)
-   Cold tubbing (in the sun)
-   Working on a construction site (usually with some clothing on for that one)
-   Seated under a peach tree if I need a break from the direct sun

Things I still need to change:

-   Set myself up to cook breakfast outside (that's a big one)
-   Make some more basic improvements outdoors to make it brainlessly convenient to be outside

I try to find spots where I can place the thing in the shade while still getting sun. Sometimes that's not possible and I'll put it into a used amazon box that I should spray paint white someday.

It's kinda fun to watch the system slowdown dramatically in the shade when I feel just fine in the sun.

### Sue-UK — 2018-04-09T16:11:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tan-thru-swimwear-and-shirts.18541/post-239043>

> [Billybats said:](/goto/post?id=239036)
>
> Can I wear tan through bathing suit and clothes all day. I read in ask jack thread that you shouldn't expose down yanda during UV light unless that is for guys only. I want to buy some bathing suits, tops, and dresses from this company.

I wear them all day if I can't be nude. I don't think I need to avoid uv ....it's the early morning light without uv that turns hormones on, and uv turns them off (although I see it like a volume switch depending on season and latitude. Signalling changes in the environment, I don't think our hormones should be "same" all year. ) So a.m light first and then uv ....I don't ever wear kiniki under artificial light or in front of TVs etc.

That's what I was thinking of how I would wear it. On another threat they are talking about not putting the "junk" in the sun at certain times of the day. I was wondering if that was only for the guys.

### Sue-UK — 2018-04-10T14:57:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tan-thru-swimwear-and-shirts.18541/post-239128>

> [Billybats said:](/goto/post?id=239123)
>
> Hi Caroline,
>
> That's what I was thinking of how I would wear it. On another threat they are talking about not putting the "junk" in the sun at certain times of the day. I was wondering if that was only for the guys.

I think there's a difference in deliberately exposing in early a.m / late afternoon, and deliberately not exposing during the main uv times. For a quadruped mammal the sexual organs are shaded by the body, less so for bipedal, but still there, and mammals will seek shade in high uv times. That's what I do, because I still get light and air.

### Billybats — 2018-04-10T15:07:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tan-thru-swimwear-and-shirts.18541/post-239129>

I guess we have the instinct to go in shade. Now that I think of it when I am out in the sun mainly very strong time of the year I want to be in shade and water. Makes sense. Thank you ladies.

It makes sense for me to do it more early in the morning than at the height of the day
And if you've already talked about the KINIKI:
must say I've been using it for a year now
I have dark skin in summer and a little lighter in the winter and you can see differences in areas that catch the sun
I see a big difference in the color of the skin in the covered areas (breast ,Bikini Bottom- bright color) compared to the rest of the body .Especially considering that I am exposed to the sun all year long
If it worked after a year I would see a change in color of my skin

will continue to search for a company that will do so and will update you

### Sue-UK — 2018-04-11T12:50:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tan-thru-swimwear-and-shirts.18541/post-239201>

I probably have less tan issues because I'm using different items. I've got 2 sleeveless tops (which I don't think are available any more), 2 swimsuits, a tankini top, 2 bikini top, 2 bikini briefs, 2 wrap round skirts, 2 sarongs, a poncho top and a pair of harem pants, a pair of men's shorts, and a short kaftan. My most used is the sarongs which I loop round my neck as a dress. Going to and from the public beach or at home if I have visitors, I'll put on bikini briefs with it, in case the sarong parts as I'm walking, If I'm not surrounded by people from every direction it's easy to discretely part the sarong to expose one of the girls, and down yanda,and then turn round and do the other side). My least used are the bikini and tankini tops (not enough support so very unflattering, which dents my confidence) and the harem pants. On beaches where I can be topless but not naked, I like the beach skirt, away from the crowds I can face the direction of the dunes and adjust it to expose more, and from a distance still look like I'm following the beach code, I'm covered walking to the sea and in the water the fabric floats so it's more like swimming naked. I've just moved but in the past I've used the bikini and tankini tops with beach wrap skirt at sunrise in the ct tub if I had visitors as if DDs BF opens their bedroom curtains and tells her your mum's in the bath again, however unflattering, I won't be caught starkers.

### Theka — 2018-04-11T16:45:50+0300

### Vervaina — 2018-05-11T16:12:20+0300

I bought a cool tan t shirt. As for sizing I am small framed 5'3 and 105lbs but it still is very short on me, but I am large breasted so it might be why. If you have darker nipples (from all our naked tanning  you can see them under the shirt because it is blue with no print. As with the kiniki stuff I find you don't really tan through the fabric much. Maybe better than just a reg tshirt though. I wear kiniki on the beach and you can see my boobs for sure. Wish there were more options out there.

### Sue-UK — 2018-05-12T15:27:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tan-thru-swimwear-and-shirts.18541/post-241080>

I think the trick is that however see through it seems when it's off, once on, my skin colour appears to be part of the pattern, which stops it being lingerie. Kiniki shouldnt attract a different conversation or sexual attention than the same item in normal fabric. If it did I wouldn't feel safe wearing it in public.

If I let him know it's really see through, I'd be revealing the trick, and/or if I tried it on "for him" first, in private, I would be the one starting the conversation. By itself, kiniki says nothing.

### drezy — 2018-05-12T23:56:45+0300

### Katie Durham — 2019-03-12T22:49:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tan-thru-swimwear-and-shirts.18541/post-265286>

No color from sunbathing on a really sunny day early February. Bummer! My front is burned from sunbathing on a really sunny day last weekend. Yeah! Now to adjust how I do it. Not impressed with CoolTan. Very sheer/see through but very little light goes through. Somewhere I read the company said wearing their clothes was the equivalent of applying suntan lotion of 6 SPF, by which I think they mean you would want to apply 6 SPF everywhere else. Found a couple privacy screens Sunday I should be able to use in my backyard so I can expose my girls longer. My goal is a stern lecture by my physician next fall when she can’t find a tan line, LOL. Apparently that will require largely ditching the CoolTans.

I found tan through fabric online, out of somewhere in the far east. Seemed to be coming through an importer, much too complicated to put in an order for a few yards. We need a local source so we can make our own clothes! If anyone knows of one (in the US), please let the rest of us know.

### Sue-UK — 2019-03-13T09:26:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tan-thru-swimwear-and-shirts.18541/post-265330>

>

I got a friend to make me a kaftan from 2 cellular cot blankets for cold weather, which lets in a fair bit of light through the holes, but gives me a little warmth. IIRC kiniki didn't let me buy more than one sarong when it was on massive sale, but if you get hold of two or three, either by waiting to the next sale, or getting someone else to buy a single for you, you'd have a fair bit of fabric to make something up. My two sarongs are different patterns, but I'm tempted to buy another and use it for fabric with one I've got.

### LieselK — 2019-03-13T17:23:47+0200

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=239199)

I found this too. Some gets through as I'm not bright white under the kiniki, but not enough sun. I feel like I've tanned through white swimwear way better! I just try to take the kiniki off as much as I can and wear them loosely. I also wear the skimpiest style and just use a cotton wrap as a dress to kinda cover in summer as needed. One time the Jehovah's Witnesses showed up in my driveway and I met them in my string bikini kiniki... after that I just didn't give a shit anymore. I'm currently sitting on my porch in my kinki strings and waving to everyone that drives by!

### Inger — 2019-03-13T17:37:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tan-thru-swimwear-and-shirts.18541/post-265355>

lol.. Liesel, that sounds great! I love how you are not giving a shit  I have the tan thru string tanga bikini too! But I have already worn out 3 pairs..uh. One pair last max one summer as I wear it so much. Of course I am mostly naked, always when on my terrace or at the oyster-nude beach by my work. I do not give a shit either if my neighbors see that, and landlord that lives next door, do not mind at all.

I cant say if the sun gets thru mine or not because I am the same brown all over my body in summer, as I am mostly naked in the sun.

You know what I think? I think deep inside people like seeing it. I think deep inside everyone would love to just be naked and do not give a shit. It is so freeing, like being an innocent child again , have the air and sun touching your skin all over. It is so beautiful.

### drezy — 2019-03-13T17:59:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tan-thru-swimwear-and-shirts.18541/post-265365>

just a second ago I measured UVA for both of you:

10:50AM 30ºN
direct --> under suit
2.5 --> 0.7 mw/cm^2 kiniki
2.5 --> 0.4 mw/cm^2 cooltan

UVA (320-400nm peak response 370nm) Not exactly UVB, but so far I've seen the ratios track. If you want me to be less lazy and get a measurement at 365nm and 335nm I will upon request.

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=265355)
>
> I have the tan thru string tanga bikini too! But I have already worn out 3 pairs..uh.

Me too... well about the worn out part not the bikini part. I'm still not 100% happy with my hips for that yet...

Word to the wise galvanized horse troughs eat tan throughs where they come in contact. I put it on and ask how I look when half my butt is clearly showing through a worn out gaping hole in the shorts. I always get good laughs when I do that, though my son made me swear o never wear them if he has a friend over. Where's the fun in that?

### LieselK — 2019-03-13T18:00:18+0200

It IS very freeing. In a climate of so much toxic masculinity and victim blaming it was weird at first for me, as a survivor, to feel comfortable in my skin and not be paranoid about what others thought. This past summer I was able to encourage quite a few mom friends to embrace bikini life and love themselves a little more. Doing it to love yourself and embrace the sun on your skin. It was pretty fucking awesome. I know many women caught up in what is "right" as a married Christian woman and if they're temping men to sin... ugh... what a burden to shoulder that is really just bullshit. So now I call it what it is... bullshit.

### drezy — 2019-03-13T20:38:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tan-thru-swimwear-and-shirts.18541/post-265387>

OK less lazy after extra sun

365nm = UVA
335 = close to UVB (actually overlapping with 320nm UVB if you count the full spectral response)

1:30PM 30ºN
direct 365nm,335nm --> under suit 365nm,335nm
5.16mw,1.85mw -->1.16 , 0.64 mw/cm^2 kiniki
5.16mw,1.85mw --> 1.16, 0.67 mw/cm^2 cooltan

They are close enough to be within the error range from what I see.

Just wanted to show what I can measure and advocate for the "birthday suit" if you can pull it off. I just figure me and my wife have decades of light missing from out lives so once our solar callous is built more is better.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-06T17:15:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-is-catching-up-to-nature.18542/post-202389>

Today I have a new blog out about this week's Nobel Prize for physics. People do not realize how sensitive out mitochondria are.......Falling snow has a sound emission that can be picked up by a detector inside our skull surrounded by an ocean of CSF; What is the personal butterfly effect? It is the act of precession of the hydrogen atom in falling snow makes air move around the particles whose spin was changed. This changes its topology. The 2016 Noble Prize was given for topology. Very few people understand topology but I have been teaching people about it. People forget quantum particles always move, they never stop and are considered a perpetual motion machine. That resultant circular motion of the top like spin in hydrogen in falling snow is called precession. It turns out the Earth’s magnetic field is “always on” like a quantum particle. Our mitochondria is the detector of this signal. In fact, nearly every magnetically receptive nucleus on Earth, including the hydrogen in every drop of water in the ocean in the clouds, and in snow flakes is quietly precessing away creating the music of nature. The mitochondria in our thalamus listens to this harmony and tunes itself to it! This is one of the ways how we tune our brain to nature and that tune helps fine tune the oscillations in the mitochondrial membranes. It is a wireless network of nature. Snow fall actually has been recorded and it makes noise we can hear if our mitochondrial thalamic detectors have low heteroplasmy rates to pick up these RF frequencies that are altering hydrogen in snowflakes. Our thalamic neurons need good mitochondria and the outer mitochondrial membrane and endoplasmic reticulum must have adequate DHA and eicosanoids in our cell membranes. Mitochondrial function is optimized by optimal melatonin cycles locally in the brain. Our ears are not tuned to this frequency of the sound of snow falling but machines humans have built can pick up these electromagnetic signals; Mother nature built them into our brains to pick them up in thalamic relays that use radio frequency waves. If our ears where to sense these waves it would change the energy and information in those waves, so they by pass our ears so that our brain's mitochondria may use them to tell winter from summer. This is how your mitochondria work in neurons in your brain and sensory organs with your environment way below your sensory tuning abilities. Anytime something is observed or measured the perception of it is altered. Many times nature tries to hide quantum effects from senses to preserve the fidelity of the signal. This is called the Quantum Zeno effect. Here is a new blog discussing the implication of the Nobel Prize of 2016. [https://www.jackkruse.com/time-24-physics-finally-caught-nature/](https://www.jackkruse.com/time-24-physics-finally-caught-nature/)

### Joe Gavin — 2016-10-06T18:19:38+0300

Coupling that with Oct webinar on life using 260-700nm light on ou surfaces. Mito/ATP created by 660/670 light. Damn cool shit. And I know there is more coming when Quantlet gets here.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-07T01:21:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physics-is-catching-up-to-nature.18542/post-202424>

So what is a perpetual motion machine of the quantum world? There's one organism that lives on the planet Earth, one living thing, and it's three billion years old, and we're all connected through time to the first mitochondrial DNA cell that appeared in the ocean three and a half billion years ago. Thinking outside the box" gives Schrödinger's cat a bunch of possibilities. Mitochondria are our cells' energy dynamos. Descended from bacteria that colonized other cells about 2 billion years, they get "flaky" as we age. A prominent theory of aging holds that decaying of mitochondria is a key driver of aging. While it's not clear to scientist today why our mitochondria fade as we age, evidence suggests that it leads to everything from heart failure to neurodegeneration, as well as the complete absence of zipping around the supper table. It should be clear to a quantum biologist why mitochondria get "flaky". As the respiratory proteins get stretched out each decade the distance between each complex SLOWS the subatomic particles at use in the mitochondria therefore the perpetual motion machine slows on a raltive basis and as this happens we age. As we age we get diseases as a collateral damage in tissues where the slowing occurs faster than it does in other areas.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-07T01:41:55+0300

You must question and critically examine those writings from every side. You must submit only to argument and experiment and not to the sayings of any person. For every human being is vulnerable to all kinds of imperfection. # Thread: Pop Corn Muscles?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pop-corn-muscles.18547/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HopingMom — 2016-10-07T03:45:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pop-corn-muscles.18547/post-202429>

I've noticed that after exercising (just briskly walking-not strenuous) that all of my muscles all over my body will be twitching. It is short fast twitching like feeling (not visible, just feel it) - the best I can describe is it is kind of like popping corn? It is mainly in my legs/thighs and will last for a good 10 minutes.
My legs/thighs are my fat-est parts now so I feel like that may have some play.
Anyone have any thoughts on what this is or what is going on?
Thanks much!

### DLO — 2016-10-07T16:41:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pop-corn-muscles.18547/post-202454>

Mild muscle spasm. Try some hot and cold contrast 3 mins warm 1 min cold 10 deg difference( the bigger the gap u can stand the better) in the shower. Idea is to get circulation flushing in there.

### HopingMom — 2016-10-09T03:41:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pop-corn-muscles.18547/post-202530>

Thanks much DLO! Yes, circulation is poor. Do you have any idea what is causing this or the mechanism for what is going on?

### DLO — 2016-10-09T06:32:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pop-corn-muscles.18547/post-202536>

How many gallons of water are you drinking? What kinds of magnesium are you taking and what types at what time?

**The CDC medical police state: the right to detain anyone**

Americans traveling between states can be stopped, detained, and even quarantined, if they seem to have indications of an illness that could impact public health.
If this sounds vague, it is. The CDC has even coined a new absurd category: “precommunicable” illness. It means that with few or no symptoms—but merely the probability of having contacted someone who had or could have had an illness—a traveler in America can be picked up and held against his will.
The CDC asserts it has police powers to do this.

# Thread: Wearing a watch?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-a-watch.18553/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2016-10-07T18:56:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-a-watch.18553/post-202465>

I am very cautious of what I put on my body and how it may affect my health. What do you think about wearing a watch? I do not own a smartphone and I need to tell the time precisely (I am a student and have classes to attend)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-07T22:45:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-a-watch.18553/post-202470>

Some watches are no good. But the good old Rolex is fine.

### Optimalbound — 2016-10-12T23:47:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-a-watch.18553/post-202703>

Hi Nicolaj,

A Rolex might be outside a student's budget, but fortunately, Rolex is only one of many manufacturers of mechanical watches. You might want to have a look at Seiko's SKX line (dive watches), Orient's Bambino line (dress watches) or Seiko's SARB models (casual/dressy), for example. Most of them are in the DKK 2000-4000 range.

### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2016-10-16T19:53:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-a-watch.18553/post-202914>

Thank u sir!  A very PRACTICAL advice but aren't all analog watches consider O.K though?

### caroline — 2016-10-17T05:13:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-a-watch.18553/post-202948>

I don't think it has to be a rolex .....LOL

### Optimalbound — 2016-10-17T15:24:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-a-watch.18553/post-202961>

> [Nicolaj Sølvsten said:](/goto/post?id=202914)
>
> Thank u sir!  A very PRACTICAL advice but aren't all analog watches consider O.K though?

No, not if you by analog mean "any watch with hands". All electronic (quartz, for the most part) watches will emit some amount of EMF. I would guess that analog quartz are the worst, given that they have tiny motors in them, but I have not verified that by measurements.

If you want a watch that does not generate any EMF, a mechanical watch is what you should be looking for. You can usually recognize them by their sweeping seconds hand. If a mechanical watch is described as being "automatic", that means it is self-winding and powered by the movement of your arm. The watches I suggested, are all self-winding mechanical watches that do not contain any electronics or batteries.

If you want to go one step further, and get a watch that does not act as an antenna for nnEMF in your environment, my guess is that a watch with a ceramic case and a leather or fabric strap would be optimal. Brands like Omega, Blancpain, IWC, Hublot and Rado come to mind, and they are all pretty expensive.

Another way to go if you do not want metal touching your skin for some reason, is to put a regular (mechanical) watch on what the Germans call a "Bund" strap. Such as this:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

### Nicolaj Sølvsten — 2016-10-17T21:14:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-a-watch.18553/post-202985>

Alright, I totally agree with your points and I am very thankful that you have made me aware of them. Check this out:

I am however worried that the metal used will attract the nnemf, especially since the backside of the watch appears to be made of metal where it will come into contact with my skin.
I suppose the optimal watch would be a 100% wooden mechanical watch, but to what extent do you reckon the metal implemented will negatively affect my health?
Link to the displayed watch: [https://www.woodwatches.com/series/dover/koa-and-black](https://www.woodwatches.com/series/dover/koa-and-black)

### Optimalbound — 2016-10-18T19:42:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-a-watch.18553/post-203022>

> [Nicolaj Sølvsten said:](/goto/post?id=202985)
>
> I am however worried that the metal used will attract the nnemf, especially since the backside of the watch appears to be made of metal where it will come into contact with my skin.
> I suppose the optimal watch would be a 100% wooden mechanical watch, but to what extent do you reckon the metal implemented will negatively affect my health?

I have to defer to Jack's superior knowledge on that question. If I were to guess, I'd say that having metal touching your skin and acting as an antenna would be detrimental in an nnEMF-heavy envirornment, but might even be a good thing in a natural environment. In any case, I suspect it is not anywhere near the top of the list of things that most of us need to prioritize.

There's a Norwegian company called Etla (etla.no) that also sells wooden watches with a wooden back. Unfortunately, they use a quartz movement. I suspect there is an inner metal case, so EMF from the electronics inside is probably shielded equally well (or poorly) as in a regular steel case quartz watch.

[](http://www.etla.no/)

I remember working with a swiss geologist who had a swiss watch (a Tissot , I think) where the case was made from rock. Quite fitting for a swiss geologist, I thought.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-18T20:13:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-a-watch.18553/post-203024>

Metal does draw EMF and stray voltages in an environment but all metals do it differently. Rolex is 18 K gold so it is pretty inert.

### Optimalbound — 2016-10-18T22:11:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-a-watch.18553/post-203035>

Aren't ceramics even more inert, Jack?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-19T02:58:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-a-watch.18553/post-203039>

not all ceramics.........some are highly magnetic and being used in TI's.

### Optimalbound — 2016-10-22T20:23:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wearing-a-watch.18553/post-203189>

Thanks. I had assumed that "rolex" was your synonym for "mechanical watch" and did not consider the diamagnetic properties of gold. (By the way, far from all Rolex models are gold.)

# Thread: Rik Vlasblom's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rik-vlasbloms-journal.18554/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rik Vlasblom — 2016-10-07T23:07:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rik-vlasbloms-journal.18554/post-202471>

Hi, my name is Rik Vlasblom, I've been wandering around on our pale blue dot for about 34 years now. Mostly in my home country The Netherlands, in South-Holland province.

I've got a general IT background. No specialty. And I am currently working at Leiden University.
Usually I know something about everything. Even outside my work-area. Very curiosity driven. And I guess that's how I landed on JK's website eventually.

I've been using the internet since I was about 7 and been learning a lot from people all over the world. I like connecting things that are seemingly not related, yet are. Reality is one "thing" and ignorance devides it.

I am an atheist (which I define as a lack of believe in super-natural) and long been interested in why people believe things, and how to acquire knowledge. So I have been listening forever to all kinds of claims and learned to be sceptical. I am very comfortable with the words "I do not know", as that's always where learning seems to start.

I think I got interested more than before in "the Health topic", when my mother got very sick (meningitis) and a few years later my father (who I think had all kinds of health troubles in his life), got diabetes type 2 (I think), heart problems, dieing/rotting/infected skin/flesh, amputed leg and eventually got some form of cancer from which he then died.

I started to read about food, as that seemed to be an important part of the environment. I learned all kinds of random things on the subject and found (like religion) people claiming all kinds of (conflicting) things. Which told me we've got a major gab in our understanding of what it means to be healthy, thus remain healthy or become healthy.

From absorbing the available content Jack puts out, I believe 70-85% of the information I do not or not fully understand. Mostly because of my lack in background of chemistry and biology, so I am working on that. Other facters are, available time, the abbreviations and general jargon and sometimes a few English words I do not understand (although I think my English isn't that bad).

I've been following Jack's website for many months now and at some point got convinced enough to support the Quantlet project, and later on to buy the suggested books. After more reading and listening I finally became gold-member as my thirst for knowledge on this subject remains.

Currently I am trying to stay up-to-date with new JK-blogs and media-content, while catching up on old blogs (currently the whole Time-series) and meanwhile learning about the basics of chemistry. This all is a very slow process for me, as I find it hard to focus on just one thing for a long period. But I'm sure it will get easier over time.

On the subject of my own health:

History of bad things (inflammation is key I see now):
\- Inflammation in bal-sack around the age of 11. Lost nothing important fortunately
\- Bursted appendix around the age of 17 I believe. I was told I almost died there. So that's gone now.
\- Some inflammation of tooth gums. My dentist measures every half a year. I wonder how all my changes will influence the results.
\- Very slight tinitus near left ear, when I move my jaw around the tone differs a bit or goes away. Might be because of the placement of a tooth-filling in the back of my mouth. Or maybe a result of wisdom teeth removal over time?

Some current things:
\- My blood pressure is normal to excellent.
\- I always bi-cycle and walk
\- I usually fall asleep quickly, sleep well and automatically wake up around 30-60 minutes before sunrise.
\- I do feel alert or anxious a lot? Not sure what that is a bout.
\- This year (2016) I embraced the sun way more (since early spring to minimize negative effects). Felt good.

Some other facts:
\- Average weekly sea-food: 2 mackerel, 2 herring, 3 oysters, 100gr. wakame, 1 eel, portion baked mussels (as snack), sometimes a small piece of salmon.
\- Using blue-blocking glasses, which of course weirds people out, which gives me at least 2 laughts a day.
\- Minding the light cycles whenever possible
\- Minding (nn)EMF whenever possible
\- I was never a fan of WiFi (rather have cable) and thus keep it turned off.
\- I've always used cold water on face for some reason, that made adapting to cold quite easy. Although may be I am not that adapted yet, because I have not found a practical way to put lots of ice in my bath for CT to get the water temperature to around 10 C. or lower for deep CT. Fortunately winter is coming. But I sure am more adapted to cold than all people around me!
\- Flux installed on devices I use
\- Television, gone.
\- Red lights for night vision
\- I am not a fan of mobile devices, but recently bought a simple not-so-smart-phone, to use D-minder application and the Quantlet device and for emergencies.
\- Unfortunately my appartment is at 4th floor, so I am spending a lot more freetime outside now. Which is better anyway.
\- Recently found an intrest in growing plants. I don't know what that's about, but I like it. Anyone got ideas for plants indoors?

Code:

```
Owned books related to health:
Epi-Paleo RX by Jack Kruse
Health And Light by John Ott
The Body Electric by Robert O. Becker, Gary Selden
Cross Currents by Dr. Robert O. Becker
Going Somewhere by Andrew Marino
The Fourth Phase Of Water by Gerald H. Pollack
Light in Shaping Life by Roeland van Wijk
Light Medicine of the Future by Jacob Liberman
The Healing Sun – Sunlight and Health in the 21st Century by Richard Hobday
Life On The Edge by JohnJoe Mcfadden, Jim Al-Khalili
Power, Sex, Suicide by Nick Lane
Life Ascending by Nick Lane
The Vital Question by Nick Lane
Oxygen by Nick Lane
The Rainbow and the Worm - The Physics of Organisms by Mae-Wan Ho
Living Rainbow H2O by Mae-Wan Ho
Cosmosapiens by John Hands
Gray's Anatomy 41st edition
```

Code:

```
Other books:
For the Love of Physics (I believe Jack recommended this) - Walter Lewin
Language in Thought and Action - S.I. Haykawa and Alan R. Hayakawa
The Tyranny of Words - Stuart Chase
Selections from Science and Sanity - Alfred Korzybski
Marshall B. Rosenberg's work on Non-violent communication
```

Your welcome . No, I have not hooked up with Wim. I did buy his course a while back.

In reply to your original post about atheistm and agnosticism:

> _'I do not know' is more in alignment with agnosticism than atheism._

Agnosticism and atheism are not perse mutually exclusive. Of course depending on how one defines words for usage.
For me, gnosticism is about knowledge and theism is about believe. Then a-theism is not being convinced of the super-natural claim. Distinct from anti-theism, where one is convinced that the super-natural claim is not true, but false.

In populair language, I would be a weak-atheist. And I see no point in trying to convince a theistic person out of his/her believe or convincing myself in to claiming that the super-natural believe one holds is not true.

I found that the best time to talk is when someone listens .

### Rik Vlasblom — 2017-12-12T21:52:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rik-vlasbloms-journal.18554/post-230165>

Looking back on more than a year of changes for me. Minding light cycles, more sun, more grounding (summer time), CT, filtering 400-500nm on my personal devices when appropriate, using Flux on work-devices, minding electronics in general, sleeping on magnetico (20gs). No more TV nor coffee. And using The Quantlet of course.
Eating mostly fish and eggs, some meat. Many mushrooms, lots of fats, plenty of greens, some fruit and of course plenty of water (all minding cycles so far as I am able).
I added Q10 (reduced), D-Ribose, Acetyl L Carnitine, Taurine, Magnesium (L-Threonate, Malate, Orotate). Occational fasting until about 11 am.

I notice I eat much less than before. I do have a better body composition and lost a little weight. I feel like I have more energy to spend. Especially so in the morning. When I wake, I am just ready and focust.
I've been sick only once this year and very shortly so (2 days). Still walking and cycling around in t-shirt at zero degrees celsius. Feeling fine.

Doing only little weight lifting and I like taking walks and cycling and occational swimming and jogging.

I am currently 35 years old, 182 cm tall, at 78.5 KG weight, while my highest weight (many years back) was about 86KG. And last year at 82KG.
Still reading many of the blogs Jack has provided. And I am now able to type all Russian letters blindly on regular US (int) keyboard.

### Rik Vlasblom — 2018-10-27T00:56:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rik-vlasbloms-journal.18554/post-254273>

I'm still around. Fulltime job and learning basics of Russian language. I estimate to be able to recognize around 2500 words now.
Learning a new song on piano and re-reading EMF2. And recently I've had bloodtests done. Here are the results from back in 2011 and now 2018.

The D3 (25OH) seems weird to me, because it should be at its highest now that UV-B is gone.
And in 2011 it was much higher in month February, where I would expect it to be lower. May be back then I had added D3 supplement. Not sure.

The triglycerides and LDL-cholesterol are both low. But this seems to be a good thing from what I've read.

# Thread: Winter light schedule
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-light-schedule.18556/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NaturalHealthForLife — 2016-10-08T08:09:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-light-schedule.18556/post-202482>

I have been reading a lot about optimizing indoor and outdoor lights. Definitely a lot to learn!

My question is about how to optimize light exposure in a low light area: specifically (Seattle) the cloudy and rainy Pacific Northwest of the United States during the winter.

Are usually wake up at seven and go outdoors for grounding and sunlight. I have noticed that it is no longer a light when I wake up. I am wondering if I should postpone my sunlight exposure until sunrise or if I should be working with some sort of full spectrum indoor light as soon as I wake up.

Wake up:
My current plan is to continue to wake up at seven and eat my BAB breakfast first in relative darkness. At 7:30, 8 AM, or whenever the sun rises, go outside for half an hour of grounded send gazing and walking.

I homeschool my kids so we are very flexible on optimizing, if I can just figure out what to do.

How does sunrise and eating (I am working through Leptin RX) tie in to wake up time.

Perhaps the ideal is to ignore the clock and stay in bed til sun up?

Day time:

Goal to be outside doing school work at a picnic table barefoot all morning for grounding, cold, and sun exposure.

Inside it is very dim while overcast, so it sounds like I should get red light, black light, and full spectrum light indoors.

Sauna in afternoon with full spectrum IR and red lights on.

Evening: it will start to get dark around 4-5 pm my goal is to be done with dinner before it is dark and then go for a sunset walk. After we return we are wearing our orange uvex glasses and keeping the lights dim in the house. Kids go to sleep at 8 and adults at 10 pm.

Does this seem like a good schedule or should I tweak something?

# Thread: Multiple Sessions a day work great
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/multiple-sessions-a-day-work-great.18559/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sfast — 2016-10-08T14:32:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/multiple-sessions-a-day-work-great.18559/post-202490>

Hi Freezers,

I try a couple of protocols that divide a bigger session into some smaller sessions. Me and my clients make great result. Instead of one big session, try:

\- Get 20-30min light movement in the cold air.
\- Get two shorter sessions of ice bath (depending on your level) per day in.
\- Reduce gradually the amount of clothes, bedding and temperature in your home environment.

I will try to add a once or twice weekly bigger session (right before the point of intensive shivering) for further experimentation.

Overall, this approach looks promising especially for people with a lower tolerance of the associated stress response.

Best of Luck
Sascha

# Thread: High Schooler Wanting to Help Our Species Lacking Direction
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-schooler-wanting-to-help-our-species-lacking-direction.18563/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2016-10-08T19:37:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-schooler-wanting-to-help-our-species-lacking-direction.18563/post-202512>

Hi,

I'm 17 and my life has been transformed by the application of this information- that my environment determines my health.

I want to do what I can with my life so that ideally no one is confined to suffer endlessly as I used to if they were simply never taught to look deeper and challenge what they are taught. I'm lucky I was; most aren't. Obviously, this involves education, and especially fixing the broken science system- bridging the gap between research and application, as Gilbert Ling says on his site.

**Does anyone have suggestions of things I could be doing?** People I could contact who are working in this field?

I just started an "Optimal Club" at my school and 100's of kids are interested. I'm hoping a few really catch on to the problem and give me someone to work with. I have free periods all morning  and spend most of my time "biohacking", reading Jack's stuff, other quantum bio, and other stuff for ideas and knowledge that might help me, and talking to friends, teachers, parents' friends, and anyone else who is interested or has advice, also just expanding my network to have the most influence possible.

Dr. Kruse, if you see this, I can't afford a membership right now, but I'd really like to know what you think a kid like me should do. I'm willing to sacrifice my health to some extent to try to help people who might otherwise be screwed.

Thanks a lot,

\- Matt

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-16T05:29:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-schooler-wanting-to-help-our-species-lacking-direction.18563/post-202891>

> [Valerie said:](/goto/post?id=202876)
>
> Change doesn't happen overnight.....baby steps...need to bring awareness first.

I don't buy this at all. WHY?

Awareness, alone doesn't enlist change. This is why I stopped joining groups for support of any diseases. All they do is institutionalize you and wallet biopsy you to death with promises of funding for research but they never do. Why? Because without their harvesting of your wallet CONSISTENTLY they would never be allowed to exist. You all have known this; you have to understand why you must change your environment. Then wellness can visit you. Just reconnect with nature and not any awareness group.

I believe your world view has to be structured in principle on some specific doctrine which shapes how you function and view humanity. Embracing QED is akin to wearing steel-toed boots in a ballet-slipper world today. This is not a disadvantage but a deep help. It stops you from being a lemming. You begin to think autonomously. Understanding that life is bigger than your set of challenges will help you overcome obstacles that will come your way from time to time. It's impossible to increase the scope of our worldview when all that we are focused on is ourselves. When 'MY GOALS' become 'OUR GOALS', success follows. Whatever a person's ultimate concern may be, it will have an enormous influence on everything else the person does or believes.

This is why I don't try communicate with folks on their level, I try my best to connect with them on my level. When someone tells me my message is cryptic they have alerted me to their own myopic thinking. I pay deep attention to that. I throw them rope and see how the react and then I decide if they are part of the solution or problem and react appropriately. I want to elevate understanding and not sink to a level where a superficial connection is made on social media. I want people who are all in. Connection is empowerment. There is a very real difference between making "contact" and making a “connection” with someone else. Contact may lead to a one-time transaction in an awareness group, whereas, connection always leads to a life-time transformation. When you connect - which "investment" holds potential for the greater return with people? Modern humans make contact through social media. That is not my game. Reality is that humans contain invisible threads to nature and they bind us most strongly to our life. Today, technology allows us to contact many people together, but we are all dying of loneliness, in spite of these contacts. Reconnection to our humanness is the key to survival. Disconnection is the key to destruction. The interesting thing - the same key opens both doors.

# Thread: Samsung Galaxy s7 RF through the roof!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/samsung-galaxy-s7-rf-through-the-roof.18568/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jeff Power — 2016-10-10T20:02:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/samsung-galaxy-s7-rf-through-the-roof.18568/post-202596>

I traded in my iPhone 6Plus for a Samsung galaxy 7 a few months ago thinking I would be better off. Well I finally got a Cornet RF reader and boy was I wrong...

My samsung pulses every few seconds at 500-1,600 mW/m2 at a couple inches away and 20-40 mW/m2 at talking distance on speakerphone. It's crazy!

I tested some iPhone 6's in my office and they sit below 1 mW/m2 most of the time and pulse on to 2-5 mW/m2 every so often.

Ugh...

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-10T20:31:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/samsung-galaxy-s7-rf-through-the-roof.18568/post-202597>

told ya on the Q & A.

Here is another thing that will astound you:

Almost every place in Nevada has one. FYI

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-10T20:32:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/samsung-galaxy-s7-rf-through-the-roof.18568/post-202598>

Still think food can fix this shit?

### Jeff Power — 2016-10-10T20:49:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/samsung-galaxy-s7-rf-through-the-roof.18568/post-202600>

The onslaught from all directions is pretty crazy right now. Reading Martin Blank's book that you recommended, very interesting stuff...

Martin Blank puts it well in his book when he observes that getting people to accept the data that exists related to EMF and health is going to be much harder to change than it was for tabacco, asbestos, etc. (which wasn't easy either), because so much good has come from technology that we/society does not want to believe the data, so it is ignored.

### Jeff Power — 2016-10-11T16:33:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/samsung-galaxy-s7-rf-through-the-roof.18568/post-202638>

After further testing, I went into a VZ store and those people working there need serious mitigation. I tested my Samsung again and the RF strength was far lower than when I tested it in my house or office. I am thinking that my house and office are relatively far from cell towers so the power turns up on my phone big league. I need to do more testing.

I am going to switch back to the iphone and trade in my galaxy today...

I am working on a way to get all my calls and texts on my hard wired computer at work so I don't need to have my phone on at all while at work.

# Thread: EMF refugees and the radiation Act of 1968 ruining society
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-refugees-and-the-radiation-act-of-1968-ruining-society.18578/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-11T21:06:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-refugees-and-the-radiation-act-of-1968-ruining-society.18578/post-202653>

How much do you know about "The Radiation Control for Health and Safety Act of 1968?" You might ask me, what does that law have to do with the issues in this thread or in the blog I have linked below? In 1962, following the birth of thousands of armless and legless babies in Europe caused by thalidomide that had been sold to pregnant women, the US Congress required drug companies to provide pre-market scientific evidence of the safety of "new drugs". In 1968, Senator Rogers, of Florida proposed applying the same social principle to devices that emitted man-made electromagnetic energy into the environment. His proposal, The Radiation Control for Health and Safety Act of 1967, would have authorized the government to perform research and regulate the safety of all devices that emitted man-made electromagnetic energy. But opponents of this principle, the industries that manufactured and sold energy-emitting devices, successfully argued that the health impact of man-made electromagnetic energy should be assessed "subsequent to marketing". This policy shifted the burden of proof to the party asserting injury and was subsequently adopted by all federal agencies since this time. Each relevant federal agency therefore NOW BEGINS with the assumption of SAFETY and not precaution, and requires any aggrieved litigant to prove non-safety of the devices man uses. This is why we need lawyers to remied the mess this law gave the USA. This law benefits the tech industry. [http://magazine.nd.edu/news/67946-i-am-an-emf-refugee/](http://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fmagazine.nd.edu%2Fnews%2F67946-i-am-an-emf-refugee%2F&h=wAQH53mvt)

### Penny — 2016-10-11T21:49:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-refugees-and-the-radiation-act-of-1968-ruining-society.18578/post-202656>

That was amazing... soon she will have lots of company I predict... every 7 or 12 houses will be near a 5g transmitter if they do this to us? Oh, there will be no escaping this - but here's the rub - they will attribute it to something else because like she said - it's too much of an inconvenient truth - we have plenty of evidence now - and still they put their blinders on - strike 3 for paradigms...

### nonchalant — 2016-10-26T23:14:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-refugees-and-the-radiation-act-of-1968-ruining-society.18578/post-203353>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=202656)
>
> Oh, there will be no escaping this - but here's the rub - they will attribute it to something else because like she said - it's too much of an inconvenient truth

Something like...."You're just getting older"? That's a convenient explanation for most medical problems, since it is always true. Assuming you are still living, that is.

### Penny — 2016-10-27T02:02:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-refugees-and-the-radiation-act-of-1968-ruining-society.18578/post-203355>

# Thread: How do you understand medicine's place in nature?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-understand-medicines-place-in-nature.18579/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1

# Thread: Toddler - HUGE appetite, tiny size
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/toddler-huge-appetite-tiny-size.18581/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dar9903 — 2016-10-12T23:40:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/toddler-huge-appetite-tiny-size.18581/post-202702>

Hi,

I am new here. I've just begun looking into Dr. Kruse's Rxs for adrenals and leptin resistance for me. My question though is actually about my daughter, who is 21 months old and a little shortypants. She is very small, with a bit of a distended belly (has had that since she was really little). Since she started eating solids, I've tried basically a paleo diet but started adding in some grains when she started in daycare a couple days a week a few months ago.

My question is about her relentless appetite. She basically always wants to eat. Not only that, but she eats way more than any of her peers that we know. And yet, she's very short -- like she's in 12-18 month pants, and her sleeves are too long in her 18 month jacket.

I feel her pain in some ways because starting at about 2 p.m., I can't stop myself from eating. I'm wondering whether we're both deficient in something (I just stopped breastfeeding her last week) or our hormones are off? Or parasites? I'm wracking my brain here.

She takes probiotics every day as well as CLO with added vit D.

Any thoughts would be appreciated!

Dar

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-13T02:47:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/toddler-huge-appetite-tiny-size.18581/post-202710>

How many ultrasounds did you have with her during pregnancy?

A paleo diet adding grains? That sounds like an oxymoron and giving a kid grains is not wise.

Has she been checked for Prader Willi syndrome?

### Dar9903 — 2016-10-13T04:23:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/toddler-huge-appetite-tiny-size.18581/post-202716>

>
> How many ultrasounds did you have with her during pregnancy?
>
> A paleo diet adding grains? That sounds like an oxymoron and giving a kid grains is not wise.
>
> Has she been checked for Prader Willi syndrome?

Thanks so much for your quick response. Unfortunately, I had a number of ultrasounds early on because of a miscarriage scare (heavy bleeding).

RE: paleo diet -- fair enough. I added them because she was constantly stealing them anyway from the other kid in the nanny share. The bulk of her diet is meat and veg.

And no, I can ask the ped about that. I do think there could be a growth hormone issue but not sure if it's genetic or not...?

### Dar9903 — 2016-10-13T04:25:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/toddler-huge-appetite-tiny-size.18581/post-202717>

And other than removing the grains I added in, and testing her potentially for that genetic condition, any ideas on paths forward? Thanks so much again.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-13T15:16:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/toddler-huge-appetite-tiny-size.18581/post-202727>

It sounds like something is up.......some kind of malabsorption syndrome or something else. It should be worked up and not by a general peds. You need to take the kid to a teaching hospital where they have specialist.

### Dar9903 — 2016-10-14T04:13:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/toddler-huge-appetite-tiny-size.18581/post-202784>

thanks - that sounds like a good plan. Appreciate your thoughts.

### BabyBe — 2016-10-14T22:32:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/toddler-huge-appetite-tiny-size.18581/post-202807>

Dar9903, we are in almost exact same situation. My youngest daughter (T) is 21 months old, is petite, has a distended tummy, low gut motility (soft poop but often no urge to go), and an insatiable appetite. I just spoke with our naturopath about it yesterday. She suggested a stool test for parasites - have you considered it?

A bit of a backstory: We had this exact issue with our older daughter (L) who is now 5. Back then, we were not quite where we are now in terms of natural living and gut healing, so it took me a long time to even begin to figure things out. L was never regular, even when exclusively breastfed, and by age 2 started having mini poops many times a day. That's when I read a book called "It's no accident" and learned about fecal compaction and how small poops get past it, but the bulk stays in. We had her take Miralax (which I now regret), first a very high dose, then maintenance for 2 years, along with a routine of potty time after breakfast and dinner. later we went dairy, gluten, and egg free which seemed to help. She is still not perfectly regular, but way better than before, and her appetite is more similar to kids her age now. So, with T, when she developed a bit of eczema and stopped pooping 2-6 x day at 5 months, I took a more cautious approach. She has not had dairy, gluten, or eggs, we started doing castor oil massage, fish oil, and a good probiotic. along with prebiotic foods (kraut juice, water kefir) daily. She goes daily, but still has a bit of a distended tummy and a huge appetite, hence my further quest for answers. Hope you find your answers soon! I'd love to be able to stay in touch.

### BabyBe — 2016-10-14T22:35:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/toddler-huge-appetite-tiny-size.18581/post-202808>

Oh, and my oldest does not have Prader Willi. She was also tested for celiac and cystic fibrosis (negative) and had traditional skin prick allergy tests - nothing came up. Youngest has not yet been tested for anything.

### Sue-UK — 2016-10-15T14:02:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/toddler-huge-appetite-tiny-size.18581/post-202838>

I have 2 granddaughters, who I have been looking after one day a week for the past year (although the oldest has just started school). The youngest (just 2) is similar to what you both describe ..... I have found though that because she arrives early in the morning, we can eat breakfast together. I started giving her a breakfast loaded with protein and fats. She would eat her fill and usually not want anything carbie afterwards. It is then protein (usually fish) with some seasonal salad or veg at lunchtime, again getting some seasonal fats in somewhere. She goes home for tea. Plenty of fluids, and as much time outside as possible, and over the summer particularly barefoot and wearing as little clothing as possible, just getting in the shade in the heat of the day. Both my granddaughters love going barefoot, and now play outside barefoot at home. I have found that my youngest granddaughter's appetite has become much more normal, on days with me she now eats an age appropriate sized meal, stops when she has had enough, and doesn't need or ask for snacks. I put it down to the 3 legged stool - light water and magnetism. The magnetism seems especially important to her, when she climbs on me for a cuddle or a snooze, she has always intuitively placed a hand on my bare skin near to my heart. She is telling me what she needs. My plan for the winter with her is that as the light levels are dropping, and when the ground is too cold for her feet, I will do the barefoot walking or sitting, and carry or hold her so that she can hold on to my bare skin and ground through me, or hold her hand if she walks in shoes. At the moment as overnight temperatures are dropping, I wait until the sun has warmed the grass enough for her. My intuition is telling me that because she is getting enough electrons from the earth, her appetite isn't trying to compensate with electrons from excessive food, so her appetite is more normal.

# Thread: postpartum depression, low dopamine?, fibromyalgia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/postpartum-depression-low-dopamine-fibromyalgia.18582/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dar9903 — 2016-10-13T00:10:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/postpartum-depression-low-dopamine-fibromyalgia.18582/post-202704>

Hi -
I am pretty new here and have to admit that my brain fog makes it hard for me to follow some of what is discussed on the blog and the forums (my problem, not an issue of the content!) I'm looking for help on where to start.

Background on me: I am just crawling out of a long bout with post-partum depression (I'm 21 months PP). I concurrently have had digestive slow-down and what seems like a total tanking of my sex hormones--no sex drive, no period (just stopped breastfeeding to try to see if I'll restart). I have achy joints and muscle fatigue/wasting (labeled fibromyalgia/CFS by rheum).

I have what seems like a way overactive sympathetic nervous system--easily startled, constipation when stressed, etc. I have low basal body temp, low BP, and I also have Raynaud's, so the idea of cold thermogenesis is totally scary to me! (Although I want to try it...) I have high iGG titers for a bunch of infections (parvo, EBV, mycoplasma) and had an active mycoplasma infection till recently.

I have restless sleep. I've had a couple hyperthyroid flares in life, but not since daughter was born. If anything, my thyroid is sluggish -- conversion of T4 to T3 is on the low side. I basically feel much older than my 35 year old self.

Oh, I should also mention that I've always been super fair-skinned and my family inculcated in me a real fear of sun exposure, which I'm working to get over because I know my vit D is low. I'm not sure I'm great at absorbing it from the sun, though--what's going on with me seems pretty consistent with low a-MSH. My job requires me to sit behind a computer -- but I did reduce my hours to 15 a week after I (my adrenals?) tanked out postpartum. I have also spent a ton of time on the internet searching for answers to my issues (tried parasite cleanses, candida diet, autoimmune paleo, etcetcetc), so I have probably been exposing myself way too much to EMF.

I know that my hormones are out of whack. I just don't know where to start. I saw on one of the threads that the leptin reset is not for people with BMI of 25 or lower, that they should proceed directly to the post-leptin reset time. Is that true? Because I have an average BMI (21) but I crave food from 2 pm to 6 pm basically. I ate strict paleo for a while and then realized it was making me shaky and sad. So I've added some grains back in.

Sorry for the long post. I guess I'm just hoping someone who's been in a similar spot can break down for me where to begin with all this.

Thanks for reading!
Dar

# Thread: The COST OF NETFLIX:  nnEMF.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cost-of-netflix-nnemf.18583/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-13T01:03:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cost-of-netflix-nnemf.18583/post-202707>

From one of my friends AB: "Why I don't have Netflix or broadband at home"
1.8 hours a day watching streaming TV = 657 hours a year.
Netflix = $15 a month.
Broadband to support Netflix = $80 a month.
17 years of Netflix (streaming started September 1999) = 11,169 hours and $19,380 invested.
11,169 hours at $12 an hour = $134,028 + $19,380 = $153408 opportunity income lost. 2-bedroom rental income condo in my area = $45,000 with an estimated monthly net profit (including depreciation) of close to $310 a month. One can buy 3-4 condos for $153,408 netting you somewhere between $930 and $1240 a month in income (not including including income taxes). That's upwards of $15,000 a year going forward at the 17 year mark.
If you're 38 years old, ask yourself what benefits you gained from throwing away that $150,000+ and how much better life would be if you had $15,000 a year in additional income (not including the $1140 a year expenses on continued streaming media).
Now extend it 13 more years with reinvestments of your profits and saved expenses. At the age of 50, you could have a likely income of close to $62,000 a year plus a positive net worth of close to $750,000.
1.8 hours a day on the couch for a little excitement versus retiring in your 40s or 50s.
To make these numbers worse, cable TV isn't in them. If I had cable TV, it would cost me upwards of $1260 a year PLUS an additional 1+ hour a day of TV watching (opportunity income lost).
Nope, no Netflix for me. Kruse tells me it would cost me over 250,000 thousand dollars in medical costs too because of the indoor blue lit world I'd inhabit. When you sum it all togehter you have to be nuts to get netflix. Seems most of my friends are.

### kyrakitty — 2016-10-13T20:43:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-cost-of-netflix-nnemf.18583/post-202762>

Fu%#k me! Sorry for the potty mouth... what a reality check!  Netflix 12 step program coming soon to your neighborhood.

### Inger — 2016-10-13T21:38:12+0300

so lucky I always hated TV...lol

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-13T04:10:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swimming-in-the-ocean.18584/post-202715>

Best place to ground? Well it might depend upon what your phyla is. The ability to sense gives the ability to detect the electromagnetic wave pulsations in th environment. A sensory system has a stimulus and a detector. If we exclude eyes and vision, detectors are one of two types- chemical or mechanical. Magnetic detectors also exist and they track the direction of the Earth's magnetic field. The intereseting evolutionary fact is that the magentic sense is best developed in very few phyla from the Cambrian explosion. This ties deep into my November 2016 webinar. Those two animal groups are insects and chordates. Humans are in the chordate phyla. What links these two arms of evolution? Water.............. In the ocean salt water has high conductivity and the oceans play a major role in generating the earth's magnetic field - magnetic fields generated in our atmosphere and by the sun induce electric and magnetic fields in our oceans. Both insects and chordates showed up at the Cambrian explosion which took place 543 to 538 years ago. The last Snowball Earth effect occured 575 million years ago. This snow melted around the earth of 30 million years and then eukaryotic life exploded when the water chemistry of the seas changed to alter their conductivity. Could this change link the the EZ rate? Could the luminosity of the sun also change at this time to warm the Earth? Might chemicals like DHA and oxygen both show up for these reasons? IS this why the paramagnetic DHA and oxygen molecule were used to photoelectrically form the eye for the first time in new transparent seas? Water is a magnetic dipole. It is magnetically neutral. When an EZ is formed it gains a net negative charge and it can be used as a magnetic compass.
