# Thread: What Materials Glow Under a Black or Ultraviolet Light?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-materials-glow-under-a-black-or-ultraviolet-light.18586/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2016-10-13T23:00:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-materials-glow-under-a-black-or-ultraviolet-light.18586/post-202772>

[http://chemistry.about.com/od/glowi...s-Glow-Under-a-Black-or-Ultraviolet-Light.htm](http://chemistry.about.com/od/glowingprojects/ss/What-Materials-Glow-Under-a-Black-or-Ultraviolet-Light.htm)

Very fun article

for instance, quinine glows:

Pure coenzyme Q10 was isolated for the first time in 1957 by Professor Fredrick Crane and four associates at the University of Wisconsin. They
demonstrated that coenzyme Q10 is essential to the bioenergetics of the mitochondria, and thus is essential for human life to exist.
Coenzyme Q10 plays a critical role in the production of energy in nearly every cell of your body. Without Co Q10 you would not have enough energy to
stay alive. Due to instability of Co Q10 in aerobic energy production Co Q10 is found in most living systems and for this reason was named
“ubiquinone” (for “ubiquitous quinine”).
Specifically it is a lipophilic electron carrier that functions in the body’s defence against free radicals. It is the final component of the respiratory chain in
the mitchrondrial membranes. These electron transferring carriers are arranged so that the reduced member of one redox couple is oxidides by the
oxidised member of the next component in the system.
While coenzyme Q10 is neither a nucleotide not a protein, it functions like pyridine nucleotides. Coenzyme Q10 serves as a mobile electron transport.
The quinine part of the molecule is alternately reduced (hydrogenated) and oxidised (dehydrogenated) as it operates between various flavin-like
dehydrogenate is to take away a hydrogen atom.
CLINICAL APPLICATIONS

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-materials-glow-under-a-black-or-ultraviolet-light.18586/post-202998>

In some chemical reactions both electrons and protons move together. When they transfer, they can move concertedly or in separate steps. Light-induced reactions of this sort are particularly relevant to biological systems, such as Photosystem II where plants use photons from the sun to convert water into oxygen. Now if you re-read Nick Lane's latest book, The Vital Question he mentions MANY times that life's key mechanism revolves around proton gradients and he says, NO ONE CAN EXPLAIN WHY! Nick says this because he is not down with the physics of light like he is with mitochondria..........I've gone over the photoelectric effect with respect to light movement with electrons........but light also moves protons in ways biology is clueless too.

The transition occurs photocatalyitically when water is the intermediary who brokers the deal. What is published in solution based biochemical books never gets to this level of sophistication because they subtracted out the water to just study the substrates in pathways so they missed the key quantum mechanisms between light and protons. Nick is a biologist and he sufferes from this biologic dogma too. So when protons transfer between hydrogen-bonded complexes in solution, like water, quantum mechanism dominate the action. These water networks (aquaphotomics) have provided us massive evidence for new optical transitions characteristic of the direct transfer of a proton.

In recent physics papers the proton transfer occured at 360 nm (UVB) and it occurred rapidly. It was reported as early as 250 femtoseconds after the laser pulse. y'all just learned in the Time series that all semiconductors are capable of making laser light. And I have mentioned the 1538.5 nm pulse with respect to proton tunneling many times here and there. Now you know why. We can make red light from UV light that comes into excited electrons at the NAD+/NADH couple. The Q-cycle then frequency shift the light to something cells can use to facilitate proton transfers optically very easily.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-15T03:24:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-sheet-on-ground-outdoors-making-me-an-antenna-for-nn-emf.18587/post-202827>

Where you live ties to population density and the more obedient idiots you surround yourself with the worse your outcomes will be going forward. Use this data as a learning tool. Wifi creates RF frequencies that interact with the copper wires in your house when an electric current is moving through the wires. This changes the RF radiation into other forms of energy that can harm us and we usually do not perceive it unless we measure it. When I use the word "zip code" I am specifically talking about what people in your environment are willing to accept for themselves and you having to endure that collateral damage. Optimizing your zip code is critically important to living and thriving and not just surviving. The take away: it's not how much you can do to be quantum but how many of your neighbor's refuse to what really needs to be done that matters in your families health. [https://www.engadget.com/2016/09/17/linksys-ea9500-review/](https://www.engadget.com/2016/09/17/linksys-ea9500-review/)

### nonchalant — 2016-10-26T23:00:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-sheet-on-ground-outdoors-making-me-an-antenna-for-nn-emf.18587/post-203351>

> [Matty\_M said:](/goto/post?id=202786)
>
> Could sleeping on a sheet on the ground in my backyard make me more of an antenna for all of the Wi-Fi, smart meter, cell signls, and power line fields around me while I sleep?
>
> Would it be wiser to not be connected to the ground, if I cannot yet afford a Magnetico?

Matty, in my own experience, living in the city and then in the country, yes, I would say that sleeping grounded in the backyard might be a problem. If you are in a city, with nearby power lines and smart meters you might get adverse effects that become apparent with time.

I am sensitive to nnEMF, and I now keep my shoes on anytime I'm in a town. Even in the country I have to avoid being within a stone's throw from an electric power line, especially if I'm barefoot. I were in a large city park with no nearby power lines or houses or people, I might take off the shoes and lay on the grass. My magnetico was no protection in the city, if I grounded to an outside rod while in bed. Perhaps all the grounding I did in the city (in my backyard, and elsewhere, as well as in bed) was the cause of my sensitivity.

One night in the city I was noticing that DH's twitches while sleeping seemed to be regular. So I brought out my RF meter, and found that his twitches coincided with spikes on the meter. From the neighbor's smart meter, no doubt. This was on a magnetico bed with a grounding cloth connected to an outside rod. I removed the cloth the next day, and noticed only a twitch or two that night.

### Samy Colddigger — 2016-10-28T06:46:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-sheet-on-ground-outdoors-making-me-an-antenna-for-nn-emf.18587/post-203396>

Yea. My bet would be then to find a cabin in the woods on some farm or something? Dont know what you have down near Philly for options. Wish you luck man....I can say that finding a safe place away from emf is worth it. I am in a cabin in the far north where I have the modern conveniances but I can ground on an earthing sheet with breakers off every night. I also have a ten gauss magnetico and my sleeping feels like it regenerates me greatly from the day of stress and emf. Missing that sun though. It has been cloudy, dark, and warm here for this time of year (its about 28 F). Need more cold. Luckily, I can afford to eat local seafood regularly . Lots of Sockeye salmon and big Alaskan oysters. I am lucky to eat raw owyters, you should try if you don't already. I get a cahrge just drinking their juices (mhmm salty)

I have also been using a grounding strap at work and it makes a huge difference I feel but I have no way of measuring.

Now that I think of it, it is probably not a good idea when close to any buildings or generators. But when I am out in the yard kicking carboard I might be making up for it? It is a wide open space bathed in light.

I would say find ways to stay out in the woods and mountains as long as possible until you can find a more permanent home. Saved me big time when I was living in Vancouver. Would go from the thick of down town to old growth forest, drinking spring water and cold cting in the river. As Jack said mother nature may not provide what she used to but she still knows better. I found walking bear foot in the forest to be amazingly therapeutic. Think of starting some sort of forest yoga retreat up here in the summer. Shame to see Yoga done indoors in the summer here when it is the only time when UV-B is present. My self I need to figure out way to best convey these quantum lessons to those around me. Seems most do not have a sweat clue up here and everybody is blue lit toxic in the winter time and everybody wonders why mental health issues sky rocket in the winter. Seems I have my work but out for me. I find the Vie-light in my nose is condusvice to constructive conversation on these matters. Many people wonder why I have it and I explain the blue light problem to them. I of course explain that red light and uv is the shiit. People give me pretty funny responses I must say. They think I'm cray! haha

### Matty_M — 2016-10-30T22:17:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-sheet-on-ground-outdoors-making-me-an-antenna-for-nn-emf.18587/post-203455>

Being present. I sometimes lose energy that could be helping others in my own worrying. I lose time because I am not present and focused on my goals.

I strive to improve this. At this point, I am focused on biohacking my environment here and being as optimal as I can; on disrupting the current and sharing what I know; learning about how I might solve these issues; and "projects"- that range from contacting various experts in the fields of EMF law and the current "conspiracy", quantum biology, etc., to my Optimal Club for sharing info at my high school (some 50 or more interested members), talking with my school board, making money to travel the world after graduating this June, and so on. If I keep working at this, especially at my projects- I feel like I will be able to have a large impact.

I spend what seems to me too much time re-evaluating, caught up in whether what I'm doing is best. I'm trying to strike a balance of this "skepticism" and of doing things that will improve peoples' lives. Thinking about it today, I just don't want people to suffer like I did growing up with constant gas, horrible allergies, daily headaches and migraines, always being tired, feeling like crap, and thinking that life is meant to suck.......... This is why I persevere. I put the weight of the world on my shoulders because any less feels like I'm shrugging responsibility; at the same time, this act steals from my ability. A balance of pressure, mindfulness, action, evaluation, re-orienting mindfulness, and so on.

### Samy Colddigger — 2016-10-30T23:46:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-on-sheet-on-ground-outdoors-making-me-an-antenna-for-nn-emf.18587/post-203456>

Agreed. I feel you. I Need more music in my life. Making music specifically. Keeps me present and focused. When I took Violin lessons in high school I found there was an initial slump in my ability to focus whenever I didn't practice violin. I learned to overcome this without playing violin but I swear the vibrations had a very powerful impact. Just my thoughts.

That is interesting you started a high school club. I should really try and get something going like that up here. It might work in a small community like this. Of course, I would still feel bad that the sickest of people would not be getting the light so to speak. To join an optimal health club requires a relatively good level of dopeamine to begin with. But I figure if one's action is for the good of all beings then helping others who are already relatively healthy will have trickle down effects. Then once there is an organized community that is together with me on this, then we can go to these other NGOs where really sick people are involved and are struggling to navigate with the help of NGOs and government alike(lots of government money here for non profitss and ngos).... and we could say... hey look, there are somethings we have come to learn and we would like to share our collective experiences with you.

I am currently part of a non profit that seeks to educate and provide support for mental health patients who are not sure who trust (ie MD, what have you). The premise of the organization is amazing but needs some serious revamping in the area of education on alternative health practices, esp when it come to light, water, and magnetism.

Good to hear to your perspectives man and wish you best of luck....sending you some warm thoughts. Its minus 2 Celsius right now and sunny and I should really be outside with my shirt off but it is nice and warm here beside the woodstove and I thought I shuold really reply to your post because I think it is important!

Sam

# Thread: Cataract surgery decision
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 123
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Emma Sabin — 2016-10-14T13:52:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-202789>

Hi Jack
I'm a 43 year old woman living in London and I am keen to get your thoughts on my situation regarding my health. I'll give you a summary and then ask you specific questions to make it easier for you.

In 2013 I was diagnosed with breast cancer (2 lumps in left breast plus 18/20 lymph nodes - stage 3a) I then had countless mastectomy ops, chemo, radio and 2 years of Zoladex injections to shut down my ovaries. I am not on any pharmaceutical drugs now (my choice) and have started ovulating again.

Since then I have been diagnosed with adrenal fatigue, hypothyroidism, osteopenia, 2 herniated discs (l3/4/5) and now cataracts.

I have always had immune problems and had glandular fever and nephrotic syndrome a few years ago.

I have worn glasses since I was 6 and contact lenses since I was 12. I am extremely shortsighted (-11.5 and -14) and had a torn retina in my right eye a few years ago in the same eye as the one with the cataract. I've known I've had a cataract forming for over 18 months now and have tried wearing my lenses less and getting more light to my eyes in the morning as well as wearing blue blockers recently at night (tricky over my normal glasses!) but it seems to be getting worse and I am booked in to have the cataract done in 2 weeks. As I am so short sighted and will have incredibly different vision in each eye after one is done I was planning on having the second eye done 2 weeks later. I am due to get Zeiss Trifocal IOL that will mean I will be able to see 3 distances which will be the first time in my life I'll remember doing that without glasses, which as you can imagine makes me feel slightly excited. However, I do not want to make my health any worse by having this done so your thoughts would be very welcome.

My questions are:
1) Is there any way of reversing the cataract naturally? I've found this product that I wondered if you knew about?
[http://www.vita-stream.com/sc/revie...code=ExplodeA248c73b4b9fe27cdaa07c92764c4bbbd](http://www.vita-stream.com/sc/reviews.cgi?storeid=*14884e28aaec508710157605&reviews_mode=1&code=ExplodeA248c73b4b9fe27cdaa07c92764c4bbbd)
2) If I have to have the cataract done as the only option is there any way of my eyes still getting light in to my eyes or will it have to be through my skin?
3) Can I make the cataract worse by getting UV light on the eye?
4) As I live in London (hopefully not for long) sunlight is rare especially for the next 8 months - is there a light you would recommend I could use to get the UV rays for the morning and again is this safe for cataracts and could I use this ifI have the surgery afterwards?
5) Can you recommend a practitioner in London that I could see that follows your protocols. I have been seeing a functional dr here and he's been very good - suggesting a lot of the things you do, but I feel you guys are streets ahead of us and I would like the best advice.

I am currently taking nature thyroid and dhea to help thyroid and adrenals along with 20/30 odd other natural supplements prescribed by my functional dr.

Many thanks in advance.

Emma

### Sue-UK — 2016-10-15T15:38:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-202839>

Hi Emma

"Yes I plan to leave London just not sure where to go yet - any ideas welcome!"

If it were me and planning to stay in the UK I would look at [http://www.metoffice.gov.uk/climate/uk/regional-climates](http://www.metoffice.gov.uk/climate/uk/regional-climates) to give me an overview of the different regional climates. You can view the UK mapped climate averages, and select sunshine as the climate variable, and see it by month or by season or annually. The sea in close proximity would be good, but I would need to research population density and how much that increases during the tourist season. Once I had selected some possibilities I would be looking at cell tower distribution. Once I had narrowed it down, I would visit, perhaps looking at rental possibilities either in a small village, or off the beaten track, and let the agent know I do not want to view a property with a smart meter. (Start letting them know why smart meters will eventually start hitting them in the wallet ) Then I would take my own meter and do my own testing, to see what is happening in the environment, and what my potential neighbours are doing that might impact on me. I live in the sticks at the moment, but when DH and I choose to sell up and move we wouldn't buy another house, we would rent. On a 6 month fixed contract, and thereafter a month's notice, if the situation changes we wouldn't have the hassle of trying to sell, we could move somewhere safer in a month .....

### Emma Sabin — 2016-10-17T22:23:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-202990>

Hi Jack,
There is so much info in this article and I am not able to understand it all however I did see this and wondered what you thought?

With age, the lens becomes more yellowish, and thus, the spectrum of blue light transmission dramatically decreases through the years. It is suspected that one reason older individuals experience sleep problems is the lack of blue light during the daytime. Ayaki et al. \[[59](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4734149/#r59)\] reported that after cataract extraction, sleep quality improved dramatically because more blue light could pass through the intraocular lens. In addition, there has been a discussion on whether a clear or yellow lens is preferable \[[60](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4734149/#r60)\]. Of course, the yellow lens may protect the retina, but the clear lens provides more blue light during the day, providing better quality of sleep \[[61](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4734149/#r61)\].

Hi again Jack,

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-09T23:01:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-206420>

Well if you can get a lens that is none UV blocker I have no issues with it. Hard to do these days. Most IOL's now block 50% blue which is good but they also block all UV. There is the down side. You have to make the call for yourself. The other thing you could ask about is cataract removal without lens implants. Monet did this.

Melanie Proctor told me all about what she did, which is amazing. The problem I have is because I am so shortsighted I can't not wear glasses or contact lenses to do things. I try but it's hard! ### JanSz — 2017-01-20T20:06:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-207155>

> [Emma Sabin said:](/goto/post?id=207152)
>
> ////

> Hi thanks for the tip. I was considering having the trifocal 3 way by Zeiss because I am -12 and -13 shortsighted and also starting to be long sighted so this would mean I wouldn't need any extra glasses. I'm presuming this is not the one you would mean? Do you know ofd any that let UV light in?

First priority (for me) is to be able to see.
The perks come latter.
Researching choices I came across;
1\. simple one, you will see, but need glasses to see near.
2.much better than #1 but most likely will not need glasses, but no guarantee, but may not be able to drive at night due to glare, again no guarantee either way.
First get it, then you will see what you got. (Real familiar topic, specially today, 20 January).
Above were official information in literature provided. Not hidden.
Possible problems highlighted. Make "better" choices, pay extra $$, and sign to acknowledge possible shortcomings.

Bad experience with elderly family member, (well about 3 years older than me, I am 77).
Super duper classy choice. Worked until it did not.
Insidious, person was not able to communicate (clearly enough) her problems.
Ended up under psychological care, likely going crazy, she thought.
Turned lenses, still waiting for next surgery, but at least not stressed and definitely sane.
Temporarily she wears glasses.
..

### Emma Sabin — 2017-04-02T20:25:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-211614>

Hi Caroline
So, to answer your qeustions first, I've no idea if the things I'm doing are helping. Tbh Jack said it could 18 months because I only have one eye open enough and without cataract to get light in. I've been using lenses since I was 12 years old so it's only been two months and without them being tested yet I can't tell. I am -12 and -13 so severely myopic and thats the bit Jack is most concerned about as that can lead to all sorts of issues, like retina problems (I've already had a tirn retina so very scared it could happen again). Jack advised doing face dunking, which I have been doing as this helps the inflammation. He said he doesn't know if I've left it too late ti undo the cataract but obviously i have to try all these things until its too late and I ahve to have the op done befire it gets to a point where its dangerous. I'm heading back to the UK in 10 days so will get it tested then and see whats what. If I have to have it done I'll have to go ahead and will try and find non UV blocking IOLs, but it's all ifs and buts right now. I'm hoping my non cataract eye will show a bit of improvement, which will mean the light is working and I will carry on doing what I'm doing. The other issues I have is that if I have one eye doen I will need the other one done too because I'm so myopic, so perfect vision in one eye with an IOL and -12 in the other is hard to live with. One sided glasses!

I should also tell you that I have many other issues (thyroid, inflammation, breast cancer 3 years ago) so I'm doing the CT to help that too. Yes I'm using the eye drops and taking some can-c tablets too. And advanced eye formula prescribed by my functional dr in the UK. I'm not sure I take in the nutrients though as i'm leptin resistent (another reason to do CT).

I also have blue blocking glasses and wear the orange ones at night too. I have a good contact in the UK if you need any.

I agree with you regarding computer and phone use - its definitely contributed to my eyes getting worse. I'm trying not to use my phone as much but its tricky. I don't use my blue blocking ones much in the day as I'm outside and don't want to block the UV on my eye either and can't be switching glasses every 5 minutes. I do for long stints at the computer.

The difficulty is knowing how much sun to get in my eyes too. Apparently it can cause cataracts so I am a bit scared of that! I am so confused by it all and feel worried I'm making things worse sometimes. However I know by not wearing my lenses and blocking bad blue light I am doing a ot of good so I am definitely doing something to help. Its just whether its enough....

I would be happy to chat if you want to. Not sure how we share numbers mind. Its all very scary and perhaps we can help each other emotionally at least

### Emma Sabin — 2017-04-18T20:46:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-212511>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=212505)
>
> If they say you don't need it yet what is the hurry?

That was the optician I saw in Ibiza. He measured my eyes and they came out at -13 and -25, which perplexed him. He reckons his machine would have known if it was a cataract and not read it as -25. He checked my retina and all was ok. He checked my cataract very briefly and has asked me to come back to see his opthamologist in May.

However, my opthamologist in London says its ready and said that last year, but he's very old school and laughed at me rudely when I mentioned not blocking UV light from my eyes and my concern that my health might deteriorate.
So I'm a little lost at knowing what to do. I don't want to wait until its too late to get it done, especially as I'm -13 in both eyes and have already had a retina tear. I'm seeing the above opthamologist again tomorrow to see if its any worse and also I want to see what the effect of wearing my glasses instead of lenses and sun gazing has done to my other eye. I'm also in contact with another opthamologist in the UK and even though she said theres no evidence of healing it naturally she does use non UV blocking IOLs and sounds more open minded than the other guy.

I just don't know how long I should leave it.

### Emma Sabin — 2017-04-19T19:51:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-212594>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=212525)
>
> You need another opinion on that then with that story.

Ok, I saw the original opthamologist again today and he said there is no change in my eyes and cataract is no worse than it was in September. He said I will need it operated on 100% and I just need to bite the bullet and get it done. After trying to ascertain exactly how soon he thinks I need it done I eventually got him to say on a scale of 1 to 10 (10 being the worse) it's a 4. I asked at what number does he consider the point to get it done as dangerous and he would not answer me. Just kept repeating that I have to bite the bullet and come to terms with the fact that I have it done. I also asked if I could have the surgery to take the cataract out with no replacement lens and he scoffed at me and said you need both operating on because you are myopic. I didn't bother asking about the possibility of non UV IOLs and I won't be seeing him again.

I will try and get a second opinion whilst in London if possible and if not I'll do it in Ibiza.

Any advice on how long I can leave it for would be greatly appreciated.

### Optimal Health is EZ — 2017-08-01T18:56:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-220638>

Emma,

The surgery decision is up to you. I've seen people with really bad cataracts like 2-3+ combined, PSC types, who say their vision is just fine. Others have trace cataracts and say life is awful. You're a high myope so kind of a win/lose: you're at greater risk for injury after the surgery, but you'll also be able to be corrected plano with an artificial lens and either not have to wear glasses to see in the distance, not have them for reading, or do one eye corrected at each focal length. That's all well and good.

The main story in my mind is the UV blocking ability of the lens. Dr. Kruse has mentioned before systemic diseases get worse years after Phaco IOLs are implanted but right after I see a lot of epiphora - excessive watering of the eye - as a red flag that the ability to use DC regeneration of sunlight is hampered. This is no trivial thing. I notice a lot of minor RPE disruptions beginning on scans after UV is blocked - the precursor sign of AMD. I'm looking into this for a couple friends/family members because ultimately, for many people, the surgery must be addressed. If anyone knows a good place to get a "custom lens" that allows some UV through please share.

### Optimal Health is EZ — 2018-04-28T08:02:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-240078>

When the medical - industrial complex starts talking about "genetics" and "diseases of aging" these are codes for "we have no idea." These concepts act like garbage bins where ideas can be casually tossed, derailing intricate discussion of cause and effect. Few concepts are less well understood than genetics and aging. If anyone says, "oh it's genetic" then you know the thought train is off the rails and smoldering on the side of the road.

The eye, like almost every biological system, is composed of mostly water. The lens of the eye in particular is a dense sack of mostly water. What clues does this give us about cataracts and the true cause? It's probably related to the water! Do aging and genetics play a role? Certainly, however like I said they are poorly understood and the interaction with water isn't even examined.

Water is an electrical generator. Water yields the main source of bioelectricity. Electricity controls genetics. A lowered "charge" or voltage at the biological level leads to cancer, neurological disease, and yes cataracts. At some point everyone succumbs to states of lowered voltage, ultimately passing on from this body entirely and surrendering the borrowed electricity back to the environment. That is, we age and die. But the manner of this transition is highly nuanced and our choices play a big role in how we age.

What does it mean that your grandmother lived in India and was diagnosed with cataracts at 80? I think you're purporting two ideas there: India gave her a sanctuary away from blue light and other problems with industrial civilization, and that because she was 80 the disease is attributable to aging alone. However, there are some issues to consider. First, nobody on this planet is isolated from our collective destructive insanity any longer. Geoengineering has gone on for 70 years, affecting everyone on the planet. The US and Russia detonated nuclear bombs in the magnetosphere in the 50s and 60s, affecting everyone on the planet. Manipulation of the electromagnetic spectrum with radar, radio and more began over 100 years ago. Check out some of the initial research into sailors being around antenna arrays in the 40s during the war - high incidence of PSC type cataracts. Cataracts have been continually linked to different aspects of electromagnetic radiation but nobody is paying attention.

And again, as Dr. Kruse always points out, the key introduction of AC generated electrical power began all the way back in 1893. Think about that timeline. If there's a correlation with AC power and human health that began over 100 years ago, how much of what we think of as "normal" health could possibly be valid? It's like every human is born impaled by a stick. Naturally we'd have shorter lifespans and increased pain and ailments from this. But if every human on the planet is suffering this way it'll be completely overlooked in the discussion on disease. Someone needs to come around and remove the stick and say here, isn't that better? Artificially derived electricity is that impalement.

( A brief aside: I'm not anti-electricity as a rule. The way electricity is generated is discordant, with violent breaking of magnetic fields between iron and copper. The downstream manipulation of that energy can't override this genesis of discord.)

So while your grandmother may not have had the challenges that we face today she definitely wasn't immune to the factors relating to human health that have been completely overlooked.

Keep in mind too that all vibration, or all frequency, has its higher or lower harmonics. Take the Schumann for example, it has n number of higher harmonic vibrations that are in harmonic proportion to the original. If we know that lower band microwave radiation causes cataracts, what does that tell us about the potential for harmonics? I see us transitioning to a time period of higher frequency use for technology, communication, etc. Basically, a shift from ELF kilometer long waves to now approaching 5G and millimeter sized waves. In the time of your grandmother there was both a decreased prevalence of EMF and tendency toward lower frequencies. This has been changing over the decades. If these mm waves are in harmonic proportion to known frequencies that cause disease, then there will be an increased incidence of diseases such as cataracts. Disease will scale with increase in frequency bands.

And obviously for human health the areas in concern relate to water. Water has its zones of harmonic proportion. To understand this just look at the work of Gerald Pollack and the specific interaction of water and light. He found a harmonic band of the electromagnetic spectrum that interacts with water to separate charge and turn water into liquid crystal. The harmonic of this can be seen as ultrasound, a lower frequency that also forms a liquid crystal but to a lesser extent than light. Discordant bands of frequency and their harmonics will interact with water to lower voltage and restrict oscillations to liquid crystal / amorphous water. Basically taking the oomph out of that electrical generator we rely on every nanosecond of every day. This adds up over the years. Your grandmother was living in a time when the assault on her biology was perpetrated by harmonically discordant _lower_ frequencies so perhaps she took longer to "age" and develop cataracts. This process has been quickened immensely with both increased prevalence of nnEMF and higher frequency EMF. Again: disease will scale with increase in frequency bands.

(A point for further discussion: as is the case of blue light, even a generative vibration can be distorted with less than optimal timing and pulsation or flicker. If someone is interested we can open the can of worms around LiFi and if this could be a good thing. Visible, perhaps infrared light, yes, good for EZ formation. Intense flicker of said infrared, no, bad for certain reasons?)

I harp on these concepts of aging and genetics because they need to die an immediate and painful death. It's a mechanism of pacification for people to say disease comes from ineluctable factors alone and you need to accept this fate. So many times I had to witness the ophthalmologist say oh, your glaucoma is genetic take these drops for the rest of your life...your macular degeneration is age related, there's nothing at all we, you, or anyone can do. These concepts need to be erased from our collective minds or we will make no progress. Start seeing the bigger picture and embrace the severity and scope of the problem on this planet. Not one person is immune and this has been the case for 100 years (and arguably for much, much longer). Every day that goes by we are increasingly assailed by unseen factors that are the true cause of what we call genetics and aging.

### Jman — 2018-09-20T22:29:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-251415>

Hello! I'm a new member and hope to be a resource in this department.

Currently, two of the most popular standard monofocal intraocular lenses (IOLs) on the market used by cataract surgeons are the Tecnis ZCB00 and the Alcon SN60WF. The ZCB00 is a clear lens and the SN60WF has a blue-light absorbing chromophore (yellow tint). You can Google the information about each lens as they are typically provided in a directions for use (DFU) PDF file (I provided them as cites below).

The measurement they provide for light transmittance (and UV blocking) is usually given as a 10% cutoff, so for instance the Alcon SN60WF transmits 10% of the light at a wavelength of 400 nm. This means there is very little transmission of light to the retina below 400 nm, and goes up in a logarithmic fashion above 400 nm. However, the Tecnis lens has a lower cutoff at 375-380 nm, so this clear lens would transmit some UV-A light.

Bausch & Lomb (B&L) lenses have an even lower cutoff, with the enVista MX60E having a 10% cutoff at 364 nm. The Akreos line of lenses (i.e. MI60) is also lower and may be closer to 350 nm. Others on this forum and even this thread have mentioned the Crystalens (the AT-45 model), which is an accommodating lens used by B&L -- this lens has a cut off of 350 nm. However, the Crystalens is a premium lens and would not be paid for by insurance. All of the other B&L lenses I mentioned are covered by insurance. So the B&L lenses would give better transmission of UV-A light from about 350-400 nm.

If you research the spectral transmission of the human crystalline lens you'll find a paper by Boettner and Wolter (1962) that shows the transmission of UV/Vis in a 4.5 year old compared to a 53 and 75 year old. You'll notice that the older one gets the less blue light is transmitted (and it appears less UV-A light) -- and perhaps this is due to the development of a cataract (typically yellow in color) to protect the retina from the blue lit world. There is also a small peak (~10% transmission) of transmittance at 320-330 nm, which is in the UV-A range that does not appear to change much with age. The IOLs that I've researched do not allow any light in this range.

I'd be curious to hear Jack's thoughts on these lenses, and if the UV-A light from about 350-400 nm is perhaps adequate, or if one really needs the small peak at 320-330 nm, since this is closer to the absorbance of aromatic amino acids at 280 nm.

-   Boettner and Wolter (1962). More recent spectral transmission of human crystalline lens by Artigas et. al (IOVS 2012).
-   **Alcon SN60WF**; UV(~400): 10% transmittance : [https://www.nuevocristalino.es/wp-content/uploads/sources/alcon/AcrySof-IQ-SN60WF.pdf](https://www.nuevocristalino.es/wp-content/uploads/sources/alcon/AcrySof-IQ-SN60WF.pdf)
-   **Technis ZCB00**; UV(375.4-380.4) 10% transmittance: [http://www.precisionlens.net/Websites/precisionlens/images/JJ ZCB00 DFU.pdf](http://www.precisionlens.net/Websites/precisionlens/images/JJ%20ZCB00%20DFU.pdf)
-   **Bauch & Lomb enVista MX60E**; UV(364): 10% transmittance: [http://www.bausch.com/Portals/77/-/m/BL/Global/dfu/4130300.pdf?ver=2017-08-14-111156-240](http://www.bausch.com/Portals/77/-/m/BL/Global/dfu/4130300.pdf?ver=2017-08-14-111156-240)
-   **Crystalens AT-45; UV(350)**: 10% transmittance: [http://www.bausch.com/Portals/77/-/m/BL/Global/dfu/4078404.pdf?ver=2016-05-07-214024-847](http://www.bausch.com/Portals/77/-/m/BL/Global/dfu/4078404.pdf?ver=2016-05-07-214024-847)
-   **Technis Symfony extended range IO**L; UV(~375): 10% transmittance: [http://www.precisionlens.net/mwg-in...d=2VWSriAT3x4ZQ8EP-1j645XqrjxWynA3zWpYTzKnhlo](http://www.precisionlens.net/mwg-internal/de5fs23hu73ds/progress?id=2VWSriAT3x4ZQ8EP-1j645XqrjxWynA3zWpYTzKnhlo),

### Emma Sabin — 2018-11-16T11:09:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-255741>

Good day to you all - wherever you are.

So, just got back from London and feeling a little disappointed tbh. The trip was exhausting (London is anyway) with lots of tests and chats with the two surgeons and the result of the two appts was exactly the same interestingly. I feel they both know what they're talking about (with relation to what they can do for my eyes) as both said the same and with the same amount of care and attention. It was just hard to hear. So a brief summary is the following:
1) Monofocal lens was advised by both surgeons. No discussion ensued really about any others as they both felt with my prescription etc they just wouldn't work and could cause complications.
2) Both highlighted the risk attached to having the surgery to someone with such high myopia and so (relatively) young. They both acknowledged that I will need the op in my right eye (with the cataract) at some point (and it has got worse since last year), but they both highlighted the complications attached to operating on my healthy left eye. They mentioned studies that showed a 9% chance of retinal detachment at any point in my life after the op.
3) In light of above they both discussed the options for the left eye instead of having the cataract op. They mentioned wearing a contact lens or an inserted contact lens. They said glasses wouldn't work with one eye. As you can imagine I have concerns about both these options with relation to blue light.

I am feeling even more concerned tbh, especially after having a chat with a friend whos dad had the monofocal lenses put in and his life is so much worse. Basically because his eyes can't adapt to the change in distances he's lost confidence in even walking so has become more disabled than with the cataract.

Both surgeons are coming back to me with the best IOLs they can find that will allow as much UVA in as possible.

Also I was staying away from high class specialists.
I was looking for a guy who does 50 or more surgeries few times a week, every week.

...
My eyes were done in this (high volume)(franchise).
3.3 miles from my home
And I have many ophthalmologists to choose from.

### JanSz — 2018-11-18T13:56:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-255887>

> [Emma Sabin said:](/goto/post?id=255883)
>
> Ignore my last post Jansz! I misunderstood you.
> So what would you do? Get a basic monofocal in my cataract eye and then wear a gas permeable lens in my non cataract eye...?

I was newer wearing contact lenses.
For the last about 40+ years I am checking my eyes once/year in the franchise, they changed names over years.
I am seeing ophthalmologist there having variety of tests done.
At one point they found
narrow angle glaucoma.
They send me to another franchise (Omni), there they drill holes using laser. Those holes are relieving any pressure buildup.
With time I got cataracts.
At first corrections were made via prescriptions.
When time came I had (simple) lens implanted first in one eye then the other.

........
Some people that I know would go to individual eye specialists. Some of them had rather serious disability afterward.
Statistically
I guess, problems are rare in both type of places.
I just was placing my bets as I described.
And I explained to you my reasons for those bets.

........

### Sue-UK — 2018-11-18T17:30:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-255906>

> [Emma Sabin said:](/goto/post?id=255899)
>
> I think I’ve pretty much decide not to have surgery on my healthy eye. Not for now anyway. I don’t think I have any choice regarding the one with cataract though. Do you?

I think it needs looking at in context with the whole eye. For example in Nathan T Oxenfeld's book he talks about in cataract surgery the ciliary muscles being severed. Also the cataract only being in one eye is interesting. Why not both? Why that eye and not the other? If its a defence in response to something, what happens if you take away the defence? Too many unanswered questions for me to risk something I can't undo once done, when the other eye is healthy. Also I haven't given up on clearing the cataract - or my retina - naturally.

I admire you Sue for not giving up like I am tempted to do!

### JanSz — 2018-11-18T22:27:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-255916>

[@Emma Sabin](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19472/)
From my personal experience.
My first cataract removal and lens replacement went away nicely.

Few years latter, same place, same doctor, I was offered use of a laser to remove cataract.
That cost me additional $1500.
I was told that this way was not available when I did the first surgery.
I did paid the $1500.
It was very well wort it.
I was able to see rather clearly the same night and all changes settled by day #2.
In retrospect
after first surgery it took about a week or couple days more for all to settle.

I still waited for over a month with getting new eye glasses.
The eyeglasses I use mostly for reading, but even for distance they make my vision slightly sharper.

...

### Alex97232 — 2018-11-19T00:37:07+0200

Too many unanswered questions for me to risk something I can't undo once done, when the other eye is healthy. Also I haven't given up on clearing the cataract - or my retina - naturally.

Hi Sue. Love your guts. Thank you for all your good ideas. What are you doing to clear your cataract/retina naturally?

### Sue-UK — 2018-11-19T11:42:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-255938>

> [Alex97232 said:](/goto/post?id=255925)
>
> What are you doing to clear your cataract/retina naturally?

I got the news the day before I went to Fuerteventura near the end of October, so I took the Hollwich book about cataracts with me to read. But because one eye does not have a cataract, I don't need to rush my decision. The surgeon doesn't want to operate on the detachment, he is so doubtful he would do anything other than take a look and close up again, and just remove the cataract. He won't take my money for the retina surgery as a private patient.

Since coming back about 2 weeks ago, I've had more blogs and books to look at and have gradually been making changes. Still a work in progress.

I've read that the lens is made up of extremely thin layers, and when focusing 15 feet or farther it thins and the layers come closer together. 15 feet or closer and the lens thickens and the layers spread apart. If we don't use our eyes properly, or wear glasses, the lens loses its flexibility and becomes more rigid, and accumulates crap between the layers. (Nathan Oxenfeld book). So overcoming my aversion to exercise has been one thing.  Doing a 2 mile vision walk every day now. (Scanning the horizon, looking at things at different distances etc, working the eye muscles naturally in natural light). I also do the exercises in the Nathan Oxenfeld book designed to avoid cataracts (along with others for avoiding other problems). I've adapted a few to do in cold dark and boring - one being "darking" instead of "sunning,"  another being a meditation version of palming the eyes, whilst flat on my back,( hoping that gravity assists . )Basically rub palms together to generate IR, place on the eyes and visualise the retina reattaching. 10 hours minimum of complete darkness at night. I think the rapid eye movement of REM sleep is important but I haven't delved into that yet.

Just for starters.

### Ski coach — 2018-11-19T22:34:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-256001>

Five years ago my eye doctor said I had the beginnings of a cataract. I believe he said I was a 1 on a scale of 0 to 5. Last year, after years of Kruseing with seafood and sunlight, Doctor said my eye was a "zero." My myopia is also somewhat improved. I'll be turning 65 in less than 3 months and I believe I can still improve my vision further even at my age.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-11-19T22:37:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-256004>

I just gave a talk 5 days ago in Mexico to a large audience of eye doctors and I used this video to explain how non linear effects of light help improve the balance of power in the retina and RPE of the eye to make it impossible to get a cataract or acute macular degeneration.........what was the predator in my example for my audience how to the improve the rivers of blood in the retina from the ophthalmic artery of the sick patients? It turns out that UV and IR light from AM sunlight is the "light predator" that the human eye must have to maintain its optimal health matrix. This juxtaposition stunned the eye doctors because all of them were taught to believe, as I was in med school, that UV light was a predator stimulus like the wolf was thought to be in Yellowstone. It turns out this linear thinking was dead wrong when it was examined closely when unleashed in nature. Moreover, when I showed them the effect of how a "hidden light predator" from AM light could 'tame" the retinal vessels and heal them because of the non-linear effects of UV and IR light our retina does not see on the fovea.......silence filled the room. When you know better, you can do better and think about your environment is a non-linear behavior I teach my tribe of misfits. UV and IR light act with a Warburg metabolism on the retina to create nitric oxide which sharpens camera vision improves the eye clock functioning and reduces the chance that cataracts form. They key is understanding where this transition occurs in your own life based on your light choices. No two eyes are the same.

### Alex97232 — 2018-11-20T00:19:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-256019>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=255938)
>
> I got the news the day before I went to Fuerteventura near the end of October, so I took the Hollwich book about cataracts with me to read. But because one eye does not have a cataract, I don't need to rush my decision. The surgeon doesn't want to operate on the detachment, he is so doubtful he would do anything other than take a look and close up again, and just remove the cataract. He won't take my money for the retina surgery as a private patient.
>
> Since coming back about 2 weeks ago, I've had more blogs and books to look at and have gradually been making changes. Still a work in progress.
>
> I've read that the lens is made up of extremely thin layers, and when focusing 15 feet or farther it thins and the layers come closer together. 15 feet or closer and the lens thickens and the layers spread apart. If we don't use our eyes properly, or wear glasses, the lens loses its flexibility and becomes more rigid, and accumulates crap between the layers. (Nathan Oxenfeld book). So overcoming my aversion to exercise has been one thing.  Doing a 2 mile vision walk every day now. (Scanning the horizon, looking at things at different distances etc, working the eye muscles naturally in natural light). I also do the exercises in the Nathan Oxenfeld book designed to avoid cataracts (along with others for avoiding other problems). I've adapted a few to do in cold dark and boring - one being "darking" instead of "sunning,"  another being a meditation version of palming the eyes, whilst flat on my back,( hoping that gravity assists . )Basically rub palms together to generate IR, place on the eyes and visualise the retina reattaching. 10 hours minimum of complete darkness at night. I think the rapid eye movement of REM sleep is important but I haven't delved into that yet.
>
> Just for starters.

Sue, thank you thank you for sharing/teaching.

### Emma Sabin — 2020-07-19T09:35:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-291058>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=291040)
>
> Hi Emma ....so glad to hear from you and that you are doing well.
>
> What kind of IOL did you decide on? both eyes done?
>
> Which surgeon did you choose?

I'll attach the IOL details for you. Had to go for both eyes in the end as my brain could not cope with one eye at -13 and one normal. It was a big decision but one I feel happy with. My surgeon was amazing - Javier Fernandez (trained all around the world works mainly in Barcelona and luckily for me lives in Ibiza). My heart chose him as it felt right. He did not laugh at me when I talked about Jack's theories and was the most interested opthamologist I'd spoken to. He was very patient as I asked question after question about the lenses and what my options were. After much deliberation I chose to go for it as my quality of life was not good and I had tried everything to reverse it. I forget I had it done and have to remind myself and be thankful that I can see so well. I get a little bit nervous when thinking about the future but try not to. Lets face it anything could happen to any part of my body!

### Emma Sabin — 2020-07-19T09:41:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cataract-surgery-decision.18588/post-291060>

I also chatted to a guy called Jay in the States who follows Jack's work too (Nathan Waltz put me in touch with him) He wrote this ....

I have reviewed you notes and am very familiar with the Tecnis Monofocal IOL that you’re surgeon is planning to use. Obviously you are significantly visually impaired and the cataract surgery from that standpoint is medically necessary. I am assuming that you have studied Jack Kruse’s perspective on the UV blocking effect of all modern IOL’s. Unfortunately we do not have an IOL that simulates the light transmissibility of the natural crystalline lens, although there is an effort to persuade the IOL companies to consider that. I do think from what we have to offer the Tecnis is a “good” IOL.
Another thing to be aware of is that as a high myope your risk of retinal detachment after cataract surgery is higher than the average population. Of course RD’s can be surgically treated, that is something you should know. I don’t think we fully understand how much Retinal detachments and the surgery further affect the eye’s ability to assimilate light. If you proceed with the Tecnis IOL I do think you can get some UV transmission through the sclera depending on your latitude and time of year as long as the underlying retina is functional, of course this takes off axis “sun gazing” of which Dr. Kruse has written about. My understanding of the “sun gazing” is that you never want to look directly at the sun except at sunrise and sunset when it’s ok. I think Jack advises looking 30 degrees off axis or more depending on your light sensitivity. Again I do believe the retinal photoreceptors can receive solar radiation directly through the sclera bypassing the cataract and subsequent IOL.

I am working with U.S. IOL companies to try to develop a more spectrally appropriate IOL. I have several design options but have not received much interest from the U.S. companies since the money for them is in the refractive implants and that’s really what the population cares about due to lack of education about the important of sunlight to optimize health. I am giving a lecture in November to a group of 40 eye docs attempting to gain more grassroots support from the physician community and then lobby the companies to get us more options for patients who desire this type of IOL. I think it’s a long road but one worth traveling.

I think it’s a long road but one worth traveling.

Hi Emma,
I have really appreciated reading your posts. My partner, Nick is dealing with a cataract in one of his eyes and is finding himself closer now to having to act. A eye exam a couple of weeks ago reported that it is now 'ripe'. We will have to work within the NHS, I guess as funds are limited, but it is great to have your IOL suggestion to work with. He has not committed to anything yet.
The pressures in his eyes were shown to be rather high in that examination, and has to be the priority right now. He has no symptoms of glaucoma at all, weirdly, and this was an utter confusion for the eye doctors at the hospital. Nick has ramped up all his JK protocols and we bought an EMR Tek red light a couple of weeks ago to add more red light into the mix. Before any mainstream intervention we are working with a UK naturopath that has studied JKs work to see what impact we can have.

# Thread: Physician burnout and quantum biology
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physician-burnout-and-quantum-biology.18593/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-15T18:47:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/physician-burnout-and-quantum-biology.18593/post-202852>

What happpens when you have no good options for people getting sick at record rates and they begin to die at high rates from things 20 years ago would not have killed them? Doctors are retiring early because they are beginning to sense some bad is afoot and they just cannot put their finger on what it is. One of the more cool things about doctoring is that we observe and have experienced many lives situations. We get to see and learn from them. Many of those on my website, including several you here have shared with me- full rich lives from birth to death. In my day job, you just have to have these rest periods in between to reflect and keep your sanity. When you're constantly around dying man and you feel powerless to save the species you check out early. That is what this story is really about linked below. It is the story that is developing between the words and lines printed. As a modern physician you have two choices; you can check out early or do something about the current situation. Find the source of the real problem in health care and share it with the world and see what they do with it. Some people propser and some decline with these new perspectives because they just cannot fathom the power of sunlight and the power of man made light to destroy cells. Quantum biology has renewed me for a decade and make me enthusiastic about the future of change in health care. Those who get the message I will burn for. Those who piss in the wind will have their toll from Mother Natrue to deal with and they will have to pay co-pays to the burning out docs remaining doing what they are taught to do. My hope is as more physicians burnout they realize that their education and insight can still be refurbished. My job is to save the history of our our profession to help our species; if fellow docs along the way pay attention then I feel I've done my part. The more docs who are infected the more people can be helped. In my opinion, burnout is a positive. It is nature's way of telling you, you've been going through the motions your soul has departed; you're a zombie, a member of the walking dead, a sleepwalker. False optimism is like administrating stimulants to an exhausted nervous system. If you realize this, you have time to change the way you think about biology and you'll be renewed and rekindle your passion. [https://healthecareers.com/article/...tm\_medium=email&utm\_campaign=physician-101516](https://healthecareers.com/article/healthcare-news/physicians-plan-to-retire-early?respmid=101516_Physician&resprid=61472094&utm_source=jmn&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=physician-101516)

# Thread: Brandon's Journal!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brandons-journal.18596/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Supensaa — 2016-10-15T19:59:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brandons-journal.18596/post-202861>

Things I've done so far:

Wearing Uvex blue blockers around artificial light including computer usage, along with using software called F.lux to further reduce blue light exposure. Also have F.lux on my phone.

Taking only cold showers twice a day (I use minimal soap, and when I do it is just simple saponified oils as soap. So no sulfates or other unnecessary surfectants. \[using Dr. Bronners Castile Soap\])

Taking ice baths by letting my freezer store up as much ice as it can, and then transferring it to a bowl and moving it to the other side of the freezer. I can take an ice bath about once a day given the rate at which ice is made in my freezer.

Grounding outside and getting fullbody sunlight. Well not fullbody, I'm in short shorts, haha. I live across from an apartment complex in a town home duplex.

FUN FACT: Someone called the cops on me because they thought I was stripping down into my underwear outside! I guess it must have looked weird that I went out side to my usual piece of grass across the street fully dressed, then started taking my clothes off, leaving rolled up athletic shorts the only thing on my body!

I'm eating a high fat, moderate protein, little to no carb diet. Standard epi-paleo fare, although my diet is not completely adhered to the protocol at all times due to money restraints, it has definitely gotten better! I have removed brown rice and beans from my diet, and am eaty leafy greens with saturated fat usually in the form of coconut oil.

I also visit this local Asian Buffet that has some good raw oysters and raw fish. Went there last night! Had a good amount of raw oyster, crab meat, raw sliced tuna, and seaweed! I go about once every 15 days on average, I will be increasing the frequency of this.

The only DHA I might possibly be getting outside of this trip to the buffet is: sardines, tuna, oyster, BUT they're all canned. I know fresh is better, but it will have to do until I increase my earning power.

Fermented foods: I have a few Pickl-It Jars, with the gasket and airlock, and I make fermented beat juice regularly, otherwise know nas beat kvass.

I also meditate twice daily, but I find that I tend to doze off if I meditate before I get early morning sunrise rays on my eyes. So for morning meditation I like to do it after I expose my eyes to the sun! I would like to wake up earlier than the sun, but I want to be able to wake myself with its light. I know that there are full spectrum bulbs or something of that nature, but I will have to look more into it. Not sure I want to make a habit of a non-sun light source setting my circadian rhythm.

FASTING: I was eating once a day for about two weeks, and I definitely felt great, during AND after. Now I am eating twice a day sometimes, but often eat once a day for a 5 days or so periodically. I just have to make sure that it is a meal high in fat, with good proteins and sulfurous veggies, etc. etc. (epi-paleo fare) I never knew that fasting would help so much!

At around 10:30 my body definitely tells me that I need to sleep. I end up waking between 6-7 AM if I fall asleep at this time. Sometimes I will go to bed earlier just to wake up earlier and start the day that way.

THINGS I NEED TO DO:

Upgrade my water source (using carbon filtered water from the sink, occasionally I drink a bottled water or a Pellegrino)

Change my lighting environment: I want to get UV bulbs and red lights inside of my room, maybe see if I can utilize a type of light source that can wake me up like the sun does as well? Since it's winter soon, it's already starting to get cloudy! I won't be able to get as much sun or UV, I really don't want my UV and overall natural light intake to decrease. (I do realize, however, that DHA + GOOD WATER is necessary for taking advantage of the benefits of sunlight)

A Magnetico and Quantlet are also on my list

23andMe is also something I look forward to doing to further tailor what my body needs and can benefit from.

P.S. I got a job! And....I can wear my blue blockers at work, haha. I work at a Japanese restaurant in the kitchen area. Sashimi, anyone?

There's definitely more I can say, but I'll save it for the next post.

My cognitive function, and focus has improved along with energy levels. My sebborheic eczema has not reoccurred for a while now, but my extremities still get cold occasionally. Things have been getting better!

Until then, school, work, health habits, and ecommerce (been studying and attempting to import goods from different countries, etc. on different sales channels. Most of my money for the past year has gone to trying to alleviate my health symptoms and getting a car! Now I can roll all of my money into ecommerce! I want to be able to move out on my own and afford the things necessary to increase my resilience and cognitive function.)

### Supensaa — 2016-10-19T20:10:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brandons-journal.18596/post-203061>

Yesterday I CT'd and I felt a strong tingling sensation all over my body that was just a surface level tingling, but it went deeper than that. Also, when I got up at out of the tub I was a bit uncoordinated due to the shivering. I unintentionally did a whole day fast the day before, and that morning of the day I did the CT, I consumed a meal high in fat. That was the only meal I had, and I did CT around 8:00PM later that day.

OH! And I've gotten a foot cramp once while doing CT! and my foot was out of the tub. Also for the past 2 days I have gotten a foot all the way to my calf cramp. 2 days ago it was on my right leg, last night it was on my left leg.

Does anyone know anything about CT and muscle cramps/spasms?

Is it because I'm not eating enough? Or lacking in some type of food? I normally eat once a day, just one big meal, because I'm out and about at school or work or sometimes both in one day. By the time I get home, it's already too close to bedtime to eat again.

Maybe I should get an insulated lunch box to be able to eat a meal when not at home?

Meditation: it's a lot easier to keep my focus and keep my mind from wandering/day dreaming after my eyes get that morning sun. Since I meditate the first thing in the morning on an empty stomach (water only) before the sun rises, when my mind wanders it is not uncommon for me to slip into what feels like the beginning of a dream! I keep my room pitch black while I do it, with a small red light. I sit on the edge of a bunch, with my spine erect and in line with my neck (by tucking my chin in).

When I go outside to meditate after the sun has come up, plus the fresh air, I feel more alert and my mind wanders a whole lot less, and if it does wander it isn't a daydream, just a passing thought. Long story short, I feel more aware now of how much more alert I am after I receive the sun's morning rays on my eyes!

### caroline — 2016-10-20T08:03:30+0300

There is a big C/T thread - check there.....

An insulated lunch box is a great idea. We do that all the time. We go early to the beach/ocean and take breakfast with us.

We take a thermos of bullet proof coffee and water....and sit on the rocks and munch away while watching the ocean.

### Supensaa — 2016-10-25T19:34:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brandons-journal.18596/post-203304>

Yesterday during my anthropology class we were discussing forensic anthropology. During the discussion about different types of traumas: ballistic, blunt, sharp... and during the slides about how disease can leave physical markings on skeletal remains I began to feel gradually light headed. As I began to walk out of the class room I began to tingle all over my body, become uncoordinated, lost most of my hearing, and began to get tunnel vision. I was blacking out. I lied down on the floor in the hallway on my back, and I gradually regained my senses over the next few minutes.

EDIT: I also did not eat at all that day, and it was around 6:45 PM, maybe that had something to do with it?

I remember having to leave the classroom during health class when we were shown a video of liposuction. I got light headed, but I didn't black out as bad as I did this time.

A year later I would black out again, but this time I would first get hives all over my body, then I would pass out completely for a few minutes. What was bad about that one, was that I lost vision while being conscious, then I passed out. For some reason my hearing was intact that time. But prior to the hives I had ran 3 miles and I think I was dehydrated! I suspect that was a contributor to this.

Oh and once a few years ago I read an article about circumcision and I felt a little sick and had to lie down! Yeah, seems a bit funny, I know

Anyways, I came back to class, saw even more morbid photos, but I didn't feel light headed from them. Didn't really enjoy seeing them, but there was no adverse reaction....so....what gives?

I'm glad I stayed in class and didn't leave because these photos showed rotting flesh and bloating, etc. The other ones were just skeletal remains of trauma and disease markers left on skeletal remains. I was curious as to whether or not I would get light headed again, but nope, I didn't.

WHY? Why did this happen?

It always has interested me that during the healing process, when one begins to adopt healthy habits, it seems like the disease process reverses. Or at least, old problems resurface so you can once again face them because last time you failed to. I know I'm reading a lot into this, but this certainly is a curiosity.

Lately I have been getting less light exposure due to work + school + cloudy weather due to winter. I really need to get some UV lights in my bedroom, and replace my lamp's bulb with a red bulb. I just started my new job a little over a week ago, and the only things I have to pay for are gas, car insurance, and school expenses.

I really need to flesh out any emotional baggage or weird mind shit that is hiding within me! I've been looking at bioenergetics for emotional release and other methods. I've always had the tendency to suppress emotion, but lately that has been a lot better.

I have backslid a bit these past two days. I wish I could alter my environment to where I remove all bad options such as unfavorable foods. It's usually not a problem for me unless I let stress build up, when this happens I am more prone to impulsiveness and willpower failures. I need to take breaks and destress throughout the day, and also try to make the breaks themselves a reward, make going outside the reward. (I am reminded of books such as: The Willpower Instinct, by Kelly McGonigal, and The Power of Habit, by Charles Duhigg)

Of course, good health is one of the contributors to a good psyche. It's all connected. Doing good in some area will positively affect another area.

All of this sounds like bad news, but I don't feel bad at all! I don't feel sorry for myself, I am thankful to be aware of this and if anything I am fascinated by what has happened as of late, and how my awareness has allowed my to observe it.

The last thing I want to do is give up. That'd be really boring, and not fun at all!

Have a good day everyone! Kick some ass!

### Supensaa — 2017-03-01T07:30:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brandons-journal.18596/post-209464>

I ordered a Quantlet Feb 2nd, should get it in a month or so. Excited!

I'm also seriously considering getting an infrared sauna. Looking at this model: [http://infraredsauna.com/sanctuary-1-1-person-cedar-full-spectrum-sauna/](http://infraredsauna.com/sanctuary-1-1-person-cedar-full-spectrum-sauna/)

It's a big purchase, but it seems like the ClearLights are the best choice.

Does anyone have any criticisms or comments on infrared saunas or on this model or brand specifically? Thank you!

Also, I'm planning on getting a magnetico as well, looks like they have a 6 month payment plan. However, I have been trolling craigslist as well, and will ask around to see if anyone else is selling theirs.

### Supensaa — 2017-05-21T03:50:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brandons-journal.18596/post-214359>

I purchased a full spectrum clearlight sauna, and am happy with it! I haven't used it with niacin yet, but plan on doing so. Starting to feel my extremities warm up during different times of the day rather intensely, but pleasantly (only about 5% of the time or less will my extremities be cold as they were a long time ago, I feel like the sauana has helped with that a bunch).

I also got a magnetic 20gauss a few days ago, only slept on it twice. The one thing I can notice is that I have been falling asleep faster, and I don't remember my dreams as well as I did before, it's just a deep sleep, and I can only vaguely remember my dreams, though usually not at all.

Overall my ability to perform has increased, I am planning on doing a niacin protocol with the sauna. I continue to eat mixed veggies, with coconut oil 1-2 a day, with sardines every other day.

Can't wait to try out the Quantlet. I've been blue blocking for almost a year now, and am also considering looking for a pair that is more comfortable! Recently I have started to use double sided tape with foam padding (from those foam pain brushes you can get at the dollar store) in order to pad the kind of pointy corners of the UVEX blue blockers (the scientific goggle type, the other version, the skypers, never really fit me too well).

Things to do in the near future:

\-Water filter on shower head perhaps? (I take purely cold showers now)
\-Get DNA sequenced
\-Niacin protocol w/sauna
\-Heart rate variability training - been considering this
\-Industrial ice maker + feed tub to do CT in
\-Gain a better understanding of SUPPLEMENTS, what forms to of what supplements to use, the benefits of super dosing certain types of supplements, etc. I'm pretty ignorant with supplements. I take lugols iodine with food, along with pink Himalayan salt. What spurred my interest was the NIACIN protocol. Vitamin B is very low in those who have seborheic dermatitis, this is why it is commonly developed in alcholics, as alcohol depletes vitamin B. So I've been wondering about what would happen if I begin to titrate up dosages of vitamin B along with sauna usage. Similarly, what other protocols exist? I'm really just looking at the cost/benefit of it. Seems like it's worth a shot.

Can't believe that it will be a year this July since I've joined the forums and started this journey.

At one point I used to have rashes all of my face in 2015, due to intense stress, and other factors I'm sure. I haven't had those symptoms to that degree in quite some time now. There are occasional small recurrences once every 3-4 weeks as I adopt new protocols while keeping my foundations strong (not eating before bed, blocking light sources at night, healthy fats, fasting, etc.) But overall I can feel a constant improvement.

As I am healthier, I feel that the same emotional feelings from before this health downfall have resurfaced. I am reminded of how when we heal many have said that they feel like they are going backwards in time in terms of their pathology and emotions.

Because of this I would really like to start taking a Sympathetic nervous system approach, looking at neurofeedback perhaps, heart rate variability, and I've READ about someone speaking of the potential benefits of psychedelics, and of course that you can legally take these substances under supervision in certain countries of course....

I'm looking for more implementations, only now am I starting to feel some of these chronic things being lifted.

I really want to learn how to reset my nervous system and put it into a relaxed state.

Also, seeing as how the skin rashes I used to get were supposed to be fungal in nature from what I've read (seborrheic dermatitis, otherwise known as WALL STREET dermatitis due to wall streeters getting cases of this rash when the great depression occurred! Talk about stress!), I'm also looking for implementations to completely rid myself of that too. Niacin + sauna seem to be a good bet, of course all the other implementations I'm performing being synergistic, too.

Well, that's about it for now! I'll most likely post in a month or so, or perhaps a few months after I've completed the niacin protocol, and have gained more anecdote from the use of the Magnetico.

Any new implementations or things to look into are very welcome, I am super interested in the benefits of certain supplements/compounds found in food. Maybe I should start spending some of my grocery money at the local farmer's market for vegetables as well. I'm also familiar with NAC, glutathione, and Ubiquinol + PQQ.

I just want to know what I can take to get a benefit, and WHEN it is a diminishing return is all. And of course, what form to take it in. I know that Dr. Kruse has said that biologic package of nutrients are usually the best, but I still don't know if that applies to everything or only some things, etc. Another example is that of intravenous nutrients, even seeing interviews by Asprey interviewing doctors who do intravenous vitamin C. Asprey also has mentioned OZONE therapy before, and has stated that it has helped him much. That's another thing I'm wondering about as I don't know much about it.

Anyways, I'm off for now. Will post again in a few months. Thanks everyone. AND - any suggestions?

We cannot live only for ourselves. A thousand fibers connect us with nature in ways we do not perceive. This leads to collateral damage and collateral benefits as this video shows from 21:00 on. Everything is quantum connected. The essence of the quantum Zeno effect is that invisible threads are the strongest ties to nature's innovation. Deep down, nature is inherently peaceful, calm, and beautiful. So are we. The universe as a whole is perfect. The chaos, we call observable life, is on the surfaces of things. What have I learned about the universe when I decided to first question the dogma I was taught in biology? All truth is quantum. It is beyond the concepts of space and time, we think we know. Why do I believe this? Relativity of all things is axiomatic, but light is not relative. It has a speed limit. Light only stamped this idea in my mind once I understood why light had to be different for all living things.
