### Michalis — 2018-01-15T06:51:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-232581>

In Thailand absorbing the sun. I got plenty of time so I read Jacks' book couple of times. Reading the blogs and watching the podcasts with him on. Small steps to try understand what is going on with me. In parallel i understand how the medical establismesment is deeply flawed.Having relieved of my savings to pay for my mums spinal osteoporotic fracture and my knee arthritis ordeal is only validating Jack's point along with the others who talk about it like Becker and Marino.I try to follow his advise to embrace my discomfort as an agent of change. This is the lesson I am trying to internalize and believe me it is a really a hard pill to swallow as I feel I am breaking down completely.I wake up I the morning and feel 100 years old with a mind like a flat line.
The sun feel good for me over here but a month won't cut it. I will need a hell lot of sun and CT to see some change. Everything that I read points to that direction so yes the data is there. The most radical change I need to do is move from London and that means I should also find a way to make ends meet doing manual work which I am not afraid of, what I need to make sure is see if I am able to do it with all the aches I got. Jack posted a something the other day on a thread about nihilism that was really inspiring and I keep going back to it for inspiration. It is really great to understand that Einsteins' equation works both ways and that actually everything is possible but it needs consistency and discipline to make like a sphinx and moreover stop listening the others who haven't got the data. Especially those who keep telling me to put my tinfoil hat on. It is a very lonely journey and tough going against the grain however I am grateful about this forum as it us a beacon of knowledge on this journey.

### Michalis — 2018-01-22T05:36:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-233104>

Observations from Thailand.
The European elderly tourists are quite fat and run down if you compare them with the Thai of the same age group. The difference? Thai people live an outdoor life and they constantly are under uv light and grounded.
However: The amount of WiFi and nnemf is vast here too. It is easier to spot the celltowers and the power and telecom cables are really wild over the streets.
I had a conversation with a Canadian teacher who was hosting me and living here for 15 years. She has noticed that obesity has started appearing on the sly.. Not like the west obviously. I have noticed a few obese people but not many. One common theme like everywhere is seeing people clutching their mobile phones constantly.

Personally I am under the sun a lot here. I go to the beach , ground, sleep a lot and early and eat carbs as obviously they grow naturally here. I haven't put any weight on , I might have actually lost weight as I have noticed the fluctuation.

But again it is really striking to see the state of all these elder European in comparison with the Thai. What the indoor blue lit world and lack of natural exposure is doing to us. Sometimes one has to see or experience to believe.

I haven't done any CT here but coupled with strong sunlight must be a match made in heaven.

I did a basic blood test and all came up great except HDL which was slightly low at 53. So more sunlight for me. Even my Bun/creatinine ratio dropped to 13. In London it had reached 29. So something good us happening. My blood glucose was relatively low too at 87.

I am curious to retest but in depth. Possibly before I leave.

my 23 and me is currently getting analysed so I am curious to see what that will show in terms for snp and such.

Generally the sun is doing a lot of good to me. My aches are still there obviously so work in progress.

### Michalis — 2018-02-02T22:35:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-234012>

Back to London. Holiday in the sun-mission accomplished. I loved the sunshine and i got dark really fast. I was seriously jet lagged and i slept a lot the last 2 nights... like 12 hours each. I feel super weird being back and not motivated at all with the Great British weather.

It is really cold here and i feel my cold adaptation has gone down after Thailand... i had a cold shower without problem as soon as i arrived but i feel cold inside the house now! That was not happening before.. I guess i got unused to the cold very fast..

It took me a while to wind down in Thailand but eventually it happened.. I had a lot to think and at some point i had a breakthrough through the clutter in my head.So for at least a week my brain stopped and let go and just absorbed the sun. I don't know if it was my mental state or eventually my dopamine levels rose or both... I guess these two are dancing together.. It was a Jack Kruse holiday really.. Lots sun, grounding reading blogs and podcast watching..

I don't really think that Thailand is optimal though unless you go somewhere with really really low population density.. I think they have got a massive nnemf issue from my observation of cell towers, Wifi Repeaters on the street everywhere, power lines and this crazy mess of telecom lines hanging. And 4G Everywhere! those who have visited SE Asia probably know the kind of cable mess i am talking about. I think it is possible to find places to live over there at least temporary , but it has to be very rural , or in remote islands.

For the first time in my life i got a smartphone in Thailand and used it over there..I wasn't very confident going about with 2 bad knees so i wanted to be able to have a map and info for my surroundings always. All i can say is i find it extremely disruptive as wanted to keep looking at it.. Eventually i was going to the beach and leaving it at the hotel.. Now i am back i have already DE-commissioned it. Enough!

Food-wise there is a lot of amazing seafood there... and really cheap oysters. When spotted i was always having them.. super cheap and already cleaned ,put into a container and it was 50 to 100 Baht for at least 20.

The ugly side of the food is MSG... it is ubiquitous there... I visited supermarkets and it seems like a staple... not good.. Also sugar.. The concept of not using sugar fleets them, which i guess makes sense because of the high quantum yield environment.
Fruit is great though.. Since the sunshine and UV was quite strong i seized the opportunity to sample the fruit and it was just great..And the greatest thing of all, i didn't put any weight on... Which for an x-obese person is great

The craziest thing of all is seeing all these products that they use over there to whiten their skin , for cultural reasons obviously. I made a Thai friend there and jokingly i was called Burmese and Khmer too, as i got really dark (They got a lot of Burmese and Cambodians working outside in construction, so they are exposed to the sun all the time and get really dark) . It is a class issue.. How people twist things...

Bottom line, it was a good holiday, i got a lot of sunshine but i need a hell of a lot more to make a difference.

### Michalis — 2018-02-28T00:46:39+0200

I have completely fallen off the EpiPaleo wagon the last couple of weeks, negating my last post... I am still wearing my blue blockers in the evening though.
Not much of London was needed to reverse the optimism and the chemical balance in my head.Got a new IT contract and i am back to work. I am not feeling great.

On another note I got my 23andme test results and i think it confirms i have to go south, not worst, just south

My maternal Haplotype is **U1a1** (Paternal _J_\-_L26_)

The U1a1 haplogroup (Found in Caucasus , Middle East, Greece, South Italy, Balkans, West India)

**Methylation Profile**

VDR Bsm rs1544410 CC -/-
**VDR Taq rs731236 AA +/+
MTHFR A1298C rs1801131 GG +/+
MTR A2756G** rs1805087 AG **+/-
MTRR A66G** rs1801394 AG **+/-
MTRR K350A** rs162036 AG **+/-**

**CYP1B1 L432V** rs1056836 GG +/+
**CYP2C19\*17** rs12248560 CT +/-
**GSTP1 I105V** rs1695 AG +/-
**GSTP1 A114V** rs1138272 CT +/-

But again i am not sure of the interplay of all these things along with my environment as well.. I will keep researching. If i have to take into account what i have been reading in my Promethease report i will just go mad.. There is so many conflicting genes and information in the genome... It is amazing how the environment can influence this blueprint and tip the scales one way r another..

### Michalis — 2018-03-05T21:07:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-236355>

No i didn't have to pay anything extra. The kit actually arrives from the Netherlands and goes back to Netherlands then DHL forwards it to the States. They give you a tracking number too.. it is just takes a long time.

Once the kit is analyzed you can request your raw data.. You can then upload them to Promethease and can be analysed for free or a fiver... i didnt pay more than a fiver.. You can get your SNPs and detox profile at Genetic Genie for free or donation.

Then , let the games begin.. haha

### Michalis — 2018-03-05T21:38:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-236358>

So today i went to see the doctor in the hospital for my knees. Both knees.. The appointment was booked 6 months ago.. You gotta love NHS...

So I was told i need a partial knee replacement for my left knee.... F\*\*\* me sideways ..

For the right knee, that i injured after falling off my bicycle now, I know i have a partially torn ACL (had mri privately because was freaking out) .I didn't tell the doctor because i wanted to see if he is keen to investigate .. I told him all the problems i got with it and the symptoms and that i feel something feels that is torn in there... He told me not to worry as it is probably some scar tissue... I asked him about stem cells and i heard the good old no evidence talk. So i thanked him and left.. The system is super flawed and some doctors follow the script and don't deviate ..

I have spoken to a Regenexx provider in Belgium and he has actually seen both of my knee MRI and said he might be able to help. Still looking for info on how deuterium ties with the whole thing.. i haven't made the connection yet. I know sunshine helps depleting deuterium from what i hear in the podcasts and definitely grounding helps for the currents of regeneration to occur in the bone... But how this is tying altogether i still got no clue... I am sure that having a stem cell treatment and being in the emf hell hole i live is not going to let the stem cells proliferate and do the job.. I need to make a boat load of money to have the treatment and fly straight to some place south. And my new job pisses me off so much already... But that's another story

Guys and galls is there any specific podcasts i need to get from patreon webinars to help connect the dots? pretty tight on the budget right now but can do one or 2 one offs...

### Antonis — 2018-03-06T05:52:00+0200

> [Michalis said:](/goto/post?id=236355)

> No i didn't have to pay anything extra. The kit actually arrives from the Netherlands and goes back to Netherlands then DHL forwards it to the States. They give you a tracking number too.. it is just takes a long time.
>
> Once the kit is analyzed you can request your raw data.. You can then upload them to Promethease and can be analysed for free or a fiver... i didnt pay more than a fiver.. You can get your SNPs and detox profile at Genetic Genie for free or donation.
>
> Then , let the games begin.. haha

### Michalis — 2018-03-25T23:39:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-237952>

So i left my work contract and jumped in into a new one... The previous was doing my head as i was getting micromanaged... I don't have patience for these kind of shenanigans as my stress levels skyrocket and i want to go ballistic sometimes..

I have ended up in the heart of the City of London again.. I landed on a great IT environment to learn new systems but extremely unhealthy.. i can feel the bloody WiFi..by the end of the day i feel like a roasted chicken. "nnemf alters collagen and alters water" i read on the forum/blog so i think this is where my connective tissue fails. Why all my joints have started clicking out of the blue.. I get the wifi in the office and the rest of the avalanche in server rooms..

I am now seriously thinking to save up as much until August that my contract runs out. Then sell all my stuff and go somewhere sunny for as much i can afford it.. Haven't got much stuff really, bits and pieces as i say less possessions - less obsessions.

But i feel i haven't got much time left and I just don't want this to be a dice roll. I am sure i got dopamine issues due to non sun exposure and i have been getting into a really dark place inside me sometimes... I feel my body completely crippled and things break constantly..Literally... I just don't know if it worths the hustle to stay here and try to fix my knees.

I have seen how my mum has ended up and how her body has been failing and knowing that i carry her mtDNA terrifies me... If she has gotten all these problems in Crete which is pretty sunny the future is bleak for me and always thinking with the environment/epigenetics in mind. I have to be at least at the tropics from what i read here. I am coming to the conclusion U1a1 haplotype along with a homozygous VDR taq snp calls for the tropics.

Even if I like computers i find myself more and more stuck in front of a bloody monitor at work and now confined at home as my body is failing.Cant do much, even in Thailand i struggled a lot.. This is not life, this is literally non-life. I have to concentrate on the short term and fix myself and then maybe look into the long term if i make it to the long term of course.

I am super perplexed, i just don't want to make a silly move while my body is failing. I don't care about making money i care about day to day function and survival as this is in stake now but money is needed to get that plane and go and also have a backup plan too in case things don't work out.

### Michalis — 2018-04-24T16:15:14+0300

_(moving this here as i think it is better to keep everything in one thread)_
I got some labs done and still waiting for my HS-CRP though.
It seems that i got low DHEA (bad environment , no sun) high o6/o3 ratio which seems pretty bad and high SHBG. Also very low Folate! My waking up cortisol seems trashed too as it is borderline low.
Light and EMF issues for sure there.

I am currently reading the forum and blogs correlating things but i would like some help as i could be missing things. As i have written on my journal i work in IT in London.. No sun. No exercise because of injuries and currently very low moods as i cant move very much. Joints failing constantly.

No CT or big BAB at the moment. Motivation trashed too and i feel like Syshyphus..

My previous work contract got royaly screwed by these political animals i was working with and my plan to have stem cell treatment and leave for a sunny place for at least some time went down the drain. Logistics and funds screwed.

Calcium test will give you approximate number of days until you get heart attack or stroke and need stents or are put on machines like my next door neighbor.
He is on machine for 5th year, he lives, but does not know about it.
Whatever else that test will tell you depends on IQ, but is that much less important.

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) still have not came clean about seafood.
I obviously buy into the DHA, but seafood come with a boatload of EPA.
All seafood, except oysters.
Note that Jack have a special place for oysters.
As far as I can tell, oysters is the only food in seafood category that have more DHA then EPA.
Live oysters are very expensive. Buy canned Korean oysters are very inexpensive.

### Michalis — 2018-08-16T14:15:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-248589>

It is really hard to stomach all this protein every morning. I wake up and i really dont want to eat anything for some time but i force myself to stomach circa 10 Oysters and extra protein. Leptin Reset is hard work.

It has a very strange effect... The oysters are giving me a kind of high and also a weird gut sensation. I remember when i was a kid we used to eat sea-urchins and the adults used to tell us that if we have a lot of this then we will get us "drunk". I think this is connected with the oyster story. I love sea urchins by the way.. I used to collect them as a kid , open then and clean them and get the "egg" out... So tasty with copious amounts of olive oil and lemon. But these days are long gone.

The oyster gut sensation might have to do with the micro-organisms the oysters carry... Viral Marketing..
ref: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...ts-into-your-tissues.14962/page-3#post-193108](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-more-dha-you-eat-the-more-gets-into-your-tissues.14962/page-3#post-193108)

However: After food i usually crash and that has been happening for a few months now.. My appetite is really strange... I have bouts of satiety that can shift rapidly to hunger and back ... I also notice that i might dont want to eat and then eat say my second meal of the day then my appetite explodes, i want to eat everything i see+ i want to eat sweets after that meal .. I suspect my leptin receptor in the brain might be well off or mismatched by blue light as well..

Since i started the new job 3 weeks ago i feel hungrier and putting on fat. Blue light causing insuling resistance. Makes me hungry.

Another thing seems to be brewing.. I get pain on both of my thumb's bones and big toes and it is getting worst with the leptin reset. I suspect it might be gout but i dont know how and why is it happening.

All joints are clicking and becoming unstable. It is getting scarier.. From what i read at the forum it is like excess of protons .. My collagen semiconductors might be overloaded by the energy carried by blue light and the absense of full spectrum one?

I now try to be at bed at 9:30 every night. I wake up slightly refreshed but after BAB i crash at some point as i mentioned above.

I am not sure if the DHA amounts that i eat will help saturate my brain and my collagen network to be able to. I suspect we need DHA over there as we need to signal from the brain down to the tissues. But if my job is destroying the DHA in my eye and body then there is no way out from this hole..

It seems madness to stay in this location, honestly .. but i can rely only on myself and my labor for the time being..I got my stem cell treatment scheduled for next week. Then see if i can move with what cash i have left.. Provided that no more shit will hit the fan..

### drezy — 2018-09-09T02:41:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-250547>

Your issues with oysters might be purine related:
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Purine\_nucleotide\_cycle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Purine_nucleotide_cycle)

Not that purines are "bad". I think maybe it's more like a check engine light for your urea cycle.

Overproduction and under excretion are two possibilities. Hydration exogenous and endogenous is a big deal.

I looked for a while and kept getting hung up on citrulline. As in Michalis' step 1 (Cirulline) is beat up at that stage or prior.

Read this post [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...d-urea-cycle-is-not-simple.21360/#post-241737](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/in-through-the-out-door-for-the-tca-and-urea-cycle-is-not-simple.21360/#post-241737)

and maybe this one [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...-journal-of-a-madman.16871/page-2#post-178079](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/diary-i-mean-journal-of-a-madman.16871/page-2#post-178079)

I'm taking a shortcut that gout ≈ cancer (or the beginnings). Oh I'm also shortcutting that your thumbs getting worse is a gout-like issue.

One experiment I think you could do is if the pain is predictable enough and you are methodical is that you could use baking soda in your water in between meals and monitor the pain.

With your previous issues and now this gout the real best experiment your could do is a few months on the equator, eating lightly in the light, pushing DDW through you as you bake in the sun.

I'm reaching out far here to back up my Sauna use, but hear me out............

Ornithine lowers Ammonia Levels. [https://examine.com/supplements/ornithine/](https://examine.com/supplements/ornithine/) (apologies for the crappy source)

[https://jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-23-looking-back-for-a-quantum-leap-forward/](https://jackkruse.com/ubiquitination-23-looking-back-for-a-quantum-leap-forward/)

"A little-known fact about a Warburg metabolism: everyone focuses in on the glucose aspect of Warburg’s findings, but if you read his original papers, he also found a lot of ammonia released simultaneously to glucose use. Why did he find that? Ubiquitin up-regulation creates excess protein turnover, and this leads to the production of a lot of [ammonia.](https://jackkruse.com/www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3528750/) **When ammonia production is increased, oxygen levels fall, superoxide levels drop, while Reactive Nitrogen Species increase. Non-native EMF does it as well.** "

Seems to me like lowering Ammonia by Sauna use increasing Ornithine was on Jack's mind when doing the EMF rx. If you are under high nnEMF then you will be making a lot of this Ammonia, and Reactive Nitrogen Species. The Sauna could be good for this, on days where you can't get yourself in the Cold tub. I also don't see what's wrong with doing both? CT before work, Sauna after work.

The Saunas also release Heat Shock Proteins, which do a myriad of things for the body. One of them is increasing Growth Hormone by massive amounts. Something you may want to do with your debilitating bones.

I do have a lot of broth, at least 2 cups per day... cant do more as i feel it is really potent... I get a weird sensation from it.. like a high or something or it could be the flashbacks of my past acid tests haha .. but it might be all the seaweed i throw in ... It definitely has impact... However i dont get any gout like issues i had with the oysters.. I cant have it in the evening though as it will keep me up. Cant sleep...
I slow cook it... After doing it the first time it is easy... Just throw everything in , set to the lowest heat and go to bed. I cook it 12-16 hours... I havent tried to do more... I would not use a pressure cooker as the pressure and high heat might destroy the nutrients..

There is lots of vinegar in the broth and i use colliander and lime..

Bonus: my poop goes straight down like a rock so i can only assume i am depleting deuterium.

As for stem cells i did a Regenexx procedure.. They injected my tibia and femur where the bone edema is with my stem cells.. And on my right knee the ACL and the ligaments too... I partially torn my ACL after a bike accident last year.. So it is a waiting game.. i am off to Thailand for a month again to give them some boost and relax...I need all the sun i can get this time..

### Sean Waters — 2018-10-17T11:38:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-253478>

> [drezy said:](/goto/post?id=253403)
>
> I think appearing stressed can be built into the workplace like a religious ceremony. Not looking worried enough can raise suspicion and pitchforks. I'm apparently very bad at it.

Totally agree with this.

I've started listening to "consciousness" books, basically self-help, and learning to "relax and release" when your Voice in your head starts to pull you towards negativity. So any angry or jealous or fearful thoughts, you have to detach and become aware of them. That is the premise of the book, and detach from my voice in my head which is leading to a negative view of my life. The book says, "stop trying to change the external environment, and change the internal". I've started using this, because despite gains in my health, I have this inner sense of an unhappy feeling some days.

**_Now look at that paragraph above with Kruse glasses on_... _In nature, in the Sun, as a member of the Hazda Tribe, would you get these feelings? Would you need to do this relax and release technique to get through your day? Do the lions and tigers and gazelles need to do this?_**

It makes sense, why millions of Londoners/ New Yorkers etc. are all buying these books, taking up Yoga and Meditation, going Vegan, and trying to go spiritual etc. Because their environment is making their subconscious go "FUCK THIS SHIT". But, they don't want to or can't leave that place, they are conditioned. So these books are actually training them to TOLERATE the situation even more, and stay in their nnEMF hell hole and be obedient little idiots some more.

My view, is that using this book has helped me feel better here the last couple days. And I think I will use it for the remainder of my time here. But I am also aware, that perhaps I'm getting angry because in truth I'm totally unhappy with the situation and the environment, and that these thoughts are there to protect me, just like if a Tribal Hazda saw a hurricane coming and got stressed by that - he would get the fuck out. Except, I'm just sitting in it.

I agree. As i currently work for a publisher i get to take a lot of books. There are a lot of them that i have read with exactly this underlying theme/pattern... Basically detach from the thought that is creating the problem. Or between the thought and the action there is a few miliseconds space , however if you train your mind (and obviously if you have fed the substrate well) that split second expands massively in subjective terms, so you can change your action. Subsequently you can cope in this hostile environment. Makes you cope for years, build a succesfull career, endure assholdes etc until you get cancer in the end or eventually get fried by emf while staying put.
I am not disregarding those practices.. Actually they are very usefull and we all need them to cope in this climate but there is a deeper level of things which is assesing what why do things and what is actually good.Those practices are ancient.. People in Asia have been using them for thousands of years.Mindfullness is an amazing tool.

Also, Ancient Greeks were the first in the west talking about "Phronesis". Think before you act.. There are may of these JK memes that are very similar to what ancient Greeks have already said. It is knowledge you won't get in school but it is known things, there are not very popular though , they are not mainstream like Rihana.. You need to either go to uni to study it or have a zest for this kind of knowledge.

I think Epicureanism is really an antidote for what we are currently living. But people get it wrong. They think that Epicureanism has to do with lust and gluttuny and indulging which is completely wrong. Actually Epicurus was Democritus' pupil and in contrast with Plato that was talking about his airy-fairy stories, Epicurus told that the world is actually QUANTIZED. 2000 years ago.

I wish i could explain my predicament to the world but there is no point.. Moral issues are relevant, Nature is objective and absolute and selects for the best ... There is no emotions in nature only necessity.

I am going to Mexico on the 22nd of Nov to 8th of Dec... exploratory trip and looking for a way to disengage from London...

### Michalis — 2019-11-24T02:44:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-282696>

So, I am in Mexico.. Close to Puerto Aventuras. The weather is really sunny however it seems like mission impossible to get to beaches. You either have to use a beach club or live in a resort.. I have been spoiled with choice in Crete and Thailand. I am a little disappointed already... Definitely a scheme..

### Novah — 2019-11-24T06:37:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-282710>

> [Michalis said:](/goto/post?id=282696)
>
> So, I am in Mexico.. Close to Puerto Aventuras. The weather is really sunny however it seems like mission impossible to get to beaches. You either have to use a beach club or live in a resort.. I have been spoiled with choice in Crete and Thailand. I am a little disappointed already... Definitely a scheme..
> Generally I feel ok, the sun does the job, however my bones and joints are killing me... So this is my exploratory trip here... I think it would be really really difficult to move down here without a remote job.

Hi,

I had the same frustration re the beaches.

I was in Puerto Aventuras this winter - wasn’t my cup of tea but if you go through the gates, there’s a very small but nice beach (free) in front of the Omni hotel - just walk down the path on the side of the hotel. The Omni hotel also makes a good breakfast.

My fav beach in the area was at the Barcelo Maya hotel - it’s fabulous. But you have to either stay there or go for a day pass.

If you have the time, head up to Puerto Morelos, and stay on the beach side. Free and easy access to a beach that goes on for miles - really good for walking and swimming and a friendly town.

Another good and free beach is in Playacar in Playa Del Carmen. You can either walk in or stay at an Airbnb or hotel inside the gates.

Friends of mine that grew up in the area strongly recommended I skip that whole coast and head to Cozumel instead, so that’s another area to explore.

Also look into going up to Holbox.

I loved Tulum’s beaches but wasn’t a fan of how much effort it took to get to them, as well as the beach clubs restricting access etc.

I think all you can do is explore as much as you can and see what areas resonate (or not) for you. Good luck!

I tried to go through the gates to Omni but I wasn't allowed as I did not have a reservation... Such a pain... Arriving on a bicycle and looking sweaty probably did not inspire security..
My initial thoughts was to rent a car and explore but then I changed my mind as what I really wanted was to relax and just hit the beach and the cenotes..I feel really tired from working in London and I just wanted to unwind but now I got more headaches as to where to go especially with two bad knees and an aching hip and back. So the moral of the story is to go to one of this resorts if you are in this kind of pickle .. otherwise explore.
Just before I book accomodation asked my air BnB host if the beaches are public there and she said yes. I took her word literally and there we are. So this is actually s half truth. Beaches are public however ACCESS to the beaches is tightly controlled around where I am.

### Michalis — 2019-11-24T13:19:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-282718>

On another note.

There is as lot of OBESE people here. I would expect the opposite.. it is just crazy.. is it the influence of America? It was not like this in Thailand.. I am really really shocked. They live under strong sun, they can eat more carbs etc, they got the great influence of the crater, what gives? Blue light? Something is really off...

I completely agree re: best to stay at the resorts - on that coast, it's the way to go, at least initially.

You may want to get in touch with Dave Key & Shah/Lloyd on here, and just head up to Isla Mujeres and stay with them; both of them have accom available I think - they seem to have things dialed and that makes things so much easier.

### Michalis — 2019-11-25T04:13:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-282757>

> [Novah said:](/goto/post?id=282755)
>
> I completely agree re: best to stay at the resorts - on that coast, it's the way to go, at least initially.
>
> You may want to get in touch with Dave Key & Shah/Lloyd on here, and just head up to Isla Mujeres and stay with them; both of them have accom available I think - they seem to have things dialed and that makes things so much easier.

Thanks!

So today it was a little bit better.. I went swimming in the morning with my airbnb hosts' mates who are diving instructors... They were driving a big BMW and we went to Puerto Aventuras at Omni.. They just said "Buseo" (Diving) to the security guard and we were let in,. The security guard took the driver's licence and flashed it to a camera as well..

Makes sense, they saw me the other day on a crap bicycle and probably they wondered what is this cheap bastard is doing here..

Later in the afternoon i cycled south and I found a place to go swimming and the beach is accessible for a fee, only 30 pesos.. The Beach club next to it charges 80..So i think i am going to stick here for the time being until i get frustrated again haha... And it is not about the money, everything here seems to be so exclusive... Everything is guarded..I wonder how the local people feel about it as they too have to pay,however if you consider that the minimum wage here is about $5 US, go figure..

The good thing is that there are 4-5 Cenotes around here so i will get to visit them too..

So i cycled from Puerto Aventuras south , about 3-4 km, on the shittiest bicycle in the world.. I can do a lot of distance with bicycles but that was another level.. So every single road to the beach is closed... Everything is private.. How ridiculous is this..

So i am kinda venting my frustrations here but maybe it is good so others know and prepare accordingly before they come down here..
Apparently the southern you go from the Riviera Maya the easier it gets as it is not that developed..

### Michalis — 2019-11-27T01:49:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-282829>

That's a nice beach. Xpu-ha close to Puerto Aventuras. If you try to deviate and start exploring on the left edge of the beach there are security guards that wont let you roam out of bounds.. But it is a massive beach and there is plenty of space.. I wish i could get rid of the kiniki and throw it around but thats another story...

### Michalis — 2019-12-01T16:16:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-282943>

So. I had enough at Puerto Aventuras. Honestly the barriers of entry to the beach is just rediculous. Yes, I can get to the beach if I pay a small fee but it is just so restricted. Boundaries, boundaries I wanna get away, the song goes...

So I rent a car and drove south which actually was the original plan. Should have done this from day 1. So fellow sun seekers that come to Mexico independently and don't want your balls busted maybe do the same. Or just go to a resort and end of story. Resorts seem super boring and expensive to me.

So I am currently at Bacalar. Lovely place. And you can get a cheap room close to the lake for 600-900 MXN peso. Same sun is shining over here. Don't know about the magnetic fields but it feels fine to me. I doubt that it will fix my broken joints overnight.

### Lahelada — 2019-12-02T17:23:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-282987>

Cheap food is key to survival. The staff of those luxury hotels are paid half a peanut. Consider the mix of different haplotypes,too.
Netflix and IG at night can kill lots of benefits of daytime lught exposure. Brown skin colour does not favour your career chances either so people avoid sun.
My suggestion is to make this trip your own. Don't expect,don't see the glass half full. Change the glass so it is full.
Bacalar usted interesting. You are near the blue hole. Interesting place. I passed it but did not stop on my way to Belize.
As far as Mexican beaches are concerned the Constitution guarantees free access. The swankiest hotel has to let the barefoot farmer cross the lobby if they have not got a separate access way to " their" beach. I hazard a guess that security reasons have overridden this law.
Enjoy...don't think .

### Michalis — 2019-12-05T00:50:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-283114>

Mahahual rocks if you just want to relax and empty your head. It is not the best beach but it is super chilled out.
Just get passed the malecon and the touts and head a little south of the walking street. Ignore the touts.
If you find people to hang out it is great, otherwise it can get a bit boring but still ok .you can also go snorkeling and all that.
So those touts exist because there is a cruise port that basically keeps this village alive.it docks in the morning it departs in the afternoon. Then everything shuts down. But there are a handful of place you can go. Food is good and there is constant fresh seafood everywhere. I advise you to eat in small places. The ones at the malecon seem to overcharge and you don't get an itemised bill.

### Michalis — 2019-12-08T16:25:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-283292>

So. I am at PDC for a couple of days and making my way back to London today.
PDC is an uninteresting place. Went to the beach and it stinks on places. There is access to the beach but from specific points and you need to walk to reach a nice spot. It is hijacked by holiday rentals. Massively overpriced but if you get off the main walking streets then you get back to Mexico. Everything for the dollar baby.There are towers everywhere. Not sure how the crater can offset that.

The places I did truly enjoy in Mexico was Bacalar and Mahahual. More relaxed there.

Would I back to Mexico?

I would, but completely skipping the north bit of Quintana Roo. I think there is benefit here staying long term in the south. I would like to test the hypothesis for my n=1. I don't think this place will fix my joints. That is out of the question. However staying here for a good chunk of time will definitely help syncing,/quantizing my clocks to the master tempo. And I think this is beneficial.

Also I love its simplicity! It a huge bonus. And you just feel so good  like for mood it is awesome too.

Yeah I definitely agree about the latitude thing. Since there is not much light to work on we need to tweak other parameters.

By the way I haven't put any weight on in mexico. Lately my weight fluctuates between 72 to 75 kg. I was 74 yesterday and for 2 weeks I took it easy. I was eating tacos almost everyday, drinking juices, malbec, the occasional beer with shit tons of lime in it, ice-cream 3-4 times and the weight has been steady. If I was to do that in London I would have ballooned.

But I had to take care otherwise it is a loosing battle with all these goodies they sell, or crap I should say. I honestly have a sweet tooth and it doesn't go well with any with my leptin electron counter.

By the way I loved the pork here. It tastes so good. The first few days I was just cooking pork belly ,sausages and octopus. Loved it, but then I started introducing carbs as I could not cook because I was staying in hotels.

Being carnivore takes discipline but I love the fact it is just simple. I just need to cook a lot of meat and broth.

Will keep you posted.

### Michalis — 2025-04-28T23:37:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-351450>

My mum passed away last year so i really do not need to provide any more financial support for her 24/7 care, food and bills. That means i got no obligation to anyone else than me, so i can get the hell out of London. I did what i had to do and she passed away in dignity, kept warm, having every comfort and cleanliness as she was bedridden for years. It took a toll on me as i have been really trapped here in London with only some breaks going to Mexico a couple of times and in Thailand to get to the sun. I spoke about this with other members here and we know what Jack's position is as well.. Who are you good for if you are not good for yourself. Others, (i won't say names) told me to ditch her and don't help her as the message has been similar based on what Jack's have been saying about family etc etc.. But i could not ditch my mum.. My mum did the best she could with the mind she had. And she caused a lot of trouble as well. Unfortunately i had to foot the bill as my brother turned against me. So that's my dirty laundry... I am not playing the victim, I had to do it and it was a conscious decision . And the pressure and stress was immense as well as the anger as i had to keep getting more and more lucrative contracts.. Not that i am rich or super talented, but i am persistent with work and i put a lot of hours in to get the return i want.

Now i need to fix what is left of myself if it is fixable...

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-29T04:04:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-351460>

You do not have to ditch people in your life, but you do have a duty to yourself to be an accountant for time. You have to realize what youre doing, why your doing it, and what the cost is. Then you decide on the path you choose.

Not everyone is good at math.

When navigating relationships, it’s not about cutting people out of your life but about gaining clarity on your actions and their implications. Take a step back to understand what you’re doing, whether it’s maintaining a friendship, supporting a family member, or working with a colleague and reflect deeply on why you’re doing it.

Are your motivations rooted in genuine care, obligation, or fear of conflict? Then, consider the cost: the emotional energy, time, or even personal growth you might be sacrificing. Weighing these factors allows you to make intentional decisions about how to proceed, whether that means setting boundaries, adjusting expectations, or continuing as is. Not everyone finds this process intuitive, though; it’s like solving a complex equation, and not everyone is good at math. You may give family more wieght than friends. Others may feel that friends are the family you choose and not who you get stuck with.

Some may struggle to calculate the balance between effort and reward, but with practice, this self-awareness can lead to healthier, more fulfilling connections.

### Michalis — 2025-04-29T10:04:55+0300

### Michalis — 2025-05-01T09:02:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-351614>

I keep coming back to this quote from Cornelius Castoriadis... It is and always has been so liberating when i read it..

_"In rich societies – which, after all, barely represent one seventh of the world’s population – with the end of political convictions and the weakening of society’s capacity to create new values that could mean something, what Pascal would call entertainment or distraction, oblivion, prevails. They do not want to know that they are mortal, that they will die, that “in the hereafter” there is neither payment nor reward. They forget themselves, watching Tapie or Madonna on television, after the metro, work, etc. And this does not mean a “society of the spectacle” but a society of oblivion, of oblivion of death, of the realization that life has no meaning except that which we are capable of giving it. The spectacle functions to facilitate and cover up this oblivion. We do not have the courage or the ability to admit that the meaning of our lives, individual and collective, can no longer be granted by a religion or an ideology, can no longer be given to us as a gift, that we must, **therefore, create it ourselves**."_

### Michalis — 2025-05-18T10:24:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-352811>

The situation in the UK is terrible... My client let me go as they don't "have" money to pay external contractors. It has become really hard to get a job, let alone being self employed contracting. Late 2024 Early 2025 i had to go to the platform Upwork as there was not much work out there and the competition was fierce. And that is to get paid peanuts.. Everything is slowly eroding.. Municipal services, Healthcare obviously... Knife crime is through the roof and I see ugliness everywhere.

Since i have seen many places and their IT infrastructures and also the culture inside these companies i have started forming the opinion that the UK is irredeemable. It is all pervasive, it is broken everywhere..

And they also want to know about your bitcoin... So bottom line any companies that offer crypto to retail customers they must be "vetted" by the FCA here. Basically they are creating the framework to know who owns it and tax it... But the same does not apply to those who offer crypto institutional players and not the normal folk..

### Michalis — 2025-06-09T14:18:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal.18631/post-354347>

So today i got my MRI results for the progression of my knee arthritis.. It certainly does not look good and does not feel good either. Sometimes i am struggling walking.

The only thing that worries me massively right now is what happens if I leave London and shit hits the fan in the tropics.. I have got the option for OATS (Osteochondral Autograft Transfer System) or just leave it as it is until I need a knee replacement. The tropics might offer some mitigation but i am not sure if i will be able to avoid surgery. I think the scales have tipped. CT or red light did not help but I do live in London which is the strongest variable in the equation..

Tough decisions ahead.. If anyone has had a bad knee like this by all means tell me your experience with the tropics

# Thread: Salutation to the Sun
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salutation-to-the-sun.18634/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 95
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Surya Namaskar — 2016-10-22T05:15:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salutation-to-the-sun.18634/post-203176>

Background:

Born, raised, and currently live in northeast Ohio.
On my 38th trip around the sun.
Bodybuilder and party animal from age 19 to 25
Diagnosed with hypothyroidism by age 23.
Clean and sober by age 25 - vitiligo began shortly after.
Started distance running at age 26 - 5K, 10K, Half Marathon.
Started yoga at age 30.
Attempted vegetarianism - lasted 1 year.
Stopped distance running and started CrossFit at age 31.
Began eating paleo with high doses of fish oil per Robb Wolf's recommendations.
Hurt my knee less than a year later - diagnosed as cartilage defect on medial femoral condyle.
Began following Primal Blueprint and Mark's Daily Apple.
Began following Chris Kresser.
Began following Bulletproof and Dave Asprey.
Ran into an article at Asprey's blog: Cold Thermogenesis in Tibet & the Dangers of Biohacking - linked to Dr. Jack Kruse
Began following Dr. Jack Kruse
Bought and installed an RO system (this thing is awesome):

14 Stages of Purification Perfection
Stage 1 - Five Micron Pre-Filter
Stage 2 - Internal Coconut Shell Carbon Filter
Stage 3 - Reverse Osmosis Membrane (Purifier #1)
Stage 4 - Mixed Bed De-Ionization Purifier (Purifier #2)
Stage 5 - Mixed Bed De-Ionization Purifier (Purifier #3)
Stages 6 & 7 - Homeopathic Restructuring - Erasing Memory, Molecule Coherence
Stage 8 - Holding Tank
Stage 9 - Ultraviolet Light - 14 Watt
Stage 10 - Reprogramming - Adding Natural Mineral Properties
Stages 11-12 - Far-Infrared Reprogramming
Stage 13 - Coconut Shell Carbon Post-Filtration
Stage 14 - Alka-Min (Alkalizing, Ionic Remineralization)

Became a Silver Klub member.
Began eating more seafood - MUCH more seafood.
Began getting outside as much as possible - averaged 10K IU vitamin D a day per my D Minder app this summer (which is pretty damn good for Ohio).
Began watching sunrises on all clear mornings (with bare feet in the wet grass).
Began getting my bare feet on the earth on ALL mornings.
Began Cold Thermogenesis protocol - currently doing ice baths at 50-55 deg F three times per week.
Purchased blue blocking glasses from LowBlueLights - wear when the sun goes down.
Got rid of all CFL's and most LED's in the house for incandescent and halogens.
Purchased a Coldtub (Icepod Plus - not here yet - can't wait - taking this shit to another level).
Became a Gold Klub member.
Purchased a combo package from EMF Center to begin measuring all three kinds of EMFs (hasn't arrived yet).

The vitiligo has been aggressively regimenting - which is good - but I'm a little concerned as the days are growing shorter here in Ohio. I've planned a trip to southern Florida for two weeks end of November beginning of December, so that should help - but I think I'm going to have to augment my light exposure with tanning beds. I've also been considering purchasing a special narrowband UVB bed for home - but I have to get with a dermatologist to get that going and the last time I saw a dermatologist they had me spreading steroids all over my skin and putting on sunscreen so the white patches didn't burn - both of which are a terrible move as I'm coming to understand.

That's it for now. Looking forward to reading your comments.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-22T05:35:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salutation-to-the-sun.18634/post-203177>

Welcome. Ohio for your story is no bueno. Tell us about Mom and grandma. With your early twenty history it reeks of a transgenerational mitochondrial heteroplasmy state.

### Surya Namaskar — 2016-10-22T17:52:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salutation-to-the-sun.18634/post-203187>

Way Background:

Mother:
Early 20's diagnosed with hypothyroidism / hashimoto's thyroiditis.
Early 40's began experiencing gastrointestinal issues - IBS, acid reflux, etc.
In her 50's diagnosed with high cholesterol and asthma - this diagnosis followed a trip to Israel.
Now, at age 65, she's been diagnosed with diverticulosis/diverticulitis, just finished a round of antibiotics for a bladder infection and now on antibiotic for diverticulitis.
She's experiencing uterine cramping and so now has an appointment with an OBGYN.
The good news (or bad news depending on how you look at it) is that she's lasted longer than her mother and grandmother.

Grandmother (mom's mom):
Struggled with weight problems.
Heavy smoker - 2 packs/day.
Always popping pills - self diagnosing - claimed she was suffering from gall bladder attacks.
Lost weight on Weight Watchers - caught a cold - settled into lungs - suffered 3 major heart attacks and died at age 52.

Great Grandmother (mom's mom's mom):
Overweight and diabetic.
Passed at 60 years old.

Yes, after reviewing this history, that sounds accurate: transgenerational mitochondrial heteroplasmy state.

there is one vitamin that is the only way iodine can be activated - nobody talks about it. I've got a ton on T-2

3,5-diiodo-l-thyronine - aka T-2

**Thyroid hormones and Mitochondria by Goglia F, Silvestri E, and Lanni**
**Oregon Health & Science University**

Two iodothyronines have been identified as effectors of the actions of thyroid hormones on energy metabolism: 3′,3,5-triiodo-L-thyronine (T3) and 3,5-diiodo-L-thyronine (T2). Both have significant effects on BMR, but their mechanisms of action are not identical. T3 acts on the nucleus to influence the expression of genes involved in the regulation of cellular metabolism and mitochondria function; 3,5-T2, on the other hand, acts by directly influencing the mitochondrial energy-transduction apparatus. A molecular determinant of the effects of T3 could be uncoupling protein-3 (UCP-3), while the cytochrome-c oxidase complex is a possible target for 3,5-T2. In conclusion, it is likely that iodothyronines regulate energy metabolism by both short-term and long-term mechanisms, and that they act in more than one way in affecting mitochondrial functions.

**Demonstration of in Vivo metabolic effects of 3,5 diiodothyronine by M cimmino, F Mion, F Goglia, Y Minaire and A Geloen**
T(2) exerts metabolic effects on energy expenditure, on both lipid beta-oxidation and leucine metabolism in hypothyroid rats. We conclude that 3,5-T(2) is a metabolically active iodothronine

**3,5-diiodothyronine and 3,5,3′-triiodothyronine both improve cold tolerance of hypothyroid rats, but possibly via different mechanisms by Lanni A, Moreno M, Lombardi A, Goglia F**

**T2 The Fat Terminator? by John M Berardi**
T2 may be benificial in rapid energy requiring situations like cold exposure - a big bonus w/ CT. BAT has the highest levels of T2  woohooo

**The Thyroid Handbook by Cy Willson**
T3 increases aerobic mitochondria function or respiration, but another thyroid hormone, T2 is actually be responsible for this action

**New compound may act to keep thyroid activity in check**
OHSU study finds T1 amine rapidly causes hypothermia, blood pressure drop, slow pulse

PORTLAND, Ore. Researchers have isolated a naturally occurring, thyroid hormone-derived compound that produces an inactive, torpid-like state in rodents and could someday help doctors stabilize surgical and trauma patients. Although the compound, 3-iodothyronamine T1 amine, for short is a derivative of thyroxine, an essential thyroid hormone that influences development, body temperature, metabolic rate and cardiac performance, it has the opposite effect of thyroxine, according to a study by scientists at Oregon Health & Science University, the University of California, San Francisco (UCSF) and Universita di Pisa, Italy.

### Surya Namaskar — 2016-10-31T00:03:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salutation-to-the-sun.18634/post-203457>

From Izabella Wentz's book, "Hashimoto's Thyroiditis: Lifestyle Interventions for Finding and Treating the Root Cause" (recommended):

**Thyroid Hormone Production**

The thyroid gland has multiple small narrow cavities called follicles that are filled with a clear material known as thyroglobulin that is produced by a layer of thyroid epithelial cells known as thyrocytes. This material contains tyrosine, an amino aacid that is a starting material used for thyroid hormone synthesis. Thyroglobulin functions as a reservoir for materials used in thyroid production, including the storage of iodine.

Iodide absorbed from food circulates in our blood and is absorbed into the thyroid gland where it must be converted into a form usable by the body through an oxidation process. The enzyme Thyroid PerOxidase (TPO) converts iodide to the active iodine making hydrogen peroxide as a byproduct. The reactive iodine is now ready to attach to other molecules, and attaches to the amino acid tyrosine in thyroglobulin through a process known as "iodination."

During iodination, each molecule of tyrosine combines with one or two iodine molecules, resulting in either T1 or T2. The molecules then bond to form either T3 (thyroglobulin with three iodine molecules) or T4 (thyroglobulin with four iodine molecules).

T1+T2=T3 or T2+T2=T4

Of the four iodinated molecules, only T3 and T4 are considered to be biologically active in the body. However, T4 is known as pro-hormone, and is 300% less biologically active than T3. Thus, T3 is the main biologically active thyroid hormone. These molecules are stored in thyroid follicles until they are needed.

Twenty percent of T3 comes from thyroid secretion, while the remaining 80% comes from T4 when T4 is converted to T3 through the deiodination process (which removes one iodine molecule) in peripheral organs like the liver and kidney. Zinc is required to convert T4 to T3.

Low levels of T3 and T4 signal release of TSH, while high levels of circulating T3 and T4 stop the release of TSH. In people with normal thyroid function, TSH levels may fluctuate in times when more thyroid hormone is consumed, such as in stress, illness, lack of sleep, pregnancy, or low temperatures.

A few thoughts from above excerpt:

-   Iodine and tyrosine appear to be important building blocks for thyroid.
-   Zinc appears to be an important factor in converting T4 to T3.
-   This author has taken the opinion that T1 and T2 are just building blocks for T3 and T4. Either way, T2 appears to be the most important building block as it is required in the formation of _both_ T3 and T4.
-   Supplementing with T4 may be only 80% effective (worse if conversion is bad). T4/T3 (and/or T1/T2) combination may be necessary to optimize T3.
-   All of this may be a moot point to the cold adapted mammal?
-   Author cites, "Ross DS. Thyroid Hormone Synthesis and physiology. UpToDate; 2013" as her source for this.

Pantetheine is the cysteamine amide analogue of pantothenic acid (vitamin B5). The dimer of this compound, **pantethine is more commonly known, and is considered to be a more potent form of vitamin B5 than pantothenic acid.** Pantetheine is an intermediate in the production of coenzyme A by the body.

\[qu ote="Jack Kruse, post: 204544, member: 1031"\]Go get your strontium levels checked and you'll see this is more alternative/functional medicine bullshit.

**RADIATION:** Until you ALTER your thinking, you will always recycle your experiences and old ideas. The beliefs of modern man are the batteries that make them fuel for the matrix in which they live. You have to begin to question the heavy metal and radiation issues when you read the science. All seafood has the antidote to mercury in it: selenium. Radiation effect on cells has been condemned by the entire scientific community as being a “dangerous thing”. This should surprising to inquiring mind, however, since there is strong scientific evidence for the molecular mechanisms of how transient “radiation drinks” could promote health. I tell my patients seafood radiation has many surprising benefits. Today we know that low doses of radiation stimulate DNA repair through the activation of four transcription factors, PARP-1, PARP2, ATM and Ku70. In response to low dose, sublethal radiation (or xenohormetic compounds). PARP-1 and PARP-2 activate two DNA repair pathways called “Single strand break repair” (SSBR) and one called “Base Excision Repair” (BER). Both of these repair single-stranded DNA breaks. In response to low dose, sublethal radiation (or xenohormetic compounds), Ku-70 activates the DNA repair pathway called “Non-homologous end joining” (NHEJ). [https://scholar.google.com/scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar)...

Science evolves even if your thinking about the subject remains stagnant. We must expand our grip of science…………or risk falling from the cliff. I tell this to my patients and all the alternative functional medicine doctors who keep telling their patients seafood is bad because it is radioactive or filled with metals. It is really bad advice based upon out dated thinking.\[/quote\]

### Surya Namaskar — 2016-12-28T02:48:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salutation-to-the-sun.18634/post-205837>

My doctor called Quest Diagnostics to make sure they wrote up the bloodwork order correctly. The lab technician convinced my doctor that their "Omega-3 and -6 Fatty Acids" test was sufficient. This test only looks at:

Omega-3 (EPA+DHA) Index
Omega-6/Omega-3 Ratio
EPA/Arachidonic Acid Ratio
Arachidonic Acid
EPA
DHA

I went into Quest Diagnostics today and told the lab technician this was the wrong test, that I needed the "Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum." Believe it or not, she tried to argue with me - but, after a phone call and a verbal approval from my doctor's office, she finally agreed to it. The "Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum" requires fasting, so I'm going in tomorrow morning.

I'm not even going to suggest to them that I do more than one blood draw for cortisol. I'm afraid their head's might explode.

Can you help me to understand the importance of the "Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum?" It is apparently not very common and I'm encountering significant resistance to this testing (although I think I'm over the hump). I would like to be able to explain what this test provides, how it will be interpreted, and what remedial measures may be indicated.

### JanSz — 2017-01-07T00:51:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salutation-to-the-sun.18634/post-206255>

> [Surya Namaskar said:](/goto/post?id=206252)
>
> Cortisol, progesterone, and pregnenolone are low.
> Considering pregnenolone-MLM to raise all 3.
> Why wouldn't I?

That is what I did after searching other ways.
It worked well for me.
Eventually after number of years on it I am not using it now and my cortisol AM stays at rather good level.

My problem.
Other then very short time, my progesterone went up 2 or 3x over its desirable level.
Pregnenolone peeked but then always was rather low.
For long time it did not bothered me.
Then (like 2-3 or more years latter) I started having having erection problems, again.
I am on testosterone supplementation since about 2000, balls shot.
At the time I was using T injectable.
Progestererone (and many other) are 5Ar inhibitors.
5Ar inhibitor slows Testosterone--->DHT conversion
DHT gets lower.
Erections are correlated to DHT level, progesterone when higher than optimal messes up with erections.
In my case, I at first replaced part of my weekly (injectable) testosterone dose with Androgel.
Androgel is transdermal testosterone. It is a thin gel that is spread on skin.
Skin have 5A-reductase, 5Ar, when it meets with testosterone it converts it to DHT.
Androgel, other than testosterone also raises DHT.
It does that sort of, proportionally to skin area engaged with that gel.
Androgel comes as 1% (thinner, large area), 1.62% (thicker, less area)
Expensive doctors give you (cheap) testosterone injections or cheap testosterone creams (trying to save you $$). They usually stay away from pregnenolone pills.
My current doc, does not want to go into nuances, and to make this service his specialty, gives only Androgel to anyone that needs testosterone.
I happen to need it.
Androgel may cost lots of $$.
\---------------
For me, pregnenolone pills or pregnenolone or progesterone creams have not worked.
Only when I found pregnenolone-MLM thing turned better for me.
\----------------
As you see raising cortisolAM is not a clear cut project.
Not everyone is the same,
you may be lucky and respond sufficiently to creams.
Cream may/will go thru different paths and may not raise your progesterone excessively.
This whole project cannot be accomplished without testing.
I want you to make informed choice.
Pregnenolone-MLM may work differently with you then it worked with me.
If your testosterone and erections are ok, better not take chances (unless Adrenal Fatigue) wins.
If you would decide to use preg-MLM start slowly
or just start somewhere and test .
More important,
if you are loaded with supplemented thyroid hormones, they may be just floating outside cells.
Higher cortisol would push it in (all maybes) and spoil couple days. If you have free weekends, start all this Saturday morning.

You would not want to have pregnenolone very high over long time.
Ask questions.

.

### Surya Namaskar — 2017-01-15T03:22:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salutation-to-the-sun.18634/post-206848>

Iodine

Switched from Kelp to Lugol's a couple weeks ago.
I was doing Kelp 3/day = 325 mcg iodine x 3 = 975 mcg iodine
Started Lugol's 5% with 1 drop per day = 6.25 mg
Experiencing mild gastrointestinal distress: pale stools and/or loose stools, mild nausea
Had a thyroid panel drawn this week, TSH quite elevated.

> Dr. David Brownstein said:
>
> Does iodine cause the TSH to rise? The answer is “yes”. Does the elevated TSH mean the thyroid gland is failing? The answer to this question is “no”. It is well known, or should be well known, that iodine is transported into the cell by a transport molecule known as sodium-iodide symporter (NIS). NIS is stimulated by TSH. Therefore, when iodine supplementation is begun, one of the first effects seen is a slight elevation of TSH as the body is trying to produce transport molecules (NIS) to move iodine into the cell. After iodine supplementation begins, it is normal and expected for TSH to elevate slightly.

I'm going to have some explaining to do to my Endocrinologist.
I probably shouldn't have started the Lugol's in the middle of a switch from synthetic T4 to NDT.
I need to increase the dose of NDT as Free T3 is still low.
But maybe should wait until I've worked up the Iodine dose and have gotten through these detox/side effects?
And also probably wait to see if I can improve Pregnenolone/Cortisol status - I wonder if the Lugol's will help with this?

Does anyone concur with that explanation from Dr. Brownstein?
How long will this last?
Will it get worse as I titrate up the Iodine dosage?

Still optimistic that I'm doing the right thing, but am cautiously proceeding.

From my personal experience:
There is a one scenario that you want to avoid.
You do not want to have
high FreeT3 and low cortisol **and then (suddenly)** increase cortisol
but even then
as long as you know why it is happening
you can wait it out
If that would happen to you, stop NTH and preg-MLM (restart after it is over plus one day)

otherwise
you can titrate this variables individually
I think I read somewhere that for TSH to settle it may take a months.

If you respond similarly to me
keep your eye on
Lugols -->50mg/day
freeT3
cortisol

but
when you are using preg-MLM
some people (another board), seen their pregnenolone,serum raised way above top laboratory range
you do not want that (period)
so you also have to watch
pregnenolone, progesterone (and DHT)

...
Overall, discussions with doctor,
depending on endocrinologist that is supporting you,
always show confidence,
I would rather propose some suggestion first,
and ask for his approval with any comments he/she may have.

This stuff is usually not something suggested to average patient.
Average patient gets low dose of Synthroid and a smile.

..

### JanSz — 2017-01-15T16:46:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salutation-to-the-sun.18634/post-206860>

As you move to NTH
Good idea to watch
FreeT3
RT3
\-------------
You want RT3 somewhere in the mid laboratory range.
If RT3 goes up, it means that you have to much T4, excess is dumped via RT3.
As long as RT3 stays within laboratory range, you may choose to do nothing.
Latter on, if you see that RT3 have stabilized at that "high" level,
you may want to replace some of the NTH with T3 (Cytomel or similar).
On table above you can see that
1Grain of NTH=18.5mcg(T3)
Just take the closest available Cytomel dose, drop one Grain,
see what happens couple of few months latter.

I always chew my thyroid hormone pills.

### PaulG — 2017-01-21T00:12:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salutation-to-the-sun.18634/post-207167>

I noticed the reference ranges on the comprehensive essential fatty acids test vary a lot between your lab (US) and [my lab (OZ)](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/paulg-weightloss-journal.13983/page-2#post-205953). For example

**What is this telling us? @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) and @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)
**
For me, I have some issues are such tests

**Problem Number 1:** [Reference ranges](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reference_ranges_for_blood_tests) are most likely based on the normalised bell curve of people tested in the past at the lab less likely to be based on what is optimal based on research. So, if based on generally 'sick' population as opposed to normal->optimal ranges reflect that bias and being 'High' in DHA doesn't tell us much.
**Problem Number 2:** What exactly does DHA level in Red Blood Cells tell us? What is the purpose of DHA in RBC's. You want DHA in tissue cell membranes, in particular in brain mitochondria and this is dependent on how well your lipid transport system is working, ldl levels etc. But the quantlet is supplying light energy into RBC's and DHA transduces light into energy so having high circulating in the RBC's is a very good thing. Is there such as thing as DHA efflux out of membranes, I know DHA is highly protected and conserved.
**Problem Number 3:** This paper talks about [PUFA's and their metabolites in brain function and disease.](https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Sophie_Laye/publication/268230414_Polyunsaturated_fatty_acids_and_their_metabolites_in_brain_function_and_disease/links/5474838b0cf245eb436de2a1.pdf) It says "In the adult brain, the amount of ATP generated from fatty acid β-oxidation is much less than the ATP generated by glucose oxidation (with the notable exception of the circumventricular areas)." My understanding is Ketosis is effective because of the increase in ATP production over glucose.

### JanSz — 2017-01-22T16:23:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salutation-to-the-sun.18634/post-207205>

****This is why people get excessive omega 6 levels in their tissues.****
**I think that we could use some additional support for this statement.**
**Because very often, this statement is not supported by fatty acids analysis.**
**Even more interesting,**
**is (apparently a fact)**
**that most neolithic diseases present with excessive EPA & DHA and suppressed AA.**
**\===================================**
**[https://www.jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/](https://www.jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/)**
**This is why people get excessive omega 6 levels in their tissues.**
**\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------**
****I rely on Omega 6 fats every day in my patients in surgery****
****buy many if not most come on operating table all pumped up with EPA and highly suppressed AA.****
****\-----------------------------****

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
@[PaulG](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1838/)
@[Surya Namaskar](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19449/)
\-------------------------------------
Adrenic acid=Docosatetraenoic acid=DTA=(C22:4w6)
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Docosatetraenoic\_acid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Docosatetraenoic_acid)
\-------------------------------------
[https://www.jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/](https://www.jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/)

AA is a major component of the endothelial \[inner arterial lining\] phosphoglycerides, particularly on the inner cell membrane layer. **AA and adrenic acid** are consistent companions in other **cell membranes.** What many in the ancestral health community do not know is that **EPA** in the marine food chain **limits the amount of AA** that we can place in our tissues. It is like an internal control switch if our circadian signaling is working well. Since most modern humans have an altered circadian signaling today this switch is no longer operational. **This is why people get excessive omega 6 levels in their tissues.** EPA must come from seafood. AA is not always a bad actor. Consider the following:

AA is the precursor for prostacyclin: a vasodilator and inhibitor of platelet adhesion; it is the most potent platelet inhibitor we know of in nature. This stops the clotting process when it is no longer needed. It is also vital to smooth vascular and laminar flow and allows us to overcome wounds we sustain in injury or create in surgery by helping seal the wounds. If there is damage to the endothelium, such as in bruising, infection or cutting, then the phospholipases release AA. In the free form, and in conjunction with activated platelets, AA is peroxidized to provide eicosanoids for the response to injury. This is how the body is supposed to work. **I rely on Omega 6 fats every day in my patients in surgery** to get people to clot and off the operating table.

### Surya Namaskar — 2017-02-19T16:46:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salutation-to-the-sun.18634/post-208897>

Iodine:

I've been using 18.75 mg (3 drops - 5%) Lugol's for about a week now. I continue to go through bouts of gastrointestinal distress, although not as bad as when I started the protocol. I'm in no rush, so I plan to hang out here for awhile and then increase by 1 drop every few weeks, depending on symptoms, up to 50 mg. Once at 50 mg I plan to do an iodine loading test with bromide levels, just to have look under the hood.

Cold Thermogenesis:

Because the city water out of the tap is about 49 degrees F in NE Ohio this time of year I've converted the "cold tub" outside to a "hot tub" at 104 degrees F. I then fill the "bath tub" with 49 degree F water. So, the protocol goes: 20 minutes in the hot tub followed immediately by a large glass of ice water and a 10 minute soak in the cold bath tub. Doing this 2-3 times per week. Wow, what an effect!

Sun:

Fiji Sun Sperti daily, 20 minutes, simultaneously with 2 IRA Rubylux bulbs. Although, we're currently getting some good sun (relatively speaking) here in Ohio so I'm going outside and soaking up the real deal. UV index all the way up to 4 with clear skies... can't ask for more this time of year, I'll take it.

nnEMF:

Currently taking Michael Neuert's "EMF Solutions for your Health" 6-week e-course. Two weeks in, good stuff. Only about 10 of us in the course so we're getting lots of personal attention and questions answered. I measured about 0.3 V/m RF in my Nissan Altima from the Bluetooth trying to connect with my phone (even higher when I actually had my phone connected). I talked to the service people at Nissan, they said just delete your phone out of the system and the Bluetooth will no longer constantly try to connect with it. I followed the instruction and viola! 0.0 V/m RF in the cabin of the car (so long as the phone is on airplane mode!).

Prohormones:

Taking 50 mg DHEA and 30 mg Pregnenolone per blood tests. Will test again in a month or so to fine tune dose.

Vitamins and Minerals:

Taking an assortment based on Spectracell results. Will test again in a few months to see how I'm doing.

Fatty Acid Profile:

Still a little confused about this one. Seems counter to what JK teaches. Everyone I've seen on here do one seems to have "excessive omega 3's" and "suppressed omega 6's." My understanding from JK is you can't have too much omega 3 so long as it is coming from good marine source. But fatty acid test indicates imbalance which could be bad.

That's all for now. Any thoughts or comments are appreciated.

I'm in no rush, so I plan to hang out here for awhile and then increase by 1 drop every few weeks, depending on symptoms, up to 50 mg. Once at 50 mg I plan to do an iodine loading test with bromide levels, just to have look under the hood.
>
> Cold Thermogenesis:
>
> Because the city water out of the tap is about 49 degrees F in NE Ohio this time of year I've converted the "cold tub" outside to a "hot tub" at 104 degrees F. I then fill the "bath tub" with 49 degree F water. So, the protocol goes: 20 minutes in the hot tub followed immediately by a large glass of ice water and a 10 minute soak in the cold bath tub. Doing this 2-3 times per week. Wow, what an effect!
>
> Sun:
>
> Fiji Sun Sperti daily, 20 minutes, simultaneously with 2 IRA Rubylux bulbs. Although, we're currently getting some good sun (relatively speaking) here in Ohio so I'm going outside and soaking up the real deal. UV index all the way up to 4 with clear skies... can't ask for more this time of year, I'll take it.
>
> nnEMF:
>
> Currently taking Michael Neuert's "EMF Solutions for your Health" 6-week e-course. Two weeks in, good stuff. Only about 10 of us in the course so we're getting lots of personal attention and questions answered. I measured about 0.3 V/m RF in my Nissan Altima from the Bluetooth trying to connect with my phone (even higher when I actually had my phone connected). I talked to the service people at Nissan, they said just delete your phone out of the system and the Bluetooth will no longer constantly try to connect with it. I followed the instruction and viola! 0.0 V/m RF in the cabin of the car (so long as the phone is on airplane mode!).
>
> Prohormones:
>
> Taking 50 mg DHEA and 30 mg Pregnenolone per blood tests. Will test again in a month or so to fine tune dose.
>
> Vitamins and Minerals:
>
> Taking an assortment based on Spectracell results. Will test again in a few months to see how I'm doing.
>
> Fatty Acid Profile:
>
> Still a little confused about this one. Seems counter to what JK teaches. Everyone I've seen on here do one seems to have "excessive omega 3's" and "suppressed omega 6's." My understanding from JK is you can't have too much omega 3 so long as it is coming from good marine source. But fatty acid test indicates imbalance which could be bad.
>
> That's all for now. Any thoughts or comments are appreciated.

You are exactly right re fatty acids test. I have not seen anyone get better by incorporating the omega-6 rich oils that are recommended. Who says that Patricia Kane has determined the right ratios? I certainly don't think our ancestors were striving to get their hands on primrose, borage and safflower...

### JanSz — 2017-04-06T17:56:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salutation-to-the-sun.18634/post-211901>

On post #52, written by you, you posted quote:

Dr. David Brownstein said:

Does iodine cause the TSH to rise? The answer is “yes”. Does the elevated TSH mean the thyroid gland is failing? The answer to this question is “no”. It is well known, or should be well known, that iodine is transported into the cell by a transport molecule known as sodium-iodide symporter (NIS). NIS is stimulated by TSH. Therefore, when iodine supplementation is begun, one of the first effects seen is a slight elevation of TSH as the body is trying to produce transport molecules (NIS) to move iodine into the cell. After iodine supplementation begins, it is normal and expected for TSH to elevate slightly.

Bromide also a problem; excreted above national average.

>
> Not quite at iodine body sufficiency yet.

> Come to find out **city water** putting fluoride in - tested at 1.08 ppm. Need to avoid, even ice.
> Checked bottled spring water at work - tested at 0.22 ppm fluoride.

>
> Had one hell of a time working up to 50 mg Lugol's - probably because I'm chasing out all this shit.
> Will continue on 50 mg for 6 months and retest to assess progress.
> Anyone else have experience with this protocol/test? Any success establishing iodine body sufficiency and/or reducing non-iodine halides?

There is a quote that Jack has used quite a few times. It's in several blogs and also in quite a few forum posts. It says:

"The easiest way to control people to be passive and obedient is to strictly limit the spectrum of acceptable opinion, but allow very lively debate within that spectrum."​

From the very first time I saw that quote, I immediately thought of U.S. politics and in particular the presidential elections.

I've had to limit my exposure to social media over the past few months because it drives me crazy to see people on both sides that I normally consider to be intelligent and thoughtful having their "lively debate" by attacking each other. I see very little discussion about options for the future and a huge amount of disinformation on both sides, together with a ton of animosity.

So, the acceptable spectrum is A or B and folks are having their lively debate. It's like telling a kid they can have their choice of cornflakes or cheerios for breakfast - sure they are different, but maybe cereal isn't good for them and they need bacon, eggs or sardines?

And what's a kid to do if they think cornflakes are evil and cheerios are scary ? If they pick one, is that really evidence they wanted it?
