From what I understand the Magnetico pad will have a socket type A (male, not grounded) or more likely B (male, grounded) and the Dutch wall socket is C (female, not grounded) and F (female, grounded).

USA
Voltage (120 V)
Frequency (60 Hz)

Dutch
Voltage (230 V)
Frequency (50 Hz)

**Question:**
Are there any negative (health) effects from frequency converting like this?

# Thread: Is Stereochemistry more important than biochemistry for medicine?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-stereochemistry-more-important-than-biochemistry-for-medicine.18637/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 29
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-22T15:11:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-stereochemistry-more-important-than-biochemistry-for-medicine.18637/post-203183>

Stereochemistry, and not biochemistry, is the key to understand life. A cell's optical abilities or disabilities determine what its present and future can be. This idea is the key to illness reversal.

As matter speeds up to lights speed limit matter is converted to light photons. The interesting thing is as things speed up they gain mass and then right at the end of mass conversion to pure energy, mass vanishes. Remember light has no mass. It is the only boson with no mass. At this rate of speed, all things act more like waves and less like a particle. The converse is true with respect to light. As light is slowed it begins to gain the ability to act more particle-like and things with a structure of mass manifest or emerge from the slowing down of light. (Vermont 2017)

So life got the idea to use protein polymers to interact with light to alter its speed by using tissue density differences in organs to harvest and use the power, energy, and data in those light photons to make different probabilities and possibilities that life has manifest.

For example, in humans, women have an overall tissue density that is less than men. This means they live a life that is more wave-like than particle like on a relative basis. Men, are more particle-like because their tissues have a higher density (testosterone deuterium density) and using Fermat's law of least time they are able to use sunlight a bit differently than women. This small ability gives their tissues abilities women do not and cannot have under natural conditions and it is why men on average live less long compared to women. I believe the variance of progesterone/estrogen/testosterone in women change their optical windows to reflect this change. I think these 3 hormones cause less deuterium to be present in their tissues as a result.

How do eukaryotes control light's speed in our tissues and how can these small variances need to be understood. This is the key to understanding the October and November 2016 webinars. I suggest all physicians consider listening to these webinars with this post in mind.

Fermat's principle or Snell's law represents the same idea in different isoforms of mathematic equations. In this way, they mimic the stereochemistry of proteins and sugars in human tissues. In nature and real life, I assure you, there is no such thing as algebra or calculus. Mathematics is just a reflective imperfect mirror of nature's hidden blueprint. Math can express "values" that reflect the cosmos, including orderliness, balance, harmony, logic, and abstract beauty. In that reflection, something essential is missing by design. Light has nonlinear aspects that are capable of not reflecting all aspects of its energy and data. It is in this shadow why life's mysteries remain hidden to physicists who've chosen to use math to replace experiments and observation. Math can describe nature, but it can never explain it. Physicists can do calculus to explain their own beliefs about the universe but they seem to fail to be able to simply add and subtract in the ways nature has built life. Like all magnificent things, it's very simple at its core.

Of the 74 amino acids, for example, found in samples of the Murchison meteorite, only 8 are present in proteins, 11 have 4 other biological roles, and the remaining 55 have been found only in extraterrestrial samples. The biological era which concerns itself with the replication of nucleic acid polymers and protein synthesis. The biological stage may have started some 3.8 billion years ago. Chirality is the science of stereochemistry. Chirality on Earth in living things is quite specific. Do you know why? Classically, a chiral molecule and its mirror image (defined by left (L) or right (D) optical/rotatory dispersion is known as stereochemistry. Both isoforms have been considered energetically equivalent by chemistry but living cells appear to have a serious bias. Life only uses 20 L- amino acids and mostly D-sugars with the exception of fructose. The L-form of fructose is used in the liver of eukaryotes. Have you ever stopped to wonder why this would be the case on Earth? Is it linked to the physics of Earth? Did you know the parity-violating "weak interactions" of the laws of physics give rise to L and D configurations? These variances are tied to energy states present on the planet.

The L or D state ensure that this equivalence is no longer exact because of the weak force in one of the two molecules, L or D, being energetically stabilized by the physics of the sun and Earth with energy differences of the order of 3 x 10^~19 eV. Pretty amazing huh? In living systems, protein molecules are composed of twenty L-amino acids coded for by DNA exclusively. The code of life is racists with respect to amino acids. There are 500 known amino acids.

Evolution on Earth has allowed a specific variation to exist in bacteria and archaea. Eukaryotes got this because they stole a bacteria and converted it to a mitochondrion. This implies that sometime in that first 3.8 billion years the physics on the Earth was not what it is today. This is why some amino acids of the D-type can and do occur in cell walls of certain bacteria today. They are remnants of a past reality on Earth tied to how sunlight and matter interacted on Earth before. This is a big clue that the solar spectrum was not always constant on Earth over the last 4.6 billion years. We know given the class star the sun is this is true. Biology just fails to account for these happenings in the evolution of cell biology.

The polynucleotides of DNA and RNA both contain sugars in D—configurations only. Have you ever asked yourself why this is the case? Clearly, once living processes had selected handedness in proteins and its sugars, the complex machinery of protein synthesis and stereoselectivity of enzymes could have assured that such handedness would be perpetuated. Could this be tied to the flavor of neutrinos from the sun? Do the neutrinos of the sun have an ability to make something in our cells we need to separate energy from information? This is the essence of a Turing machine or a perpetual motion device. Members will soon get these answers.

Read Time #25 and came across sentence on left-handedness of neutrinos, the attached calls it "intrinsic chirality". Just piecing together in my head the relationship between right-handed sugars in DNA and left-handed proteins; electrons can be right or left handed. Neutrinos are left-handed only. Anti-neutrinos are right-handed only (bigger clue now to why you'd like to find them!).
All tied together with Faraday effect, TIs and transient magnetic monopoles, weak nuclear force, spectral density, left-spinning spiral galaxy, non-linear optics, water and the massive number of protons in the universe.

[http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/particles/neutrino3.html](http://hyperphysics.phy-astr.gsu.edu/hbase/particles/neutrino3.html)

**While you did write about the non-symmetrical world of nature, how can we reconcile how nature uses handed-ness in what appears to be a symmmetrical/complementary way?**

Also piecing together the weak interaction more: it allows us to make protons. So its essential to the building block of the proton-gradient in cells; the reason the sun burns, allows the build up of heavier atoms due to proton making. And "**The weak interaction acts between both quarks and leptons, whereas the strong force does not act between leptons. Leptons have no color, so they do not participate in the strong interactions; neutrinos have no charge, so they experience no electromagnetic forces; but all of them join in the weak interactions- (Griffiths)"**

_I think I'm understanding more your focus on the weak force._ Is the single unifying energy somehow going to be related to protons and hydrogen?

### Da-mo — 2018-02-24T01:08:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-stereochemistry-more-important-than-biochemistry-for-medicine.18637/post-235569>

> A wandering monopole induces proton decay: A proton with two **u** and one **d** quarks encounters a monopole. One **u**and **d** quark, under the influence of the monopole, "forget" their identities and become an anti-electron (positron) and an anti-**u**(antiparticles represented by reversed images). **The proton has thus become a positron (e+) and a po meson, which immediately decays into two gamma rays.** Notice that electrical charge is preserved during the decay process

[https://www.npl.washington.edu/av/altvw01.html](https://www.npl.washington.edu/av/altvw01.html)

### Da-mo — 2018-02-25T01:31:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-stereochemistry-more-important-than-biochemistry-for-medicine.18637/post-235660>

Analysis of the Chirality Effects on the Capacity of Wireless Communication Systems in the THz Band

> **Abstract:**
> The potentialities of the Terahertz (THz) frequency band in the context of nano-scale communications are increasing, thanks to specific features that allow issues related to the spectrum scarcity and capacity limitation to be overcome. Apart from high molecular absorption and very high reflection loss that represent the main phenomena in THz band, in this paper, we investigate the chirality effects that affect the propagation medium, in the frequency range (4 - 10) THz. It is observed that in this interval, the chiral parameter shows resonance peaks in specific frequencies. In this paper, we investigate the channel capacity in a special medium affected by chirality effects, such as biomolecules, DNA chains, and so on. Specifically, we analyze the signal propagation in a chiral medium where a giant optical activity (GOA) is present. This effect is typical of the so-called chiral-metamaterials. Through simulation results we distinguish the behavior of a chirality-affected channel with GOA in line-of-sight and non-line-of-sight propagations, assuming different power allocation techniques and also comparing the performance to the case of No GOA.
> **Published in:** [IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications](http://ieeexplore.ieee.org/xpl/RecentIssue.jsp?punumber=7693) ( Volume: 16, [Issue: 12](http://ieeexplore.ieee.org/xpl/tocresult.jsp?isnumber=8170465), Dec. 2017 )

**The human skin as a sub-THz receiver – Does 5G pose a danger to it or not?**

> **Highlights**
> The sweat duct is regarded as a helical antenna in the sub-THz band, reflectance depends on perspiration.•
> We outline the background for non-thermal effects based on the structure of sweat ducts.•
> We have introduced a realistic skin EM model and found the expected SAR for the 5G standard

> One must consider the implications of human immersion in the electromagnetic noise, caused by devices working at the very same frequencies as those, to which the sweat duct (as a helical antenna) is most attuned. We are raising a warning flag against the unrestricted use of sub-THz technologies for communication, before the possible consequences for public health are explored.

[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0013935118300331](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0013935118300331)

Circular polarization induced by the three-dimensional chiral structure of human sweat ducts
[https://journals.aps.org/pre/abstract/10.1103/PhysRevE.89.042715](https://journals.aps.org/pre/abstract/10.1103/PhysRevE.89.042715)

> “Nanoscale chiral structures offer an approach to modulating optical signals with relatively small variations in input power,”

[https://www.ece.gatech.edu/news/588...ical-shift-under-incremental-power-modulation](https://www.ece.gatech.edu/news/588139/chiral-metamaterial-produces-record-optical-shift-under-incremental-power-modulation)

### Jack Kruse — 2023-04-18T19:11:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-stereochemistry-more-important-than-biochemistry-for-medicine.18637/post-320607>

What absorbs gamma rays? Melanin. Think Chernobyl and radiosynthesis.

How do you go from diamonds to Davinci but end up in Robert O. Becker's bone lab?
A thread on semiconduction in biology, art, and crystals......

[https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1627826337808359424](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1627826337808359424)

How do you go from Becker's lab and get to monopoles, melanin, protons and neutrinos.....

[https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1648356291251777537](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1648356291251777537)

A mysterious cosmic “bullet” hit Earth — with energy beyond anything humans can create in 2021. And it came from \*nowhere. An ultra-high-energy particle nicknamed the Amaterasu particle slammed into Earth’s atmosphere with an astonishing 240 exa-electron volts of energy.

The Amaterasu particle refers to an ultra-high-energy cosmic ray (UHECR) detected on May 27, 2021, by the Telescope Array experiment in Utah. This ground-based array uses surface detectors, scintillators spaced over 700 km², to measure charged particle showers from cosmic rays interacting with Earth's atmosphere.

The event triggered 23 detectors, with a reconstructed energy of 244 ± 29 EeV (exa-electron volts), making it the second-highest-energy cosmic ray ever observed after the 1991 "Oh-My-God" particle.

240 exa eV is millions of times more powerful than anything we can create on Earth, rivaled only by the famous 1991 “Oh-My-God” particle.

Its trajectory backtracks to the Local Void, a sparsely populated region of space with no obvious high-energy sources like supernovae or active galaxies, no known sources. Just… nothing. This baffling discovery shakes the limits of modern physics.

Potential explanations include magnetic deflections, heavier atomic nuclei as the primary particle, or even exotic physics like magnetic monopoles. The last one is why I am interested in the event.

The Amaterasu particle defies the Greisen-Zatsepin-Kuzmin (GZK) limit a boundary on how far and how fast such particles can travel without losing energy. If it came from far away, it shouldn’t still be this powerful; if it came from nearby, we should be able to see its source. Now, scientists are left questioning whether unknown cosmic forces, exotic physics, or entirely new phenomena are at play. Whatever the answer, the universe just reminded us how little we truly understand about its most extreme mysteries.

CITE

“An extremely energetic cosmic ray observed by a surface detector array.” Science

I am Susan from South Texas, retired nurse practitioner and lab tech...been following Dr. Kruse for about a year off and on and need to read and reread all the free stuff and podcasts before I become a paying member!!! I have found the podcasts an easier place to start....many on YouTube...

# Thread: Poster Girl Optimal Diary
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poster-girl-optimal-diary.18647/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Emma Sabin — 2016-10-23T19:27:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poster-girl-optimal-diary.18647/post-203221>

Hi there Jack

I recently joined the forum and asked you about having cataract surgery. I explained my history and you called me a poster girl for this website. I have now upgraded to Gold membership so I can get more help from you and your network.

Ok here goes,
As a baby I had difficult sleeping and couldn't take my mums milk. What followed was a life full of difficulty sleeping and being very highly strung. My mother is very anxious and has numerous digestive problems and my father was an alcoholic who died last year. My maternal grandmother was a large lady but pretty healthy and died of old age at 90 and my maternal grandfather died of prostrate cancer at 72.
Age 6 - started wearing glasses as became short sighted.
Age 12 - started wearing contact lenses as glasses were interfering with my life.
Age 12 - had glandular fever.
Age 16 - diagnosed with an allergy to dairy resulting in stomach pains so switched to goats products.
Age 16 - got braces.
Age 17 - started taking birth control pill to help with painful periods (took for 18 months).
Age 18 - out on a lot of weight as result of the pill and then followed years of emotional eating to help control weight, including obsessive exercise, anorexia and bullemic episodes and later used sport and food to control emotions and continues to this day.
Age 21 - started taking birth control pill again and did so for 13 years.
Age 34 - developed Nephrotic Syndrome after end of 13 year relationship with someone suffering from depression. Had to take steroids and immunosuppressant drugs to help combat it.
Age 39 - found out I had hypothyroidism. No treatment.
Age 40 - diagnosed with stage 3a breast cancer - 2 lumps in one breast and had full clearance of lymph nodes on one side. Had chemo, radio, double mastectomy, 2 years of hormone suppressing drugs to shut down my ovaries.
Then followed with 2 slipped discs (L3/4 L4/5) and found out I was hypermobile. Used pilates to help my back strengthen.
Age 41 - refused to take Tamoxifen as felt the effects of all the rest of the drugs on my system. Turned to functional dr who put me on a strict cleansing regime of enemas, infared sauna, etc plus diet of no sugar, dairy, all organic, no transfats. Tried ketogenic but didn't work well for me so changed back to low sugar and mostly vegan lifetstyle.
Age 42 - following fathers death my health went downhill again and 'adrenal fatigue' got worse (stage 3 apparently). Sleep was awful and was sometimes getting 3 hrs a night. Started useing high quality cannnabis oil to help with cancer prevention, which also helped me sleep but think it made me depressed.
Discovered I have problems methylating and have double positive chromosomes CBS/C699T along with single positive MTHFR/A1298c/C677 T/3/A66G/11, BHMT 8, COMT V158M, COMT H62H, MACA R297R, NO D298E, VDR/FOK1
Age 43 - went to Thailand for 5 months to do an Ayurvedic cleanse and yoga retreat and ended up staying at Melanie Proctor's amazing raw food retreat in Phuket. Learnt about effects of light from her and tried to wear glasses less and look at sun more. Sadly I had to come back to London, where my health has deteriorated following more grief, a reconstruction operation 5 weeks, worsening cataracts, various viruses etc.

Now - I've tried to get back on the Ketogenic diet again in June 2016 but have found it hard. In fact it intensified my disordered eating and I have put on fat and lost muscle. I seem to be putting on weight at the moment and can't seem to shift it. I have difficulty exercising as 5 weeks out of my recent breast recon and my back has been playing up again so can't do any jumping or even yoga, just a bit of stretching and pilates. I try to swim when I can (not using my arms) however the pools around here are not that nice and full of chlorine.

Recent discussions with my functional dr and your blogs have led me to postpone the cataract op for a few months to see if I can improve things with some drops. I try to get natural light to my eyes in the morning although in London it's tricky and now there isn't any! Also, as I am -14 in both eyes with a cataract in my right eye (where I also had a retina lasered when it was damaged a few years ago) it is tricky to not wear my contact lenses. I am also starting to need reading glasses. I use Flux and take the blue light off my iPhone and try not to use it after 8pm. I also used blue blocker although this is tricky with my glasses at night. My sleep has improved although I was using cannabis oil again on my gums and this has made me feel quite depressed. I'm going to start using it anally as have heard there are no side effects and it helps cancer prevention.

I am hoping to leave London and am considering Portugal and fly there next week to check it out although with a fiance tied to his job in London this makes life hard. I am aware my health must come first though and he understands, especially after seeing my health deteriorate here. I also find I am not as calm in London as when abroad in the sun and by the sea.

I should also say I have a very addictive personality and as well as using food to emotionally regulate me I have used alcohol and drugs in the past, however since being diagnosed with cancer I obviously no longer do this.

Finally, I try to have a cold shower every morning and am trying to do intermittant fasting but as I have low blood pressure and thyroid problem I find this hard. I am trying to do the ketogenic diet still but find it very hard.

After blood tests in August my dr said I was slightly anaemic, too low lymphocytes, too low creatinine, too high VIT D, too low Gamma GT, and too low thyroid. I am doing new bloods next week.

I attach my functional drs list of supplements I've been on since July 1st this year. He is changing this now after a recent chat with him. He said a lot of my problems are emotional - he's right - and I don't need to take too many supplements as my system can't handle it.

I am 161cm and 52kg with a small frame.

I look forward to hearing from you.

Emma

List of supplements I've been on since July 1st until my op on Septemeber 15th. Waiting for a new list from my dr now.

Before Breakfast

1) Oilswill with Olive Oil and clove and Myrrh.

2) Liverflush (Olive oil, ½ lemon and water with 100 drops of Candida Yeast, 2 750g Malic acid, 20 Phosdrops). Take with 2 Artichoke and 2 Bio Silymarin

3) Immediately followed by TMAZ (1 teaspoons in water).

4) Immediately followed by Parasite Cleanse. 45 Artemesia and Cloves, 45 drops Black Walnut tincture in a glass with 1 Kyolic Garlic (30 min away from food)

5) Cilantro – 15 - 30 mins before Chlorella. 3 droppers (3 ml) 3 times a day in LARGE glass of water.

6) Chlorella 15 - 30 mins before breakfast.

7) Charcoal – 2 first in morning.

8) Biofilm Defense – 1 first in morning.

9) Serrazyme 250.000 IU - 3 first morning -3 last night.

10)Laminine - 2 first in morning and 2 last at night (with the others at that time)(crosses the BBB).

11)Liposomol Glutathione – 1 teaspoon first thing in morning.

12)Restorelife – 1 tablespoon with 2 scoops of Primal Defense just before breakfast.

13)Breakfast – with Enduracell 2 and the following:

14)HHIZymes - 2-2-2 (2 breakfast, 2 lunch, 2 dinner)

15)Nature Thyroid 1/2 grain - 1 in the morning with breakfast (I'm now taking 1 and 1/4 grains of nature thyroid each morning)

16)DHEA 5 mg - 1 in the morning with breakfast

17)Immune Senescence Protection Formula - 2-0-2

18)Holy Basil -Lotus -Bacopa - 1-0-1

19)Inflamaway - 1-0-1

22)Triple Action cruciferous veg with resveratrol - 1-0-1

23)Xanthomega -1-0-1

24)Salvestrol platinum - 2-0-2

25)Biosilymarin 500 mg -b2-0-2

26)Ginger extract - 2-0-2

27)Berberine Thorne 500 mg – 2-0-2

28)Mix – 30 mins after breakfast. 2 teaspoons Pectasol-C powder; 1 sachet Carnosine Komplex ; 10 drops Fulvic Acid; 2 teaspoons Black seed oil Plus; 10 drops Vit D3/K Thorne ; 1 tablespoon liposomal Curcumin ; 4 scoops MCT oil powder ( [questnutrition.com](http://questnutrition.com) ): all together (mixture) in a large glass of water, 1/2 h AFTER breakfast AND dinner or with a (separate) juice (see below) or if you have eaten nothing from 1/2 h after food and have no intent to eat for 2 hours after taken the mixture.

29)Magnesium Flakes ½ oz in morning to be rubbed on chest arms and legs.

30)Silverspray – 5 sprays under tongue in morning.

Before Dinner

33)Cilantro - 15 - 30 mins before Chlorella. 3 droppers (3 ml) 3 times a day in LARGE glass of water.

34)Chlorella - 30 mins before dinner

35)Restorelife – 1 tablespoon with 2 scoops of Primal Defense just before dinner.

36)Dinner – with Enduracell 2 and the following:

37)HHIZymes - 2-2-2

38)Immune Senescence Protection Formula - 2-0-2

39)Immiflex - 0-0-2

40)Holy Basil -Lotus -Bacopa - 1-0-1

41)Inflamaway - 1-0-1

44)Triple Action cruciferous veg with resveratrol - 1-0-1

45)Xanthomega -1-0-1

46)Salvestrol platinum - 2-0-2

47)Biosilymarin 500 mg - 2-0-2

48)Ginger extract - 2-0-2

49)Berberine Thorne 500 mg – 2-0-2

50)Mix – 30 mins after breakfast. 2 teaspoons Pectasol-C powder; 1 sachet Carnosine Komplex ; 10 drops Fulvic Acid; 2 teaspoons Black seed oil Plus; 10 drops Vit D3/K Thorne ; 1 tablespoon liposomal Curcumin ; 4 scoops MCT oil powder ( [questnutrition.com](http://questnutrition.com) ): all together (mixture) in a large glass of water, 1/2 h AFTER breakfast AND dinner or with a (separate) juice (see below) or if you have eaten nothing from 1/2 h after food and have no intent to eat for 2 hours after taken the mixture.

Before Bed

51)Magnesium Flakes – ½ oz in evening to be rubbed on chest, arms and legs.

52)Colon cleanse – before bed. ½ cup of fresh apple juice, ½ tablespoon apple cider vinegar, 2 teaspoons of liquid chlorophyll, 1/8 teaspoon Aloe Vera Concentrate, 1 heaping teaspoon of Psyllium Husk, 1 capsule of Cascara Sagrada, I teaspoon of TMAZ. Follow by another glass of water

53)Cilantro - 30 mins before Chlorella. 3 droppers (3 ml) 3 times a day in LARGE glass of water.

54)Chlorella – 15 - 30 mins before bed.

55)TMAZ 1 teaspoon before bed.

56)Serrazyme 250.000 IU - 3 first morning -3 last night.

57)Laminine - 2 first in morning and 2 last at night .

58)Charcoal – 2 last at night.

59)Biofilm Defense – 1 last at night.

60)Liposomol Glutathione – 1-teaspoon last thing at night.

61)Oilswill.

62)Melatonin and Omega V3 – chew 3 (30mg) and mix with 2 teaspoons of Omega V3. Absorb under the tongue for 5 mins and then swallow the rest.

63)CBD Oil - 2 full droppers under the tongue, leave for a minute and then swallow.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-05-18T02:53:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/poster-girl-optimal-diary.18647/post-241478>

Another new blog is out on the developing NAD+ blue light story from Patreon and how it all links to the story of hydrogen and the sun. The chronic loss of NAD+is the critical sign of a loss of negative feedback control of the ubiquitin cycle. This data scales directly to our molecular circadian clock and our peripheral clock genes (CCG's). The LCHF cancer researchers are way off on their beliefs about the demonization of glucose in most cancers. This one links to the May 2018 webinar and you do not want to miss the links.

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/18855002](https://www.patreon.com/posts/18855002)

I told you I would be proven right about breast cancer is due to a lack of sunlight on one side of the circadian mechanism. Breast cancer is a clear-cut circadian disease tied to a LACK of sunlight on the female breast. In 2015 I did a string of 3 webinars for my members on this very topic in 2015. In them, I said the current model of the mammalian circadian clock includes two interlocking transcription-translation feedback loops comprised of several so-called “clock” genes and their protein products (info quanta), which ultimately regulate the transcription of “clock-controlled” genes. These feedback loops consist of positive and negative components. I believed the negative feedback component should be the target of research. The positive feedback components of the peripheral clock genes include proteins called the CLOCK and BMAL1 proteins. The negative feedback control proteins components are called PERIOD 1 & 2 and CRYPTOCHROME 1 & 2. The E-box circadian mechanism proteins link both sides of this couple. Ladies with the BRCA genes better pay deep attention. Now we have this paper from Texas A&M showing you my educated guess based on my work was DEAD ON. I hope all ladies forget ever buying anything pink ever again and go out and change their behavior and the behavior of the females in their family. [https://scienmag.com/texas-am-study-links-breast-cancer-to-the-bodys-internal-clock/](https://scienmag.com/texas-am-study-links-breast-cancer-to-the-bodys-internal-clock/)

### Anton Chigur — 2016-10-24T12:32:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-structuring-functional-state-corrector-emf-defense.18649/post-203254>

Hey Guys,

What do you think about water structuring? Here in Russia we have a scientist who used to work on Russian space shuttle program (Buran) and when the program closed he developed so called Functional State Corrector, basically he claims that he recorded some frequencies of curable plants on the small plastic plate and this plate can structure water and cure illness, defend from EMF, etc. I have 3 of such plates, I use them, but do not feel any effect. Probably I do not fully believe in these things. My wife is very deep into Qigong and she feels everything very well, she says that she can feel Iphone EMFs in the pocket but if she puts this plate with Iphone she does not feel it. Anyway, please take a look:
[http://en.kfs-korrektor.ru/fsc.html](http://en.kfs-korrektor.ru/fsc.html)

# Thread: The Vit D3 conundrum.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-vit-d3-conundrum.18652/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3

### K123 — 2016-10-24T23:51:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-vit-d3-conundrum.18652/post-203269>

I'm Indian dark skinned and quite overweight right now and newly moved to the pacific northwest. The Dminder app is telling me that my next opportunity to get Vit D from the sun is in 115 days!! Is that a mistake?
I understand that it is best to get Vit D through the sun but if you are in a place where there wouldn't be any sun anytime soon and you fall in the category of people who do need a lot of sun, should you be taking d3 supplementation.
I used to supplement because I was low. Then I read it is best to get sun so I was trying my best to do that this summer but now the sun has all but disappeared it feels and I feel a but nervous to think I will be getting no vit d for several months according to Dminder. HELP please if you understand what is the right thing for someone with my stats.

### Rubicon — 2016-10-25T00:40:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-vit-d3-conundrum.18652/post-203270>

115 days is not a mistake, it's now vitamin D winter in the pacific northwest. You need UVB light to make vitamin D. At sea level, UVB light only reaches the surface when the sun's altitude is above 30 degrees and that won't happen for you for 115 days...where I'm at in Canada it's going to be 135 days

I'm not planning to supplement any vitamin D this winter since it's only the chemical signal of sun exposure, and it misses the photonic power. Instead, I plan to use a ExoTerra Solar Glo Reptile light for a few minutes every day and occasionally visit a tanning salon that has beds with UVB light. I'm also planning to spend as much time south of the tropic of cancer to get some natural sun.

Other strategies to stay healthy when there's no UV light is to do CT & ground as much as possible, and avoid carbs to stay in seasonal ketosis until the sun returns.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-25T01:07:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-vit-d3-conundrum.18652/post-203272>

[https://www.jackkruse.com/time-10-can-you-supplement-sunlight/](https://www.jackkruse.com/time-10-can-you-supplement-sunlight/)

Hi everyone,
I was wondering if anyone had any ideas about any promising jobs or careers that are closest to being optimal. In other words, jobs that involve optimal conditions (e.g., jobs that don't involve high levels of non-native EMF exposure or high levels of harmful artificial lighting, etc.). I have some ideas, but I would love to hear from others.

I would be interested and grateful to hear of ideas in general, but I would also be grateful to hear of any ideas on my particular case. I am 22 years old, I graduated last year with bachelor's degrees in philosophy and in French; but I am also electrohypersensitive (EHS), so that really limits my options. Before realizing I was EHS, I was planning on studying philosophy at the graduate level and becoming a teacher or professor in philosophy. I don't see that as an option anymore because I would not tolerate EMF levels in classrooms with students with all their wireless gadgets. I can still use technology; I am typing on a keyboard just fine right now, but I can only tolerate using wired technology, that is grounded, and that is low in dirty electricity. Thank you in advance for any suggestions.

### JanSz — 2016-11-16T23:33:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-jobs-careers.18654/post-204003>

One way to look at it.
Bet on USA.
Assume that USA, your bank and your broker will newer go under.
Figure out life style that is acceptable to you (anywhere on the world, where you will have ability to reach your bank and broker when you must pay your bills or re-balance account).
Find out how much you must spent a year to have that life (today).
Multiply that by 30.
That is the sum you must save or get.
That is the sum that will set you free.
The smaller will satisfy you, the faster you can get to your goal.

Take 5 years worth and put it on checking account, this is your spending money for 5 years.
Take 25 years worth and buy SPY.
Attempt to re-balance above once a year except for years when S&P500 is below its 200 SMA
When 200SMA is under, remember that it will not last forever.
.
When re-balancing take all that you have on both accounts, divide by 30, put 5 portions on checking account.
\------------------
Example
Yearly living expenses=100k
You need 3mil to be free

Yearly living expenses=30k
You need 900k to be free.
\----
This should newer run out of $$.
You can live until 150yo or more without financial worries.

### Anton Chigur — 2016-10-25T11:45:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antons-journal.18655/post-203289>

Hey Guys,

I am starting my own journal here, I have read Jack's book and key articles on the blog. The key takeaway is that my lifestyle is pretty fucked up in terms of light, EMFs. I am an office clerk so I stare all day at a blue computer screen. Hopefully, I drink water from my own well. I am 30 and do not have any particular health problems except chronic Epstein Barr Virus which leads to high level of chronc inflamation (I am ill at least 5 years). I took horse doses of valcyclovir for 2 months and the virus stepped back, now 2 months later it is in full play again. This bothers me a bit as EBV might lead to various unpleasant things and I would like to solve this. I also have chronic rhinitis due to EBV which causes allergic reaction. Suprisingly having cut out all carbs and fruits, my rhinitis got much better very quickly already and I can breath through my nose, that is a small win))

So my near term goal is to get rid of EBV, my long term goal - I believe new lifestyle will allow me to make better choices, think clearer and I will leave my dull jobs and start my own project, I have a couple of ideas already.

I will do Epi paleo (plus lots of protein for immunity), CT and will sort out mess with light and will let you know how the things are going on. Not a lot of seafood in Moscow but salmon roe is quite accesible so I will focus on it)

I have done CT already, I did it in May and I am doing it now, unsuprisingly it is much easier to do CT when it is October and is snowing outside, so I can do 40 minutes in 10 degress C and I do not even tremble afterwards ang get warm, but in May it was much tougher.
Up to the point CT has given me flare up of the virus as I have a sore throat but the sleep is increadible.

Soon I will also install floating chamber in my house and this will be very beneficial I hope.

GRAPHENE'S SUPERCONDUCTIVE POWER HAS FINALLY BEEN UNLOCKED, AND IT'S CRAZIER THAN WE EXPECTED

It's official: graphene has been made into a superconductor in its natural state - which means electrical current can flow through it with zero resistance.

Last year, physicists managed to do this by doping graphene with calcium atoms, but this is the first time researchers have achieved superconductivity in the material without having to alter it. And the results so far show that the material achieves an incredibly rare type of superconductivity that's even crazier and more powerful than scientists expected.

[http://www.sciencealert.com/graphen...en-unlocked-and-it-s-crazier-than-we-expected](http://www.sciencealert.com/graphene-s-superconductive-power-has-finally-been-unlocked-and-it-s-crazier-than-we-expected)

If anyone wants to know the cause to cancer on Earth the answer is buried right here. Salmonella and mitochondria have something in common. Both are bacteria at their core. So what happens when you put then in a highly energized environment not connected to Earth or the sun cycles? They under go massive growth. Big lesson here. When you live on the surface of a microwaved blue lit planet might this be why mitochondria undergo more virulent changes in our cells to cause humans to get cancer, a mitochondrial disease? It should no longer be a mystery unless your mind is addicted to a blue light device and you cannot think well. I hope y'all get this message. [https://www.washingtonpost.com/news...ria-get-dangerously-weird-in-space/?tid=sm\_tw](https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/to-your-health/wp/2016/10/25/bacteria-get-dangerously-weird-in-space/?tid=sm_tw)

What is salmonella? It is a species of bacteria. A mitochondira is derived from a bacteria. Got it? What happens to bacteria in space? They grow like mad. Why? When both are disconnected from the native magnetic field of Earth and solar cycles completely this sets up a pro-growth environment in things derived from bacteria. OK........where did a mitochondria come from at the Cambrian explosion? Endosymbiosis. It came from the bacterial kingdom 600 million years ago. So this means a bacteria is very much like Salamonella linked in the story above and therefore is very similar in how it replicates with respet to a mitochondria. Where are human mitochondria located? In all cells but RBC's. They formed on Earth via evolution under the power of the visible solar spectral frequencies and the native magnetic field. Today the Earth is no long in its native state because humans have made its surface alien by electrifying it around 1880. What else did we do to make the surface of the Earth more space like? Along with an electrified surface, the ionosphere is now filled with RF and microwaves and the night is now blue lit and everyone buries the sun by wear clothing, sunscreen, living indoors under blue light, while wearing sunglasses. These environmental changes man has created have simulated the disconnection of space mentioned in the link above..........so since we know a bacteria undergoes virulent explosive growth in space what might you think would happen to a mitochondria in the same kind of situation on Earth surface today? Might mitochondria undergo explosive growth on the surface of Earth today like bacteria did in space? I think so. What is cancer called? A loss of growth control in cells, isn't it. Simple analogy to help you understand why cancer has exploded since 1880.

### Scompy — 2016-11-11T01:29:47+0200

As a game designer, I think of these processes from more of a "MOBA" (e-sports) type of archetype POV a lot of the time. I'll simplify it down to a basketball or football team. If there is a weak link on the team, it's wise to find someone that will fill in the role for team-survival to win. Similarly, if there is no one on the MOBA team with the ability to play the role in the first place, you've gotta go search out some other resource to help you out, like a potion, elixir or new item to adjust to the negative situation. But that's more of a last-case scenario because it costs resource to acquire new resources -- a gamble of sorts -- for overall survival into the future to win. I think there is something to be said when there is more volume of mitochondria overall so there is more respiratory potential, even though the larger system has to also be supported. Same goes for a 'Super-Mitochondria' or a 'Hero in a MOBA' for a comparison or a Michael Jordan that is much more robust and skillful and ultimately brings about more survival for the entire team. I think nature has already found a way in all of these regards... makes me curious about mito-to-mito comparisons, which heteroplasmy appears to be a measurestick of that.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-12T17:09:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cancer-as-a-mitochondrial-disease.18660/post-203884>

What is the ultimate link?

Electromagnetism can be discussed in two ways: #1 in terms of the field and #2 in terms of the radiation. The field is something that exists in space around an object that produces it. An electric field extends outward from electrically charged objects. Both magnetic and electric fields are static and unvarying. When time is introduced to them, by varying the intensity of the field, like a radio antenna, an electromagnetic field is the result. This kind of field fluctuates often and emits waves of energy that behave as massless and chargeless (photons) energies. Each energy wave consists of an electric and magnetic field at right angles to one another. The energy wave generated is traveling at right angles to both the magnetic and electric field. This gives it a 3 dimensional look when you reconstruct the view in your minds eye. This is precisely how the brain reconstructs time. The number of waves formed in one second is called the frequency of the EMF wave. The distance the energy travels during one oscialltion is the wavelength. The higher the frequency, the shorter the wavelength is, and vice versa.

EMF’s span large frequencies. Gamma rays are very small. They are a tenth of a billionth millimeter long and vibrate at sextillions of times a second. They are related to Xrays, short wave UV radiation are called ionizing radiation. There photons are so powerful that they can knock out electrons from atoms and cause highly reactive ions to be formed where they should not be. The next band of EMF’s are the longer wave UV radiation and then we hit EMF we see in visible light. Infrared radiation comes next and we feel it as heat. Below the infrared spectrum is the area of EMF’s is where modern communications frequencies are found. Here, microwaves (giga or mega Hz), and radio frequencies exist. The RF (radio frequency) spectrum is broken up into many smaller groups with names you know (UHF, VHF and ELF). Extremely low frequency (ELF) waves are where the Schumann resonance is below 10 Hz. At 10 Hz, one wave is 18,600 miles long. The key point is, except for visible light and infrared EMF, we can not perceive any of these energies without instruments designed to find them. The problem is our brain senses them all without sensation being perceived consciously. WifI frequencies vary by country law: 2.4 GHz (802.11b/g/n) Most countries. United States. Interference concerns.
3.65 GHz (802.11y)
4.9 GHz (802.11j) public safety WLAN.
5 GHz (802.11a/h/j/n/ac)
5.9 GHz (802.11p)
60 GHz (802.11ad)
900 MHz (802.11ah)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-14T05:10:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cancer-as-a-mitochondrial-disease.18660/post-203940>

My Ubiquitination 21 blog post discussed the details of how circadian mismatches cause cancer over a year ago. Now we have another post deepening the data to show cancer is an environmental disease and not really a genetic one. Let us quickly review why is the bending of blue light a modern problem? It has massive implications of how fast or slow the circadian clock in the central retina operates. It is driven by blue light detection via melanopsin. These neurons project directly and exclusively to the suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN). Light synchronizes mammalian circadian rhythms with environmental time by modulating retinal input to the circadian pacemaker— SCN of the hypothalamus. Such photic entrainment requires neither rods nor cones, which until very recently, they were the only known retinal photoreceptors. We covered the details of the eye clock earlier in this series. Today, we know that retinal ganglion cells innervating the SCN are intrinsically photosensitive. Unlike other ganglion cells, they depolarized in response to light even when all synaptic input from rods and cones was physically blocked. Their sensitivity, is tied to spectral tuning by the frequency of light. Because blue light bends more than any other frequency, it develops a slow kinetic of this light response; it matches those of the photic entrainment mechanism, this is how we figured out that these ganglion cells were be the primary photoreceptors for this system.
Solar radiation directly link to the ubiquitin protein that control protein replacement in humans. This occurs initially via this eye clock timing mechanism. How? Color and frequency, is the short answer. All crytpochromes that are clock timers also use blue light flavin pigments. All proteins are either flurophores or chromophores. This means proteins have the ability to both absorb, emit, refract, and scatter light. The most important ability in mitochondria is fluorescence and absorption of light. Fluorescence is the property of some atoms and molecules to absorb light at a particular wavelength and to subsequently emit light of longer wavelength after a brief interval, termed the fluorescence lifetime. The process of phosphorescence occurs in a manner similar to fluorescence, but with a much longer excited state lifetime. These molecular processes can even be used as redox measures in the color of human teeth. This implies that tooth color might even be used as a predictor of oncogenic risk. I am head to New York this week to lecture on this very topic. The fluorescence process is governed by three important events, all of which occur on timescales that are separated by several orders of magnitude (see Table 1 above). These timescales are extraordinarily fast because protein side chains work at the quantum scale. Moreover, this is why the 3 D atomic array must be precise, sensitive, and specific for optimized function in the respiratory proteins on the inner mitochondrial membrane. Excitation of a susceptible molecule by an incoming photon happens in femtoseconds (10-15 seconds), while vibrational relaxation of excited state electrons to the lowest energy level is much slower and can be measured in picoseconds (10-12 seconds). The final process, emission of a longer wavelength photon and return of the molecule to the ground state, occurs in the relatively long time period of nanoseconds (10-9 seconds). Although the entire molecular fluorescence lifetime, from excitation to emission, is measured in only billionths of a second, the phenomenon is a stunning manifestation of the interaction between light and matter that forms the basis for the expansive fields of steady state and time-resolved fluorescence spectroscopy and microscopy. Because of the tremendously sensitive emission profiles, spatial resolution, and high specificity of fluorescence investigations, the technique is rapidly becoming an important tool in genetics and cell biology. This is good news for people realize QED is going be the key breakthrough for modern medicine and dentistry. [http://caloriesproper.com/circadian-rhythms-and-cancer-potential-mechanisms/](http://caloriesproper.com/circadian-rhythms-and-cancer-potential-mechanisms/)

When I practice both a lowered glutamate diet with Keto and/or IF, it appears that the weight comes sliding off of me quickly. The moment I add back glutamate-rich foods, I can stall easily and/or the weight piles back on quickly when I overeat... Calcium-rich foods also ties to this in my observations with myself. So what is my link? I did a 23andme and know I'm broken with glutamate clearing...I suspect there is a slower time it takes my blood-glutamate to baseline again. Also, many calcium receptors are broken according to GeneticGenie. I am pretty darn sure this is due to my many decades playing around with nnEMFs. I'm a good lab rat for the medical community to analyze because of what I do. Increasing redox potential is absolutely critical... because the root issue are the nnEMFs, and to fix the epigenetic sensing to the environment, it may be years before things get more optimal so I can get away from such a military-type of eating schedule.

One diagnostic/monitor has been my morning-breath when I'm doing keto. The mornings I wake up and my wife wants to slap me silly with toxic-dragon-breath is when the previous day I had limited both my glutamates and my sugars to just about nothing...meaning, I get better fat-burning, and I suspect that my pancreas isn't being told to output as much insulin, so more ketone bodies can form. In the late spring and summer when I relax keto, and bring in more carbs, I find the fat-levels remain about the same unless I take in some alcohol or increase the glutamate. ### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-03T02:53:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cancer-as-a-mitochondrial-disease.18660/post-204686>

Is sunlight nature's wireless vaccine for cancer? I think it is and I have company now.

Cancer is a loss of growth control. Since man moved indoors out of the sun cancer incidence and prevalence have exploded. This is a 200 year old story. It turns out nature prescribes that no life ever grows properly until it is focused, dedicated, disciplined by the sun's light. I believe heart disease and inflammatory bowel diseases are pre-cancerous states. I also believe autoimmunity is a precancerous state. I believe autoimmunity and cancer are just steps apart. I also believe we will soon find that these diseases are linked to an altered light environment and bad circadian signaling. Epidemiological studies link all these disease to inflammation generation. The pathway in humans tied to inflammation is the NRF2. In the June 2010, American Journal of Pathology published a study that caught my eye. It reviewed at the Vitamin D Receptor (VDR) and the generation of inflammation in the large intestine in a mouse model. The paper looked at gut inflammation in the presence of a Salmonella infection. Salmonella is a bacterial infection and mice are nocturnal. Mice which lacked VDR’s, seemed to have higher states of gut inflammation and worse outcomes than control groups. They key difference however was the high levels of IL-6, an inflammatory cytokine. IL-6 is tied directly to the NrF2 pathway. IL-6 is a bad actor in cellular homeostasis and in circadian signaling. It is seen in most cancers and all inflammatory conditions in humans to some degree. Most gut absorption is done via the transcellular pathway. (90% through enterocytes) The remainder of gut transport occurs via intracellular methods (tight gut junctions) that are controlled by sulfated vitamin D3 status. The less sulfated Vitamin D3 you have in these tissues, the more leaky your tight junctions will be and this exposes you immune system in the GALT to direct assault. This assault in the small bowel is then aimed directly at our liver because of how the circulatory system diverts blood to the liver for processing and detoxification. Our liver's redox potential is key. This is why Doug Wallace called our liver a proxy for sunlight's action. And our liver has to defend the rest of our body from inflammatory cytokines, as I laid out long ago in the VAP blog on my website.

The key finding in the article was that animals with an intact VDR, faced less gut inflammation and had lower rates of Salmonella infection. The VDR mechanism of action is used to block the signal transduction of NF-Kb (911 signal of the cell also from the nrf2 pathway) from the cell membrane to the nucleus of the cell. Once it gets into the nucleus it binds to DNA to promote many inflammatory pathways that lead to more cytokine production. One pathway leads to IL-1b production seen in all autoimmune conditions. The other pathway leads to IL-6 and TNF alpha generation that alter the promotor region of p53 gene (protector gene of our entire genome) by hypo-methylation. Cobalamin (Vitamin B12) is an adenosyl- or methyl-donating cofactor for many enzymes, yet many proteins with unknown or nonenzymatic function also contain B12-binding domains. Recent studies have shown us that light excitation energy can promote covalent linkage of B12 to transcription factors with this linkage, affecting gene expression. Thus, B12 now has a newly described regulatory function that few physicians even know about. Most alternative health practioners paradigm has no clue about how these link to the epigenome. Sunlight, not supplements is the key driver of these cycles. B12 is a photochemical stimulated by sunlight just as Vitamin D3 is. Most people deficient in sunlight will have low B12 and D3 levels and this is especially true in cancer. This lowered methylation allows for tissues to build a heteroplasmy state in cells to favor cellular oncogenesis. If you bury the sun, the light “switch” is not operational to run this system in cells. VDR and D3 up regulates a protein called lkB which won’t allow the NF-k beta to enter the nucleus of the cell to set the plan of oncogenesis to commence in action. It also appears that the VDR does not even require active vitamin D3 to be present for this to occur in this pathway. This makes sense because sunlight on the skin is the stimulus that up-regulates the VDR receptor in mammals because 99% of D3 is made by light and not food. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25207372](https://www.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov%2Fpubmed%2F25207372&h=sAQEVuwlHAQFInihAzhn8yUUAQm3g4WrdgNvGqqEN9muMjQ&enc=AZNVTdf_sUkaCiAxlk4ZbIoIj4xTLWHLvFoVZ6RHcHE-XC_AjLLF94Rqkv6kchilUYoHv7mQ3rFgWNYiVLm_KM4ACRoLUKbQ7MXoeYZAUBHsXa4u_FV3NVMi3hAdq4km5mKGCkfbkLsVXohujW6vvYTUQNsswU1tIK2v9A8FFVD7f_GNZFwvpcYzfsr0RSZYmJPOxg21ozjdC-6-jdFKowXC&s=1)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-03T03:16:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cancer-as-a-mitochondrial-disease.18660/post-204688>

Today's fun factoid: The highest cancer rate for men and women together was found in Denmark with 338 people per 100,000 being diagnosed in 2012. The age-standardized rate was at least 300 per 100,000 for nine countries of Denmark, France, Australia, Belgium, Norway, United States of America, Ireland, Republic of Korea and The Netherlands.
Take a look at the their latitude and altitude in Denmark. Denmark is high latitude and flat which means there is no UV-B light. Did you also know Danes are tall? Could it be that they are like plants and trying to grow toward the light? Sounds ridiculous doesn't it? Of course it does.........but is it? You should also know they are the tallest people in the world. This puts their "eye clock" a further distance from every peripheral clock in front of every gene in every cell of their body. From a biophysical standpoint this means by the laws of general and special realitivity mechanisms alone, their risks of cancer should be highest because they are both tall and live at a high latitude where no UV light is common. What if we look to the literature what does it is say? Well, here is the shocker........their cancer rates are higher, a lot higher. Why are you surprised? The laws of physics are universal. They might offend your common sense but common sense is useless in quantum mechanics. Common sense in an uncommon degree is what the world calls wisdom. Now that we went for this random walk through bio-physics now come tell me the sun does not matter in wellness or trying to avoiding cancer. I've got a bridge to sell you too. Check the cite in the picture if you think this is nuts.

### seanb4 — 2016-10-26T12:08:20+0300

\-What wavelengths are used?

\-What proteins do these wavelengths interact with and what effects do they produce?

\-Can you use it too much, what would be the upper limit?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-10-26T17:03:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantlet-questions.18662/post-203336>

1\. Patent protected.
2\. All
3\. Yes. We do not think you should sleep with it.

### smith5 — 2016-11-11T14:21:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantlet-questions.18662/post-203828>

1\. Can I place an order now and make a paymet/get a shipment later?
2\. If so, will the full description of the device (frequency range, power, etc) be available attached to the device?
3\. What are main classes of evidence it is based on and what statistical weight do they have relative to the device function?
4\. Can cold and light be used interchangeably by switching off one or the other? Like for example if you are traveling in the Mediterranean or Asia with plenty of sunlight and would need only cold.
5\. So the thermoelectric effect is used by the Quantlet only to produce its own cooling surface, which would extract body heat using skin temperature sensors, right? Does it have any batteries for of that effect to operate or how is it self-sustainable?

Thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-11T17:30:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantlet-questions.18662/post-203835>

1\. You can buy it and tell them when and where you want it shipped.
2\. Yes
3\. This is covered on the Quantlet website. Stanford and Harvard studies were used in development. Please view them.
4\. Yes
5\. It has batteries.

Like for example, to program light frequencies be emitted in succession or simultaneously for variable time intervals while recording the temperature data on an external memory?
7\. Can the bandages for wearing on the wrist be removed and replaced with other provided bandages to fix the device on other ares (like, e.g. acupuncture points)?

6\. Can its light function be programmable on variable time intervals simultaneously and be used in conjunction only with skin temperature sensor for real-time recording on an external media ? **YES.** Like for example, to program light frequencies be emitted in succession or simultaneously for variable time intervals while recording the temperature data on an external memory? **YES**
7\. Can the bandages for wearing on the wrist be removed and replaced with other provided bandages to fix the device on other ares (like, e.g. acupuncture points)? **IT IS ADJUSTABLE TO A DEGREE. DEPENDS UPON YOUR ANATOMY

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-13T16:00:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantlet-questions.18662/post-203924>

We use 850nm frequency and the user can vary the pulse rate via the software we are including in the launch versions of the device. Most people have not been shown this. Our partners have. It will be our surprise gift for the time delays we had to go through with the global battery nightmare tied to Samsung. We do not share power density because that is part of our patent and proprietary information.

For example:

Light and electricity are one and the same thing fundamentally but they have some nuance. Electric current is more bulky to work with because electrons have mass and have to be controlled. Light is made of photons and they have no mass. If you do not believe light and electric current have something in common why do we have something called the photoelectric effect. Take a look at this video and notice where the device that uses electric currents and vibrating motors are. They are on the wrist. [http://www.bbc.com/news/magazine-38208814](http://www.bbc.com/news/magazine-38208814)
Anyone remember we built the Quantlet to work with your wrist to use light and cooling photoelectrically? [www.thequantlet.com](http://www.thequantlet.com)

More important
how to figure optimal hydration
how to manipulate to get there
''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''
**Human hydration indices: acute and longitudinal reference values.**
[http://www.europeanhydrationinstitute.org/human\_hydration.html](http://www.europeanhydrationinstitute.org/human_hydration.html)

Results indicated that the mean total fluid intake in beverages, pure water, and solid foods was >2.1 L/24 hr (range 1.382-3.261, 95% confidence interval **0.970-3.778 L/24 hr**);
mean urine volume was >1.3 L/24 hr (0.875-2.250 and **0.675-3.000 L/24 hr**);

### JanSz — 2016-10-29T15:25:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-a-short-question-re-sauna.18665/post-203426>

Excluding some stressful situations, dehydration is not about lack of water.
It is about loss of ability to hold or manage water.
We are mostly light + water.
So my questions would not be about supplemental Florinef (Fludrocortisone) or Vasopressin, antidiuretic hormone (ADH) use, but rather **why
Renin Angiotensin Aldosterone System
gets out of tune**
and how to bring it back to normal.
All while drinking 1-2L of water daily.
Forget about gallons.
I would exclude common and obvious:
fever, heat exposure, too much exercise,
vomiting, diarrhea, diabetes.
Likely anything that have ICD-10 code could be excluded (or put on hold).

What is left:
messed up Circadian Rhythm
nnEMF

Is dehydration a neolithic disease like Parkinson's, MS, ADHD??
Is it membrane problem or mitochondria or %heteroplasmy?

### Rik Vlasblom — 2016-11-14T11:31:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-posts-pdf-in-one-zip-file-for-download.18666/post-203947>

> [Jamie Ward said:](/goto/post?id=203942)
>
> I get this and I have tried on two computers?

It looks like the program you are using to unzip, does not have enough permission to do that.

What may help:
\- try another location
\- try another [program](https://www.lifewire.com/how-to-zip-and-unzip-files-and-folders-on-a-mac-2260188)
\- try another account
\- try another device

### Alain Dwight — 2024-12-22T03:11:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-posts-pdf-in-one-zip-file-for-download.18666/post-344239>

I ran a script to combine these all into a single PDF so I can drop it in chatGPT and get a budget Kruse LLM

Obviously not a replacement for reading the blogs, but can help with bouncing ideas and searching (if you ask for direct quotes). It seems to push the edge of the context window so I've gotten a bit better responses when directing it to a specific series eg. "Review Time series and provide one sentence thesis as well as supporting evidence."

### [kruse\_all\_free\_blogs.pdf](https://drive.google.com/file/d/14aHlyThxAyQpZerNN5XM-fL2JJLzd2xS/view?usp=sharing)

 drive.google.com

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-did-jack-mean-vielight.18668/post-203370>

I asked a question in the Ask Jack section about using intranasal red light therapy device, like the Vielight here: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/question-about-the-vielight.18535/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/question-about-the-vielight.18535/)

Jack answered that "he would not use one by default and that they need a context", but there was never any more explanation to this. Do anyone know what he meant by this?

My context for wanting to try out a device like the Vielight is that I have heard that is should be very beneficial and without any side effects if not overusing and since I have a job sitting by the computer (blue light) about 12 hours a day I think that it could be beneficial to get 20 minutes of red light at the end of the day?

It should not hurt at least?

I was actually under the impression that red light is always good and it was just blue light that should be avoided? But by the "not use one by default" I assume that is not always the case? I am a bit confused by this though.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-did-jack-mean-vielight.18668/post-205872>

He hasn't commented much on it aside from if you have any previous nasal issues such as a fixed deviated septum or issues in bone around the nose than it's not wise. But he has mentioned he has used it from time to time

# Thread: My Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.18669/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DebraGM — 2016-10-27T21:45:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.18669/post-203373>

I just turned 50 years old. I have a grad degree in OT and I am a board certified health coach and personal trainer and holistic cancer educator. I am a food guru! Or was, after reading Dr Kruse's information. I have had Hashimoto's for 5 years ( I take 35 mugs T3 2x/day) and low vitamin D and low ferritin. I have been very sick with low energy, low body temp, weight gain, hair falling out, generalized anxiety marked by claustrophobia and difficulty driving on highways. In other words, my world got very small.
I got some relief having my mercury fillings removed and by have IgG food testing done. I removed gluten, dairy, almonds, eggs, beans, peas, and shrimp, crab and lobster from my diet per the results.
I also took 5 mg of hydrocortisone 2x/day to let my adrenals rest for a few months.
I lost 20lbs in 3 weeks and lost another 15lbs over the next 2-3 months. My energy came back, I went back to the gym and looked great for the last 2 years. I am strict with limiting toxins, I don't use the microwave, all my food and products are organic etc

Then my parents both got cancer. With the stress I totally fell apart. Hashimoto's symptoms came back and I gained 15lbs in 2 months. I've gotten 5lbs off. But I feel fatigued and the last 10 won't budge! And I wasn't thin to begin with but my waist size was 27" at 5'6" so I was ok with my weight. I don't have the energy to workout hard, but I have been walking everyday. I lost a lot of my muscle that I worked hard for at the gym.

I have been getting as much sun as possible, putting my feet in the grass, and eating lots of high quality seafood. I have spring water delivered in glass bottles. I just started following
the Leptin Rx. I realized I snack more than I thought, I get nervous leaving the house without a snack in my purse, and I must eat at night!! Trying to change all that!
I am wearing orange glasses at night and when using iPhone but I am terribly addicted to my iPhone. I did get a case to block RFs (but that only works when it's closed and talking on the phone). My head buzzes from iPhone like I am plugged into an electrical outlet. I actually feel it through my whole body. I live in a townhouse development where we have smart meters! Luckily there is an open field across from me. I'm not in the middle of the development at least.

I desperately want my energy back, I'd like to lose 15-20lbs and I'd like my hair to be thicker!! I'm tired of taking thyroid hormones. I'm tired of my neurotransmitters feeling off!

I would like to know how to read this info in the safest matter to minimize EMFs.
I did buy a Coronet meter.
Thank you for reading!!

Rejoined the tribe. Unfortunately I have only gotten worse since 2016. A lot worse. I guess I wasn’t sick enough to make a commitment to myself. I am now. Coronavirus woke me up.

I am also a Director for a holistic cancer organization. I want to be a good example and a source of real truth (a Rooster). I gained 60lbs, and I have anxiety, claustrophobia, sleep issues, etc etc.
I started watching sunrises about 10 days ago, along with Leptin RX, elimination of ALAN, and sunning at solar noon or Sperti D light. It’s cold in Pa now.
I’ve been nervous about a possible shellfish allergy. I had shrimp last night for the first time and I woke up with reflux and my lungs are bothering me today . I’ve been eating wild salmon and tuna and grass fed beef and pastured pork and eggs.

I’m wondering if I need to relocate. Listing my genetic snips:

MTHFR 677++
MTHFR 1298 —
VDR Taq ++
VDR BSM+-
CBS C699T +-
CBS A360A +-
COMT V158M ++
COMT H62H ++

### Liam Taylor — 2016-11-15T20:51:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.18669/post-203982>

How are you getting on with that coronet meter?

Sucks re: those smart meters. Keep posting!

### fitness@home — 2016-11-16T17:47:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.18669/post-203995>

@[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) - you have a lot of experience with meters. Can you help?

### nonchalant — 2016-11-16T18:04:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.18669/post-203996>

@[DebraGM](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19441/) , for your iPhone addiction, I suggest you do what I did to get rid of my computer addiction. Do not touch it after a certain time at night. Then gradually make that time earlier and earlier, until you are not touching it after dark and before sunrise. It is difficult at first, but then the addiction just goes away. Make a paper list of things you want to discuss or investigate on it the next day, if necessary.

### Scompy — 2016-11-16T19:01:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.18669/post-203998>

I'm recommending now the 88T Cornet because it has electrical EMF detection as well. The 78s model is great too, but just doesn't do the electrical "dirty electricity". So if you have a TriField meter that already does electrical, that may be the way to go. It's around $130ish, and the 78s is about the same for around $260. That is versus the 88T that does everything. The TriField does RF poorly...more like broad strokes and you won't be able to pindown any Radio-frequency sources with it. For instance, you'll see the TriField ping up when you connect your cellphone to a celltower, but that's about all. Some cell towers in close proximity will get the TriField to start registering, so you can see my point about sensitivities. Instead, the Cornets are best for RF. The acoustameters are really, really good, but most people don't need that amount of detail with the RF for about $350 later.

How you use the Cornet matters...it can also output sound to find RF sources that you may not have considered. Don't pass off magnetic and electrical EMF sources either. It's easy to get stuck in a space that exceeds 3 milliGauss without even knowing it. For instance, pushing back your monitors even 1 inch may take you out of the field. Recently I bought an old-school vinyl record player and the magnetic fields were fine on it. However, the electrical EMFs ('dirty electricity') has about a 2 foot falloff from it. Knowing that, I have it tucked back in a place away from humans and unplug it when not in use so it doesn't keep emitting a field (and I also save mula from the power company).

### Scompy — 2016-11-17T01:40:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.18669/post-204005>

If you ever fork out a bunch of money for a state-of-the-art meter, this one is it:

### Liam Taylor — 2016-11-17T11:21:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.18669/post-204013>

Fair play Scompy, that's some useful input. Would I be correct to say that you would reccomend the acoustimeter as the best all round meter for emf (over the 88T cornet)?

### Scompy — 2016-11-18T01:26:03+0200

For awhile now I have been neurotically questioning where has my energy gone, what am I missing? And it just might be a possibility of me missing what I have too much of. I've been through the ringer of hypothyroid symptoms including hypogonadism. And it could all be due to hemochromatosis, depending on what my iron levels are. You could name this Triple H.

I'm looking to DIY a home "bloodletting". Seems easy and practical, don't know where I can donate blood. If symptoms don't give up, then I'll most likely go to a useless urologist or endocrinologist to get some tests done. Any recommendations for what I should get tested? Maybe I don't need to get any tests done, and see a pelvic floor specialist, or see a sleep doctor to see if I have sleep apnea. ### JanSz — 2016-10-28T17:10:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hemochromatosis-hpa-axis-dysfunction.18670/post-203401>

Get
Complete Blood Count (CBC)
look up
hemoglobnin(12.6 - 17.7)g/dL

if your hemoglobin is above 17.7 you should donate blood
if you are on the lover side, careful with donations.

Some people must get rid of some blood but are not allowed to donate.
Ask doctor for script for therapeutic phlebothomy.

Someone who let the blood out should monitor ferritin.

### JmanGigHarbor — 2019-07-16T03:48:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/led-light-bulbs-vs-led-diodes.18672/post-274367>

I have copied over a post with useful info on led's that I have researched. This is a way to use LEDs with DC power.

I have been researching this lately and would like to make it simpler for some new people.
So the overall problem with "LED's" is that when they run on "AC" current, it causes them to flicker from the way AC current runs through a normal US house. This AC current will pulse as it is used. In order to get around this issue, you can use LED's that run directly from "DC" current. Because this is a direct line of current and does not pulse like AC current does. This can be easily tested using your smart phone on video record, setting it to "slow motion" mode you are able to see the LED light pulse on AC current. Now, if you start looking around there are actually AC to DC wall adapters for running off a standard US home power supply. What I have found is on amazon you can find a UV 12v Led flood light, that is 10w. this can then be ran off an AC-DC power supply. And by doing this it has a perfect, constant light output with no flickering.
There are also easier to test devices that are 5v DC UV light bars, that come with there own wall adapter. I have also found a red light LED that uses this same 5v DC adapter method. I will provide links below to all the devices, I hope this helps someone out

UV Flood Light (395-400nm)
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B012CUL180/ref=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o02\_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B012CUL180/ref=oh_aui_detailpage_o02_s00?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

AC to DC power supply

# Thread: Vagus Nerve Stimulation

**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### prime1 — 2016-10-28T05:37:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vagus-nerve-stimulation.18673/post-203394>

.
Ran into an amazing Neurosurgeon this week - electrical stim on my tongue to activate the vagus nerve. What an amazing exeperience and a huge help with improving my health. I'm surprised it's not used more widespread to help patients out.

Forbes Magazine

Is Your Tongue The Key To A Neuroscience Breakthrough?

Nov. 2015

The team at [Helius Medical Technologies](http://www.heliusmedical.com/) believe combining physical therapy with stimulation of the tongue may improve impairment of brain function and associated symptoms of injury. “We have already seen that stimulation of various nerves can improve symptoms of a range of neurological diseases. However, we believe the tongue is a much more elegant and direct pathway for stimulating brain structures and inducing neuroplasticity. We are focused on investigating the tongue as a gateway to the brain to hopefully ease the disease of brain injury,” said Dr. Jonathan Sackier, CMO at Helius.

### seanb4 — 2016-10-28T15:09:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vagus-nerve-stimulation.18673/post-203400>

Could something like this be replicated with a TENS machine / Alpha Stim / Biotuner ?

# Thread: Porfiriy Ivanov - CT guru
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/porfiriy-ivanov-ct-guru.18674/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Anton Chigur — 2016-10-28T10:52:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/porfiriy-ivanov-ct-guru.18674/post-203398>

Hey Guys,

I would like to share a nice example of what we are capable of. Actually this guy initiated Ice bucket Challenge long ago. There was a guy in Russia called Porfiriy Ivanov who was born around 120 years ago from now. Once he got sick with terminal cancer, he decided to commit a suicide by freeze himself, so he began pouring called water on himself in minus 30 Celsium and recovered quickly. He realized that all problems are from disengagement from nature and he gave all clothes for 50 years, he left only underwear. Once in winter he made a walk in his only underwear from Saratov to Moscow (around 1300km) and returned back. Now he is considered a God of seals (ice smimmers) in Russia. His method is simple - every morning go outside and pour a bucket of cold water on yourself
A few photos for you to realize how literally cool this guy is:

### Sujoe123 — 2016-10-28T20:35:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.18677/post-203408>

I live in far south Texas in a rocky Bay. It is an unsafe area to walk. Will I ground adequately sitting on a dock with my feet in the salt water? I would say yes! Comments??
