Also another question. Why does my avatar appear twice ...a larger and then a superimposed smaller image and what about the eyeglasses on my post!

### fitness@home — 2016-10-28T23:44:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.18677/post-203414>

I only see one avatar. The glasses usually mean you are following a thread.

### Sujoe123 — 2016-10-29T15:06:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.18677/post-203423>

Thanks...dont know how to undo it

### Echosyn — 2016-11-11T06:08:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.18677/post-203816>

> [Sujoe123 said:](/goto/post?id=203408)
>
> I live in far south Texas in a rocky Bay. It is an unsafe area to walk. Will I ground adequately sitting on a dock with my feet in the salt water? I would say yes! Comments??

Moisture wicking footwear such as leather sole moccasins serve to protect the feet and ground you. Due to MCS I wear only natural fabrics like cotton and wool which ground well when slightly moist. A twin size cotton bed sheet serves well as a grounding ground cloth when you want to lay on a grassy area or sandy beach. If things are too dry just dampen the sheet a bit. Polyester, nylon and rayon are non-conductive.

### Sujoe123 — 2016-11-11T21:27:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.18677/post-203848>

Thank you for your reply Echosyn, but I am referring to a blog or podcast where Dr. Jack suggested standing in the ocean and getting Sun at the same time. There is no beach in this area just a tiny rocky harbor. I live in a condo that has a pier and I can sit in the Sun and dangle my feet in the salt water without having to traverse the rocks to get in the ocean. My main point was: am I grounded just dangling my feet in the saltwater? I would guess, yes I am grounded.

### Echosyn — 2016-11-12T00:55:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding.18677/post-203851>

> [Sujoe123 said:](/goto/post?id=203848)
>
> Thank you for your reply Echosyn, but I am referring to a blog or podcast where Dr. Jack suggested standing in the ocean and getting Sun at the same time. There is no beach in this area just a tiny rocky harbor. I live in a condo that has a pier and I can sit in the Sun and dangle my feet in the salt water without having to traverse the rocks to get in the ocean. My main point was: am I grounded just dangling my feet in the saltwater? I would guess, yes I am grounded.

"I see." said the blind man. Yes indeed we are like mobile trees that are rooted in the earth and our bodies bathed in water, sun and air. Remove any of them and the tree dies. There is a low rock promontory beneath aTrinity Willow where I would often sit with my feet in the river water, notebook in hand and the evening sun at my back. Minnows would nibble at my toes. Can it get any better?

# Thread: Hello! Adrenal Fatigue and Low Testosterone
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-adrenal-fatigue-and-low-testosterone.18681/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Janers — 2016-10-31T01:41:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-adrenal-fatigue-and-low-testosterone.18681/post-203460>

Hello everyone!

I'm 36yo with some lingering health issues that have gotten better after adopting a paleo/epi-paleo diet/lifestyle. I'm still recovering from adrenal fatigue, testosterone is in the crapper, hair loss has slowed down and no matter how many hours of sleep I get, I don't feel refreshed and it’s still a bit of a struggle to wake up in the mornings. I'm a recovering cellphone and laptop addict. I just married the man of my dreams and we'd like to start a family, but I want to try to be as healthy as possible before we conceive so I can keep up with them.

Current stats:

36 yo, 5’0, 92 lbs. (always been betwee 92-98 lbs. since turning legal). Live in southern California, born and raised.

\-Partied like a rockstar (plenty of drinking) from 20-28yrs old.

\-24 yrs old: 2 left breast mass removals with bilateral mammoplasty

\-GI problems begin: constipation becomes a norm

\-post nasal drip

\-26 yrs old: stressful life event -insomnia starts

\-27 yrs old: stopped taking birth control pills after 8 years, hair loss began

\-32 yrs old: up until this point, I was eating a SAD, mostly low fat and mostly chicken as the protein

\-33 yrs old: tried being a vegetarian for a few months but I started looking like skeletor so I stopped.

\-35 yrs old: Started eating paleo. Met a functional practitioner who gave me the following diagnoses: adrenal fatigue, heavy metals (high in cadmium and antimony), gut dysbiosis, low in all sex hormones. Started meditating, stopped watching shows online/phone use before sleep, installed f.lux on computer and phone, wearing blue-blocking glasses at night. This helped me fall asleep and stay asleep.

\-36 yrs old: Discovered JK via podcast, eat epi-paleo, but still eat a little rice (I’m Asian and when I go too low on carbs, I start to feel terrible). Started cold CT with just face dunks a few weeks ago but only find time to do this once a week. Try to go to the beach 1-2x/month, get sun in my eyes first thing in the AM. Got re-tested via DUTCH testing and all my levels improved (but cortisol, melatonin and testosterone remain low). My metabolized cortisol 1771 ng/mg, testosterone 1.9ng/mg with high 5alpha reductase activity, and melatonin 15.5 ng/mg). Constipation has almost resolved in the past month-wahoo!

### caroline — 2016-11-02T03:31:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-adrenal-fatigue-and-low-testosterone.18681/post-203508>

Hi and welcome. Please give Dr.K. health history of your Mom and Gran.

Where do you live? work?

to optimize FreeT3 use thyroid hormone from pigs

### justin potier — 2016-11-02T03:55:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-dont-understand.18687/post-203510>

Dave Asprey has repeatedly claimed that while eating 4000 calories a day for multiple years he managed to lose over 100 lbs. I always thought that was possible until recently a co-worker started debating that there was no way that could happen based on the "calories in calories out theory." Is it possible for Dave to have done this? or is my co-worker correct?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-02T14:55:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-dont-understand.18687/post-203516>

Dave has a problem with telling the truth when it collides with his marketing tag lines. Caveat emptor.

### kyrakitty — 2016-11-22T10:46:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-dont-understand.18687/post-204202>

> [justin potier said:](/goto/post?id=203510)
>
> Dave Asprey has repeatedly claimed that while eating 4000 calories a day for multiple years he managed to lose over 100 lbs. I always thought that was possible until recently a co-worker started debating that there was no way that could happen based on the "calories in calories out theory." Is it possible for Dave to have done this? or is my co-worker correct?

My experience with Dave Asprey is that thanks to him I am not dead. I learned about cholestyramine from him for toxic mold exposure and he talked about it so much that I finally tried it when I was deathly ill and NOTHING else worked. He personally took time to answer questions I had to help me understand. Dave literally saved my life 1 1/2 - 2yrs ago. Also, recently, last month, I listened to a podcast on thyroid medications with Elle Russ and once again it has turned things around for me. I am now on thyroid medication for hashimotos which I had been resistant to until I learned more through this podcast. I am so happy that I found out about Dave Asprey if not I would be dead.

In my opinion the co-worker that debated with you is like most people uninformed. He probably thinks sun isn't important, there is no such thing as EMF issues, grounding is bullshit and water is just water. Don't waste your time on people who are uninformed and refuse to read or expand what they know... It is possible to eat that many calories on an extremely high fat ketogenic diet and loose weight but it is not a healthy way to loose weight IMO. Better way is to do intermittent ketogenic diet in general but it depends on all the issues you struggle with. There are many other basics that need to be implemented simultaneously like Sun, Water, nnEMF, Where on earth you life, grounding, eliminating blue light etc....

I never had any of those side effects and I took it for 3 1/2 months. It was an absolute savior and it worked well for me.

TRUST ME I know my body and no one else but me knows what is right for me. I was literally at deaths door 1 1/2 - 2 years ago I am not joking or fudging reality when I state this. I am not anywhere near deaths door now thank God to Cholestyramine I love it and I love Dave for teaching me about it. I saw many stupid ass holistic doctors (the ones that don't take insurance) who don't know shit and don't listen. I tried all sorts of IV therapies, ozone via IV a couple times a week for 2 months, got a home ozone machine for rectal insuflations, supplement programs all through doctors that don't know shit. They literally almost killed me and they didn't give a shit... as I was an open credit card to them and that is all they cared about. I say all that with an angry tone because what I learned is that me and only me is the single person I can count on to pick and choose what is and isn't good medically speaking. I know what is going on in my body and what my body needs and no one else does. That is what Dave taught me.

The day I TOLD the doctor he was goong to prescribe Cholestyramine to me which I got from a compounding pharmacy with zero excipients was the day I saved my life. No longer do any doctors or other people make any medical decisions for me. I research a lot,I ask a lot of smart and stupid questions and then I make decisions because I know what is correct for my body. All doctors who work for me Today... I tell them what labs they will order and what dossages they will give me. I allow them to advise me when they are asked because they work for me. When I make a decision at that point no one else's opinion matters unless I change my mind due to information that makes sense.

The reason I took Cholestyramine for 3 1/2 months almost 2 years ago is because I had 6 years of toxic mold exposure, severe lung issues from the mold and possitve Igg and igm blood tests for mycoplasma infection all from mold. No other therapies helped but Cholestyramine did **in the first week of taking it**.. it sopped severe nightly drenching night sweats, stopped the feeling of tripping on LSD, stopped the panick attacks by 80%, stopped massive pain in my arms and legs that was 24/7 and stopped the compulsive deep desire to kill my self before the symptoms killed me first.

I knew before taking it that I had to replace my fat soluble vitamins and I used vit C and magnesium to keep my bowels moving. I never had stomach issues from it or constipation from it. My cholesterol at the time was near 300 so no issues there if it lowered my cholesterol. I have always been fit looking, zero excess fat, lean and muscular. I didn't loose or gain weight.

Point blank I would be dead without Cholestyramine I do prefer to be alive so no Cholestyramine is not dangerous in my book. We all should have access to anything we feel would help our lives be better. Blanket statements that any compounds or treatments are dangerous is a hindrance to our freedom to choose and to run big clinical trials that really matter instead of the bullshit clinical trials being done by pharmaceutical companies today. The FDA uses the approach of choosing what is or isn't safe and they have robbed us of our freedom and birthright to optimal health by telling us so many things are dangerous. So I say be carful in saying anything is too dangerous to use. Better to make lists of its positive and negative potentials than label anything as dangerous and keep an open mind. This idea of being careful to call things dangerous I also learned from Dave. I used to believe differently until one of his podcasts opened my mind.

Funny, I learned about Jack Kruse from Dave too. We are all connected no man is an island. It takes a tribe even if everyone doesn't see eye to eye on all issues.

My understanding is that it binds the toxic bile filled with biotoxins from the gallbladder and you poop out the toxic bile. Bile is something the body typically recycles and useses over and over again and if it becomes toxic the body wont get rid of it it keeps using it. Biotoxins accumulate AND get stuck in the bile. Every time this toxic bile recirculates symptoms gets worse wile also accumulating more toxins in the already toxic bile. So one way to get rid of the toxic bile is to take cholestyramine which binds the toxic bile and you literally poop it out. Then the liver makes new fresh clean bile.

However, I do agree with JanSz in that cholestyramine is not helpful for everyone I was lucky in that it had zero side effects and all positive effects. Also, as JanSz stated fat soluable vitamins have to be supplemented and stomach issues can develop if precautions are not taken specific to each individuals needs. It is only a short term solution in fact 3 1/2 months was probably pushing it. The real problem was that I was living in a moldy home the first 1 1/2 months while on it like Jack says all health issues stem from our environment. We were house hunting and then when we bought our new home I took it for an additional 2 months. Taking it while living in a moldy home is not advised by "doctors" but it made my life bearable and got rid of really bad symptoms in the first week. The way it is supposed to be used is to get out of the bad environment first then take it for a month to 1 1/2 months or so. I took charge of my health and did it the way I did it because I knew it was right for me and it was the best decision in the world I made... but if you ask any experts they will all say I did it wrong I ignore their opinions because it is specifically an N=1 for me that was perfect. That is how everything is for all of us.

I have a 208 slice 3D scan of my brain/skull that was done 3-Tesla (3T) Siemens magnet resonance imaging system. I also had an high density EEG done as well (while I was performing tasks in the MRI). # Thread: Where to go if I leave home as a mimor?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-go-if-i-leave-home-as-a-mimor.18690/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2016-11-03T03:56:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-go-if-i-leave-home-as-a-mimor.18690/post-203542>

Probably shouldn't post this on the internet... but

I'm 17 for 9 months, almost an entire year. I'm in my senior year in high school. I set my schedule with all AM classes free until 10:30am so I spend all this time outdoors.

As winter comes it will be more difficult to get anything done outdoors but I'll bundle up if I must. I can handle a lot of cold but at a certain point I cannot.

I'm 12 miles Northeast of Philadelphia so my environment sucks... 87 cell towers within 4 miles. I want to get the hell out of here, drop out of high school, so I can focus on my studies and actually helping our species- not dealing with all of the constricting, distracting, illusion of importance of shit like homework, grades, college applications (probably not going but Dad wanted me to apply...), and all the other stuff that steals my ability.

The upside is that I started The Optimal Club at my high school and some 20-30 kids are pretty interested... because everyone is really tired and feels like shit all the time (school gives each student a macbook air to take home- FLUX not allowed though I'm pushing for it).

I want to get the hell out of here, but I'll need food and and a place to stay and these are difficult without an income. Of course, I'm trying to figure out how to "get rich quick" (ie make a lot of money somehow without losing so much time nor health).

**Any ideas, suggestions? Where could I go live for minimal money? How could I maybe convince my dad to support me? If I were to "run away" where would I go?**

One side of me wants to stick it out- it's just 8 months till I graduate. The other thinks that's way too long to be in this shitty an environment. I can biohack it a lot but only so much when I spend 3 hours a day in a Wi-Fi shithole and the rest in a cell and smart meter bath (PA suburbs).

### caroline — 2016-11-03T07:54:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-go-if-i-leave-home-as-a-mimor.18690/post-203550>

Please do stick it out ....look at it as an investment in your future. Spend as much time as possible reading Dr. K.'s blogs and learning.

Family is important ........ don't throw the baby out with the bathwater if you can help it!

The group you formed at school is a great tool for you ....you are all learning and bouncing ideas off of each other.

Don't underestimate networking and quantum entanglement - it is very important to achieving what you want in life.

Dr. K. has been on this optimal path for about 12 years now ....slowing building a tribe and asking them to pay it forward....

That is what you can do as well.

Everyday make connections, everyday smile at people and show you care.

Dr. K. always tells us .... people don't care what you know - until they know that you care.

Please always remember that.

Care about others, especially your family ..... and build your knowledge, and build your tribe.

BE PATIENT! - you can't force feed people but you can arm yourself with knowledge and kindness ...and a smile speaks volumes.

Good Luck!

### caroline — 2016-11-03T08:00:30+0200

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthy2015 — 2016-11-03T13:13:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203556>

Can anyone tell me what the below could possibly be
We have a TriField Meter to check our house and surrounding areas and a constant reading 2 (0-100 milligauss) when putting it on the Radio/Microwave setting. Could this be from a cell tower or what could it be from and is this a high reading?
Thanks

### healthy2015 — 2016-11-03T19:36:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203574>

Any ideas please?
Thank you

### Penny — 2016-11-03T22:20:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203579>

> [healthy2015 said:](/goto/post?id=203574)
>
> Any ideas please?
> Thank you

Not sure about the radio setting, but 2 or under is pretty good - at least when checking dirty electricity on a wall...

### healthy2015 — 2016-11-05T15:03:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203631>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=203579)
>
> Not sure about the radio setting, but 2 or under is pretty good - at least when checking dirty electricity on a wall...

Thank you once again penny, you are so helpful and I am always grateful of your replies!!

The 2 reading is everywhere in the house, garden and down the street which I assume is very bad??!

### Penny — 2016-11-06T00:42:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203649>

I don't think it's that bad - if you were super hyper electrically sensitive, you would want to get it under 1 - like a Michael Neuert type of person - who is incidentally being interviewed by Lloyd Burell here:
[http://www.electricsense.com/11948/emf-shielding-protection/](http://www.electricsense.com/11948/emf-shielding-protection/)

google: Michael neuert and you get a lot of hits - he is quite an expert on EMF and how to use these meters -

### Scompy — 2016-11-06T02:45:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203652>

The Trifield is not sensitive for RF... it really has to hit ultra-high radio frequency to register. Cornets and other meters that are more sensitive help to pick up more in the 1 to 100 milliWatt per Square meter (or cm) and they're great for focusing sources to know the causes/locations. I.e., milliGauss is not for measuring RF.

### endless — 2016-11-06T09:04:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203655>

Use your Trifield for magnetic and electric fields.... Get another for RF.

### Inger — 2016-11-07T10:20:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203683>

I just tested my home with my TriField.. in the Radio/Microwave setting to compare. I got 0,5-0,6 in the 100 milligauss range. And I get 3 weak wifi signals from neighbors in here. So it could be better. But we have no smart meters at all.
I think 2 is not too good... there must be something in the environment..?

But you sure should get you another meter for RF as suggested above.

### Butters — 2016-11-13T00:56:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203908>

Should one start with a trifield first? I looked the RF-meters up and they are horrible expensive for a student.
How is the trifield - meter from trifield?

### Scompy — 2016-11-13T03:25:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203910>

Magnetic and electrical EMFs should not be discounted. The TriField is great for them; don't expect the RF meter to do much because it's not very sensitive. I say this because I get issues in all types of fields, and some magnetic/electrical fields in the past have been brutal to my visual spots and tinnitus. In a weird way, I'm a walking meter because I know when I'm in a strong field. Sadly, even in a weaker field, I don't know what my mitochondria think, but I'm sure they are yelling at me to stop.

### Butters — 2016-11-13T12:52:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-203921>

Ok. Most things you can control easily with WiFi etc.. At least what is in your own responsibility. So I will go for the TriField.
But is low ELF important? Or is 50Hz for europe enough?

### Dean6789 — 2017-12-16T23:54:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-230442>

> [Scompy said:](/goto/post?id=203652)
>
> The Trifield is not sensitive for RF... it really has to hit ultra-high radio frequency to register. Cornets and other meters that are more sensitive help to pick up more in the 1 to 100 milliWatt per Square meter (or cm) and they're great for focusing sources to know the causes/locations. I.e., milliGauss is not for measuring RF.

Hi Scompy,

I'm now looking into a tri-meter. The two that I've come across the most so far are:

1\. The Trifield 100XE EMF Meter and
2\. The Tri-Mode High + Low Frequency Meter - Cornet ED88T

A guy that wants to sell me the Cornet says that it reads RF, magnetic1 & 2, and electric fields.

Do you know which one is best (or if some other one is more highly recommended)?

I'm trying to keep it as simple (and still effective) as possible and get an all-in-one meter.

Thanks.

### Scompy — 2017-12-17T06:27:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-230471>

Cornet 88T will probably be a better choice, since the Trifield's RF/Microwave is very poor. The Cornet's magnetic and electrical EMF detection is not as precise as the TriField, but pretty close. And the Cornet has a built-in sound mode for RF.

### Dean6789 — 2017-12-17T19:45:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nnemf-meter.18692/post-230511>

> [Scompy said:](/goto/post?id=230471)
>
> Cornet 88T will probably be a better choice, since the Trifield's RF/Microwave is very poor. The Cornet's magnetic and electrical EMF detection is not as precise as the TriField, but pretty close. And the Cornet has a built-in sound mode for RF.

Cool. Thanks a bunch!

# Thread: First Day!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-day.18696/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lvr413 — 2016-11-04T23:31:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-day.18696/post-203618>

Hello, I am Jessalee and today is my first day starting my leptin reset! I am open to any advise or tricks if you have them? I have struggled with my weight my entire life I also have an underactive thyroid condition and am on medication for hereditary high blood pressure. I also struggle from anxiety and am currently on medication for it.

### caroline — 2016-11-05T00:41:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-day.18696/post-203619>

Welcome Jessalee .....

There are no tricks! honestly just do your due diligence - read everything you can and apply!

Have you read the C/T protocol? start with face dunks ....just take baby steps. Rome wasn't built in a day. You didn't get sick overbite - it is going to take time to turn your titanic around.

Start a journal in the optimal journal section. It is great to keep track of what your are doing and fun to read back at a later date and see your progress.

Give Dr. K. some health history of you and your Mom and Gran...

where do you live? work?

Do you have blue blockers?

How is your sleep? libido?

nnEMF? Is your bedroom free of electronics??? do you limit computer time?

How much outdoor time do you get? You need lots and lots of sun and grounding and as much mother nature as you can manage.

This is a light story!

Have you read Dr. K.'s book? The Epi-Paeo RX ....it is the first couple of years of the blog and a lot easier to get the info.

Please read John Ott's book ... Health and Light. It is a very easy read.

Listen to all Jack's podcasts .... He has a very earthy way of speaking and distills down all the info.

Good luck on this amazing journey ..... your amazing journey to optimal health and wellness.

### Inger — 2016-11-07T09:41:05+0200

### Inger — 2016-11-08T16:14:37+0200

### Inger — 2016-11-08T16:19:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brie.18698/post-203718>

I found this too, looks like Brie is great too

> In my interview with Dr. Rheamue-Bleue, she identified the cheeses highest in K2 are Gouda and Brie, which contain about 75 mcg per ounce. Hard cheeses are about 30 percent higher in vitamin K2 than soft cheeses. In perusing the nutritional tables myself, I found it interesting that the cheeses highest in vitamin K2 also tend to be the highest in protein and calcium — so the most nutritious overall. Just realize that the values listed for "vitamin K" in common nutritional tables are of limited value because they don't specify what TYPE of vitamin K they're measuring.
>
> As it turns out, scientists have found high levels of MK-7 in one type of cheese: Edam.[11](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/06/17/heart-healthy-cheese.aspx#_edn11) This is wonderful news for those of you who would much rather sit down to a slice of Edam than a bowl of natto! (Natto, a strongly fermented Japanese soybean product, has the highest MK-7 level of any food.)
>
> Earlier, I made my case for selecting raw cheeses from grass-pastured, grass-fed animals. However, cheese contains a bacterially-derived form of K2, so it doesn't matter if the cheese was made from grass-fed milk or not — the bacteria used to culture the cheese is the same. Grass-fed dairy is important for the other reasons I've already discussed — just not specifically for the K2.
>
> To summarize then, if you're going to select cheese with your primary goal being a good source of vitamin K2, the best ones are:
>
>
> -   Gouda
> -   Brie
> -   Edam
> -   Other cheeses with lesser, but significant, levels of K2: Cheddar, Colby, hard [goat cheese](http://recipes.mercola.com/goat-cheese-recipe.aspx), Swiss, and Gruyere.[12](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/06/17/heart-healthy-cheese.aspx#_edn12)

[http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/06/17/heart-healthy-cheese.aspx](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/06/17/heart-healthy-cheese.aspx)

### Jenny S — 2016-11-14T11:00:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brie.18698/post-203945>

I believe Natto is good source of k2

### Alper — 2016-11-14T16:47:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brie.18698/post-203956>

> [Jenny S said:](/goto/post?id=203945)

> I believe Natto is good source of k2

New Uncle Jack podcast out this AM. Let's just say the tech industry centralized system got punched in the mouth. A lot more details of the POMC story is given out.

You don’t need people’s expert opinions on a fact of nature, says Mother Nature. Understanding how light from the sun is tied energy and information transfers to hormones and cholesterol in our membranes, skin, and eyes to support this idea. The modern paradigm has carefully crafted a meme that the sun is toxic for us and this is ludicrous when you consider we have massive data that the sun reduces all cause mortality.

The skin is a cholesterol laden insulator of fat with lipid raft antenna's deisgned to connect us to the sun. Melanin embedded in the skin is the semiconductive protein that creates electrons and light to work with cholesterol. These melaninated sheets act as a solar panel for the brain to give it more energy from the sun to run the Ferrari engine in our head. This has to be optimized for neurological function. Most modern human disease is linked to a break in this quantum biologic connection.

Eating cholesterol is of zero consequence to mammals. Creating it in the liver is critical in understanding the biophysics of cholesterol non-visual photoreception. The lipid raft's ability to change in mammals occurs by seasonal light variation and collection via the non-visual photoreceptors via perception on the skin, eyes, and gut. That external light determines the reality the mammal faces. When the solar cycles change so do the lipid rafts. This photoelectric change alters biochemistry in mTOR, PPP, glycolysis, the TCA cycle, and POMC cleavage. When the lipid content changes they induce changes in the semiconductive proteins embedded in them. This changes the physiologic ability. This is why the clock mechanism in mammals is linked to light and temperature. Both signals change to the surfaces of mammals.

This change in the skin has massive implications for the circulatory system, arteries, blood, and especially the liver. Most people do not know the deuterium content of blood and the lumen in the gut is also plastic via light and temperature signaling for two reasons. Deuterium has an extra neutron so this heavier atomic mass means more energy is needed to move it. Deuterium has a different magnetic moment than H+ so this means it reacts differently when the electromagnetic signal from the sun varies seasonally. This is what causes mammalian membranes changes on their surfaces and interiors.

Because semiconductive proteins like melanin are embedded in the skin, what type of cholesterol and fatty acids matter to the functioning of VGCCs. Why? Because the lipid rafts are like Morse code for the creation of Vitamin D from the cholesterol system in the skin. This Vitamin D system has to share information with the Vitamin A signal in the opsin system. this is the non visual photoreceptor system. Most people have no idea that cholesterol is a non visual photoereceptor. The lipid rafts alter the functioning of voltage-gated channels that control the photoisomerization step of the conversion of cholesterol to 25 D (OH). This chemical has to go to the kidney and/or liver for final conversion to act at target receptors in this system of the mitochondria. This only happens when cholesterol is sulfated and in its HDL formati because this form of cholesterol has more electrons allowing it to capture the UVB light to complete the first conversion in the skin. At the same time UV-A sunlight increases NO and oxygen deliver to mitochondria to alter their function because sunlight controls the oxidation state of Fe and keeps it in the +3 state. This increases the sulfation of all things in the system and it makes them MORE WATER SOLUABLE. When cholesterol is sulfated it does not need LDL particles to transport it. It uses HDL to do it. ApoB and LpA drops as a result when the system is properly attuned to the solar cycle and they cease to be an issue for the mammal. This mechanism simultaneously thins the blood while lowering calcium flows in the mitochondria. This lowers our BP along with NO actions in the RAS. Lowering calcium and raising NO both act to reduce mitochondrial power. What takes over when all this happens to create H+ and oxygen and electrons to run the system? POMC creates melanin and melanin makes all three things to fill the system with massive energy. This is why NO slows mitochondrial metabolism and lowers BP. Centralized medicine does not understand this wiring diagram in 2023. Their longevity experts are still advocating the use of statins which completely ruin the fidelity of this system. Sulfate platelets and GAGs in the vessel wall are less sticky and there is better laminar flow. This is why we have an epidemic of patients on blood thinners. No one is going outside enough. As a result, clots cause both heart and brain damage. This is why PAD is linked to both diseases. The cholesterol made in this way links to hormone creation in us.

Hormones effectively are light ferries boats and most human hormones are made from choesterol. Understanding how cholesterol operates in sunlight is critical. If your hormone panels is trashed and your LDL is up and blood glucose high you are under a chronic BLUE LIGHT STRESS. That is your problem. Few centralized MD understand why statins make absolutely no sense in this modern world. Hormone panels are a reflection of the cells reaction to their light environment.

When you view your world exclusively through the lens of science, the scale you see "your world" through is altered and never parallels reality.......leaving 'your prescription' too weak to explain what is really at the source of the paradox.

I dropped some bombs about water in this podcast but I think the interviewer did not ask follow-ups on these things. I hope some of you do.

The modern mind can't fathom the primacy 'of scale' because their scale for understanding biology is not at a nano/QED level yet. This is where the microcosm meets the macrocosm.

Life is a kind of prison for us. We are connected to the universe, but we experience this world disconnected from the whole. It is an optical delusion of our conscious perception allowing us to separate space from time. This prison is unique. We can constrict our life or we can broaden its walls via our perspective. What is decentralized medicine, and why do you need it?

The extra mile is a vast, decentralized, underpopulated wasteland where the Black Swan mitochondriac goes.

People say they go the extra mile, but almost no one does. Most people think, "Wait, no one else is here. Why am I doing this?"
The Black Swan is rare because their mindset is unique.

That's why the extra mile is such a lonely place for the common man. That's also why the extra mile is a place filled with opportunities. Be early with the sun. Stay late, but don't use manufactured light. Make the extra phone call to Nature. Send the extra email. Do the extra research. Help a person solve a complex problem.

Help a customer unload or unpack a shipment. Don't wait to be asked for help; offer help. Be uncommon. Whenever you do something, think of one extra thing you can do--especially if other people aren't doing that one thing. In this unpopulated wasteland, many will ask you why you persist. Tell them you are using nature's light to sculpt your life by thinking better than before.

Sure, it's hard to think anew and adapt. Being solo in the modern world is not something to fear........it is something to conquer.
But that's what will make you different and unique--and over time, it will make you incredibly successful when you begin to write your next chapter. It will be a book all will want to read when they see the words of your story spill all over the pages...........

### Jack Kruse — 2023-10-14T18:23:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/podcasts-dropbox-collection.18701/post-327256>

I've done hundreds of podcast interviews by this point. I've seen their process and the product with a big sample.

The true decentralized scientific podcasters are ones that want to explore multiple perspectives and challenge themselves, rather than be tied to always reinforcing one theme.

But there's more to this radical idea. True decentralized clinician scientists keep track of their industry, specifically seek out guests that differ from each other, and importantly, they research enough to understand the nuances of how they disagree and want to explore those questions and bring the answers to their audience. But... But there are two false paths here.

The first false path is the path of the middle ground. It's the idea that between two ideas, the answer is necessarily in the middle.

No, sometimes the answer is one or the other. Explore perspectives but don't assume the middle is right. The second false path is of audience capture. They just tell their audience what they know they want to hear. Get the clicks! This is the Rogan, Attia, and Huberman path.

This is the short time preference method; it works in the near term but lacks long-term decentralization respectability. You eventually get written off by the public when your luster fades. The most impressive decentralized interviewers/scientists to me nail all of this?

\-They know their industry well, and purposely seek out competing views.

\-They honestly want to learn and build truth, not just assume the answer is in the middle. They explore and press each idea individually. They go down the rabbit holes and seek the chaos. This is where the truth in nature lies. Dr. Ted did that.

Why was I worried that Cutter 1.0 from polio would show up in mRNA jabs in 2021 when I had the member event at my clinic when I invited Judith Vary Baker to speak to my members? The nuclear envelope is a major barrier for nuclear uptake of plasmids and represents one of the most significant unsolved problems of nonviral gene delivery in centralized science. The literature has previously shown that the nuclear entry of plasmid DNA is sequence-specific, requiring a 366-bp fragment containing the SV40 origin of replication and early promoter. Data from 1999 was reported to show show although fragments throughout this region can support varying degrees of nuclear import in humans, the 72-bp repeats of the SV40 enhancer facilitate maximal transport. Big Pharma read this work just like I did. I knew they had to get this virus in to make this technology functional. This is why in the COVID Revealed documentary movie I went to great lengths to discuss the legal wording of the EUA and why it seemed they would use two jabs to get passed the FDA safety meeting. One would be submitted to the FDA sans the SV-40 and the other would be given to the public that had SV 40 in it. When those people who attended and look back on that July 4th meeting they should realize how much value I delivered to them that day.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/podcasts-dropbox-collection.18701/post-329348>

A new podcast is out, PART 2, with my VIKING BUDS!

One of the most overlooked health risks today is chronic exposure to blue light. It is the dominant form of nnEMF but not the only one. Abuse of technology screens can lead to diabetes, obesity, depression, suicide, and many other modern-day diseases. Blue light actually looks white to the naked eye. It is emitted from screens on smartphones, tablets, TVs, computer screens, eBook readers, and energy-efficient fluorescent and LED lights.

So, if you work indoors, you are chronically exposed to high levels of blue light. This issue is getting more attention. In May 2016, Apple released Night Shift with its new iOS 9.3 update in an effort to reduce the amount of blue light emitted from iPhones and iPads. In June 2015, the AMA adopted guidance for communities on selecting LED lighting options to minimize potential harmful human and environmental effects. But the effect still needs to be mitigated by most modern humans.

### Jack Kruse — 2024-01-06T06:48:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/podcasts-dropbox-collection.18701/post-329885>

A new podcast with Dr. @alexisjcowan is out tonight.

We talked about decentralized science and medicine. We covered a ton.

The transition to decentralized medicine represents a shift towards a more evidence-based, patient-centric approach. This system values the proof of work concept, rewarding clinicians for their efforts in generating positive patient outcomes rather than their adherence to pharmaceutical company directives in the education curriculum.

This leads to more personalized care and encourages innovation and experimentation in the medical field.

Dr. Alexis Jazmyn did her doctoral research in Josh Rabinowitz's lab. I have brought up Josh's NAD+/NAD precursor paper during many podcasts (which, in many ways, debunked a lot of the claims made around NAD and aging). So, she cut her teeth in metabolism while specifically looking at changes in tissue metabolism that occur in response to different dietary inputs and in response to fasting. She said she honestly felt grateful that her research was in this lab as it's one of the more open-minded and least reductionist groups she had ever encountered in academia.

Josh Rabinowitz slayed much of the marketing nonsense pushed by PhDs like David Sinclair and Lenny Guarente around NAD supplementation. Josh was also Dr. Attia's classmate in med school. Alexis was a pleasure to talk with.

What is the 2024 centralized medicine trend I am to reverse now? When we look at how centralized doctors or PhDs perceive disease-screening tests, we find that they tend to underestimate the potential harms of screening and overestimate the potential benefits. Decentralized clinicians note that a “striking feature” of recent research is how much of it contradicts traditional centralized medical opinion. Decentralized medicine is bringing healthcare back into balance with Nature by elevating the importance of the mtDNA genome and pushing the nuclear genome to the rear.

Consider gorillas/chimps body plans compared to ours. Our cousins have way more strength and power, lack frontal lobes and have 3.8 billion base pairs in their DNA to our 3.2 billion base pairs. Humans also have 23 pairs of chromosomes. Apes have 24. They have more musle hypertrophy and their guts are 30 ft longer than ours, but their DNA is not more complex than us. They do not live as long as we do either. Their longevity is stunted compared to humans.

We took those excess mitochondria from the larger Neanderthal brain and moved them. As neurons degraded so did melanin and dopamine surged on a raltive basis and we got language and art in one fell swoop. As the genome shrunk the integumentary melanin over dermatomes internalized to our CNS/body plan.

Most of the mtDNA were shared in the brain and immune system in humans to explain human abilities, speech and creation. It is also why our gut shortened 30 feet compared to theirs. Our GALT is the result of this quantum engineering of the combination of melanin and water as the body of Interlandi showed up to sculpt the gut via the splanchnic nerves.

This was done at the same time sonic hedgehog, melanin renovation, and mitochondrial redeployment went into other cell lines. This explains the condensation of the Great Apes' 24th chromosome to the human number two chromosome should make colossal sense. This is where the POMC gene lives in humans. Strength is based on different metrics than just hypertrophy. It is based on dopamine and melatonin levels selecting fiber type to make smaller muscles stronger. This defines the muscle changes in our eyes and guts compared to Great Apes. Sonic hedgehog is a member of the hedgehog (Hh) family and was initially recognized as a morphogen protein possessing critical characteristics for neural development during embryogenesis.

Recently, however, Sonic hedge hog has emerged as an essential modulator in adult human neural tissues through different mechanisms such as neurogenesis, anti-oxidation, anti-inflammation, and autophagy. This is a major player in the transition to human. The changes in the immune system from the muscles and gut were transferred to our immune cells, resulting in longevity. Inside each immune cell is a stockpile of POMC. Mitochondria create the light to make melanin do its magic. This new podcast gets into a lot of it in the first ten minutes.

How did this story in the Danny Jones podcast below link JFK, Israel, MOSSAD, and MKULTRA to mRNA jab of 2021? It began with an intelligence failure of the CIA and John Kerry's father. Documents published in Wilson Center from the National Archives now have shed light on a particularly notable intelligence failure: how Washington missed warning signs that the Israelis had a nuclear project underway, but also how the U.S. belatedly realized what the Israelis were doing, and how Eisenhower and his senior advisers reacted to this discovery. Among the new documents are: The June 1959 Israel-Norway secret agreement providing for the sale of Norwegian heavy water to Israel (through the United Kingdom), transmitted by Oslo Embassy political officer Richard Kerry (father of former Secretary of State John Kerry). Reports about information from a then-covert source — University of Michigan nuclear engineering professor Henry Gomberg — who learned that the Israelis had a secret nuclear reactor project that involved experiments with plutonium. A telegram from the U.S. embassy in Tel Aviv reporting on Finance Ministry official Addy Cohen's statement that "we've been misbehaving," and one by an unidentified official close to Prime Minister David Ben-Gurion that the secrecy surrounding Dimona was unjustifiable and that it was "a stupid mistake on the part of Israel." Reports by U.S. Ambassador Ogden Reid on conversations with Ben-Gurion. A State Department message to the embassy in Tel Aviv conveying irritation that the responses of the Israeli government showed a "lack of candor." Messages about a role for the International Atomic Energy Agency in inspecting and safeguarding Dimona. When Eisenhower left office Kennedy assumed this mess but he knew Ben Gurion for over a decade as the picture above shows. Now President Kennedy pressured the government of Prime Minister David Ben-Gurion to prevent a military nuclear program, particularly after stage-managed tours of the Dimona facility for U.S. government scientists in 1961 and 1962 raised suspicions within U.S. intelligence that Israel might be concealing its underlying nuclear aims. This went on during the Cuban Missle crisis and Kennedy was worried about nuclear proliferation post WW2. Kennedy’s long-run objective, documents now show, was to broaden and institutionalize inspections of Dimona by the International Atomic Energy Agency. Of all U.S. leaders in the nuclear age, John F. Kennedy was the nonproliferation president. Nuclear proliferation was his “private nightmare,” as Glenn Seaborg, his Atomic Energy Commission chairman, noted many times in interviews. Seaborg discovered Plutonium for the Industrial military complex leaders. On 30 May 1961, Kennedy met Ben-Gurion in Manhattan to discuss the bilateral relationship and Middle East issues. However, a central (and indeed the first) issue in their meeting was the Israeli nuclear program, about which President Kennedy was most concerned. According to a draft record of their discussion in the National Archives, which has never been cited, but has now been released for the first time by the Wilson Center, Ben-Gurion spoke “rapidly and in a low voice” and “some words were missed.” He emphasized the peaceful, economic development-oriented nature of the Israeli nuclear project. Nevertheless the note taker, Assistant Secretary of State Philips Talbot, believed that he heard Ben-Gurion mention a “pilot” plant to process plutonium for “atomic power” and also say that “there is no intention to develop weapons capacity now.” This information stunned JFK. Ben-Gurion tacitly acknowledged that the Dimona reactor had a military potential. The final U.S. version of the memcon retained the sentence about plutonium but did not include the language about a “pilot” plant and “weapons capacity.” Anyone who understands basic nuclear physics (see my Patreon Thorium blog on BTC) knows the only reason to use plutonium as a fuel is because you can make military grade uranium for nuclear weapons. The differences between the two versions suggest the difficulty of preparing accurate records of meetings. But whatever Ben-Gurion actually said, President Kennedy was never wholly satisfied with the insistence that Dimona was strictly a peaceful project. This really was the main precursor to his death in my estimation. Neither were U.S. intelligence professionals. A recently declassified National Intelligence Estimate on Israel prepared several months after the meeting concluded that “Israel may have decided to undertake a nuclear weapons program. That CIA information was leaked to the mob in 1963 after the Bay of Pigs invasion went south. This information was critical to Santo Trafacante because it allowed him ot gain leverage over Castro and the attempts of the Industrial military complex to kill him versus JFK. Trafacante ensured it was JFK that would pay the ultimate price. He viewed his appointment of RFK Sr as Attorney General as the ultimate act of betrayal to the mob bosses in Chicago, New York, Florida, and New Orleans. In fact, Carlos Marcello was on trial as JFK was shot in Dallas on November 22, 1963 because of an RFK Sr prosecution that Joe Kennedy swore would never happen if the mob swung the swing state of Illinois to JFK in 1960. These details matter big time to understand how things really unfolded. I'll have an explosive blog out soon with even more details on this story. The truth of what one says lies in what one does. Sometimes, the people you care about are walking through life dead. How you react says a lot about you. Redemption requires action, not empathy.

When someone you love dies, you, the living, are given the gift of "second chances." Their eulogy reminds us that the living can turn their lives around at any point. You’re not bound by the past; that is who you used to be. Your decisions and choices help bury your old past. You’re reminded that your feelings are not who you are but how you felt at that moment.

Your bad choices defined you yesterday, but they are not who you are today. Your future doesn’t have to travel the same path with the same people. You can start over when you embrace life. You don’t have to apologize to people who won’t listen. You don’t have to justify your feelings or actions during a difficult time.

You don’t have to put up with people who are insecure and want you to fail. All you have to do is walk forward with a positive outlook and trust that there is a natural plan more incredible than the sorrow you left behind. The people of quality that were meant to be in your life won’t need you to explain the beauty of your heart. This is the best podcast I've done to date. I've been deeply influenced by a group of people I speak about in this podcast. Over the last 35 years, I have been entangled with many powerful people who have inspired me to write about my philosophy for life.

How do you touch others in your own life? Do you use your hands? Your words? Your ideas? Is touch physical for you, or can we do it wirelessly? This podcast reflects this.

Must it be used in close proximity, or can your ability to reach people expand? Can you connect well to anyone anywhere and at any time?

Reading deep thoughts of ideas you get is like undressing before a bath. You don't undress out of fear that your clothes will become wet. You undress because you want the water to touch your body. You want to immerse yourself entirely in the feeling of the water and to emerge anew from the experience.

The most substantial ties we touch others with are the invisible threads nature uses to connect to us.
Touch is a very complex thing that is intoxicating and intangible for most.

Friends might lose touch, but they never seem to lose feelings........Friends tend to misplace their power to touch us. Wounded animals touch each other, and surgeons must touch their patients to heal them. The sun has to touch us to regenerate ourselves to live. Touch is incredibly complex, and how we apply it or misuse it defines our lives.

THE WISE IN LIFE DO NOT FEEL THE TOUCH OF PRESSURE.......THEY APPLY PRESSURE TO TOUCH THINGS THAT MATTER TO THEM.

# Thread: Trying to get what the deal is with latitude
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-get-what-the-deal-is-with-latitude.18706/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DC Brown — 2016-11-08T05:41:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-to-get-what-the-deal-is-with-latitude.18706/post-203703>

I just listed the top 20 countries for longevity, then added for kicks the blue zones (five areas where people on average live longest). I then added factors: average age, latitude, and population density.

There is absolutely no correlation between (average) age and latitude.

There is absolutely no correlation between (average) age and population density.

So why the message being close to the equator is better.

Blue zones (average age > 90)

Okinawa: Lat 26.21, pop/sq mi 1084

Sardinia: Lat 40.12, pop/sq mi 178

Nicoya: Lat 10.14, pop/sq mi 208

Loma Linda: Lat 34.05, pop/sq mi 3094

Ikaria: Lat 37.6, pop/sq mi 86

So these five 'shining examples' (well documented and now under intensive scrutiny) of mean longevity cover a latitudinal range from 10 - 40 degrees, and population densities range from 86 - 3094/sq mile.

The rest of the data (top 20 countries for longevity from CIA factbook) is similar - covering a broad range of both latitudes and population densities with higher and lower extremes for both.

### Dia — 2016-11-08T18:21:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sperti-vitamin-d-lamp.18707/post-203724>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

That's the UV Wavelength for the Sperti D above. The Sperti D is a Vit D lamp. You make Vit D when the wavelength is between 280 and 315 nm, see the range above. The Sperti Fiji is predominantly UVA , 70 - 75% UVA and 25 - 30% UVB (315 to 400 nm), so you will still make some Vit D with it. Sperti Fiji is mostly used for tanning because of the high UVA. UVA also has it's numerous benefit, but if it's D you're after, then the Sperti D is more effective at that.

Indeed it would be nice if @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) would chime in specially that they talk about CRP getting higher.

IIFC, Jack had opinion on why some people can't raise their vitD by supplementing.

I know, I got mine up to 130.0 ng/mL
usually I do not test vitD by regular blood tests.
I do that using Spectracell Micronutrient analysis.
[https://www.spectracell.com/order/](https://www.spectracell.com/order/)
What I get is opinion on my level in personal functional context.
So, supplementing I always had enough.

The other thing is their claim that supplementing with vitD raises crp.
21ng/mL is very low level per the chart I posted and also Sperti have (the link I posted), so something does not sound right.
The chart says that such a low level invites breast cancer, MS and other.

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) is fond of Hs-CRP and many people do it and many have it high.

I wonder what would be Jack's opinion on ESR?

to help detect inflammation associated with conditions such as infections, cancers, and autoimmune diseases
Sedimentation Rate, (ESR), Modified Westergren
(ESR) Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate
Test Number: 005215 CPT: 85652

> But he says there is no set amount of supplementation that can bring someone up to 21 nanograms per milliliter because the way people process vitamins varies. In research published in January 2012 in the American Journal of Cardiology, Amer and Qayyum found that increasing levels of vitamin D in the blood are linked with lower levels of a popular marker for cardiovascular inflammation -- c-reactive protein (also known as CRP). Beyond blood levels of 21 nanograms per milliliter, any additional increase in vitamin D was associated with an increase in CRP, a factor linked to stiffening of the blood vessels and an increased risk of cardiovascular problems.

Nice article, but..
They start with talking about vit D deficiency causing rickets.
Rickets are very important for those who have it, but look at the chart on post #14 here.
Rickets is separate category. Category all by themselves. Category of super low levels of vit D.
I worry more about breast cancer and MS. With that we do not have to worry about rickets.

Good to know that magnesium works with vit D and it is helpful.
That is nice discussion one can learn a lot that way.
This is a type of research that doctor's or scientists and many of us learn from.
I was making lists of this types of dependencies,
until
I found
Spectracell Micronutrient Analysis

If you have not used this analysis, I suggest that you do.
It is rather unique analysis and what they claim it works in practice (for me).
It gives functional status of 35 micronutrients, including vit d and magnesium.
It cost a cash, can be ordered over internet.
Insurance would pay for it if ordered by a doc.

[https://www.spectracell.com/order/](https://www.spectracell.com/order/)
Micronutrient Test (MNT)
**$** 390.00
\----------------------------------------------------
I am using it many years.
What you can expect is consistency from one test to another.
And logical changes when you introduce some protocol.

At one time I was looking for something like that and found NutrEval.
The description and details are impressive there.
To figure it our I did 3 tests one after another, all cash, almost 3x more expensive than Spectracell.
Lots of $$ just to find out that I was not able to make heads or tails out of results.
Each one was very different,

Yes. I assume that the UV exposure from the Spertis, at recommended distance, is much stronger than that from the sun, since the recommended exposure time is much shorter.

I guess they have made the lamps this strong because people want efficiency, just having to use them for a few minutes at a time. But question is if this might be healthy.

Couple ways you can test.
1\. Put light sensitive eyeglasses on Red Sperti, observe if/how fast they get darker
2\. get app with sensor then test
3\. Have a sun deprived, light skinned "friend" standing in front of Red Sperti for 15 minutes touchng lamp, then observe results for the next week.

>
> I've never had PG/E2 test done.
>
> Ive been seeing functional medicine for 2.5 years. Never once has someone mentioned my iodine level, hormones, getting sun, or red light therapy. And out of the thousands of labs they did (at one appointment they took 18 vials of blood) they've never test my iodine.

My personal view about supplementing with Lugols.
It is about halides.
Halides are:
fluoride, chloride, bromide, **iodide** and astatide

We are overloaded with a variety of halides.
We need a large excess of iodide to possibly deal with that excess.
You do not need to test for iodine.
Any excess will be peed out.
Actually, the proper test for iodine is to take a certain measured amount of iodine, test urine and have all that iodine peed out (there is a number, it is in high 90%of excreated iodine after certain amount of time). Otherwise, you would be considered iodine deficient.

PG/E (ZRT) test you can buy on amazon.com

..

For the last few decades
I am squeezing the rubber top and pouring the content into my morning coffee, then I drink the coffee.
Assume about 1cc volume.

Note;
most often those rubber tops degrade and become hard, not flexible.
When you find a good one, cherish it.
Prevent lugols from wetting rubber.

# Thread: way to avoid PPD testing ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/way-to-avoid-ppd-testing.18710/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### motivatedhealthseeker — 2016-11-08T21:21:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/way-to-avoid-ppd-testing.18710/post-203730>

Is there a way to get out of PPD testing for tuberculosis that is required for healthcare workers ? It has live antigens in it and has the possibility to wreak havoc on the immune system. One other option might be a chest x ray which isnt that great of an alternative either. Push comes to shove, what is the lesser or the two evils? Any other ways around it? They also require flu shots which is not requiredddd but then its said you have to wear a mask all the time.

### motivatedhealthseeker — 2016-11-16T23:01:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/way-to-avoid-ppd-testing.18710/post-204002>

A blood test called quantiferon gold could be done in lieu of ppd but ofcourse its not as cost effective.

### Jenny Young — 2020-01-09T18:42:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/way-to-avoid-ppd-testing.18710/post-284365>

Late to the game, but this might help someone else. Just tell them you already tested positive. I tested positive as a teen and work in healthcare and never had to get it redone. They used to recommend a yearly chest x-ray, but now they don't even do that unless you're having symptoms.

-   [**Health and Light**](http://www.amazon.com/Health-Light-extraordinary-Affects-emotional/dp/0898040981) by John N. Ott
-   [**The Healing Sun – Sunlight and Health in the 21st Century**](http://www.amazon.com/Healing-Sun-Sunlight-Health-Century/dp/1899171975/) by Richard Hobday
-   [**Going Somewhere**](http://www.amazon.com/Going-Somewhere-Truth-about-Science/dp/0981854915/) by Andrew Marino
-   [**Power, Sex, Suicide: Mitochondria and the Meaning of Life**](http://www.amazon.com/Power-Sex-Suicide-Mitochondria-Meaning/dp/0199205647) by Nick Lane
-   [**Light in Shaping Life**](http://www.amazon.com/Light-Shaping-Life-Biophotons-Medicine/dp/9081884328) by Roeland Van Wijk
-   [**Life on the Edge**](http://www.amazon.com/Life-Edge-Coming-Quantum-Biology/dp/0307986810/) by Johnjoe McFadden and Jim Al-Khalili
-   [**The Vital Question**](http://www.amazon.com/The-Vital-Question-Evolution-Origins/dp/0393088812) by Nick Lane
-   [**Cosmosapiens**](https://www.amazon.com/Cosmosapiens-Human-Evolution-Origin-Universe/dp/1468312448) by John Hands

### Sun Disciple — 2017-03-15T22:36:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/book-list.18711/post-210431>

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/img_0881-jpg.4522/)
I just finished reading the healing sun. It does a great job at laying out the history of Sun therapy. It chronicles Its use in ancient times from the Egyptians through to Greece and Rome. Also Its disappearance through the dark ages and finally with strong resurgence at the turn of the twentieth century sadly to be again suppressed after the advent of antibiotics. I was intrigued at the traditions of morning sunrise exercise among eastern cultures. I would like to look to find a group that practices tai chi, chi gong or yoga in the traditional way grounded outside at dawn. The one thing is when it comes to diet it fails somewhat as it pushes low fat. Other than that it should be on every mito hackers reading list. A good quote that stuck out from the book is from an old Italian proverb -'Dove non va il sole,va il medico' or 'where the sun does not go, the doctor does.'

Just about to start Cosmo sapiens. Thanks for the list Im almost half way through the above titles.

### MonteD — 2016-11-09T16:52:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-203748>

There's always those things that you know you should be doing but until something makes it real enough you never do it. Well tinnitus was it for me. I've been following Jack's work since he was on Mark's forum years ago. Until now I was just a casual observer happy with repeating the knowledge to everyone I see but not following it myself. Then tinnitus happened. I woke up one morning with a bad headache to the back of my head and a really achy neck. It didn't go away all day, by the next day I got a horrible ringing in my ears that hasn't stopped since. It's affected sleep and given me anxiety. Doctors have been predictably of little help.

Reading through the literature and Jack's work I know this is a byproduct of a sub optimal environment and inflammation. I just purchased the Cornet ED88T Electrosmog Meter so I can remove any sources of nnEMF from my house.

Background:
32yo old male
5'7" @ 230lbs
living in Fort Worth, TX
No other health conditions that I know of...

Protocol: (In progress)

1\. Blue blocking lenses (done)
2\. Removal of nnEMF (ongoing)
3\. Epi-Paleo Rx
4\. Magnesium L-threonate
5\. Epsom Salt Soaks 3x weekly
6\. Ginger and Turmeric daily
7\. Vitamin K2
8\. LLLT therapy (hopefully)

I'll update back here when I know more and especially after playing around with my new meter!  If anyone has any suggestions or sees anything I'm missing let me know and thank you!

### MonteD — 2016-11-20T18:49:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-204120>

Thanks for all the responses!!! I believe it is time for an update!

First some history: My tinnitus, I believe, started two weeks ago after I woke up with a bad headache at the back of my head and neck. I went outside and stretched really hard at the back of the neck and then bam I got this ringing in the ears. At first it was rather severe but with the advice of a very good doctor who is part of the Quantum Biology group told me to take high doses of turmeric and systemic enzymes. By day 3 I was had a crazy pop sound in my left ear and the tinnitus got much quieter. Ever since then it has been a very high pitched background noise that rarely goes away. My ears still pop and I did feel a bit sick for a while. The sick feeling is fading in the last 3 or 4 days and finally yesterday I felt major breakthrough in my eustachian tube not popping all the damn time. This morning however, the high pitched tone is still their. If I move my head I can change the sound of the tinnitus. This, to me, is a clear indication of something physical whether that be some type of joint issue or just inflammation I don't know. The tinnitus seems to get better during the late afternoon and then get worse in the early AM or second part of sleep. I don't understand this pattern.

Now the good stuff:
In the last week I've started taking therapeutic doses of

1\. Turmeric (3x daily),
2\. Ginger
3\. Grape seed extract
4\. Systemic enzymes
5\. DHA (1 grams per day) from fish
6\. At least a half gallon of RO water daily.

The Cornett has shown me that:

1\. My noise canceling headphones were cooking my brain. No longer.
2\. My microwave was effectively cooking my entire house. No longer.
3\. The smart meter is now wrapped in foil.
4\. Cellphone is in airplane at all times I am not using it.
5\. The router is unplugged after 10pm.

A non coil spring mattress is on the way as the coils may be interfering with the tinnitus as well by acting as antenna for magnetism or nnEMF.

Reading the latest blog it appears like adopting a ketogenic seafood based diet Epi-Paleo Rx may help me a bit during this time. It also seems like the quantlet may help.

### PaulG — 2016-11-21T01:17:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-204127>

With regards to wrapping smart meter in foil, if it is on an external house wall, that may be a good strategy for your neighbour but not for you as it reflects back the radiation into your house.

What I did is put up a large book case against the wall opposite the meter, covered the back in multiple layers of foil and then grounded it by attaching a crocodile clip and running a wire out the window into the earth.

Also worth a try ringing your electricity provider (more than once!) and asking for it to be swapped out for an analogue one as it is giving you medical issues.

If the tinnitus is coming back in the middle of the night or in the morning, you may want to check the back of your walls in your room for magnetic EMF sources like a gas or electrical meter or even a cable cord running on the outside of the wall. I'm assuming you're shutting down power to your room from the circuit breaker. If you're not, consider unplugging all lamps, clocks or any devices near your bed when you are sleeping, and just to play it safe, move the bed itself about 2 inches away from the wall into the room. If you have a game console, also unplug from the wall because of the RF emission. If these things don't fix the problem, then at least it helps to eliminate variables.

### Sue-UK — 2016-11-21T15:34:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-204143>

I started getting intermittent mild tinnitus in September, usually when I woke up, or sometimes after visiting my Mum in the nnemf hell nursing home. I did a search of the word tinnitus on the forum, and cut and paste any comments Jack had made on the subject. My Dad had chronic tinnitus, my mother didn't but started losing her hearing in her early fifties, by her 70s she was extremely deaf, and developed AD in her 80s, (dots connected from things Jack had said) so with the maternal mitochondria link I was interested in why I was suddenly getting tinnitus. I notice it more in the mornings after I have had a good nights sleep with vivid dreams .... So its got me wondering if, if tinnitus is the brain's way of trying to heal itself by creating phonons to raise the DC electric current in the brain to increase the local melatonin levels to shrink the respiratory proteins, whether - in my maternal mitochondrial context- in some cases this could be a good sign? At least my brain is able to try after a good night's sleep......? My main "treatment" is to drink a lot of water, and go outside and CT (30 minutes of 5C in the pouring rain this morning), with grounding, and photon scavenging. To start with it would take 20 minutes for the tinnitus to disappear, its now down to about 15 minutes, maybe because the water is getting colder and shrinking the respiratory proteins faster.

### Scompy — 2016-11-21T20:48:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-204159>

Since I have a tinnitus super-power that comes and goes very quickly, I've observed some longer periods of tinnitus CLEARING after eating 85% or higher dark chocolate. The nnEMF gets the tinnitus to kick in within a minute, but if I had just eaten like a half bar of dark chocolate about an hour previously, the tinnitus is louder and takes a little longer for it to go away (like about a half-hour instead of 10 mins).

SPECIAL NOTE: I have a massive control over my eardrums and opening/closing them. I had over 100 inner ear infections as a child, and I can literally get fresh air to be pulled in and out of my eardrum whenever I want and somehow open/close my inner ear drums. Secondly, part of this airflow can even go into my sinuses and up into my eyes. Case in point... one time eating salsa and doing this breathing technique, I got hotsauce into my eye itself from the sinuses.

Here is what I suspect as a possibility: due to the caffeine and dehydration influence, it's somehow influencing the EZ within my inner ear fluid. Possibly because of my breathing, I'm able to cool down the area quickly and pull in water/moisture from the air to shut down the tinnitus as another thought.

### Valuation Man — 2016-11-22T02:14:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-204188>

I have struggled with Tinnitus for well over two decades. My tinnitus is much louder on my right side and it could be related to an injury I had as a teen when I knocked out teeth playing football. It changes tone and loudness with movement of jaw and neck.

There is someone on the following forum that has said he has cured his tinnitus by using very similar protocols as we learn here - except 1.4 . There are some good conversations there that may assist you in coming up with hacks for this.

[https://www.tinnitustalk.com/thread...faster-from-tinnitus.16236/page-2#post-200859](https://www.tinnitustalk.com/threads/health-tools-to-recover-faster-from-tinnitus.16236/page-2#post-200859)

My failed hacks include:
1) TMJ Splint therapy
2) Regular and Cervical Chiro
3) Massage
4) Various diets and supplements
5) Seeing ENT

I have seen significant improvement in all my other health issues and energy since I became aware of this site and switched my focus to increasing my Quantum Yield (Sun, Grounding, Water, CT, Magnetico, limiting Blue light). But the Tinnitus remains the same. I do accept that I live in nnEMF area (UHF TV Tower very near) and will be moving soon to more remote area away from all towers and wifi.

This year I was accepted at Ohio State University in their study of Tinnitus and they are providing free services and advice on this. Right now, they are testing me with hearing aids that provide a zen like tone. This is both masking and habituation. Only wear them when I am home because they are ugly and unsure about if there is emf issues with wearing them.

Read Jack's recent blog and made an appointment with craniosacral massage therapist and there are some positive comments about this on the Tinnitus talk forum.

A couple of questions about some of the post on this thread and perhaps future hacks for me:
1) LLLT - How long do you put the light on the impacted area
2) Oxygen - Could increasing oxygen to brain through breathing exercises or EWOT reduce tinnitus?

### MonteD — 2016-12-02T07:11:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-204616>

Well finally a good breakthrough. I was sitting down after eating my morning DHA and the volume of the tinnitus did a sudden drop to half of the volume it was before! It has been getting better slowly but surely every day but today was a dramatic drop.

I've been doing the turmeric and system enzymes every day. I added monolaurin for an immunity boost in case this was due to some sort of viral infection. At the same time the Cornet Electrosmog meter was very very very helpful. It showed my headphones were cooking my brain and this was likely what led to inflammation and infection because of what we know about nnEMF and bacterial growth. I'm sure turning off the wifi at night, going to bed earlier, keeping the phone in airplane mode, eating DHA every morning and drinking lots of alkaline water has been a big help.

Also, I stopped obsessing and sending negative energy to the ear (psychosomatically making the noise louder if nothing else) and I started getting better almost immediately. Now I can only here in a very quiet room. I suspect that will improve in the next few weeks as well as I can still pressure and tingling in the ear and my tubes are still popping and crackling at times but no where near what it used to be.

p.s. ....I dropped 15 pounds in 3 weeks. wow....

### MonteD — 2017-01-12T20:04:48+0200

I'm hearing the tinnitus in less and less situations now. Now I only hear it in very quiet environments or if I wake up in the middle of night. My ETD which started the same day as the tinnitus has consistently gotten better and better. In fact, every time it improves the tinnitus improves right along with it. It seems like every two weeks I get a lot better. I have no doubt that adhereing to quantum principles saved me from more long-term/permanent damage. At this rate I would be very surprised if I'm dealing with this in 6 weeks.

My protocol is still daily DHA, epsom salt baths, grounding, and nnEMF mitigation.

Monte there are not sad to say.........and one more thing.......your tinnitus in Texas should be worse at night than at day.

Why?

protons.

How can you hack that? Pull out your RF, trimeter, and gausmeter and check day and night. Texas has a massive swing from day to night for several reasons above and below ground..........

So why will your EMF meters varying during the day in the same environment when you test? Sunlight makes protons rise and darkness makes them condense on things on Earth. Is Vitamin A the brain's EMF meter the same way your devices are? Yep. This means tinnitus is too.

Vitamin A and your cochlea are akin to your brain RF/trimeter/gaussmeter device. These meters should give different readings at different times of the day or night. It should give different readings at different times because electric and magnetic fields change as night and daylight changes. In fact as the January 2017 webinar tells you Vitamin A is a bio-molecule that is how the brain measures electric and magnetic fields. It is not as big a deal for hearing as vision but it matters.............This is why all opsin are bound to Vitamin. It tells the opsin something powerful about light.

The sun’s light is a cathode ray. When it hits the Earth in daytime, the Earth acts as an anode in this cosmologic circuit and this allows for more evaporation of water on the planet's surface to create more protons to dissipate in the atmosphere. Simultaneously the evaporaation effect will liberate more electrons from Earth that our tensegrity system connected to the Earth via our limbs is designed to collect. We collect and harvest this energy to store or use to do physiologic work to lower the resistance of our inner mitochondrial membrane. This reduces the electrical resistance of mitochondria and this helps stimulate autophagy and not apoptosis. During daytime the electric field of Earth is higher than it is at night when light is absent. Magnetic fields, however, are higher at night, and this is likely why sleep is linked to a loss of the DC electric current in diurnal animals. Protons at night are more plentiful around you and this makes the tinnitus worse at night.
