TINNITUS FACTOID: Cranial Nerve 8 (acoustic/vestibular nerve) is a bigger canary........hence why tinnitus is a big clue to a poor environemnt around your brain case. There is a growing body of research showing radiation from cellphones, wireless devices, and wifi is harmful. Minimize your exposure by placing it in airplane mode while carrying against your body, use an airtube headset or speaker phone while talking, and turn off your wifi at night.
"Trigeminal neurons were capable of detecting temperature changes far below skin temperature increases caused by cellphone radiation. Simulated cellphone radiation affected brain electrical activity associated with nonlinear cognitive processing of radiation-induced thermal afferent signals. Radiation standards for cellphones based on a thermal/nonthermal binary distinction do not prevent neurophysiological consequences of cellphone radiation." [http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/15368378.2016.1194294?src=recsys&journalCode=iebm20](http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/15368378.2016.1194294?src=recsys&journalCode=iebm20)

### MonteD — 2017-02-15T06:51:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-208631>

Time for an update.

The tinnitus has subsided almost completely with a dramatic decrease in the last few days. My 6 weeks prediction seems to be on track. I don't notice it in the day and at night it is so faint that it doesn't bother me at all. If I get better, at all, I'll consider myself completely healed. I hope for the best but I'm cautious because I've gotten better and then much worse before......

Here is what I'm doing/have done:

1\. New mattress without coils. Wooden bed frame as well.
2\. sleeping with the windows open.
3\. Daily seafood intake. Aiming for at least 1/2 pound.
4\. At least 8 cups of RO water daily
5\. LOTS MORE SUN AND GROUNDING. Sun has recently gotten much stronger in Texas. I have been in the backyard for at least 1 hour every morning.
6\. I got rid of an old electric fan in the room. I think this was big. I tested it with the Cornet and thought it was far enough away from me....it was not apparently because within days of removing it...I got better. Coincidence? Maybe. Maybe not.
7\. I wear the blue blockers more...but still not nearly enough
8\. Phone charges in another room. Phone is in airplane mode most of the day.

Supplements:
Nature's Plus Krill Oil (high astaxanthin) Only on days I don't eat fish.
New Chapter MindForce. Contains Lion's Mane Mushroom which is excellent for stimulating BDNF, NGF, remyelination, anti-inflammatory, immune boosting, anti-anxiety, anti-stress.
New Chapter Turmeric Force
New Chapter Omega 7. Excellent for inflammation and hydration.
Systemic Enzymes. Various brands, Wobenzyme, American Biologics, Helion.

Rx:
Nasal spray: Azelastine (Astepro)

I'm excited. Let's see where I am in two weeks with even stronger sun.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-15T23:57:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/goodbye-tinnitus-montes-optimal-journal.18712/post-208669>

Poor mitochondrial function strikes again in another sense under attack in this modern world we've created. The small mitochondrial protein, Fus1 acts like a small carbon nanotubes in the inner mitochondrial membrane. Membrane fusion allows the exchange of cellular contents including cytoplasmic proteins, nuclei, mitochondria, secretory organelles, and vacuoles. Fus 1 is such a fusion protein. This protein acts like a carbon nanotube connecting the cytosol to the outer and inner mitochondrial membrane. When this nanotube like structure is defective structurally, electronically, or atomically it acts like an aberrent carbon nanotube that disrupts the inner mitochondrial membrane to increase ROS and lead to hearing damage. It is now well known in the literature that carbon nanotubes (CNTs) are capable of instirgating damage in cells. CNT's are novel materials with unique electronic and mechanical properties. There are many published reports that multi-walled carbon nanotubes (MWCNT) have potent, dose-dependent toxicity on cultured human cells. Molecular characterization studies have revealed that MWCNT induced substantial ROS production and mitochondrial inner membrane depolarization at sub-toxic doses that lead to end organ damage leading to tissue failure from energy deficits. MWCNT have been shown to stimulate the secretion of a panel of inflammatory cytokines and chemokines (TNFalpha, IL-1beta, IL-6, IL-10 and MCP1) from macrophages (Raw264.7) by activating the canonical NF-kappaB signaling pathway linked to leptin control. Activation of NF-kappaB signaling involves rapid degradation of NkappaB alpha, nuclear accumulation of NF-kappaBp65, binding of NF-kappaB to specific DNA-binding sequences, and transactivation of target gene promoters leading to cellular and tissue wide damage. Sadly this basic research has not reached the clinical world as this article from Yale clearly demonstrates. Quantum clinicians do not miss this kind of information. Tinnitus is a warning shot from your cochlea that your redox level in Ammon's horn is not good at all. Sound waves (phonons) are the pulse of the ocean in our cells, in our cochlea, or endolymphic sac and gives the spark of life that nature is capable of giving to life. Hearing is a sense built around air and the atmosphere. The atmopshere is a sphere within a sphere and it can create and electrical tension within the magnetosphere and the surface of earth where the gases we breathe and exhale is located. The Earth is a sphere surrounded by the sphere of ionosphere. That tension becomes an "acoustic pulse wave" for things that live within both spheres. When the tension changes in the environment the pattern of signaling in the hearing mechanisms changes things in these sensory cells. Blue light and nnEMF change the tension in that sphere to increase ubiquitination rates on all things. Tinnitus is the result you get when this occurs. These waves in our sensory cells connect to the tension present in tides of the sea in our cochlea and our endolymphic sac and they connect to us in ways few observe. Tides bring food to creatures and tides take boats out to sea. A sailor has the impression that the ocean is made of waves more than it is made of water because of how they sense them. The same is true with the sensory system. Heredity is about waves too but few realize it. Most humans believe life is about genetic waves in the nuclear genome but guys like me think it is about the environmental waves the crash and collide into our mitochondria and make mitochondrial waves that impact the inner mitochondrial membrane and the respiratory proteins to effect electron and proteon tunneling are they in turn make free radical signals and they quantize to the light a mitochondria liberates. It is general expression of the periodicity of organic life that is buried in our mitochondria. All generations belong to a continuous succession of waves of some sort, in which every single one resembles its predecessors and its followers yet changes because the world around forces that change.[http://news.yale.edu/2017/02/07/antioxidants-and-hearing-loss](http://news.yale.edu/2017/02/07/antioxidants-and-hearing-loss)

### notsoperfick — 2017-02-16T19:59:36+0200

I've had 24 hour pulsatile tinnitus for 30 years! It's only on one side, the right. I also have significant hearing impairment in that ear. I was once told I had conductive deafness but wasn't offered anything to determine that and whether or not surgery might effect a cure. My latest assessment suggested otosclerosis which may be an inherited disposition which makes sense as both my mother, sister and both my daughters suffer in the same way with the same ear. # Thread: Jaguar's Journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jaguars-journey.18718/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jason Siegel — 2016-11-11T04:32:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jaguars-journey.18718/post-203812>

I am excited and honored to introduce myself here in the forum. My name is Jason and I am in Monmouth County, New Jersey. The most important thing in this world to me is individual freedom- the right of every person that is born into this world to their own body and all of its parts, and all of our natural and maybe infinite potential to physically and emotionally feel, to connect with our bodies, each other, and the Earth, to critically think, and to explore and create. Every day I still feel surprised and hurt when I see that humanity is largely under a spell of cognitive programming to a accept and be complicit in beliefs and behaviors that hurt us and limit those capacities we all have to feel, connect, think, and do.

I present myself as a rebel and leader against any religious/cultural/pseudo-medical efforts to suppress in any way our capacity to physically feel, to critically think, to connect to each other and to the Earth, and to create. I like to explore how even though it hurts and we experience so many scary and hurtful feelings because of the controllers, it is also so exciting and such an honor to be here standing for freedom and health and critical thinking against these forces of domestication and control, and the way it feels exciting and beautiful and fun to be in it together- the resistance.

I graduated from school with a degree in a biology in 2012, and was wondering about going into a PhD program but decided to work on learning, experimenting, exploring, and building resources in my own ways outside of the system for now. My goal is to unlock the ability of full tissue regeneration in the human body, so that someone who has lost part of their body is able to grow it back with 100% correct structure and function.

I suspect that there is an information field body- a sort of holographic blueprint or map of the exquisite three-dimensional architecture of the body. Projected from the central nervous system, this system of storage and processing of morphological information imbues every cell with a certain resting membrane potential (e.g. 43.7 millivolts), and this voltage directs its differentiation into into cell type (e.g. nerve cell), and it's awareness of its position in the body (eg. "position 24" in this Meisner's corpuscle nerve in this part of the fourth finger on the left hand).

All of the information and abilities are there, and now we just have to unlock the permission/instructions for the body to dedifferentiate and mobilize stem cells to fill in their positions properly in the map just as they did the first time, in embryonic development. I have read Dr. Robert O. Becker's book and this approach is inspired by the work of Dr. Michael Levin and his lab at Tufts University ([https://ase.tufts.edu/biology/labs/levin/](https://ase.tufts.edu/biology/labs/levin/)). I have been in contact with him through e-mail, and he is following the work of Dr. Gerald Pollack. Although he works with optogenetics (eg. expressing/activating a blue-light activated cation channel and a green-light activated proton pump to prevent and normalize tumors in tadpole embryos), Dr. Levin may not have considered yet the effects of the presence of extensive nnEMF and fluorescent light bulbs and the absence of sunlight and Earth magnetism in his laboratory.

My two main personal health challenges now are to heal from my chronic injury/pain along with returning to my athletics and to break though a kind of anxiety/sadness/fog which involves me being controlled by intrusive/irrational thoughts and stops me from doing things that I really want to do in situations where I actually have everything to gain and nothing to lose.

I eat an Epi-Paleo style diet, experiment with cold thermogenesis in the ocean, walk around barefoot every day in the wet grass and look toward the sun in the morning, and pay attention to WiFi and nnEMF exposure. I have not been doing well yet, however, with going to sleep early enough and waking up early enough to get into the sun between sunrise and 9AM.

I am fortunate enough to have the freedom to go anywhere and do anything that I want, and I have been struggling with anger towards myself and shame for all that I am not doing- being held back by anxiety/sadness/lack of focus largely related to a healthy body body part being taken from me against my will as a baby, "approach/assessment anxiety" relates to approaching any girl or group of people that I am curious about, and by chronic physical injury at the left sacroiliac joint, thoracolumbar junction, right knee, and left ankle.

Since I am free to be where I want now, I am going to start by getting a one-way plane ticket to a more safe and healthy environment. I think I will first go back to Costa Rica, where I lived for 3 months last year. I would like to learn more with all of you about determining the most ideal environments for me to be in. In Costa Rica, my home base has been in a place called the Diamante Valley, in the more sparsely populated and visited Southern Zone of the country, in a house within an organic permaculture community with multiple cold water springs for the drinking water. The altitude is about 2500 feet, and there are old growth rainforest ridges rising up over 1000 feet around. There are and many cool water river and forest realms to explore and two big waterfalls nearby. It is about 7 miles away and a 30 minute drive away from the beaches of the Pacific Ocean. It is also about a 30 minute drive from the nearest city, San Isidro de el General, which is a fun place to explore and hang out with my friends who live there and go to the University and to make new friends. Last year I began to get to know some good new characters from all around the world who came to the area to help create new communities with permaculture and new kinds of school and ways of doing things. I would like to learn and work together more with my friend who is the farm manager in this community. There is an special "feria" or organic farmers market for the valley every Tuesday, which is a good place to come together and learn about what's going on, play with the little kids, meet new friends, and try new foods. I also got to meet new Costa Rican friends and begin immersing in Spanish. I met three girls exploring on the river near the farm. I started hanging out and exploring a lot with one of them and soon she invited me to come stay at her house and also to come to culture class with her at the University where the girls are in the Diplomacy and English program. I got to meet more new friends and started to come to class every Wednesday evening, helping the students, helping the professor, and getting to know my new friends, staying over at friends' houses that night before going to the San Isidro feria market on Thursday mornings.

There is a lot of potential in that area, but I am also wondering about exploring somewhere new soon, and concerned about some things- There are "WiMax" cell towers in Costa Rica that enable internet access throughout rural areas and do work at my home base there in the valley. People like to get wi-fi modems that pick up this signal and then transmit it to their computers, or just get the internet access and cell service on their phones. In many of the Costa Rican homes there are satellites to pickup television signals. I really like being in the rural valley area and having the cool night temperatures and peaceful more isolated feelings there, but I wonder if it would be most optimal for me to down at sea level close to the beach, where I could easily jump in the ocean any time. The beach area near there around Dominical and Uvita has much more population density and nnEMF going on, but I would consider staying in the Osa Peninsula, which is further south and is mostly protected by Corcovado National Park. It is a bit of a challenge not having a vehicle there or in the valley as well but easier in the Valley with ride sharing and hitchhiking being more accessible. Maybe I should go to the Yucatan Peninsula for awhile? I am curious about the area of Dzilam de Bravo, which is at the coast right near the edge of the Chicxclub Crater cenotes ring and the zone of incredible abundance of cenotes to the east and south, away from major population centers and tourism. I am also wondering about other places including higher altitude areas pretty close to the coast in Panama, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, French Guiana, and Brazil.

The day that I began to realize that something is very deeply wrong with our situation here was the day that I began to learn about the shocking and unethical practice of child genital cutting- the amputation of healthy functioning parts of the genitals of innocent babies or children, obviously without their consent or any medical emergency that could call for proxy consent. In males this brings about a loss/inversion of the internal private realm that every boy and girl is designed to have and the tissues that are removed are extensive and possess an incredible neurological complexity, with approximately 20,000 Meisner's corpuscles just at the mucocutaneous junction alone (also found in the fingertips and the lips of the mouth) discerning fine touch in a detailed high-resolution manner, stretch perception, and much more. ([http://www.doctorsopposingcircumcision.org/for-professionals/sexual-impact/](http://www.doctorsopposingcircumcision.org/for-professionals/sexual-impact/)) This must be performed on infants so that men will "never know the difference" and "won't complain".

Fortunately, every day, more people are joining the effort to help activism and educating about this issue and also the practice of "foreskin restoration," which involves non-surgical techniques to place tension on the tissues near the amputation scar that induces mitosis and slowly regrows skin tissue and inner mucous membrane tissue (on the other side of the scar), until enough tissue is grown to cover the glans and close at the tip again, recreating the inside space and finally allowing all the remaining mucosal tissues to be not be exposed to clothing and air and to shed the layers of keratin that formed as permanent emergency callous over the exposed surfaces.

On that day I was devastated and in shock that I was forbidden from experiencing the structure and functions of the parts that were taken from me and that my remaining interior parts were not actually supposed to be keratinized, scarred, and rubbing against clothes all day. I wanted and still want so badly just to be able to feel what it felt like to be intact and feel like I would trade anything just to have my parts back. Working together with my friends to support each other and try to be positive, doing activism, and most powerfully doing "foreskin restoration" and "retaining" the skin forward and closed with a silicone cone and medical adhesive, have helped me to get through it, but this problem affects me deeply emotionally every day, wanting so badly to have the right to my own body and to feel each of those tens of thousands of missing nerve endings and those parts that are part of the proprioceptive map of my body and connect to parts of my brain that are all off-limits right now. I know that it will be one of the issues I can fully address after unlocking complex tissue regeneration.

It is really important to me to be able to talk to any girl or group of people in any time or place, even though there is all this initial fear of embarrassment and rejection, and so much going on right now that makes people people like its "awkward" or abnormal to reach out to others and go outside of your comfort zone. I have this strong dread/anxiety feeling that makes me want to avoid approaching girls and being "assessed" by them and it is very important to me to overcome this irrational feeling so it doesn't bother me all the time and so that I can approach anyone I want at any time- so that with my curiosity and playfulness and passion I can open up infinite corridors of possibility. I like to be curious, adventurous, and playful, and create situations where I can encourage that in others and help move towards really connecting and feeling alive.

The problem is irrational anxiety about approaching and being "assessed" by a girl that I am attracted to and curious about. I want to be happy to go anywhere with lots of people around and able to, without much hesitation or worry, approach any girl or group of people in any place, and, coming from a place of genuine expression and curiosity, talk to them and open up the door to connecting with that person, learning about them, having fun, and all kinds of exciting situations that would otherwise not be possible. If they don't feel like talking to me right now, or "reject" me in some way, or if I don't feel like talking to them anymore, then I can politely say goodbye and walk away satisfied and content knowing that I checked what was currently behind that door of possibility. I can rationally fully understand this but I am still controlled by irrational dread and anxiety that makes me want to avoid situations altogether, because I will be so angry and ashamed with myself if I skip out on approaching someone and then have to wonder what would have happened. I need to be rational and know that I have everything to gain and nothing to lose any time I introduce myself to a new girl, and I need to regain control of this hijacked fear instinct, feel fully feel safe and loved, and love myself no matter what anyone thinks, so that I can be free, and not enslaved to this very social control and fear of rejection that I so adamantly speak out against. I now understand that in my poor environment I have been in all this time, I must have fundamental imbalances in my circadian biology, hormones, and neurotransmitters. I need to address this on physical/environmental level, not only through forcing myself into tough situations (which makes me feel much better temporarily), figuring out how to truly love myself, and meditation and working on gaining captainship of my thoughts.

My chronic pain started with a small muscle spasm in my left lower back in 2006 following a comeback from a minor hamstring strain. Since then, I have had constant dull pain in the left lumbar and sacroiliac joint area with some kind of nerve entrapment affecting down to the left foot, and I have been all over the place with extensive exercise and chiropractic and other modalities in my attempts to get better. In 2014, I had a minor ankle sprain, In 2015, I had a minor knee strain, and in August of this year I took a hit from a wave that I caught too late right around the thoracolumbar junction which hyperextended me a bit there. All of these injuries have seemingly stayed about the same without healing, and I more recently began to realize that maybe something was wrong on a deeper full-body level, rather than just investigating the biomechanics of the sacroiliac joint and lumbar spine, and muscle activation/development. I understand now again that, my poor environment has got me in a state of low net negative charge, low redox potential, and ongoing parasympathetic "fight-or-fight" type state that does not provide me with the setting and raw materials I need in order to heal.

I am ready to experiment and learn do whatever is necessary to begin to heal, so that I can best help others, and work towards my missions and dreams. Thank you so much to all of you for your compassion and hard work getting to the truth and understand the way things work so we can move towards being truly safe and free.

### Jason Siegel — 2016-11-12T06:38:58+0200

Thank you Jenny for your warm welcome. I am excited to be here with all of you and begin learning and experimenting with myself, and to move towards a position of being able to help everyone else too.

Thank you Caroline, I am actually about to acquire Jack's book in a few days.

I am really eager to work on my dreams including unlocking human tissue regeneration, finding my full uninhibited voice for creative expression and interaction with others, and illuminating/eliminating the threats to individual freedom such as ubiquitous nnEMF, unethical use of drugs and surgery on children, and the harmful cultural cognitive programing about the body and Nature that make minds susceptible to accepting these choices in the first place. I will work to promote critical thinking and expose corruption and indoctrination, in the realms of science, medicine, law, education, and everywhere.

I recognize, however, that I have some serious work I need to do with myself in order to feel better, start functioning optimally, and access my potential.

I love my family and friends here but I have to go away from here now. I wish that I could go to work as a quantum biology apprentice with Dr. Kruse, a volunteer to help with experimentation and true innovation in health. I am free to go work as a volunteer anywhere now in health/biology, activism/conservation, permaculture, alternative education, or any appropriate field. I am ready to get my one-way plane ticket and just start with reseting and my Dr. Kruse protocols in a more optimal/safe environment, such as the Diamante Valley in Costa Rica, where I already have some friends and leads for projects, adventures, and missions.

Knowing that I am able to go anywhere in the world and do anything right now, does anyone have any leads or suggestions for me?

For now some of my basic qualifications are a Bachelor's Degree in Biology, beach lifeguard, experience working with children, intermediate Spanish speaking and of course Spanish guitar playing and singing.

# Thread: Functional endo says to convert T4 to T3 lots of vegetables/fruits activate Deiodinase. Leptin rx?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/functional-endo-says-to-convert-t4-to-t3-lots-of-vegetables-fruits-activate-deiodinase-leptin-rx.18723/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 1

### Stanislava — 2016-11-11T17:04:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/functional-endo-says-to-convert-t4-to-t3-lots-of-vegetables-fruits-activate-deiodinase-leptin-rx.18723/post-203833>

Hello
Ive seen my functional endo. And I told him Ive tried cynomel T3 100mcg with T3/T4 medication he prescribed me.
And I have felt great and healthy for a first time in 3 years.
I know I know I dont convert well.
He freaked out with dose of 120mg of T3 in total.
And said to activate Deiodinase we need lots of vegetable and fruits. 80% veggies for 20 %.
And high fat is not needed.
Not very epi paleo leptin reset friendly.
And it was going against what I was trying to with leptin reset.

He changed my meds. Instead of T3/T4 mix. Ive started on levo. He increased my T4 with levo from 100 slowly to 150 and later try 175.
And 30mcg of T3 cynomel.

Only problem with high levo I get
dizzines, black spots fainting episodes. Basically my Postural tachycardia syndrome comes back.
Not on T3 higher dose.

Then later in the night got horrible constant adrenaline surges with hot feeling on my head and whole body tremors which lasted whole night when too much thyroid hormones lowered my cortisol.

Now he wants me on betablockers for 2-3 days to stop adrenaline surges.

But my functional endo is trying to convince me that high T4 is not issue when I get Pots episodes.

Im on Hydrocortisone 20mg in 2-3 doses for last 2 years with thyroid meds. And adrenal adaptogens.

And lots of supplements for those who would be interested
Like coq10 200mg, pqq 20mg, magnesium malate 800mg (threonate and all others give me nausea and closing throat)vitamin A betacarotene complex
Vitamin B complex
B12methyl
B9 methyl
pregnenolone 50mg
selenium 200mcg
vitamin C 3gr
vitamin D3/K2 8000UI
N acetyl cysteine 1800mg
biotin 5000mcg
zinc copper 50mg
quercetin
ginger
glycine 1gr
b6 as p5p 100mg
Fish almost daily cant do seafood with histamine intolerance mcas
progesterone cream 200mg in 2 doses
No iodine bit apprehensive with histamine intolerance.

# Thread: April's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aprils-journal.18724/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ab70bandit — 2016-11-11T18:29:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aprils-journal.18724/post-203838>

I thought it's time I write my journal.
I my self have health issues, but I desperately search for others in my family, if I can help them it will take a burden off me.
I came across jack after searching for help , years of clean eating and supplementing just wasn't showing a difference.
I don't come from a family with longevity, **on my Mom's side** - grandmother died at 60 of massive coronary, my grandfather died at 69 after 2 bouts of cancer years apart(bowel & Throat). My Mom's siblings(2) - Aunt Jes died at 42 of Pancreatic cancer and my uncle Mick had a major stroke at 50 and after 9 years in a nursing home dies at 59. My grand mother suffered for 30 years before she died with severe depression(shock treatments) and terrible digestive issues before finally dying of the heart attack. I was 11 when she died...I remember her to be short tempered & sad. My grandfather died when I was 18, I remember him as a happy, easy going man, my mom said she had never heard him raise his voice.
My mom is a mixture of both parents: Kind, caring, short tempered(that's the red head). She has suffered from depression for the past 20 years. It was mainly seasonal for many years but it all changed 2 years ago when she was diagnosed with an asymptomatic UTI found on a routine blood test, six months later & 5 courses of antibiotics, she became quite ill & her depression became 10 times worse & it just never went away.
My mom is a pale skinned, blue eyed red head.
**On my father's side** \- I did not know my father, my parents split when I was a toddler, my mom was left a single mother of 2 toddlers. My father started a new family & never looked back(he tried to contact me when I was 20 but I wasn't interested).
All I know about my father's side of the family is that my grandfather was a bad diabetic, who died early from diabetic complications in his early sixties and my father was a bad diabetic that died from complications at 56.
I am also fair skinned, although not as fair as my mom & I have dark hair & dark eyes. None of my family members are or were obese or even over weight. I've been a little over weight ever since having my daughter(prior to that I was always under weight).
**My only brother** \- was diagnosed at 30 with an autoimmune disease - **Ankylosing spondylitis(**I hope I spelled that right) and his condition is progressing.
**My daughter Meg** - is a high functioning Autistic young lady of 20. She also takes tonic-clonic seizures. Skin issues(eczema) She is my only child and the light of my life. Meg is high functioning and very smart. She loves online crossword puzzles - she can finish a puzzle in 30 seconds - she has the highest score posted online. Her brain can work at lightning speed visually but verbally she is like a small child. She has difficulty with expressive/receptive speech...you may have to ask her a question twice or give her time to think about a simple question that is asked of her. Her expressive speech can be somewhat incomplete or broken sentences. Visually you can teach her almost anything. Since applying the 'Six Healers, as well as, using natural calcium blockers daily, I have seem a little progress with her speech and socialization, not as much as I would like. One thing I have noticed, she use to have a strong smell of ammonia to her(I thought it was her diet but even changing her diet didn\`t take that away) not I don\`t smell it now. Meg is on a Ketogenic diet for the seizures, she had her first one at 13 then she didn\`t have another until a year later, 2 in one week, I think it was stress because I was getting gallbladder surgery and she liked to read up on these things & in the write up she read that in rare cases people have died from gallbladder surgery, she had 2 seizures that week 2 days apart. We decided not to put her on medication...it was scary because they were tonic-clonic seizures, I just kept up the Keto diet. She went 11 months after that before her next seizure, then 23 months after that. Then she went 13 months, then 4 months, then 3 weeks. I started her on the Six Healers in june with myself, a couple of weeks after a seizure. Things were going good, & I was really hopeful, but she had another seizure 3 weeks ago, I had her following everything pretty much to the letter. That got me down.
I joined early the site in early summer of this year. I found everything I was reading fascinating and although most of it over my head, the material I understood resonated with me. I had tried many things nutritionally to help family members with some success but it was limited, I was searching for more when I came across Jack's site.
When I told my mother about the things I was reading on the site(at this point she was in really bad shape with suicidal depression), she was very skeptical, but because of low dopamine, easy to lead.
It took months of reminding my mother to take off her sun glasses(which she wore 90% of the time - saying that she was sensitive to the light & it covered up her sad eyes) eventually it became a habit. We ground every morning between 8 & 8:30 before I leave for work(she won't ground at her house because of nosy neighbours so close on either side) my home is in a more wooded area.
It has taken many months, but my mom is now having more good days that bad(which is amazing because they were pretty much all bad days for almost 2 years) I not saying everything is great, she still has some depression and difficulty with socializing, but slowly that is changing but a long way to go. Mom was on many medications for depression over the years, most didn't work or barely worked. Mom has a very high tolerance for medication(I think I read somewhere that those with less melanin have a different tolerance for medication. My mom was under the knife a few times(C-sections/ hysterectomy/ Cholecystectomy) and each time she can recall feeling/hearing everything that was done/said but couldn't move or talk - the Doctors didn't believe her. Any way, mom's spirits(without meds) following Jacks RX's, are better than any med that she has tried. The only problem is having the runs for well over a month now.
**My story:** I have had digestive issues since as long as I can remember - a doctor diagnosed me with a nervous stomach at age 7(I was a C-section premie with many issues and was not breast fed). I had reflux from a young age but was not tested for GERD until my late teens. I was treated with many PPI's for over 20 years. I came off them about 6 years ago, they were not working anymore. I also have seasonal allergies most of my life. When I was 23, I had a severe anaphylactic reaction that landed me in the hospital for 2 weeks on deaths door(I looked like the character Martin short played in that old movie when he was stung by a bee, remember that one)I was unrecognizable. When the PPI\`s stopped being effective, I was getting a feeling of having a global object in my throat, my breathing wasn\`t affected, but that constant feeling was there and I was getting heartburn 4-5 times a week on the meds.
I had a severe gallbladder attack about 7 years ago, the duct was completely blocked and they ended up removing my gallbladder(hindsight), since than I have had worse digestive issues, with distension & constant discomfort after eating, especially high fat foods, but sometimes it could be any food...if I don\`t eat there is no issue. It was a constant condition for 2 years, I had my doctor do upper and lower ultrasounds with no results, I was basically told to watch my diet.
I tried many elimination diets, but nothing has helped as much as the 'Six Healers', I am feeling a lot better - been doing this for 5 months, it\`s not totally gone but most days I feel normal.
I worried a lot years ago knowing that my aunt Jes died younger that me from pancreatic cancer and she suffered from many of the same digestive issues that I did. She was also like my mom...Pale skinned, green eyed red head, who spent most time indoors. She was an extremely smart lady, she wanted to make a difference...she had her masters in Special Education, which makes me so sad because she never got a chance to meet my daughter who I believe she would have been her biggest advocate had she lived.
That pretty much sums it up...I know, its a lot of info, but I had to get it out. Thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-12T15:41:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aprils-journal.18724/post-203872>

What about the environment of your maternal side.......and your specific one. That is the key

### ab70bandit — 2016-11-12T17:52:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aprils-journal.18724/post-203891>

My environment is not optimal, I work in an office on a computer...I work during the day and have been leaving the lights off(I have a wall thats all window. When I do have to put the lights on I use full spectrum lighting. My daughter also works in the same office.
My mom is retired. She hates computers, but she does spend alot of time indoors, much less the past couple of months.

### Liam Taylor — 2016-11-15T20:47:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aprils-journal.18724/post-203979>

Lots of detail there, lots of useful info. Keep posting what you're learning from the blogs, posts etc.

# Thread: Lucien's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lucien Burke — 2016-11-11T20:07:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/post-203841>

Hey Guys!

I'm new... seems like a journal is a good way to begin, I'll try to keep it short and sweet...

I'm 20, live in Burlington VT, and have "Chronic Fatigue Syndrome."

I felt healthy most of my life until about a year and a half ago when after a slew of infectious problems including Lyme, mono, and strep all within the span of a few months, I became crippled by severe fatigue and neurological problems that have manifested as chronic depersonalization/brain fog.

Since then I've been on a journey for answers... And have entertained everything from candidiasis to chronic Lyme and Biotoxin illness. Then last summer I heard Jack and Ruben speak at Shelburne Farms in VT and my mind was blown... Puzzle pieces slowly started moving together.

After spending a little time sifting through Jack's work, my intuition tells me that the answers to my problems don't lie in antimicrobial and detox treatments, but in mitochondrial revitalization (Recharging a dead mammalian battery).

I'm sick of the bullshit that alternative health leaders spout about killing every last pathogen in your body and detoxing until the grave. I've come to the belief that we're sick because we're weak at the cellular level and pathogens and biotoxins have become opportunistic because of this deficit in human resilience.

So I came here for support in my quantum journey.

I'm curious what steps I should take and in what order, and how they should be executed...

I'm open to any and all suggestions...

Thanks!

Lucien

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-12T15:38:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/post-203870>

welcome.....I think Dr. Wallace's data show that CFS is really a function of a broken mitochondrial flux system. Redox before detox. I have a huge problem with alternative pratctioners who do not understand this basic issue. They just make huge $$$$ going detox over redox.

### Lucien Burke — 2016-11-21T18:47:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/post-204153>

_**ROS Signaling**_

I've been dissecting the quilt document to begin with, and the ROS levee caught my eye. It talks about the importance of ROS signaling among our mitochondria in terms of cell health and maintenance. For someone like myself with weakened immunity due to a broken mitochondrial flux, optimization of ROS signaling seems critical. I know ROS signaling is closely tied to circadian biology and light exposure to the full spectrum including Blue (at the right times), UV, and Infrared. In addition to this, I'm curious about oxidative therapies such as Ozone and UV Irradiation of the blood. I'm excited about researching ROS signaling and oxidative therapies in greater depth...A potent immune system and resilient cell infrastructure rely on healthy ROS rhythms from Mitochondria.

Factors that interrupt proper ROS Signaling:
Mismatched Circadian Rhythm ~ Circadian Biology dictates waves of ROS production in cells
nn EMF exposure ~ facilitates chronic oxidative stress, loss of proper ROS signaling

Again, I wonder if adding oxidative therapies like Ozone can work synergistically with minding light and nn EMF....

I'll be investing in Ozone soon and keep an update on that.

### Lucien Burke — 2016-12-11T19:30:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/post-205162>

_**Lake Champlain CT
**_
It's early winter in VT and that means that although the atmospheric temps are dipping into the low 20s and teens up here near the Canadian border, the Lake hasn't frozen yet. Perfect for early morning polar dips down at the beach.

The solitude is centering and my relationship to water has become a deeply spiritual experience. It's amazing how the cold water can pierce through all the layers of prejudice, false security, and ego that I have become accustomed to insulating myself with. Each session seems to take me deeper and deeper into new frontiers of my conscious mind and the cold seems to have an amazing ability to enhance my personal strengths and dispel my weaknesses. Who new a freezing lake could be such as incredible mentor and healer... I'm still physically very weak so the CT is intense, but the mental and spiritual benefits I've realized keep me going back down to the beach in the AM...

### Lucien Burke — 2017-01-20T23:05:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/post-207164>

Just purchased an ozone unit and getting started with therapies. Any and all advice is welcome especially if anyone has experience with this. After doing some research oxidative therapies seem promising for CFS/Lyme/Biotoxin Issues... ozone along with UV.

### Lucien Burke — 2017-01-31T19:02:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/post-207665>

CFS/ME has become severe, mostly bedridden now. All reading/research points to mitochondrial dysfunction, as JK wrote above ^. The question is how to improve with such broken mitochondria. DHA and water are obviously key, but cells can't become hydrophilic with extremely poor electron flow from busted mitochondria like mine...

Lots of info on this site about how when heteroplasmy rates are high and the mitochondrial flux system is so broken, ketosis can be a dead end or even dangerous because of senescent mitochondria being unable to produce adequate electron density. I'm trying to decide whether or not to utilize ketosis, as I don't know whether it will help me or hurt me based on the status of my mitochondria.

Currently focusing on loads of DHA, water, and electrolytes, mitigating artificial light and nnEMF, but unsure about ketosis due to broken mitochondria.

Does anyone know of hacks to stimulate autophagy, mitophagy, and mitochondrial biogenesis in an affective and safe way for someone with severe cfs/me?

So I can restore electron density in mitochondria, assimilate light, and restore DC electric current to tissues.

like I said...

SEVERE cfs/me.

### Lucien Burke — 2017-03-01T01:24:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/post-209453>

Interesting new study from the Naviaux Mitochondrial Medicine lab at UC San Diego... "Metabolic Features of chronic fatigue syndrome"

[http://www.openmedicinefoundation.o...016/08/Naviaux-PNAS-CFS-Metabolomics-2016.pdf](http://www.openmedicinefoundation.org/wp-content/uploads/2016/08/Naviaux-PNAS-CFS-Metabolomics-2016.pdf)

Shows many metabolites extremely low in cfs, including intermediates of the citric acid cycle. This confuses me because from all the research I've done, including on this site, the etiology of cfs seems to be a mitochondrial defect from a poor environment. However, if there were a mitochondrial defect, shouldn't there be a buildup of TCA intermediates? Because glycolysis should still be intact and producing TCA intermediates, they just wouldn't be burned by faulty mitochondria. Since TCA intermediates are low, this would suggest that glycolysis is blocked. This hypothesis is supported by another recent study that concluded a dysfunction of the pyruvate dehydrogenase enzyme:

[https://insight.jci.org/articles/view/89376](https://insight.jci.org/articles/view/89376)

Im just confused why these findings show an interruption in glycolysis when CFS appears to be the result of disturbed mitochondrial function. Glycolysis produces the products that are burned by mitochondria, and as far as I know isn't under control of the mitochondria. Does glycolysis and the PDH enzyme somehow have to do with redox?

Hoping someone can clear the air on this...

### Lucien Burke — 2017-06-10T20:22:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/post-215762>

### Lucien Burke — 2018-01-18T01:37:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/luciens-journal.18725/post-232796>

I can't believe it's already 2018... 2017 flew.

I can't think of a better place to document my dreams and focuses for this year so I'll do it right here.

**1\. Continue to prioritize and improve my health.
**
If you don't have your health you don't have anything. You can't be of service to anyone or add energy to the system. For too long I've told myself stories about why I can't put my health first, and this is why I've lost my light and lost my path. It's time to find my way once again. The energy of my spacesuit is #1, the rest follows.

**2\. End my technology addiction.
**
It drains my energy and gives nothing of value in return. Instead of being used as a tool, my technology has been using me. Not anymore. I've already deleted many apps, deactivated many accounts, and gotten rid of many devices that weren't of service to me in these first few weeks of the year. I have a good head start on this and it feels amazing.

**3\. Follow the Sun.**

I've made good on this one already as well. My families house on Marco Island, FL was damaged in the hurricane, and I volunteered to live here for a few months and oversee the repairs. I managed to escape a big chunk of vit D winter in the north this time around. I'm not gonna miss a single sunrise this year.

**4\. Reconsider my relationships
**
If I'm not resonating someone in my life, I'll let the distance grow naturally. My inner circle needs to ignite my spirit.

**5\. Simplify**

Drop extra baggage. Let go of extraneous emotions. Live in the moment. Accept the things I can't change. Reconnect with nature. This includes following my inner compass. If I disconnect from the radio static and pay deep attention to my inner signals, I find they point me in the right direction. I'm focused on finding the courage to follow my instincts.

...

**_Focused on bringing light back into my life this year._**

### Alper — 2016-11-12T12:51:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-water-temperature-and-cold-urticaria.18729/post-203860>

Dear all,

a couple of days ago I followed a podcast with Jack (Ironman Executive - Cold Thermogenesis) where he mentioned that at some degree before water freezes it contains the highest amount of electrons and thats why there is much more life in the polar sees compared to the equatorial sees or something like that.
Let us suppose that I could stand water in a lake (5°C water temperature) without having used the ct-protocol lately. Is there any chance of getting cold urticaria above 0°C??? I got cold urticaria once when I was using the protocol in the past (I know about the omega 6:3-ratio issue) at the point where I took off the compressing shirt and put ice on my naked thoracial skin. Cold water though doesn´t seem to cause any problems for me. The skin gets cherry-red but doesn´t turn white at 5°C. I stay in the water for 1-1:30min at the moment daily. It seems to me that water above 0°C (with most electrons in it refering to Jacks podcast) instead of ice seems to be more "healthy". It that correct? Maybe healthy is a confusing term.
If might be relevant that I´m at about 18-20% bodyfat at the moment.

DHA within the cell membranes allows mammals to repel cold that is one of the reasons why marine mammals have used DHA for 600 million years. It repels the sense of cold when the DC electric current is high using non linear aspects of light like the Pockel's effect

I know from the ct-protocol that you recommend the "whole body immersion" after the use of ice on the torso. # Thread: Everyone needs to watch and share this on social media: Devra Davis
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-needs-to-watch-and-share-this-on-social-media-devra-davis.18731/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-12T17:07:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everyone-needs-to-watch-and-share-this-on-social-media-devra-davis.18731/post-203882>

Electromagnetism can be discussed in two ways: #1 in terms of the field and #2 in terms of the radiation. The field is something that exists in space around an object that produces it. An electric field extends outward from electrically charged objects. Both magnetic and electric fields are static and unvarying. When time is introduced to them, by varying the intensity of the field, like a radio antenna, an electromagnetic field is the result. This kind of field fluctuates often and emits waves of energy that behave as massless and chargeless (photons) energies. Each energy wave consists of an electric and magnetic field at right angles to one another. The energy wave generated is traveling at right angles to both the magnetic and electric field. This gives it a 3 dimensional look when you reconstruct the view in your minds eye. This is precisely how the brain reconstructs time. The number of waves formed in one second is called the frequency of the EMF wave. The distance the energy travels during one oscialltion is the wavelength. The higher the frequency, the shorter the wavelength is, and vice versa.

EMF’s span large frequencies. Gamma rays are very small. They are a tenth of a billionth millimeter long and vibrate at sextillions of times a second. They are related to Xrays, short wave UV radiation are called ionizing radiation. There photons are so powerful that they can knock out electrons from atoms and cause highly reactive ions to be formed where they should not be. The next band of EMF’s are the longer wave UV radiation and then we hit EMF we see in visible light. Infrared radiation comes next and we feel it as heat. Below the infrared spectrum is the area of EMF’s is where modern communications frequencies are found. Here, microwaves (giga or mega Hz), and radio frequencies exist. The RF (radio frequency) spectrum is broken up into many smaller groups with names you know (UHF, VHF and ELF). Extremely low frequency (ELF) waves are where the Schumann resonance is below 10 Hz. At 10 Hz, one wave is 18,600 miles long. The key point is, except for visible light and infrared EMF, we can not perceive any of these energies without instruments designed to find them. The problem is our brain senses them all without sensation being perceived consciously. WifI frequencies vary by country law: 2.4 GHz (802.11b/g/n) Most countries. United States. Interference concerns.
3.65 GHz (802.11y)
4.9 GHz (802.11j) public safety WLAN.
5 GHz (802.11a/h/j/n/ac)
5.9 GHz (802.11p)
60 GHz (802.11ad)
900 MHz (802.11ah)

In my strength training (2h) I am eating 3500-4000 kcla at those days I have issue with sleep. I wake up at 2-3 at night. In normal days i do not have this problem. I do ketogenic diet ~ 2 years. I assume that because of low cortisol and low blodsuger (ketosis) my adrenalin wants to increase blood sugar (spread glycogen) so it this particular situation this is my problem. These are my assumptions, I would consult my supposition.

What I do ?

Eat clean, drink good quality of water also ice and a lot of green tea.

My microelements are GOOD.

Grounding every single day (before I go sleep 30-40min), I do not live in city.

Cold baths with diving

1-2 before sleep in completely darkness

I use blu-blocker 550nm after sunset

When there is no sun I use Solr Glo bulp (160W) in the morning.

**ALA** is an **omega-3** fatty acid

according to Yehuda
people (on average) have best results when their diet contains
4:1 ratio of omega6/omega3
you have to be very careful at this point
the press is not reading full story and we are left with
4:1 ratio of omega6/omega3
but
when reading rest of the story
it is actually
**(4:1) (LA)/(ALA)**
\-----
Number of people studied our diet from point of view of fat content.
One may get (short lasting - few months) good results,
using other ratios.
But on the long range other ratios are not successful.
Even using diet with recommended (4:1) (LA)/(ALA)
it is better to do blood testing from time to time.

(Important for latter story)
fat=solid or semi-solid
oil=liquid
LA & ALA are oils

There are oils designed to have this ideal ratio.
They are expensive and really meant for sick people.
There is a natural oil that have that ratio close enough.

The reason potassium is critical inside a cell, and supersedes magnesium and sodium ion concentration is because it is naturally tied to beta and gamma carboxyl side chain groups found in proteins, where potassium specifically binds because of “quantum advantages.” These beta and gamma carboxyl bonds allows K+ to ADSORB to this protein site and donate electrons (this is why K+ has the second highest redox potential to Lithium in life; to the polarized water gel crystal.

Each molecule of ATP in a cell controls 8,800 water molecule binding sites and 20 potassium ions to make this liquid crystalline semiconductor inside every cell of your body. Potassium acts like “the glue” to keep your protein back bone and water in a gel state inside your cell to maintain the semiconducting plates together in a cohesive form. This is why K+ is critical in setting the redox potential of water in a cell. In this way, you are building a special type of semiconductor that forms “the fourth phase of matter” and can act like a “topologic insulator.”(TI) A ‘TI’ allows quantum effects to happen in warm wet environments.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-15T02:54:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mito-connections-to-heart-disease.18737/post-208614>

The take home? Potassium (K+) signaling goes awry in an environment loaded with nnEMF. What are the implications? When K+ goes awry so does your cellular redox potential and so does your mitochondrial heteroplasmy rates in tissues affected by this. Potassium levels tell us about the relationships to cellular ATP made by mitochondria. In this way, they are like CO2 and water levels in cells because mitochondrial ox/phos makes CO2 and water as their normal exhaust products since the reverse photosynthesis. K+ stoichiometry: For every 0.3 mEq below 3.8 mEq that potassium is on a standard blood lab draw, means there is 100 mEq deficit inside a cell. The atomic size and its redox potential is huge for potassium “gluing of water” for it to function as the optimal electrical adapter to transfer energy throughout the cell coherently. ATP is designed to unfold proteins fully to open their carbonyl and imino side chain groups on all amino acids to intracellular water. This action allows binding and polarization to separate water into subatomic particles that are positively and negatively charged. This action is called building or expanding the exclusion zone (EZ) of water.

Expanding the exclusion zone allows water to form polarized layers around the hydrophilic proteins, and the earth’s magnetic field then orients these polarized liquid crystals by a principle called “spintronics.” Controlling the electron spin at a right angle to the direction of flow of electrons allows for the formation of massive super conducting proton cables all over your body. This is why sleeping on a magnetic pad at certain times might be a smart move when you understand the context of your redox potential.

“Spintronics” was described in the February 2014 webinar for members. This gives you alternating positive and negative magnetic dipoles of water molecules that have their electrons locked in at 90 degree angles to the current of flow around these proteins. In this way, the magnetic field can control the action of water and collagen where they meet, both inside and outside of cells AT SMALL SCALE around the respiratory proteins that feed the ATPase. Gilbert Ling found that the orientation of positive to negative water molecule binding was 3.1 Angstroms apart when theses conditions are met.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-18T16:26:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jamaicans-and-arthritis.18740/post-204057>

That is exactly the issue.......most of these people were brought from Africa to the this area. Now we surround them with a generator power grid. Go down there with a RF meter and Trimeter and you'll see why they have issues.

Jamaica and Haiti are around the 20th latitude. People with the darkest skin are equatorial. So have dark skin at the 20th latitude is akin to a brown out from the sun to lower your quantum yield. The reason is simple. Very dark skin blocks solar light to decrease light assimilation. This is an epigentic advantage at the equator and an albatross around your neck when you move away from it. I just did a webinar on this with Ben Smith this year when he went to Uganda. You might want to listen to it. Dark skin at the 20th latitude is a CIRCADIAN MISMATCH.

This situation means the afflicted person does not get enough sunlight and because they are not able to retain the light. In this way arthritis and black skin at a non equatorial latitude is one of the MOST COMMON DISEASES I see as a spine surgeon in the Gulf South. Arthritis is the symptom/signal in the muscle skeltal system from the spectral deficincy upon the skin that you are living a light deficient life. Might this signal or symptom on the skin be foretelling something more concerning happening in your cells below? Of course it does. Consider this analogy I made recently in my Time 9 blog on my website: Melatonin and melanin are biogenic amines linked to light's local effect where it interacts with our surfaces. Let us expand this idea to the laws of physics to see how biology follows those rules. Melatonin recycling changes local symmetry therefore it affects the space in tissues and the timing mechanisms in cells below this "diseased" spectrally defcient skin to affect the joints and ligaments below.

Symmetry is often thought of globally in science, but it turns out Einstein's general relativity, put local symmetry front and center in physics; it appears melatonin and melanin control local symmetry of light collection for cells too at the surface and at deeper levels to directly effect mitochondrial heteroplasmy rates. LOCAL melatonin levels made in the blood in your skin is a proxy for how effective we collect light locally within cells. Einstein's idea was unorthodox in physics, but it appears the melatonin cycle uses his relativity theory locally in cells to determine outcomes by altering energy flux in mitochondria by using local meltonin levels as the gears to change clock genes in cells. These things are both related to how light can flow in cells and tissues locally. We call this process the "redox potential". I have two redox blogs, read them.

Physics tells us energy and momentum tells space in and around our cells how to react to this light deficient state. As light falls to us from any source, it gains energy and momentum, so in this way it has a weight or inertia to it. That weight and inertia affect our surfaces to make things like Vitamin D3 as a chemical signal. With respect to sun light, gravitational lensing is tied to energy flows by weight and momentum changes in light as it falls to the Earth surface no matter where you are. This story linked here is related to the IRF4 gene they recently found in hair color that accounts for haor color. This story was published linking gray hair to melanoma risk by interfering with production of melanin in the skin due to a lack of UV light frequencies. People with arthritis tend to be melanin and eumelanin deficient or they can be light deficient because of their dark skin. This offends common sense and I like that because this is how nature is designed to work. No one understands light well enough in biology. What did they seem to forget in this published link on the IRF4 gene?? They never mentioned how the local control of symmetry of melatonin is lost with respect to a spectral deficiency sunlight. That loss directly controls mitochondrial DNA expression which directly links to how energy flows in a cell from our mitochondria to cause a loss of energy in bones, muscles and joints, ligaments and tendons to cause arthritis. It is time you learn this science to change your choices. Nature offends common sense by design.

# Thread: Clarifying: Why Is Artificial Blue Light Bad in the Morning?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarifying-why-is-artificial-blue-light-bad-in-the-morning.18743/>
**Subforum:** The Cave

### Matty_M — 2016-11-18T16:45:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarifying-why-is-artificial-blue-light-bad-in-the-morning.18743/post-204058>

People in my Optimal Club at school keep asking this question. I want to make sure my understanding is as correct as possible.

\- artificial lights are not full spectrum; the eye and brain are designed to utilize and need the full spectrum (blue red UV IR etc) to work properly.

(Can someone provide some specific examples? UVA makes melanin granules in RPE move faster, blue wakes us up; **what do the other colors or their absences do?**)

\- Artificial lights have 4x the color temperature of sun (this is problem because it causes a chronic excess of cortisol and mitochondrial damage, right? **Are there other reasons as well?**)

\- there is no "red" to balance the blue. (Is this shorthand for infrared, just red, or both? Is it correct that red increases mitochondrial efficiency? How else does it act to balance blue?)

Just want to be sharing this information properly, and to silence all half-truths! Thanks

\-Matt

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-18T19:43:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarifying-why-is-artificial-blue-light-bad-in-the-morning.18743/post-204063>

Sunlight has a 1800K color temperature. The LOWEST color temp of LED now avaialable is 2700K. The vast majority of LED light have a color temp of 5500-6500K. These are the light used indoors, in TV's, computers, and phones.

Cells need Color temp to vary all day........start low and grow.......and them drop fast at dusk. The fast de-crescendo is what activates the melanopsin receptor.........and this is what sets the stage for sleep and regeneration under the control of local melatonin coupling to repair mtDNA which lowers heteroplasmy rate by optimizing autophagy and apoptosis during sleep. The quotient is optimized energy flows from mitochondria.

AM blue light stimulates pituitary hormone release.......and UVA and UVB light photo-inactivate these hormones in the skin and eye. If clothing or eye protection are worn while you are in a blue light environment the result will be quick over supply of hormones early in life followed by complete loss of hormones because the pituitary cannot maintain release when the blue light switch is chronically on while the UV turn off switch is covered by clothes and glasses. This leads to dramatic low sex steroid hormones and multiple endocrine dysruptions.

# Thread: Best dopamine-building-vacation locations
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dopamine-building-vacation-locations.18744/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Darleen — 2016-11-18T17:49:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dopamine-building-vacation-locations.18744/post-204060>

Hi All,
Looking to go on a vacation and get some sun during the months of December-February.
Any suggestions are greatly appreciated!

### michellehp — 2016-11-22T16:57:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dopamine-building-vacation-locations.18744/post-204211>

We recently went to Tortola, BVI. Stayed in a villa through AirBnB. They stocked the kitchen with all of our requests- seafood, grass fed beef, water, wine, etc. We had a chef come cook for us, rented a boat (with driver) for some days to visit other islands, 18th parallel, no one around... except goats, chickens, roosters, the beach, water and sky. I had never come home from a vacation feeling that good and its effects lasted for weeks. We never turned on a television the whole time and the electricity went out for a day and it wasn't missed.

### Rubicon — 2016-11-22T23:00:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dopamine-building-vacation-locations.18744/post-204219>

Last winter [I spent a few weeks on Isla Mujeres near Cancun](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/optimal-vacation-spots.17315/#post-187906). Some areas of the island are relatively sparsely populated, and I felt really good after that experience.

This winter I'm planning to go to Progresso. Hopefully it's a little less populated

### Darleen — 2016-12-04T00:33:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dopamine-building-vacation-locations.18744/post-204744>

# Thread: Worms in raw fish

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sujoe123 — 2016-11-18T19:23:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worms-in-raw-fish.18746/post-204062>

Does anyone know if Jack freezes his fresh fish before he eats it to kill the worms. There are many species of fish here in the Gulf that carry worms and parasites in their flesh at one time of the year or another.... I do use lime juice to make ceviche but the acidity can also change the conformation or folding of proteins.

### Rocky — 2017-01-30T18:07:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worms-in-raw-fish.18746/post-207579>

Assuming you are eating raw fish? If there were worms our parasites in the fish wouldn't chewing and stomach acid take care of them in short order?

### Samuel Brunic — 2017-02-26T06:55:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/worms-in-raw-fish.18746/post-209295>

I asked Trader Joe's about their fresh Alaskan salmon filets and they told me they freeze them on the ship at -40 F fot at least 15 hours to kill parasites. I have been eatin raw oysters, salmon and tuna for months with no issues.

Dr. Kruse also mentioned in his Twitter account in early 2017 that he does not worry about worms from fish.

### Andrea — 2016-11-20T06:45:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-harmful-is-pink-clothing-effects-of-clothing-colors.18750/post-204112>

Thanks, Matty, for asking. I've been wondering about this for a while myself.

But why? What is it about pink that's bad?

I've been taught that pink = red + white. And white = all colors.
So a pink cloth reflects - and transmits - a little of all colors and especially red. Or, if you will, pink filters out much of all colors except for red.

So why is pink detrimental while red is considered healing?

Or is it due to the effects of certain wavelengths or frequencies?

### lohd2015 — 2016-11-22T02:57:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-harmful-is-pink-clothing-effects-of-clothing-colors.18750/post-204191>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...nsdermal-magnesium-working.17980/#post-195283](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/how-is-transdermal-magnesium-working.17980/#post-195283)

From Dr. Kruse's FB April 12, 2016 post:

"A fresh paint color is all that might be all that’s needed to prevent a giant asteroid from raining destruction upon our planet. Why? Light has another surface effect physics knows about since 1902. Sometimes, very small forces can alter the evolution of an entire planetary system. The Yarkovsky effect is one such example. An imbalance in the radiation of heat (IR) off an asteroid changes its orbit.

The Yarkovsky effect, named for a Russian engineer who discovered it in 1902. I learned that this effect has a diurnal and seasonal effect that is unique and tied to size. This is when I got the idea to bio -hack this effect. I came across this in my research over ten years ago and I decided to use it in several bio hacks on my skin, eyes, and hair to learn more about the biologic effects here on Earth.

Our blood circulates and spins cells that are small (RBC's,WBC's, and platelets ) right below our eye and skin surface. They also undergo diurnal and seasonal fluctuations. This gave me the idea to see how colors could effect the bio-physics of plasma in us. This surface photoelectric effect results from the fact that asteroids heat up as they bask in the sun’s light in space. When they heat up the side facing the sun emit's anisotropic thermal photons causing motion to change on the asteroid. What the resultant motion is on the asteroid, depends upon the spin of the asteroid. It works best on smaller asteroids. Today, most people think this effect only occurs in space. No one knows if it occurs on Earth, which has a different environment.

It is believed that in space in an asteroid, it is a surface effect that only requires a coat of paint. It should be very thin like a wrap of plastic wrap. Aerospace engineer David Hyland of Texas A&M leads a team that has been studying this method for several years. I've read a lot of their papers. This is when I started using methylene blue, magnesium sprays, and topical chemicals to see their effect. What I learned about changing our hair coloring was quite interesting bio hack. NASA scientists now believe If we paint with the correct color and in the right spot, we can push an asteroid in the right direction, away from the Earth. If we can get the asteroid to cease crossing Earth’s orbit, we can completely eliminate the threat to life and avoid a KT like extinction. I have found that when we change our surfaces with frequencies some interesting things happen at deeper levels.

Here is a hint: Dont ever wear pink lip stick, pink hair, or pink sunglasses. If you do, consider getting yourself a term insurance policy.

[https://www.jstor.org/stable/20488532?seq=1#page\_scan\_tab\_contents](https://www.jstor.org/stable/20488532?seq=1#page_scan_tab_contents) "

And some things to think about:
[http://gizmodo.com/if-the-color-pink-doesnt-scientifically-exist-why-can-1464266788](http://gizmodo.com/if-the-color-pink-doesnt-scientifically-exist-why-can-1464266788)

[http://wonderopolis.org/wonder/pink-flamingos](http://wonderopolis.org/wonder/pink-flamingos)

The distinctive pink flamingo color develops thanks to their selective diet, which primarily consists of organisms — such as shrimp and algae — high in pigments called carotenoids. These carotenoids are the same pigments that cause shrimp to turn from gray to pink when we boil them!

Carotenoids are essential to maintaining the flamingo's signature color. If a flamingo were to adopt a meal plan similar to other birds who feast on insects, seeds or berries, his feathers would eventually become white or a faded pale pink.

PINK ANIMALS

[http://www.treehugger.com/slideshows/natural-sciences/pink-animals/#slide-top](http://www.treehugger.com/slideshows/natural-sciences/pink-animals/#slide-top)

Axolotl (Ambystoma mexicanum), also known as the [Mexican walking fish](http://www.treehugger.com/natural-sciences/ubercool-mexican-walking-fish-nearing-extinction.html). Not only are these amphibians just ridiculously cute, but they never undergo metamorphosis and thus stay in larval form their entire lives. Plus, they have super healing powers that allow them to do things like regenerate limbs! Rock on, Axolotl.

Sea anemone

Look at that beauty! Named after the terrestrial flower that is equally showy, the sea anemone comes in a rainbow of dazzling colors; pink being among the loveliest. But this creature that's related to coral and jellyfish is more than just a pretty flowery glob, the anemone has some surprising traits: They are carnivorous; they can live to be 50 years old; and some of them can reach a whopping 6 feet in size.

### lohd2015 — 2016-11-22T02:58:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-harmful-is-pink-clothing-effects-of-clothing-colors.18750/post-204192>

Roseate spoonbill

While flamingos may be the poster children for pink animals, we couldn't pass up the beautifully quirky roseate spoonbill, a gorgeous pink wading bird with a charming spatulate bill. Sadly for these guys, their pink primary feathers were highly prized for use in ladies' fans in the late 19th century; by the 1930s, the once healthy Florida population had dipped to a total of only 30 to 40 breeding pairs. Fortunately for the roseate spoonbill and all of us admirers, full legal protection against hunting was enacted and there are now over 1,000 nesting pairs in Florida.

Elephant hawk moth

Hi, pretty! This creature – which has the unusual distinction of being named after a mammal, a bird and an insect – is one of 1,400 species of hawk moths found across the globe. Hawk moths are the only moths able to hover in front of flowers to feed, like hummingbirds do, and are among the planet's fastest flying insects, reaching speeds of up to 12 mile per hour.

Amazon river dolphin

The Amazon river dolphin – also known as the boutu, boto, or bufeo – has the distinction of being the largest of the freshwater dolphins. And sadly, like all freshwater dolphins, is endangered thanks to hunting and habitat destruction. It stands apart for its incredible color which ranges from soft pink when it's young to near fuschia as it ages.

Pink hairy squat lobster

Part Dr. Seuss, part arachnophobe's nightmare, this beguiling marine crustacean known as the “Pink hairy squat lobster” (Lauriea siagiani) is not really a lobster at all. Also called a fairy crab, the "lobster" belongs to the group of crabs called Anomurans, and at a mere half an inch in length may be just the cutest little crab we've ever seen. See one in action in the video below.

Flamingo

Well we couldn't do a gallery of pink animals and not include the most famous of pink creatures. So for our final blushing beauties, the epitome of pink: flamingos. Although when flamingoes first hatch they are a drab grey, they develop into glorious shades of peach and coral largely due to their diet. The red and blue-green algae they eat is chock-full of beta carotene which contains a reddish-orange pigment; and the mollusks and crustaceans flamingos favor also possess pigment-rich carotenoids.

And if their romantic color and heart-shaped kisses weren't lovey-dovey enough, consider this: Although they group together in flocks that can number in the hundreds of thousands, a flamingo picks a single mate and generally remains monogamous for life. Sweet!

[http://webecoist.momtastic.com/2011/08/23/blushing-hides-10-amazing-pink-animals/](http://webecoist.momtastic.com/2011/08/23/blushing-hides-10-amazing-pink-animals/)

The garish bird above isn’t a flamingo but is shown here because of its various shades of pink ranging from salmon to neon. Take away the color and it’d be pug-ugly… like most vultures. Yep, it’s a California Condor chick!

Pink faced Uakaris are monkeys… monkeys from Hell!! OK, not really, they come from isolated areas of the northwest Amazon basin and just look like Skeletor’s pet. There are 4 known species of Uakari but our focus here is on the Bald Uakari. This odd-looking New World monkey has copious hair all over its body with the exception of its head – much like your average middle-aged human male.

Pink Hippos are rarely sighted outside of Hanna-Barbara cartoons but they do exist, and for several reasons. Most hippos are a brownish-gray color with pink undertones. They can appear even pinker on hot, sunny days when they tend to sweat: hippo sweat is pink!

For a few hippos, even sweating pink isn’t enough: so-called Leucistic hippos lack the normal amount of gray pigment in their skin and, by default, tend towards a more pinkish aspect. Hippos can tolerate leucism more than other creatures as they spend a lot of time in the water and, as a bonus, secrete an oily substance that acts as a sunscreen.

I couldn't say. I'm trying to figure all of this out for myself... quantum biology, as well as our society and how I can have the most positive impact.

On one hand, I believe that there is an "ideal"; a path on which I will be able to most positively impact as many people as possible with this information. I feel an immense amount of personal responsibility with this knowledge because of the way I suffered growing up and would have continued had someone like Dr. K not done the work that he has.

On the other, I am constantly questioning what that path is, because I don't want to "climb too far up a ladder on the wrong wall", like Dr. K has been posting on his facebook page. Another side of me says to do what "feels right", but then I wonder whether what "feels right" is actually what will have the most positive impact. At the same time, my whole goal is for people to be able to fully enjoy their lives...but how can I advocate that when I often worry about whether what I'm doing is the best I can do?

I've practiced mindfulness, but will begin to do so more because the thought process above^^^ that I live with is stealing my enjoyment from this existence.

Any advice you have on this is greatly appreciated.
