Your questions do not come with easy answers. I believe all of us struggle with similar issues when we are faced with life changing decisions.

We can never know what the full impact of any of our actions will be, and I don't believe that is the purpose of Life. You will never be able to move forward if you are constantly thinking if you are doing the right thing. We also cannot interpret the outcomes of any of our actions after the fact because we simply are unable to see reality as it is. That is how Life is, to me, at least.

Be brave and trust that no matter what you do, if your heart is in the right place, you can ALWAYS find your way onto a brighter path, should you end up straying.

You are doing all the right things. You are reading and asking questions. Give yourself some time for ideas to grow, and you will know when it is time to do what is right. If nothing comes and you are still confused, toss a coin. If you don't like the result of your toss, then you will know what you really want to do.

Happy Thanksgiving.

But as I recognized, that I couldn't change the circumstances of my life so fast and convince my social environment as I wanted, I wished I hadn't taken the red pill.

But I don't give in. Everyone's journey down the rabbit hole is different but getting there is definitely a gift. The supposed enjoyment from this existence (before knowing the truth) is based on self-deception. Real enjoyment will happen when you have learned to use all your senses and get connected to nature again. So be happy for what you know already and expect more wonderful things to come into your life. And they will, if you keep your focus on what really matters for you.

You will never be able to move forward if you are constantly thinking if you are doing the right thing. We also cannot interpret the outcomes of any of our actions after the fact because we simply are unable to see reality as it is. That is how Life is, to me, at least.
>
> Be brave and trust that no matter what you do, if your heart is in the right place, you can ALWAYS find your way onto a brighter path, should you end up straying.
>
> You are doing all the right things. You are reading and asking questions. Give yourself some time for ideas to grow, and you will know when it is time to do what is right. If nothing comes and you are still confused, toss a coin. If you don't like the result of your toss, then you will know what you really want to do.
>
> Happy Thanksgiving.

Thank you so much. I've sort of known this all along, but I've got to apply it.

### DavidL — 2017-08-02T18:35:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-harmful-is-pink-clothing-effects-of-clothing-colors.18750/post-220727>

EFFECTS OF COLOURED LIGHT AND COLOURED PLASTIC

It has been noted that work areas **lit by the pink fluorescents** for a "warm" effect have the opposite effect with the staff. Tempers flare, everyone becomes irritable and generally difficult to control.

When the **pink fluorescents are removed**, within a week, congeniality and a spirit of working together redevelops. These results seemed quite in line with the preliminary reports on mink kept behind different coloured glass.

**Mink exposed to natural daylight through a deep pink glass became increasingly aggressive, difficult to manage, and in many instances actually vicious.**

Mink normally are quite fierce, but when some of the **mink were placed behind deep blue plastic they became friendly and docile.** The same results can be applied to coloured eye-glasses; pink tinted glasses can cause personality disturbances

### Jack Kruse — 2017-08-02T18:48:05+0300

July 2017 webinar and Q & A talked about it too.............

It has to do with the experiments on mice - cancer - using pink pink fluorescent tubes..........It´s in the capital AN EXPERIMENT WITH PHOTOTHERAPY ON HUMAN CANCER PATIENTS. It says that mice exposed to pink light developed cancers 3 months earlier than those exposed to daylight filtered through ordinary window glass, and had had only 2 offsprings instead of normal 6-15.

[
There are quite a few examples with the pink glass actually: "The reports indicated that the mink exposed to natural daylight through a deep pink glass became increasingly aggressive, difficult to manage and in many instances actually vicious. Ordinarily, mink are kept in open sheds with open window areas containing no glass.", "When a female mink does not become pregnant after the first mating, it is common practice to give her an injection of a pregnant mare serum before attempting the second mating. This was not necessary with any of the female mink in the cages with the blue plastic, as all became pregnant after the first mating. Furthermore, to use the language of the mink industry, all the males were found to be “working males.”

But the situation was quite different with both males and females in the cages behind pink glass. After three attempts at mating the females, which included two injections of the pregnant mare serum, only 87 per cent became pregnant and 90 per cent of the males were classified as “non-working."

"The principal investigator of the project was Alex Ott (no relation), who also advised that four animals under the pink glass died during the experiment from a strange malady that he had never seen before. An autopsy of each animal indicated what appeared to be a cancerous condition of the abdominal area including a number of vital organs. Unfortunately, an actual biopsy was not performed due to the abrupt termination of the entire project. Approximately 500 female mink were used in each experiment."

"However, one rather clear relationship did show up. Although not statistically significant because only three cases were involved, three students did reply that they constantly wore “Hot Pink” glasses and a check with the faculty ratings indicated that these same three students also were considered to be the most psychologically disturbed students in the college."
](https://www.facebook.com/sana.vita.96?fref=ufi)

It does a very quick explanation of how **pink light doesn't exist**, and that the concept of pink is **our brain's attempt at filling in a gap** in the spectrum.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-harmful-is-pink-clothing-effects-of-clothing-colors.18750/post-222256>

Here's what I'm thinkin' . . . .

JK has made mention that we only perceive some of the light that comes to earth as visible.That has to do with the quantum effects of the invisible light for signaling.

> What's the other band that sunlight is absorbed in water? Turns out it's infrared. So if you ever meet me or see me in a talk, you'll notice that I usually have red and purple on. Now you probably know the reason why. Because this is also the reason why that the human eye is invisible to UV and IR light. Why? Because we use those two specific frequencies to signal things throughout our brain. We don't use blue, red or green. That's the part of the eye camera. The eye has a clock and a camera. It turns out UV and IR are really important for the clock, which is based in the suprachiasmatic nucleus and it turns out the other three are all tied to how we have camera vision.

[https://www.sott.net/article/304590-The-Health-Wellness-Show-A-conversation-with-Dr-Jack-Kruse](https://www.sott.net/article/304590-The-Health-Wellness-Show-A-conversation-with-Dr-Jack-Kruse)

If we perceived those frequencies they wouldn't be able to do their magic because, observing a subatomic wave/particle changes it, or collapses the wave function.
(see twin slit experiment

)

So being that pink isn't a part of the visible spectrum, perhaps perceiving it somehow shuts down the beneficial effects of the light that is not visible to us on signalling and gene clocks (IR, UV) . . . . see the part of the video JanSz posted above that says that between IR and UV is percieved as pink.

Also interesting is that an absence of green gives arise to pink - could green be the complimentary color? - If so, maybe blocking green light from full spectrum light might not be such a good idea ?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-01-30T13:37:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-harmful-is-pink-clothing-effects-of-clothing-colors.18750/post-233802>

Read John Ott's book and find this:

"On another trip to Florida I gave a lecture to an advertising club, and after I had finished my talk, Mr. Richard L. Marsh, manager of radio station WILZ near St. Petersburg, told me of a similar situation. He said that some of the staff at the radio station had taken it upon themselves to try to brighten up their surroundings in both the studios and the control rooms by replacing the regular white fluorescent tubes with those of a deep pink color.

About two months later, they began to have personnel problems. For example, announcers began performing poorly on the air. Everyone became irritable and consistently at odds with management decisions and generally difficult to control. Two resignations were received from employees without any known reason for their wishing to leave other than general dissatisfaction with themselves and the staff. Then, one morning one of the men said, “If those pink bulbs aren’t removed I’ll go out of my mind.”

That sparked an immediate reaction, and that very day all of the pink tubes were removed and replaced with the white tubes. Within a week, as if by a miracle, tempers ceased to flare, congeniality and a spirit of working together began to redevelop and resignations were withdrawn. The airwork improved, with mistakes at a minimum."

Salt should come present with Lithophilic organism to assist in its breakdown of inorganic minerals. The enzymes also assist this process, alongside natural acids.
It is not amphoteric therefore it creates issues with the sodium-potassium process. An amphoteric substance is an oxide of an element that can act as both an acid or a base.

Furthermore, it is not below it's 9 atomic clusters. This means it can not either pass through or enter the body without causing tissue damage on the atomic level. High in deuterium and does not fit as a sodium source since it has a drastic effect on ionized blood levels.
Celtic Sea salt is great. A phenomenal wide variety of salts are in existence. I Like the exotic sea salts from Fiji I also love the Fleur De Sal from France.

The way Himalayan salt is procured through its intensive mining process is atrocious and the salt usually contains heavy metals from the grinding process and trace amounts of Potassium Nitrate from gunpowder explosions.
Rich in fluoride, this results in overall electron transport and mitochondria inefficiency.
Know your salt!
[www.Biologicalresearchinstitute.com](http://www.biologicalresearchinstitute.com/)

I had problem with eating more salt.
I use for long time Redmond Salt.
After the video that initially @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) posted
I started eating 1 teaspoon daily.
After two weeks of that I got swollen.
So I stopped eating it and it and it took about whole week for the swelling to go away.

**I may be exception.**
On my Complete Blood Count (CBC) usually my electrolytes are ok.
Sodium, Potassium
But I have (had) frequent nightly cramps in my legs.
Also on one of those tests I noted potassium close to lower margin.
\-------------------------
I was trying variety of magnessiums for my cramps, even when Spectracell told me that my Magnessium is ok.

Eventually I resigned and used tonic water (for it quinine content), or outright quinine pills.

Recently I was successfully using (NOW Magnessium and Potassium Aspartate.
It was special situation, the day I took those pills, no cramps, forgot pills got cramps.

That was interrupted by my salt experiment.
I was using only salt, no NOW pills and no cramps.

In the mean time, @[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) mentioned sodium free salt (NoSalt), which is based on potassium salts.
I use little speck of it daily, works ok.

So I think I am lowish on potassium.

//////////////////
I knew about dr Brownstein recomending Redmont Saalt.
Will have to watch this one about recommended daily amount.

Little time currently
I would appreciate if someone posted time when he does that.
Thank you.

I've been trying to get my K+ from a few more greens added. I'm doing breakfasts and lunches only with IF now, but now my lunch will typically consist of a larger greens shrimp or chicken salad. For some reason, avocados do not agree with me if I consume more than one per day or eat them alone.

The salt-bloating is a problem I've noticed as well. I use it sparingly, but still get enough to find the right balance. I typically blend my NoSalt to Salt ratio around 20% NoSalt to 80% Redmond/Celtic salt now.

We have been using Redmond salt for about a month now. I no longer get up in the middle of the night to pee - and I seem to be able to wait indefinitely to use the bathroom in the morning. Also I haven't had leg cramps for the last couple of weeks. I do sometimes take natural Calm before bed
However I do seem to pee more in the day. We are drinking SP exclusively now with some Icelandic water.

We have moved so I can't be sure what to attribute it too .....except when we are in the ocean - it seems to be much more salty here. That must be a factor too.

One negative - I am being eaten alive by sand flies ....I have never had this bad an issue before........

I do not know about Australian sand flies.
The Brazilian, you do not feel when they suck your blood, unless you look at your legs and see that they run with blood.
Different story latter, specially next few days.
Lots of itching, then you scratch, then the legs are getting infected, the hole works.
You can see there lots of people with ugly marks on their legs from those infections.

When you lay down on sand, then when you get up, you think that fine sand is stuck to your skin, every other piece "sand" may be a sand fly.

In my place they were specially tough to find by looking.
The sand there contained shiny flakes of mica. Perfect camouflage for them.

'Baker-Miler pink' cames up when I stdied environmental psychology. Many years ago they even painted one of the inner city police station lock-ups here in that pink in an attempt to pacify the detainees. They should've looked at all the research first:

There is a little gadget that is a piezoelectric generator that will stop the itching on mosquito bites and prevent the infection from starting. It should work on sand flies, also. I have used a piezoelectric generator since 1995 for many kinds of healing. The one on line is [https://www.ebay.com/itm/173135029946](https://www.ebay.com/itm/173135029946)
It's called Zap-It Mosquito Insect Bite Relief device.
In 1995 I bought my "zapper" for $150 which is powered the same way as the Zap-it. Then it was by chance that when we were getting a new bar-b-que, my husband dropped the bar-b-que starter in my hand and I recognized the red button on the end and the size of it matched the "zapper" I paid so much for. The bar-b-que was from Sears and I ordered the Parts Specification info on the starter. The total energy delivered is 5 microJoules for the starter. The "zapper" I bought also delivered 5 microJoules. I purchased several bar-b-que starters and discovered it fit into a small metal case used to connect the trailer lights with the car brake lights. The "zapper" does not work well without being in a case. I tried electrical tape, but that did not work.
At current prices, the metal case and the bar-be-que starter are about $10. I decided to order the Zap-it Mosquito device on eBay to see how it compares. The spark should deliver the same feeling as static electricity.
I can not locate a picture of the bar-b-que starter, but I recently ordered a case that I hope will be the correct size for the 2 starters I have.
Here is the web site: [https://www.aliexpress.com/item/Fre...505.html?spm=a2g0s.9042311.0.0.326a4c4dB0OjYq](https://www.aliexpress.com/item/Free-Shipping-7-Pin-Trailer-Plug-ROUND-TRAILER-CONNECTOR-MALE-AND-FEMALE-RV-LIGHT-PLUG-CONNECTOR/32597244505.html?spm=a2g0s.9042311.0.0.326a4c4dB0OjYq)

# Thread: How does leptin link to sunlight by way of D3 and B12?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-leptin-link-to-sunlight-by-way-of-d3-and-b12.18751/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-20T04:02:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-leptin-link-to-sunlight-by-way-of-d3-and-b12.18751/post-204105>

Vitamin B12 and Vitamin D 3 are linked to solar exposure. If you lack sunlight, leptin resistance develops and diseases that ruin the NAD+/NADH redox couple at cytochrome 1 becomes ruined. Obesity and T2D are diseases of darkness due to a lack of sunlight. Obesity and T2D are effectively spectral solar deficiency diseases. A lack of solar light during the day is one side of T2D and obesity, but the presence of blue light at night effects another pathway that the human eye uses at night to optimize the mitochondrial in melanopsin, RPE, and the central retinal pathways that control the eye clock mechanism that controls circadian cycles. The absence of blue light at night is critical light effect needed to catalyze the solid state conversion of serotonin by using the process of methylation. Methylation during darkness mediates circadian clock plasticity both in the SCN and in the peripheral clocks. This solid state biochemical process requires a large exclusion zone (EZ) and the presence of vitamin B12 and folate. B12 is made in the liver under the presence of SOLAR daylight. Folate, on the other hand, is destroyed by full spectrum sunlight and it is produced under darkness when blue light is ABSENT. These are the two chemical arms that tell the quantum clinician if a patient is solar deficient and/or blue light toxic. Obesity and T2D are two such quantum diseases that manifest when this situation occurs in humans. B12 and Vitamin D3 are linked to specific sunlight frequencies and this is why pernicious anemia is associated with a lack of sun. In fact, most anemias are related to a lack of proper solar exposure. This link to anemia and B12 was found by Fritz Hollowich in the 1940's. In fact, I have found a link to low solar exposure and low B12 that dates back to 1927. It seems these links are well published on a long time ago but seem to be unknown by modern humans. It is nice to see medscape making the connections. T2D and obesity are epigenetic diseases of light that ruin cytochrome 1 and the Q-cycle. [http://www.medscape.com/viewarticle/872008](http://www.medscape.com/viewarticle/872008)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-20T04:05:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-leptin-link-to-sunlight-by-way-of-d3-and-b12.18751/post-204106>

How does sunlight link to high blood pressure? High BP is part of the metabolic syndrome that is linked to leptin resistance.

With more folks diagnosed with high BP these days due to their environments, we should consider the impact of EM radiation from all sources. Why does sunlight lower BP while nnEMF raise it? Sunlight lowers BP because it raises Vitamin D3 which increases NO release in the skin to vasodilate our skin surface to deliver 40-60% of blood to the surface to become irradiated by sunlight. The Vitamin D3 made in the skin also affects the renin angiotensin system (RAS) directly by lowering renin release from the kidney to affect the circulatory system. Vitamin D3 lowers renin in the kidney which lowers BP. Vitamin D3 and the RAS is even more plausible when taking into account that Vitamin D3 (VDR) and RAS receptors are distributed in most of the same tissues throughout the body.

From an evolutionary point of view, both systems were developed simultaneously, having an active participation in the regulation of inflammatory and immunological mechanisms in humans.

Changes in RAS activity and activation of VDR seem to be inversely related, making it possible to speculate that both systems can have a feedback relationship. Renin's primary function is therefore to eventually cause an increase in blood pressure, leading to restoration of perfusion pressure in the kidneys. Renin is secreted from juxtaglomerular kidney cells, which sense changes in renal perfusion pressure, via stretch receptors in the vascular walls. So the more phone calls you make the more sunlight you need........cause nnEMF and blue light RAISES BP and causes HYPERTENSION. They also dehydrate you lowering your intravascular volume so the HR and BP rise to offset the lower volume.

Sunlight increase plasma volume and inhibits anemia. nnEMF cause anemia and lower plasma volumes via dehydration.

Now:
**Fundamentally, Leptin Resistance (LR) is loss of UV light from cells. Why does the Leptin Rx tell you to hold off on exercise in the beginning stages? LR is a defect in excessive light release from cells due to their “relative mitonuclear mismatch”. Exercise causes you to lose more light in this situation. This further lowers your redox potential and can hurt you. LR is results when any respiratory protein deficit causes improper signaling to the nuclear DNA. LR is the signal the hypothalamus gets when the proper redox shift is not made in reference to supply and demand between the mitochondria and nucleus. What results instead, is an incompatibility between the mitochondrial respiratory protein functional capacity and your nuclear DNA. This situation causes mitochondria to move away from your nucleus, distance wise, when researchers look at the relationship via electron microscopy. This effect is magnified in species (humans) who use oxygen as their terminal electron acceptor in their mitochondria. Oxygen has a very strong affinity for electrons, hence why it is able to provide a pull for electrons on our inner mitochondrial membrane to improve the current and magnetic flux in mitochondria. When the respiratory proteins are not properly aligned, not even the valence shell desire for those electrons can be met fast enough. This is why pseudohypoxia exists in these states. Humans, birds, and bats are a species who has a very high aerobic capacity because they have a large mitochondria capacity. They all use oxygen as their terminal electron acceptor. This is why David Sinclair’s paper, mentioned above, on pseudohypoxia, is incredibly important for you to understand completely, on your road to optimal. For a human to survive this situation they would need to retain the ability to alter how electrons flow in their mitochondria to optimize function to their current environment. Why is the Exercise Rx important for you to understand now? Because how the electrons are created in mitochondria is also a variable few consider. I got the insight long ago when I found the melanocortin pathways connects to and from the eye and brain. Laser surgery taught me about free radicals and LR.

**When the respiratory proteins do a poor job of this, low NAD+ and SIRT 1 ratio’s are the result in cells. How does ketosis fit into this scenario? If you read my book, The Epi-Paleo Rx, you will see that ketosis is a “small critical part of reversing most diseases. If you don’t understand my blogs, you will think I have just contradicted myself in my book. I have not. Ketosis can change the flow of electrons, But it has zero effect on the electron spin or state, moreover it has no effect on size of the ECT protein components.

Hello

Ive had one car accident at 8 rolling over car and another minor head trauma when fainting and hittinh my gead on door frame at 34 years old.
Since that night Ive develloped adrenaline surges, extreme anxiety palpitations, bloating diarhea, heavy digestion. And started fainting only for 3 weeks one hour after falling asleep waking with a jump horrible tachycardia, loosing urines and it felt like pseudo seizures whole body tremors.
Then this fainting episodes stopped and Ive started to have histamine issues. Couldnt sleep and anxiety was uncontrolavle for hours.
Doctors put me in antidepresants, benzos and sleeping pill but my muscles would become mire and more stiff and painful.
Til 8 months later I had pheochromocytoma like symptoms without tumor thats what doctors said.
Functional doc put me and thyroid T3T4 hydrocortisone and progesterone pill. And few vitamins Bs, D, pregnenolone, nac, ala, zinc, adaptogens formula etc
But after 2 years we still couldnt explain why without progesterone I had this horrible adrenaline surges. And in Paris France I would always get constantly high adrenaline sweating light sensitivity, tachycardia.

Then 10 days ago doctors put me on betabkockers to stop adrenaline surges and it burned my head.
So Ive taken 500mg phosphatidylserine and it stopped burning and adrenaline.
I took Gaba supplements like l theanine and slept like a baby for a first time after 3 years with phosphatidylserine.

And light went on chronically elevevated cortisol.
Head trauma or even activated virus and estrogen dominance?

Well doctor told me to get on 7 keto dhea to balance cortisol and progesterone vit E orally to lower estrogen dominance.
Is it right path?

And how high cortisol can create histamines intolerance mcas?

Ive have been treated for hashi and low cortisol and mast cells till now.
Looks like I might not even need thyroid meds maybe.
And my leaky gut and leaky gut brain barrier can finally start to heal with balanced cortisol and no hydrocortisone.

Even if in this state without thyroid medications I was in dark room and it felt like every cell of my body was burning.

Im getting those 2 this week.

### Queen of Elves — 2016-12-02T21:33:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-learn-from-the-information-given.18754/post-204654>

In the beginning of reading the blogs I felt totally overwhelmed by all the information.
The first blog post I read was EMF 7: Quantum Prometheus. I couldn't believe what I read and wanted to understand all the basic physics. I read all things in English and indeed it got better and better. I printed everything out and highlighted the most important parts, I wrote down questions and tried to find the answers. I took notes on podcasts and searched for blogs with more details. I looked up some basic terms in Wikipedia. Then suddenly I began to remember things I read in former blogs or in the forum.But I still take many notes. I love to take notes

Now I even prefer to read things in English, for example Jim Al Khalilis book. And I'm so happy that I am even able to follow the webinars. But it's also difficult for me to explain things in german, for example to my physician who is very interested in what I told him, but I only know the english terms  I think I will try what Lahelada said, translate in German, in your own words...

But it's not only about understanding the science behind but also about implementing the knowledge to your daily life, trying things out, see what works for you. I need people to share my experiences with but my problem is, that I understand the special language and scientific terms pretty good but I am not used to speak common language, so that it's hard for me to have a conversation with native speakers. Therefore I feel a bit insecure here in the forum to ask/say something and asked for a meeting of german members. Now the fb group helps me to get some special information for our place here and how to apply things I just know theoretically.

Don't miss the video guys!!

Please share with people who need it.
Everyone who uses a phone before bed needs this setting.
Disclaimer: This setting only protects yours from blue light frequencies from your phone. To be fully protected in the modern world, we need Blue Light Blocking Glasses, like those from my company Ra Optics [https://raoptics.io](https://raoptics.io) to protect yourself from blue light emitted by other sources that cannot be easily filtered or you can't control, like light bulbs, TV's, streetlights, car headlights, and all other artificial light besides phones and computers.
Many companies make blue blocking glasses with clear lenses, but these generally have little to no effect except a false sense of hope, as when the proper colors are blocked to the extent necessary for health, there will always be some level of color distortion. At Ra Optics, we select the most attractive and popular frame styles to house our Blue Blocking lens technology and are constantly working on improvements.
\------
How to use a hidden setting to remove harmful sleep and melatonin-disrupting blue light frequencies from your iPhone at night.
If you do not use this, you will likely need glasses at a young age, your vision will continue to get worse, you will have low quality sleep, and you may experience related health issues including low-energy, carbohydrate cravings, and weight issues. Everyone who uses their phone before bed needs this setting.
For Android users, an equivalent is the "Twilight" app, used on full strength.
For computers, use the app "Iris", from iristech.co.
This is an update of a previous video. You can use the discount code “Optimal” to save a few bucks if you buy the glasses.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-01T01:49:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-using-blue-tooth-in-car-is-better-or-worse-than-having-the-phone-stuck-to-your-ear.18763/post-204568>

Robert O. Becker, best known for his research on “currents of injury” and the role they play in regeneration, made significant contributions to many areas of electrobiology. He was later drawn into public controversies over health effects—Becker is credited as the first to use the term “electro- magnetic pollution”—and in the end paid dearly for speaking out. For instance, in 1967, writing in Nature with Howard Friedman and Charles Bachman, Becker described how modulated magnetic fields could affect reaction times—now a hot topic among those studying cell phone radiation from higher G networks. Some years earlier, they found that admissions in psychiatric hospitals were associated with geomagnetic activity. Later, in a series of papers with Stephen Perry, a medical doctor in northern England, Becker and Marino linked exposures to power frequency fields to depression and suicide. Becker and Andy Marino faced a concerted and coordinated effort to suppress the truth which emanated from the military establishment and was simply aided and abetted by the greed of the utilities and the tarnished testimony of scientists for hire. Becker is one of my hero's. He wanted the general public to know that science isn’t run the way they read about it in the newspapers and magazines. It certainly is not runin via the scientific method any longer. He wanted lay people to understand that they cannot automatically accept scientists’ pronouncements at face value, for too often they’re self-serving and misleading. He wanted our citizens, nonscientists as well as investigators, to work to change the way research is administered by the federal government. They only fund research that they deem worthy. They see to it that good question are rarely funded if they will impede their modes of commerce. The way it's currently funded and evaluated, we're learning more and more about less and less, and science is becoming our enemy instead of our friend. Now we have hard data that cell phone radiation and glioma are linked. This will be no surprise to my members but I imagine it will shock the many skeptics who think technology offers progress and nothing but benefits. People need to understand how these things affect time scales of the relationship of NAD+ and SIRT 1 in mitochondria. They are critical in understanding how acute and chronic inflammation affect DNA expression. All inflammation is positively charged. Chronic inflammation has a much lower pH and more sustained pseudohypoxia. Acute inflammation usually leads to neolithic diseases that do not kill. Chronic inflammation leads to diseases like oncogenesis that extinguish life via higher grade and malignant tumors.

In acute inflammation, chromatin works as it is designed by evolution. Chromatin should departs from, and returns to, equilibrium on DNA in an orderly sequence to control protein synthesis and ubiquitin marking. This temporal sequence depends on proper circadian shifts in NAD+ availability for SIRT1 activation and deacetylation of signaling proteins, which support orderly gene reprogramming during acute inflammation by switching between euchromatin and heterochromatin. In contrast, in chronic inflammation states where the redox potential is low, oncogenesis is much more likely. The lower the redox or the more blue light one faces the more malignant the outcome is. This is due to the limited availability of NAD+, in concert, with the reduced expression of SIRT1 in the retina and in the gut, so this becomes able to sustain aberrant chromatin structure and functions. SIRT1 also influences inflammation and cancer by directly de-acetylating targets like NFκB, p65, and p53 in the genome. This is why RF/microwave nnEMF from cell phones is a huge problem.

Chronic inflammation sustains pro-inflammatory chromatin. With chronic inflammation, several things occur connected with SIRT1. A key point, is a high fat diet cleaves SIRT1 and lowers NAD+ levels to reduce SIRT1 synthesis. The net result of a high fat diet and lower NAD+ levels is a loss of SIRT1 function. This is the predator/prey relationship that I mentioned in the Ubi series that describes the loss of negative feedback control in cells. This results in hyperacetylation of the p65 subunit of NF-kB, which produces pro-inflammatory products. These proinflammatory mediators have a “positive feedback” on the formation of obesity, diabetes, cancer, and aging.[http://www.medscape.org/viewarticle/837035](http://www.medscape.org/viewarticle/837035)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-you-think-using-blue-tooth-in-car-is-better-or-worse-than-having-the-phone-stuck-to-your-ear.18763/post-206031>

Another stupid casualty. My sister-in-law (my age, 56) was just diagnosed with a stage II glioma. Presenting symptom was seizure and syncope. They found two masses...wait for it...directly over her ear on the side where she is welded to her smart phone. They have done a biopsy, but are holding off on a resection to get other opinions. Maybe radiation, maybe chemo, but no defined plan yet. And this is from Johns Hopkins. I'm angry, I don't understand why they could take a tissue sample and 10 days later still have "inconclusive" histopathology. Just venting really...I'm assuming the reason is that it is too diffuse to be operable and that her prognosis is poor.

And I still can't get my family to listen to me about the dangers of cell phones and nnEMF, much less blue light exposure.

# Thread: Plastic free ice cubes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plastic-free-ice-cubes.18766/>
**Subforum:** Community Cookbook
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sujoe123 — 2016-11-23T15:19:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plastic-free-ice-cubes.18766/post-204246>

Use Ramekin cups to freeze ice. They can be found in glass or ceramic and they stack nicely on a cookie sheet. You have to allow for a few minutes of thawing to dump them out to store in a glass bowl. These are the little cups that creme brulee and puddings are made in individual serving sizes. You can purchase them cheaply at a restaurant supplier. I think I paid about $30 for 50 of them.

### Sujoe123 — 2016-11-23T15:23:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/plastic-free-ice-cubes.18766/post-204247>

> [Sujoe123 said:](/goto/post?id=204246)
>
> Use Ramekin cups to freeze ice. They can be found in glass or ceramic and they stack nicely on a cookie sheet. You have to allow for a few minutes of thawing to dump them out to store in a glass bowl. These are the little cups that creme brulee and puddings are made in individual serving sizes. You can purchase them cheaply at a restaurant supplier. I think I paid about $30 for 50 of them.

It seems that with all the warnings of plastics that someone would have created an impact-resistant glass reusable ice cube that is partially filled with water to allow expansion for freezing. My daughter and I look for these online every few months and so far nobody has manufactured them! I think it would be a great idea!

### Sujoe123 — 2016-11-24T00:19:09+0200

Color me deeply, deeply skeptical about these products that claim to neutralize EMFs by generating a _'harmonic resonance to interact with fields and transform them at a subtle energy level',_ or some other such vague and unprovable mechanism.

Even _Safe Space_ admits that their products' EMF neutralizing effects can not be measured quantitatively with EMF meters-- they say it can only be measured at the **DNA and 'qualitative' levels**.

That said, there may indeed be a mitigating and positive effect at those subtle energy levels-- but how would you know it's working except maybe by 'how you feel' which is very subjective and can be impacted at any given moment by other environmental/biological factors.

_**The real danger in using such devices**_, in my opinion, is that product users may get a false sense of security (that they are being protected fully from nnEMF exposures) because they feel somewhat better using them, **BUT**, and this is a big BUT, their bodies are still getting adverse nnEMF effects at the quantum biological as well as biochemical level (for example, voltage gated calcium channel fluxes that start a whole cascade of oxidative chaos in mitochondrial and other cells).

Caveat emptor.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/understanding-melanoma-in-australia-by-way-of-viagra.18771/post-204302>

Why would drugs, like Viagra, cause a cancer like melanoma? Well Viagra causes massive release of nitric oxide (NO) without the stimulus of sunlight. Most people take Viagra indoors under blue light before they want to have sex. NO is made naturally by UVA light. UV-A light is not present all the time during the day. This various by latitude, altitude, and by mitochondrial haplotypes. What happens if your cells never see UV-A light because you live and work indoors? Blue light and nnEMF cause a rise in heteroplasmy rates in mitochondria and whehn you force NO higher, blood flow will increase and since RBC's carry oxygen to tissues. When there is a "forced release" of NO, we get more free radicals from the oxygen. delivery. NO and oxygen have to have a precision or a be associated with light quanta to work properly, and when they are not yoked as they would be in a natural endogneous coupled cycle to solar light we get spikes in oxygen radicals that lead to diseases of mitochondrial flux. Melanoma is one such disease so this should not surprise anyone. Several of my MD colleagues were very surprised by this link when I showed it to them today. Nitric oxide is one of the biggest stimulators of angiogenesis in the body, and increased angiogenesis is a hallmark of the oncogenic state. Most men who use this product have poor mitochondrial function to begin with. Why? Erectile dysfunction is due to a LACK of blood flow to the penis to get erect. Where does the blood come from? The heart. The heart has one of the highest mitochondrial capacities of any tissues, so if the mitochondrial energy flux is DECREASED we should expect less blood flow from the heart to distal tissues like the pelvis. This links heart disease to erections since the erect penis gets hard from the flow of blood to the penis. A recent medical study links sildenafil, best known by its brand name, Viagra, to an increased risk of melanoma, a deadly form of skin cancer. According to study results published by JAMA Internal Medicine, Viagra may have nearly doubled melanoma risk in an estimated 45 million men who opted to take the drug since it hit the market in 1998.

Skin cancer is the most common form of cancer in the United States, and melanoma is the deadliest form of the disease. According to the Melanoma Research Foundation, melanoma is usually, but not always, a cancer of the skin that begins in melanocytes, which are pigment-producing cells in the skin that synthesize melanin, the pigment that gives skin its color. Melanocytes are the cells responsible for the formation of moles, within which melanoma often develops. Rarely, melanoma can develop in mucus membranes or eyes, rather than the skin.
This is what happens when one decides to use a drug that goes against how our cells work with nature and sunlight. Perhaps utilizing the sunlight as the best drug possible can stave off the cancer effects of Viagra?
Also what happens is It also brings out all the lawyers looking for a big payday in representing Viagra users. If you know someone who uses these drugs you might want to share this with them. [https://www.drugwatch.com/viagra/melanoma/](https://www.drugwatch.com/viagra/melanoma/)

This various by latitude, altitude, and by mitochondrial haplotypes. What happens if your cells never see UV-A light because you live and work indoors? When there is a "forced release" of NO, we get more free radicals from the oxygen. delivery. NO and oxygen have to have a precision or a be associated with light quanta to work properly, and when they are not yoked as they would be in a natural endogneous coupled cycle to solar light we get spikes in oxygen radicals that lead to diseases of mitochondrial flux. Melanoma is one such disease so this should not surprise anyone. Several of my MD colleagues were very surprised by this link when I showed it to them today. Nitric oxide is one of the biggest stimulators of angiogenesis in the body, and increased angiogenesis is a hallmark of the oncogenic state. Most men who use this product have poor mitochondrial function to begin with. Why? Erectile dysfunction is due to a LACK of blood flow to the penis to get erect. Where does the blood come from? The heart. The heart has one of the highest mitochondrial capacities of any tissues, so if the mitochondrial energy flux is DECREASED we should expect less blood flow from the heart to distal tissues like the pelvis. This links heart disease to erections since the erect penis gets hard from the flow of blood to the penis. A recent medical study links sildenafil, best known by its brand name, Viagra, to an increased risk of melanoma, a deadly form of skin cancer. According to study results published by JAMA Internal Medicine, Viagra may have nearly doubled melanoma risk in an estimated 45 million men who opted to take the drug since it hit the market in 1998.
>
> Skin cancer is the most common form of cancer in the United States, and melanoma is the deadliest form of the disease. According to the Melanoma Research Foundation, melanoma is usually, but not always, a cancer of the skin that begins in melanocytes, which are pigment-producing cells in the skin that synthesize melanin, the pigment that gives skin its color. Melanocytes are the cells responsible for the formation of moles, within which melanoma often develops. Rarely, melanoma can develop in mucus membranes or eyes, rather than the skin.
> This is what happens when one decides to use a drug that goes against how our cells work with nature and sunlight. Perhaps utilizing the sunlight as the best drug possible can stave off the cancer effects of Viagra?

hi,
I'm Helen, I'm a new member, I'm curious if any one knows any info on how much and what kind of EMF are emitted from metal detectors. I work in state courts and they even make employee to thru those daily, but not judges. Recently Disneyland started using them on adults only, they let the kids go around it. That make me think Disneyland thinks it's damaging. I like to find out more about the possible damaging effects to help me determine whether I should change my work. Thank you.
Helen

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-26T16:41:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-little-info-out-there-on-emf-from-metal-detector.18776/post-204337>

a lot. Use the Cornet and trimeter..........always opt out to be felt up.

### Helen Hall — 2016-12-09T23:29:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/very-little-info-out-there-on-emf-from-metal-detector.18776/post-205066>

I used my RF meter (Acoustimeter) on my work's metal detector this week. The result was not as shockingly high as I feared. It hit peak at .65 V/m which is roughly equivalent to 1000 micro watts/meter square. I was told by the security guard that it's not emitting RF signals but it is emitting magnetic field. So it's possible what my RF meter picked up was a cell phone near by or wifi. I'll report back with magnetic reading when I get a chance to measure.

# Thread: General questions for living in the cold
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-questions-for-living-in-the-cold.18781/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2016-11-27T19:27:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-questions-for-living-in-the-cold.18781/post-204377>

What I wondered since knowing about CT:

Can men actually live in cold areas like russia or sweden without much clothes or say naked?
But when you can why so many people died in the past from hypothermia etc. like in WW2?

Its easy to do CT for us and go outside with no much clothes on but we have houses and heating. So what happens when we have no heatings and even at home its is sick cold, like say in the crises in the past?
Is it then doable?
Speak of that Wim Hof also lives in a heated house and wears normal clothes outside his challenges.

### nonchalant — 2016-11-27T20:52:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-questions-for-living-in-the-cold.18781/post-204384>

> [Butters said:](/goto/post?id=204377)
>
> What I wondered since knowing about CT:
>
> Can men actually live in cold areas like russia or sweden without much clothes or say naked?
> But when you can why so many people died in the past from hypothermia etc. like in WW2?
>
> Its easy to do CT for us and go outside with no much clothes on but we have houses and heating. So what happens when we have no heatings and even at home its is sick cold, like say in the crises in the past?
> Is it then doable?
> Speak of that Wim Hof also lives in a heated house and wears normal clothes outside his challenges.

I think they would have to stay naked, actually. Like those copper-skinned natives in frigid, windy Tierra Del Fuego that died once they adopted the clothing of their civilized discoverers.

### brent-next — 2017-02-12T17:21:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-questions-for-living-in-the-cold.18781/post-208457>

> [Butters said:](/goto/post?id=204377)
>
> What I wondered since knowing about CT:
>
> Can men actually live in cold areas like russia or sweden without much clothes or say naked?

There is a big tradition in eastern an northern Europe of winter swimming. I spent last winter there and did this every day for the first half of the winter.

People prefer winter swimming naked because getting your naked by dry and safely dressed is a lot easier than getting a swimming suit (that may be freezing) off your body with fingers that are getting stiffer each minute.

The winter swims that people do are almost always less than 30 seconds. There is even a separate word for it in Russian (coophatsa), that means to get in and out of cold water quickly. There is a different word for longer swims that you might do in the summertime (plavit).

### Inger — 2017-02-12T19:36:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-questions-for-living-in-the-cold.18781/post-208465>

I also take short dips only... around a minute or so. But the water is freezing cold. It somehow feels enough to just stay in that short. I am in awe how people here sit for an hour or more in icebaths!   Well I am not overweight at all so maybe that is one reason.. or I am just a wuss....
I do think short dips hav a lot of benefits tho! Feeling very healthy here

### shah78 — 2017-02-12T19:57:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-questions-for-living-in-the-cold.18781/post-208472>

> I do think short dips hav a lot of benefits tho! Feeling very healthy here

Yeah, a big(thin) wuss ! Spoken by the woman who sleeps in a horse barn ,in Northern Germany, in the Winter. Yup, a big wuss.

### brent-next — 2017-02-14T09:40:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-questions-for-living-in-the-cold.18781/post-208556>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=208465)
>
> I also take short dips only... around a minute or so. But the water is freezing cold.

The short dips in very cold water definitely can trigger a release of epinephrine and norepinephrine to make you feel good.

But my current guess is that they don't help you very much on your road to optimal.

I've just started a new thread explaining my experience with winter swimming last winter.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/does-winter-swimming-cold-thermogenesis.19082/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/does-winter-swimming-cold-thermogenesis.19082/)

I'm thinking that sitting motionless in a 50 deg. F tub for 20 minutes would do you a lot more good than a minute of winter swimming.

Yesterday I added wool socks and did my first 17 minute soak (motionless) in a sunny 50 deg. F shallow pool. The wool socks helped a LOT. Sitting motionless helped a LOT. Every time I would move slightly I got colder.

### shah78 — 2017-02-14T10:41:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/general-questions-for-living-in-the-cold.18781/post-208559>

I endorse this message.

UV and IR light from the sun and cold are a medical solution to a medical problem because the sun's light is the off switch for stress hormones.

**Sunlight shifts blood into the skin by changing polarization in tissues that allow for dermal pooling due to molecular resonance changes in eNOS and NO.** More than 50% of your blood volume can change your skin surface when specific solar spectral density is present. This can lower your blood pressure and it raise you sulfated vitamin D3. It changes the anatomical structure of the skin by changing the optics of surfaces. It becomes a sensory stimulus for the interoceptive system to induce biochemical substrates via photosynthesis in plants and changes in the ATPase using red light and water in animals. Sunlight increase Vitamin D, histamine, and sulfhydryl groups, while lowering (photolysis) **ADRENALIN**, steroids, testosterone, estrogen, thyroid hormone, DNA and RNA. **Sunlight induces biochemical reactions via photolysis and it induces coordinated endocrine adaptation effects in the eye and the skin surfaces. It affects the sympathetic and parasympathetic systems. It is the stimulus for the circadian timing mechanism of the body clock via the central retinal pathways. All these effects are built into the electronics of your proteins under solar power and magnetic flux.**

Cold water immersion at 14°C increased metabolic rate by 350%, norepinephrine by 530% & dopamine by 250%. Wow. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10751106/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10751106/)

# Thread: Low - Secretory IGA - Anyone know about this?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-secretory-iga-anyone-know-about-this.18785/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### __D__ — 2016-11-28T02:24:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-secretory-iga-anyone-know-about-this.18785/post-204399>

Hello I recently had a stool test done and my Secretory IGA was on the low end @ 3. I looked up the potential cause and it could be allergies, stress, primary immune dysfunction, celiac, chrons, etc....

I am hoping to see an immunologist as the rheumatalogist said he still could not make a diagnosis other than auto immune phenomenom. I am also thinking Low Secretory IGA could be a clue for me, as if it were because of primary immune dysfuntion, then it would make sense why I get sick for longer, more frequently etc...

However, I have been unwell after a severe gut infection in Indonesia - I never fully recovered to a baseline I was used to. In fact, I am pretty sure I was having some immune problems prior to all of that as well. I have tried Paleo AIP, an elimination diet, and copious amounts of blood tests -

What has helped was cutting gluten. My sinus infections went away quickly. But at times, I have unusual autoimmune markers that pop up and go away (ANA, Low C3, C4, DNA Antibody, RNP), along with consistent low platelets.

After years of experiencing painful joints, stiff muscles, and cognitive problems (memory issues), chronic fatigue, and now neurological issues (balance problems), I am still at a loss of what is causing this kind of situation in my body. If its primary immune dysfunction, then it would make sense that I cant fight pathogens (i have dysbiotic ones in my stool) / viruses not matter how hard i try. At least this is my theory after looking back over the years of countless sinus infections, low energy, hormonal disruptions, and illnesses that seemed to drag on much longer than my peers.

As mentioned, I am hoping to find more answers with an immunologist, but I was wondering if someone has more expertise or has come across this themselves?

I came across this site after researching a man named Wim Hof and cold therapy. I have not tried this yet as something that could help me.

Thank you

# Thread: Compression shorts biohack failure

### PaulG — 2016-11-28T07:44:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/compression-shorts-biohack-failure.18787/post-204412>

For the past few months I have had chronic pain in the groin/pelvic area normally triggered by playing tennis but generally good after 1-2 nights. Issue seemed to be resolving as I gained more pelvic stability.

However, the last three nights I have woken up with major pain and pelvic instability that got worse during the day, last night I could barely walk to bed. I also haven't played any tennis for two weeks due to a wrist sprain, so I figured something was seriously wrong and I was wracking my brains as to what had changed in my environment. The only thing I thought of was that I had been wearing compression shorts for the last few nights/days. This morning I took them off and had a 20 min TENS session and within minutes the pain went and I gained pelvic stability (i.e. I can life up my leg).

Here is the shocker, the compression shorts were **copper threaded.** I bought them thinking they would help increase semi-conduction and therefore healing based on what I have learnt here (or not learnt!) . For example you can buy grounding shoes with copper plugs. I do have a grounding sheet on the bed that connects to the outside earth but not sure if the ground contains dirty electricity. I also know copper can act as an antenna for nnEMF but I think my environment is ok on that front, though I have never tested during the night, it might be when all the neighbours smart meters connect and upload to the mothership.

# Thread: Sun Disciple's journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 47
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sun Disciple — 2016-11-29T01:43:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/post-204455>

Hey everyone, Im from ontario canada about 45 minutes north of Toronto. Its a small city but i live right on the edge of town.
I am currently trying to recover from adrenal/Hpa axis disregulation. It started almost a year ago I thought it was mainly caused by high stress and overtraining. But know studying Dr K's work i believe lack of sunlight and artificial blue light to be the main factor. The previous summer to my taking ill,I was not out in the sun nearly as much as usual. I was working under artificial blue light more than usual. I did own a pair of blue blockers but i was only using them an hour before bed. I first went to see a local naturopath and took some labs. My Testosterone was low, thyroid was off, cortisol in the am was slightly low but not too bad i could still get out of bed. I was wired but tired as they say. Liver enzymes were elavated. I Had zero motivation, no sex drive, low energy brain fog, depression and inability to handle stress. I would get this wierd tightening sensation on my lower back around the kidney area. At the time i crashed i was low carb following bulletproof intermittent fasting which i had been doing for a few years. I was overtraining lifting heavy under blue light at the gym. after crashing I stopped working out,stopped intermittent fasting, upped my carbs, started eating protein for breakfast, targeted herbal supps and adaptogens as well as getting a single NIR Light to shine on my thyroid. After a few months of this I retested and managed to get my liver and thyroid in better shape. Then i was introduced to Nutritional Balancing and began working with Dr L Wilson. I started then on mineral and glandular supps based off of a hair analysis test. I also started collecting and drinking local spring water, grounding to the earth by visiting the forest as much as possible, getting midday sun and eventually morning sun. I also was eating about ten cups cooked veg daily. I bought an NIR lamp sauna and did daily coffee enamas for detox. I was religous about getting to bed by nine oclock and getting as much rest as possible.

Over the summer i began to make serious progress with the nutritional balancing program. last month i got interested in dr gerald pollocks work and then that rabbit hole led me back to Dr K who I had known from Ben greenfield. I started really digging in and catching up on his work. I began putting together that maybe some of the things had done over the summer that coincided with dr K's work really were the main reason for my progress thus far. I realised with the seasons changing i might have to start implementing some of the protocols found here. Im kind of torn on how deep to go right away. Qauntum medicine really facinates me and the more i read the more its making sense of things for me, in many different areas, but I still feel hesitent to completely abandon my current regimen because I have made good progress and acorrding to my current labs im not done detoxing. Currently im trying to do the hacks to my inviroment that dont interfere to much and gradually work my way over completley.
Right now im working on hacking the light in my enviroment catching every drop of sun i can squeeze from the overcast skies, increase my seafood intake and slowly lowering carbs. Tomorrow I will post a complete inventory of my light hacking arsenal and ask for some tips on what application might be optimal for my situation.

Cheers,

Paul

### Sun Disciple — 2016-11-29T17:21:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/post-204486>

This is my light hacking regimen. But first let me say that I understand this is no substitute for the sun and i make it my first priority to be outside when its shining.

winter regimen at 44th parallel

\-wake up usually hour or so before sunrise wear blue blockers and get outside drink spring water and ground myself.
\-at sunrise get in the sun and do yogic breathing practice. If there is no sun i will look directly at solar glow 160 for five minutes.
\- for daytime indoor lighting im combining natural daylight with black lights i have 2 globe 13 watt black light cfls and 2 Feit 13 watt black light cfl. I also have some edison decrative incandesant bulbs that i use sparingly. They are quite amber colored and easy on the eyes.
\- I have gunnar computer glasses for computer use in the daytime.
\- sperti vit D lamp Im using in winter only 5 minutes 2 days on one day off will adjust as i monitor my d levels. I wish i had seen Jansz's spectral analysis before i made this purchase as I would have rather have the fiji red lamp.
\- f.lux running on all devices 24/7
\- rubylux all red 660 led bulbs for night time
\- uvex blue blocker from 5 pm to 6
\- red laser glasses from 7 till 9
\- Near infrared sauna powered with four red incandacent infra red bulbs 250 watts each. I dothirty minute session at 105F
\- completely blacked out room for sleep

Now i was listening to a recent podcast with Dr K recommending purple light/cold/uv with the sauna so i was thinking about moving the sauna session to early afternoon,putting black lights in the sauna follow it up with cold shower and finish with sperti session.

Ive got my local optometrist to track down 550 Bpi lenses for me im just trying to decide on a frame that doesnt have to much leakage in from the perimeter.

### Sun Disciple — 2016-11-30T21:00:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/post-204552>

This morning I visited the Elmvale spring to re-up on water. Its an artesian housed under a little gazebo beside some farmers fields. On a nice day you get some really beutifull views over the field as the sun reaches out over the pastures. Today It was sun showers and i was able to catch some rays in between the intermittent trickles . The spring is famous for being one of the cleanest on earth. tested to have less arcenic than deep ice core samples from 10,00 years ago. Im gratefull to have this close by. I probably wouldnt have started using it unless I had burnt out so thats one positive out of many I can take from this.

### Sun Disciple — 2016-12-11T17:32:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/post-205160>

well its been a busy week of biohacking where do i begin...

\-I bought clorine filters for shower and bathtub which i have been using to make ice for my CT.

\-I completed 7 days of CT face dunking. My tap water is coming out at 51 F so it wasnt hard at all. I filled up a little wash basin with some ice and filtered tap water. I would dunk my face until surface skin temp reached 51 F. I also got a infrared thermometer on sale at a local hardware store.

\-My diet is now 75% seafood eating around 50 grams of carbs more or less. Im trying to go for 2 to 3 dozen raw oysters and 1 to 2 dozen raw clams a week. the rest in cold water fish and shrimp. specifically Ive had mackerel, sockeye, cod, artic char, sardines, anchovies and tiger shrimp.
This morning I had raw clams on the halfshell with sardines and Chinese cabbage drenched in olive oil turmeric black pepper corn and real salt. I was up before sunrise so i had to prepare under rubylux all red bulbs in the kitchen. My neighbors must think im a maniac they can see me through the window as they leave for work.

The guy at the seafood market warmed me not to eat raw clams but I didnt listen. Ive had only one incident so far I had a loose stool a couple hours after ingesting once. Other than that feeling fine. I have a pretty good immune system, i figure if i did get sick I would be stronger for fighting it off and id be able to handle more raw shellfish in the long run. Shucking those clams makes oysters seem like a breeze. But im pretty patient and I dont give up easily. Taste wise they were similar to oysters but less sweet more salty. They were significantly meatier as well because there about twice the size of the mapleque oysters ive been getting. The other thing i like about them is they are a third of the price of oysters which is allowing me to consume shellfish almost every day.

\- i purchased a cheap wired usb mouse and keyboard to give me some distance from my laptop and to replace my blutooth setup on my MAC Mini. I also disabled bluetooth in my car for my phone and im now putting my phone in the trunk while im in my car, giving me some extras distance while driving. I have an old Ipod which Im dedicating to Airplane mode hardwired into my car deck through usb which im using for audio books and podcasts.

\- Ive been scouting out locations where ican get some sun exposure out in the forest with minimal clothing. I found a good spot to watch the sunrise within 10 minute walk from my house. Its on the edge of a corn field. It is private property but there are 4 conifer trees that give me perfect cover from the road and farmers house as I face the sunrise in the east. I also found a nice spot in the forest for miday sun and grounding located just off the main trail. It has been pretty cold and snowy but im still managing to get outside for a couple hours bare chested and barefoot. Yesterday morning it was Minus 15 Celsius so i only lasted a half hour in my shorts. Im waiting on a pair of all leather mocasins but they wont be here for a few weeks. In the mean time im trying to cover up my toes as much as possible still leaving the rest of my foot bare to the ground. Ive been out every day even if I cant see the sun which is most days. I figure every bit of daylight i can get on my bare skin the bettter even if its off of dim gloomy clouds.

\- I recived my new custom blue blockers yesterday! I custom ordered lenses from a local optomotrist. The only company in canada they could find for BPI 550 was from HOYA. They were 550 BPI tint deep red with anti reflective coating and TV transmission of between 40-45. I got a deal on them because they misqouted the price before we ordered at 185$ CAN. I saved 200 bucks because they are really 385$ CAN just for the lenses. I got them put into a pair of oakley frames. The lenses themselves are amazing! crystal clear no glare and perfect for driving. However Im kind of regretiing the frames i chose because there is light leakage on the bottom and sides. Not to much but enough to piss me off. Im going to have the frame heated up and bent to give them a bit of a wrap but they still wont be perfect. Worst case Ill look into somone customizing the frame to give me some light blockage. So check out my "Alien Sunglasses"

\- This week ive started flicking the breaker to my bedroom before sleep, I bought an all beeswax candle for reading in the evening and im gonna try and be in complete darkness between the hours of 7 and 9 before bed listening to audio books on airplane mode ipod.

I think thats it phew

### JanSz — 2016-12-11T21:33:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/post-205164>

Sun Disciple
Gender: Male
Birthday: Dec 25, 1984 (Age: 31)
ontario canada about 45 minutes north of Toronto
\----
I am currently trying to recover from adrenal/Hpa axis disregulation.

My Testosterone was low, thyroid was off, cortisol in the am was slightly low but not too bad i could still get out of bed. I was wired but tired as they say. Liver enzymes were elavated. I Had zero motivation, no sex drive, low energy brain fog, depression and inability to handle stress.

My diet is now 75% seafood eating around 50 grams of carbs more or less.

I have made good progress and acorrding to my current labs im not done detoxing.
I bought an NIR lamp sauna and did daily coffee enamas for detox.
\------------------------------------------------------------------------
\=====================
Thinking of detox.
Why we need detox?
We collect bad guys because we do not have enough good guys.
Think about process of optimizing good guys
as a detox.
No specific separate campaign of detoxing is necessary.
Campaign of optimizing good guys is highly beneficial.
\-
Note that you are changing to a diet that is skewed toward Omega3.
I have not seen advice that would specify desirable amount of quantity of seafood as % of one's diet.
That is neither here no there.
But one of those o3 may throw in monkey wrench and must be monitored diligently, EPA.
\--------
List of good guys that is relatively easy to identify, measure and optimize:
\--
This list measures 35 fatty acids
you want them ideally in the mid range,
practically 25% + or - of the middle is still acceptable
this is the test you want to get:
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
\------
Probably good place to say 4:1
[http://www.direct-ms.org/pdf/NutritionFats/Yehuda](http://www.direct-ms.org/pdf/NutritionFats/Yehuda) Omega 3 6 ratio.pdf
4:1 of what
4:1 of eaten food, should contain 4 parts of consumed LA for one part of consumed ALA.
It is not easy to be precise.
Good start can be had by having 1 tablespoon/day of
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000GAO91K/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000GAO91K/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1)
\------
Another 35 items can be measured and latter optimized using this:
[https://www.spectracell.com/order/](https://www.spectracell.com/order/)
Micronutrient Test (MNT)
blood draw for this test you can get in place near you, at:
[https://www.spectracell.com/find-a-draw-site-results/?branch\_country=CA&search\_by\_other\_go=Search](https://www.spectracell.com/find-a-draw-site-results/?branch_country=CA&search_by_other_go=Search)

Work there not only on outright deficiencies but also on borderline cases.
\-------
Testosterone and cortisol belong to
Steroid Hormone Panel.
This hormones are based on cholesterol.
It is good to know if one's total cholesterol is good, high or low.
Specially if it is low, gets interesting.
Other than (the usual) tests listed on
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-199717](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-199717)

you may want to get also this test:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-199890](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-199890)

This will give you overall view of your
Circadian Rhythm
and
Steroid Hormone Panel
in rarely seen details.
\----------
.

### Sun Disciple — 2016-12-12T16:40:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/post-205184>

Hey Jansz,

Thanks for that rundown of recommended labs. My next step is getting some done as soon as I have the funds. This will make it much easier to decide as I was getting a little overwhelmed with all the endless options.

When I said 70% seafood diet I really meant to say 70% of protein is from seafood. Im really trying to move towards the epi peleo diet completely. So far with all my adjustments in diet environment and lifestyle I have noticed improvements in mood,energy and less brain fog. I have noticed some old symptoms coming back which i resolved over the summer following nutritional balancing program.
Im having cold attacks where im freezing cold no matter how many layers of clothes or increase of ambient temperature. Im getting one a day usually lasting for an hour. I used to get these when in ketosis in the winter when i was following paleo. I always assumed it was a thyroid issue perhaps because of low carb low iodine? Right now im eating seaweed with every non seafood meal but perhaps i could use some added supplementation. Ive got a bottle of Lugols 2% but Im a little wary of how much to take as Ive read a few cases on this forum and else where of further trashing their thyroid from too much supplementation. I guees I have to do some homework on that too.

The other issue is this weird itching underneath the skin of the bottom of my feet. It only occurs at night when in bed.It is not on the surface so scratching or any topical treatment does nothing. Every doctor which looked at it was completley stumped but I think It was tied to high liver enzymes because it seemed to cease once I gave some liver support through supps and getting off of high fat low carb for summer.

As far as detox is concerned I understand that the philosophy towards heavy metals and toxins is completely opposite or at least lower on the list of priorities compared to my previous program (nutritional balancing) which I am switching over from. The more blogs and podcasts I consume the more Im getting the message. I have cut down the sauna sessions and coffee enemas to once or twice a week from doing them daily.

### Sun Disciple — 2016-12-19T23:03:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/post-205495>

So where Ive had some success with mood boost, less brain fog, energy and motivation In switching over to Jacks protocols. Im seeing some old symptoms returning. I have been waking up at 430 on the dot every morning with a horrible tingling itching sensation on the bottom of my right foot on the inner edge of my heel towards the middle of the foot. I had this last winter as well but it went away as I did a nutritional balancing program. I really never figured out what it . The Dr's I visited had no clue. It nearly drove me nuts last year as it wrecked my sleep. I wondering if its connected to me returning to low carb? It goes away as soon as i move my feet but returns as soon as I remain still lying in bed. Its not a skin issue as there is no visible sign of any marks or bumps or anything. Its almost as if its a nerve issue or something. Anyone with any insight would be immensely appreciated.

The other concern is waking up to pee during the night has increased as well. I was reading Jamie ward's journal [here
](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/jamies-journal-uk-based-weightlifting-hormone-imbalances.17513/)
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...based-weightlifting-hormone-imbalances.17513/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/jamies-journal-uk-based-weightlifting-hormone-imbalances.17513/)
