### Sun Disciple — 2016-12-21T17:53:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/post-205597>

Its been a rough few days. I stabbed myself in the middle of my hand with an oyster shucker. Got myself good. I was opening up some of the big cherrystone clams and the last one exploded on me. I was being impatient with it and forcing it too much.
Last night I ate some spanish mackerel and I felt sick all night. I also woke up to pee at least 4 times not to mention being tortured by this itchy crawly feeling on the bottom of my foot. I was warmly greeted by an actual sunrise this morning though. I made my way out to the farmers field and stripped down to shorts,hat and gloves. I managed to recharge for an hour before the clouds rolled back in. I came home to start the next level of CT. I have completed the 2 weeks of face dunking and today was my first of icing my torso. I picked up a compression shirt and a plastic container to make the ice yesterday. The container didnt freeze all the way through and i only ended up with a 10 pound block. I laid down in the bath tub and used some gloves to press the block into my chest and stomach. I did ten minutes and will gradually increase to an hour.
I tried searching the forum yesterday to try and figure out this foot issue and I came across jacks blog on peripheral neuropathy.
[https://www.jackkruse.com/what-is-peripheral-neuropathy/](https://www.jackkruse.com/what-is-peripheral-neuropathy/)
[https://www.jackkruse.com/what-is-peripheral-neuropathy/](https://www.jackkruse.com/what-is-peripheral-neuropathy/)
Im experiencing the third symptom Jack speaks of here "Dyesthesia which is an unpleasant burning crawling, itchy, sensation in a numbed or dulled area".
My foot isnt really numb though but its deffinetly itchy and crawly.
Im wondering if this is what Is causing it? Its funny how It seems to pop up always in the winter months for me and only at night mainly.
Jack prescribes epi paleo, low carb and CT Which I have already started. The other main thing is to increase iodine. I have been eating mostly seafood and kelp daily but maybe this isnt enough. I have some lugols but as Ive mentioned before Im unsure of proper dosage. The last thing I wanna do is further trash my thyroid. Also he mentions dehydration as a key factor. I think im showing signs of that as im waking duriing the night to urinate more often. This is so frustrating because I thought I had fixed these issues last spring.

### Sun Disciple — 2017-01-02T02:17:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/post-206021>

well its been a long time between posts trying to keep my screen time down but here it goes....

I have been consumed by trying to catch up with jacks work. I just crushed jim al kalilis life at the edge and a bunch of his documentary's.Also Just finished the epipaleo Rx and tomorrow ill be moving on to the body electric. Jacks lit a fire under my ass I cant get enough of this stuff. Ive got all the other recomended reads in the mail.
So my new favorite hack is my brand new pair of leather sole moccasins I got for X\_mas. I no longer have to check if my toes to see if there are gonna fall off while im grounding in the morning sunrise. I got these bad boys right here.
[http://www.theearthingstore.com/col...ootwear/products/mens-heavy-oil-tan-moccasins](http://www.theearthingstore.com/collections/mens-earthing-footwear/products/mens-heavy-oil-tan-moccasins)
[http://www.theearthingstore.com/col...ootwear/products/mens-heavy-oil-tan-moccasins](http://www.theearthingstore.com/collections/mens-earthing-footwear/products/mens-heavy-oil-tan-moccasins)
They have alot of other good grounding shoe options check em out.

I started Full body CT in the tub. Ive got the water down to 47 F and i set a 40 pound block of ice on my chest and breatheDEEP for 45 minutes. Oh ya Also ive been doing whim hoff breath before hand 4 rounds of 40 breaths before i jump in. After Im done I try to let my body naturally restore its temperature before warming myself up. I remeber reading that somwhere its beneficial to do if your trying to kick start your hormones. If i have time Ill hop in my near infrared sauna for 15 mins or so with added purple light and sperti vit D. Before bed ive been doing face dunks and if i have time I might add in another full ct round.

In my last post I was experiencing dehydration and frequent night urinations. I figured out that It coressponded with me stopping coffee enemas. So I added thenm back in and voila it seemed to have fixed the problem at least temporarily until I find the source or CT eventually sorts it out. I undesstand im only treating the symptom but I would recomend it in the short term to anyone suffereing from similar effects. I only use spring water a small amount of ground bulletproof coffe boiled together for 12 minutes and cooled with fresh spring water. I hold for atleast half hour to 45 mins and I know Im dehydrated when I absorb 70% of the liquid.

My other issue with the itching/nerve issue on the bottom of my feet at night has been toned down. I followed some of Jacks supplement recommendations on his peripheral neuropathy blog . I addded three drops of lugoils 5%, Nac and R alpha lipoic acid.

I also got really sick off of raw oysters last week and ever since Ive been having digestive issues whenever I eat raw seafood or shell fish. I grabbed some digestive enzymes and they helped a little but I think I gotta listen to my body and take a break for ahwhile. I have no problem with cooked fish and grass fed beef so im gonna stick with that for ahwhile.

Last night I hads some gigantic gulf shrimp and scallops which tasted great but the intestines did not like me an hour later.

Today was new years day I woke up just before sunrise ran out the door and hit the farmers field just in time to catch sun peaking through a thin crack in the cloudy horizon. I got about 5 mins of glorious morning rays on my bare chest and retina. It was seriously needed I havnt seen the bare sun IN altleast 5 days . weve been getting dumped on with the fluffy white stuff.
Once it set back up into the clouds i suited back up and exitedly headed home in anticipation of the epic breakfast that awaited me. I started off with sauted red onions and cracked 2 eggs in the middle to artistically represent the fleeting sunrise i just witnessed.

Then i carefully poured that over some smoked Artic Char with olive oil.

I savored that for as long as I could, Sooooooooooo good.

After Ct the clouds parted and made way for a full afternoon of winter sun. I made my way out to the Forest and found a quiet trail. I threw on my mocassins and plowed through the fresh powder until I found a well lit spot between the trees. I dug myself a good area where i could do some body weight exercises. I stripped down to shorts and got to work. That infra red light and my blood pumping through my viens felt good. I continued on my way taking in the white dusted scenery before I headed of to the spring to clollect water for the week.

So Ive decided that a vacation to mexico Is my next step. I need seven days to recharge my battery. Right now im gathering everything I need for the trip and hopefully taking advantage of a last minute deal somtime in the next month. Id like to do the yucatan but its expensive. I dont need anything fancy. My requirements are acces to beach for CT, morning sunrise and some decent food. Ive never gone away anywhere warm so I figure this year ill try a resort but next year Ill do progresso and be a little more adventurous. Im trying to heal so Id like to have as much of things taken care of for me so i can take full advantage of the climate. If anybody has any recommendations or tips I would apreciate it.

Signing off....

### Sun Disciple — 2017-01-03T16:08:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/post-206082>

Wow so yesterday was a break through day for me. Energy was through the roof, mood was amazing, libido was back, felt strong, motivated . Its been a week of fullbody CT and it seems to be paying off already.
I started the day off with an hour of sun exposure and grounding. Did CT and then hopped in infrared sauna with 10 min sperti session. It was a beautiful sunny day so I took off to the forest to spend the early afternoon hiking in the sunshine.

The IR light was strong I found a nice spot threw on my leather moccasins stripped down to shorts and did some inner fire breath work followed by drinking in the quiet of the forest. I was sudddenly startled by a large crack then a thud. My eyes shot open to see a large branch lying in a mist of white dust about ten feet to my left. It had buckled under the weight of the heavy snow we had the night before. I took a few steps back and looked up to examine the trees above my head and decided it might be best to continue on my way.
Ive been taking a steel canteen of spring water with me and burying it in the snow before I go on a hike so when I return I have some cold water awaiting me. Ive found the taste and effect I get from the water is heightened, perhaps its absorbing thermal or IR energy?

This morning I added in some IR light to my CT bath.

Originally my plan was to get some Labs done to check my progress but im having such success with hacking my enviroment I think the money is better spent continuing that endevour.
Id rather spend that 700 bucks on a gauss and RF meter or a qauntlet or magnetico. As long as I am continuing to make progress I dont see the need to test just yet.

### Sun Disciple — 2017-01-19T01:34:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/post-207074>

Well we got hit with an ice storm a few days ago. I stepped out in the morning to get my morning light and slipped on the second concrete step from my house. Two feet up in the air and i landed right on my tail bone. I was writhing around on the frozen snow next to the step. Luckily I had already drawn a cold bath just before heading out. So upon crawling back in side went immediatly into the tub. After a good 45 minute sesssion I felt no pain just stiffness. Ive been able to maintain that state for the last two days jumping in the tub and spot icing my backside whenever possible. more time to catch up on the jack kruse syllabus. Just finishing the fourth phase and then its on to The Body Electric. It sucks being injured but I am gratefull I didnt crack my skull or mess up any nerves. It happened so quick and I felt that sense of helplessnes. Just another reminder of how delicate things are and how quick things can happen. So slow down, recognise and be grateful for what you have nothing is permanent. Ok im done preaching Im feeling stiff here in front of this screen time to CT.

### Sun Disciple — 2017-01-20T00:49:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/post-207120>

I stayed in bed for 10 hours lastr night woke up a few times stiff as aboard but woke up feeling prettty good and even better aftrer a morning dip in the tub. Ive had the craving for liverwurst the last few days but couldnt really find anything that wasnt loaded with crappy ingredients. I bought a pound of pork belly sliced and a pound of beef liver to see what i could come up with. I started out with melted ghee turmeric and rosemary in 2 pans. Then added meat and salt to taste as they cooked on medium heat. I took some hydrated sea spaghetti out of the fridge to use at the end of my meal to sop up all the spicey juices left over . It was really good I forgot to take a picture before i devoured it but i snapped a quick pic of the leftovers before I threw it in the freezer. I know it doesnt look apetizing but boy It cured my craving big time.

### Sun Disciple — 2017-03-15T15:34:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/post-210401>

I got back from my trip to the Yucatan just little over a week ago. It was my first time going to mexico and it was a good learning experience especially because I was traveling alone. I ended up going to cancun and stayed at Bel air collection resort and spa. It was a3.5 star resort right on the beach. It was on the more quiet side of the strip which was good cause I went to relax. When I got there I soon realized that my phone, credit card, and debit card did not work. I was limited to what I could do by the amoiunt of cash I had in my pocket. It was my plan to grab some groceries and spring water so I could eat half decent while I was there. Unfortunatly I had to rely on the resort food. I kinda went off the rails a little bit eating too late and way too much. Other than that It was good weather so I spent the majority of my time on the beach and in the ocean. The weather held up the whole time I was there so I got lots of sun with an emphasis on the morning. I was out at dawn in board shorts and practiced meditation and breathing excercises facing the rising sun. My plane ride home sabotaged my recharge somewhat. I was already dehydrated before take off as my resort cut me off bottled water after an early check out time. I didnt have many pesos left to make sure i was covered for water for the rest of the day. The plane got held up on the tarmac for hours while a thunderstorm hung over our heads. My midnight arrival time turned into 5 am before we touched down in Toronto. I had my blue blockers and sleep mask handy but I didnt sleep much. I was pretty beat up for the next few days and tried to rest as much as possible. I definetly benefited from the experience and glad I went. Now I feel much more confident that I will be able to plan a more optimal trip when It comes time next season. I feel like I only had 2 of the three legged stool working for me as I didnt have great water or food. But I soaked up as much sun and beach as I could to make up for it. It was also nice to be off the grid and disconected even if it was a little more than I bargained for. I found it a humbling experience not having my cards or phone to rely on, not to mention my Spanish is extremely limited.

I had a few labs drawn before I left and I got the results back a few days after I returned home. I was surprised and discouraged to see that my testosterone was still almost non existent. Im going to have to rethink my strategy somewhat. I posted my results here on the forum for some feedback here [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/sun-diciples-labs.19204/
](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/sun-diciples-labs.19204/)
I have been pretty strict keto this winter and I fear I might have been maybe putting to much stress on my system by eating very little carbs. Im not trying to lose weight so im gonna add some back in and see if that affects my thyroid and libido. Thanks to forum member Kris90 for the suggestion.
Ive also ordered in a Gauss meter and RF meter to test my enviroment to help reduce exposure to EMF's.

### Sun Disciple — 2017-03-15T16:01:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/post-210404>

I just finished reading the healing sun. It does a great job at laying out the history of Sun therapy. It chronicles Its use in ancient times from the Egyptians through to Greece and Rome. Also Its disappearance through the dark ages and finally with strong resurgence at the turn of the twentieth century sadly to be again suppressed after the advent of antibiotics. I was intrigued at the traditions of morning sunrise exercise among eastern cultures. I would like to look to find a group that practices tai chi, chi gong or yoga in the traditional way grounded outside at dawn. The one thing is when it comes to diet it fails somewhat as it pushes low fat. Other than that it should be on every mito hackers reading list. A good qoute that stuck out from the book is from an old Italian proverb -'Dove non va il sole,va il medico' or 'where the sun does not go, the doctor does.'

### Sun Disciple — 2017-03-18T15:58:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/post-210594>

Ive been lucky enough to catch a few full sunrises back to back. In fact the last two days have been straight sunshine. I was able to get out for about 4 or 5 hours yesterday between observing the sunrise, hiking in the woods and reading Cosmo Sapiens in the back yard.

I was even more lucky enough to observe a large fox that got pretty close to me while out in the field this morning at dawn. I very rarly bring my phone with me but I just happened to have it with me. (airplane mode of course). Ive been jealous of seeing Jack's sunrises from playa del carmen this week on facebook so I thought Id try and capture some nice ones from up north.

If you look close right to the left of the tree below the tree line you can see him

Here again just to the right of the tree. It was hard to see what i was filming as the sun was bright as hell most of the pictures came pretty washed out as he was right under the sun.

I thought it was a dog at first of course. A he got pretty close so I could positively I.D. from the tail and snout.

In other News, I recieved my RF meter in the mail yesterday. As you can see by the scary photo below Im currently being wifried on a daily basis. I took some basic measurements before I went to bed and lets just say I didnt sleep so well last night knowing that im being microwaved 24/7. This is a big wake up call and poses some difficult challenges for me to overcome. I have failed to convince my roommates to remove the wifi and moving into my own place is financially out of reach at the moment. so Im going to have to figure out a way to shield myself as best I can for now. Im going to relocate my bed and take further measurements today. I need to be mindfull not to add in any unnecessary stress surrounding this Revelation.Nothing has changed but this new information so I must stay positive and continue to move forward.

### Sun Disciple — 2017-06-17T21:29:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sun-disciples-journal.18790/post-216449>

Damn its been a long time since Ive posted in my journal but I have made mito hacking my full time job make no mistake. I am seeing some massive changes in me but I have made some massive changes in my enviroment to get here.
so lets get caught up shall we?
nnEMF mitigation
step one...

Measure your damn EMF! I wish _would have made this the first thing I did 7 months ago I would have made better progress from the begining this is real important folks.
From left to right we have a acousticom 2, alpha labs uhs 2 and a voltmeter I converted to a body voltage meter.
So first thing we learned is that RF was my biggest issue and magnetic fields were not an issue. As far as body voltage we learned where the hot spots are and where not to be spending much time in order to avoid french frying my mitochondria.
Here are the steps I tool to mitigate my enviroment in terms of nnEMF.
1\. Hardwired computer
2\. switched router to JRS eco wifi found here [https://www.jrselectrohealth.com/pcat/low-radiation-wifi-routers-jrs-eco-wifi/?c=cf13ce20305c](https://www.jrselectrohealth.com/pcat/low-radiation-wifi-routers-jrs-eco-wifi/?c=cf13ce20305c)
unfortunatly I cant have wifi switched off alll the time so this was next best option. First benefit is that you can controll the amplitude to decrease signal strength. This was usefull because my bedroom is as far away as you can get from the router and I barley get a signal in my room now. It also modulates the pulsed signal so it does not go off as much and reduces the intensity of RF measured on my acousticom. It aslo has a feature to schedule in wifi shut off times which is usefull._

_
Bed canopy!
Even when turning off all wireless devices within the house im still getting penetration from neigboors on both sides from cordless phones and wifi. Let me tell you everything from CT, oysters and UV Benefits have increased in there effectiveness after sleeping under it for a month. It was not cheap but it is effective. [http://www.slt.co/Products/BedCanopies/BedCanopySwissShieldNaturell.aspx](http://www.slt.co/Products/BedCanopies/BedCanopySwissShieldNaturell.aspx)
[http://www.slt.co/Products/BedCanopies/BedCanopySwissShieldNaturell.aspx](http://www.slt.co/Products/BedCanopies/BedCanopySwissShieldNaturell.aspx)
4\. shut breaker off at night as well as moved my bed to lowest reading using body voltage. Especially important with this canopy as its not grounded and will attract AC electric fields as I have confirmed through my measurements.

5\. Find some shelter! I searched long and hard for some outdoor areas of refuge where I might find some RADIO SILENCE.

I found a beach 15 minutes out of town that has zero RF. Most beaches I found were nearby cell towers sadly but this one came out hands down the safest spot. So I bought a parking pass for the summer and away I go. It provides an area to watch sunrises and CT which is huge! I spend all of my sunny days here.

_

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-30T02:41:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-panic-attacks.18792/post-204518>

You do..........and NE is not the problem. In my opinion, panic attacks and flicker of modern lights are the major issue.
Fluorescent light is four to six more efficient than an ordinary light bulb! so are the LED's in TV's and this is why Obama got the nation to stop using analog signals and began pushing LCD and LED under the guise to save energy. All TV's and technology screens are supreme blu elight flicker creators. This is why John Ott's Sarasota scholl experiment was so beneficial to teachers. If you don't know about the experiment read Helath and Light by Ott. You'll begin to see why you panic. If you ask them how to calm students they will tell you turning off the fluorescent lights is the best way to do it. Turning off the TV, video games, and laptops also works.

If you live in Europe you gave more to worry about with these lights because of the oscilaltions of their power grid and how they can interfere with mitochondrial oscillations in the retina to ruin melanopsin and retinol coupling to cause higher heteroplasmy rates.

Fluorescent tubes flash at twice the main frequency (100 Hz in Europe). With
aging, 50-Hz brightness modulation appears. A survey of fluorescent tubes used in most hospitals
showed that 42% exhibited brightness modulation up to a depth of 20% or
occasionally 30%.

_For any color changing system you have three different LED's today, a red, a green and a blue LED, and the intensity of these three colored channels has to be changed in order to achieve different color use, which is perceived by the retina in the end. The control of the intensity output of an LED is realized in a digital manner because it's very difficult to have a low intensity in many different steps._

_The dimming of LEDs is realized by a so-called pulse-width modulation, which means the LEDs switch on to the full intensity and then they fully switch off, and then they switch on again. So we have the constant on and off in frequencies, which are higher than our eyes are able to discriminate. But on the cellular level, it is still perceivable for the cells._

_This causes a flicker, which is not perceivable for let's say 90 percent of the population. But it's still biologically active. And flicker is something that is very harmful to your biology.
_
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4038456/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4038456/)

You've likely experienced this if you're old enough to recall the older TVs that had a very visible and intense flicker. Modern flat screens do not have this perceptible flicker, but they're still switching on and off below your ability to perceive it. What is affected by it is the coupling of melanopsin and retinol. Melanopsin works directly with the Vitamin A cycle (retinol) in the retina and in the brain at places like the paraventricular nucleus and amygdala to cause stress, fear, anxiety and panic. This occurs because the input to the amygdala is affects by the central retinaol pathways to allow neurons from the non visual imaging pathways to create a timing mechanism to tell the amygdala and para ventricular nucleus the proper information about the photoperiod of the environment. When this system does not get proper information anxiety and panic are the result. This is one of the cornerstones of how the "eye clock" forms the gears of time within the human brain. Vitamin A coupled cycling in the brain helps set the molecular clocks to fine tune the timing mechanisms of the light induced circadian rhythms.

Scientists are now trying to develop systems capable of transmitting information via high-frequency flicker in the LED lighting to replace the wireless LAN system. This is a very bad idea, from a health perspective because of how melanopsin couples to retinal, RXR, VDR, and DHA in the retina.

_I call these LEDs/modern lighting Trojan horses because they appear so practical to us. They appear to have so many advantages. They save energy; are solid state and very robust because they last so long. So we invited them into our homes wothout asking any biologic questions. But we are not aware that they have many stealth health-robbing properties, which are harmful to your biology, harmful to your mental health, harmful to your retinal health, and also harmful to your hormonal or endocrine health because of the effect on the central retinal pathways._ This is where your anxiety and panic come from.

Unfortunately, the use of LEDs has been mandated by federal policy in both the U.S. and much of Europe, in an attempt to conserve energy. While inarguably effective in that regard, the biological impact of these bulbs has been completely ignored, and by mandating them, options have been restricted. This means they are ubiquitous in their range and effect........blaming cold or NE is very disingenuous and shows a complete lack of understanding of light in my opinion.​

### MSH — 2016-11-30T16:28:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-panic-attacks.18792/post-204534>

Something else I might add that seems odd with regards to my panic attacks is that they didn't start until I quit cold-turkey using caffeine. My doctors said there was no connection between the two. But, without a doubt they started almost immediately after stopping caffeine ( I consumed large amounts of caffeine thru coffee, tea, & Dr. Pepper).

The first time I quit and I started experiencing the PA's I didn't know what was happening to me, I thought I was dying! So after a few weeks I couldn't take it and started back drinking coffee and they quit almost immediately!

But, the symptoms that urged me to quit caffeine in the first place started up again ( digestive issues and heart palpitations) so I quit again, and sure enough the PA's started back again and this time they were worse! Anytime I would have caffeine the PA's would stop but always within 3 days of stopping my PA's would come back with a vengeance!

I bit the bullet and quit for good knowing that although the PA's made me feel like I was going to die they couldn't kill me, so I endured all the while seeing all sorts of doctors and getting no answers. I was having multiple daily episodes for a little over two years. Now, in about my fourth year I still have them but only occasionally, but even that is more than enough!

I have talked to others on forums who experienced the exact same thing after quitting caffeine and like me their doctors had no answer except, " here, try this pill ."

Is there an answer as to why this happens and what does one do to overcome/fix it?

Thanks

Not sure if there is awareness that flickering may cause problems to some people.

Any comments? @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

.

Electrical frequency can easily be modified.
If 2x50=100Hz is problematic (for mitochondria) and 2x60=120Hz is somewhat less problematic,
how about converting frequency to some that is not hurtful.
Is there any frequency that you guess may be least hurtful?

### JanSz — 2016-11-30T18:25:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-panic-attacks.18792/post-204539>

[http://spectrum.ieee.org/nanoclast/...nable-next-generation-of-led-lighting-systems](http://spectrum.ieee.org/nanoclast/semiconductors/optoelectronics/quantum-dots-enable-next-generation-of-led-lighting-systems)
**Quantum Dots Enable Next Generation of LED Lighting Systems**

With [Samsung’s first quantum dot televisions shipping](http://www.hdtvtest.co.uk/news/ue65js9500-201502234012.htm) last month, [production is being ramped up](http://spectrum.ieee.org/view-from-the-valley/semiconductors/materials/behind-the-scenes-at-the-nanosys-quantum-dot-factory-in-milpitas-california) and the decade-and-a-half of promise appears to be coming true. Now researchers at the University of Hiroshima in Japan have used silicon-based quantum dots for a type of light-emitting diode (LED) that promises to revolutionize lighting systems. The Japanese researchers have [fabricated a hybrid inorganic/organic LED that produces white-blue electroluminescence](http://www.eurekalert.org/pub_releases/2015-06/hu-niu060515.php) using quantum dots. This white-blue electroluminescence LED promises a next-generation of illumination for flexible lighting and displays.

### JanSz — 2016-12-01T16:33:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hcv-daa-treatment-question.18793/post-204586>

.
This is, likely incomplete, list of diseases
that likely would match @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) description:
Neolithic Diseases
\=======================

ALS
Alzheimer’s
ASD
Autism
CFIDS
Fibromyalgia
Gross Fatty Acids Distortion
**Hepatitis C**
Lou Gehrig’s Disease
Lyme Disease
Motor Neuron Disease
Multiple Sclerosis
Parkinson’s
(PDD) pervasive developmental disorders
Post Stroke
\=========================

PKProtocol
may be a one way to deal with those diseases or at least get significant relief.
The idea of this protocol is rather simple and rater easy to comprehend, almost home made remedy.
One of the problems this protocol faces is verbal.
How to explain (to a lawyer) that
phosphatidylcholine
mixed with oil is a totally different product, and behaves differently then
phosphatidylcholine
devoid of oil.
\==========================

[http://pkprotocol.com/](http://pkprotocol.com/)
Dr. Patricia Kane’s Protocol

.
\------------------------------------------------------------
[http://pkprotocol.com/](http://pkprotocol.com/)
**The PK – Membrane Stabilizing – Protocol For Neurological Disorders**\[/SIZE\]
Patricia C. Kane, Ph.D., [Mark O’Neal Speight, M.D.](http://pkprotocol.com/speight.html),
[Katrin Bieber, M.D.](http://pkprotocol.com/bieber.html), [Meinrad Milz, MD](http://pkprotocol.com/milz.html),
[Shideh Pouria, MB BS BSc MRCP (UK) PhD CMT](http://pkprotocol.com/pouria.html),
[Damien Downing, M.D.](http://pkprotocol.com/downing.html), [Annette L. Cartaxo, MD](http://pkprotocol.com/cartaxo.html), Edward Kane

**Background:** The lipid soluble nature of toxicity has led us to seize the complexity of neurological presentations by addressing them from a cell membrane perspective. Examination of red cell lipids and identification of nuclear DNA adducts as chemicals, pesticides, microbials and metals in subjects with Motor Neuron Disease, Autism, Multiple Sclerosis, Post Stroke, Epilepsy, Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s Disease over the past eleven years in 9000 analyses has revealed a characteristic accumulation of very long chain fatty acids (VLCFAs), which comprise lipid rafts, or ceramides, revealing cell membrane derangement. Membrane phospholipid abnormalities with elevation of VLCFAs may be indicative of exposure to fat soluble neurotoxins resulting in suppressed peroxisomal beta oxidation of VLCFAs. Disturbances in methylation due to toxic exposure may destabilize the membrane phospholipid structure and alter DNA expression due to deficits in the enzymes Methylene Tetrahydrofolate Reductase (MTHFR) and Methionine Synthase.

**Objective:** The use of oral and IV lipids may facilitate stabilization of phospholipids in cellular membranes thereby addressing cell membrane integrity. The addition of intravenous phenylbutrate addresses neuroinflammation by increasing the beta oxidation of VLCFAs.

**Procedure:** To clear the bioaccumulation of toxins and stabilize membrane function we have embarked on a clinical treatment plan for the past five years to address the accumulation of aberrant lipids and ceramides with oral and IV phenylbutyrate, bolus intravenous phosphatidylcholine as Essentiale, methylation factors (folinic acid, riboflavin, methylcobalamin, tetrahydrobiopterin), and sulfation support (IV Glutathione).

**Results:** We have noted significant and sustained clinical neurological improvement within the first few weeks after initiation of oral and intravenous treatment in our patient population of 300 subjects.

**Conclusion:** The administration of Phosphatidylcholine, Leucovorin/Folinic acid, Glutathione and Phenylbutyrate may offer a new therapeutic strategy for neurological disorders involving neurotoxic exposure.

# Thread: Shocking thought of the day: Entanglement vs Spooky action at a distance
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocking-thought-of-the-day-entanglement-vs-spooky-action-at-a-distance.18795/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2016-11-30T03:06:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocking-thought-of-the-day-entanglement-vs-spooky-action-at-a-distance.18795/post-204519>

So, this shocking thought occurred to me in the early hours:

In terms of N=1, if we are entangled at a sub-atomic level can the biochemistry of those we are entangled with effect our own biochemistry. So, as an example if I struggle increasing my melatonin and vitamin-d levels and my three kids are in wifi environments all day and blue lit screens all night is there a possible sub-atomic cross-generational causative mechanism. I know that [couples in love synchronise](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/21910541) their respiration and heart rates but not sure if that has anything to do with entanglement - probably not.

In terms of nnEMF science, in Marino's book "Going Somewhere" in the last few pages he says that 50% of nnEMF experiments show harm, 50% show no harm and this is due to the inherent uncertainty of the human condition. Perhaps Marino is alluding to quantum entanglement/uncertainty principle.

In terms of Evolution, perhaps change/revolution/momentum only occurs when an entanglement threshold is met.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-30T05:01:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocking-thought-of-the-day-entanglement-vs-spooky-action-at-a-distance.18795/post-204522>

I think I stated my case clear in the recent Time series. It has to do with all quantum, processes.......entanglement and superposition and many others like the Kondo effect and frequency shifting of light by quinones.

When dealing with living things, one can best feel how primitive today's biology/medicine truly is. Mother nature was undaunted by the huge amount of work she needed to employ by designing cells. Such a task, on the surface, might be indistinguishable from a miracle until you realize she had all the time in the world, all materials one could need, and quantum mechanics.

Cells were built to connect to the entire fabric of Earth's environment. They are attuned and coherent to that end.

The environment on Earth is designed to create a "**de-coherence stimulus**" by using waveforms to affect the mitochondrial matrix which is situated in "a sea of cell water" surounding your mitochondria. Waveforms are the stimulus of change because they alter energy flows. Mitochondria are quantum sensors, like tsunami warning beacons in the ocean, buried within our cells to signal all other parts of the cell what energy can be made.

These waveforms are able to slightly alter the complex quantum possibilities built into living cells by Mother Nature. Those slight changes in the standing waves from the environment alters the % heteroplasmy rate to drive evolutionary trajectory because it can vary energy output in mitochondria. In this way, energy from a mitochondria perfectly reflects situations that develop in our environment as it varies with time.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Being Epi paleo while traveling Italy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/being-epi-paleo-while-traveling-italy.18797/>

**Posts:** 12

### John Smith — 2016-11-30T05:03:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/being-epi-paleo-while-traveling-italy.18797/post-204523>

How to be Epi paleo while traveling Italy?

I am travelling in January and February and will be there for a month.

I will be trying to eat little to no carbs while I am there. What are some good suggestions to eat there such as when at a restaurant and on the go. Keep in mind I may not always get access to a fridge. I was already thinking of cans sardines, a few pounds of grass fed tallow and ghee in large container as I need to load up on good fats during this time. I am not sure whether dry sausage (French saucisson sausage) a bit like salami is an okay option. It can contain sugar and unwanted preservatives and will most likely not be grass fed. What would you eat if you were there? I know there can be good pork and seafood there.

Since it is winter, should I be going around in a short sleeve shirt and wearing no coats of jackets since it will be most likely 30-45 degrees Fahrenheit. Is this too much CT?

I will try to drink as much spring water like San Pellegrino etc...

I will always wear blue blocking glasses indoors after sunset and before sunrise.

Since we are in short light cycles I will try to be outside when I can during the few hours of light. I will ground myself whenever it is possible.

I will do meditation everyday and reduce my stress levels as much as possible

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-30T05:15:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/being-epi-paleo-while-traveling-italy.18797/post-204526>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/being-epi-paleo-while-traveling-italy.18797/post-204535>

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-05T06:15:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/being-epi-paleo-while-traveling-italy.18797/post-204790>

### Lahelada — 2016-12-05T14:04:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/being-epi-paleo-while-traveling-italy.18797/post-204803>

What we know as Italian eating is only the commercial side. You would be surprised how little pasta for example is actually eaten. I lived in an Italian family in South Italy for a few weeks and the pasta is traditionally an appetizer ,followed by meat and or seafood and fruit as dessert. Pasta is fill up food for the poor. As most immigrants here or in the US came from poor backgrounds mostly the cuisine that arrived in the New World is a distorted version of the ancestral cuisine of the immigrant. So no worries,you will find plenty of food that is Epi paleo.Just stay away from the franchises and look for local places. The news that cheap refined stuff is marketable has arrived in Italy,too.

### Lahelada — 2016-12-05T14:06:27+0200

### freesia — 2016-12-16T05:36:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/being-epi-paleo-while-traveling-italy.18797/post-205329>

I was there earlier this year...local springtime.

We bought fresh produce every day at the markets and yes pork products and seafood are excellent! From deli type places we got really amazing sticks of preserved (fermented) pork with lots of fat, local olives, buffalo cheese (unless you don't do dairy). Ate a lot of mushrooms (different types) while we were there and little tomatoes which were right in season (they won't be for you). There is more than one type of mineral water in Italy and each restaurant will probably want to serve you a local one.
We stayed right away from franchises. We did eat gelato...artisan stuff not crap. (Sorry that's really not helpful if you want to be low-no carb!!)

Just wear what's comfortable for you. If you're well cold acclimatized so much the better...means you can wear less.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-16T16:02:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/being-epi-paleo-while-traveling-italy.18797/post-205337>

freesia for the win!

# Thread: Deciding where to live
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deciding-where-to-live.18800/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Juha — 2016-11-30T19:17:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deciding-where-to-live.18800/post-204546>

Hi!

I'm currently looking for a new place to live. Before I purchase anything I want to be sure that I make an educated choice EMF wise.

My current situation is such that I'm deciding between two alternatives - an apartment building or a townhouse.

Both of these options would place me in close proximity to other people, which means that I'm not in control of the EMF environment inside my home.

Here's the list of factors that I will be taking into account when making my decision:
\-above ground power distribution lines
\-the number of people living in close proximity (right behind my own walls)
\-any transformers nearby
\-cell phone or radio frequency towers
\-closeness to the city
**
Am I missing something in my list of factors to consider?

What are the best sources to study this subject so I'm able to make a wise decision?**

### kyrakitty — 2016-11-30T19:51:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deciding-where-to-live.18800/post-204548>

Check where the smart meter is in reference to your unit and especially your bedroom. If you will own it then you can probably have the smart meter exchanged for an analog one you'd have to check your state rules.

### Juha — 2016-11-30T22:08:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deciding-where-to-live.18800/post-204555>

Hey, thanks kyrakitty! I'll keep the smart meter in mind as well.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-30T23:51:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deciding-where-to-live.18800/post-204561>

hire an EMF enginneer before you spend $$$$

### JanSz — 2016-12-01T01:59:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deciding-where-to-live.18800/post-204572>

And in the places that you put on a short list
check with engineering department
and everybody else
for any development plans in the next 25 years

/

### Lahelada — 2016-12-01T13:54:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deciding-where-to-live.18800/post-204580>

If a townhouse or apartment is your choice you will not be optimal by looking at EMF alone. Also look at your escape options,the mitigation options
Availability of fresh seafood, good water and even places to escape to rebalance.

### Juha — 2016-12-01T19:59:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deciding-where-to-live.18800/post-204592>

Thanks for all of the answers guys! I will take these in consideration.

There sure is a lot to consider when picking a place to live.

# Thread: Enlarged Spleen & Positive ANA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/enlarged-spleen-positive-ana.18801/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NateToup — 2016-11-30T23:26:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/enlarged-spleen-positive-ana.18801/post-204558>

I decided to start a journal, as I begin down the road to optimal health.

Step 1 consisted of receiving a CAT scan to show an enlarged spleen that I have had for some time. I always thought it was just an uneven rib cage. But now this may make even more sense as to why I may have trouble with my serratus anterior. There is no chronic pain, but I remember a few rare instances where I did feel some discomfort in my spleen after eating and or laying in a certain position after eating

I also had some inflammation testing done, everything can back ok except for the ANA screening. I have a speckled pattern and titer of 1:40 H representative of a low antibody level.

Step 2 consists of hearing what comes next from my primary, whether its more blood work, an ASI, or food allergy testing.

Overall my general symptoms consist of; not sleeping, digesting, thinking, feeling as well as I should. I have blueish streaks in my bottom inner eyelids. The outside of my eyes are somewhat yellow and veiny. There is one other major symptom, that I can only best describe as more frequently impaired vs non impaired endothelial dysfunction. I certainly felt exponentially better doing movnat, barefoot in a nice grass field during the summer, but the same inflammatory issues have always been present ever since about a year ago when I destroyed myself on a vegan diet in the winter. The only good thing that came out of that was more research that lead me here.

At 20 years old I've decided when the opportunity arises I am moving further down the east coast, or out west, perhaps alone. There's nothing holding me back from staying in another New England Fall, Winter and Spring. I must relocate to a higher quantum yield environment. I've always been one to have Seasonal Affective Disorder, and a poor circadian rhythm.

Next post will be December 5th.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-11-30T23:48:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/enlarged-spleen-positive-ana.18801/post-204560>

Sounds for sure like and AI in evolution.........the link you mentioned to MovNat is interesting.

### NateToup — 2016-12-01T00:31:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/enlarged-spleen-positive-ana.18801/post-204565>

I've also had my tonsils taken out at a very young age, other than that I rarely got sick, only had spring allergies, but I do have pretty bad nearsightedness and there is no doubt there is some mold exposure in my home. X1 infinity wifi. possibly a painless but impacted wisdom tooth infection. It sure will be interesting and relieving to find the source of inflammation. I put the Epi Paleo Rx on my Christmas list. I'm familiar with most of your principles and as well as all the biohacks Ben has been doing, but I've been at odds dealing with this AI, and I am only qualified to do warehouse work. A rough cycle for something I took for granted.

### Jamie Ward — 2016-12-18T17:00:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/member-event-mexico.18802/post-205435>

For those going to Mexico:

Jack has agreed to do a video podcast recording on the beach. I thought it would be nice to do this with everyone there. I'll probably set the episode up, but then open it to a live Q&A with everyone.

So, if you fancy asking something then get thinking. I think a general theme would be this:

\- Ask Jack about the process he goes through to break his own beliefs. I heard him mention this somewhere recently and I really want to hear more about this.
\- Connectedness. I want to briefly talk about entanglement and how important social connectedness is. Essentially, why are we all in Mexico and what does this bring.
\- What's the end goal here? What's the vision?

We get so much technical information from Jack, I thought it would be interesting to hear more about the 'softer' side of things. How he thinks etc.

Anyway, I won't try to steer you all too much so over to you guys!

PS - If anyone has had any wild health journeys they would like to record a little segment over (say 10-20 minutes) I would be up for it. These could essentially be little case studies/testimonials. I think these are important to share as they motivate people.

I can't wait for Mehiiiiiccoooooooo!!

### cinnamon — 2016-12-20T21:49:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/member-event-mexico.18802/post-205545>

I won't be there this time, but reading this thread, I'm finding myself reliving some very nice memories of the Kruse Cruise in 2015. Did I tell you all about when I first met Jack?

Jack and Sandy hosted a wonderful bon voyage party at their house on Bourbon Street in New Orleans. Jack had been very insistent that those attending the party should come as their future selves - as they would be on the same date in 2025. So, I walked up to his house along Bourbon Street with @[yewwei.tan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6824/) and @[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) , in a long red and black dress. The dress was the one my future self had worn to the Academy Awards where I had just won an Oscar for Best Screenplay for a thriller called "Sun Game" (directed by Steven Spielberg and starring Sandra Bullock and George Clooney, naturally).
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/red-oscar-resized-jpg.4071/)

Jack came out the front door of his house wearing a pink shirt, pink shorts and a captains hat. (Yeah, I know, pink! I'm sure there is a reason for that. Is pink going to be good by 2025? hehehe ).
[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/jack-and-sandy-in-costume-pre-cruise-jpg.4072/)

He walked over to the gate to greet us. As I shook his hand and let him know that I was "cinnamon from the forum", I said, "Actually, we met for the first time about 10 years ago." He cocked his head and looked at me oddly, pausing as if trying to remember. I suspect if you could have been in his head, he was thinking I'm sure I've never met this woman before! I smiled and said, "Yes, back in 2015, I went on your inaugural cruise!" He grinned and wagged his finger at me. I think he liked it!

This was just one moment of many in what was a very magical trip. More magic is ahead for those going to Playa del Carmen.

I'll be entangled with you all in spirit and I'm looking forward to seeing Jamie's podcast and all the wonderful pictures and stories that will be shared.

love,
cinnamon

### Matty_M — 2016-12-22T00:10:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/member-event-mexico.18802/post-205624>

> [Jamie Ward said:](/goto/post?id=205435)
>
> For those going to Mexico:
>
> Jack has agreed to do a video podcast recording on the beach. I thought it would be nice to do this with everyone there. I'll probably set the episode up, but then open it to a live Q&A with everyone.
>
> So, if you fancy asking something then get thinking. I think a general theme would be this:
>
> \- Ask Jack about the process he goes through to break his own beliefs. I heard him mention this somewhere recently and I really want to hear more about this.
> \- Connectedness. I want to briefly talk about entanglement and how important social connectedness is. Essentially, why are we all in Mexico and what does this bring.
> \- What's the end goal here? What's the vision?
>
> We get so much technical information from Jack, I thought it would be interesting to hear more about the 'softer' side of things. How he thinks etc.
>
> Anyway, I won't try to steer you all too much so over to you guys!
>
> PS - If anyone has had any wild health journeys they would like to record a little segment over (say 10-20 minutes) I would be up for it. These could essentially be little case studies/testimonials. I think these are important to share as they motivate people.
>
> I can't wait for Mehiiiiiccoooooooo!!

I would be more than glad to record a section for you. I'm all in

At this point, I see the end goal as proving to Mother Nature that she didn't screw up completely by creating humans...The way I see it is that humans are the organism "furthest from entropy", so to speak; I don't believe it's anthropocentric to say that we can feel the most complex pleasure (e.g. dopamine/serotonin/oxytocin) of any organism alive; we know this because we have much bigger frontal lobes than any other organism and more dopamine, etc..

I see pleasure as manifestation of the "escape from entropy", which I currently see as the whole existence of life. Negative entropy, enthropy, etc. Of course, all of this is caused by energy from the SUN that energizes particles on the surface of the earth in specific ways that created us. UBI series is my current reading.

See you down there!

### Rubicon — 2016-12-23T02:37:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/member-event-mexico.18802/post-205688>

It's looking like despite the excellent housing selection in Progreso and ideal location at the center of the crater, the beach is just not going to work here (water is murky/cloudy this time of the year). So, I'll be heading to PDC on Saturday for the next couple of weeks.

Just a heads-up to anyone staying at a location where you plan on doing your own cooking...bring non-fluoridated salt! In my experience, throughout the Yucatan almost all the people I've seen look consistently unhealthy. The mandated fluoride in the salt that makes it into all the food at the restaurants must have something to do with that (HT to lohd2015 for that observation)

### Lahelada — 2016-12-26T14:17:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/member-event-mexico.18802/post-205791>

When you land and walk out of the gates you will probably be met with everybody vociferously trying to get you to their taxi. Do not respond to any as the offers almost always have a catch. Use the official taxi stands where you prepay your cab and then get in. Many hotels have their own shuttle bus. However if you are going straight to Playa del Carmen and are on a budget you will need to plan ahead. It is a 45 min cab ride or one hour and a a half bus ride. I have no current data of the cost of taxis. You can google for it though.

A lot of people speak one way English, meaning they can explain something but may not readily be able to assimilate a question.

Generally if you pre book the shuttle they have the driver standing in the airport hall holding a sign with their company name.
This sort of explains the set up . [https://www.tripadvisor.ca/Travel-g150812-s301/Playa-Del-Carmen:Mexico:Arriving.And.Departing.html](https://www.tripadvisor.ca/Travel-g150812-s301/Playa-Del-Carmen:Mexico:Arriving.And.Departing.html)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-26T15:37:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/member-event-mexico.18802/post-205792>

Pre booking your ride to PDC is very WISE. You can do it on line.

### Rubicon — 2016-12-26T17:37:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/member-event-mexico.18802/post-205800>

I find the experience at the Cancun International Airport is terrible. When you exit the terminal you'll be in an enclosed area with shuttles & taxis that will aggressively try to court you. The airport has gates setup to keep you from walking from the arrivals area to the departures area where you could catch a taxi that's dropping someone else off or a private ride. If you go through those gates, you may not be able to go back. The airport does this to control which taxis can pickup at the airport. It's very weird and scammy.

If you want to be very economical, take the bus to PDC. They're comfortable and run often. The name of the main bus line that goes to/from the airport is ADO. They have a stand inside the airport arrival terminal and outside at the bus stop. For ~180 pesos ($15USD) it will take you directly to the bus terminal in PDC ~Cancun~. ~From there you can buy a new ticket for perhaps $10-20 and transfer to another bus that will go to the main terminal in PDC.~ It's a short taxi ride from the ADO terminal in PDC to the Gran Porto that should cost around 50 pesos (~$3USD). Most taxis don't have meters, so always ask how much it is before accepting a ride. ~Most~ Some Mexican's working in transportation/tourism speak at least enough English to get by. I find if you speak slowly and clearly, many of them should be able to understand you. (but I do speak a bit of basic Spanish)
Note: ~The bus may not be running from the airport after 10PM.~ There is free wi-fi at all the bus terminals.
[ADO Bus Schedule from Cancun airport to Playa Del Carmen](http://everythingplayadelcarmen.com/ado-bus-schedule-from-cancun-airport-to-playa-del-carmen/)

The 'best value' is probably to take a shuttle directly from the airport to PDC. You could probably negotiate between $30-$60 for that, or book online as Jack mentioned.

Taxi would be the fastest option as Lahelada mentioned. My guess is it would be $60-100USD to PDC

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-27T04:07:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/member-event-mexico.18802/post-205815>

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-27T04:12:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/member-event-mexico.18802/post-205816>

> [Marbear said:](/goto/post?id=205814)
>
> I get into Cancun around 2pm. My travel agent already booked my transportation. She said just go past all the people trying to sign you up for stuff and look for a driver with The Royal hotel sign. She said it could take some time to get to the hotel depending on how many stops there are.
>
> I know Jack said he already had pesos but is this for when I go out of the resort? Like I said, first time to Mexico so I'm not sure what to bring/how much to get, etc.

If you pre pay you dont need any peso's until you get to PDC. Their are ATM in the hotel and on fifth ave. I got mine form my bank. I usually have pocket money to burn with my wife. She likes to spend money. At the resort you wont need any money unless you chose to tip. The service at the place is exceptional so you may want to allow for tips if your service is good. 5th ave has a ton of shopping but almost every place takes CC. They have fresh coconuts for sale everywhere for a few bucks. They do take american dolars but you will get screwed on the exchange if you go this route. There is also a nice mall a few blocks form the resort and a nice place to get a message and pedicure on the cheap one block from the Royal if you dont want to spend th emoney at the resort. Me.........I get tons of spa.

### lilly antic — 2017-01-09T23:27:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/member-event-mexico.18802/post-206423>

Back from Playa and the member event in Mexico.

We had a beautiful week there and I would like to share some of my experiences:

First of all a big **Thank you** to Jack and Sandy for having suggested this wonderful trip to this amazing place and resort- and then of course **THANK You** to all of you guys who were there and made this week so special!!!

Apart from great weather, sun and beach this stay was from beginning to the end filled with laughter, fun, jokes and great conversations.
I really miss you now back home and looking at the pictures brings a big grin to my face remembering those funny moments we shared together....

While there I observed some remarkable details, which make me want to explore the whole topic a bit more:

1\. Like on my stay in Cuba, I noticed a change in my sleep.
Usually I have no issues with sleeping, only a slight annoyance with the intensity and quantity of my dreams. It is great to have dreams, but if the night is filled with movie-like episodes, sometimes the morning feels exactly like that-like after a night full of movies.
In Mexico, it resembled more the dream state of my youth ( and while in Cuba)- some dreams but also some deep. deep, deep restful sleep. Like putting your head on the pillow and next thing you know it is morning.
I was wondering about that fact, as the place is filled with a high density of antennas everywhere. It must be connected to the strong magnetic field, which is found in this area....
Another remarkable fact is my weight.
We were staying in an all-inclusive resort, with many great restaurants and food around the clock.
At home I am eating mostly 1, sometimes 2 meals per day. I don't have weight issues, but was struggling the last year to get rid of some ( for my taste) annoying 2 kg, which were resistant to go, no matter what method or trick I applied.
So here I was in Playa, eating ( and complaining about the fact) all day- although there was no real hunger- the meals were more for social reasons.
You can imagine my surprise when after a few days those 2 extra kg vanished into thin air ALTHOUGH I have been eating more than usual.
(My complaining stopped of course immediately hahaha)

I am so happy that I could make it this year ( after I already missed the cruise), and am really, really looking forward to the next event.

If you can make it, I highly recommend to come. Would love to meet more of you guys and spend some time together.

Meeting someone set the stage for collapsing the wave form. within 10 seconds I can tell a lot about somebody and when I met Sazzle I knew Ruben was right to drag me to London. I talked to Jeremy about this at lunch. Meeting you set the tone for what happened at lunch with the teacher over there. I entered a state of being grateful instead of being pissed off about the flight. It change how the London trip unfolded. Be grateful not hateful. When we remain entangled to gratitude, we radiate a frequency of love and tranquillity. That radiation makes us glow to others. That signal draws people to us. We become irresistible to those who value goodness. This attractor field of loveliness tends to bring out the best in other people without much effort. With gratitude in our heart, our state of mind, our state of being, and our outlook on life is contagious.

### Sun Disciple — 2017-02-04T19:33:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/member-event-mexico.18802/post-207944>

Hey All!

I am looking to visit playa del carmen in march when Jack is heading down again. Im looking to book somewhere to stay this week and the Royal and grand porto are out of my price range unfortunately this year. My question is did anyone stay anywhere else or have any suggestions for accomodations close by. I am traveling by myself as well.

### Rubicon — 2017-02-04T21:52:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/member-event-mexico.18802/post-207950>

Hi Sun Disciple. I was at the 2017 event in January and stayed in a nearby apartment I found on AirBNB. The problem with doing this is that the grounds of the Royal are off limits unless you're a guest. They check everyone for armbands and it's not really practical to repeatedly sneak in without one.

About PDC:
The Royal/Gran Porto are in the touristy area of PDC and as a result the prices of everything there are substantially higher than what you might typically find in Mexico. Typically as you go from the beach into PDC the prices of everything rapidly falls. Along the beach up to 5th ave (Calle Quinta Avenida) you'll find taxis/restaurants with inflated prices. From 10th ave to 20th is a little more reasonable and you can find a taxi probably at half price. At 30th ave and up you're back in Mexico with lots of cheap places to eat and normal prices on everything...particularly if you can speak a little bit of spanish and try not to look like too much of a tourist.

Here are a few ways people did the January event, and the results they had:

Minimal expense ($30-$60 per day): stay in a hostel or a cheap AirBNB accommodation in town located 30th ave or beyond. It's practical to walk to the beach or take a taxi for 30-50 pesos each way. You can survive on cheap taco's/groceries without spending much. With this option you'll get to hang out on the beach when Jack & others are outside the hotel grounds, especially at sunrise and throughout the morning/afternoon. After sundown typically Royal guests go for dinner inside the grounds and it will be difficult/impossible to join in on that.

Medium expense ($150-$200 per day): You can buy a daypass at the Gran Royal for $105USD per day that gives you access from 9:00AM to 11:00PM without booking a room. The daypass lets you use the towels/chairs on the beach, CT tank in the spa, and eat at the restaurants (including all-you-can-eat sushi at Asiana). With this option you pretty much get the whole experience of being able to hang out with Jack & other members, but you'll have to travel back and forth to your accommodation in town.

High expense ($350-$600 per day): Stay at the Royal, all inclusive. Note: The Royal is adults only, whereas families can stay at the Gran Porto. In January, those staying at the Gran Porto could not easily access the Royal and they typically would eat apart from the main group at restaurants inside the Gran Porto.

I did the daypass thing and found it to be good value considering it includes 3 meals per day.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-02-04T23:07:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/member-event-mexico.18802/post-207954>

Rubicon was the meals worth the money of the band????

### Rubicon — 2017-02-05T00:38:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/member-event-mexico.18802/post-207969>

I'd say the meals were worth the cost of the band.

The restaurants I dined at on nearby 5th ave typically cost $40-$60 for dinner, and the quality wasn't spectacular. Going to 10th ave or further, I found some quality options for ~$25-$30 per plate.

At the Royal I ordered as much of anything on the menu at the Pelicanos restaurant and/or accessed the buffet for breakfast/lunch. Dinners at the seafood/sushi restaurant Asiana's featured huge platters of sashimi, roast duck, rack of lamb, etc. Good quality food all around.

### Freader — 2017-02-12T03:27:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/member-event-mexico.18802/post-208439>

PDC was a wonderful experience! I'm so glad I decided to go and take my husband because he loves the sun. He is now curious and asks questions. We are both looking forward to going again in March. I'm learning to make the sun my friend and for me that is a big deal! Thanks to Sandy and Jack for their hospitality and generosity and everyone I met. I hope to see you again at another event soon!

### JMO — 2017-04-04T06:16:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/member-event-mexico.18802/post-211705>

We were fortunate to overlap our spring break vacation with Jack and Jeremy and a few other members. We were lucky to meet a woman at our hotel, Eva that hooked us up with an awesome taxi/tour guide. We ended up going to Tankah cenote close to Tulum in the middle of the jungle. The water was so cool and invigorating that we stayed for close to 3 hours. We tried to leave at least 3 times and ended up jumping back in...like it was calling our names. Finally Señor Cruz said he got a phone call from the owner and said we would have to leave because they were going to let the alligators out at 4...lol. We went into Tulum and had some great seafood then went to Paradise beach. The beach was beautiful but was crowded. The water beautiful blue. We walked down the beach at the edge of the water down towards the ruins then back. It was a magical day! The next day we were fortunate to hook up with Jack and Jetemy. Our 17 year old daughter was with us and it was amazing to see how Jack and Jeremy focused in on her to educate her. We have been trying to teach her about light, water and magnetism for awhile now and she has implemented some protocols but how this meeting has impacted her has blown us away! She has heard us listening to so many podcasts and our conversations about Jacks teachings but it became real when she was able to meet Jack and Jeremy and others from the forum. She has become a warrior and taken steps to help her friends. She took some Ubex glasses over to her friends and explained why she is having problems sleeping. Last weekend she was over a friends and their parents had some friends over and she was educating the adults about whtat she knew about quantum physics. The next day she dropped Uvex glasses off for one of the parents. She can't wait to go back in Dec for the next member event. The Royal is an absolutely beautiful hotel with even a cold tub and infrared sauna. I have found myself dreaming about Playa...the cenote and feeling very grateful to Jack and the others on the forum for a memorable experience for my family.

### Butters — 2016-12-01T15:11:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pulmonary-blood-pressure.18803/post-204583>

Im 26. I just had an echo echo for my heart. All functions were right, valves ok, ejection fraction 75%. The doc almost forgot to mention my pulmonary blood pressure and it was 20.5 mg/Hg. Thats on the upper end of normal. I looked up and 21-24 is slightly latent hypertension in the pulmonary artery.
So I am a bit worried.

What could be the cause and what can I do about this?
I had no asthma etc. in my past.

Edit.: The numbers are varying. I dont know if it was mean or systolic pressure. So its pretty worthless. But just for information, the question is standing there.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-01T20:28:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pulmonary-blood-pressure.18803/post-204594>

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-08T04:42:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheers-from-43rd-parallel-in-sw-ontario-canada.18810/post-204965>

Tonight's factoid: Polar environments can teach those people above 50 latitude North some great things. The relevance of polar observations scales to other regions like the UK and Scandinavia where heart disease, autoimmunity, and cancer incidence grows with every decade since the 1880's. Polar regions are greater teachers of the 3 legged stool of how life is organized. Polar regions provide a useful environments for observing and optimizing the intensity and spectral composition of ambient light. Most of this light is sun light in the summer and artificial light in autumn and winter. Many circumstances in the high latitudes during winter with the short photoperiods allow for unique windows to see how humans react. When we add the complexity with an interior workplace, which are associated with a lack of bright natural light, the observations paint a clear picture of why shift work at night in low latitudes is deadly. In midwinter in the UK, for example, London at 52°N, travel to and from work will often be completed wholly in artiﬁcial light, since from 4 December to 7 January the sun rises after 08:00 h and sets before 16:00 h. Right now Londoner's are in this veyr window. Workstations may well have no windows, and outdoor activity at lunchtime or during daylight hours may be difficult, if not undesirable in poor weather conditions. Thus, data from polar regions are likely to have applicability in urban environments with winter daylengths shorter than the working day. And those observations have not been kind to those subjects. “Midwinter insomnia” has repeatedly been described for regions of northern Europe, particularly Norway, and is characterized by difficulties falling asleep. These people tend to lose Stage 4 sleep routinely. The available information suggests that internal desynchrony will be associated with metabolic abnormalities, particularly risk factors for heart disease, and possibly cancer. Similarly, cardiac function and, indeed, all physiology governed by circadian rhythms (Hastings et al., 2003) is likely to be affected. Subjects free-run infrequently in a structured polar environment in winter, but this does happen, especially after a period of night shift. Most readily adapt to the night shift, but the readaptation to day work is often very slow in winter. All of these problems can be addressed by maintaining a consolidated and suitable circadian phase. The treatment of choice, both in winter and summer, is carefully timed light exposure and avoidance as a function of endogenous phase, with or without carefully timed melatonin treatment.

No Kidding!! I grew up in Scotland, ON, just up the road. My parents still live there on highway 24 in the house I grew up in....and the world continues to get smaller....

My property used to be a sheep farm when I was a kid at which time my paternal grandparents owned it. Then it was used to grow Ginseng and where I worked as a teenager. Now it's an overgrown Christmas tree farm that has about 10 acres of differing conifers which will be eventually cleared. Goats, chickens, a jersey cow and bee hives will be populating my place within the next 18 mos. Right now I do a little organic gardening, mostly dill, cilantro, oregano, sage, carrots, cherry tomatoes, strawberries, mint, cucumbers and sugar baby watermelons. Lots of mullberry trees and black caps (black raspberry) grow wild on my land.

So you live in Canada home of the low UV world right? Do you have any fluoride in your life too with the shitty light? Say in your water or drugs you use? I bet you do........can this cause floaters? Yep.

Fluoroquinolones can be dangerous in people with a low redox and low levels of UV light assimilation and utilization in mitochondrial at NAD+. Why? They contain fluoride which lowers the ability of UV light to be absorbed and stored in the exclusion zone of cell water. They also cause unwinding of cellular collagen even in your lens of your eye and the vitreous. Quinolones also absorb UV light because of their quinone ring stores light inside their structure and not allowing it to make it available to the cell when fluoride is around because it is a dielectric blocker in water. There is reason to believe this affect may have long lasting effects. The possible side effects number over 100, including death, blindness, deafness, hearing loss, peripheral neuropathy, extreme muscle wasting, tendon rupture, joint pain, electric shocks in arms, legs, and chest, insomnia, anxiety, panic, brain fog, memory loss, muscle twitching, confusion, abnormal dreams, headaches, depersonalization, depression, suicidal thoughts, hallucinations, seizures, itching, hair loss, loss of collagen, wrinkles, skin rash, phototoxicity, difficulty swallowing (aphasia), abdominal pain, diarrhea, nausea, acid reflux, maldigestion, food sensitivities, dry eyes, **_floaters_**, double vision, retinal tears, tinnitus, dizziness, loss of balance, hearing loss, difficulty speaking, heart palpitations, tachycardia, purpura, petechiea, shortness of breath, hypotension, hypertension, lightheadedness, sweating (hyperhydrosis), lack of sweating (anhydrosis), feeling cold, unexplained weight change, hypoglycemia, diabetes, excessive thirst and urination, loss of libido, low testosterone, dental problems, extreme fatigue, chemical sensitivity, food sensitivities, and many more.
If you take Cipro, Levaquin, Avelox, or any other fluoroquinolones, including eye and ear drops, according to the FDA, you are at risk of developing any/many of these symptoms. If your water is fluoridated..........you need to do better. Become aware and talk to your doctors about these issues.

I do live in Canada. South east corner of Saskatchewan. Yes there is next to no UV light in Canada for much of the year. There is naturally occurring fluoride in the ground water that is used for the water supply in the town that I live in. They do not add any though. I am not on any meds. I do consume some supplements but only started to do so after the eye floaters started. I weigh approx 150 lbs and have never really been over weight. When I was younger I did consume large amounts of refined sugars and carbs but don't so much now. I try to follow a paleo diet but like most people I slip up now and then. I have been a chronic iPhone user for probably the past 5 years. I have spent a lot of time under artificial light and in front of a computer screen (thanks to post secondary education). I do not spend much time in front of a computer anymore and work out side quite a bit. I am starting to suffer slightly from myopia, I have been given glasses by the eye doctor but cant wear them because they make me feel messed up. It seems like some days I am myopic and others I am not. I don't know how to explain it. I also suffer from tinnitus from time to time. I do drink alcohol frequently in the form of red wine, beer or vodka and mix. I don't seem to have any trouble sleeping but my energy levels are very up and down. The floaters have caused me a great deal of stress and anxiety. I have joined many eye floater forums and facebook groups but there is no real information to help with the problem.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-06T01:00:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eye-floater.18814/post-204840>

If you abide by your educational ignorance for a long enough time you hope you’ll stumble into wisdom, in my opinion. This small success makes you less fearful to ask the big questions about things at the boundary or circumference of our knowledge. This is how progress really leaps forward and improves the heft of our intellect. People who ask the big questions usually become innovators of new ideas. Rarely will you find them spending time worrying about their critics or skeptics in the temperate latitudes between the two spaces called good and average, where most people spend 99% of their lives. We must become more comfortable with probability and uncertainty and realizing the boundary of our knowledge and the wall of our ignorance is an easy way to do it. With nature and life it is a given that there's always the risk that we’re suffering because there are unknown unknowns. Don’t fear that……..chase them. I chase my ignorance to ask better questions all the time.
